text:
This Chipotle location was great! I wanted to thank Chipotle for recognizing teachers for Teacher Appreciation Week! 
The line was very small and they served quickly as a pose to another location that had the line wrapped around the store! 
Their food is always fresh, tasteful and fulfilling! 
My go to is always the bowl with white cilantro lime rice,black beans, steak and all the toppings! 
Must eat with a bag of fresh tortilla chips!!

Although the chicken is always very good, there was a "sulky" male employee on Sunday, Jan 1 with a puss-face putting my package together 16 pc grilled chicken meal.  Out in the open he was complaining about work.  Honestly, if he doesn't like working there or the pay, the manager should allow him to make a decision, change his attitude or leave.  This doesn't leave customers with a positive feeling about the place, especially when he didn't load all that was supposed to be in my order.  Where's the rolls/biscuits?  Pay attention, it doesn't cost anything.

I was hoteling nearby and was hankerin' for sum friiiiied chicken!   Did the drive thru 05.06.16 and found no reason to fault them for food or service.  Ordered the #10 box meal + add a breast.  Comes w/ drink.  The visit hit the spot and was very typical of the experience I've had at several other KFC's.  My service was fast & friendly.  Employees on point.  Ringin' the bell as I leave like at Long John Silver's.  Good job crew!

Can't believe I haven't tried Chipotle until today!  Me and my girlfriend decided to go for dinner this evening, and from what she told me I was pretty excited.  As you walk in and approach the counter you can see the different protein choices, salsas, rice, etc.  I decided to go with a burrito with white lime-cilantro rice, black beans, steak, pico de gallo, and cheese.  The burrito was massive, and was less than 7 dollars!  Ingredients all tasted fresh, very refreshing.  I honestly can't wait to go again!  My only complaint is that the chips we shared were stale, and horribly over salted.  4 stars.

Do not ever go to a Fried Chicken Joint and complain about the side dishes, especially the standard factory cans of commercial puddings ? lol. who does that ? The Pinellas Park eatery is a place to GORGE yourself on all the Chicken you can eat ! Do it and do it well.

Oh boy... why do I continue to torture myself with this KFC location? After 2 months of a diet I allow myself a cheat day and I stupidly (Why God, why?) pick this location for a fried chicken craving I had. Firstly, more than half the time my wife and I have been to this location they are always "out" of the extra crispy all white which is my go to order. It doesn't matyter the time of day OR day of week, "Sorry sir, we just ran out". How and WHY does this happen on a continual basis. Well, this time my wife had the forthought of recording the conversation in the drive thru. Sure enough, the female employe started with the "Sorry maam, we don't have that enymore". Well, someone must have seen my wife holding the cellphone up to the speaker box through the mirror because  another man started yelling something in the background and the lady said, "Excuse me maam, we DO have some, if you'd like to wait 10 minutes we'll get it for you." My wife said "yes" and waited for the order..... Well, WTH! She brought the order home, I opened the box and Yippie, a 3 piece ALL white freaking ORIGINAL recipe. Seriously? My wife felt horrible she didn't check the order before leaving but after all of that didn't feel it was necessary, and you know what, she shouldn't have to. Raise the minimum wage for fast food workers to $15 my %^#  !!!

I hit the drive-thru at this White Castle last night with my aunt on our way home from Carmel. I was starving and it hit the spot. 

Our order was taken right away and in a friendly manner. The food seemed to take several minutes, but was hot and fresh and worth the wait. It was my first time trying the new veggie sliders. They aren't THE best veggie burgers I have ever had, and are quite high in calories from what I hear, but were good. I appreciate that White Castle started offering these for the cravers that no longer do meat.

The only small disappointment was that when we were asked if we wanted sauce, what kind of sauce I do not know, I requested mustard on my sliders and that was left off. There also were no napkins or salt in the bag. It was hard to tell my veggie slider from my aunt's fish sandwich digging in the bag in the car. The large portions on our medium fries made up for it though as well as being a quick and convenient place for someone like me to grab a meal on the run that contained some much needed protein.

Service was friendly and the restaurant seemed clean. It appears to be either a newer store or a very well-kept one. Bonus was seeing one of the Indy chicken limos down the road.

Updating to say, while good tasting, the food I had did not sit quite right with me.  It was possibly that I'm not used to greasy combined with being out of sorts lately. Tastes good, but I may need antacid or something next time.

We received a coupon for a buy one-get-one, so we decided to try this place. We go to Qdoba all the time, so I thought this would be a similar experience. 

Service: All of the staff we interacted with, while placing our food order, were very polite and upbeat. We took our food to go as we live a two minute drive away. 

Food: I ordered a chicken burrito bowl and my husband ordered a steak burrito with fajita veggies. I was eating in the home office and bit into quite a few cold chicken pieces. I ate around the chicken as I have an unnatural fear of food poisoning. We live very close to the restaurant, so either the container the chopped chicken in was cold, or the meat was not cooked all the way. I went to go check on my husband in the living room and he stopped eating all together. Half way through his burrito, he found a 4 or so inch long brown hair. Yay for finding hair in your food! I asked him if he wanted me to call the restaurant and he said no. He just tossed his food, as did I. 

Four hours later and my stomach is still not agreeing with me. So, go to Chipotle with caution... At least the staff was nice.

We don't eat at KFC very often. I had the lame idea of trying it again. First off, the cashier, Michelle, had a SERIOUS attitude problem. Rude as the day is long. No smile, no hello, no thank you. Looked like she really hated her job. We told her our food was dine-in. It was put in a to go bag, no plates, butter, sporks, etc. I didn't dare ask for plates. Chicken was under-cooked. Slaw and potatoes were OK. Biscuits fair. Are we going back? NOPE!!! Bad attitudes don't fare well with me.

I just love to dispel a trend of bad reviews! We stopped in at this Del Taco despite a series of bad reviews and we are glad we did! We wanted to try their fish taco special at two for three bucks. We were not expecting much. First let's discuss the product. The fish fillet was about the size of a wiener cut in two. A little on the smallish size. It was wrapped in two small corn tortillas. Tasted great,not greasy, beer battered. Special price so I bought six for a total of nine bucks, we were stuffed!
    Let's talk about the wait help, in this case Trent. What a jewel! A tremendous asset to any business! He welcomed us quickly and efficiently! A tittle talkative but in a very good way! The tables were clean,the food served quickly and tasty! Five stars for sure!

25 Sep 2018

We went in and found the establishment was worn. It was difficult to read the menu. The menu did not say one could purchase an individual piece of chicken. The lady behind the counter was as slow as molasses in the wintertime.

When we finely stepped up to the counter the employee was unable to address my questions about the menu. Her speech was inaudible.

I asked if she spoke english, I'm pretty sure she did, but not very well. My wife and I walked out without ordering.

We will not be back.

Chipotle is the Targét of the Mexican fast food game. 

What's not to love about Chipotle?  Burritos (or tacos/ bowls) made your way. Quick. Hot. Fresh. Generally great service at this Cherry Hill location.  They also have a really good soundtrack playing whenever I go in. 

My go to is the carnitas burrito but you can't really go wrong with anything.  Good rice, fresh salsa and guacamole.  

And yes, we are aware the guacamole is extra. 

As for the price, it's a bit more expensive than your normal fast food joint, but then again the quality and freshness is a step up.

The decor is old and in desperate need of an update but the food is always consistent   The chicken tenders are the best in the business and what else needs to be said about the taters?  Just yummy!!  Not good for you but OMG are they delicious  the prices are a little higher than they need to be but it's a nice change

Not the best KFC.  I ordered a family dinner, but apparently it just comes with loads of wallpaper paste (mashed fake potatoes) as sides, and in the tiniest prunt on menu, shows that any other side is an upcharge. 
Ended up paying over $32 instead of $25 because we opted out of fake potatoes. Clerk was ignorant. Got home and many pieces of extra crispy chicken had breading cracked off. 
Not agreat value any more for dinner.

The staff was friendly and the service was fast, but the food was mediocre at best. I ordered a simple double whopper combo. The fries were cold and stale, the edges of the bun were hard, and the vegetables on the burger were warm and wilted like the burger had been under a heat lamp. Overall it wasn't totally inedible, but I definitely expect more for a fast food place that charges almost $10 for a combo meal.

Traditionally I have no issue here but my last two visits haven't been great. I ordered sofritas on my lunch break. Drive back to the office to eat. I mixed it up and notice their is beff mixed in it. Had to drive all the way back. Being more careful could've avoided this.

Avoid the steak. I have eaten their twice and found several pieces of it were mainly fat or grisle. One time I complained to the manager and he said it was hard for them to tell the difference when they grilled it. The other meat items are more reliable although I am no fan of shredded chicken either. My friend had the vegetarian bowl which looked better. Other ingredients looked good and were tasty.

So chipotle is so amazing! I feel like everytime I think about going, gods killing kittens! Why? This addiction is evil!! Lol. Anyhow I love this place for all their organic meats, the chicken is amazing, the Barbados is even better, what mood will I be in next week when the carnitas is going to end up in my burro?

Decor is awesome, the place is well laid out, and they always have some good tunes playing. 

You ready for the bad part?
Ok, so my only gripe, is how these bastards don't mind a HUGE spoon of rice and beans but then they go in with the tongs and shake out half the meat!! WTF! don't skimp on the best part! 

P.S. I always tell them to add my sauce to a cup on the side!! And I slather my burro in it as I eat! Get the hot, it's insane good.

Nuff said.... K bye!
I had the steak bowl and it was delicious.  Since it's so new in our area the lines have been out the door so I recommend ordering on line which is very easy and you walk right up to get your food.

Horrible I gave it a one-star because I couldn't find a zero star this place is the absolute pit of a restaurant drive through when we pulled up they said we had to go around because the drive-thru wasn't working come to find out all they were doing was counting out the till and switching over cashiers biscuits no biscuits had to wait 10 minutes for those, pieces of chicken look like little piece of chicken wings at least six employees just walking around  doing absolutely nothing the place looked dirty horrible will never come back

We have been living in the area for two years. I have watched the steady decline of this chipotle. 
We eat at chipotle at least two times a week because of our jobs.
This place has become the great guacamole nazis. Instead of the normal sized spoonful that everywhere else gives, this place taps the spoon until it's leveled off. Leaving one with about a 1/4 cup of guac on their burrito or bowl. . I should'nt have to pay $2 for that teeny bit!!!

Next situation I have had with this chipolte is that we stopped in fifteen min before closing. With people in line BEHIND me as well, the counter girl explained they were out of chicken and steak and sour cream and only had enough guac left for one serving .....I called them out "you mean u guys were too lazy to cook up more and restock?!" 
Shrugging her shoulders she replied "do I want something or not?"
The manager person came out after hearing my complaints about paying $10 for a bowl with nothing in it! Comped my bowl but still lazy. 

I've also ordered twice online. Both times they claimed they didn't see it and had to rush to complete once I got there. 

Whomever does the hiring for this chipolte needs to be fired. Their workers suck lately

Chipotle gets a big "meh" from me.  I admit I might be hard on them because of their reputation for being amazing, but I just find it to be okay.  And yes, I do get to pick all of my ingredients, but I've been 3-4 times and ordered different items and combinations each time.  I just don't find the flavors to be very inspired.  There are other fast Mexican/build your own burrito/bowl places in town that I find to be much better, and will way more variety to choose from.

The one thing I really appreciate at Chipotle is that they offer "sofritas" which is a vegetarian option.  I'm not a vegetarian, but I appreciate having the option to go meat-free!

I thought it was marginal given all the hype. I was under the impression that the burritos were relatively large - I guess mostly just full of rice. Also choosing steak was a mistake - it was all kinds of chewy and gristly. I'd go again if someone else wanted to and I'd probably get something else.
Too many problems to note.  Bad service; seem to be or are currently unavailable one type of chicken; missing item with drive-thru orders; the list goes on....

I think 3 stars is generous. It's really a 2.5. The staff was really friendly, restaurant was clean, and food was fast. Thats just it. It's fast food. I went with a basic famous star burger and it was identical to a whopper from burger king. This was my first visit to a Carl's jr, and i was expecting a little better. A combo with onion rings cane to $11 and change. So, in par.with fast food.

Nothing right in this place except they had a clean restroom. Fries were cold and dark, as they usually are. I ordered a burrito, it took 20 minutes to get it after many people were served in front of me. Service in this establishment SUCKS!!!!!!  Everything in this restaurant needs to change , they need to train and hire people who truly care about their work. they really need to toss out the old food they over cooked and not force it down the throats of their customers. French fries, Beans, Salads, Egg, Taco meat. Im sure the corporate offices would agree, but no one comes out here to correct it. I predict if they don't fix these issues soon, they will close for a lack of customers.

This particular KFC gets one star only because I can't give it zero stars. How is it that the last three times I've been here over the last couple months there is no extra crispy chicken? YOU'RE A CHICKEN RESTAURANT! Also my container of green beans was mostly water this last time. Every time I come to this particular location it's always something and it's always disappointing and on top of that the customer service is always awful. It seems the employees are miserable and go out of their way to be rude. I kept going to this kfc because I love kfc and it's on the way home but never again. "Keyla" working the drive through window needs to learn some respect and some manners as well as some customer service.

I have been to the Chipotle at 126th street 2 times now and each time they have been out of either Chicken or Steak.  I was not there at odd hours, and they did not seem in any type of rush to get the food prepared and out.  I decided to have steak instead since they were out of Chicken, the Steak was cold and chewy.  Chipotle has lost some of its appeal.

Thursday night at 7pm shouldn't be too bad right???? NOPE! 25 minutes so far in the drive thur! Two cars still ahead of me and it's not like I can just leave. Love Del Taco but you are turning into Taco Bell with this crap!

Used to drive out of my way to come to this location, (I live closer to the one down 4th st)
A few times we have ordered our regular steak bowl, they barely put any meat or rice. I felt like I wasn't given what I payed for, regardless of they are busy or not . I won't be coming back due to it being an on going issue. They also never have tea or lemons!

It's never packed here but the service is always slow. I've never seen a worker crack a smile and the vibe is just ... dry. 

I only come here because it's the only fried chicken place in goleta and it's decent. Biscuits are always dry and the chicken never seems to be warm enough, but It gets my fried chicken cravings satisfied. If there were any other KFCs or even a Popeyes, I wouldn't even bother with this place.

Chipotle is a reliable place for a good burrito. It pales in comparison to SoCal for burritos, but here in the east coast, I'll take what I can get.. Particularly for a good price and fresh ingredients. The sofritas is amazing and would be loved by carnivores if they gave it a chance. The guacamole could use a little more lime and I prefer it chunkier, but is above average for being mass produced.  

This location is generally pretty quick with their service. The parking lot is usually filled. 

Pro tip. Get a veggie burrito or bowl, and your guacamole is free99 son. Easily the best deal. Also, they have redeemed expired coupons for me in the past, so don't be afraid to give them a try- you never know. The Tabasco chipotle and Tabasco like sauces are a required addition to anything you order here.

Really surprised this place isn't busier.  I really love the Sofritas bowl.  It feels like home.
When you don't eat meat, having a place like this to go to is great.

Reasonably clean, and the people are super nice.  I wish they were more reliable in their portions.  Some servers give more, and others much less.

A clean store a good crew  and well prepared food  a little pricey compared to
Most fast food establishments but how can you resist the mashed potatoes and gravy  I usually choose the chicken bowl  all the essential vitamins and minerals a growing boy needs all in one bowl  lol

I don't even know where to begin. This is the closest KFC to my house and it is the biggest disappointment. Every time I go there, there is always something wrong. For instance one night they were out of chicken tenders and mashed potatoes. Almost every time I go there they forget something in my order or get it wrong. Like ordering a number six and a number eight and only getting one number 6. Or not getting any sauces that I asked for. Or 4 getting my cookie AND my sauce.... AND my utensils in the same order. And there's always a weird smell coming from the window when I pull up for the drive thru... And when I peek in it just looks like a mess. Of course the floor looks disgusting. Honestly I don't even know why I come back. If I could give them zero Stars I would but fortunately I can't so they get one star. And I guess that one star accounts for the fact that everyone is always friendly. At least they're nice.

They give you noooooooo meat. Ive tried them about 3 times and each time they give me like half a spoon of meat. Today I wanted half chorizo half steak and I swear I probably got 3 pieces of chorizo in my bowl. So furious. & it's not like you can say anything bc they always give a dirty look and a smart remark about not being allowed to serve more. I pay to eat a meal not to get served what you would feed a fish. Never coming to this location again. Recommend you making the extra trip to the one in Tyrone. They know how to actually portion.

It was busy when I went but the service was as fast as they could go and the food was delicious. The sofritas are great! It was my first time going to this location and I didn't have an issue with it.

The food here is yummy and it is clean but today I had the worst experience but it wasn't the first time I have.

Right away it took forever for someone to help me and I was the only person standing there. 
Second, right away I ordered my usual bruito bowl with extra rice. The girl puts less rice then you typically would receive if you didn't ask for extra. The second I realized I said, "can you please add more rice. Sorry" 
She looks at me annoyed and says sure only to turn to the girl next to her and complain about me right in front of me about wanting more rice. Then she proceeds to give me so little half and half chicken & steak that I couldn't even tell I was eating any meat!



After finally getting everything made I had to stand there forever while people just kept walking by just to pay. I honestly was about to tell them I didn't even want it anymore! I can honestly say this was definitely not with the 8 something I paid.

My boyfriend and I have waited for Chipotle in Reno to open for so long, and it's finally open!! We no longer have to come up with excuses to drive all the way to Roseville to get our Chipotle fix (yes it is that good!). The food is always fresh and delicious and the price is very reasonable.

Last night was the grand opening. It was packed with a long line and we had to wait 30 minutes to get our food, but it's worth it! The restaurant is a little small compared to others I have been in, so getting a seat might be tough if it's as packed as it was last night.

My boyfriend got a barbacoa burrito, I got a steak salad, and we shared chips and guac. He said his burrito was as wonderful as it could be and my salad was super delicious. If we were just passing through, we wouldn't have known that this place had just opened. As far as the quality of the food, there were no grand opening flaws. The chips were fresh and perfectly seasoned and for once the guac was not overloaded with onions.

Overall, a very good first experience at the new Chipotle!

Just had maybe the worst chipotle in the country. There was no flavor to the food at all like I am used to at chipotle and half way into my burrito bowl me and my girlfriend both found raw chicken and steak that was totally raw and pink in the middle. Never even got warmed up. Portions were kinda small too but I eat a lot so no matter what I want more. At least the staff was nice but chips and guac isn't compensation for the $10 worth of food that I ended up throwing away.
Good food came here for whopper Wednesday when I used to work at the mall

I have visited this place a number of times, so I must be insane, as I keep doing the same thing expecting a different result. Stopped by on may way home to grab something for dinner. I am not one who will make changes to a menu item, and was looking forward to the promotional Nashville Hot Chicken. Was told there would be a little wait, which I expected. 25 minutes later, as my order is being prepared, I am told that there is no dark meat, which isn't a big deal, but then I hear how the drive thru continued to sell the same items I have been waiting for. The crew appeared to be doing what they could to keep up, but there did not appear to be any coordination between the dining room and drive thru when it came to filling orders. It was very difficult for my not to be "That guy" as I got my order. May have to drive to a different town next time I want KFC.

The past four times I've ordered online, each order was wrong, all four times. Once the order was so wrong they took half of the ingredients in my mom's salad and put it in my brothers steak bowl. They took half of the ingredients in my brothers steak bowl and put them in my mom's salad. They are complete opposites and ended up not eating either of their meals. How do you mix up an online order that bad? The ingredients are all listed for you. My order has also been completely wrong before. It's like I get someone else's bowl and thy take the lid with my name on it and stick it on someone else's bowl. I used to go here once a week. Haven't been there in a month now which really is unfortunate because I love their food. Staff is a mix of nice and rude, depending on who you get. Wish I had a better run location near me.

All I have to say is the Chipotle staff is ridiculous! They are beyond stingy with the ingredients!! Don't put too much we may have to restock the burrito bar and then we would actually have to work!!! Seriously they lined the bottom of my husbands taco perfectly a straight one level line of steak in a rather large crispy shell!! Don't go to over board with the meat! After all whats a taco with more than 6 tiny cubed pieces of steak! I have given up on this location. Time after time of staff being so lazy about the amount they put in a taco or in a bowl. I don't feel I should have to ask for more or pay for double meat when the double meat portion is what should actually be in my order!! Ever other location I have visited knows how to make a proper burrito or taco, this location is just simply lazy! save your money and go to Qdoba's they are not chincy with their meat!!

YUM. I am stuffed as I write this, and I'll probably still manage to somehow cram the last two bites of my carnitas tacos down my throat. 

I work at 96th and College, and I only take a half hour lunch break, so I don't have a whole lot of time. And to cut it even closer, today, I wanted to check out Huddles, so I had even less time. So I tried to think of a place that was super quick to grab some to-go, so I could try something new that wasn't just lunch for later. Ding!- Chipotle online ordering! 

One of the best inventions- online ordering. The phone app works great, too! You can log into the site, save certain meals, so it's so quick to order what you want online, and just as quick to pick it up. Chipotle has dedicated spots (although this time I went, either there were cars parked in those spots when they shouldn't have been or they removed the signs). But I stopped in pretty late, and there was still a pretty close spot open. 

As soon as you walk in, you get to bypass the line if you ordered online. Just walk right up to the register. In this case, again, it was after lunch, so there was no line. The guy at the food making line asked me my name, grabbed my little brown bag with a little nametag on it. 

The carnitas tacos were super fresh- I had them with hot chili tomatillo salsa, rice and blackbeans. I will definitely get this again. 

And I still had plenty of time to spare to get some frozen yogurt, and still made it back to work like 10 minutes early.

This KFC sucks. The young guy at the counter was pretty nice and allowed the option to sub any side instead of mashed potatoes in the family fill up, but still, it's dismal in that place. This is not a place in which you want to dine so get it to go.

All the sides were the standard and the chicken was on the bland and dry side because it had been sitting out a long time once we got there around 7pm on a week night. 

Whoever runs this KFC, do you just not care?

This is by far the worst KFC or Taco Bell I've ever been to.  Every time we go it's something else.  They were once out of Fried Chicken; yes - KFC was out of fried chicken.  Another time, the power went out for a few hours and they lost all their food and were closed for a week (all the surrounding fast food restaurants reopened the same day - guess that $10K backup power generator is sounding pretty good after losing 5 days of sales).  Their latest shenanigans is that they don't accept Citi Visa cards.  They said they "always show up as card not accepted" on their POS machine.  I asked if the management was aware of this.  "Yes."  Are they working on fixing it?  "No, it's just those card"; that's right kids, "just" the #1 credit card issuer and credit card processor in the US.  No big deal.  This place also used to have monitors facing the customers with wait times.  They've since turned them around and covered them up since they were so embarrassingly slow.  They'd literally all be 500+ seconds.  Then, they systematically keep receipts rather than give them to the customers so you can't fill out negative surveys.  I never ask for my receipt as I assume I'll get it with my order, but they systematically remove them from the tray -- I saw a whole stack of hundreds of receipts under the cash register.  I think the employees are told they have to turn them all in at the end of the night since the manager/owner knows if you give them to customers they will fill out bad feedback.   We usually come in the evening when the dining room is empty.  There usually isn't anyone at the cash register.  I've waited 5 minutes just to have someone come and take and order, and 10 minutes for the food on top of that - 15 minutes when they aren't even busy.  The employees here are always on their phones and cutting up while you are standing their waiting at the cash register just for someone to acknowledge your presence.  The employees are not friendly, and downright rude.  Do you have Taco Bell kids meals?  Blank stare (literally, nothing).  About the third time asking, they get all sassy and say no.  Way to be kid friendly in the most suburban area of Carmel.  Then on multiple occurrences when paying with a KFC gift card that the strip doesn't work they've told me the card isn't accepted.  The first time this happened after arguing with Sassy McFrassy that it's a gift card so there is no such thing as it not being accepted, and explaining to him the magnetic strip may not work, and he may have to manually type it in, he finally agrees to type it in and proceeds to type it in wrong literally 4 times in a row, each time getting sassier and saying my card is not being accepted.  Finally a manager comes over and types it in and it works, which you would think he would at least acknowledge he was wrong.  Nope.  Then, since they don't have a Taco Bell kids meal (Cheese Roll), we ordered a Quesadilla, which was spicy and my daughter didn't like it (who puts anything other than cheese in a Quesadilla?)  So, I go back to order some $0.79 nachos.  Sassy McFassy gets the card right this time, and rather than turning around and grabbing the nachos sitting under the heat lamp and squirting some cheese in a cup, proceeds to walk off,  Literally no one filled the order for 10 minutes for just a bag of chips and cheese sauce -- during this entire time there was no one at the register, and no one even looked up from messing around in the back to see someone was obviously waiting for an order.  This experience is just a *typical* experience for this place.  Avoid this place like the plague.

Ok, I was willing to give it another chance. I stopped by and I saw a better working team. Even the manager was focused and involved. The place was much cleaner and spiffy. I ordered the 8 piece bucket Original recipe. The worker pulled the pieces from what looked like freshly fried tray. I am thinking YAY! Now why is the mash the obligatory side to have? I asked to swap for spears and was told it was extra. I was like, let's not spoil this good moment and I said OK. No biggie. Got home and was ready for some good stuff, so I thought. So far, so good until I took a bite. Did someone at KFC change the recipe? I was looking for that Original recipe bite and goodness. What I got was so bland! It is like no one seasoned the chicken! Well I'll be a damn.... And the consistency was like boiled chicken, refried and breaded. Quality control is clearly lacking here. Kudos for the team, MEH for the chicken. Publix Fried Chicken still the winner!
Good service, good food. The $5 meals are a decent deal, but other than that I couldn't find cheap items to chase the meal. I got a chicken little for  $1.79. Rip off.

This was my go to KFC growing up in Goleta. I'm not around Calle Real Center much anymore, but it's nice to go grab some food to go every now and then. If you don't have enough money or if you are on a college budget, Albertson's Chicken Dinner Deal isn't too shabby either.

I know that it'd be better for my arteries if I didn't make a habit out of visiting this place.  However, I feel like once a year or once every six months is okay.  

I also realize that many folks love to hate on the chains, and the non-organic, non-local aspect of this "cuisine."  I'm cool with that, it takes all kinds and I do sympathize with may of those creeds.  

Sometimes, you just need some fried chicken, mashed potatoes, and a biscuit though.  This is as good a place to indulge as any.

Whopper with cheese, fries from mcd's 1000 yards away,  great meal

Expect to wait a little longer if you go through the drive thru.  Always preferred burgers from burger king

I have been coming here are years & after today's visit this place is slowly on the decline. I have never had a problem here but the floors were wet & dirty today, 3 seats/booths are started to fall apart, the walls are in bad shape, this place needs a do-over bad. I got the KFC $5 pack today, skins were good but the chicken sliders I got had (I think) stale bread because most of the bread was hard. I like this location because it is in front of the AMC I always go too but it needs help.

This is a KFC/Taco Bell combo place.  Both are housed in same building.  This place isn't unlike many fast food places along an interstate.  Restrooms stink, women's restroom's entrance door is too long and you have to body slam the door just to get in.  Seems many fast food places can't count past 5, left off one of our orders but we deal with that all the time with our party of 6.  During a holiday weekend, lunch hour, ran out of chicken on the bone which would be a 15 min wait...oh well...appears the facility is nearly new with modern tables and chairs, very large, open area, and fairly clean, no food on the floors and tables pretty clean.  Just hard to tolerate poor restroom facilities, especially in this location where you can count on foot traffic.

Surprised that they no longer have grilled chicken available.  Is that all stores or just small ones?  Just checked KFC.com and it's still on their main site so I guess it's just small stores.  Anyway I'm not a fan of fried chicken so I wanted grilled.  Came in here and they don't carry it.  Was hungry so I ordered a couple of sides instead.  Mac n Cheese, cole slaw and some biscuits.  Staff was very helpful and friendly.  5 stars for them.  Location is tiny, with just a few tables.  minus a star for no grilled chicken and I didn't see a bathroom or indication of one either.  Tables could have been bussed faster.  The store was pretty dead when I stopped in but the table I wanted to sit at wasn't clean.  I brushed the crumbs off and sat at it anyway.  Parking is okay - has a parking lot but this strip mall is super busy so it's easy to get into a fender bender pulling in or out.  Be wary for the excess of cars.

Mid century food in a place which is slightly below average in cleanliness and buffet was poorly stocked at 6 PM which is pretty much peak time. The seasonings are as the Colonel wanted but because they cook at less than ideal temps It's greasy. Popeyes and Church's chicken is better and so is Publix.

I hadn't had KFC in a long time and had the itch so I walked in here one day recently and got a bucket of chicken, and first thing I thought, was "When did the buckets of chicken get so small, especially for the price?" Oh, KFC still rocks so it may be worth it, yes?

Well I got home and start eating, and first thing I thought was "When did KFC start tasting so NASTY?"  Maybe it's just this location, but no matter - it tasted awful and won't be doing KFC anymore.

I'd rather go to Publix where you get a box of much better tasting chicken, where you get just as much chicken, if not more..  For roughly half the price.  $7.99 at Publix for an 8 piece mixed box.  2 breasts (and they're huge), 2 legs, 2 wings, 2 thighs.  Trust me - much better option if you want some quick fried chicken where you can just grab and go.

KFC....  YUCK.  Overpriced crap.

Shame they've seemingly gone so far downhill..  Used to eat KFC all the time years ago and loved it, but no more.

Line out the door and yet there was virtually no wait. For a new opening the staff were on their game. Efficient service, delicious carnitas burrito and holy hell a pretty damn fine margarita to boot! Love Chipotle is now in Reno!

Meat was so skimpy.  I have been to Chipotle many times in various places but last night I had a coupon for buy one get one item free.  The barbacoa on one taco was good the other barely covered the bottom.  I mentioned how little meat whole he put in she'll and thought he fixed both.  I had ordered one steak and several of the pieces were very chewy and tough.  I had steak ones in Seattle and they were much better now chewy pieces.  I noticed one other guy in front was getting very little meat on his he asked for more for his burrito.   Why so skimpy with the meat and then put in lots of the other fillers like rice and beans. Not sure I will be going back to this one again.  It's happened once before there, if it's the new skimpy way for the price it's not worth it.

KFC quality has gone downhill very small pieces batter is barely on chicken. Not like when the Colonel made the chicken. I think it's the worst fried chicken out there no quality control anymore. Mash potoes taste like wall paste. Got the Box today was horrible .don't waste your money here. If you get a chance go to MARYLAND  FRIED CHICKEN IN PLANT CITY.It taste like the Colonel cooked it  big nice pieces and each piece  is cooked perfect best quality control and good prices check out there website they sell real chicken and it delicious

On their window they advertise chicken little meal combo for $5... 2 chicken littles, mash potatoes , cookie and a drink... we went in to order and we're told "we don't have that" and it's advertised right on their window... too bad customers are victimized by false advertising!  Oh ! They also don't have lettuce...obviously that was our one and only time! How do they stay in business?

Went there for lunch I ordered a #1 with no ketchup I hate ketchup got back to work with a whopper with cheese and only ketchup and you dumb fucks want 15.00 you can't even follow orders it's really not that hard

I don't understand all of the Chipotle hype! We were all in!! But.... Went out last night, 3 of us... All of us said; eh, not that impressed. We ate because the food wasn't disgusting however, it wasn't good. I personally eat a lot of bland food, but mine was REALLY BLAND! Ordered a beef bowl, white rice, (changed my mind to brown but was SO RUSHED, I didn't change it.) salsa, corn, black beans. Husband had beef burrito, daughter, just been and cheese burrito. Steak was very gristly. Yuk! Had to spit out 3 pieces. Hard to eat after that. Bluck! ~ I know it's really busy at times, though to be rushed through like a heard of cattle with the threat of a whip behind you is not my idea of pleasant either. We're pretty bummed. It was nice to have a place that was somewhat healthier than other alternatives. Qdoba for example. They are not healthy at all, my understanding of Chipotle was the same idea however at least healthi...ER.

If I could give this location a zero I would!! Worst tasting KFC chicken I've ever choked on!! They fried the chicken in the same grease they cooked the Dorito taco shells in...what a bunch of lazy @sses.....

someone needs to report this franchise to the Corporate Office and have the franchise REVOKED!!! Obviously the owner could careless about quality..I don't even want to think about cleanliness..I already feel like throwing up

Love the Chipotle chain but this one has variable quality on the food.

I mostly get the bowls and chicken always comes out great.  Steak is spotty, tonight's was very chewy, ended up giving three pieces to my dog.

Place is always busy, definitely recommend online order and pick up.

I ordered two whopper meals.  One with cheese, one without.  I pulled up to the window and received two drinks and one whopper meal.  With no lettuce on it.  The girl opened the window and said "they didn't give you all your food?"  And I said "no I'm still waiting on the other whopper meal, and also my sandwich didn't have lettuce on it."  They came back with two whoppers and no fries.  Then I asked for the other fry and got about 5 French fries total.  The place was not busy at all.  Not really sure why my order was so hard for them.  Very sad.

Great chain and food is fresh and wonderful.   Steak cooled to perfection where at other chains the steak tends to be a little tough.   Met a girl who told the history and story of the first Chipotle she use to go to in CO.  Very cool.

Not up to chipotle standards. As a whole, I love chipotle but this location is subpar. They are always out of something. Yesterday it was medium salsa, chips and hard tacos. Two weeks ago no brown rice, veggies or ice. The time before that no lemon or veggie. Before that, no steak or chicken. 

In the last 6 months I haven't been here when they haven't been out of at least two things. For such a limited menu, it is very impactful when they are out of items. And worst of all, there are no signs ahead of time so you wait through the line to the door to find out you can only get half of what you want. I've reached out to corporate as this is the only location I have these issues with, but nothing's changed. Go to the West Chester location or Taco Maya. Won't go back.

We love KFC and were happy we had one so close when we moved to Dunedin.  Unfortunately this store has been a constant disappointment.  Not once have I been here without a problem.  They routinely DO NOT have chicken ... or all they have is grilled which is too dry to even eat.  They have not had green beans on occasion.  Today they had no crispy wings and gave us original wings the size of my pinky finger.  The crispy chicken is clearly not made correctly.  The breading is heavy and hard as a rock.   We keep giving them another chance hoping they will get their act together but I think today was our final visit.  This meal was not worth the money paid.

People always talk about how great Chipotle is. So, finally I tried it. I was thoroughly underwhelmed. First, the way they made people order was messed up and chaotic. Everything moved so fast, I had no clue what I was ordering. There were other people ordering at the same time, so it made them hearing me difficult. My meal was far from great. The chips were stale and greasy. The steak or whatever meat I ended up getting was tough and didn't have much flavor. The burrito was not memorable. What was memorable, however, was the awful music playing in the background.  If you're in the mood for good Mexican food, don't go to Chipotle. My suggestion is go to Senor Taco or Red Mesa. They are much better.

I was having a late lunch and stopped in at this Hardee's for a snack/meal to tide me over until dinner. It was not busy at all but it was also mid afternoon so I didn't expect it to be that busy. I ended up getting the Star meal box. If you're not familiar with the Star box meals they are a burger or sandwich, fries, a cookie and a drink for about $5. The promo that is going on right now is 2 sliders, fries, drink and cookie for $6. I opted to up size the drink and fries to large. My food was cooked to order and was good but I was disappointed that they didn't have any brewed iced tea with the drink machines. I had to settle for a raspberry tea which was too sweet for my liking. Overall the food good and a  good value for the price. The staff was friendly but nothing over the top.

This Del Taco is truly hit or miss. Unfortunately, for at least the last year or two... mostly miss. I go for long periods of time in between visits and think that surely they will now have their act together...but no, not really.

Tonight we were getting a few things in the drive through. From the time that we got up to the window it took us over nine minutes (we were third in line after we finished ordering and I am not counting this time waiting behind others), so probably a total of close to twenty minutes at least.

She took my money and gave us our drinks and straws. When the clock said that we had been there over seven minutes another girl comes to the window, looks back at the people backed up behind my vehicle, and asks me if I paid yet. I told her I did. She then asks what I need then. Well.... our food. She was like, "Oh!", studies the order screen, and then asks, "Are you the order with...?" I told her yes and she left. In just about two more minutes she came with a bag and said that we might have two of everything, she isn't really sure. I was like, "Ok...?"  

It turns out that two of our items were totally incorrect and we also got one additional item (unfortunately it was something neither of us like), so no... not two of everything and lots of problems.  We also did not get any of the sauce and not one napkin.

So much for something quick and easy!

Most of the other times lately, there is a big problem with getting the orders correct. Also, the ice tea (which is wonderful usually... a tasty brand) through the drive through tastes a bit fermented. Basically, it has an after taste that tastes old. I drink my tea plain (no sweetener or lemon), so it is really noticeable. I'm thinking that they are making it fresh but not cleaning the pot very well if at all. I had this happen at another restaurant, too. Only in the drive throughs. My guess is that they either get some out of the pot that is inside (and therefore notice it and clean that one often enough) and/or have people complaining while in the restaurant if they don't clean that one often enough. The drive through people are usually gone before they notice something is wrong.

Their service here is usually friendly. I just wish they would figure out how to get orders correct... and clean their ice tea container.
Didn't experience any issues while getting drive-thru. Thank you! Delish chicken, of course. Omnom. No silverware tho :(

1st time in this BK & I must say it is very clean and it has a Courteous staff...The fries were hot & fresh! The Whopper was packed nicely and tasted "Superbly"!! There was NOT too much of a wait. IF there was a wait THEN IT MEANS the Burger is being prepped!! It MEANS your Burger isn't sitting under a HOT LAMP!! This Burger King Offers a Military Discount of 10%!! Patronize this Patriotic Establismen
My friend and I had a chicken and steak bowl on Thursday late afternoon and got food poisoning. I will not be eating at this location anymore.

We usually go to the KFC in Spanish Springs, but this time stopped at the one across from the truck stop.  No wonder nobody was in there.  The bisquits were like rocks, the chicken was dried out and tasteless.  When I questioned the young man as if my wings were in the bag also he didnt know what I was talking about.  Upon being told to add the forgotten order, he had no apology, but instead acted like it was no big deal.  Never again.

By far the worst DD I have ever been to. The staff are so miserable you feel the need to apologize for making them work.  I might be able to overlook that if they actually produced decent food. 
I ordered the "#6" with a turkey sausage egg white flat bread. They were out if turkey so I said veggie was OK. They didn't ask me how I wanted my coffee - so I told them and the guy looked at me like I had two heads. "oh you want coffee?". Well yes, it is a COFFEE combo, according to the menu. Then he lectures me on having to order it separate. Really? Then why have it listed as a coffee combo?  When I finally got the flat bread it was covered in a gloppy cheese-whiz substance. It was disgusting. 

On a good note the coffee was fine, but so is WaWa's, which is right next door.

Usually, every Sunday I allow myself a "Cheat Day" to indulge in some sort of fast food. I don't often pick KFC, but right now they have their Nashville Hot Chicken, and OMG, I really enjoy it! It has this sauce that is spicy, sweet, and smokey and it's a nice change of pace from that standard Buffalo option.
I've been going to this location since I moved to my current home 2 years ago. I'd say I've probably gone about 10 times total and I can't happily report that I've never had a bad experience! 
The food at this location is always well done, fresh, not overly salty, and the biscuits are fluffy - not hockey pucks. I used to go to the one at Meadowood Mall and it was consistently horrible! Overcooked,  over seasoned, over salted, missing items, bad service. It got to the point that I actually didn't go to ANY KFC because that particular location had just turned me off to KFC all together. I will do a separate review for that one.
However, this Sparks one is awesome! One time I came through the drive thru at 9:45, I didn't realize they close at 10pm, which kinda sucks. Anyway, I ordered the $20 Family Meal in Original Recipe which comes with 8 pieces of chicken, and when I got up to the window the very nice manager said "So we had to make a whole batch of fresh Extra Tasty Crispy for the last 2 customers and there's a lot left.  Would you want 20 pieces of that instead?  Otherwise we are just gonna throw it away." HECK YA I'LL TAKE IT! Lol. He also have me a bunch of extra biscuits, gravy, corn, and apple pies. I could tell that everything was FRESH and thought that was really nice of him. Mom's co-workers were treated to a KFC feast the next day because she brought most of what we got to work!
I've never gotten bad food here. I've never had missing items. I've never been treated rudely by staff. I've never been disappointed. For most national fast food chains we've learned to expect to mistakes and just deal with them. It shouldn't be that way. One time I was here, inside,  and the lady at the counter was showing the manager how a piece of her chicken was burnt. He apologized, got her a whole new meal, gave her some pies, and a gift card for $10 right on the spot!  He then inspected all the chicken to make sure it was ok and nicely told his employees to keep an eye on what they were doing, how they were doing it, and if anyone needed help or was overwhelmed to ask for help because they are a team and making the customers happy was priority #1. I don't know what his name is, but I think this manager is the reason that this KFC is head and shoulders above the rest! He seems like a very supportive, helpful,  positive, cool manager and having worked in the food industry myself,  I know that having a good manager such as him can really make a positive difference and make a place great overall.
Kudos to him and the entire team at this KFC!  I feel this location is a great example of good a fast food restaurant should be! Stop in and try the Nashville Hot if they have it!! And they still DEEP FRY their Apples Pies!!

I just love this place and have reviewed other chipotles but I want to say this one is my favorite one the employees are always friendly and they always serve up generous portions for you. Grab a steak bowl some chips and a beer and your all set

Came here for dinner tonight at 5:40 pm.  I saw the new commercial and wanted some tasty fried chicken.  I went through drivethru only to leant that they were OUT of Chicken!!!!????  They said 10 mins to wait.  With no other options, I waited and went inside.  I heard other angry customers state they had no ground beef for the Taco Bell menu!  Zero apologies from staff and no sense of urgency.  They never offered any of us anything.  Not even free drink!

After 12 min wait, I left with the Family Fill Up at 5:53 PM.  Got all the way home only to find they did not pack any gravy for the mashed taters.  The chicken was at least hot, but tasted very rubbery and was almost not edible.

I can't believe these people want $15 an hour.  Prove to me you value a customer.  Prove to me you know how to work hard.

I will never ever come back to this location.  I just wish I could get my 20 dollars back!

Went to this KFC even after seeing the reviews because we love the food. Now I see why the reviews are so low. Ordered an 8p bucket meal and an extra side of mac and cheese for my son. The person taking my order seemed like she had no idea what was going on. When I got home, my 8p was a 7, 3 of the pieces looked like they were crewed on, meat was ripped off them, and worst was the mac and cheese was not in the bag. That was the only thing my son would eat. So now I have chicken no one wants and a 3 yr old who didn't want anything but the one thing missing. I called the store and was told a manager would call me back. NO one called me back. Will not be going back to this store ever again, always read and believe the reviews is the lesson I took from this.

Want to know how to piss off everyone in line at Chipotle? 
Fax in your order.

Then you get to smugly sashay past the ordering queue, which stretches across the length of the place and nearly to the door, and go straight to the cashier, grab your bag, and sashay back out. Princess wave, if you dare.

They. are. pissed.

Honestly, though, I wouldn't have subjected the queue to that if only Chipotle delivered large orders.  
I'm not convinced yet that the burritos here are great, but I'm willing to give it another go. I had the carnitas burrito. 
Points off, also, for Chipotle's indecipherable labelling method, and for trying to overcharge the guac on the veggie orders.
Ate here not to long ago an I must say I was not impressed.  I got a steak burrito an the steak had such an odd taste to it.  I will not be returning it's very pricey for just a simple burrito.

This was my 2nd time to Chipotle, and my 1st time at this location. My husband has been to the Chipotle in Marlton many times for lunch, but this was his 1st time at this location. We both got burrito bowls. I got sofritas, and my husband got his usual - carnitas. Both were delicious - and large. We were there around 2:30 on a Sunday, and it was pretty quiet. The employees were all very courteous, and the restaurant looked clean. Although the table layout was odd - mostly long tables. So, if you don't like sitting near strangers, I wouldn't recommend coming here at busy times. By the way, if you like unsweetened iced tea, you'll love Chipotle's. It was delicious!

I've eaten here 4 times per week, on average, for the last year or two.  I could probably leave it at that, but I'll go on, only because I feel so strongly about this establishment (in general) and this location, more particularly.

This is the best run Chipotle I've been to.  The staff is fun, friendly, and cool.  The food quality is always consistent, as is the service.  I'm a paleo eater (not a crazed health nut -- I just dig real food) and this is hands down the most "primal" chain out there.

I'll distill down what I've learned over my ~1000 trips here into some pro-tips that I hope will add value to fellow yelper's experience.

1.  Don't fear the salad
--It's essentially a bowl with some lettuce on the bottom.  I throw a little white rice on.

2.  Skip the dressing
--It's unnecessary (and it's made from crappy oils) -- just get all three salsas and you are golden.

3.  Barbacoa is really dependable
--It's also the only meat on the menu that is not cooked with soybean oil (soybean oil = not desirable).  I get the steak when it looks fresh off the grill.

4.  Guac rules

5.  Get shredded lettuce on top of the guac -- keeps it from sticking to the lid.

6.  Skip the soda, pimp the lemons
--I ask for a cup for water -- squeeze three lemons in and fill with water.  Lemon aid, for free.

Roughly one pound of real food for under 10 bucks.  Amazing.  I want to write a book -- "The Chipotle Diet".  Wait, I think I just wrote it.

i work right next door @ visionworks and my associates and i walk to chipotle practically 3x/week during our lunch break. the place is very clean and food is well prepared even on busy lunch rush. My go-to meal is normally 3 soft tacos with barbacoa and carnitas w/ rice and beans, guac and cheese w/ a little bit of sour cream. NOM NOM NOM!

Our visit started fine, my wife ordered the chicken bowl, my son, the steak, I ordered barbacoa. My wife went through and got her toppings, they ran out of steak, they finished mine.  They put an aluminum cover over my sons incomplete bowl of rice and left my wife's and mine at the register uncovered.  In the mean time, the manager shows up and announces there would be a wait for steak orders and will continue to take all other orders.  This is at the six minute mark from order time...our two bowls still sitting uncovered at the register, I see the cook taking the steak off the grill, then he disappears...another four minutes.  I ask for a refund for the steak bowl, but still want it.  The manager has to come over and approve, he does then disappears in the back and starts cooking.  We're now at the 14 minute mark.  I get a two minute eta from the cashier.  Fourth customer pays and leaves, our two bowls are still sitting on the counter uncovered and paid for.  The cook starts cutting the sitting steak and hands it to the prep line.  They grab my sons cold rice bowl and put the steak in it, bring it to the register to match the other two cold bowls.  Thanks Chipotle...go from poisoning all of us to who gives a shit and watching a shit show behind the serving line!  Came by request of one of my teenagers...won't happen again.

Nasty place! I love KFC but this place turned my stomach. Got there at around 6. There was a bad accident across the way. Walked in and saw about 7 or 8 people in there, all cleaning. I stood in line for about 6 minutes, 4 of the front employees looked at me but seemed preoccupied in other things. I then saw a tray of what looked like raw chicken on top of a horizontal surface while the other employees sloughed around a mop. No one even asked me if I was helped, but it was too late anyway. I left and got Publix Fried Chicken!

Land O Lakes KFC, you have a huge problem and based on the reviews here I am not the only one. What I saw was disgusting! Customer service is horrible, not a single friendly face. And is it customary to have everyone cleaning during peak hours? I was willing to pay a premium for white meat...you lost out to a supermarket! 

Nasty!

We stopped in to try the Angriest Whopper. Staff at the counter was friendly, if not a little confused, but the kid seemed new, so I'll chalk it up to that. He was friendly and calm when he didn't know how to input an Angriest Whopper combo meal, and instead of panicking and screwing up the order, he went and asked his supervisor for guidance. Well done, kid! 
The food took a little while, but I'll blame that on the "we're the only people in the world" group in front of us that insisted on special ordering everything, and then pestering the staff about it while they tried to get the order ready. 
Once the food came out, the Angriest Whopper seemed more like the Mildly Annoyed Whopper. The burgers were on regular buns, not the red buns with hot sauce supposedly baked in. This was kind of a disappointment, as I really wanted to see/taste that. Oh well. The burgers were good, but not nearly as 'angry' as I would have liked. Overall, a decent place to stop for a bite. Standard BK. Nothing extra special, nothing terrible. I do give kudos to the young man that took the orders and asked for help when he wasn't sure. 9 out of 10 other fast food workers would have just guessed, and ended up screwing the order up 8 ways from Sunday.

The food is great, I recommend the sofritas. They have good vegetarian options. The staff was okay, I felt like they wanted to gossip more than help the customers. Also, shouldn't be discussing the matters of the staff out in the open.

.I had the erroneous idea that this was more like a Chili's. I'm thinking Chipotle~Chili. Not even close. This place doesn't have fancy plates, table cloths, or a bar serving alcohol. You get a totally casual seat-yourself environment, with stainless steel table tops, and stiff wooden seats.
.The custom burrito building idea is nothing new. We have seen this at Qdoba, and Taco Del Mar. Who does it better? If you want your burrito to have a tomato or spinach flavored tortilla, and refried beans, it's not here. However, if you want it to have barbacoa or sofritas (a vegan blend) or corn, this is the place.
.Oddly enough, I was not offered chipotle chili (look it up). When I asked the charming young lady about it, she showed me a bottle of Tabasco branded chipotle sauce, available near the soda fountain.
.Mexican fast food - Okay, I agree with that part. Just don't expect Mexican decor, Mexican music, or Mexican waitresses.

Deep deep down, my inner snobby liberal arts school demons constantly beg to call out Chipotle for the mass-produced travesty that it is -- an Americanized caricature that definitively proves corporate America's tendency to selectively co-opt palatable ethnic flavors. BUT ALAS, "hungry me" always trumps "crazy rabid liberal thinkpiece me," and DAMN, do I love a burrito bowl.

I am almost positive that every time I order carnitas from Chipotle, an abuela starts to roll in her grave somewhere in Michoacán and begins to seriously consider haunting me. So each time, I regretfully send out a prayer and a heartfelt sorry while I leave my better-minded hang-ups and my shame at the door and bite into a burrito smothered in "medium" salsa (alternative title(s): "salsa de Kansas" or "salsa de gringo"). Though this location in particular could afford to be a little more heavy-handed with the fillings (I need dat rice!), it'll still fill you up in typical Chipotle fashion. Having practically lived off of burrito bowls as midterm/finals sustenance for several years, I am no stranger to "Chipot-bloat": it's that first-trimester food baby look that your stomach takes on when you walk out of a Chipotle after downing a bowl... with chips and guac... and a corona... and chips to-go.

Mild [pico de gallo-level] gripes: I came in for a late dinner bite maybe around 8:30 and chose to dine in. Maybe an hour before closing, they started to clean the grill. No big, right? Typical routine. Then begins the most ungodly screeching noise -- I'm talking Satan's rusty metal harpsichord -- as someone starts to scrape the grill clear. I've been to Chipotles late before (minutes before closing, being "that guy"), and never have I ever heard something like that. Even as her co-workers and customers alike complained, the jarring screeches went on, so I smacked the tin lid onto my bowl, grabbed a refill and peaced out as quickly as possible. Also, the last batch of chips I got from here were salty enough to clear my driveway. Herp.

BOTTOM LINE: Do I consider Chipotle Mexican food? I'd risk deep and existential anxiety if I tried to give an answer that even remotely resembled a yes. Is it good food? Admittedly, sortakinda. That chicken/steak bowl has never failed me. And really, any place that's got me willing to play e.coli-style russian roulette has got to be at least halfway okay, right?

I have to be honest: I hadn't eaten at White Castle in nearly 20 years, after becoming violently ill from a slider as a child. My husband talked me into it the other night . They have online ordering, which is kind of cool. You can pay either online or in the store, and you select your pick-up time. I ended up with a chicken sandwich with cheese -- not bad, although a bit pricey at $1.60 -- and some mozzarella sticks. Mmm. Mozzarella sticks.

Go to KFC for dinner and ordered an original chicken. They only have grilled or crispy!! What's up with that?!! Ordered a chicken sandwich and get the snack chicken sandwich. Definitely won't ever go there again. Can't even give a star. Nancy 6-60-2016

The prices are pretty high compared to other Burger King restaurants and the Whopper Jr I had was the tiniest one I've ever eaten but I feel it's one of the tastiest I've eaten, too. The little burger patty was so juicy. Good job here. The place has gotten a facelift in recent years so it looks pretty nice inside. The seating looks limited but remember there's a second floor, so that's a plus. The area is a little iffy at night, but for daytime excursions it's fine.

I am updating my previous review of 3 stars to 4.
I ate here today for lunch, accompanied by two expert patrons of Chipotle. I asked them what they liked best and the response was overwhelmingly for the bowls, with chips. So I got the carnitas bowl with beans, rice, cheese and a little sour cream.

First, the chips are lightly flavored with lime, so they are good to scoop up your fillings, but they are delicious on their own. 

Second, I love their queuing method. I once read somewhere that a larger contributor of buyer stress comes from the queue. You know, the one where there are muttiple queues and you don't know which one to get in and the one you do get in is always the one that has the slowest people and you get all riled up that people who got in other lines waaay after you have breezed through before you? That kind of stress. It doesn't exist at Chipotle because there is just one common queue, so everyone's equal. Well, almost. If you want to know how to jump the common queue, look at my first review. 

And if you have any sense of moral obligation to your environment, Chipotle actually got the highest rank of social and environmental responsibility in the fast food category, according to the Better World Shopping website!

http://betterworldshopper.com/r-fastfood.html

For reals!

I ate inside today and watched the people there. It was truly your every person place. There were hipsters, young professionals, a group of cops, a group of homies bopping to the music, disenchanted youth, trust fund kids, and more. They could have been there because of Chipotle's "Food with Integrity" ideals, or because they just like the food. Kinda cool when you can have both.

One of my expert patron companions said that this chipotle in particular is generous with the veggies and guac. I've never been to any other, so I cannot say this is fact, but it is fact that he did eat a chicken bowl than seemed more guacamole than anything else.

Everyone knows Chipotle and people generally love it or hate it. It's not 'authentic' in the way people seek and I know this, but overall I enjoy it. It's a modern take on pseudo-Mexican food with a concentration on quality ingredients. No hormone sour team, locally sourced produce, ethically raised cattle, hand made chips, etc. 'The usual' as far as ethical food trends go. Again, not authentic, but definitely better than any fast food (and in some cases better than the SB-catering so called 'Mexican' around here. What I find notable about this location is a problem with consistancy. Often, the rice will be grossly overlooked. Sometimes the chips are so overcooked they look dark and dry. Things that are visually hard to miss. Coming here, you never know and it appears to be due to a very high turn over rate. After years of eating at the Ventura location (to the point of being recognized by staff) and getting great service, this place obviously has a problem with consistency and it still seems to go unaddressed. Overall, I still enjoy it, sometimes I just get a craving for a steak burrito, their blend of (so called) 'medium' salsa and their lightly cooked hand made chips.

I don't like giving places bad reviews especially if your a fan but coming to this location I was not pleased what so ever! Driving by my husband wanted to stop by for the first time. Husband order a pulled pork burrito which seriously was the size of sausage! Like it was pretty small and not filling at all and I ordered the steak taco. The girl who made my tortilla and added my meat was pretty nice until she passed it on to the guy...... So he was pretty rude not sure if he likes his job or not but he totally just slabbed my salsa and sour cream to splatter everywhere!!! Sorry but can't go back to that location.

Been hear twice and had a good experience.  Menu is a bit limited with basic Mexican style choices.  I've had their chicken and carnitas burrito bowl dishes which tasted fresh and generously filled with all the toppings.  One bowl easily makes for two meals.  Service is very fast and friendly.  Area seating is clean and kept.  Prices are good too.

Pork Carnitas were still unavailable but I went with the steak burrito and was still a solid burrito, I consider this place a chain but it doesn't taste like chain food. It always reminds me that it's quality tex mex when I randomly stop there to get a quick unplanned meal.

The staff has no personality at all and don't seem happy to help. The portion sizes are inadequate (I would know, I worked for chipotle), the chips aren't salty and citrusy, they're also stale, the steak is over cooked and dry, and my order was messed up twice in the same visit and when I spoke up about it the response I received was quite rude and condescending. It's worth it to drive a few miles to the Cool Springs Blvd location. They've gotten much better recently. The second bag of chips I received because my first was stale were not filled as much as they should have been (one burrito bowls worth) and are under cooked.

We almost never stop at "Taco joints". The other day we were out near the lunch hour and decided on Del Taco as it was close. I had a balance left on a $50.00 gift card that I got when buying my new Sonada Hybrid ( spend $30 grand and get a 50 buck gift card) what a deal! I figured the balance left would cover the lunch and it did. I decided on the promotional burrito which was the "Epic Chicken and Avocado Burrito" and it was! It came with a medium drink for six and 1/2 bucks or so. It filled this Bubba up! Truly epic! As this is no easy accomplishment. Tables were clean, food came fast, great taste with your choice of four levels of different "heat" little ( but numerous) packets that I have to gnaw open like a big overstuffed Rat! I had to do the three bears on them.....one too hot , one too cold, ah- one just right for this wimpy  Gringo!

The new owners are of this place have made it a little cleaner but overall you are still getting the same quality KFC service from Norristown. The staff look like high school drop outs, bizarre and inappropriate convos going on while customers are trying to order, drive thru always slow, out of chicken ALOT, forget trying to get pot pies, whole place is a joke

First of all let me say I LOVE BURGER KING but at this one place the food is always cold. I order a double cheese whopper with extra onions and large diet coke and large fries. My burger when I got home was cold it only had 1/2 small onion ring size of half dollar. The fries were also cold and rubbery. The only reason they get 1 star is the diet coke was very good. I have been to this site a lot of times but the last 3 times the food was awful. The drive through staff are always nice but if you go inside forget it. Nasty nasty people. I won't be going back. 3 bad meals and I don't go back. Sorry

I love Chipotle. Fresh ingredients, tasty recipes. I usually get the burrito bowl with steak. It's really good and you won't be hungry for a long time. If you aren't concerned with calories, get a burrito b/c the tortillas are perfect. 

I can't give this place 5 stars b/c it is a chain. But, as far as chains go, this one is awesome.

This Del Taco location is the best in the Treasure Valley!  I've never had reservations about ordering at anytime whether it be 2AM, 4AM, 8AM, 1PM or 6PM.  Staff is always friendly and food always tastes great.  I wish I worked near this Del Taco instead of the abomination of a Del Taco on Broadway in Boise.  Dear Del Taco on Broadway in Boise, maybe you should take a lesson from this Del Taco and figure out a better business model.

No complaints...good to have a Chipotle in Meridian.

Food is typical Chipotle (Mission-style burritos). I note they've added "sofritas" for vegetarians. My family had a steak and a carnitas burrito, plus chips and guac, and they were as good as I've had at any other Chipotle.

Staff here was notably more courteous than at other places...really went out of their way to be friendly. 

I'm not sure I'd go too far out of my way for Chipotle over Qdoba, but I think it's slightly better...I'd certainly drive a mile down Eagle Road for it.

I'll be back!

This is a really tough place to review. I've wanted to try White Castle forever so on a business trip to Indy I finally got my chance since they are everywhere. The menu is very broad. There are way more choices than I expected including a pretty awesome looking breakfast menu. The sliders were thinner than I expected but very good. The chicken & waffle slider was good but could have been elite if they cooked the waffle a little better. The restaurant cleanliness itself is a disaster. The floors were dirty as hell; there were straw wrappers everywhere, spilled soda on tables, and this was before the busy lunch rush even started. Whomever the manager is should fire themselves for allowing such chaos. The soda machine was also pouring out flat sodas. I'd go to White Castle again but I would never go to this White Castle again.

I have been a avid Chipotle lover for many years now! Ever since I moved to Franklin, Tennessee from the San Francisco Bay Area, California I have missed getting my steak burrito at least once a week... I was not aware for the longest time that a Nashville location had opened just a year or so ago. Even after I found out, that is just to far for me to drive to get my craving fix! I needed one near my home! 

To my surprise and excitement I saw that a Chipotle location was opening in Brentwood (NOW OPEN!). 

This location is wonderful! Near all the wonderful Brentwood shopping! The location is right next the the REI, and Starbucks! You must get here ASAP! If you have never had Chipotle, you need to get with it! Everything they offer is great! I love the steak burrito, with black beans, rice, lettuce, cheese, guacamole, mild brown sauce, and the corn salsa, and the tomato salsa!!! This is one huge burrito! Great prices as well! 

Overall, I LOVE Chipotle!

This Taco Bell/KFC should be awesome. The concept is excellent. I love both of these restaurants, and they go together weirdly well. It's the execution at this particular location where the failures are downright embarrassing. 

We go here fairly often, and they have never once gotten our order right. Today, we ordered tacos with no lettuce. I said it twice, and it was clear as day on the receipt: no lettuce on any of the tacos. We got our food, and all of the tacos had lettuce on them. I went back to get the order corrected and was given incredulous looks while the kids working there argued amongst themselves over whose fault it was and who would have to fix it. I saw no evidence that any of them were in charge or that the store even had a manager. 

One mistake can certainly be forgiven, but this is just the most recent in a long line of orders that have been consistently messed up. The only reason this store gets a second star from me is because the food (whichever parts are correct) is generally very tasty (which I can only assume is because the pre-made stuff is basically idiot-proofed at the corporate level before these kids get their hands on it), and about half of the time the mistakes are in out favor (e.g. free chicken, extra nachos, bonus mashed potatoes, etc.), which is nice but not really comforting.

When i go here i expect my order to be wrong. I try not to eat fast food that often and i haven't been to Burger King in over 6 months and now i remember why. I only had a few singles on me so i choose from the value section. I got a BK Stacker, Nuggets, and Sweet Potato fries. The sweet Potato fries were awesome, its about time somebody has them. The nuggets i know there only a dollar but you only get 4 everyone else gives 5, they are nothing special. I usually don't eat nuggets because of how much they process them but i forgot and after eating them i remember why i stopped getting them. The Stacker was a joke, it looked like i got a kiddie burger on a whopper bun. It felt like i was eating a bread sandwich with meat flavoring. So besides Waiting 20 min to order and then another 15 to get my food and it was only 9 pm with hardly anyone in line. Wont be going back to this one or probably any other any time soon, if i do it would be only to get sweet potato fries.

GREAT FOOD, its a line just like Moes only this place does it right. for a few more bucks you get REAL food, I love the spicy steak that is just a tad pink in the middle, this place does it right. Yes you will have to shell out maybe a few extra bucks but it is WORTH It. Skip on other things-not your health. They do get busy and sometimes the kids that work here can be rude but aside from that I LOVE IT!

I've tried the Nashville chicken from other Kfc locations and it is really good..M
 I have tried it from this location Twice now the 1st time they didn't put pickles on it and there was very little sauce.. totally unlike my past experiences at others Kcf,s.. tonight we tried it again,  same thing no pickles a very little sauce... not only that they never even called our name to pick up.. the stuff was sitting there and pretty cold.. I do not plan on returning to this Kfc, unfortunately it's the only one close to my house.

This is my favorite KFC and Taco Bell. Well, that's excluding the Taco Bell in Spring Hill where the famous Summer with a smile works!! Everything great and piping hit! Try the Tuesday 2 Pc Chicken with a side and biscuit is only $2.49 and white meat is not extra!!  Would be 5 stars but need to keep the dining room a little cleaner.

I have been hearing people rave about how good Chipotle is, since before Reno even planned on bringing the restaurant to the area. Maybe it was the anticipation of trying this seemingly amazing food, but I don't understand what the hype is all about. I had a burrito with cilantro lime rice, black beans, carnitas, cheese and sour cream. All I can say is it was a decent burrito. It wasn't spectacular, but it wasn't bad. Even though it's not my favorite place, many others have a different outlook on it; I definitely recommend ordering online.
Worse KFC I've ever been to. I went there at night and they gave me chicken that was noticeably old. The chicken was tough and the skin was extra brittle
It's really just another chain burrito place.  The food is good.  The steak is made medium rare.  The store is very small.  I'd go to Qdoba instead just for the Queso.

The food is on par with any other KFC/Taco Bell that you go into. However, the wait time is here insanely long. Even when no one else is in the dining room. 

Last time I went and ordered a $5 box, it was a twenty minute wait. Which would be fine if someone had said, "Hey, we're cooking the chicken fresh" or something like that.
