But from the dining room it's very easy to see inside the kitchen. So it's pretty obvious that they're just taking their sweet time. 

If you have some extra time and don't mind waiting, go ahead. But I definitely wouldn't go to this location if you're in a hurry.

(And if you're feeling adventurous, try the Taco Bell sauce on the chicken tenders or wedges. It's A+.)

I placed an order online and payed with my debit card and when I got there they told me I still hadn't paid yet. Now, I've done this numerous times before and never had a problem but this time it was terrible. I waited for about 30 minutes in the store for my 2 steak tacos and chicken burrito and to make things more execrable the gentleman at The register comes up to me and says, "Hey, can I see your receipt I don't have record of what you ordered."He then tells me to get in line and tell them what I ordered! Now this defeated the whole purpose of ordering online and wasted time instead of saving it. This Chipotle location gets 1 star from me and I'm usually not a mean guy.

I love Chipotle food... Huge fan! Could eat it everyday but the service isn't the greatest. I prefer the bowl but for the price you pay they jip you on the contents. They run out of chicken a lot it seems... this day in particular they had only half portion of the chicken so instead of waiting 10 mins for it to be ready I said a little bit off steak would be fine to make up the difference. At the register they tried charging me for double portions. Doesn't make sense that since they can't keep items available that I have to pay more.

I work in the Paseo Nuevo, so if I don't bring lunch to work, Chipotle is my go-to spot.

Excellent portions, I am always full after I leave!! I usually get a chicken or steak burrito, with rice, black beans, a little bit of mild salsa, lots of the corn and a little bit of cheese. Got guac once, and as much as I love guac, it was a bit too much inside. So if I get it, I get it with chips. Service is always fast, however they could be a bit more friendly. Oh, and offer a mall discount please, hahah!

I have a question though, does anyone else think that their food goes cold quickly? I will sit down and start eating right away, but I feel like my food is already lukewarm. Kind of off putting. 

Anyway, I keep going back because I think they put crack in the food.

We came in on a Saturday afternoon (around 2:30-ish) and it was PACKED.  I don't think I've ever driven past this place and not seen it packed, so I wasn't really surprised.  The line moved fairly quickly and I noted that if we had gone next door to Five Guys, we'd be waiting just as long.
It seems we hit them on a bad day though - they were out of chips, lettuce (and later, cheese, but luckily that was after we already got our food).  Also, when we got to pay, we ordered margaritas, but then learned that they were also out of stuff to make margaritas.  Hmm. Oh, and there were no forks, only spoons.  I guess it was a delayed shipment, but it's surprising that a business open for a few months is still running into these problems, especially since this is a chain.
Anyway, all that aside, the burritos were awesome. We both got steak burritos (and a negra modelo).  I got the corn, guacamole, the hottest sauce, black beans, and brown rice on mine.  The corn was super sweet, the guacamole was not skimped on (which almost makes the $1.75(!!!) worth it), and the sauce was awesomely spicy (but I did put some chipotle Tabasco on a few bites).  The pinto beans looked a little weird, but I typically go with black beans anyway.  And, although the rice wasn't crunchy, I think it could have been cooked for just a tab longer.
The restaurant was a little dirty, but I'm not surprised - there were so many people and tables were getting snatched up so quickly, there wasn't really any time for them to get cleaned.
It's too bad that you can't have your margarita or beer out on the patio, it'd be nice on a warm day.
Anyway, the wait is worth it, but don't go in expecting a quick experience.

The most horrible dissociating place ever! Unfortunately my 88 year old mother likes Burger  King so I took her. Home of the whopper did not even have whoppers then she said well then I'll take a milkshake guess what no milkshakes and she wanted an apple pie guess what no apple pie needless to say some months later we  tried again stayed in the drive-through line for about five minutes no one answered the drive-through so we drove around to the window the guy at the window tells us that we need to place an order through the speaker when I told him what happened he looked at us like oh well that's your loss. Of course we left I will never be back again I don't care how bad my 88-year-old mother wants a whopper . This post made me give at least one star they get zero stars

It was late, it was cold, I was kinda craving something hot that was on my way to the train. This was right next to the PATCO stop.

I haven't been in a Burger King in a while, so I opted for a Whopper Junior and asked for no onions and no ketchup, mustard, any of that.  I'm pretty sure she heard me - I asked as she was ringing it up one more time, "No onions, no ketchup mustard, any of that, right?"  She enthusiastically nodded her head.

What did I get I get? A Whopper Junior with everything on it. But I had read the reviews and saw that they throw away anything you return, so I just scraped off all the crap I didn't want.  

Fries were hot and good and free wifi.
Terrible...drove 5 miles out of my way in stiff traffic on the wrong assumption...to try to buy grilled chicken...this joint doesn't sell it....just like what they say in Jersey: forget about it..!!

This place always awful and trashy! The staff was so rude and the buffet was completely empty. It took 20 minute to cook mac and cheese and chicken. THEY RAN OUT OF CHICKEN!  The manager April was extremely rude and that must be where The staff gets it from. They constantly rolled their eyes at us. We will NOT be back

It's hard to match the level of incompetence here. I'm not sure why we go back. Oh yeah, the chicken bites. 

We've been here at least a dozen times and they have literally never gotten our order right. Often it's wrong in our favor, but today we ordered tacos with no lettuce. We said it twice, it was on the receipt, but we made the fatal error of not checking before we left. Lettuce. Ugh. Went back to get new ones and they acted inconvenienced. So sorry, dudes. Do it right the first time. At least they gave us 14 chicken bites in our ten pack.

This is the place that finally did it, the place that broke me. I used to swear by the Whopper and still love it but I'll never eat at another on in my city. All I wanted was a whopper with just ketchup and a hotdog with just ketchup. This is what I ordered, a complicated order I know. I will exaggerate nothing about this experience

Attempt #1 I get a whopper with the works and a hot dog with the work, I return it
Attempt #2 I get a whopper with cheese and mustard and a hot dog with the works, I return
Attempt #3 I get a whopper with cheese and ketchup and a dog with no ketchup, I return

*pause*
Now the most damning thing about this experience actually wasn't the screw ups. Apparently its company policy that whenever food is returned its immediately thrown out. So they threw out about $50 of food because I couldn't get an order with just ketchup.

Attempt #4 I got my order and someone else's. I return the other person's food and it goes right in the trash. This was when I tapped out. I ate my food, then swore to never set foot in the place again

Here's a chance to go to an establishment known worldwide in the industry of fast food. Additionally, it specializes in good eating and good atmosphere. Nevertheless, the trend goes on in society, to beat down and delete brain cells and exchange  thinking power for dependency by what we eat.

Are we going to continue to be tantalized by Satan's  "tempting tactics"i. e., Pastor Adams, MLK Center 1985 and become lodged into traps for all eternity and become snared and remain in torment forever? "Let freedom ring" as told by Dr. Martin Luther King begged the question "loose the shackles" let us out! -- stop ISIS running, wanton and amuck! While these KFC,  always to find ways to make its eateries more balanced, and more attiactive to public. 
great chicken, mashed potatoes and soft drinks.

Without a doubt, the world is becoming more friendly, and this inside arena can be found at KFC. We are posing today changing in offerings by investing in pool tables and possibly weight rooms and how about, conference rooms? We have got to get " the word out! Life 
Change is popping up and hop in and seeing joy of the needs and offerings. There is hope on the horizon, and that can be found by shopping Kentucky Fried Chicken--Main Street Dunedin and be come be served associate Barbara W. who wants to show you that her --guard is up, and she knows that she can win her customers as they will find no gimmick in her style.

Ms. Barbara W wants to show off her place of business and she wants you know that she has favor in KFC. Yes, Route 580 in Dunedin; come in for chicken plates that will dazzle you and where customer service is "job one!" Moreover, feeling somewhat thirsty? Try out the Dunedin "drink machine..." for refreshment, with sparkle from unlimited Hi-C and Coke products.

Nevertheless with somewhat cooler weather setting into the Dunedin area, Floridians coming want to seek that rest from the "busy-ness" which they may not know they need. Sit down for a moment with fried chicken, dealt to you by KFC, "crispy or original"' with no sparing on "flavor," because that's the way it is served.

Kentucky Fried Chicken RT 580 Patricia Ave Is coming into an "active alive" phase, a wake up call, is pending and KFC beyond just another fast restaurant; KFC is a new factor. It is new a system.

Best chain to get a burrito in my opinion. Their menu is concise, the food is reliably good, the service is fast and professional and the place is well designed, making it an overall good experience. I've been getting the sofritas, which is tofu mixed with poblano peppers,  since they opened. I hope they never retire the option for vegetarians and vegans, because it's rad.

I guess this is really a review about the new Double Down, which is two chicken filets that sandwich bacon and cheese and the Colonel's secret sauce.
If you like Kentucky Fried Chicken, you'll like the chicken meat. it's got all that deep fried fatty chicken taste. It's also supernaturally white. I wonder if they process it some how.
But after that deep fried chicken flavor, you don't have much else. They didn't melt the cheese on mine and I think that hurt the experience. The bacon was pretty skimpy, too.
One positive is that you can get a wide variety of sides here.

Long story short, myself and 2 others went. I ordered 2nd and was done before others received their food. My chicken was cold and my Cole Slaw was sub par. The people at the register were below average for fast food and turned a short trip into an hour ordeal.

One word. Terrible. My wife and I went through the drive thru at this location and I dont know if today's youth all have ADHD or they just dont care. It never fails, when you order your food and you never get what you ask for. Seriously, I really dont know how Booger King stay in business. Im not only pissed with this one but all of them. They need to change the name of the whopper to the pancake. Aint nothing whopper size about it. "Where's the beef!"
The only reason I go the Burger King's anymore is because I need something quick and in a hurry.
My wife asked for a Whopper meal, off the broiler (that crap dont work) no pickle, cut in half. Either the staff have special diplomas or like I said before, they dont care. She got a cold sandwich with pickles, a spot of ketchup and few pieces of lettuce. This thing had no tomatoe, and if you eat at Burger King you know they load it up with lettuce and mayo. She came home with it and was pissed. It was so late, she had to nuke it herself, add her own tomatoe, new lettuce, mayo and ketchup. 
Burger King please get it together. Yelp made me give one star, but you dont deserve it.

This del taco awful!!! I went in at 11:33pm and tried to order their breakfast items and they told me they find server breakfast till the morning, but their menu clearly says they start to server breakfast at 11PM to 11AM!! False advertisement!!!

I don't feel any shame in admitting that this was my first experience with this chain. That is not to say that I was unfamiliar with it, but opted for more "authentic" spots when burrito etc. needs arose. After dining here I can better understand their popularity though.

Trish's steak burrito was huge and like everything else here, made to order. I found the beef to be a bit tough and am always turned off when protein is cubed into such consistent pieces. My chicken bowl was perhaps better. The poultry was tender and nicely seasoned with a Tex/ Mex blend. Again another great portion and more suited to a dinner than a lunch. A side of guac was ok, definitely not on par with most Mexican spots.

I would be all in favor of returning if convenience and serving size was my main concern. C's is definitely a good option in comparison with chains that serve similar grub. Maybe even the best option. I prefer to seek out more independent establishments where this type of food is not part of a business plan, but instead the backbone of their food culture. 3.25 still thinking about that whole cilantro fiasco Yelps

Eating here was the first thing I did when I flew into Indy.  It was 3am, so I was glad the place was open 24 hours.  The workers look miserable...like they hate working at a fast food place at 3 in the morning.  

The burgers themselves are good for munchies, but low quality.  They are better than the frozen ones from Lucky, which is what I buy back in Cali to get my WC fix.  The Barq's red cream soda was pretty legit...and I don't even drink soda!  A friend back on the west coast sent me a coupon for 1 free slider (not valid with any other discounts or special offers).  I saved 41 cents!  

Definitely overrated by Harold & Kumar, but now I can cross it off my life's "to do" list!
Love their turkey sausage, egg and cheese breakfast sandwich on a croissant. If you want some good sweet tea well in the mornings this is the spot.

We were the only one in line at the drive thru and it took about 5 mins to receive our order. It was 12:30 in the afternoon. I know that's still pretty fast considering but it's a fast food restaurant and I ordered two whoppers with no special modification from the basic whopper. Hum. It seems like Burger King has lost its touch. As for the whoppers they did not resemble the pictures out there at all!!!! The bun was hard and cold, the meat was cold and thin and there was hardly any lettuce on my whopper. Good and service was poor.

Let's set something straight off the bat: Harold and Kumar were wrong.  White Castle is disgusting.  Even if I were as stoned as those two were in the film, I still would not have had the stomach to finish my meal (let alone the dozens of sliders the two of them scarfed down at the end of the film).  After almost a decade of yearning to taste White Castle's supposedly scrumptious sliders, I could hardly control my excitement as I entered the restaurant and laid my wide eyes on the menu.  I confidently ordered a slider meal and a side of chicken rings (chicken tenders in the shape of rings, sprinkled with buffalo seasoning).  I was so excited that I documented the entire experience!

As soon as I took one of the sliders out of its miniature box, however, I knew it was going to be bad.  The bread was soggy, and the burger patty was less than a millimeter in height (it was really just a thin slice of burger-like lunchmeat).  There were also random holes in each patty (because they apparently weren't scrimping enough with the meat).  The only thing that redeemed White Castle was that they had Barg's Red Cream Soda on tap (I didn't know that existed either until today).  So, I got to try something new.  Although I am happy that I finally tried White Castle, I cannot think of a single reason to ever set foot in that establishment again.

Well My family and I walk in around 3:00pm, Just a couple tables are occupied. We noticed how music is blasting in the back but we know it's fast food and sit far away from the counter as we could. First we're told they can't print a receipt ,my husband asks why and the sweet young lady says I'm not sure I've only worked here 4 days and it hasn't worked since I started. We wait for our food. The yelling to one another in the back was getting to me but I just wanted to eat and leave at this point. We get our food, chicken was good but my Mac and cheese was old and over cooked. I see not one but 2 people from the drive thru come in with missing items. Then a very loud obnoxious employee comes in reached in front of a elderly man grabbed a cup and yelled to the back he was here. Two more people come in to order and the sweet young girl at the register " Danielle" apologized that she couldn't hear what they wanted to order due to the noise. At this point I get up and ask her the managers name and what store number this was.. the assistant manager "Sam" comes over and asks "what's the problem?" I told her about the noise and how a high school locker room would be more quite. She wouldn't give me the store number because she didn't know the difference between that and the manager "Jackie" phone number...Really?, She then yells for a employee  CLOCK IN CLOCK IN!!!...I filed a complaint with corporate... the only one to even apologized was the sweet girl at the register... guess I won't go there anytime around 3pm...

This was my first time ever trying Hardee's/Red Burrito. I have eaten at Carl's Jr./Green burrito many times in the past, and thought because they are the same parent company that they would be similar, but are in fact very different. There is very few menu items that are the same, unfortunately they do not have the Western Bacon Cheeseburger or a Famous Star at Hardee's...

I tried the bacon thick-burger w/seasoned curly fries which was actually pretty decent. I also tried the red burrito, which was basically a beef & been burrito covered in enchilada sauce and cheese (decent). My wife had the chicken fingers w/fries and they were pretty good.

Overall, I gave this a 3 star rating based on comparison to other fast food chains. Wouldn't go out of my way to come back to this location in Kenner.

This is by far the slowest fast food I've ever been to. It's insane. Sitting in drive thru for 17 minutes and only two cars ahead of me. This chicken better be magnificent, because this wait is crazy. I can see through the drive thru that it's dirty. Perhaps I should cancel my order... The cashier took five minutes to come to the window after I pulled up and when she did, she was trying her best to hurry and was nice but damn. Won't come back
Where is Harold and Kumar?! (sorry I had to) and what happened to the chicken parmigiana slider?!

Several times out of extra crispy, always rude and act offended.  They are a world class chicken place everywhere else in the world.  Kuwait has a better KFC than this location.  My family will never attempt to eat here again.  Shame.

I've been coming here awhile now, and I have never had an issue, ever. I usually order the steak bowl every time, sometimes I have the chicken burrito. 
The food here is always awesome and the staff is always nice and engaging. The tables are almost always clean as well as the drink station. 
There's a pretty decent amount of seating, including outdoor seating; most is shaded. 
You can expect to pay about 20$ for only 2 people there, and that's including fountain drinks. 
There's always good music playing too. Sadly there's only a couple booths, but those are mainly for a group. 
So come here with friends, family or just you for lunch. Love this place!

The worst Chipotle I've ever been in. The place is disorganized behind the counter, food sitting in line getting cold, and the woman helping us couldn't fold a burrito. Even the steak in the burrito was inedible. It was all gristle and cold. As well as the beans, rice and cheese all tasting old and bland. The only thing we could eat were the chips that were also going stale. The seating area was dirty as well. There are 6 people in the front of house and no one knows, or doesn't care what they are doing. Never again will I go to another Chipotle. Gross.
We go to Chipotle at least once a week.  We have NEVER been disappointed!!  The staff is friendly and the food is excellent.  We love the Sofritas and substitute it for the meats. Yummy!

Went through the drive-through for a fast bite after a long day and no one in the mood to cook or go out in the cold.  The drive through was a great idea for all so my dining partner wanted a Whopper and fries while I wanted a double cheeseburger.

When I ordered "just the sandwich" for the Whopper I was advised that it was Whopper Wednesday and that it would be cheaper to order the meal.  It was a decent deal.   

The food was hot but I was appalled by the size of a "medium" drink which had to be 20 oz plus.  That's not this BK's fault, but it was scary big.

This experience was OK, but it is still fast food.   Getting in and out of the parking lot if you are headed westbound on Baltimore Pike can be challenging.

It's seriously unreal how long you will wait here... drive thru, inside... doesn't matter! Currently inside, 15 minutes and counting. I'll attempt to fry my own chicken, prior to wasting my  time here again. And this is me being extremely polite.

**update!
Took 20 minutes. 
AND! Although the bag sat upright in the position they gave it to me... it leaked ALL OVER MY CAR.

This place is right on my way to work so I must confess-- have been tempted on more than one occasion  to swing into the drive-thru and treat myself to the sausage biscuit and gravy---coffee---sometimes the hashbrowns....The biscuits are really top notch here and I was raised on some fine southern cooking by a mama born and raised in Georgia so I can appreciate a good biscuit.  The gravy is just white sauce but I snarf it down anyhow.  The coffee isn't very good and I wish it were better because I always order it anyhow as I would rather have bad coffee than no coffee in the early morning!  If the coffee and gravy were better I would raise this one more star.....I have never had a burger from Hardee's in my life....I don't care for how sloppy and manly the sandwiches look so I am never tempted...

Quick meal for your childten, I can always count on Burger King to solve hunger fix. My son, on his grown man, has impressed me and surprised with the kind of appetite he has. Food of choice, Whopper meal. Handled the Whopper meal as he has other times like a pro. My youngest main food from the BK menu is the chicken fries and fries combo. Tears it up and eats every once of food when its ordered for him. My oldest enjoyed a Whopper meal with fries and a sweet tea. All meals were eaten and enjoyed.

I agree with other reviews. Meat is very bland and poorly seasoned. Crunchy tacos were not that crunchy. My taco had  so much cheese it was simply gross. Looked like I had ordered a cheese taco and not a beef taco. Taco Bell is much better than this. Now, if you want to lose weight, by all means eat here. The food  is so bad/bland that will kill your appetite. I ordered 3 tacos and only ate 1 simply bc I worked 12 hours, then went hiking on the mountain and it was 10 pm and desperate for some food. But not desperate enough to eat all 3 tacos. I will not go there again. Drive thru staff girl is super sweet! But the food is .

Service is hit or miss. When it gets busy, expect to wait for your food for quite awhile depending on who is currently on staff.

Sometimes the food and service is great but then comes the times when your waiting 20 minutes for a bucket of chicken or you have to have your sandwich remade since they put mayo on it when you told them not to.

More recently the quality of service and the consistency of the food has been a lot better. I know the chicken and even the potato wedges can take some time to cook and I prefer the food to as fresh as possible but at least explain that to the customer instead of just messing around behind the counter, etc.

You know the sandwiches are good, same goes for the chicken (crispy, original or roasted). For the most part the food is good and of decent quality overall. If you have been to one KFC then you know what you want. Go get your bucket of crispy chicken and a BBQ chicken sandwich with wedges as the side and your good to go.

It's hard to review because both times I went there, they didn't have what I wanted. Last night my husband and I went through the drive-thru, we ordered a 12 piece extra crispy. They didn't have 12 extra crispy so they said they could give us 6 regular and 6 extra crispy. Fine. Then they said they were out of biscuits, honestly my favorite part, but okay they said they would give us an extra side. We ordered the mashed potatoes, but oops no they were out of potatoes. We said never mind and went to wawa. The other time we went they were completely out of chicken.

I ordered a bowl with sofritas and when I went to take a bite I saw something crispy in it. I took it out and it was a piece of plastic. I immediately brought it to the managers attention. She said it might have been a piece of glove which is absolutely disgusting. I barely started trusting chipotle again and they go and do this. I was given a refund but idk if chipotle will ever be as good again.

This is the worst kfc ever every time I go there to order chicken they always blame someone else I have live here for over 17 years and I hear the Same excuses every time it's we didn't get our order the manager didn't order enough I can see if it was once or twice but god dame every time same thing all they say is we close at 10 nothing I can tell you stay away from this location trust me you won't be happy just go to churches or Popeyes chicken what a freakin joke of a place to go eat at I just went to same Kfc for 2 $5 fill ups of chicken tenders and it's only 430 on June 14 they are out of tenders this is the worst ever manager should get fired

Great value on the all you can eat buffet. $8.50 which includes a drink. Not bad at all for some great KFC food.  The hot food bar has a good selection of fresh and hot mashed potatoes, gravy, stuffing, several types of KFC chicken, and a cool bar which has the cold items like jello, cole slaw, puddings etc.  They have some fresh carrots and beets which is unusual in a fast food restaurant and these are good to nibble on after you have eaten your fill.  The staff here is always helpful, busy, yet attentive to the needs of a buffet style restaurant. If you need extra anything that they may be short of on the buffet bar, just ask them, and they will get it for you.  Its nice to take your time and relax and have a great KFC meal. I have been going to Kentucky Friend Chicken restaurants since 1966 when my grandmother used to bring it home for us.
Do not eat at this KFC. We went at the dinner hour and they had no chicken. The place was dirty and littered.

This is still the best Burger King in the Reno/Sparks area.  Pepe is mainly to thank for that.  He goes above and beyond to give customers a friendly greeting and make sure every experience is an excellent one.  If Pepe is working, order with confidence.

Brenda, however, is another story entirely.  Four or five months ago, she was a trainee and is now apparently in the manager training program.  She can't seem to fill up an ice cream cup to save her life, she's stingy as all hell with the Zesty sauce, and she ignores customers who attempt to get her attention.  I'm not saying she's a bad person, she just has a lot to learn about fast food.  Maybe she should look to Pepe as an example of how to do this job.

I'd give Pepe an 8 out of 5 if I could.  I'd give Brenda 1 out of 5, because she does manage to hand me food and return change.  She seems plenty intelligent and capable, but not too friendly or generous.

I also tried the Burger King Chicken Fry Rings and they're pretty good.  The Angriest Whopper is fantastic.  Both are on sale for a limited time, so don't hesitate to try them out.

That said, not much beats the 2 for $5 menu.  My family has become avid fans of the Extra Long Fish Sandwich and the Extra Long Philly Burger.  You can't beat the value for the price.

And my husky still adores Burger King's ice cream.  :)

Stopped in tonight for dinner and noticed three employees standing outside smoking. No big deal everyone gets a break. Walked inside and there was a woman and her young son standing at the register. After standing there for a minute or so I asked her if she had been helped, she replied "no I have not seen anyone."  I told her that I think the whole staff is outside smoking. We talked for a moment and one employee walked in talking on her headset to someone in the drive thru, and she said she will be in in a minute she is still smoking! 
Yeah I think not, I walked out and went to Church's Chicken down the road. 
I will not return. I will stick to the location in Speedway.

I've spent many hours in KFC doing research for my new book, "A Connecticut Yankee in Kentucky Fried Chicken."

In this time, I've come to get into a kind of groove with my food selection and have pretty much stuck with the chicken dishes and strawberry shortcake desserts. I feed the biscuits to my dog, Dr. Kevorkian von Hamburgler.

I even started dating one of the employees briefly, but mostly for the discounted chicken and "French benefits." Unfortunately I finished my book last week and decided my time with KFC had to come to an end. I'm moving on to bigger and better things. To Moniqueca, my darling, "Nuestro amor vive eternamente en el piso de cuarto de baño de KFC. "
I placed an order for pick up and my bowl had a few steak chunks in it. I ordered sofritas. Beware !!!
It's ok - they need to fix their speaker . Chicken is overpriced but good.

This is the worst kfc ever every time I go there to order chicken they always blame someone else I have live here for over 17 years and I hear the Same excuses every time it's we didn't get our order the manager didn't order enough I can see if it was once or twice but god dame every time same thing all they say is we close at 10 nothing I can tell you stay away from this location trust me you won't be happy just go to churches or Popeyes chicken what a freakin joke of a place to go eat at
Just ordered a 3 piece chicken meal to go. Got home to realize I ACTUALLY got 1.5 pieces of chicken. Counting to 3 should not be this challenging. Extremely disappointed with this service

Sharaya the cashier I had tonight was excellent. Great personality, pleasant, and patient. I can't always say that at this location, but tonight that was the case. Other than the 19 minute wait for the grilled chicken (which can happen) I was pleased with my visit tonight. I have 2 KFC's near me, and I definitely prefer this one to the one on Champlost (the worst). I only go to that location because there's a Taco Bell attached and it's not one at this location.
On our travels we decided to stop at KFC. We order a box meal and both our orders came out wrong and missing legs and a thigh. Once we started to eat, the chicken was rather greasy..

They have friendly staff and good portion sizes here, which is pretty much all I need to be content. The last time I was here one of the employees accidentally put cheese on my sofritas bowl. Everybody was apologetic and they remade my bowl and comped it for me, which was really unexpected. I make it a point to eat here whenever I know I'll be in the area.

Oh White Castle, land of the sliders, home of the fried, I know exactly the quality to expect and with expectations inline with reality I am never disappointed.  

If you're not sold on the idea of White Castle or you cling to the stigma - I encourage you to give their breakfast menu a go. While there are classics like the breakfast sliders, there are also just standard, no frills, affordable breakfast options - ie the bacon, egg, and cheese on toast. More adventurous? Try the breakfast waffle slider!

How is this place still in business? Needs new management or be  be closed down. Waited 40 minutes in line tonight. Chicken was cooked alright but had an "off" flavor, no chicken flavor at all. I took one bite and tossed it. Biscuit was good as were the mashed potatoes and gravy ( Cartman's right about the gravy!) But the service here was THE worst fast food service this 60 yo has ever experienced. It's sad when I think back to what KFC USED to be like.

AWFUL! My Dad and I went here for a change of pace, as neither of us had eaten at D.D before. When we got there the place was empty but the server/barista/cook (I use the term 'cook' loosely) was busy with the drive-thru so we waited for about 5 or 6 minutes. We both ordered a sausage egg and cheese biscuit, a large tea and a donut. I was surprised that the total came to over $17.00. Afterwards we waited another 5 or 6 minutes for our food, which was a brick-flavored biscuit with floppy square egg, cheese glued to said brick-biscuit, and a burnt circle of sausage. It had obviously been microwaved and was inedible. The donut tasted like it was made the day before and there was more ice than tea I our drinks. Needless to say we will opt for Dylan's across the street for a decent breakfast!

Won't be back. Long line. Didn't have any steak for my burrito. They didn't take any measure to make that right. Cashier had a canned BS spiel about why prices went up. Didn't give me napkins. No drinks available except soda (really). Not worth the $11.10 for my burrito and drink.

I love the options here.  My favorite is the Sofritas salad.  I also like that you can see how many calories are in each item you choose.  Which is nice to know you can save some extra calories by opting out of one or two little things.  This place is close to work so, It's a good lunch spot for me.  The staff is always helpful, the music can be a little loud depending who is on shift, but that's just me.

Of the 3 Chipotles in town, this is my favorite. The staff is fast, attentive, and friendly. I have tons of food allergies and they are getting better at avoiding cross contamination, but once in a while some sofritas will sneak into my chicken and I end up having to take Benadryl. But this hasn't happened in several months. So good job guys.
3/3 visits and out of chicken?? How is this possible when all they do is chicken. I have given KFC so many tries but I'm always disappointed. Makes me sad because I used to love it

Newer location by the Shops and Perry Crossing in Plainfield. I like the interior design...modern, still feels like "Chipotle" but somehow seems cleaner and more open than the older design. The kitchen is also totally open and it looked very clean. 

About the food... who hasn't been to a Chipotle? I've been to MANY over the years. I recommend the sofritas, they are a yummy spicy meat free protein that's totally not just for vegetarians. I usually get them with crunchy tacos and hot salsa.

Twas the crack of noon when I journeyed far and wide with my brothers in arms to conquer the craving for the quesalupa. Eat something that won't give us violent diarrhea, we asked? Ney, we need that which we desire. Arriving at the taco fortress, we had to brave the frigid cold during the trek from the mighty Mazda, across the mud moat, and through the gates. To our fortune, our bodies didn't have to adjust from the cold, as the restaurant was just as cold, perhaps even colder than the tundra from whence we came. The guardian of the register greeted us with a frozen glance, as she stood facing her icy kingdom. We placed our orders for the newest items that KFC and TB added to their expansive menu. While waiting for the food to be prepared (microwaved), we noticed that this fortress could be described in one word, "grim." It was as if we were in catacombs. The clientele and the staff looked as if they were begrudgingly forced to come to this cold restaurant everyday while every member of their family was systematically murdered by terrorists each time they stepped foot through the doors. When our numbers were called, we tried to stand up from our seats, but one of my friend's butts was frozen to his grease coated plastic chair. The chicken was supposed to come with cole slaw. There was no cole slaw. This Taco Bell is the reason why there is war.

Went in tonight ordered two breast and wing 3 piece combination. They had no biscuits ready at 5:00 pm. When I got home I found out they gave me 2 thighs and a drumstick.  Do they not listen when taking orders.  I didn't even eat the chicken. 
I noticed when I was there all the customers rechecking their orders I know why now. 
These employees should do their job right or be supervised better, very disappointing.

Honestly, I can't stand it when an overhyped chain finally comes to Reno. Reno people LOVE chains, and when something finally opens after a few months of hype, they swarm the place. That's exactly what's going on with Chipotle right now. I vowed to avoid this place for as long as possible. I guess I made it a few days. I've been to a few Chipotles before, so I wanted to see how this location stacked up. As expected, it's clean and neat. The staff is friendly. This Chipotle is a bit small compared to other locations I've been to. The bathrooms are in a weird spot. The small size will probably be less noticeable when the honeymoon phase dies out and the crowds get smaller. Between Five Guys and Chipotle parking kinda sucks right now. 

Basically, It's Chipotle. It's quality white people mexican food at a reasonable price. On this particular day I decided to go with a barbacoa beef burrito with sour cream and pinto beans. Their burritos are huge and overall pretty tasty. Personally I think the barbacoa beef is the best thing they have going for them. The carnitas are pretty good, the chicken is okay, and the steak is decent. I like the flavors, but the execution gets me every time. More often than not the chicken is kinda dry and the steak too chewy. I've never understood why their sour cream is so runny and why they use white rice. Their chips are awesome, and they have good salsas. The mild salsa, and the tomatillo green chile salsas are their best in my book. If you're into hot salsa the hot one is pretty good too. 

So basically, if you want Chipotle, congrats. Reno has it now. So rejoice, Chipotle lovers. It's consistently Chipotle every time you visit no matter the location, and for that I commend them. 

If you want Mexican food, go to Super Burrito. Go to Speedy Burritos. Find a good roach coach. Go anywhere but Chipotle. Go get an al pastor burrito at Super Burrito for like $4.50 and try to tell me Chipotle is still worth 5 stars on yelp and standing in line for 20 minutes.

Solid, fresh, dependable. Not much more to say. Guac is extra, as you know, but is sort of uncalled for in a bowl anyway. Chicken, steak and carnitas are always perfectly seasoned. I've started to go for simplicity with just double rice, beans, lettuce and double meat, and honestly I prefer that to the mushy confusion of flavors you get when you order everything.

Never again. Went in to get chicken and sides for our normal Sunday dinner. Had 8-10 of us to bring food home to, and at KFC prices, we were going to be dropping $40 easy. We walked in and weren't even acknowledged. Four parties stood in front of us waiting for service. Employee at the counter was too busy carrying on with the two customers already at the counter who'd already paid and were waiting on their food. No mention of "be right with you" to anyone waiting. After waiting 10 minutes and watching her still acting like everyone else in the restaurant could go to hell, I was over it. Two additional people had come in and lined up behind us. The one other African American customer was welcomed as he hit the door. Interesting. One person in front of us left, and I gave up. I'm not going to beg you to take my money and be apathetic and rude while you do it. We drove all the way over to Carlyle Ave., and it was a total 180. That's where we've been going since, and that won't change.

We don't go to KFC all that often - maybe two or three times per year. It's not high cuisine and it's not healthy, but the chicken is decent and the sides are usually consistently just as one would expect. You know what you're in for at KFC, or at least that's the theory.

But my last two times they've been out of stuff. Last time, it was mashed potatoes. Tonight, biscuits. 

Okay, fine, stock runs out. I get that. Although, come to think of it, they don't have a wide-ranging menu with a dynamic ingredient line up, so it's kinda odd to run out of basics when all that you serve are, well, basics... Anyway, I digress...

Here's the thing: I don't understand why they sold me two meals which are supposed to come with biscuits - clearly (read below), they knew they were omitting them, but did not tell me, or offer a substitute. Oh, but I did get the fake butter and honey packet in each meal box, so yay! (The last time I was there, when they were out of mashed potatoes,another customer in front of me came back to complain, which was how I found out. Why are they not up front about the availability of their items?)

Back to tonight's episode, I got home and opened the boxes up and discovered the lack of biscuits, called the store, and the manager, in a tone utterly lacking surprise, said, "Oh yeah, we're out of biscuits tonight. Sorry the cashier didn't tell you that. Do you have your receipt?" Well, I suppose there's a tiny chance that I would guess EXACTLY the item that they're out of on the very night that they're out of it, and then proceed to try to pull a con job on them by lying that I had just been there to pick up to-go meals, so that maybe I can wrangle me a FREE meal at some point in the future. But really? I just wanted the freakin' biscuits. And no, I don't want to get back in my car at 5pm to drive back to their store to pick up my two biscuits.

I know, I know, we're talking biscuits here, people. It's not a major life crisis. But here's the context: I was up all night long with a sick child (yeah, the worst kind of sick to have to clean up), and had planned to give him the biscuit and a little chicken w/o the skin. I had not slept all night, had gone to work, and picked up a fast food meal because I was in no shape to cook a regular dinner at home. So I expect KFC to fulfill their part of the social contract into which they had entered by allowing me to purchase their freakin' meals!! And the contract terms explicitly state that I get a damn biscuit with each meal!

Train your staff, Mr. or Ms. Franchise Owner. Customer service matters even in the take-out-fast-food biz.

Seriously delicious, but bring your appetite!!

I love the carnitas burrito bowl with brown rice and guacamole.

The ordering process is quick, sleek, easy to get in and out.  A great variety of meats (and now they offer brown rice!!).  The lime cilantro rice is delicious, and normally I hate cilantro, that's how good it is.

sourcreamandcheese?  That's usually how they ask and I always say YES!  The guacamole is worth the extra cash, and ask for it on the side so you can distribute as necessary.  Sometimes I get chips too, so I can use that as a shovel/eating utensil to get the delicious food down my trap even faster.

I've always loved Chipotle- consistent and good without feeling like you sinned in the fast food world.

This location is Chipotle's hidden gem.  Or maybe it is just that this is the newest location in the area, but hardly ever is there a long line here.  The 146th St.  location, only 2 miles away,always has a long line, frequently out the door.

The food is Chipotle's usual quality, good!  Chipotle serves the highest quality food of any of the quick-dine restaurants.  

I like the convenience of this location as well.  It is next to Blaze pizza so I can quickly pick up a pizza for the non-burrito member of my family when taking Chipotle home.

Can't go wrong with Sofritas burrito bowl with sautéed veggies, black beans, rice, roasted corn salsa, cheese, sour cream, and of course guacamole.

It was the worst experience I have ever had at a KFC...
Took an hour to get 8 pcs of chicken.
The place was dirty and "friends" of the staff came in and helped themselves to sodas. The manager did nothing to get them under control or ask them to leave. I tried to send my comments to KFC but their website would not accept my comments.
I was told this was a Franchise owned by a company out of New Jersey... I'd say it's time for a visit by KFC management to see how they are being represented.

I love the flavoring here but the proportions on what they put in usually arent up to par. Could be my fault for not specifying though. Usually theres way too much rice and beans and then when it comes to the meat theres almost no room left. However the biggest flaw that I find everytime I go here is that the steak is SO FATTY. Theres nothing worse than expecting the steak texture and getting a mouth full of fat and having to spit it out. They really should be sourcing better cuts of meat or at least cutting that much fat off before they cook it.

My favorite taco shop is now in town!  I can't speak much for the other menu items, but the soft corn chicken tacos are an excellent menu choice.  Brown rice, pinto beans, peppers, chicken, salsa, corn, cheese, guacamole and sour cream.  The staff is real friendly and the line moves fast. 

Only complaints:

Location is too small.  Most Chipotles are cramped but this one is a stretch. Bathrooms are very close to where they cook the food.  Not that's it's a health hazard...just funky.   

Steak - it's chewy.  I've never had this problem at a Chipotle before, so I'm not sure what's up the the steak here?  I'll give it another shot down the road.

This is not a five star restaurant...so don't expect to be wowed.  It's great place to get a good meal at a fair price.

Stopped in at 5:30 PM waited in line for a burrito bowl only to find them low on black beans.  Only enough left for a half tea spoon.  They were out of steak and then I noticed they were out of lettuce.  That's when we decided to leave and take our business elsewhere.  They were not busy at all either.  Very disappointing.

The people who work here have always been cool and the food always come out as expected of this chain. You have to be careful with order accuracy but i forgive them. The Nashville Hot Chicken is bangin.

This place deserves a zero star if I could rate this low. There is no management. Absolutely none. Waited 15 minutes at the counter. Employees lazy, sitting on their fannies, dragging their feet. They move slow. Their receipt machine even broke and could not even give me a receipt after paying with a credit card. I told them to cancel my order. They did give me a refund with cash. Young people these days. No respect for elders. No signs of training. No discipline. No customer service. This place won't make it much longer. You are much better off going to Panda Express on Overland.

Worst KFC Taco Bell ever. Every time I have been here, something either gets messed up or doesn't taste right. This time, they don't have extra crispy chicken or breasts in original. How does KFC run out of chicken!?

This is a quick & easy place to go for some inexpensive taco-type grub.   Dad & I prefer the shrimp tacos.   The standard 39¢ tacos haven't made it all the way home in the best shape, so tend to eat in.

We've been waited on by quite a few different staff members.  They've all been pleasant, but Carlos has been the smiling standout of the group.

Oh Holy Lord no!

I've been Yelping for years. In that time, I don't think I ever left a "scathing" review. But this time. This time is the exception.

I walked into this cesspit of an excuse for a fast food restaurant and there were zero other customers in the place. No one in line. No one ordering. No one sitting in the many empty, dirty seats eating. Nadda.

So did someone take my order as soon as I walked in since the place was deserted. Nope. How long did it take to even get to give my order, you ask? I got there at 5:34pm and my order was taken at 5:39pm. Literally 5 minutes to take my order in an empty restaurant. Was that the end of my wait. Not even by a long shot!

I ordered a small #1 meal. That's the Whopper meal. The Burger King signature meal. Should be super easy. We'll gloss over that the clerk asked if I wanted cheese without telling me there's a 60¢ charge for cheese. Whatever. Again, with zero other customers in the store, it took them another 7 minutes to get the Whopper cooked and to me. They weren't waiting on fries. There were some drive-thru orders happening. But that was it. 7 minutes AFTER ordereing in an empty store to get me the signature burger.

You'd think that the burger would then be super fresh and made just for me. Nope. The patty was a hockey puck and there was barely any sauce. I'm glad I upgraded to the cheese or I wouldn't have been able to choke it down.

While I was waiting 7 minutes for them to cook me a Whopper, another gentleman came in. He also ordered a Whopper meal. The clerk asked what size and he was confused by the question. Rather than simply clarifying "The Whopper meal comes standard with a small fry and drink. Would you like to upgrade that to medium or large?" she starts actually raising her voice at him and just repeating the same "What size?" question.

There was no ketchup out in the restaurant. So after my 7 minute wait and getting my food set down, I had to go ask them if there was any ketchup available. She barks at me that there's none on the floor because the pump's broken and huffs while reluctantly giving me some packs.

So I'm eating my hockey puck of a burger and two African-American teens walk in. They looked 15 to me. Seemed like OK young men. They go up to the counter and ordered some food. I'm at the back of the restaurant, so I couldn't hear their order. But I sure heard when the clerk huffed and slammed a cup down with "Here's your 'water'!" The boys took their cups and went over to the drink machine to fill them and she watched them like a hawk and then barks again "The water is the BLUE button!" So we can safely add racism to the list of things this Burger King excels at.

I wouldn't eat at this location again if Burger King paid me. The story above doesn't make it clear but there were TWO different clerks running the registers. So this wasn't just one grumpy lady. And there were cooks behind the counter. Why it took me 12 minutes to order and receive a Whopper in an empty Burger King is a dang mystery. And then you compound that with bad attitude, shady upgrades, and mistreatment of other customers of all races but highlighted with some specific racism, and this is the pits.

Only reason for. This many stars is because they do have vegetarian options and the "IMPOSSIBLE BURGER" 

The reason they are more of a 2 Star or less place is because it's not clean inside or out usually. The building looks bad too. The drive thru is designed poorly and the wait areas are awkwardly in the way if u have to pull up. 

The staff consistently makes food incorrectly and the manager makes you feel like it's ur wrong when you come back bc of messed up food. Never felt so bad for returning due to inaccurate orders before. I get it that this is a low need fast food joint but common courtesy is a human right not just a necessary job duty. 

Please get it together White Castle, the impossible burger is a huge shock and plus to find so cheap and tasty but the staff and overall look of the place is pretty bad for the most part. There are some nice window workers in drive thru, just wish they understood orders on the screen better and cleaned the place better.

Cashier was very nice but that's where the positive experience ends. I ordered 2 of the chicken & waffles. Paid $7.50 each. Then ordered 2 other meals and biscuits. I didn't check the bag before I left. They left out one of the waffle meals. An annoyance but we'll make do. After several phone calls I finally got someone to answer my call. They would not credit my card or notate that I'm owed a meal unless I came back that evening. It's s 35 minute ride on a Sunday night. I asked if I could come in at the beginning of the week for my credit. I was told it's up to the manager. Really?! Well instead I've just put a claim in with the cc company and my whole order will now be on hold. Small mindedness. Most likely we would've gotten the meal that was owed us and then another one for my son. Obviously poor management.

Save your money. My family and I have been coming to this KFC for years but recently it's the worst place to visit. Always an issue when visiting from staff not knowing what's on the menu and arguing with customers, long waits, and even their system being down multiple times in drive-they but was able to ring up customers inside. There are many better options around for fried chicken around the area so don't waste your time with this place.

First off I was just got off of work expecting a nice meal of fried chicken, knowing the hours were open until 10 pm. But what do you know?! The god damn drive through was already closed and the lady was just sitting there cleaning. You think I didn't see her?! I arrived at 9:52 with 8 minutes to spare. Where is the Christmas spirit at. Sadly enough I would never recommend this location better that the chain itself! I'm bringing my business elsewhere such as Popeyes. Good day and good luck to anyone else who decides to go to this location.

PS my friends now are so freaking disappointed with me and now I'm so embarrassed. I let them down, they expected f****ingchicken potatoe wedges and a famous f***ing chicken bowl! Now I'm red in the face.

Went in at about 3:15 in the afternoon. The place is inviting. Clean windows, friendly staff. We decided this was the KFC for us. We had the buffet. It was very good and hot. The only drawback was we always had to wait for them to add drumsticks. Potato wedges are not included in the buffet so you have to eat mashed if you don't want to pay extra. Also the hot and spicy chicken they advertise so much is not in the buffet. All in all we were happy with our visit at this particular KFC. We visited the one on Seminole and were not impressed.

It's review catch up day! I went to this location two days ago it was a little disappointing but not all bad. Ordered the medium spicy chicken meal and a double whopper with cheese ( shush it was lunch and dinner for the day :p ) It was empty and took a while for anyone to come take the order; they saw me and just didn't care. I waited was given my bag and offered sauces and they walked away without handing me a cup. I stood there a moment and said excuse me a few times and was ignored and the guy waiting to order next just reached over and handed it to me. It's fast food so meh. The chicken and fries were good, the whopper I'm pretty sure they opened an old cold one and just added slices of cold cheese,and the soda machine gave me seltzer because they hadn't hooked it up to the Sprite syrup so that was poured out once home. Fast Food is usually a hit and miss but some places and locations care enough to actually work at work. It's a little sad to me when people don't realize a slump in sales usually means cutting employees and locations being closed when too many customers are alienated. Received a call from the manager wanting to rectify the experience. I try to be reasonable about these types of things and certainly appreciate an attempt at providing better service. If I visit this location again for my own meal I'd gladly change the review after a few visits as I usually have no issues with this location. If in the area again I'll at the very least stop for charities sake and give it to one of the many homeless people I see in the area from time to time because I don't usually keep cash on me but certainly don't mind helping someone else have a meal.

Went through drive through Sunday night.  Waited at least 15 minutes.   Spent 32.00 only to find when I got home that they didn't put my 2 orders of mashed potatoes and gravy in the bag.    Called when I got home to complain and to determine whether I wanted to drive the 10 minutes back to the store.   The young lady that answered the phone apologized and asked if I wanted to come back for the items.   I told her it was unacceptable to not check the order and I referenced their two stars on Yelp. She said it was unacceptable and they have been receiving complaints because "some people here don't know how to check the orders".  When I told her I would drive back to get the items she told me that the lobby closed in 10 minutes.   I guess that was her way of saying, sorry you spent 32.00 on your meal that we didn't even have the courtesy to ensure was correct but hurry if you want us to get you what you paid for.   Adding additional insult, the chicken breasts were puny.   Not sure if this will let me add a photo but I will try.   Keep my 7.58 worth of mashed potatoes KFC Dunedin.   It will be the last money you ever get from me.

I live 2 minutes from here and eat here once a month or so. They never get my order right. One time the guy told me f your taco, when he forgot it  in my order. Right now they are out of beans on the Taco Bell side and chicken on the kfc side. Another time I ordered and the lady gave me a free drink. When I asked why she said we are really slow and I can't give you a receipt so I thought a drink would help. I have also seen a cashier sing for 10 minutes instead of take my order. On the bright side I think this place is the inspiration for every fast food joke you can think of

The Vessel: Burrito Bowl (no rice) with double order of chicken/steak/carnitas with black beans, pinto, salsa, guacamole, lettuce, cheese and small dab of sour cream. Devour.

Service: No problems at this particular location.

Suggestion: Tabasco is okay in a pinch but I would appreciate if you would carry   Tapatío or Cholula sauce! (the wife refuses to carry it in her purse, the nerve of her). I would love to see a grilled vegetable option to add to my bowl (maybe some yellow/red peppers, squash, zucchini, etc.). I like lots of veggies with my meat.

Verdict: Great fast-food chain, simple menu, fairly healthy ingredients and easy-on-the-wallet. Parking lot sucks here during prime-time hours.

I agree with the review below! If you are plant based and refrain from the meat + cheese, you still get charged extra for guacamole and charged the same price as if you have all of the animal products in your meal. I feel if I don't get cheese or sour cream or meat, guac should be at least included for free-I am eating less. The black beans and especially the Sofritas are always old and crusty looking, or they are out completely and I have to wait for them to cook. Customer service was blah. Workers were bickering about "where they put the tofu."
Def a bigger Flame Broiler fan after this visit.

This is my favorite place to go for a quick great fresh meal. But the last two times I have in there they have been either out of chicken or steak. Not sure why that keeps happening but it does. 
Last night June 13th was no different. The steak was out and however I did get last of chicken. The staff seemed overwhelmed or disorganized. The staff is always friendly. No one seemed to be cooking anything either. 
The place last night looked like a bomb had gone off in there. Dirty tables, lids for cups were out, the spoons were empty. When I was there around 8:00 pm there was a family of 4 and myself inside so being busy was not an excuse for cleanliness. 
I do love getting my burrito bowls fresh and fast but the cleanliness of the place last night was horrible. 
I hope on my next visit they have improved.

After waiting 7 minutes for a Whopper with cheese, everything on it and small fries, I get back to work and enjoy my cheeseburger...NOTHING on it. Seriously, you had one job!!! 
Seldom eat Burger King and this isn't going to make me want to come back anytime soon.
Is fast food really that complicated?  Ordered food through drive through.  Missing chicken? Yes at a chicken place! This KFC always has a help wanted sign.  Pretty much sums it up

I won't lie,I didn't buy, try, or eat anything... I just wanted a burger, so I went to order a whopper... it was like 5 bucks... just for the sandwich. ..nahh, I passed... I don't remember it tasting that good...

Atmosphere:

The atmosphere is always similar to this chain. This Chipotle is small in size, but has modern décor and design.  The aroma was a kick to your gut. Your hunger pains will kick in when you enter the facility.  Like many Chipotle restaurants the atmosphere is always hip and "cool". 


Staff:

The sole reason why I'm giving a 2 out of five stars. The staff needs training in "customer service". I asked for double fajitas because I wasn't getting any rice or beans. The gal assisting us seemed like it was the biggest ordeal. She even rolled her eyes.. rolled her eyes at me. I even said "May I please have extra fajita" with a big smile. She didn't acknowledge me, just rolled her eyes. She sent my burrito bowl down the assembly line without a further acknowledgement. 



Prices:

The price is about 10 bucks a person, which includes a drink. 


Location:
At the Village, next to Zupas. 



Parking / Venue: 
Limited parking in that little square. However, I've never had a problem finding a parking spot in front of Chipotle after the dinner rush or lunch rush. 



Food:
 The food is fresh!  You get to pick the food which goes into the bowl, burrito, taco, or salad. There are many options which includes of: meat (chicken, pork, steak, or beef), two types of beans (Pinto, black), two types of rice (cilantro, brown), fajitas, corn salsa, lettuce, sour cream, salsa (medium, hot, mild), guac, and cheese. 

Specialities / what "they" are known for?
 
The "assembly" line. The quick convenience and fresh "fast" food options. 

Star rating:
2 out of 5


More importantly Would we ever go back? 

Yeah, especially when I'm on a calorie crunch.

Today I read the menu and placed an order. The girl at the drive thru said that's not what she sign meant. I graduated grad school with a 3.8, but cant understand how to read or comprehend a chicken menu? This location has dirty entrance doors, a girl on the drive through with minimal comprehension, can the food be trusted? Not taking any chances. I drove from East Nadhville to Clarksville Hwy instead.
Don't come here after 10PM unless you want to wait 30+ minutes for a stoner to make a single street taco wrong.

Ask for a scoop of both black beans and pinto beans!  Once your bowl is complete, grab the smoked chipotle Tabasco from the condiment bar and add a couple shakes!  Final step... Grub down!

I LOVE Chipotle!  My favorite thing to get is a burrito bowl...  brown rice, black and pinto beans, steak, hot salsa, sour cream, and cheese!  
Beware- if you are sensitive to spicy foods, you won't want the hot salsa (it's the red one that is smooth and blended)!  I LOVE IT as I love love love spicy foods!  Though my stomach doesn't always appreciate the torture :-(
They have an assembly-line type setup where you go down the line and tell them what you would like (think subway for burritos).  

The steak at this location was WAY overcooked.  I was tough like jerky.  Most of the locations I have been to cook the steak to a nice medium rare.  And... you can always ask for extra scoops of anything while you are going down the line.  PILE IT HIGH and you'll have lunch for tomorrow!  The only ingredient that they charge for extra scoops is the meat (and they also charge for guac).  Sometimes you get employees who are suuuuper stingy about giving you extra scoops of anything.  That was the case at this location.  My husband always asks for extra rice, and the employee was getting annoyed with him.  
Then... as we were eating, someone started cleaning the grill.  It was making this god-awful screeching noise like nails on a chalkboard.  The other family in the restaurant had the same expression as we did.  It was just so inconsiderate and made for a bad dining experience.  I am always the first to say something, so I went and asked him if he could do that after people had left.  His response was that he had to close the store.  It was not even 9:30pm, and they close at 10pm!  So he turned around and continued.  The rest of the employees just laughed.  It seems to me that they need some training in good customer service practices.  :-(

It is so true that the quality of customer service can really make or break a dining experience.

They don't even know how to make a whopper. Went through the drive thru and ordered 2 whoppers, 4 hamburgers, 2 cheeseburgers. Not a single sandwich had any ketchup like they're supposed to. Whoppers didn't have cheese (like I asked for) and they didn't put any onions on them like they are supposed to have. This dinner was a boring bust. And no receipt...image that. What a terrible team of workers.

This KFC is next to BK and Black Bear Diner (across from Wienerschnitzel in Sparks). I used to eat Kentucky Fried Chicken as a kid and really liked it.  I'm still down with greasy fried chicken but damn I want that Popeye's back!!  Popeye's in the Western Addition neighborhood SF got me stuck on that greasy ass (terrible for you, but damn soooo good) flava.  KFC, judging by this Sparks location, and the "new" style chicken....oh man....my stomach just lurched up into my throat by the thought of it.

I mainly went there because Jr. Thirst wanted to go.  Sometimes eight-year-olds (he was @ the time anyway) can make surprisingly good calls on dining choices.  umm not UURRP this time by the way.  Holy God and Baby Jesus, dare I continue?

There was some kind of "bonus deal" (more on this later) that involved a literal ass-load of chicken.  Like a whole extra box o-chicken along with your regular 8 piece, or 10 piece, or whatnot.  OK you got me pricewise.  Gimme the usual sides of the biscuits and the cole slaw, and we're good.

Biscuits are dry but taste OK, like always.  That butter that comes in the nonrefrigerated packs is like chemical warfare material BTW.   ICK!  dip those mothers in the gravy or something, but don't use that warm, nonperishable butter substitute shit, whatever you do.

Cole slaw--I freaking LOVE KFC cole slaw.  Even after the upcoming (UURP) experience, I would still gladly eat any amount of KFC cole slaw.  It's always the same with the sweetness and that fake mayo sauce or whatever it is.  I wouldn't try to compare it to anybody's homemade stuff (or anything else fresh), but it's crunchy and juicy, and makes a great side.  Plus, after the next (EURGHH) experience, cole slaw will probably be the only thing I'll ever eat at any KFC again.  Period.

Long story short = ate about four good-sized pieces of chicken.  I eat a lot, so not a big deal for me usually.   Also had a couple small portions of the sides, I hadn't eaten all day...should be just what I needed.  I had eaten at this one before, actually I had taken it to go about three times, your typical fast food mediocrity, but pretty satisfying.  Last time (which was probably late 2008 or so) was a very different matter.

Before we even left the restaurant (20 minutes after the initial chicken ingestion) I started to feel dizzy and not right with the world.  Let's see...I'm sober (at that moment) and  with no major health issues, according to my doctor.  The only thing that I can compare the sensation to: about 20 years ago, after dating a girl who was vegetarian, I had been vegetarian for about six months after giving red meat up for about a year beforehand.  I visited my German grandma's house a day or two before Xmas, and she insisted on cooking  sausage from the German deli, despite me saying I was only going to have the potato and vegetable sides.  I ate about 2/3 of one veal-based, fatty sausage (which still tasted fresh and high-quality) and had to sit down.  I felt dizzy and slightly nauseated, retired to the guest room and passed out for over two hours.  To this day, I know that was my body not being used to producing the enzymes needed to break down meat (especially fatty meat).

But now I eat meat.  Lots of it.  Almost every meal contains meat.  And KFC still gave me that PHYSICALLY SICK feeling anyway.  At the time they were trying to promote some kind of "no trans fat" fad thing, so they might have been using a synthetic, weird shortening to deep fry their chicken.  Whatever the case, I had awful food-poisoning type symptoms (I'll spare the obvious details) for two days straight and felt like I was going to die.

Which brings me to the "bonus" chicken.  To steal a comparison that I heard years ago, getting "bonus" KFC chicken is like having a surgeon amputate a "bonus" limb when he goes in to remove a gangrenous infection.  sooo, if you're slow and still don't get it, WHY THE LIVING HELL WOULD I WANT LIKE 8 PIECES OF CHICKEN WHEN FOUR MADE ME FEEL LIKE I WAS GONNA CRAP AND VOMIT OUT MY ENTIRE DIGESTIVE SYSTEM??  It's like they're trying to get rid of that toxic crap.  I threw out all the chicken after getting home, but couldn't bring myself to dispose of the cole slaw.  After eating some slaw the next day and not getting a bad reaction, I can say it was definitely the chicken that  was to blame.

EECHHH, I can barely watch a KFC commercial even now.  And now they're promoting that supposedly heart-healthy "grilled" chicken.  

sorry KFC, some of us don't mind overly fatty foods, if they are done right, taste good, and don't make you sick.  Your attempt at prolonging my life(with that "no trans fat" grease you fried the chicken parts in) made me want to slit my wrists with a rusty razor, rather than undergo more gastric agony.  I can't bring myself to try any other chicken from y'all.  Just give me a lifetime supply of cole slaw, and I suppose I can call it even.

Update:  tried it again.  Very rude employees. Filthy place.  I'll never step foot in here again.  William is the only person working hard.   Sent my fries back, which are always great at other Del Tacos, and the asst. mngr was soo rude, when the timer went off, she left them in the fryer and gave me a dirty look, and William got them for me.  Where is the owner of this place???  Wow!!  Unbelievable. 

What the heck? I have been here a few times and they put so much cheese on the tacos you have to rip some of the cheese out of it. Yuck.  Today I go for the chicken tacos fries and a drink, when I opened the taco it was a disaster, so glad I was not eating it in my car it would've been everywhere. So sloppy why?   I open the second taco which I have a photo of it, and most of the chicken was very dark almost burned, inedible, so I took it up to the front and I was given a dirty look, and then she made me another one , it looked just as messy,  I could even finish it that's how messy it was who does that?   Definitely won't come back to this place now.   I love Del taco or not after today.   Take no pride in what you make??? The place inside was very filthy, all the employees or not that busy and they were all standing around talking and carrying on, all the front of the house looked horrible.  Unsanitary.    Take some pride.   Very disappointed

Worst service ever I ordered a two for six and got charged for a whopper meal and then the three piece tenders that I ordered I total I was charged $6.89 whopper meal (only got the whopper) and then $3.99 for my tenders that should have both cost me six dollars... 
I should have checked the receipt before I left I know but when I went back the manager wanted to "void" out the previous bill and "recharge" me for the correct amount... I didn't trust her shady a** ain't getting me twice! 
She acted like it was my fault for ordering something off the menu even when the guy who took my order was telling her it was an honest mistake she threw shade at me... not cool Ashleigh...
Also the fries where cold...

A few months ago I stopped at KFC to order and was told there was no cook there so no chicken was available, ok I understand..... Yesterday I tried again and was told that they were out of chicken but if I waited in the parking lot for 35 minutes it would be ready, I get back in line 40 minutes later and was told that they had not started the extra crispy chicken... said 40 more minutes, I'm finding that this is a very poorly run franchise and corporate needs to step in.

The worst Burger King known to man. We haven't been here for months after the time that all the employees were playing on the roof and it looked like they had a food fight in the kitchen. Yes we walked out. We gave them the benefit of the doubt and tried again. Bad mistake. We sat in the drive through for 20 minutes with only 2 cars in line. We finally placed our order and made it through. We got home and found the order was wrong again. Ordered a Texas whopper, impossible whopper a medium and large onion ring. We got a regular whopper, impossible whopper and 2 small onion rings, all old and cold by the way. We will never give this Burger King the benefit of the doubt again. Never going back!!!!! Should get 0 stars!

Yes showed up there at 8:30 at night to get a five dollar fill up Chicken potpie was told they're all out go to order two chicken Littles I was told they're all out was told that they were frozen customer service area is not very good very unhappy with this store. Seen the shift supervisor or manager having a good all-time with another employee in the office asked to speak to her was too busy not a very good business I would give them zero stars not one.

Went to Burger King today for their heavily advertised 1 cent whopper by downloading their Ap at a McDonald's. Did all that, went to the Burger King just across the freeway to find their web site was closed, they weren't participating. They said to go to the 82nd st store, did that, they said their web site was closed, they weren't participating...HUGE disappointment! They had a corporate guy there who started to give me some gobbledegook about stores are owned by different people. What? Yeah, they did enough to make me not want to go back...sad day

This is the absolute worst KFC I have ever been to. The customer service is horrible. I don't think it's the employees, I think it's management. The fact that it takes almost 20 min to order and receive 2 chicken Little's is ridiculous. There was only one person in front of me in line. This is not fast food

This is the WORST Burger King. I live close by so it seems convenient, but every time I go there I am seriously inconvenienced. I have been here multiple times and have had multiple issues. The people are not very pleasant, they always seem like I am bothering them when I order. I drove up to the speaker to place my order and was put on "hold" for approximately 5 min, this is a very long time is a drive thru. The place was NOT busy, no other cars in the parking lot or drive thru so I doubt there was anybody inside. I placed my order anyways after waiting and then waited about 12 min for a simple burger... ridiculous. I have had to correct them multiple times in the same order because they would read my order wrong. I also received the wrong food on several occasions even after correcting them. My orders are not generally complicated, all I do is add and pay for cheese on a whopper or have them remove onions and pickles for my husbands burger. I have given this place chance after chance, the benefit of the doubt time after time and have been disappointed EVERY SINGLE time. Maybe they're just having a bad day, maybe they're short staffed or SOMETHING. Nope. I don't know what the problem is, poor management, poor training or poor hiring managers. Maybe all of the above. This place is not worth it. I drove up today and there was this confusing note on the speaker about driving up and going in for multiple orders. I left and went to the one on 9th - completely out of my way. But the BEST decision. I placed my order with a WORKING speaker, drove up, paid, and received my CORRECT order all in under 3 min. I was so excited and pissed that I had been wasting my time at the this crappy location. I will drive OUT OF MY WAY to avoid this location on 19 from now on.

First time trying the $5 "fill up"  where do I begin? For starters, when I think of a bang for your buck its usually more than 4 and a half potatoe wedges and the chicken sandwich usually isn't smaller than the bun. Which is what this KFC provided. Also the chocolate cookie apparently escaped its baggie and attacked the inside of the box because as I opened it there was melted choclate on EVERYTHING. But the (small and cold) cookie was safely tucked into it's paper bag. Interesting.... What could've been a good rival to Wendys 4 for $4 can't even hold a flame to the red haired american sweetheart.

Del Taco is OK. Relatively cheap food and tastes pretty good...will satisfy the hunger. And this location does a decent job. They tend to be friendly and quick to wait on you.
I prefer to walk in rather than use the drive-thru as orders sometimes are exactly the way you ordered and I've found that to happen more in the drive-thru. Plus when you walk in, you can sometimes actually watch them make your order.

I went to this KFC about 3:30 today and ordered a 3 piece meal; two legs and a breast, extra crispy.  It came with a biscuit, mashed potatoes and gravy.  This was the best fried chicken I have had a long time.  The white meat was very juicy and the gravy was seasoned just right.  I'll be coming back.  The server was very nice.

Ordered via DoorDash and was absolutely appalled at the quality of food delivered. Burrito bowls were half filled, ice cold, and no chicken in our half steak / half chicken bowl. Bad enough to make us stop coming to this establishment.
So slow. Not fast food at all. I would have leaft the drive thru if i hadn't been blocked in. I feel like i could've made tacos at home faster. I will hit Taco Bell next time.

Wish there is a 0 rating, this is the WORST EVER KFC, they never have chicken ! You can go 5 miles further in any direction to get chicken, and be back home and eating before these guys take your order!
Stopped by to order an 8 piece original dark meat and they had no original chicken ready. Would be a 16 minute wait. Really?

What the heck? I have been here a few times and they put so much cheese on the tacos you have to rip some of the cheese out of it. Yuck.  Today I go for the chicken tacos,  fries,  and a drink, when I opened the taco it was a disaster, so glad I was not eating it in my car it would've been everywhere. So sloppy why?
