I open the second taco which I have a photo of it, and most of the chicken was very dark almost burned, inedible, so I took it up to the front and I was given a dirty look, and then she made me another one , it looked just as messy,  I could even finish it that's how messy it was who does that?   Definitely won't come back to this place now.   I love Del taco or not after today.   Take no pride in what you make??? The place inside was very filthy, all the employees or not that busy and they were all standing around talking and carrying on, all the front of the house looked horrible.  Unsanitary.    Take some pride.   Very disappointed.

While visiting family in Plant City Where there are NO vegan restaurants, I decided to try the Impossible Burger. Though I am not a Hardcore Vegan, I am Plant Based, and The Impossible Whopper Was Not... I ordered it to be separately cooked, no cheese and no mayonnaise, my request was in vain. Burger was cooked exactly as my sister's traditional Whopper and the Bun definitely Was not Vegan.... Good Try BK, but I'll continue preparing my meals unless Truly Vegan Restaurants are an option.
Sooooo good! I got a steak burrito and I can't wait to go back. The employees were very fast and friendly. My only complaint is that the restaurant was dirty.

Well that was the worst experience I've had at this location... I ordered a spicy chicken jack quesadilla and a chicken soft taco... Received a quesadilla with no chicken but got my chicken soft taco. I was given no receipt and guy at drive thru practically threw hot sauce packets at me.

This is the absolute worst KFC I have ever been to. The customer service is horrible. I don't think it's the employees, I think it's management. The fact that it takes almost 20 min to order and receive 2 chicken Little's is ridiculous. There was only one person in front of me in line. This is not fast food

Ordered the steak salad and the steak was so tough and chewy that I had to spit it out. I cant understand how you can charge 8 something for that and cant eat it. The portions are small on the salads also. There other items are always fresh and tasty, but I wont be rushing back there, too many other places to eat that are better..

This is wordy but deserved observation. You normally know what to expect when you arrive after Midnight at your favorite White Castle. Cars filled with various personalities of folks who are hungry, loyal slider fans coming from God knows where. I had the good fortune of pulling up behind 4 cars full of patrons who I immediately knew were still having a good time; no different than most Saturday late nights. Hoping for a quick, uneventful trip, but prepared for, loud language, maybe a bottle tossed if the patrons ahead became upset or even a drive off I sat surprised as cars pulled through quickly and orderly.

This visit found EVE staffing the drive-up with authority and respect for each customer while making slight, almost unnoticeable, distinctions for each customer.  She performed her job with efficiencies of an assembly line with an occasional Mom undertone sneaking out.  Overhearing the mumbled, confusing orders I then heard a pleasant, FIRM, voice exhibiting respect and a politeness confirming each person's order. Even a couple of the entertaining cars were surprised Eve understood the order by their colorful comments. No delays or confrontations as each car made their way through the line. As I approached the window she smiled, announced my order with no signs of distress and carried on completing the transaction. 

Realizing a quick THANK YOU was all the time she'd probably allow with the line behind me, I knew the next morning a call to her Manager was in order. And yes, it did happen! TONIGHT I was NOT served by an employee resembling "night of the living dead", "wish I wasn't here tonight" or "focused on the conversation with another coworker" but a genuine, caring and observant individual with a work ethic second to none. What an unexpected and pleasant observation I experienced tonight. White Castle, you have a true GEM here!
biscuit and Mac n cheese are tasty, quality of chicken is not consistent. Popcorn nuggets are bomb as always tho

Picture this: It's dinner rush hour and only one lady is working ("working") the burrito line while the customer line is so long it looks like it's chasing its own tail. Another guy is in the back chopping steak having just a lackadaisical good time, doing his thang--oh, you know--and some teenager who was no doubt totally sober (like, definitely sober), doing something, or pretending to do something, with the soda machine. I was fifth in line when the flood began and it took 40 minutes to get my meal. 40 minutes! I can't imagine how long it took the 20th person, or the 40th. Very likely they're still there, and wishing as I was wishing, hoping as I was hoping, praying as I was praying, to God above, that more soon than late a flaming comet screaming through space somehow collides into earth, pulverizes everyone in an instant, and ends, for once and all, the fantastic, purgatorial nightmare of those enduring the unbearable hellscape known only at this Chipotle.

I really seldom do fast food. With moving & the holidays, I have done more fast food in a few months, than in my entire life.
I had a BOGO whopper, so I had dinner for 2 nights on the cheap. The initial whopper was better than I expected, keeping in mind it's fast food.
The second didn't nuke very well. Overall for a fast food burger, it was fine. I could have done with less bun, but hey, ya get what you pay for.
Drive thru service was good, courteous & thorough.
What's a girl to do? When certain times call for fast food, just go with the flow.

For a fast-food place, Chipotle rules. Good value for your dollar, and tasty food made fresh. They don't offer an exhaustive menu, but what they do, they do well. You can have your choice of 1) wrap, taco, salad, burrito, or bowl; 2)  chicken, beef, carnitas, veggie, or (one more--can't remember) as the protein, 3) mild, medium, hot, or corn salsa; and 4) everything else--guacamole (extra $), black beans, cilantro rice, lettuce, cheese, sour cream, etc. 

This location had decent parking (big shopping center) and the typical decor. The counter staff were friendly and professional. We ate our food in the restaurant, and we were in and out in under 20 minutes. They also can do to-go versions.

In short, if you're in a hurry, Chipotle is the way to go!

Late night i order through the drive thru 2 combos chicken tenders spicy and a large mashed potato. When i got home my mashed potato was missing, and my chicken tenders was regular non spicy, try calling the store but no answer drove back there and try to enter the front door and it was locked. So, I get back in the drive through and waited 10 minutes to let them know about my missing item it was really frustrating.

Living in the Santa Barbara area has taught me an abundant amount about appreciating chain restaurants. I love the consistency especially having moved so far from home. That said, though I appreciate the access to KFC as they're on Uber eats. I ordered an 8 piece bucket (4 original /4 extra crispy) and I'm not going to lie, my chicken was a little on the bland side. 

I think I'm use to KFC packing a bit more punch in the flavor department. Though a chicken restaurant I actually have to say that the accompanying potato wedges were the hi light to my meal. I plan to try this KFC again and will update y'all after I do!

I get the same quality and an amazing meal every time I come to Chipotle.

Before I moved to Alaska, I ate Chipotle weekly. Now, it's the first place I visit when I return to a location with a Chipotle. 

The menu is simple but the ingredients are flavorful. 

Salad, no dressing 
Pinto beans (sometimes black beans) 
Veggies
Carnitas
Pico or the green salsa...sometimes both. 
Hot salsa on the side
Cheese
Guac (never the sour cream)

Chips

Glass of ice water. (Lemons available upon request)

10 bucks!

Where else can you get such a stellar meal 10 dollars? 

And it's fast. Easy to take out or eat in. 

They have an app that will save your order and let you skip the line. 

I love this place. Miss it too!

2/2 visits they've been out of chicken. 

Terrible food quality. Terrible service. 

I can't even comprehend how this is tolerated in such a well known, successful company and brand.

Not uncommon for them to be out of the chicken pot pies.   Today a 20 min delay.   They give me all the other food while I wait for the pot pie. That way all the food can be cold when they bring it out to the drive thru.   I never expected much from the employees, but the manager should be smarter.   Wouldn't hurt to clean up the place.   Not the KFC of several year.   Update: place still sucks.  Out of grilled chicken.  Ordered extra crispy chicken, all white meat.  Over cooked dried out lousy tasting.    I'm done with this location.

20 Jan 2020 Monday: 
My autistic teen got an epic queso chicken burrito and wanted to share it thinking it's too big just for her. It has chicken on it but it had French fries on it also?!!!!and the liquid cheese so it is more spicy than I care for. 
I told her apparently we need to ALWAYS ask what's on something we haven't ordered before at Del Taco. I could only eat a couple bites. Not really yummy, just spicy and weird. And I LIKE flavors.

Fish taco was good. Value soft taco was its usual basic good self. Mild salsa was good.

And I'm apparently having a bad sensory processing day but the environment can and does affect the whole experience.
When we first walked in the music playing was choppy, etc., and was too loud. I was getting an almost instant headache. 
So I asked the employee and she turned it off. 
Thought it would be better. 

But then there was a horrible ringing sound and she said she hadn't noticed before now but asked another employee and she said it's their hvac system.

I'm glad I'm rarely in this area. I guess I'll probably have to give up on this location.
Busy busy but tables weren't clean either. We found a cleaner one than most.

My other teen arguing nonstop was the straw that broke the camel's back.
I had to pick up my value taco and eat in the car alone. Glad it's not too cold today.

10 months before Jan 20,2020: Being in line for 10 minutes and barely having the line move when there are 4 to 5 cars ahead of you is a complete fail. When the car got through the drive-through just as I was pulling up and then the line didn't move again until about the time I finally gave up.

My husband and I dined here on Thursday evening, it was late near 10 pm when I picked up my food through drive through. The chicken was dried out in the Famous Bowl and it was very sloppy, potatoes coming out of the sides and gravy in the bag. The chicken little was so hard I could not even bite it and the two chicken breast I ordered were also dried out. I tried to call the restaurant but nobody every answers the phone. My advice is I would not eat at this location late, the food is close to being tossed out and apparently they still serve it to guests.

How does a White Castle get four stars? It does when it surpasses your expectations. I'm an out of towner from the west coast. Where I live, the only access to White Castle is in microwaveable form. It's always been considered a snack, not high cuisine, but serviceable.

I decided to do a sampler of the cheese slider, the jalapeño slider, and the bacon cheese slider. The cheese slider is surprisingly close to its microwaveable counterpart, but thankfully is slightly better thanks to the crisp, fresh, pickle.

The bacon used in the bacon cheese slider is surprisingly high quality for the price (a little more than a buck). Often bacon lacks any texture or flavor at similarly priced chains. While the bacon wasn't crispy, it was thick and flavorful. 

The jalapeño was a surprise hit, delightfully spicy and an order of magnitude better than the original cheese slider.

The onion rings were solid, and the ranch dressing was accessible without me having to beg for it.

Compared to similarly priced chains, this White Castle location consistently exceeded expectations and provided a nice, light meal. The location was clean, quiet and fairly empty when I was there on a Monday afternoon. Staff was courteous and friendly.

This the worst drive thru experience that I have had in forever. After waiting to pick up my order for 10 minutes I was told that they were out of original recipe chicken breast meal I had ordered and that I could have crispy chicken breasts or wait until the product that I ordered was finished cooking. I was surprised that the lady that took my order 10 minutes before did not tell me that they were out and suggest another item at that time. When I questioned how they can be out of chicken breasts at 1:00 pm, I was told they didn't know what people were going to order. I expressed my displeasure to the young lady at the drive thru and the manager and mentioned that I usually went to a competitor and was told that I should leave the drive thru and go to that competitor.  No receipt was provided so I will look up the number for the corporate office to complain. A reminder that customers vote with their feet and I will not be back!

Worst chicken sandwich ever.  Dry and rubbery.  So much a couple of bites and I stopped and tossed it out

Thinking it must have been cooked hours or maybe days before and sitting.

I've had good ones here before but this was beyond terrible.   KFC should up the quality control

Yesterday I ordered six pieces of original fried chicken to go but there were only 5 pieces of crispy fried chicken. I found this problem today since I only ate two pieces of fried chicken last night, and I called them. They asked me to drive there to get another one. No kidding. Driving 20 minutes for only one fried chicken. I will never go to KFC again.

Pathetic excuse for a bean burrito and taco!! We ordered the 6 taco 6 bean burrito combo and it's ot good. I love del taco but they really let me down. Taco barely had meat a few strands of cheese , burrito pooped out one end all over the wrapper (all 6) I called to complain and was told that's how it comes unless you specify you want their "Del Taco"

Absolute garbage service. We arrive at drivethru at 9:38pm and they say they are closing in drive thru and only have chicken and nuggets available, no sides?? Closing time 10pm posted?? They also would only offer the tenders at full price when no sides available? And RUDE ABOUT ALL OF IT. This is quite appearently a management issue and lack of leadership. KFC at 314 Long Hollow Pike, Goodlettsville, TN 37072

Very disappointed. 

Went through the drive through and order a breakfast sandwich combo with a coffee. The girl at the drive through processed to dump the VERY hot coffee on my leg as she hands it to me. I scream in pain, and she was totally oblivious.

When I asked her to replace the coffee she had dumped on me and had burned my ankle, she had no idea what had even gone on. 

She also kept the receipt, otherwise I would have been glad to report this location to corporate.

Unless you enjoy minor burns in the morning and an oblivious staff, avoid this place at all costs.

Would give a zero star review if I could.

Unfortunately they have two whoppers for five dollars but they don't have the two impossible burgers for six dollars deal anymore. So I ordered one impossible burger for $6.51. It was well prepared very tasty. It's so tasty I kept checking if it was regular whopper. But it wasn't. How do I know? It was not greasy.
Cashier heard me well and I heard them well even though there was a lot of traffic. Very polite people and employees. Very busy crossing.

I always get the same order famous bowl with chicken on the side because it sucks up my gravy and makes chicken soggy once again a new one I went to said I can't get it on the side they don't have a container?? Ridiculous put it in what the others do a mashed potatoes container!!!

There have always been reasons for avoiding the Chipotle chain. It was subsidized by the McDonald's corporation, the mass e coli outbreaks that severely and rightly damaged their reputation and, now, these ridiculously priced "lifestyle bowls." Like many others, I stopped going there for a while. When I heard about the their new "Lifestyle Bowls," I decided to give it another go. Your regular bowl there costs roughly 6.50 to 7.50 bucks. These new menu items' prices are hidden to start with, (not listed on menu) and they are outrageously priced for what you get which you don't find out until they ring you up.  Example is the "Keto Bowl":  a small to moderate amount of lettuce, carnitas meat, red salsa, cheese (if you want it but no thanks from this paleo person) and a dollop of guacamole. Almost 11 bucks. Screw you, Chipotle.

Stopped in last night 8:18pm 08/22/2018 to have a quick dinner with my wife. Greeted at the counter with a snarky "You ready to order?". Ordered a Whopper with no cheese, no mayo and no lettuce. Ok was the reply and by the way we have no tomatoes. My wife ordered a chicken sandwich we both ordered medium fries and drinks. Waited about 10 minutes for order. No one inside but us. Got tray of food. Fries under cooked and greasy. Took back to counter and was told by same employee this is how they are, "Its the brand of french fry". I looked over at the fry station and seen a completely full (to the top) basket of fries sitting over the fryer. I asked for them to be remade and not to fill the basket that full as the grease cools down to quick and makes them greasy. Waited about 8 minutes and I hear a faint "Fries ready". I could not see the counter where the voice was coming from. I hear another "you want your fries?" At this point I'm over the the entire experience and want to ask for a manager, then she walks into visual contact with a tray raised and says your fries are done. Again no one in the waiting room. I walk up and get the tray. No apologies from the staff for the bad fries, making me wait or not bringing out the new fries. All high school kids that do not care about the job they have or any idea of customer service. After spending $17.19 on two very poor meals I want to call the owner/manager and let them know about my experience so they can use the feedback for better training. Mailbox full is the message. So I post to yelp so everyone knows to stay away unless you have money you are not fond of and want to throw out. Negative 1 Star

Used to really like coming here but the last time I was at the still Taco was the worst experience I've ever had at a Mexican drive thru.  

I ordered a churro, medium fries, couple tacos. Tacos were broken and a mess and my French fries were soggy limp and disgusting. They were so greasy I couldn't eat them. And the churro was still frozen inside, it was slightly warm on the outside but once I get in it went in the garbage. I was pissed and it was too late to complain because I already got home and had to get up for work in the morning so I didn't have time to deal with going back or calling and going through the hassle. The kid at the window had a lousy attitude, the greeting wasn't even a greeting it was basically asking for my payment turning away, running my card and handing it back. He shut the window and when my order came out finally, he basically shoved the bag at me and shut the window. I should have known to check my bag but I was tired after work. Sad.

We got a sheet of coupons in the mail so we thought we would give Burger King a try. We had a whopper meal for two, two whoppers, two small Fries and two small drinks.  The food did not disappoint.  The
Veggies were fresh and the burger was very tasty. The fries were still hot by the time we got them home. Food was a pleasant surprise and I can assure you we will return even without a coupon.
How does a chicken place run out of chicken?? This KFC told me they dont have enough for my order. Fyi I ordered 8 pieces.. Totally disgusted DO NOT PATRONIZE THIS PLACE!!!!

Very dirty and unorganized but the food here is still good. Service is hit or miss. This is the only KFC this far out so if I want good chicken this is where I go. They need to get a Popeyes out here to make them up their service and restaurant appearance.

UPDATE
So Taco Bell is gone and it's just KFC now. Plus they have a buffet. I thought 'Ok, I'll try it again.' Big mistake. 1. The prices for the buffet weren't posted. For us it was $9.19 per person. Other KFCs have buffet and they aren't as expensive. I guess that's the 'Carmel-Zionsville' price. 
Kids were $3.99 but the manager couldn't tell us the age limit and (I believe) he just made up a limit off the top of his head. Plus, he was so rude about it! 
The buffet was ok but there was not a lot of chicken on it. Lots of sides and desserts but about 6 pieces of original recipe and less of extra crispy. 
We won't be back to this location. My previous experience should have reminded me but no. I know better now. Never.Again.

This is one of my favorite Chipotle locations. It is easy to get into with a lot of parking, they are always well staffed and service is quick and efficient. It also has a bigger dining area than most of the other Chipotle's I have been in. 

My favorite is a Carnitas burrito with no beans, I sometimes skip the rice depending on my diet, green salsa, sour cream and cheese. Yummm. 

TIP: You can order a quesadilla at any Chipotle even though it is not on the menu. It consists of just meat and cheese unless you ask to have something added. I normally get salsa on the side to dip it in. 

This Chipotle will continue to be my go-to for a quick, easy dinner.

RUDE, DIRTY, and SLOW!!!

First they mess up my order at the drive-thru. I walk in to get my missing items. While I wait behind a man waiting to place his order, the man that comes to take his order was absolutely the rudest human being I've seen at a restaurant. He talked down to the man, interrupted him and then asked him why he was giving him attitude. That customer was incredibly patient and nice, had that been me I'd of lost it on that employee.  Then that same employee attends to me I tell him my issue, he takes my receipt gets my missing items throws me the bag. No apology or empathy. 

Oh did I mention that this same employee was filling a bucket a chicken and when he needed another bucket he grabbed one but another empty bucket fell to the floor. He grabbed the bucket from the dirty floor and placed back in the shelf with the clean buckets. 

If I could give this place negative stars I would. How can the owners of this franchise read all these reviews and do nothing!!!

My advice, never eat at this kfc....

You guys SUCK! Your food sucks, your attitudes suck, your Manager Corey has a shitty f'ng attitude! I'm a grub Hub driver and every freaking single time I go to this location, because I'm forced to, you're out of something or you're down a fryer. I arrive to your location and I'm notified that my order is ready for pick-up. Then I get in there and you tell me that you don't have extra crispy but offer me original recipe instead. I try calling my client right in front of you and instead of holding the chicken for me you sell it to the next person. All of your employees are hateful, ungrateful idiots!!!! It will be a cold day in hell before I ever set foot back in your stupid restaurant!!!!

This Chipotle needs help. They're always low on something every time I visit. Did they know that I was coming? *looks around* 

They are usually low on rice, beans, & steak. They work very slow & they are terrible at keeping the dining room stocked with napkins, sauces, & plasticware. The floor is a mess & the tables are never wiped down (at least when I'm there). I usually get half chicken & half steak on my salad bowl. The steak & chicken has been overcooked each time I visited. I definitely won't be returning to this location. 

If you have good customer service skills & can work in a fast-paced environment, please apply! Thanks. I'll appreciate chu. *in my DJ Khalid voice*

Avoid this place!!  The fries have sooo much salt on them that they seem to have been dunked in a vat of salt while wet from the grease in the fryers.  They have five times as much salt as they should and they are completely inedible.  I called, thinking that perhaps someone had spilled the salt, but I was assured that they had put that much salt on purpose.  I also tried the impossible burger and found it to be greasier than the one made from beef.

This hip happening hybrid is a joint KFC and Taco Bell.

I like Kentucky Grilled Chicken and the green beans that come with it. Really, I do. I'm a fan! But this particular location is a cesspool of weirdness and oddness and all around messed-uppedness. KFC, what's going on here, man? WHAT IS GOING ON!

Two expereinces in two weeks, both weird, both lunchtime.

1. I was told that the meals don't come with drinks, even though drinks are pictured with the meals and that's a pretty standard fast food practice. I was told they were out of grilled chicken. I ended up with green beans and water. Based on experience number two, I think they were messing with me. 

2. Second experience: Drink comes with a meal, (a. duh! b. what the hell?)  wait time is about 15 minutes of sitting in a KFC sitting area and watching a bunch of people sit around and wait for food. Full lobby, nobody is eating. 

So if you go there, you may or may not be able to get chicken, you may or may not get a drink with your meal, and you may or may not go to a fast food restaurant and have to wait 15 minutes for food they may or may not have. 

Why bother?

I called to place an order for a vegan bowl. The associate said that they do not make vegan bowls. I went with vegetarian bowl instead. The website clearly shows rice, beans, and sofritas with additional toppings.. and the option below that says "make it your own". We did not customize the order. When we picked up the order there was no beans and no sofritas. I called the store later to speak to the manager. A girl Rhiannon said that she was the manager and that she has no idea why the website is set up that way and it's not her fault that the website says that it comes with sofritas and beans, but that I have to make those choices. I informed her that it was evident that the standard bowl comes with the items listed and a customized bowl would not be pictured. I said I would like to have the veggie bowl remade but not at this moment. After going back-and-forth about the ingredients in a veggie bowl and what the website says, her interrupting me and dismissing every single suggestion I made, and talking over me, she said I needed to stop yelling at her and then I could call corporate. I said I know you guys monitor these calls and it's clearly obvious that I am not yelling. I said that I would call corporate instead. She said "and I'm the GENERAL manager Rhiannon" and she hung up on me. It's fast food... it's not hard to make..  it's not that hard to accommodate a customer when an associate under your management has made a mistake. And management requires a level of maturity that Rhiannon has not reached yet. A manager should not get sassy and talk over the customer at the slightest bit of inconvenience.

Tried it for the first time yesterday.  Had soft tacos with carnitas.  Meat was great.  They put rice on my tacos and it wasn't cooked all the way.  Also expected corn tortillas and they were flour.  My son got a steak burrito and the meat was gristly.

Great food and amazing service!!!! Such sweet people that work here. And I'm never disappointed with KFC's extra crispy fried chicken. Maybe they brought in new management or something because I can't understand previous reviews based on my wonderful experience. Just such sweet employees.

This quite possibly could be the worst "fast" food restaurant I have ever experienced in my life. And that in itself is saying A LOT. I really didn't think that this place could suck as bad as my previous experience(s), but I was proven wrong. I'm a baseball fan and this place...solid 3 strikes in a row. First time I thought, well it's lunch time and it's busy, places run out of ice, it happens, $5.00 burrito looked like a $2.00 burrito...I let it slide. 2nd time, after 15 minutes in the drive thru...when my turn was up to pay, I kept driving. Now the 3rd, you are probably thinking I am glutten for punishment. I am. I was hungry and from some cheap mex, Taco Bell was too far. Should have driven the extra 5 minutes since I waited in the drive thru for 13 minutes, only 4 cars in front of me!! It gets better. Dude gives me food after me sitting at the window there for a solid minute. A minute later I get my drink. I was speechless. As I am driving away, I realize there is no ice in my drink. It gets better, I do not dairy, I am allergic so I order everything with no cheese. So after all of this, when I finally get the chance to bite into my burrito, I unwrap it really quick just to check...Mofo'ers filled it with cheese!?! The only thing good were the fries. Only come here if you are playing the game "how bad can they F up my order" with your friends. Instead of taking my burrito back, I am giving it to my dog and writing this review. Worst EVER! Go check it out for yourself. #turndownthesuck #ithinkihearbanjos

My favorite Del Taco a huge disappointment today.
No one else in restaurant this morning.  I ordered a burrito  and it was ready  before I was even finished at the counter. It was barely even warm. No reason for this. Should have been prepared fresh but this was not fresh or hot! Wont be back here anytime soon.

What can I say? Sucks a dick. I paid $11 for a three piece chicken meal that apparently is supposed to fill me up, but I suppose I ordered the three piece embryo meal. I take shits bigger than the amount of food they gave me. Got my order wrong twice, and was hit on by a lady who only had three teeth.

I nornally don't go to MCD/BK unless I am going on a road trip to Miami or ATL. However, there were 2 items I was interested in trying.

This BK is one of the newer style ones from 2016. The restaurant was clean and the customer service was friendly. I got my order within 5 minutes. There is an electronic soda machine , but it was out of many flavors .

Impossible Whopper - This burger costs $5.50 (I believe a standard whopper is $4.30). It is made of soy and potato oil/sunflower oil. No meat is present at all. It has lettuce/pickles/tomato/ketchup like a standard whopper. It tastes pretty close to the whopper but not quite. I have to give Impossible food credit for that. The closest thing I feel  the taste is similar to is Boca Burgers from Publix/Walmart. It doesn't feel as substantial as a regular burger though. Nutrition wise, it is mostly the same with it being 30 calories less and 1 gram of sugar more and 1 gram of fat less or thereabouts. So overall, one shouldn't be eating this to eat healthy or lose weight. The carbon foot print /environment is the greatest benefactor.  I would recommend this for people who don't beef for religious reasons who are in a crunch with limited options.   3.25/5

BK Taco - I can't believe I'm saying this but this makes a Taco Bell standard taco look like Salma Hayek.  The taco shell is crisp but only at the top near the opening and the bottom is soft and shriveled up. The "beef" is some amorphous compound that is worse than TB beef. It has cheese and lettuce but is minimal. Not worth the $1.    2/5

Apparently you don't get want you order! Order two cheeseburger plain.  And a whopper jr and what do I get I get two cheeseburgers with everything on them and a bacon cheeseburger  not what I order but apparently they can't not hear want you order

This was the meal my husband got through the drive through at around 7:25 this evening.  Not only was the meal missing a full quota of chicken strips but I was given Mac and cheese.  Sorry sorry sorry.  It's too far to drive to go back but I hate the service at this location.  Here is my receipt so you can see the order was placed right.  Shame on you for not checking the order before it went out the window.  What a lack of attention.   SMH.

Col.Sanders would roll over if he ever visited this location. They have a sign that says " need cashier" they really need a lot more than that.. I waited for 5 minutes with no one coming to the front today and then we left. Often they say "we don't have any white meat or we are out of chicken right now". They seem to have different kids working there each week.  Not a good place to eat !!

After a couple of years I decided to try if she wants again. The new hot spicy chicken feeling of me and was ever ties well. The menu selection was minimal    And I decided to go for the two piece chicken combo which came with a biscuit coleslaw or mashed potatoes the chicken combo.
     The hot spicy chicken was only available in extra crispy, it was very oily and not very hot at all.  It add add a sickly sweet taste to it and was quite a disappointing. I felt a little ill after I ate two pieces of chicken and coleslaw but got better as the day went on. It will be at least another two years before I go back if that. I would like to give him the following morning if you order any of this chicken you going to get sick from it.

A restaurant should be graded on how well they mee the QSC criteria and not on the price of the food or the elegance of the building. That being said, I want to give credit where it is due.
I rarely eat at Hardee's except for the occasional quick breakfast; they do have good biscuits!
A fan of Shark Tank, I was intrigued that a startup company featured there- Bubbas boneless ribs - was inking a deal with Hardees/Carl Jr.s. So naturally I had to try their new baby back ribs burger. WoW! A carnivore's delight! This burger tasted just like something you'd cook on a backyard grill. It is the best marriage of pork and beef since the bacon cheeseburger was invented! One caveat: Hardee's seems to be good a "tricking" people into upgrading and have done so to me in the past. They got me again by asking if I wanted to try the new Monster Baby-Back Ribs burger. I agreed, thinking it to be the same sandwich I has seeen advertised. Apparently, there are two sizes of that burger and instead of saying Would you like the large or regular version, they cleverly worded the question. Hence the lack of a fifth star, The burger was still incredible but easily big enough for me to share with my wife.

I tell you, it doesn't matter whether it's morning, noon, or night, the entire crew is horribly slow. Pandemic or not, they've always been like this. 15 minutes for a simple order of an original chicken sandwich and a whopper from the 2 for $5 deal. Either the employees are slacking or the manager of this location doesn't know how to schedule people accordingly (including placing employees on call). Get it together!!!

We stopped by to grab a bucket of original chicken at the spur of the moment. Not a good decision.

The chicken was extremely dry and the breading was very greasy. I have not had good luck with two KFCs in a row. It might be time to move on from this chain. Plenty of other restaurants have better chicken.

This was actually one of the first Burger King's that made my food the right way and it was actually good! Most of the BK's in Pinellas have gone down quick. I just had a whopper here yesterday and it was fresh and hot and made perfectly. Good job! Hope it stays on the up and up!

This is your typical Chipotle franchise but if you haven't been to Chipotle before then you're also in for a treat if you like burritos, salad bowls and custom made to order bowls. They have a variety of meats like carnitas, steak, tofu, and chicken. My ultimate favorite is the steak and carnitas. The burritos are really big and choose what you like to put inside. The restaurant here is clean and there's a line during the work week at lunch, of course that's expected. The line isn't too long only about 5 - 10 minutes.

Horrible experience.  

For starters I ordered the impossible burger sandwich. I am vegan so I requested the burger to be cooked in the microwave instead of being charbroiled. The sandwich was pieced together so carelessly.  It was drenched in ketchup. So much to the point where the bottom bun felt like it had been dipped in water. That alone made the meal inedible. 


Most importantly none of the employees were wearing masks which is incredibly irresponsible and careless. 

The cashier in the drive thru seemed like obe a nice

Ah, Burger king... a lifetime ago
I would spend my lunch breaks here while "working" at walmart...I'd have the latest pearl jam cd in the walkman (remember those things?) and a greasy whopper in my hands.

Good times.

The interior of this joint has not changed at all since then. It's still just as grimey and just as dirty. 

This section of Franklin mills has been dead for years and yet this fine restaurant continues on... Good for them.

Must be those dag gone whoppers. 

No real point to this review (it's BK afterall)- I just felt like taking a trip down memory lane. 

Fries suck. 
Whoppers Good. 
restaurant Dirty. 

Typical.

Im not sure how every piece of chicken is both dry. Over greasy. Tastes mushy and nasty. All at the same time. I think it takes talent for a company to have such terrible tasting food across such a broad spectrum of ways to ruin it. Not only that they are twice as much as churches chicken, which is always good. I would recommend not wasting your money here.

I ate here on oct 4 2019  and got 2 whoppers with a free whopper . a few hours later i had the runs . I degrease my burgers with bounty paper towels  . I use to eat here around last year and the food was awesome . I will never eat here again . Just to be clear the next day i tried another BK and got the same 2 whoppers and no issues .

This place really sucks. Ordered 2 of their chicken bowls...all we got was mashed potatoes with very little corn and drop of gravy some cheese thrown about on top but where the heck was the chicken. There was no chicken. This is not the first time they have messed up my order. last time I told them and they got mad. this time I didn't notice till I got home. not worth the hassle they won't do anything about it.

My daughter and I just had their Impossible Burger....and OMG ITS AMAZING 
Honesty one of the best sandwiches I've ever had!
We went through the drive thru and our server was as nice as nice could be! Very friendly and polite!!!!
Trust me you have to try one!!

Unless you like Kentucky Fried Chicken to begin with, you should avoid them at all costs. As nasty as KFC dishes are, they have some mean advertising, because I return time and again, and never learn my lesson. Once you get past their roundabout menu, you'll see that their chicken isn't expensive, and that their flavors are caricatures of what proper fried chicken should be. Try their Honey BBQ or hot wings and taste what I mean.

Service here is fine, and used to be much, much worse.

Chipotle is my go to for a healthy, filling, and quick meal. And, considering it is healthier than other quick meals, we usually choose Chipotle when we are conscious about our diet. I load up on beans and toppings. This time, we ordered Chipotle to go to supplement our dish at home. 

I usually have enough Chipotle in one serving to have leftovers for the next day. I ask for a tortilla so that I can make my own wraps on the side. Even though they began charging $0.25 for a tortilla, I think it is still worth it to get their steaming hot tortilla to wrap my bowl of half chicken and half steak.

Love Chipotle. Despise this location with a passion. Come here all the time as its close to home, and tonight was the final straw. The one star review is strictly for this location and its service:

-On numerous occasions they are out of ingredients. Tonight they were out of steak, chicken and salsa. Honestly Management, I can understand a delay on cooking the meat but just not having salsa and then skimping on all the other sides as people are trying to compensate for no salsa - WTF? Get it together. I'll drive the distance to the 4th street location just so I know that I can get what I want in my bowl. They don't pull any of that "we are out" bull, there.
-they FINALLY took the chick off the burrito rolling station who would take at least 2 attempts to roll a burrito. Felt really bad for the guy in front of us last time, as every new tortilla attempt she made caused the slop inside to fall out more and more. 
-we always feel like there are employees or employees' friends in there hanging out, as they are constantly chit chatting and holding up the line. 

So sad, since every other Chipotle franchise rocks. This location is a pathetic representation of the company brand.

Ordered the $20 fill up meal with chicken tenders, got the 8 pc instead of what we ordered, not sure if it was original or extra crispy. It was very dark in color, way darker than I've ever gotten it before, was soggy and greasy. Would have definitely returned it, if we would have been able to. This is are regular location, never before gotten chicken like this before, very disappointed.

The Grant and Swan Chipotle is very disappointing. We've had past experiences of them forgetting to put meat in our burrito bowl and the carnitas is super fatty. 
When I am craving their guacamole and chips or the carnitas burrito bowl, I'll drive the extra distance to the Tanque Verde location, across from Target. We have never had any problems with food quality or service there.
This location should be shut down by the health department.  Raw greasy chicken.  Nasty staff (attitudes).

This KFC is very close to my work and convenient for a quick lunch when I have forgotten, or didn't have time to pack a lunch. It is not a full menu KFC, it is an KFC Express. Its' limited menu offers; wings, tenders and popcorn chicken. It also offers Chicken Little Sandwiches, salads and fries. Yes, I would like a full regular chicken dinner with their cole slaw & mashed potatoes, but this menu does the trick when I'm hungry. 

It's tucked inside the small food court located inside the Gateway Station downtown. At times, the lines can be long due to the arrival and departure of buses and trains, but the crew moves the lines quickly. My food has always been hot and fresh. The staff has a positive attitude, which can be a challenge when dealing with stressed or rushed travelers. 

Will I return? Yep, it's a good quick lunch when I need it. 

When: Numerous times.
Why: Work lunch
Their flagship whopper sucked ass. Dry, and obviously hours old... dont go here ever. Ps long time whopper fan. Just not from this shit hole

Meh.....One of the inevitable burger chains in W. Tampa offering standard BK menu & service.  I generally avoid chains except when rushed, traveling and hunger/rush trumps quality.    

Probably a Whopper is as good as most other standard burgers offered by the new wave of boutique burgers, but nearby you can get a really good and distinctive Cubana Frita burger with tangy chorizo and crunchy potato stick on top........and their secret sauce dares to include garlic.

See the reviews of the West Tampa Sandwich shop for example.

Wanted in drive thru line for 15 minutes last Tuesday night. If I could give it no stars I would. Not only were they "all out of steak" but they were so slow and rude. I have never waited in line that long in a drive thru let alone for Mexican food how hard could it be. The food was sloppy and thrown together. The cashier dropped half of my coins on the ground and said whoops. Smh...... Really wish it would have been better being that I live close to there and like del taco. What a bummer :(

First time and most likely the last time ordering here. Ordered 2 sofrita bowls (vegan friendly) which one bowl had beef mixed in, they were out of lettuce, they were out of lemons and chips were stale and tasted like they were at least a week old. Came back and asked for a refund. Food/Kitchen counters were messy. Employee had a face mask but was pulled under her chin (mouth and nose all exposed); either wear it or don't, looks tacky.

we rarely eat out anymore since COVID and my son wanted this tonight as his birthday dinner. So we splurged. 
The chicken was all burned and dried out. It tasted like burned oil. It had obviously been sitting under the lights for too long. The sides were great, but $30 worth of chicken was practically inedible  we were so disappointed.

I ordered and paid for a bowl with extra steak, rice, and sour cream via the mobile app. I got home to the skimpiest, tiniest bowl I've ever seen in my entire life. The portions are absolutely ridiculous and I see other yelp reviewers with the same complaint. Please see the attached photo, if you ever have order chipotle before you'd see that these portions are tiny, especially when paying for extra.

The employees were really sweet but the manager was a rude. And the food was not all that great and if you come, ask for a little bit of lettuce or else you'll immediately get a salad. The steak was not all that great. But the restaurant itself and the vibe of it was 10/10

Just went through the drive through. Drove home 5 minutes away. Double Dell : Stale bun, old meat. Chicken Soft taco was hard and old. These people need to be reported. My wife had already stopped going here and now I'm going to stop and tell everyone I know about how terrible the food is. It's a shame because It's usually good during the day.

Lunch drive through for Famous Star w/cheese combo. I usually visit this location for b'fast. I spoke to 2 friendly people on the squawk box. Must be training, which is great! I got my food fast. They didn't even need to ask me to pull forward.

I grabbed a fry to eat before pulling back onto Dorsett. Not a great first impression bite. I ate a couple more on my drive home. Still disappointing in their over-cooked (very brown), chewy and lukewarm state. I was most looking forward to hot & crispy fries. Boo

The burger was pretty good and tasty for a fast burger. I would order it again.

Current hours are 6a-2p.

Famous star number one combo made to perfection special  Super nice fresh hot fries excellent I could see from the drive-through them sanitising the tables very clean efficient, Nice and hot hamburger,,Thanks

We live nearby and I was just reading all of the really negative reviews of this KFC. We have been here numerous times and we have never had a bad experience. Not once. We have ordered to go (never ate in) inside and through the drive thru. Each time they got our orders correct and it was fairly quick to arrive in my hands. Staff have always been friendly and accommodating. The only thing I can say is they run out of unsweet tea quite a bit, or maybe they just do not make it frequently enough. The chicken has always been fresh. Cleanliness of staff has never been in question. It is an older KFC and you can tell when you walk in that it is definite need of a makeover. However, it has been clean when I have gone inside. Never been to the bathroom there so I cannot attest to that. Anyway, it is closed. Hopefully only for the time being with all of the health concerns happening.

The employee who took my order was nice and patient.  He helped us pick what made more sense for my family.  The food was tasty and typical of KFC, even late at night the chicken wasn't dry so I appreciate that!  However, when I went to the restroom to wash my hands, I witnessed an employee leave a stall and head straight out into the kitchen without washing his hands.  There was definitely an "All employees must wash their hands before resuming work" sign.  I would've said something, but I didn't want to embarrass the employee in front of his co-workers.

I've never had worse KFC in my life! First, the employee operating the drive-through was rude to us while someone in the background was yodeling and screaming incoherently. We were told that they were out of chicken and we would have to wait 7 minutes for it (the wait took over 20 minutes), and when the employee finally brought us the food, we asked if he was sure it was our order and he shrugged and said "I guess, I didn't look at it." and he left, leaving us to figure it out.

When we brought the food home, the chicken was sitting in water. I don't mean that it was wet, but there was .5" of standing water at the bottom of the bucket! The chicken tasted awful, and a couple of the pieces weren't even cooked all the way through. 

I hope this was a one-time problem, and the owners of this KFC assign an adult to supervise the staff. Otherwise, I won't be returning and I will pass the word along to my friends.
They have bulletproof windows. Yikes! Aside from that, I love KFC! The chicken thighs are so juicy!

This location used to be my go-to work I wanted a Whopper, but no more chances. The wonderful gentleman who used to work there is home and now the three times I've been back a particular female employee makes the experience uncomfortable and unpleasant. I'm assuming this person is a lead of some sort, as she barks orders at other employees and makes insinuate comments within earshot of the customers. Even the other customers are getting rude in this place. A random guy in line got way too into my business because he didn't understand a joke I was making with my husband. It's also impossible to get condiments at this location... I know the dumb policy that only ketchup comes with burgers so if I want BBQ, I have to pay a quarter. It's the policy and I try to make it easy...but they'll forget to ring it in and then get pissy when I ask after the fact. The aforementioned female employee threw some sauce at me and said not to "worry about it this time." I was holding cash for it in my hand, very obviously. Glad I didn't pay because the sauce was expired anyway.  Thank goodness for the location a bit north that has decent acting employees. I get my Whopper fix there now.

Do not eat here. Went last week for dinner. The staff just stared at me for a good five minutes while I waited at the register. A man and his daughter walked up to the counter with a bucket of chicken. A man named John (it said on his name tag he was a five star employee, what a joke) walked up to the man and his daughter. The man explained that his chicken was slimy and wet. All the chicken seemed to be in the bucket. I've worked at food places where people have eaten all the food and said it was bad for a refund, this was not the case. The man who purchased the chicken was told that it was fine and that they wouldn't give him a refund. They didn't even take a piece out to look at the texture. The man walked out unhappy. Finally after being waited on after seeing that disaster take place, I got my food which was extremely cold. The bathroom had urine all over the floor as well. This place needs a health inspector/new management. What a disgrace. Also John and the rest of the staff need to know how to treat people.
This place is the worst KFC in the world. I never believe how bad the chicken is. I never come back here.

I ordered online for the free whopper with a CH'King sandwich. Never received the whopper. Can't refund and redo an online order and store workers will tell you to take it or leave it. The the Spicy CH'King Deluxe I supposedly ordered was to come with lettuce tomato and sauce. I paid to add cheese and onion. Instead I received the plain sandwich that is $.60 cheaper without the lettuce and tomato and it instead it had pickles on it.  Additionally I ordered the large combo and it had the small fries which I did notice before I left and I received the how dare you question us looks before they eventually replaced with a half empty large container. Just to add to the humor of it all it appeared they must of ran of napkins at the drive in and were grabbing them out of the dispenser so I received a bundle of packing material at the top of my bag. Never go here. Very apathetic workforce. They absolutely do not care.

Horrible service and the line is ridiculous!!!
Right away, there were already a couple cars in line. Okay, whatever. Lol Then the next guy ordered. Then he moved land my turn. I had to wait. No prob! Then she took my order. And of course the grilled chicken was another 15 minute away already on top of the wait you were in. So I just asked for ALL crispy. I pulled up like an inch. Lol waited.... Waited... Waited... Nobody was moving!!! Not even one car. Lol So I got out of the line and went next door to Wendy's! I recommend them. Lol They were fast and friendly!

I ordered online for the fishers location, when I went to pick up they seemed confused and then started getting my order together. I was given the wrong kind of chicken and wrong sauces. When I got home I realized the fries were soggy and the chicken was inedible. The breading was wet and the chicken was tough. The biscuits were raw on the inside. This was the worst fast food I have ever had.

Nothing spectacular about this place...your typical KFC. Although I've previously said that I avoid fried food like the devil, ....college really breaks ya. I think KFC's colonel strips have been become my comfort food when I'm having a stressful time with school.

I always order the 6 piece colonel strips and the people working always give me like 8 strips. I think it's because I usually go around closing time...or the people working there are just nice. What I like is that a little bit more than halfway in, I get full and can save the remaining 2 or 3 strips for lunch the next day (I think it's because I chug root beer when I have KFC. Probably fills me up a lot more than the chicken)

The lady who's usually at the register when I go is nice and really gives me a LOT of hot sauce when I ask for it.

Anyways, I'm really thankful for this KFC location. It really feeds me when I'm feeling down or just craving KFC and cannot hold back.

This is a review for the chicken pot pie only.
I saw an advertisement for the chicken pot pie for $4. I decided to give it a try.
Save yourself the trip if you're curious. If you've had Banquet chicken pot pies, you know what this tastes like. It's about the size of two Banquet pies. And it's $4, versus what, $3 for two Banquet pot pies. I believe it's $6 normally.

Chicken Bowl, which I had for the first time, is very tasty,the people working here are very personable. 
 What made our visit unpleasant was all of the FLIES buzzing everywhere! I came in 15 years ago and it was a problem- STILL NOT FIXED?? It was actually amusing to look around and see every  diner eating their food with one hand and shooing the flies away with the other- there seemed to be one fly assigned to each table, and it didn't matter if they were window tables or in the middle of the room. Incidentally,these were very small, young adult flies- so that means they were recently hatched nearby, which is kind of gross wondering where the "nest" is.

I'd like to thank this Burger King for helping me with my weight loss goals. After one bite into the awful tasting Whopper and one soggy fry I realized that this food was both unhealthy and disgusting. I immediately threw the Whopper and fries out and went and got something healthier. Thanks Burger King!

We stopped by the drive thru to get my daughter chicken strips. We wanted three strips, no meal. They informed us that they no longer offer just strips. However, we had gotten them recently at a different KFC location. 

So instead we got the "Go Cup" with two strips and potato wedges. This was the worst $3 I have ever spent in my life. The potato wedges were stale. But the chicken was the worst chicken I have ever received from any KFC, or anywhere in general! They were so dry, I couldn't even break them apart with my hands. It was a similar consistency to jerky.

Avoid this KFC at all costs.

* I like the beef "Del Taco". Really really. Hard to beat. Yet I think I prefer the tater tots at Taco Johns as opposed to the fries here.
* Today I drank some Cherry Coke before eating the tacos, and then the meat didn't taste all that flavorful. Was it a change in recipe, or was it the phosphoric acid messing with my taste buds? Somebody help me out here. 
* Last week I had a strawberry lemonade, which seemed like a great deal, at only 1 dollar. In their haste, the server didn't mix up the muck of strawberry preserves at the bottom of the cup with the weak (not fresh squeezed) lemonade on top.
* My server offered a free replacement lemonade today, and it was an improvement. Yet I would not want to spend another dollar for it.
Yay! Sofritas!  And the pinto beans are now vegan.  A great place for quick fresh vegan meal on the run.

Went there today for lunch because BK had a huge line and KFC had no one in their drivethru line (should have been a clue). Ricardo (name on the receipt) was nice enough when I placed my order, but when I pulled away and looked in the bag I realized he had gotten the order wrong. I went back around and told him nicely that I had gotten the wrong order. He immediately gave me an attitude.  "NO! You ordered a #2!" I didn't even know what the #2 was. He then said "I  even asked you crispy or original" (which I thought he was asking what kind of chicken on the sandwiches. I rarely go here, how was I supposed to know that question was reserved ONLY for the #2 menu item?) He then said, "well come up and I'll fix it I GUESS". I told him never mind and drove off, I wasn't going to let the guy handle my food again. I went through BK's line anyway and got something else. My coworkers got a free lunch from me. I definitely will not be back to this location again, and I strongly advise anyone who reads this to go somewhere else. I also work in customer service so I get how crappy it can be but seriously? Fix the attitude dude, or maybe find a job where you don't have to work with people.

I don't usually review fast-food chain establishments but this place thrives on being subpar. I started visiting this location out of convenience because its proximity to my home. However, it looks like I need to restart braving the traffic on route 73 and go back to the location at the Promenade where the quality of food is a hundred times better.

The food at the Chipotle in Marlton is borderline garbage. Besides the chicken, some of the proteins look as if they are never replenished; especially the steak. I don't think they understand the meaning of aged steak. It most certainly is not steak that resembles something that has been refrigerated and reused daily, for over a week. I have visited this location and handful of times since its opening and the steak container is never more than a quarter full. I swear it is the same steak rotting away. As for the other ingredients, let's just say I have devoured four day old left overs that looked, and tasted fresher compared to the stuff served this at this location. Three straight trips of consistently bad food is enough for me.

As a DoorDash driver, I often come face-to-face with the plague of malingering, miserable, mindless low-wage workers that engulfs the fast food industry. At Chipotle, this plague has reached its apex. 
It is important to note that the duty of these workers is to put all mobile orders on the mobile order shelves. After the order I received was already 5 minutes late, I asked what was going on. I was informed that the order was still being made. After another ten minutes I peered behind the kitchen to see what was going on. Lo and behold, my order was there, hidden from my view. I asked this rude, intolerable, racist woman who worked there why my order was not on the shelf for me to pick up. She literally yells "F-- you and your DoorDash white boy" whilst being supported by her fellow sub-human cretins. I had to grab the order and run away for my own safety. This woman is the personification of society's wrongs (and what happens when anti white racism is pushed by the media). After ordering from this horrid location as a customer with my steak missing from my steak burrito I should have known I would encounter problems.

TLDR: DO NOT ORDER FROM THIS CHIPOTLE. YOU WILL NOT RECEIVE YOUR FOOD ON TIME AND THEY WILL EAT YOUR STEAK OUT OF YOUR STEAK BURRITO BOWL. IF A MANAGER IS READING THIS, FIRE YOUR ENTIRE RACIST MIND-SLAVE STAFF. Sh-tpotle would be a more appropriate name for this establishment.

This is the second time I have been to this location. The first time they messed my order up but they were more than happy to fix it. This morning, my order was messed up and the lady in front of me also had a messed up order. The are understaffed, having only two people isn't working for them at all. The bacon on my sandwich was disgustingly burnt but I didn't even want bacon, I ordered and paid for sausage. Hash browns were cold and the back only had 5 in it. Very disappointing.

I must say I am highly disgusted with this KFC and I will never come back to this one again ever. Back in the day it used to be good but over the years it has got worse and worse. I went in there today to grab some lunch on my way home. It was slow in there and the employees as well as the manager have to much free time on their hands. The woman took my order wasn't even paying attention when I walked in and one of the cooks had to alert her I was at the counter. She took my order paid etc. when she gave me my food I checked it per usual to make sure I had everything and that it was correct and fresh. The chicken strips were lukewarm and my wedges and biscuit were cold. Again the woman and the manager were engaged in conversation so I had to flag down the cook so he could come assist me. I let him know that my wedges were cold and that I would like fresh ones. He let me know it would be at least 5 mins(which was fine). When the woman came back to the counter I asked her if they had any fresh cookies because the one she gave me was hard as a rock. She checked around and was able to find more cookies, however that one was hard as a brick(which is completely unacceptable) to be given to me or any other customer for that matter. As I'm still waiting on my wedges, she begins wiping down the food trays. I guess my wedges had just came up because she immediately took my bag out the warmer(which I had to tell her to do so my strips wouldn't get cold while I was waiting) I stopped her and asked her to wash her hands before she touched my food. She seemed a little annoyed but I didn't care because that is just unsanitary,I mean really that is disgusting to be cleaning trays with a rag and your bare hands and then try to prepare a customer's food without washing your nasty hands first. Not to mention the woman and the manager were engaged in yet another conversation where she uses profanity and I'm sure you don't want your "employees" carrying on in this manner. Overall there is a lack of good customer service, cleanliness, and management at this establishment and I wouldn't recommend it to anyone. As a company you should put more effort into who you hire to run your stores because your managers(there was two in the store when i was there and neither one of them even came to talk to me about my food not being fresh or tried to rectify the situation)because it will always show poorly on your brand. Horrible horrible service and establishment.

Just left here. Had a horrible experience.  The guy who rung up my order had to be new. He knew nothing about the food at all. He kept sayinvd, i dont know. well can you ask somebody??? I ordered a chicken pot pie and was told 20 min. By that time i had 4 cars in front of me. I get to the window, pay for my order and realize i wasnt charged for the pot pie. so i ask and he says oh we can put in orders with more than a 5 min wait. Well why didnt you say that. I ask how long left on the pot pies he says 13 min. I wait a little go get back in line. get the mic at the drive thru and ordered my pot pie. He says 20 min. I said you just told me 13 min 15 min ago. Ask ti speak to a manager. by this time there are 5 cars in front of me. I get to the window. the allege manager comes and i explain to her what occured she like yea he never put it on. No apologies or an. So I ask how long and she says 20 Min. How so after all this time no one never put it on. done with this location.  This is closest to my house but I will drive to baltimore ave just to get better service and better food quality.

Food tasted fine. But they totally skimped on the order. Ordered extra steak and it was definitely not it. Other Chipotles have the correct proportions and this one was just off. Probably won't be back here. 3 stars only because the food was decent.

5 stars for little greasy slider burgers?  Why yes - And lets keep in perspective the kind of food class this place is. And for this kind place - its a 5!  If you like greasier type hamburgers in general. If you like onions on your burger. Then these sliders will be perfect for you. If these aren't your go-to tastes, you better go with the chicken rings or some other menu option.  

Sliders are fairly small in size. 4 or 5 will be equal to that of a regular burger.

What the heck, guess I should've read the reviews first.   There was no one in line in the drive-through and no one in the inside, I ordered a whopper Junior with cheese meal.  Heavy on the pickle.   It took for ever to get my sandwich and fries, I drove away and headed home.  The fries were very hot and I was very happy for that.   If my sandwich was just done, you would think the cheese would be melted and everything would be hot. No, my whopper Junior with cheese was cold as heck and there was no pickle.    This girl does not eat a cheeseburger without pickles.   I was already stuck in traffic and was not able to turn around, it was so unsatisfying so cold , and I wish I would've gone to McDonald's.    I rarely eat a lot of fast food, and this is my treat when I go get my nails done, and wow.   I used to work at  Burger King when I was a teenager and if we would've turned out cold burgers,  we would've been fired.   I ended up throwing half of it out the window as it was so cold and no pickles, cry cry cry.  Lol.  She even repeated my order twice, and even out the window, and there were no customers.  I have liked their chicken sandwiches when my husband picked them up and I do like their new fries which were hot.    Of coarse I will give them another chance as it is close by.  But I will go inside instead and not drive away and not check my order.   If they were swamped? That's another story but OK already.

They are the worst my back yard shares a fence to this shit hole I've been living behind it for 15 years it was good about 13 years ago you don't see the same faces for more than a month or two the chicken skin is rubbery it's greasy falls off the thing just horrible managers arguing with workers and customers my advice don't

Don't waste your money!! Today, I ordered dine in for the new stuffed carne asada quesadillas tacos combo. We waited 15 minutes, mind you, we were the only ones, 2 ppl, in the dining room. My husband finally went to the counter & was told they ran out of steak so they changed to chicken. So if we didn't ask we would have waited forever,probably. There was very little meat in the taco, nothing like the pics in the ads. This reminded me why we stayed away for years from del taco. But we thought w/ this new item, they could have improved.  But no. Big disappointment again. Not coming back.

Rude  employees ,  Nasty  Bathroom,  Flies and unclean  dining room.   I  shutter  to think of the foodservice  violations  that  are probably going on here .   Had  some   crispy chicke  in a $5 fill up.  Chicken was old  and dry .  Cookie was 1/2  burned .  Not recommend this location

Hello Folks, Been Going to Burger King Since the 70's, when a Whopper was 6 to 7 inches

wide.

We Went for the "New" Hot dogs last nite, and my wife asked that they not overcook

them, as our special needs son will not eat them if they are overcooked.

Both hot dogs are totally Black, burnt, and unedible... I have pics. Nobody would eat these.

She also asked the evening Mgr. Herman if we could have some Chili and cheese

on the side, and not on the hot dogs. He said, " I'm sorry we have nothing to put that in..."

Another hot dog container or a small cup perhaps? The hot dogs were plain and we thought

asking for some chili and cheese wasn't a big request... as it was paid for.

Looking forward to contacting the home office at BK to send them some pics, not like the 

ones on their "Commercials" on TV.  So Sad. 

We are not whiners, but we think Mr. Herman needs a new occupation...

Ed, Deb, Sean and Amy Moore
Land O Lakes
Nice friendly service, food was good and hot, had to wait a few minutes for chicken tenders, but it was worth the wait. Also tried the new donut and chicken tender meal, was really good.

Horrible- horrible- HORRIBLE customer service.  Rude is an understatement.  If I didn't have to give a "star" to post this rating, it would be zero stars, but their average is "one star", so the other reviewers  may have felt the same pressure to allocate "one star".
No popcorn chicken- "out of it" at speaker.
Ordered 6 piece bucket of chicken and one mashed potato bowl- $16.26!!!!!!
Waited in drive thru 11 minutes - one car ahead of me.  Finally get to the window.
I apologized for not ordering a regular mashed potato at the window, but nicely (and I'm being honest here- said please/smiled/ apologized for not ordering at the speaker) and tried to add a  mashed potato on at window- someone from the back yelled "you can't add on at the window" and in case I didn't hear that obnoxious voice, the cashier repeated it to me just as obnoxiously and adding- "there's a line"- so I looked in my rear view mirror- no cars all the way back to the speaker.  So I said "there's no one behind me", so she rolled her eyes at me, took my credit card, and closed the window, turned her back to me and I waited an additional 4 minutes.  If she didn't still have my credit card, I would have left.
She opens the window, hands me my order, my credit card and says "your receipt is in the bag".  I nicely asked her "who is your manager"- she responds- "all the info is on the receipt".  I ask a 2nd time not as nicely, but still politely  "who is your manager please- was it him shouting 'no orders taken at the window?'", and she repeats- "the receipt is in your bag".  The 3rd time I asked who the manager was, she literally huffed at me and said "Chris" or  was it "Kris"- I don't know because it isn't written in the receipt in my bag.  After all this time waiting at the window (4+ minutes),  there is still NO ONE behind me in the drive thru.  
I'm a nurse- not in the food service industry, but if someone asked me for something nicely, I would do my utmost best to honor that request- I guess the food industry (at least at this KFC) doesn't feel the same and can afford to lose business, and KFC has lost mine forever- at this location and any other.  Shame on you KFC, and shame on you manager "Chris" or "Kris" and your waitress Geannnnyyyy (because that name was on the receipt- spelled exactly this way) for your disrespect.  Colonel Sanders would be ashamed of you.

I can't speak about all the KFC's but this one is the worst went there Sunday night and was greeted by the dirtiest cashier I have ever seen ! He must have used his apron to wipe the floor , he was so busy looking at the girls coming in he didn't notice I was trying to pay for my order! They never have certain chicken parts and forget about getting more than 5 hot wings . I wrote to the company and am waiting for some kind of response! Someone needs to check this place out, there is definitely something wrong with management in this location

Haven't been to a KFC in Y E A R S and what a great surprise! This spot was fast and friendly service drive thru. Delicious spicy crispy chicken sandwich and the fries were so crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. Plenty of napkins and parking if you wanna eat inside.

After several visits to this store and encountering rude employees and issues with my orders, I think I am done with this Burger King. 
Tonight I stopped by this store and was greeted with "Order when ready." at the drive thru. Not the best customer service, but OK. I order a Diet Coke and a Whopper with cheese. I am informed after I reach the pick-up window that the Diet Coke is out of order. I am offered another drink, but decline. I am diabetic so diet is all I can drink. I just ask for a refund. Then the drive through employee offers to get me a Diet Coke from the front machine. Great! About halfway home, I take a sip and it tastes like soda water. I drive back, go inside and ask for a refund. To their credit they happily refunded my entire order...the burger was fine and I just wanted a refund for the soda that was not consumable. However, it was inconvenient and the straw that broke the camel's back.
Other issues have included rude employees...some who don't bother to say a word to you at the drive thru window, getting regular Coke when you order Diet, and having to wait several minutes for food. I have gone to the drive through at 6:30am and had nobody answer when it says they open at 6:00am. Don't bother messaging corporate...the messages go unanswered and there are nothing but complaints on the site about various stores. McDonald's across the street is always busier than Burger King...now I know why.

Chipotle is really great. It is. Their guac is always on par and their food is consistent and is good every time. However, EVERY TIME I order a salad in a takeout or doordash or Ubereats order, they 100% of the time forget to put the salad dressing in the bag, which is literally the only reason I go to chipotle because I'm obsessed with the vinaigrette. If you're thinking of ordering a salad, and it's takeout, just make sure you emphasize the dressing and ask them to double check.

I hated to be a "Karen" because I usually only share my true opinion here on yelp and I'm not one to "send food back" kind of thing, but after the third time (which happened a few months ago in Utah), I called and they sent me on a wild goose chase through door dash, chipotle customer service (which, by the way, only exists in the form of you submitting an "experience" online) and I never heard back. I know it sounds ridiculous, but I had had a horrible day, hadn't eaten all day, and wanted that salad and that dressing and they forgot it... I was so upset. And they never ever contacted me about it. Fast forward a few months, I was ready to forgive them, but similar situation happened and I didn't get the stupid dressing lol. Our power has gone out along with most of Nashville and surrounding areas, I missed a class an exam because of it, had to go to the vet, etc. I was exhausted. And once again they forgot the salad dressing!! Even when I order in the store and watch them make it, every single chipotle worker forgets to slide the vinaigrette container down the line. I feel like they need to rearrange the whole set up so that vinaigrette's are next to the cashier. Maybe I'm just dramatic because it's just a salad dressing, but if I wanted a bowl...... I would have ordered a bowl.... but I wanted the salad. Also both doordash and chipotle both claimed that it wasn't their fault and to contact the other company... but at least they replied this time!

But when the vinaigrette does come in the order, the steak salad is to die for.
Del Taco is my favorite and this location was gross. 4 meals and 4 different people. Everyone in our group said it was nasty. Definitely not going back again. The food taste was not right.

Ordered online for pickup at 7:40pm. We didn't our food until 8:15pm after standing in line for 25minutes. Got home, 2 chicken bowls got no meat on them after we asked for steak instead (out of chicken). Started eating our food and rice was NOT cooked all the way. Honestly was barely cooked. Beans were uneven. No mild salsa on any bowls. Just an overall lack of customer service and quality of food. Do not eat here.

Service here is always somewhat friendly which is more than you can ask for at a fast-food place. However, the chicken is not so fast...

This establishment has never figured out that their culinary purpose in my (yes, it's all about me) life is to actually *serve chicken* relatively quickly. Every time I've ever ordered, the response is always "Ummm. We're contemplating making some chicken now... can you wait 20 minutes?"

I've repeated this experiment about once a year, hoping against all odds that the story will change. Nope. Consistent. Every time.

DON'T ORDER FOR DELIVERY!!! Everytime we order through delivery they give us like half a bowl but this was the worst, we could literally see the bottom of the plates.Is it really that hard to serve a proper amount? is it really rocket sience? Unbelievable, never buying from these useless people that need to be held by the hand, I will just do it myself (rice, guac, steak, done you got yourself chipotle )

Whenever I get chipotle I typically always go to the meridian location and I always enjoy the environment and the staff is always friendly. I love the food and always felt that my meal was being handled properly and that the greens are cleaned. HOWEVER, last night my husband brought me home a steak bowl with lettuce from this location, and as I'm eating my meal, I find a VERY LARGE green bug in bowl. I immediately stopped eating it and showed my family members I was with, and then threw it away. I didn't even want to try and take it back for a new bowl because I was so disgusted. This isn't the first time I have ever had a bug in my food(not previously from chipotle), but this is the first time that there has been a very large bug in my food. Definitely something that should have been caught. It was very noticeable, and so gross!. I have zero intentions of ever visiting this location again, and am considering not attending a chipotle in general ever again. Definitely left a bad impression, which is unfortunate considering how much I loved chipotle.

Worst KFC I've ever been to
ALL pieces of chicken were dry as a bone. They had the appearance of sitting around all day. If I could've given this place a zero, I would have.
Even SIRI warned me that this Establishment only had 1 1/2 stars.
I usually don't review places, but this place sucked. The cashier didn't have any teeth and slobbered thru her sentences. Which in itself,
Was very un appetizing. DO NOT EAT AT THIS RESTAURANT

We ordered thru Yelp via Grub hub for our dinner order. We ordered the $20.00 fill up with Original recipe of thighs & legs!  
Ordering was easy to do. I used Yelp. 
Grub hub kept me informed of my order status. When it was ready I received your food is ready! 
Drove to the drive thru lane. Told the order taker I had ordered thru Grub hub. He said pull up to the window. I showed him my order confirmation on my phone I had received and he handed us a huge bag of food.  The food was plentiful, tasty, potatoes were warm to hot, 4 biscuits in a separate box  then we had 2 portions of  mashed taters with 2 gravies and of course cole slaw.  The chicken was great and tasty warm to be ready to dig into once we got it home. They included plates, utensils, napkins and butter sauce along with honey for the biscuits.
