The flour tortilla was stale and the edges broke off like pieces of chips. It was barely edible.  The soft taco was ordered with sour cream and of course they forgot that.  But they made sure to charge me for it. We will drive the extra 7 min to the other one for now on.
Ordered the Cheetos lovers meal and was given regular pop corn chicken without the Cheetos sauce. Tried calling and no one answered, not surprised.

I love chipotle so I going this location I got my usual pork carnitas which I love and my guacamole and chips it was very slow so I thought something might be wrong but everything was fine very friendly staff and my food was great. I got a Coke but it was flat so I tried the Sprite and it was flat then I asked manager, he checked and the co2 was low so he offered a refund which was nice so I give them 4 stars...

I haven't been to a Burger King in years..now I remember why the good looks better in the photos ..couldn't our fries because they were like a while shaker of salt was dumped on them and the whopper jrs where tasteless and the orders where wrong..I liked my grape Fanta zero but that was it...never agian

It's fast food. 

Location is a little slow, but in all honesty I am just so happy that more and more places are including vegan / vegetarian options on their menus. It wasn't that long ago that the ONLY fast food restaurant I could eat a meal at was Taco Bell, Port of Subs or Subway. The staff was also very nice. 

I have cooked with Beyond Beef myself, so I like it. Del Taco makes it a little too salty in their taco mix, but the Beyond Beef Taco (for vegetarians with cheese) and Beyond Beef Avocado Taco (for vegans) were still tasty (better than a been burrito at Taco Bell) and as I stated above - I AM JUST HAPPY THAT THEIR ARE MORE CHOICES FOR US!  In my opinion the Beyond Beef Avocado Taco (hard shell) was better. 

I'll definitely go back if I need a fast meal.

If you want a big, satisfying meal at a low price, Chipotle is your go-to spot.

Food: Chipotle serves huge portions and typically doesn't hold back on the toppings. Treat them nicely and they'll definitely make you a worthy meal. I usually prefer the chicken or sofritas salads as healthier options (and skip the cheese, sour cream and guacamole--using salsa as my dressing), while my husband usually goes big with a giant steak/chicken combo burrito. 

Drinks: Self-serve soda and refrigerated case of other beverages.

Atmosphere: The Eagle location is the lightest and brightest, with a separate room that tends to be a bit quieter during the busy times. Great and quiet outdoor seating as well. 

Service: The staff is generally nice. 

Healthy Hack: Go basic with a sofritas or chicken salad with black beans, fajita veggies and salsa. Plenty of flavor, low on cals.

Tips: Ask for sour cream and salsa on the side if you don't want a drenched burrito. Try salsa for salad dressing instead of the vinaigrette.

Great food, great prices, great staff! Also, the restaurant and restroom are kept clean. A plus in my book! :  ) My fave are the carnitas tacos and of course their chips with guac... even my 1 1/2 year old likes them! So much better than Taco Bell and much healthier too.

The worst dunkin ever! If I could give it 0 stars I would. Every time through the drive through, without fail, I am given the wrong item. If I ask for a sandwich on a croissant, it comes on a muffin. If I ask for turkey sausage, they put bacon. Today I got a burrito and it wasn't even wrapped. All of the contents were falling out into the bag. Also they never give napkins. AWFUL

This location is really dirty and dingy. The cashier was pleasant . I wanted to try the Nashville chicken. You can't just order the strips, it comes as a very small meal. The strips were very dry and not spicy at all. It's like they sprinkled black and cayenne pepper on at the end (a very lite sprinkle). The biscuit was great as always. Publix's  fried chicken is the way to go.
Used to be a 5. While the stock price is going up, the food quality is going down. Is this even steak, or just griddle and tendons in disguise? Ugh
Dont come here for fried chicken again. The chicken is greasy, over cooked. I paid $9 for the buffet and seem like they dont want to put anymore chciken out

A chipotle is a smoked jalapeno, a preparation born out of the necessity to preserve these peppers for the colder months. For me, dried chiles conjure up images of the American southwest and Mexico where, even today, you can find communities drying many of the different chili varieties in the streets or in homes. Chiles, like anchos, chile de arbol, guajillo, chipotles and many other varieties have been used for generations to create complex flavors in amazing dishes, like moles, salsas, and stews like Chile Colorado, dishes that are deeply rooted in the cultural foundations of these very people, "Grandma Food" aka comfort food.

In the US, chipotles have become synonymous with the American Southwest, and are a big part of our regular palate thanks to the wide spread popularity of salsa and other southwestern and Mexican flavors and cuisines. We can now find chipotle mayo, chipotle cream cheese spread, chipotle jack cheese, and many other products made using the sweet, smokey, spice of this delicious pepper.

So what does this have to do with Chipotle, the restaurant? Well not much. I wanted to find something else to write about because I just don't know what all the hype is about. I mean don't get me wrong, I'll eat here from time to time, but I just can't see going out of my way for it when the abuela around the corner at the taqueria is serving up delicious homemade food. It's just not for me.

I appreciate what they are doing by bringing sustainable and responsible eating to the masses, I applaud their attempts to be more inventive than other similar chains, and I like seeing our community support this concept. I also like that I can get a  burrito or bowl of meat, salsa, and veggies in short order and have a comfortable place to hang out with friends, but I'm just not a fan of the Americanized versions of Mexican classics like "barbacoa" or "carnitas" that I have to  drown in salsas because the spices and seasonings are just not enough. 

For me, Chipotle is a meal of convenience. I'll grab it if it's what's nearby and open, I'll go if that's where friends want to eat (but I'll fight tooth and nail and it's possible I'd leave to go get something and bring it back), or if it's delivered to me in the comfort of my own home. With such a strong and vibrant Mexican population in Reno, there's fantastic food all over and I will always go out of my way to get that first.
Had their chicken wings...the worst! Only spicy with lots of flour.. small wings and not crispy at all

This is by far the worst del taco ! Waiting 20 minutes with 2 people working one in drive thru packing orders taking them while another boy sits on his damn phone watching her struggle. Learn to manage your staff .

I Come Here More Than Once A Week It Is Always So Tasty And The Workers Are So Friendly-

I Always Get A Steak And Cheese Quesadilla And Its Always Good You Should Try It!!!!!!!!!

Ordered drive thru Nashville hot chicken littles and got regular. Instead of trading out and giving me what I ordered, they literally gave me a small bowl of sauce. What the what! So I have to wait u til I get hime to eat because I have to prepare the sandwich myself ? Lord Jesus.

Before the pandemic this Burger King was stellar, no complaints. Now, the Whoppers are always overcooked and cold. I am truly disappointed because I am one of those Americans that prefers the Whopper to a Big Mac any day. Heck, even Burger King's fries are superior to the golden Arch's. But not anymore.

Worst chicken I have ever eaten. Couldn't even finish it. So geasey and bad tasting. I also got 4 pc chicken strips not and my wife got sick. 1 strip was hard as a rock. Never will eat kfc again. This is not the first time it was bad but it was the worst and just had to say something.

I would never go back there and eat again. I was there for dinner Friday night. The third time going there out of Baked  Beans .  Chicken was served raw . Took the chicken up to the lady at the register and told them  the chicken was raw they just looked at it. Pick it up . Did not even  offered me any more chicken.  Very pour customer service .
It's a fast food chain. That being said I applaud them for keeping up with the times and getting this vegetarian to a fast food place in 20 years for the Impossible Burger.

We drive out of our way to eat at this location when we get the craving for KFC because they have a buffet. Back when KFC first started doing buffets they had a buffet attendant who kept everything nice and stocked but now it's just another added duty to the already busy workers there so you usually have to ask for items because they have no clue its empty. But for the price you cant beat it. They only do the buffet every day from 1030-2. They have both original and crispy chicken, mashed potatoes, fried okra, chicken and noodles, livers or gizzards I'm not sure and then the full salad bar.

The positives: 

I didn't get norovirus or e-coli after eating here. That's always a plus, right? Giving your customers possibly life threatening illnesses HAS to be really bad for business...

Build your burrito/taco/bowl your way with choice of meat, fillings and sauces.Their business model is successful and has been copied by everything from sandwiches to pizza.

The food tastes OK. It's good, not great.

As long as you stay away from flour tortillas, most of their menu is gluten free. That's a plus for my wife when we're looking for something fast and easy that could potentially be eaten on the go.

The negatives:

Portions - I don't like filler in my burritos so I ask for no rice, beans or anything besides meat in my burrito. At least one attentive person recognized that I was gonna get a twig-skinny carnitas burrito and offered to just charge me for a side of carnitas. I give that guy four stars.

Add-ons - two bucks for a side of chips and salsa. Mexican food is supposed to come with chips and salsa and you're not supposed to be charged for it. I think it's a law in some states. I can understand charging extra for guac, but that same two bucks is still a little steep for a tiny cup of the stuff.

Chipotle will do in a pinch and, if we're in a hurry, we'll hit it up, but there are a couple of other really good Mexican places close to me where I'd rather get my taco, burrito or chips and salsa fix.

I feel inclined to write a review because for a long time, I was weary of any fast food restaurant that makes tacos (ie. Taco bell).  But I have some friends who are die hard chipotle fans. I just figured, they don't know good tacos.

Now, let me preface first by saying, this is not a review on the best mexican food or the best tacos even.  

I give it 4 stars because a) tacos and burriotos are customizable, b) service is fast and friendly, c) decent guac and chips, d) taco meats are good.

If I wanted an awesome out of this world burrito or steak tacos, I'd go to Pura Vida in Nor Libs, but unfortunately it's out of our way now.  Chipotle on City Ave was a great alternative for a monday evening when I didn't feel like cooking and we were starving.

The restaurant itself inside was clean which is always a plus for a fast food chain.

This location puts the one in Port Richey to shame.  Food was fresh, not greasy, chicken wasn't falling apart and nasty and the Cole slaw and macaroni and cheese were fantastic!  This is the quality restaurant it should be. Great job guys!  Bucks well spent here.

A big thanks to Nicole and Crystal for making today my best trip to Chipotle ever. They provided comic relief during the busy lunch rush. They were very friendly and got us out the door fast. I've never laughed so hard while paying for my order. These two made my day. The food was hot and delicious. High five for sofritas!
Good, fresh, Mexican version of Subway!  The taco I got was super crispy and filled with all kinds of yummy.  I got a carnitas bowl and it lasted 3 meals!

So it's kind of like Izzo's but I would say quieter. I split one of those steak burritos with my teddy bear lol it was yummy. I don't know if it was better than Izzo's taste wise (equally tasty) but it was healthier food so... I pick this place over Izzo's. Those burritos had a lot less cheese and were less sloppy.

It's Burger King. Home of The Whopper.  Definitely not to confused with 3- 4- or 5- star dining, it's a place to grab a quick .50 soft serve to cool down in this miserable Florida heat and humidity.

Located in the outskirts of historic Ybor City, its' convenient location just off the highway give you the perfect reason to quench that junk-food craving. 

Now, get back in the car, already, and stop thinking about that Big Mac.

I am a huge fan of chipotle! I've been eating it for years since this chain is very popular back home in LA. It's a simple menu Mexican grill where you can choose from chicken, steak, barbacoa, carnitas, and tofu. Then you choose whether you want a burrito, burrito bowl, salad, tacos, or quesadilla. Then you go down the assembly line and tell them what toppings you want and pay at the register.

I always get the chicken burrito w/cilantro-lime rice, black beans, cheese, sour cream, guacamole and the medium salsa. My wife usually get the steak salad bowl with lettuce, black beans, corn, sour cream, guacamole, and medium salsa. Both fantastic!

We have never been disappointed by the food (it's always fresh) but the reason it's always fresh is because of the volume they do. If you go during a lunch rush be prepared to have a line out the door and bring someone with you who can snag some seats when they become available. I can't wait for their location on Veterans to open up soon!

My burrito bowl was made exactly the way I wanted it to be and the staff made it all with a smile. There was no steak but the staff nicely asked us if we wanted to wait 5 minutes for steak to be done. As soon as it was done, the staff called me back up to the counter to order my bowl. I was pleasantly surprised that the staff were so courteous!
I love the Chicken bowls!! Buuuuut the fountain drinks always taste watered down and nasty no matter what flavor i choose, other than that its ok.

staff is great but lines are long. we used to frequent this place a lot but especially during busy hours, their steak is severely under-cooked and shows a lot of red. I don't really like a lot of red in my meat so I will not be going here again. also is very pricey compared to Qdoba and chipotle does not allow you the same amount of options. guacamole also costs more money which I find ridiculous

Probably the worst KFC in Tennessee. Stopped to have dinner last night and ordered the buffet.
it was not stocked and not complete as two of the spaces on the buffet line were empty as well. The chicken was rubbery from sitting so long and the biscuits were hard as rocks. Add that to the shitty music and the incessant yelling by the staff in Spanish and it doesn't make for a great dining experience. I won't be back and if you are ever in LaVergne Tn. I would avoid this place at all costs.

Only giving 2 stars because of the meat in my vegan meal. At a teacher training right down the street. Went here for lunch 2 times in a week. Friendly and quick staff. However, I am a strict vegan and found a chunk of beef not once but both times in my sofrita salad!!!!!! Cross contamination? I noticed the sofritas are located at the far end of the service bar/station so not as if the meat was dripped from a passing spoon. Not sure how this happened but twice in one week? I wont order the sofritas again too much of a risk.

Wish I could go here on the daily, but having college student loans only allocates me to go once a week.

Fresh tasting chicken, steak, guacamole, tortillas, lettuce, corn, beans, etc. This place isn't authentic. Nothing beats greasy mexican carne asada burritos. But, eating Chipotle makes me feel good about myself. I guess that's what matters.

Last time.  Never again.  Ordered chicken breast and a thigh.  Got a breast and a wing.  I'm sure I got charged for a thigh.   Overcooked and crust was burnt.  Customer service - apathetic.
Look it up.
Was really enjoying my sofritas salad until I just pulled long hair out of it. It is now in the trashcan and I haven't eaten lunch not cool @chipotle not cool...

Seldomly do we eat fast food but we all wanted burgers. Stopped and ate inside Burger King.
I have to say, one of the worst experiences I've had. Service was beyond slow, other than 1 teenager working the front end and drive through at the same time.
Fries and chicken nuggets were burnt to krisps. My kids love fries and they wouldn't touch them.
My wife ordered the Rib sandwich. For $6.00 for the meal. I thought I bought a slider from White Castle way too small for the price.
I ordered a double whopper. Tasted like a whopper without the normal fixins! 
They were not even busy and still couldn't keep up the pace or do it correctly. Never again will we be visiting the AVON Burger King.

My last experience wasn't a good one. I will report the cashier who I will not name to corporate. I wanted a whopper veggie burger. How hard is that. Make the damn burger. Cashier asked me why I wanted a veggie burger. The answer is simple, because I'm payin for it. She said rudely "I didn't asked you that."  I was like whatever. Then she was like you don't eat meat, the burger has bacon on it. I didn't care. That's what I wanted. Black folks dees days hmmmmm....................?

This is in regards to the location on Seminole blvd and park blvd.

Worst food I have ever received!
After listening to the staff in the back making rude comments about customers returning because their order was incorrect for 15 min I finally received my order.

My whopper was a complete mess and prepared wrong and joined the nuggets we ordered under a warmer while they made one order of fries at a time. 

The manager Joseph did nothing more than work at the drive thru. Not checking to make sure orders are prepared correctly and cooked fully. 

This place is horrible and the prices do not match the quality. 

Adding a photo of my burger!

Your expectations lower when you find yourself at a place like this already, but they actually made me wish I got treated that well here.  I went inside because the drive through had a pretty good sized queue (should've turned away at this point). Moving through the line to order was deceptively quick as we spent 20+ minutes after that waiting for the chicken to be moved from the warmer into a box for us.  When order's behind ours were handed out before us, I asked about ours.  The person behind the counter commented to a coworker that he wasn't looking at the electronic order screen because it was confusing.. there was a huge sigh of disbelief from the hungry crowd of customers I was standing with.  We each had to hand back our receipts to get our orders filled from that point.

Somehow we got a bag of food and got home.  Of course, it was wrong.  We called the manager of the store to report the muffed order and he sounded like we were just one of the many he heard from that night.

I can be quoted as saying, "Never, ever again."

I love Del Taco. The building is the wierdest building around, but it makes me laugh every time I see it. What the heck was this thing before being a Del Taco? If anyone knows, PLEASE let us know.

I LOVE the Bacon Double Del. It is a good ole messy fast food hamburger. Everything sort of melts into itself and compliments eachother well. 

St. Louis has seen quite a few closings of Del Tacos the past 2 years. I am hoping this is not the next one on the list as whenever I am in the area, I stop here.
I took my daughter and her friend and we got a bug leg with the feelers on it and and a hair in a whopper! What the heck!!? Terrible service I am just outraged!

Sadly, it wasn't as good as the Chipotle's I've had in Boston. 

First, they were out of tortillas.  How can you be out? Thats the main thing you sell! Then, the taste was overwhelmingly lime. Yes, I know the rice is lime, but once topped with pico, thats all you could taste. The chicken had some nasty pieces in it and I had a huge chunk of pork fat on my carnitas plate.  

Besides the fact that their menu is limited and they dont have margaritas, I'm going to vote Felipe's holds King of burrito joints.

Oh Dunkin Donuts, you had me at donuts. 
The tale starts as always, I was hungry and had not yet had breakfast, and it is fast approaching 11am. Must find a place quickly before the breakfast menus are shut down! Lo and behold DD came to the rescue.  
From the moment I saw those two pink bubble letter D's that were this stores' door handles I knew I'd be ok.  I went in and bought their egg whites sandwich on flat bread.  Yes, I'm tryin' to eat healthier! well...sorta.  The flat bread is toasted and it comes with an egg white omelette  topped with provolone and turkey sausage.  It was pretty good!  That, along with a small original blend cup of dunkin donuts coffee was a great late breakfast.  I love DD coffee. Of course, then I craved something sweet, dessert!  I got 4 glazed munchkins for $1!  
I probably won't see DD again for a while, not unless a Krispy Kreme-ry woo's me and I succumb to its warm, sweet, gooey tenderness.

I've had some of the best burritos I've ever had here. Duh-li-cious. 

Chipotle is always a treat for me since I am a total fatty and get extra rice (but light sour cream- it balances right?) on my steak fajita. It's a guilty pleasure that I always am sinfully happy to indulge. I love the burritos and the workers are both friendly and efficient. The prices are quite reasonable, even with extras like guac or chips and drink. 

I also appreciate that the have lemons available at the drink bar so that I can add it to my diet coke. 

This is the best Chipotle I've been to and the only thing that would make it better would be easier driving/parking access. Whoever designed the parking complex should be kicked in the back of their knees. Seriously, who makes only one entrance on city line avenue going in but doesn't make one coming back out??
This place is always known for having good chicken at a good price sometimes things go little roughly but in the end they smooth it out.
Excellent customer service!  Had to wait for my steak to cook (they ran out) so it was free!

Upon entering a restaurant I expect all 5 senses to be catered to. I want to be visually stunned by a pristine dining area, tastefully decorated to reflect current standards. I want to hear as little noise as possible. I want to feel glossy wood and shiny brass and soft linen napkins. I want to smell delectable orders from the kitchen and on trays of food as they are passing by and eventually placed in front of me - then there is taste - the primary reas...OH! Cut the crap - basically I'm just hungry - and this is KFC...

I have always loved the taste of KFC original recipe chicken. I pick it over any fast food fried chicken on the market - even over my mom's fried chicken. Keep your crispy - keep your grilled - to me there is not a whole lot I enjoy more than this chicken, their mash potatoes and gravy and their coleslaw. Don't need a biscuit, don't need green beans or mac and cheese or any other side items they offer - don't want anything taking up space in my stomach that could be filled by these three - the trifecta of chowing down on chicken. Rarely is there a crumb left when I am through. Bones are picked cleaned. The bottom of the little Styrofoam cups are scrapped clean. That is the one bad thing about a spork - it want allow you to scoop out the remnants of mashed potatoes left in the bottom - it snags on the foam - glad I have fingers!

I will never forget when KFC introduced buffets into some of their restaurants - it was like I had won the lottery! "You mean I get to sit here and eat ALL the chicken potatoes and slaw I want?!" The next house I purchased I based my decision on the proximity to a KFC buffet - even named my first kid Colonel Sanders Taylor...she hates her name... When I go, I usually go by myself - my wife just doesn't have 4 hours to kill in a restaurant - something about having to work or something. If she does go she takes a pillow so she can nap in the booth beside me.

I have been eating KFC for years and have never tired of the taste of the original recipe. I love it when I go to a pot luck or church dinner and see that big stripe bucket on the table. I start there and work my way back - and I have only started two fights when I almost didn't get to it in time - 8 year olds can eat the broccoli casserole...

I know there are a lot of negative reviews for the Lavergne location, but I honestly have never had an issue the many times I have been here. It does seem most issues occur at the drive-thru, while I always eat inside and get the buffet. It always appears clean and the buffet is usually well stocked and clean. As noted earlier I rarely eat anything other than chicken, slaw and potatoes and they are always consistent. I have never had any issues with the staff, either. I used to manage a Popeye's Famous Fried Chicken in the early 80's - so I do give a little grace because I know how easy it is to run out of chicken. You base how much you cook on projections calculated from prior sales - it's not a perfect system, but otherwise you would waste a ton of chicken.
Freaking love chipotle to death. This location has the sofritas also! (New veggie protein option made with tofu) You can still get the plain veggie also.

I've recently started frequenting Chipotle now that they offer Sofritas, a delicious vegan option.  Every time my boyfriend and I go, the food is fresh and the employees are quick and friendly. I will never step into another Izzo's after eating here. Chipotle is 100x cleaner and the employees are happy to serve everyone.

I've only been going here the last couple months, but have been enjoying this Chipotle so far. The burritos aren't wrapped tightly and the amount of guacamole varies, but I'm really liking the salads. You can see from my photos that this chipotle hasn't been skimping. 

The chicken is my favorite so far and I hope to try carnitas soon. 

Did you know chipotle also has cilantro? It's under the counter, ask for it at the end.

I was a huge Baja Fresh fan but unfortunately it appears that all my local Baja Fresh locations are closed or closing for some unknown reason.  I went to this Chipotle location for my first visit to satisfy my faux Mex cravings.  The store was nicely decorated, clean and playing some good music a bit loudly.  I like the simplistic menu and streamlined ordering process.  We tried the steak tacos, chicken burrito and shredded beef burrito with an add on of chips and salsa.  The food portions were generous and tasty but a little salty.  The prices were reasonable and I would eat here again.  The staff was pleasant.  Wish they had a salsa bar and free chips like Baja Fresh and I wouldn't miss my old mainstay as much.

Vicinity: Somewhat clean,
Staff: Prompt and acurate,
Customers: Chill,
Bathroom: Fair,
Service: Pretty fast when i used to go,


To be honest, I haven't been to KFC in years because of all the growth hormones and grease in their chicken and foods. It tears my poor tummy up like crazy. But, when i did eat from them, the presentation of the food was always on point. No sloppy containers, no spills, no cold food.
Great food. Very good quality and value. Please bring back carnitas! Would go here a lot more often if they did. Recommended.

Why do I keep visiting this location? Because I keep hoping they'll get it right, and I can enjoy this delicious chain restaurant once more! It's so hard to find real food, that is fast and inexpensive, but every single time I visit this specific location, my order is very wrong. Sometimes even to the point where I get sick. Yes, sick. I am a vegetarian, and close to every time I order the Sofritas (YUM), I get chicken mixed in. VERY DISAPPOINTING. I haven't eaten meat in over a decade, and if my food so much as touches meat, I get very very ill for 24-48 hours. Thank you Willow Grove Chipotle for countless nights of what feels like food poisoning. Food is fantastic, this location is terrible.

After living abroad for months, all I wanted was a delicious burrito. Last week I went to Chipotle at the King of Prussia mall, and it was terrible. I was SO grumpy that my triumphant return to burritos was lackluster.

After a long day of running errands, my husband (who also was abroad for even longer, and had yet to have his first burrito) and I decided to stop at the Marlton Chipotle. Boy, we are glad we did.

We WENT for it. I'm usually a burrito bowl kinda girl, but not this day. Burrito. Sofritos. Sour Cream. Guac. All of it, in my belly. Staff was friendly and attentive, and they made me one heck of a burrito. We grabbed a bag of chips, and sat in silence for ten minutes as we devoured our burritos. 

They were delicious and it was a very satisfying experience! All I could keep saying was... I'm so happy we came here.
I love it here! If only I can eat here everyday, I would. I could never decide between chicken and steak so I get both and with guac, super yummy!!!

This location is absolutely the worst BK I have ever been to. Never have a good experience here. Will not be returning after this time. These pics are what is supposed to be whopper jr. Its incredibly disgusting and had to throw both away. I'm not sure what the person making these was thinking when making these but seriously it's more like a ketchup and mayo sand. Not to mention waited 15 min in drive thru for this crap. The manager needs to get it together and enforce rules for employees if he wants this location to stay alive but I dont know how you serve food this way and continue to stay in business. Hopefully they accept some advice. Dont waste your money here.

I waited at the menue board for 5 mi ntues till the worker waved me in towards the window. They had no grilled chicken so settled for something else. I asked for honey  twice. Once I got home, I discovered they left out the two biscuits. Very slow service to say the least!!

Change your mind NOW!!  You don't want this place under any situation! Waited over an hour for them to fill a drive through order because I have an i jury and couldn't walk in. , then when they finally brought it out after all that wait, AND calling them, watching everyone else leave they said they gave us 3 extra pieces of chicken and a cake!  Seriously!! They couldn't have sent someone out to the 2nd handicapped spot ( where they told us to wait) to let us know they were moving like snails! Customer service is NOT #1 here! It's not even # 100!!  No chicken is worth that kind of service!

Oh my goodness I am embarrassed to be reviewing a Burger King. However, we decided to go because of my son really wanted to try the Halloween whopper. Well surprise surprise it was unavailable (strangely the cashier could not give us reason why). We decided to stay anyways... Big mistake! After we ordered I looked around and realized that this restaurant is absolutely filthy. The prep areas are disgusting, the tables were not wiped down, the bathrooms were horrific, I could go on and on. My children's cups were corroded with some sort of sticky goo, so we passed on the drinks. The chicken fries that they are advertising everywhere were cold and chewy, they just threw our order on the dirty tray and fries were scattered about (so we could not eat them) they also messed up our order. The workers were rude and one said loudly "she have to wait!" to another worker when she saw me standing by the counter to fix our order. Worse than that the manager "Crystal" was on her personal cell phone the entire time we were there. It was horrifying we pretty much just walked out. I will not go back to that location... Or probably another Burger King again. SO GROSSED OUT!

Stopped in for some chicken tacos today had to remind the associate to put beans and rice on my three hardshell tacos.  Had the black beans on some dirty rice. Topped it off with some salsa. Overall the meal was flavorful, With fresh vegetables. Although the chicken was flavorful the texture was tough which these me to believe it was sitting for a while. I'm taking one star off for the chicken and the other star will be for the overall environment and employees are not very helpful. Seems every time I've gone in there which is three times and if I order a chicken taco or steak taco the employee I mediately skips everything else and goes directly to the meat and tries to get you through the vegetables they avoid the rice and beans not sure if that's protocol with chipotle but it's annoying. Then they always act put out when you say hang on can I get rice and beans. Acting put out can be defined as stopping rolling of eyes as the employee goes back to the rice.

Okay so I'm knocking out some chain fast food reviews for the area today! I don't frequent KFC (I try not to frequent fast food places but sometimes it's hard to pass up for a quick lunch when you're hungry) too much but every now and then I get a craving for it and as such I've been to this location about 5 times in the past year. Each time has been good. The Georgia Gold and the Nashville Hot, for fast food faux-interpretations of the dishes, are both very tasty. My most recent visit was for a $5 fill-up with the new Chicken Zinger. KFC's abroad have offered popular chicken sandwiches for many years but for some reason stateside they've never dipped into that until the recent mini-sandwiches so when I heard they had full sized crispy chicken sandwiches I gave them a stop. Fast food is not really that economical today but KFC's $5 fill up in this case really surprised me---a great full-sized sandwich with spice and crispiness, an order of potato wedges, a medium drink and a surprisingly good chocolate chip cookie. If I wasn't worried about calorie intake I'd stop in for one once a week! I've dined in once and grabbed drive through the other times and customer service at this location has always been good.

I come to many chipotles, and this one really disappointed. The carnitas looks very watery, and the rice very dry. The lettuce looks extremely wrinkly and sad. The worst thing was the portions. The lady gave me a half a scoop of rice, barely any carnitas, and a measly scoop of mild salsa. My friend got a bowl and it barely reached the brim of the bowl. It usually piles higher than the brim. We usually leave chipotle full and satisfied, but for today, we left hungry and frustrated.

Eek!  Me DEFINITELY thinks not.

The service was friendly enough, don't get me wrong.  They had a sweet southern charm that I appreciated.

The fact is, the food was SO SO bad!!!

My family and I were staying right across the parking lot at the Hilton, and we didn't want to pay $10 per person for the buffet, and we needed something quick.  So we figured dunkin donuts would have what we needed.

Not.
So.
Much.

I got their egg white/turkey sausage flatbread sandwich and a chocolate donut and some hash browns.  My husband got a breakfast wrap, and the kids got donuts and my daughter also got a bagel.

The "wrap" that my husband got was the size of his finger and barely edible.  My flatbread didn't taste like anything--no flavor to it whatsoever.  The actual bread was stale and hard :(  And the hasbrowns-BLECH!!!  They were wibbly and overseasoned.  So disgusting.

They also forgot cream cheese for my daughter's bagel, so my husband had to go back.

Never again.

KFC - despite PETA boycotting the place I still go there from time to time when I'm in the mood to work on the clogging of my arteries..... which is an art form in and of itself  :-)

KFC back in Texas is either different or just managed better, because I've been to two different KFC's in Reno and both - were really lacking.
In fact I've yet to find a great place to get fried chicken in Reno! When is Churches Chicken? Where is Popeye's? Where is Williams Chicken (aka Ghetto Chicken)

I see a KFC commercial on TV and they are making their newest "deal" look and sound like a dream come true...... only to find out its more of the same - arranged in a new fancy box, or container...... they should have just said NEW BOX - because its the SAME OLD CHICKEN!!

3 things at KFC that are consistently good - cole slaw - biscuits - mash potatoes with brown gravy. The mash potatoes are GOOD in that NASTY sort of instant way! Still I love them.

I think KFC needs to reinvent itself one more time! 

Good things I like about the place is parking is easy if you choose to go in..... the drive thru is quick.... its about $20 bucks for 2 people. 

The only REALLY bad thing I HATE about this place is when going through the drive thru...... its almost impossible for the passenger to see all of the menu.... that annoys me to no end.

If I could less then one-star I would! Came here 10/2/16 4:10 pm, the chicken when I arrived home was so dark brown on the outside the grease had to be bad the chicken was absolutely saturated in grease! By far the most disgusting chicken I've ever seen. The manager on staff that would allow chicken to be served like that should be terminated.

Gallatin has a buffet.  Its 7.79 for seniors incl.  a drink. Must get the drink. Pros.....mountains of mashed potatoes and cole slaw and fried chicken and biscuits, 2 kinds of gravy. Veggies like kernel corn, carrots and green beans. Had cabbage, peaches, jello, pudding.  Cons...  Didnt see any popcorn chicken or chicken tenders or potato wedges on buffet.   Cons....never have baked beans its always white beans or pinto beans. A nice brown sugary baked bean would be so much better with the cole slaw.  They wont put georgia gold chicken on the buffet or hand u a piece.!!!!!!!!  Ran out of the one dessert item they had and said a replacement would be 45 minutes. 3  1/2 stars.
This is what happens when you only have three people working at 12:30.  The chicken was not ready and the service was horrible   We ended up going to churches down the street.

I'd like to call this place...drumroll please... Kentucky Fried Abortions. Why? This has nothing to do with chicken treatment, but simply because I came here on my son's first birthday with a friend of mine and her son for lunch. We had the free chicken coupons and the girls behind the counter were discussing abortions and whether or not it was too late to get one. Are you f'ing kidding me!? Other than that, the food was good, place wasn't super clean and the chicks were then telling another customer about how Oprah bought the chicken like they're her bff. I guess if you consider the conversations of the cashiers it was a little amusing but I wasn't thrilled. Of course what was I expecting, it was a KFC. If all you care about is a decent bite to eat for a reasonable price, yea it's great. If you want a classier environment, take it to go and eat it at home.

I would eat here every day if I could.  The cilantro-lime rice really makes the meal.  I usually get the steak burrito with mild salsa, cheese, sour cream, and the rice.  Smoked chipotle Tabasco to top it off.  Great value for the amount they give you.

REALLY KFC??? I  usually drive to 38th Street for grilled chicken from KFC but was driving past and thought I  would give it a try.HUGE  mistake! Drive-thru was packed so I go in. Waited for someone to AT LEAST acknowledge me but after waiting and watching and  employee slurping down his drink staring at me at least 2 minutes I decided Boston Market would be a better choice! ! As I was leaving an employee was coming into the restaurant--- maybe from a smoke break, asked if I had been help.  After telling him NO he offered to help me but I declined and went across the street to Boston Market!!

Super close to St. Joseph's hospital.  Drive through attendant was prompt and thorough.  Food was packaged quickly and delivered to me without a wait at the window.    

My only complaint would be that my meal box was put in the paper bag on it's side, so all the grease from the chicken ran out of the box and all over the bag. (at least it wasn't all over my car)

When I was younger I loved going to KFC for their popcorn chicken and biscuits. But I have to agree with every bad review of this location. Every time I have come here they have been out of something or the food wasn't up to it's usual standard. The review about substituting items is true. Some employees will let you, most of them like to be jerks. Sadly, I never feel welcome here. Go down the block to Popeyes, or better yet, across the street to Caines.

They were out of burritos when I went opening night but the steak bowl was mmm...scrumptious. It's new so they are working out the kinks, the line was long and they were out of a few menu items I guess because it was so popular when it opened. I'm happy this is a new addition to the local area though.... consistently tasty food at a reasonable price.

It's been 3 years - $7 gets you a long way here.  The steak burrito with black beans, rice, veggies, salsa, cheese,  and romaine lettuce was decent.  I hear they source their meats carefully, I think everyone should do this. I'm not sure of the calorie count, but filled me for 8 hours, and I had to eat it in 2 shifts.  My new go to for quick, healthy, inexpensive eats.

OK...the food here is great, but the serving size is large. I would give them five stars if they also offered smaller servings and a price to match.

I typically get a burrito bowl, either grilled chicken or steak. It's just easier to eat than a tortilla packed with a lot of great stuff falling all over the place as you wipe off your face and hands over and over again. Some folks may like the tortilla, but for a quick, easy to eat lunch...I recommend the bowl.

Chipotle is my favorite fast food ATM! I always get a bowl with double chicken, steak, barbacoa, lettuce, cheese, brown rice, and pinto beans with a tortilla on the side and guac and chips and a blackberry izze and it is pure sex. I advise you try it out sometime! And this place usually has fast, good service with a friendly staff. The one in voorhees is good too, although i found hair in my food once or twice there. Will definitely be coming here for my weekly chipotle fix for as long as I can!

If I owned this business, I would be firing everyone that works there, including my manager.  First and foremost, every time I go there, they are out of something!!  EVERY TIME!!!  You are a chicken joint, for you to NOT have any chicken product you sell on a regular basis is an absolute joke.  

This might have something to do with the fact that, although the restaurant closes at 10pm, the employees are ready to go with chairs up and sign off at 8pm.  Either you are too lazy to fire the deep frier up, since you shut it down two hours early, or whoever does your ordering is incompetent.  Needless to say, I live a mile from you, but I drive six miles to the next closest KFC just to avoid the bad news that you don't have whatever I order available, consistently.

Just 1 star because they had chicken a couple times, but didn't have any chicken (only sliders) on my last, very last, visit. Say what!? Yup, a chicken place with no chicken and actually out of mash potatoes too. I believe the food went to the people there (notice I didn't say working). Lets just say they must have run out of Doritos and needed something else to suppress their appetite. Never again to this location.

Literally had the worst burrito here today. Couldn't finish it. All of it was cold and my rice had too much cilantro and lime and the rice was hard and undercooked. It was  soaked in sour cream and my steak was tough and burnt. I was so disappointed. I was really looking forward to my real burrito. They get two stars cause the service was good.

I was not happy with this visit to Dunkin.  I was on my way to the Airport when the husbeast and I stopped. 

I get it, they were busy, but they messed EVERYTHING up. I ordered a combo, and while I got a combo, it wasn't the one I wanted. I wanted a Bacon/egg/cheese on bagel, but I got a sausage/egg/cheese on a croissant. Not that it wasn't tasty, but if I'd wanted that, I would have ordered it.  The combo comes with hash rounds, and I can never eat them, because frankly they never feel/taste cooked through.  And my coffee.. *sigh*  No, just no. I'm not sure if it's because the ratio of their caramel to coffee was off or something else was, but it just wasn't satisfying. 

They missed my husband's order for a large coffee but at LEAST they got his order for his sandwich right.  He didn't want to waste any more time there, else we would have said something to the window.

Sadly, this place has been over-hyped.  i was expecting AMAZING, and all it was passable.  I would only give the food 2 stars (way too much cilantro in everything!) and too much sour cream, salsa, etc. slopped on.  But I added a star for two things.  one, I appreciate their commitment to quality ingredients.  Second, the employees are GREAT.  One of the managers came over to see how we were doing, saw I wasn't thrilled, and promptly subbed out my meal for something else.   Now I didn't love the new meal, but I was able to eat some of it - the original steak tacos, I couldn't even eat.  The cubed steak was seasoned unpleasantly to me, and had a weird texture.  But good customer service means a lot!

Friday lunch with my wife has become the norm. Consistent good mexican at a reasonable price. Has replaced all other local chains in our repertoire for lunch. Love the tacos and salads. Plenty of combinations. Barbacoa to the new Sofritas, with a little hot sauce or corn or peppers & onions always makes the salad or tacos a delight.

This store must be the red headed step child of the pepsi co family. While i waited in line for almost 10 minutes for food the 2 people that ordered before me had to wait at least another 5 minutes, with 1 gentleman who ordered a chicken pot pie was told it was a 20 minute wait. 
There seem to be plenty of workers but no real leadership guiding anyone. Come on KFC/A&W get your crap together and train these people correctly.

Tried this location since my previous one has been shut down. Went on a Tuesday evening, and they were out of half the stuff I wanted to order. They ran out of something else the guy in front of me ordered because someone at the window got the last of it instead. Then they ran out of one more thing I intended to order just before I got to the counter. I never even got any food. Employees obviously must withstand angry customers often, and don't seem to want to be there as indicated by their rudeness. The place was a dirty dump and parking is ridiculous. They didn't even bother to turn on their sign. Two other angry customers stomped out of the store cursing before I could even get to the counter, and one of them warned me about the terrible service and lack of food before they announced that they were out of chicken strips. He promptly left. I will never go to this store again, and I'm glad I never got any food from there. I would not have expected it to be good or even cooked properly. it seems like KFC on the whole (at least in Tucson) is far below what it used to be. Their prices are much higher and the size/quality of the food has decreased dramatically. If I want good fast food fried chicken in Tucson, I'll stick with Lucky Wishbone, Church's or Popeyes. Sad because KFC used to be the best. Totally at the bottom now.

Phyllis still works there, she is the most condescending rude person you would ever come across. White Castle should be ashamed to call this person a manager let alone employ them.

Yesterday 11/11/2017 I ordered 2 waffle slider breakfast sandwiches that were incorrect (came on a bun instead waffle). Today I went inside to get sandwiches and discuss that incorrect order with the manager on duty. I did not seek anything for free placed my order and paid for it (yep I forgot to check my order). Not knowing to whom I was initially speaking I simply suggested/asked her is it possible for White Castle to repeat "the made order" back to the customer at the drive through window to help insure correctness she said it is "not possible."

Phyllis, in a weird defense seemed to think it was I who ordered the product wrong by using the word SLIDER upon ordering instead of just saying only waffle breakfast sandwich. When I explained again that I ORDERED OVER THE SPEAKER WAFFLE SLIDER SAUSAGE EGG AND CHEESE BREAKFAST SANDWICHES she said the "bun slider" order was still correct. I then pointed out on her menu board it describes breakfast sandwiches as a BREAKFAST WAFFLE SLIDER. 

She said she made my order today the way I wanted therefore "making it right". How is it she made yesterday's order right by me coming inside to FORCE my order be made correct today??

https://www.whitecastle.com/food/menu/Sliders/breakfast-waffle-slider-s

I love Chipotle very good Tex mex cuisine. 

It is a very clean establishment. 

Food here is very tasty and staff are friendly! 

Unfortunately options are very limited- I dont even think they have queso. 

Chicken and steak are my favorite meats here!

Quick and easy food that is relatively healthy and not for a really good price. I always love going to chipotle. Excellent tex mex.

 I always get the burrito bowl and never have I been disappointed. 

#win

This is probably the worst of all the KFC locations. They are always out of product and I'm not even talking late at night before closing either. I tried to purchase their chicken pot pie at 12 noon on a Sunday and was told they were out.
Another occasion they had those little mini commercials on constantly so I decided to go and purchase a few. But upon ordering during a Sunday afternoon they were out of the buns for the mini's. can't see how this place stays in business.

I have been to this store several times. Generally not to often and with good reason. But stupid me keeps going back for lack of a good fried chicken places nearby. They are slooow, have to wait for a long time. Speed isn't their job. Also chicken is usually dried out, even for the dark meat. The chicken I got last night smelled of old grease that I don't think had been changed in awhile, also breading was dark also. Either from over cooking or dirty grease. I hope it would change but don't think it will. For as busy as they are I'm surprised. Wish there was another place that is inexpensive and good.

I'm absolutely addicted to Chipotle. I make a trip there at least once a week. My staple order: burrito bol (burrito without the tortilla), with rice, half steak half chicken, lettuce, and doused in hot sauce.

I've tried other burrito places such as QDoba and Moe's, but chipotle is fat superior to the others. Their rice is ridiculous yummy, with lime juice and cilantro, I swear they add crack to it! All the meat is hormone-free and delicious. When I want to switch it up, I will order crunchy tacos with barbacoa (spicy shredded beef). 

The staff at this location is very friendly and warm. Could be because they see me so freaking much. We just got our meal comped today because we are loyal patrons. Yes, very addicted. I could rave on and on about this, but you should just go in and give it a try. You'll love it.

You know that childhood memory you have of your favorite restaurants...!? Well, forget your recollection of KFC as a child. 

Stopped in for a to go lunch and ordered the fill up boxes, 1 chicken breast and 1 tenders. The tenders were thin and tough with very little meat. The potato/gravy was bland and lifeless also very little gravy. They also forgot to put the butter in the boxes for our biscuits. :( The food was cool by the time I got home (5 minutes drive.)

Staff did remind me to do the survey and get a free Gocup with purchase of a drink. Since I am moving this week I will more than likely stop by and buy a drink for my free lunch meal. Other than that, I doubt I will be back. Oh they got rid of Nashville Hot... total bummer. Was my favorite thing on the menu and Georgia mustard just isn't the same.

I've been to this location a few times.  A few months ago, it was a total shit show; the traditional Chipotle flavors were slightly off, they'd run out of stuff constantly, and they were sloooooooow.  Fast forward to the present and this Chipotle is operating at par with other Chipotles around the country.  My steak tacos with corn tortillas were phenomenal with juicy steak cooked to a perfect medium rare topped with flavorful salsas.  A supremely filling and satisfying meal for $7 and some change; the same meal is over $12 at Felipes and Felipes gives you less food.  

This location has a nice outdoor seating area which is a definite plus.  Since it's one of only 2 locations in the metro area it does get pretty packed, but it's definitely one of only a couple places worth mentioning in Elmwood.

In dire need of food, and no where else to turn before my Chiro visit, I stopped into KFC and got myself one of their $5 boxes. For $5 I got 3 chicken strips, a Mashed potatoes and gravy, a cookie and a drink. That's a pretty decent deal, if you ask me.  Unfortunately I wasn't terribly hungry, ate 1 of the strips, a few spoonfuls of the taters and threw the rest of it away.

I walked in this location at 10:55am (10 mins after opening) and was served undercooked rice, yesterday's guacamole (I can't imagine guac turns brown THAT fast,) the blandest tasting meat I've had (carnitas,) and a side of attitude from employees for pointing out that the soda machine wasn't quite working. Yikes! Won't be back to this location.

Okay I have gone here twice in the last few weeks and the first time they ran out of chicken. The second time, yes you guessed it..(they ran out of some chicken). Now what of kind restaurant runs out of the very thing that they sell? Whoever owns this restaurant must not be aware of the ordering process.. who knows? Ridiculous though. Like who is in charge of stocking the kitchen? DUH..(Maybe the staff is stealing it or eating it all).

This is Taco Bell on steroids. I absolutely love this place. Get the breakfast burrito, fish tacos, or quesadillas. Although each of these might have like 1000 calorites, also get the Cheesecake chimichangas. A party in your mouth. A great late night place.

Came here about 9:30 pm and there was a little line. It was perfect timing because right after at least 20 people came right after us.  They were a little slow at first but as soon as a couple workers came out to help it went by quickly.  Ordered the carnitas burrito with cilantro lime rice, pinto beans, salsa, lettuce, cheese, and guacamole and it was delicious as usual. I will definitely come here again when visiting.

Chipotle has come a long way since being owned by McDonalds.  Prices have gone up a little, but the quality is usually there.  This location is mostly reliable.  I've eaten at many chipotles, and there is real tangible variation from location to location.  This one seems to be the front runner in the St. Louis area.  The chicken and steak are usually fresh, and without gristle.  

I prefer chipotle to Qdoba, because I think with a chipotle burrito you can taste the difference between the different mixings in the burrito.  At Qdoba, everythins tastes like the same stale cheesy sauce.  Chipotle has fresh ingredients, and I think it really comes across in taste, and presentation

Oh Del Taco...my vegetarian fast food savior...

I'd give you five stars if only my life didn't flash before my eyes when I try to hang a left out of your frightening parking lot...if only...

In the months leading up to this place opening, lots of people I know were GOING NUTS about New Orleans finally getting a Chipotle. It's like, yo, you live in a city known for its cool food. Cool it.

So from the get-go, I wasn't expecting much, but I was intrigued.

If you're by chance unfamiliar with Chipotle, the format of this place is, like, "wait in a line, pick out a thing that can hold meat or whatever, tell them what you want inside apart from meat (or whatever), then get that thing, and probably eat it." Options aren't super wide, but it's decent Tex-Mexy stuff, and for cheap.

What I like about Chipotle, or maybe just this location (?), is that they seem to HOOK IT UP when it comes to pleasing customers. A lady who ordered in front of me was kind of awful and wanted the steak in her husband's burrito to be cooked to a temperature different from the standard of the batch-prepared steak. In response to this, the Chipotle dudes grilled up a meat especially for this burrito. On hearing this request, I was just all, "WHATEVER," but it's cool that they did this fresh.

So check em out, I guess? To people who've lived or currently live outside of the New Orleans area, I suppose this is like saying, "Hey, check out this place called McDonald's," but I do wanna give a shoutout to Chipotle's employees for being cool. And -- anyway -- they're probably your best bet for a quick, cheap and pretty good burrito in that lil part of Elmwood.
Consistently the best fast food I eat. Love the steak bowl. Healthy, but watch the calories. Build your own meal and enjoy!

Worst kfc I've ever ate at!!! You can tell they take absolutely no pride in their food!! biscuits were rock hard chicken was very greasy and cold very disgusting!! Do not eat here you will be very disappointed!!
No customer service, no receipt, our orders were messed up. My chicken was old and stale, not fresh and hardly even warm. Mashed potatoes taste like they buy dry mix from the 99 cent store.

Terrible, i wondered why every other place had a wait and kfc didn't have 2 people in it, i waited 15 minutes at the drive through, I than was told to pull to the side the sensor was going off, i waited another 20 minutes for my food, got my food, cold food, the potatoes were easily 3 days old, and cold, the gravy wasn't the gravy you get at other kfcs, chicken was cold, it was good to through away the money like that...thanks the next time i think of stopping, i will continue on.......

I was honestly afraid of this restaurant. But one day I was starving and there was nowhere else to go within my cheapskate college student budget. The food is good, not too greasy but kinda bland. They could definitely amp it up on the flavor and seasonings. The hot sauce is intense - just the way I like it. I recommend the basic hard beef taco - better than Taco Bell and just 59 cents!

If I could give 0, I would.  Arrived.  The manager, a female,  was literally yelling at an employee in front of me.  Neither looked up or stopped their tirade.  Next,  ordered the 4.99 chicken pot pie.  Waited 24 minutes because this is how long it takes for fresh ones.  GO TO Boston Market,  spend the extra 1.30, you will get way more chicken.  My pot pie was cream,  carrots,  and celery.. .maybe corn.  Hardly any chicken.  What a waste.  Next,  no one could tell me the cost of four biscuits.  Someone told me $4.00 for 2...another quoted me 5.89 for 4. Quite literally,  it was a mess.  It's a freaking side,  get it right.  Hire a better store manager,  know your product,  and keep the bs  at home.

KFC is not generally my favorite fast food place. Visits here are usually hit and miss when it comes to food quality. Today was a good day. Friendly staff and hot decent food. Only real problem today was the Mac and cheese was really bland and my 2 year old wouldn't eat it. The potatoes were good, green beans were a little too oniony. Chicken was really great today. It pays to go during lunch time because the food will be fresh.

Mmmmmm. Great breakfast and good coffee.  Plus this location has al fresco dining on a small patio. The slider egg and cheese is tough to beat as a breakfast sandwich but is a bit pricey for the size at a buck fifty. Hash browns are decent but the coffee is best on fast food.

The store is very old style it's well kept but clearly not up to date with the rest of the other stores. The cashier was quick to serve us and really nice. The chicken popcorn and poutine was hot and fresh and the gravy didn't taste like crap. The 20 piece wings were crunchy and very fresh. Highly recommend this location!
I'm a regular. Sofritas bowl is always delicious and fresh.  & the staff is always friendly.
I used to go to this location when I worked downtown. It's a shame this location closed. I have always been a fan of Del Taco.

I recently went to Burger King on Park Blvd. around 5:30 pm. One other customer, service still slow. I usually get a Whopper with cheese but decided to try the new "fish" sandwich. I will not make that mistake again. The piece of fish was small, undercooked and very fishy tasting. Not what I expected. I recommend sticking with the much better Filet-O-Fish at McDonald's.

Horrible the food is never cooked. The wake up wraps are still cold in the middle. They used to heat the food before new management. Also god forbid you walk into the door and order food it take at least 20-30 minutes for the following order: 2 small hot chocolates, a donut, two sausage egg and cheese wraps, three hash browns, and a coffee. To make matters worse they screw it up on top of everything being cold. My family won't even go here anymore and it's around the corner.

This place is not bad when you have craving for fried chicken and don't want to drive out to popeyes. The only complaint I have is that my potatoe salad was frozen so I didn't get to eat it. But the chicken and coleslaw were good. I do hate how they charge extra for the  white meat breast. But whatever we will keep coming back when the cravings hit.

I hit up this location on my way into or out of the city without having to worry about parking/tickets/crowds. 

I usually get the vegetarian salad with guacamole or the steak salad.  I'm not sure why people get the burrito bowl over the salad bowl.  With the salad you can get all of the same things for the same price except with fresh romaine lettuce on the bottom and an amazing dressing on the side if you so choose. With the burrito bowl they add shredded lettuce at the end, like it's a decoration or something.   

I like that Chipotle now offers brown rice in addition to white.  It's probably not much healthier in the grand scheme of things, but it sure feels healthier. 
I also like the music... And the fact that you can ask any employee (or go online) for a playlist.  Good food and good tunes make me swoon. Thanks, Chipotle.
Food is good cause chipotle. Staff at this one is friendly, have a solid roll game, and are not too skimpy on the steak. Writing this review makes me want to go right now...

No matter where, I love Chipotle.
Their food is consistently good and I've never been to one that changed my opinion.
Here, however, the employees are SO nice!
We had to wait a mere 2 minutes for fresh steak and they gave us a bowl for free for having to wait.
My husband and I are regulars there and prefer this specific Chipotle over any other in the area.

The only meat I get from Chipotle is Carnitas. They didn't have any ready and were just putting it on the grill raw. i dont understand why they would wait until they were out of it to start cooking some more. Instead of giving me a time, she just said they didn't have any.

The cleanest looking Burger King I've ever seen. I didn't get a peek at the kitchen but the customer dining area looked pretty spotless.

Except they gave me a small Whopper Jr. instead of the medium that I ordered. They must have known that I was breaking my New Years resolution already.

This place is generally a solid one to go to. However my last experience here put me in a foul mood after dealing with some of the jankiest staff I've ever encountered. 

I came here for my regular, a steak fajita burrito with extra rice. I prefer extra rice to combat the already thick spread of sour cream they put on the burrito. When I ordered the burrito the woman making my burrito started in what would be a string of stank looks. I was the only one in the line and it's not like I was asking her to pull a unicorn out of her behind. So I have no idea why she had an attitude. She put on the smallest amount of rice I have ever seen. Me asking for extra was me asking just to get the regular amount of rice the give. And every other item they added to the burrito was the skimpiest portion ever. It was like a struggle for them to put food onto my burrito. I seriously was ready to just say no thank you and walk out. But I was there for my mom and I was hungry.

And when I got a second burrito (for my mom) the stankness increased. I feel like if I hadn't been there watching them make my burrito, the two burrito makers would have coughed in my food. Really don't know what rubbed them the wrong way, but I have never had a bad experience and this definitely left a bad taste in my mouth. At least I was hungry enough that the burrito somewhat satiated my hunger and was sort of worth dealing with their janky attitudes. 

I love Chipotle overall and have had wonderful experiences at everyone I've frequented. So I'll give this place the benefit of the doubt. But I definitely won't be back here for a minute. I'll stick to the other locations where I have NEVER had a problem with service.

I have been here twice and both of my experiences have been good. 
I won't lie that the line is a little long, whether I come at lunch time (which is normal for any restuarant) or if I come at 3pm in the afternoon (not a common eating time, I would assume). So I would recommend to NOT come here if you are in a rush...definitely not recommended. 

The staff though is REALLY nice and they serve great portions (if you're watching what you eat though...maybe just eat half of whatever you have and save the rest for later-because they serve BIG). 

I got the steak burrito and it was delicious. One of the servers suggested I try a new beef they added but I wasn't ready to put it in my burrito just yet, so he let me try it in a cup. He said he loved it and he was right. It was delicious! I appreciated that he offered that to me.

Once they finish your meal, the ringing up is fast. I saw a lot of people studying, hanging with friends, and some just eating alone. There is outdoor seating and everyone was really comfortable inside. I recommend this place and will definitely be returning!

Also, the parking is really limited so be mindful of that. :)

Hit or miss service at this chipotle location. Sometimes the employees are polite, other times they are extremely rude. I understand places get busy and I can be rather patient but some employees decide to take this out on the customers here. The food is usually always good though! Which is a plus. However today I had pieces of steak in my chicken bowl, and I can't eat red meat

I was not a fan of burger king is previous years but i have to take the time to give them a 5 star rating based on my last half dozen visits over the past 6 months or so.  I dont live in edmonton or I would be visiting every single sunday because I have just absolutely fallen in love with their king meal deal offering on sundays.

I wish I could speak about the rest of their menu, but selling these whopper juniors for 1.99 each is more then enough reason for me to take the time to do this review.  The whopper junior comes with a good set of toppings... i believe there is tomato and lettuce.. some sort of special sause... maybe some onions... basically the typical traditional toppings for a burger we all grew up with.  The meat holds up to the starndards you would expect at any of their competitors but youd be paying 4-5 dollars for this same burger there.

this isnt 5 star gourmet cuisine.. its a fast food joint but its real tasty and real affordable.

i have tried their poutine with bacon on 3 of my visits too... and the first vist it was the best poutine i have ever had in my life.  I had dreams about it for like the next 3 weeks just dying for my next trip to edmonton.  Sadly I have been unimpressed by the bacon on the poutine the last 2 visits finding it a bit too rubbery perhaps or fat... and then last time there wasnt enough gravy in the mix and the whole thing came off a bit dry...

but that said i still have to go 5 star for their sunday  burger deal of the week offer.  it has to be the best value menu offering of any fast food restraunt ive tried that i can rememebr.  great job burger king and please keep it up

We were passing through headed to South Lake Tahoe and I was craving chicken! Lol We stopped at another one close by and they said they were about to close and they wouldn't cook any crispy chicken. We called this one before just to make sure. They gladly said they would. We walked in with 30 minutes to closing time. The place was clean and the 3 workers were so nice and welcoming. They quickly made our order and I was a happy mama with her chicken.

If your on a fixed budget and need a quick meal , this is the place to go!! The Mgr took my order twice and he was pleasant and very nice!! My grandma said the food was great string beans and chicken, my daughter ate all of her food which is a surprise because she is very picky!!! I thought the food was decent for the price and good because I did not feel like cooking on a hot summer night !!! Great place, keep up the good service!!

How do they run out of something everytime we go there? This time it was chicken, macaroni, green beans and pepsi. After taking my money and order, We had to pull forward, also after waiting 10 min at the drive through window. Then at least FIVE people pulled forward and left while we were parked. I went in to get my food and they still hadn't put ours together. They acted like we were such an inconvenience But sending buckets out the window. wth is wrong with these guys? They also forgot my food in our bag after insisting that everything was in there. I freaking hate KFC now.
Ordered the KFC bowl meal and it was still hot by the time I got home. Just a basic KFC, no frills. The chicken wasn't greasy like others I have been to.

Not sure where all the hate is coming from considering the other Mexican options in the area. It's not going to change your life, true. But of all the chain burrito places, Chipotle is the best. Maybe everyone else is ordering wrong. Here's what I get: steak burrito bowl w/ fajita veg., black beans, rice, pico, a touch of hot salsa, sour cream and guac. It's filling and delicious. And no I don't destroy my toilet the next day. Some of you might do yourself a favor and see a doctor.

Well, it's KFC another chicken chain that serves soulless fast food. All the food here is good and acceptable if you are in a rush and have nothing better to eat. I recommend the Hot chicken. It's probably a temporary item, but if you order it at lunch it will probably be better than at dinner. If you like hot chicken then give it a try.

There was a time when I didn't eat chipotle. There was also a time when I didn't know true happiness. Now I know true happiness and it comes in a little cardboard bowl with light brown rice, double steak, medium salsa, sour cream, guacamole, and corn all assembles before my very eyes by the most ironic and eccentrically tattooed minimum wage workers St. Petersburg has to offer.

Seriously, I don't know if the previous reviewer was on a heavy dose of medication or something but the only accurate part of her statement was the location being in a bad part of town.  I've gone to this burger king 4 times, placed 6 orders, and received 1 order correct.  Here are the incorrect orders I received:
1. Ordered two Rodeo burgers and received two WJrs instead -- WJrs are more expensive so I guess I got more than I paid for
2. Ordered a Whopper with cheese and no onions.  Received a Whopper with no cheese but got the onions -- and still paid for the cheese, mind you.
3. Ordered 2, 10-piece nuggets and got shorted a nugget (big whoop I guess).
4. Ordered a large Coke, got a medium.
5. Ordered a Spicy Big fish, got a regular big fish

Actually, the last two mistakes were on the same order, but you get my point.  Also, I went into the bathroom to wash my hands and when I left felt much dirtier for having walked into the bathroom.  I didn't even wash my hands because the sink was so nasty -- again, I didn't want to get dirtier.

Lastly, the service is SLOOOOWWW.  All I can say is they better get some new management if they want to exist much longer.  BTW, the coke machine with a 100 flavors actually worked as intended, so that was a plus.  But since they never clean anything up, there was a dune of ice piled up so high that it was difficult to even fill my drink.  Not to mention the gross fingerprints all over the screen.  To be fair, the fingerprints occurred because the patrons had probably just left the bathroom.

I do have something positive to say -- The fries, as always is the case at burger king, were FREAKIN AWESOME!

OK, this is 4 stars in view of the fact that it is a fast food joint.  Don't go here expecting to find freshly grilled carne asada, but it is probably the best fast food spot in STL.  Everything is much fresher and tastier than Taco Bell, that being said it's still fast food and will probably make you spend some time in the bathroom.

Had undercooked chicken and when I went and showed it to the cashier they told me tough luck then turned around and started speaking Spanish with the staff and I can only assume they were laughing at my misfortune. It was a sad day as I love KFC. But this restaurant just wasn't that good. It's not good when the staff can ruin a chain.

I ordered a bucket of chicken, original all dark.What I got was about six pieces dark.And the rest were white extra crispy. 12 piece bucket. The corn was dry and tasted like it had been sitting out all day.I will never go back to KFC.Called and asked for the Mgr. And found out Marcellis is not the Mgr of this store.He was flippant. I'm recommending to my friends not eat at KFC.Unless you want your order wrong and the food substandard.

We decided to explore the area when we started getting hungry. I yelped the closest chipotle and we followed the directions to this chipotle at PH. This chipotle (and its bathroom) was clean and it has a great outdoor seating area. We ordered 2 sofritas salads (To my surprise, even my carnivore fiancee tried and loved the sofritas so much that he ordered it). Overall, It was a good portion (not stingy) and sans the subpar service from the Chipotle at Countryside mall, so i would definitely  return.

When I go to this location, the customer service is not the best.  The chicken is usually dried out at this location and depending on who is working you may or may not be able to substitute a side. It seems each person has a different set of rules.   I favor other locations much better!

I really like Chipotle. I don't think I've ever had a bad experience here. The food and service is always great. I love how they take care in selecting which food vendors they use it's reflected in the finished product. Today I had the sofritas bowl which is their vegan option. The tofu is seasoned so well you would swear you were eating ground beef. My bowl also consisted of brown rice, pinto beans, corn, and medium salsa. I enjoyed every bite. The only thing missing from the menu is a seafood option.

I have not had KFC for a couple of years, I got 12 piece bucket of original.
It came to over $20.00 thats  $1.66 for a piece of chicken.
I took it home and started eating the first thing I notices was the amount of broken bones in each piece, they must beat the hell out of their chickens to break that many bones, each piece had several broken bones in it.
I got the original it sure didnt taste like it use to.
I will never eat KFC in any form again.
Publix has very good chicken at their deli, good price, taste good and very rarely find broken pieces of bones.
Last purchase at KFC.

It was late at night, I was working overtime covering the midnight shift at work and needed food. Not much was open at such an ungodly hour. The service was quick, the food was hot and fresh.

I was impressed that the lot was pretty much litter free, which is impressive considering its downtown location. Normally I do not use a drive-thru window, so I was shocked with the speedy and polite service I received. I had a good giggle while in line for the drive-thru, the car ahead of me was actually a golf cart. He was a security guard from a nearby nightclub. 

Not my typical WC, but I'd come back for a slider or two.

Love the chicken, love the sides...  Having said that.... When you walk into this store, nobody is standing by the counter to greet you or take your order.  I waited until I had to yell for someone to wait on me.  Then I was worried to order anything because it appeared that the kids working were not thrilled that I had to speak loudly for them to understand that someone was in the store.  Waited about 25 minutes for the food.  Not going back and it is a shame because the food is good.  I did call the franchisees and got to speak to a manager, who basically treated me the same way her employees did.  I have to think that it is the ownership that is a problem in this particular location.  Not very smart, not very fast, and most certainly not very courteous.  With soooo many other food options, Why would anybody waste their time in this location.  Train your managers, train your workers, or just shut it down.  There is a good reason there are never any cars in your parking lot.

Ive now move to edmonton and learnt the burger king deal extends to serving bk surpremes on mondays and the whopper on wednsdays.  Im not a huge fan of the other 4 days of the week but these are amazingly good burgers for 1.99.

i wouldnt go 5 stars at regular price... in fact i probably wouldnt even order these burgers at regular price.  They are very good burgers but not measurably better then good burgers at the competition.  What is OUTSTANDING is the price.  The competition has NOTHING that can touch the quality of these burgers at this price point.

Good job, Burger King.  I will be dropping by 3 days a week and getting a poutine order too.  I have no problem at all giving you 5 stars here

Mmm Chipotle; the mother of manifold comfort. Since there is plenty of writ upon this chain, we'll zero-in on the nutritional facts. I'm not scared that the % hit the triple digits; I will still house 2 burritos, easy.

Question. Is it just me or has taste/texture of the rice changed? I don't like it one bit. It was the best when McDonald's held most their shares.

Can't remember how many years it's been since I ate at a KFC. Recently while moving, it sounded like the right food for the occasion. 
Yup, it's fast food. Yup, it's friend. But, there is no denying they have some BIG pieces of chicken & it is friend perfectly. 
The chicken is never over cooked or undercooked. It is always nice & juicy. The faux mashed potatoes, I can live without, really pretty disquesting. The coleslaw is good & crisp & not drenched in dressing. Since my last visit was so long ago, the biscuts have changed & are smaller & more commercial. No flaky big biscuts here.
What's a girl to do? Moving ya gotta eat, the chicken was good & hit the spot.

We had two burritos to go, and we thought it wasn't a good experience at all.
