When we were at the cash register, the shift manager apparently was not paying attention to which-was-which when he bagged one of our burritos after it had to be re-wrapped. 

When we got home to eat, I was starving more than usual, and we really didn't have anything else to eat until going to get groceries the next day. When I cut into my burrito, I noticed it was carnitas, not veggie! I thought "oh, I guess I'll be eating this not-spicy-enough non-vegetarian thing tonight" and I went with it (actually, my first thought was that it was shredded chicken instead, because the carnitas wasn't seasoned well at all). Then the deal breaker was when I found a tiny black hair in my burrito when I already ate 1/3 of it. I was PO'd, but I was so hungry and desperate I didn't really have a choice but to finish it.

I usually cannot speak highly enough about Chipotle, but all of these flaws really turned me off from this location.

Wow talk about amateur hour. The chipotle's that I have visited in the past have been smoothly run establishments where I am in and out in the minimum amount of time. Not this place. I really don't know where to start but I will start with the homeless guy on the patio eating ice cream. What is that all about chipotle? You just gonna let mr homeless guy come in a call the shots like he owns the place (if he is the actual owner I apologize)?  Next lets get to the service. I stood in line waiting to order for approximately 10 minutes because they didn't have any steak or brown rice ready for someone who ordered it in front of me. Aren't those two pretty crucial things in the chipotle operation? Maybe y'all couldve thought about making more a little earlier but that might just be me. Anyways, when I go out and pick something up, I don't wanna be standing there like Steven Glansburg for 10 mins feeling like I'm unemployed or something. I want to be in and out and heading back to my house to eat in comfort as fast as possible. Chipotle made me feel like Steven Glansburg today and for that I am not appreciative. Not to mention the lady had a little 'tude with me. The only thing rescuing this hellish experience from 1 star is the food itself. Now I don't know Carlos Q. Chipotle personally, but I'd have to believe he would not be very happy with what I saw in there today. Not at all. Needless to say it might be a while before Chipotle is receiving my patronage.

This is absolutly the worst place I uave ever been to. It should be shut down. Their have been times when the employees were locked out cause their wasn't anyone to let them in. The food is bad and I have had blood in my chicken. Ill order food pay for it and only get half sometimes. I have waited half an hour just for a famous bowl. And it still hasnt changed.

This is my favorite place to grab a quick bite since I've worked in Bala Cynwyd.  I consistently order their steak taco, hard.  The staff is so friendly and since I am a regular, they usually have my order up from when I get in the door to when I arrive at the counter.  It's not fancy, but it's always clean, clean, clean.

I love that everything is fresh and there are no additives in the meat.  I am never disappointed with the quality and taste.  Consistent.  An entree and a drink is almost $9 - a bit costly for semi-fast food - which is why I did not give five stars.  Lunchtime parking for the shopping center where it resides is pretty congested, too.

They taste like microwaved burgers, but when they are open 24hrs that will have to do!

It was 3am and I was so hungry. I'm guessing that's how the majority of us ended up here. Nothing besides White Castle was open, according to the door man. Only the drive thru is 24hours so I had my Uber driver cruise through the line. 

The burgers were still warm by the time I arrived to my room, the fries...eh. I only ended up eating 1 slider and my "Nah. I'm not hungry" husband ate the remaining burgers.
Got the order wrong. I tried something new saucy spicy chicken(?) and it was AWFUL !

Love chipotle but none close to where I live so when I'm in an area where there is one I always go to it and this happens to be one of those times. I ordered what I always do, burrito w/ cilantro lime rice, black beans, guacomale, fajitas, corn salsa, and at this location they had sofritas available which is tofu prepared like there other meat and it was so good. The burrito was amazing like always but a little pricey (understandable since they charge extra for guacamole) but as I was eating I came across a problem I won't get into detail about and when I informed the staff they refunded my entire meal (including my drink) and gave me some awesome coupons. Will definatley be returning for more yummy chipotle
This was the worst KFC I have ever had. Chicken was dry, and disgusting. Went back and asked for a refund. The manager was rude, and refused. I will never go to this run down KFC ever again.

Id like to give this a NO STAR rating.  We have enjoyed KFC for years.  The food has generally been very good.  This trip to KFC was a different location than our usual.  The experience was also very different.  The chicken; regular, all white, 8 piece was unedible.  My husband ate 1/2 piece and I ate 1 bite.  The breading was soft, the chicken had a green tint.  We looked through the chicken and could not really identify what the pieces were.  There was some pretty gross stuff under the breading and it was all green-looking.  The potatoes and gravy were awful, biscuits hard, corn cold and old-looking.  All of it went into the trash.  I communicated this experience to KFC on line.  I have heard nothing back.  I do hope this is addressed by the company.

I don't usually get chicken an if I do it's usually from the grocery stores. But today my gf said she just had to have a KFC famous bowl so of course being the good man I am I come to the nearest location. After waiting in the drive thru in 102 degree weather an still no one has came on the intercom to take my order, I drive around to the window still nobody, I drive around to the door an 2 of the 3 workers are outside smoking. I go inside an make sure they know how long I was waiting. The worker just says it's not his fault they don't have enough staff so they can't work the drive thru. I order my food an they guy proceeds to throw the bowls around like a child would to let you know he is mad. If this place doesn't get better management I will definitely DEFINITELY not be coming here again. Wish I could give less stars!
salad's are great! sofritas are a new awesome add to the menu, I get it every time I come now. Fast service, great food, location, and atmosphere.

I got 3 tacos with sofritas. And the server didn't let it drain from the spoon so I had soggy tacos :( and I went into the bathroom to wash my hands.... OMG, it was disgusting. No toilet paper, trash all over. I told one of the workers as I left so hopefully it was taken care of. The food is good and fresh. Just wish it wasn't so watery.

Chipotle is one of my healthy go to places. You always know what you're getting and the calories, protein, and macros are always in line. Also it's a filling portion. That said I ordered my standard salad with lettuce, carnitas, black beans, roasted chili corn, tomatillo green-chili salsa, and guacamole. It was delicious. The place good have used a good wipe down. It was challenging to find a seat inside that didn't have a mess even though there was only like 1 other person sitting inside. I'll be back but I'll hope they clean up!

I have been to this location on several occasions. Two times I ordered a turkey sausage flatbread. I noticed when I order this item from other Dunkins, it had a sharp cheddar type cheese on it which is delicious, however this location put American cheese on it - not what I wanted. They also don't make macchiatos correctly. They use coffee instead of espresso which, I feel, is a tactic they use to save money. Another seems to be overcharging. I was asked to pay $4.55 for a small caramel iced macchiato.... Please double and triple check your order and make sure they're not trying to rip you off too!

I have to say this Burger King is one of the  better bk's in the area clean and nice inside and out. I really like the coke dispenser pick a drink add a flavor and your on your way to satisfaction. The whopper is my go to burger here can't beat it.

Chipotle is a filling and healthy meal so when I was running around like a crazy person on a Monday afternoon I stopped in for a quick lunch. I always get Salad with black beans, carnitas, medium salsa, hot salsa, corn, and sometimes guacamole when I'm feeling extra. Service is friendly and speedy!
Ordered two chicken littles and hey we're just bread and chicken. Kind of disappointed....

It was confusing to order. Waited at the beginning and no one helped us, walked to the register and they sent us back. Ordered a chicken burrito bowl. Taste is ok, but cold meat. My boyfriend tried to get a steak burrito bowl. They couldn't hear him well enough and made a burrito instead. At least his was warm. Way too loud. 

Waited to check out and the employees just stared at us. Finally called someone who was on the floor to check us out. 

Service meh. Fresh taste. Cold meat meh. Not worth the price.

This particular chipotle gives such small portions that it is ridiculous. Today I got a steak Bol and the girl gave me half a spoon full of rice, half a spoon of beans and actually gave me about 5 cubes of steak. It was really unbelievable. It seems to be getting worse. 

I love the food but I can't continue to support a place that charges so much for so little.

This restaurant is pretty similar to Izzo's as other reviewers mention. You get a pretty standard burrito with pretty standard choice of meats, veggies, rice and cheese. I had their burritos twice over this last week. First time it was the one with steak and second time with chicken. I liked the steak one better. When I ordered the one with chicken, it smelled weird, like either the chicken or rice was cooked in some weird oil. So, I didn't enjoy it as much. I heard that lines were long, especially at lunch time (I got sometime get the burritos for me so I never actually been in the restaurant.) I guess it's a nice addition to a plentiful choice of restaurants in the Elmwood shopping center.

Went there today to get a quick bite to eat before going to the movies. The line was huge and was moving slowly. We got steak burritos and chicken bowl. They put tiny amounts of steak on the burritos. It was smaller than the other times I ate here so we asked to add the meat. The server told us that's how much meat they put on their burritos and would not add any more. I asked to talk to the manager, who came out and also said it was the amount they give - 4oz of meat. Everyone who was with me agreed that it was probably 1/2 of the regular portion we usually get. Well, Chipotle, you lost us as customers.

This place is a joke. Do yourself a favor and go to the other locations. Chicken is always full of fat and today when I ordered a sofrita salad the sofrita was ICE COLD! It's usually not the hottest but never this bad.

I really don't like being THAT customer but first off we let them know we had a coupon for 2pc deal for a leg and a thigh with mashed potatoes and a biscuit. They said the coupon was fine. We also asked for SEPARATE order of a chicken little sand which. We pulled up to the window and they said they couldn't do the extra crispy and we couldn't get a thigh. So we asked if we could get an extra leg for the inconvenience and gave them our coupon they couldn't give the extra leg and that's understandable BUT they couldn't accept our coupon either and if we knew that we would've ordered a bowl. Why didn't they double check the coupon before we made our choice and ordered? Our coupon wasn't expired nothing was wrong with it they just said "it wasn't participating location" and gave us the total of BOTH orders together $9. The honey mustard chicken little sandwhich was very delicious though but, #FUCKTHISKFC

This has to be my favorite Chipotle location in the New Orleans area! I was a little hesitant at first, because I'm so used to the speedy service at other Chipotle locations around the states and this one was pretty slow and inefficient a few months ago. The last few times I've been there, I've noticed they're a lot quicker. Good job on improvement!

Secondly, I went to the Veterans location and they weren't as flavorful and seasoned like this Harahan location. I would actually  drive 10 minutes further to eat at this Harahan location than to eat at the Veterans location again (see my Chipotle on Veterans review).

If you've never been to Chipotle, it's a quick service burrito restaurant setup similar to Subway. You pick your medium (burrito, bowl, taco, etc), meat, and toppings. Very easy!

My favorite thing to order: Burrito Bowl, brown rice, no beans, steak, pico, red sauce, green sauce, sour cream, corn, guacamole, and lettuce. Then, top it off with some Chipotle flavored Tabasco. Perfection!

I love Chipotle. It was a college staple and I was so durned happy when they opened that I made sure we had a dinner date. It's a healthy and fast choice on evenings where we can't cook, and between this or Popeyes, both my belly and my tastebuds are always pleased with the decision. 

I have come to be a fan of the burrito bowl. It has all the filling and comes in an easy-to-carry bowl with lid so you can take your unfinished half to work the next day for lunch. The black beans and all the veggies taste awesome while still being fresh and vegetabley. I also have a hard time choosing meats: do I want the tender pulled pork? Tartly stewed barbacoa beef? Or the chargrilled chicken or steak? LIFE IS HARD! 

 I was stoked to see that they now have a brown rice option, which is still as flavorful with cilantro and lime. I know the guacamole price is a ripoff but it's so good that I get it on the side and, again, it's so much that I have to finish it later anyhow. 

Don't forget to download the app if you need to order ahead! You can put in your order and just pick up when you get there, so for those of you with a short lunch break, you can have your deliciousness without the long wait.

I have a fish sandwich, a breakfast sandwich, a slider, cheese fries, and a lack of decision making skills. 

This White Castle is quick and conveniently located blocks away from the south meridian strip. I grew up in an era where you yelled what you wanted through a speaker, took a ticket number, and waited eons until your order magically appeared behind a thick glass window. 

Asking for "no onion" really meant asking for two-thirds less onion because, Rhondisha ain't got time to clear off that entire grill. You're getting onions on your burger, your bun, in the sides of your burger box/bag, and possibly in your soft drink! 

Back then I would not have taken 10 minutes to decide on 10 things, and actually had half my order in any time less than 10 (million) minutes. This place does it right. They are nice, fast, and friendly! I would recommend going on a not so busy night mainly because the bar riff raff (as expected) can be rowdy or entertaining?

I'm not a fan of how the prices seem to go up, and the portions look smaller. All things considering the sliders are hors d'oeuvres without the cellophane toothpicks or fancy silver platters fooling us! At almost $1 each they are competing with the times, and I get it. Keeping it classic, yet still modern...maybe.

Overall, this location has got it going on when the crave sets in, especially the fourth meal. 

Thanks, White Castle south street, officially still the King of all things Onion.

Continuing with the Wilmington/Philly trip from Memorial Day weekend, I had said I would review two more businesses.  DD is a common place, which I've gone to many locations in MD & NoVA throughout the years, but this is my first DD I've ever visited in the great state of Delaware.  The typical DD in the DC-Baltimore area is either good to great or mediocre.  This location ended up being less than mediocre for our late breakfast visit.

I was telling both my friends about the DD App, where you get points. They are old-fashioned and don't use apps to get good deals.  I explained about the point system, and my friend said that he could give me cash so that I would get more points.  I agreed that would work.  Both my friend & I each got an iced coffee and the 2 for $2 wraps.  I opted for sausage for $1 more.  

The cashier seemed confused even though it was fairly simple. 
 My friend got his food & iced coffee very quickly.  My iced coffee was fairly quick, but as for the food, I was standing there 10 minutes, and I was seeing other people get their food who had ordered after me!  I wanted to spend time with my friends who were sitting at a table already eating their food.  Finally, I asked, and they said it would be a minute; it was another 5 minutes...I was observing, and there was no sense of urgency.  In general, it seemed chaotic in there like no one knew what they were doing, which frustrated me.

I finally got my wraps, which were good as usual, but it delayed us in our day's activities.  Overall, I was annoyed with this Dunkin' and therefore, they only earned 2-stars.

Listen you either love WC or you hate WC it's just the way it is. My self I have grown up with White Castle burgers so every time I come back home this is the first stop I make every time. The frozen ones you get where I live (cause we do not have White Castle) do not come close to the real thing don't even try to compare. You have all these fancy restaurants trying to jump on the bandwagon of what we in the mid west call "a Slider" only to not get the point of why we originally called White Castle burgers "Sliders" my friends it is the after math of a fresh giant yummy 30 pack of White Castle burgers that gives these little packages of onion meat flavored burgers their nickname. Please don't let that scare you as long as you don't decide on playing a game of one on one basketball after devouring a good number of these tasty bad boys you will be fine. There are many other things on the menu that are good as well, fries, shakes, chicken rings and a lot more yummy tummy stuff. Just give it a try at least then you can say you tried the real Slider not a fake one at some overly priced restaurant where kids are not even allowed to act their age. Stake em up and give me 30 chef!!

Maybe it was an off day...

The food needs tremendous work in order to compare to the rest of the Chipotle's :T
1. the brown rice was undercooked [so a little hard]
2. the beans were also undercooked
3. the carnitas [pork] was overcooked
4. the steak had no flavor and kind of dry...
5. the guacamole was majorly lacking in flavor

The service, however, was quick and friendly.

Hint: maybe the workers/manager should taste the food before you serve it?

Obviously I'll be back, but probably not for a few months...

The most over-hyped, over-priced, useless chain restaurant experience possible.

First impression; It was clean, but the music was way too loud. I'm a musician and I like loud music, but this was inappropriately so. 

First item: My girlfriend doesn't care for smoked chilis at all. I tried to avoid any products containing chipotle, and asked every employee in the restaurant about the ingredients I was choosing. Chose 3 steak tacos for $10 (added guac) which were delivered ice cold. Not tepid, cold, like straight out of the refrigerator cold. The tortilla didn't even stay warm for long enough to open the foil and eat it, they were cold like an Antarctic penguin's feet. There was plenty of chipotle on the meat and in the salsa, too.They don't know what is in their seasoning, or the employees were uniformly clueless as to what chipotle tastes like, or they blatantly lied to me. None of which are acceptable.

Second item: I ordered a $10 barbacoa burrito, it was served room temperature or cooler, and was wrapped in two tortillas. I watched in horror as they almost served it to me with no meat at all. Then, when the young lady couldn't wrap it on the first try, she asked if it was okay if she just wrapped it in a second tortilla, I just let her do whatever she wanted because I was beside myself with shock that anyone would serve another person such a monstrosity.  Most of the guacamole ($2!) ended smashed on the outside, and largely stuck to the foil. She couldn't wrap it to save her life. 

The ingredients I ended up with are OK quality, it tastes acceptably good. 

I don't like lettuce much anyway, but the lettuce looked disgusting, and in my opinion, unsafe. 

One block east is Los Betos, which is a real mexican restaurant, open 24 hours, where you can get better food, served hot, for literally half the price. 

My takeaway is this; If you like to eat at Chipotle you either have too much money, no palate, or no self-respect.

I cannot understand, whatsoever, why this chain is successful.

I ordered a chicken little and the pictures attached to this review will show how disappointed I was.. 
I ordered two bowl meals for them to be a little on the cold side with no melted cheese. But the girl at the window who got called into work on her day off was very nice, so these two stars really go to her.

Ran out of original chicken. Left a piece of chicken off my order and substituted green beans for mashed potatoes without asking or telling me. I hate green beans. And gave me part of the people's behind me order. Asked for it back once it was in my car and I was driving off. I am sure that is a health code violation.  They just don't care.

This is my type of fast food. I've gotten really into Chipotle recently! It provides a filling, healthier-than-fast-food meal for cheap. And you customize you meal, which gives extra points in my book. I always get the "bowl" option - the burritos are too big and hard to eat for me, and the fillings are never equally distributed. The bowl option lets me go without a tortilla and lets me mix everything up. I also think you get more food that way. My preferred bowl is sofritas (tofu), black and pinto beans, brown rice, cheese, lettuce and sour cream. Very flavorful and delicious! 

The only downside is that this location is always busy at lunch and dinner, the line gets long and can be slow. And the parking lot is weird - some of the spots can make it easy to hit or be hit.

Past through this town on a road trip and got a steak bowl from this Chipotle. It was terrible. Steak was dry and had no seasoning or salt on it and the rice was like chunks of rock. Disgusting. If I hadn't taken it to go I would have taken it back. 

Pros- the space was clean
Found THREE pieces of long strands of hair in my chicken/carnitas burrito! YUCK! Only giving three stars because it's still Chipotle.

I couldn't wait until chipotle opened up on cityline so much so that after I finished classes, I drove myself out of the way to that area just for a carnitas burrito when I heard it was finally opened.  

But there was something different compared to others.

I got in and waited for the tortilla flattening guy to put on his latex gloves for like literally 4 minutes. When he finally stopped fidgeting, he was finally ready to take my order (how convenient of him) and then when it came down to the end, the other chipotle handler had problems wrapping the burrito, tearing the tortilla.  After seeing all that, my burrito didn't taste as good.

I'm not an unreasonable guy, the place just opened, I realize that, but this happened to me again a week ago.  Compared to all the other times i've had it in Chicago, SF, Atlanta and NYC, this one is a train wreck.

Hopefully they'll get their act together before their food suffers.

I like the carnitas in taco form, but the service and the seating arrangement make it hard for me to enjoy my food.  We went at a pretty busy time of day and there was a line of folks waiting to order.  We found out why when we got up to place our order.  We asked for carnitas tacos and the servers were confused and not focused.  They were trying to rush orders through, had too many people on the line, and were trying to do too many things at once.  It's not like their menu is hard.  You make some tortillas, put some meat on it, and add toppings.  I had to get the attention of the person putting my tacos together multiple times to get the toppings that I wanted.

There is something about the seating that is just not right.  Maybe it is the small amount of room inside, the fact that the tables are so close that you have a hard time getting in and out of the seats, or that you sit very close to the line of people who are trying to get the servers to make what their food.  Then we had a hard time finding a place to put the high chair we require for our youngest son.

If there is a next time, I will just go by myself and take the food to go.

Me and a friend ordered simple meals, a Whopper Junior meal and a 2 for $5 sandwich deal. My friend and I, along with about fifteen other customers had to wait a good twenty minutes to recieve our food. The manager was so slow and innefficeint in the way he worked it was unbelievable. The three other employees were also frutrated with him but h didn't seem to crae. I've heard similar stories from friends who have been also! Burger King please find a new manager! Food was what I expected from Burger King. Good, but took too long to come.

I love kfc but this place every time I go there you always have to wait. Wait 5 minutes for the chicken wait 5 minutes for the wedges, wait wait wait. That's not the worst part you park outside and they forget about you. Will not be going back there. Went to the one on ogantz ave, they were polite and very quick service. No wait no wait no wait.

My intial though on this place was a glorified Taco Bell with better ingredients.  Boy was I correct, but in a good way.  The food was pretty good.  I got steak taco with basically with the fillings of your choice.  They have rice, guac, salsa, hot/mild sauce, cheese and stuff like that.  I mistakenly tried the hot sauce and it was burning my mouth.  It was good, but too hot for my taste lol.  

Theres not a whole lot of selection, either taco, burrito, salad, and I think thats about it.  Im probably forgetting a item or too so please forgive me.  Its a place worth trying and taste better then Taco Bell.

Happy Eatings!!

I always get the salad with their honey vinaigrette.  The dressing is delicious and what makes the salad so delicious.  I'm so glad that they brought the sofritas (Braised tofu)to the East Coast.  My husband is a meat lover, but actually enjoyed it so give it a try!  The ingredients are fresh and delicious.  Hubby always gets the burrito when we come here.  The only complaint would be that the ingredients don't get mixed together when they put it into the burrito.  You might get a nice big bite of just sour cream (ewww) because they just glop the ingredients on and wrap it all up.  Nice big portions and decent prices.

In total we waited 32 minutes for cold chicken. Adonis (name on receipt) was rude when taking our order and then made sure to swipe my card before telling me I needed to pull up for "four minutes" while my chicken cooked. Over 15 minutes went by before I ended up just going into the store. Our order was sitting on the counter, and the "server" at the register was annoyed that I even bothered coming in. Never again at this location.
The only reason this Burger King got a star is because I'm eating a frozen Whopper off my floor right now.  I went through this place with my taxi driver and the fare jumped 13 dollars.

I've been a fan of chipotle for quite some time but lately I've been very disappointed. First off is the cleanliness of this particular restaurant. We arrived around 12:30 so during the lunch rush, there were a ton of dirty tables. Nobody was cleaning them. There wasn't anywhere really to sit. I had to clean my own table. 

After the debacle of cleaning we ordered. I went with the chorizo bowl and my wife the steak. The steak was extremely overcooked and chewy. The chorizo was like eating sand. The veggies were also undercooked. Chipotle used to be a solid lunch. Things were always fresh and delicious. Sadly, for me it's taken quite the downturn at numerous locations. 2 stars

Awful! Had a business meeting decided to meet up and to eat here because they are new in the neighborhood. I ordered the Steak Bowl, unfortunately I didn't get steak but a bunch of grisly and fat I ended up throwing my meal away. Maybe something else would be a better option but I would pass on the Steak.

I had always thought the idea behind the chain store was one gets the same thing and quality no matter which store one goes.

So it does surprise me the difference between the KFC's even with in the Reno Sparks area.

I was out and about with my sister today and she wanted some cole slaw and she favors KFC.

So we decide to get a quick lunch at the same time. She ordered the bites and I the original chicken sandwich.

Well the bites were hard little bullets and the chicken sand was dry and stringy and my slaw was dry with no zip.

If all food came out of each store was as this they would close with in a year.

The guy taking the order was very enthusiastic in his manor but for me a little hard to understand, but ok because he truly was giving it his all.

The store was clean.
The food was okay but $6.97 for a breast and a wing with a couple of sides? I like the chicken but the value just isn't there.

In Indiana White Castle, known for the tiny little onion burger in a box- also called a slider- has table service and reservations on Valentine's Day. We went! We have been to some of the finer dining places in this state but I will say the amount of fun we had at this fast food turned full service was unbelievable. The atmosphere was light and fun, employees into their jobs. We met some fun couples who say they do it every year because it's just something unusual and fun. I would do it again!

I've been to Chipotle a few/several/lot more times since my past review and they're still as awesome as ever. But because a lot of people somehow still manage to confuse how to order food from Chipotle or the sheer variety of food there... [ahem]

-- The Chipotle Ordering Process -- 
1. Go to the left side of the line. No, don't stand in front of the cash register, that's the end of the line.

2. Pick between a Burrito, Bowl (Burrito without the shell. I'm here all day), 3 Tacos, or a Salad. All of these are the same price -- you only pay for the meat you select.

3. Pick between White or Brown Rice, Black or Pinto Beans, and if you want Fajita Veggies.

4. Pick between Chicken, Steak, Barbacoa (Pulled Beef), Carnitas (Pork), Sofritas (Vegan Tofu), or Veggie (Guacamole). This is practically the only time in this whole operation where the price could change. Just about everything else is 'free'... so don't skimp on the toppings. Load up.

5. Pick between Mild, Medium or Hot Salsa; cheese; sour cream; and guac. You do pay extra to add Guac unless you selected a Veggie item in Step 4.

6. Pay for your item. Yeah this step isn't optional, sorry about that.

--

...not saying it's hard to order. In fact, it's pretty easy and straightforward. But for those of you who are indecisive like I, and like a little heads up before showing up... this is my gift from me to you.

So why Chipotle over Moes? Chipotle is a bit pricier and has less variety, but you get what you pay for. I find that Chipotle's food quality is a bit higher up than Moes and because they serve less options, everything's just fresher and tastier. If you want a  freestyle Coke Machine or free nachos... yeah Moes is your option.

But if you want good Mexican Food, at a fair price, with great ingredients, you'd be crazy not to pick Chipotle methinks.

Keep it up!! We love you! Delicious vegan options. The sofritas are amaaaaazing! The fajita veggies; The corn, tomatillo and pico de gallo salsas and the guacamole all on point! Big burritos, delicious crunchy tacos. Fantastic service. I'm so thankful these guys offer a vegan options for our family of seven, sometimes 8! Love this location, great staff and super clean.

I enjoy this place when the right person is serving the food. The food is always fresh and with the right amount it's great. I always order the burrito bowl with  barbacoa as my choice of meat. I try the steak before and it was good as well, but barbacoa is my main one. There's a few server that's not shy on putting a good amount of meat in the bowl and there's some cheap ones that give you like a pinch and have the nerve to take some back from the bowl and place it back in the tray. As stated it all depends on who is making the bowl. With everything balance it's really good.

Most of the time it's packed, but you can always find a parking space outside. The wait time isn't bad either even when it's crowded. I think the longest I wanted in the line was about 5-7 minutes at lunch time. The workers aren't really friendly, but as long as my food is good I don't really care.

If I could give it a zero stars I would... I usually don't go to this location but since it was near school, thought I would give it a chance. Ordered the famous bowl fill up, had to wait for the popcorn chicken. After I got my food, headed back to school, once I opened up the bowl.... there was only about 1/4 of food in there. Probably 4 pieces of chicken, almost no corn or cheese. I now see why people don't recommend this location.

This location needs to be cleaned up on the exterior. I'm actually scared to see what the inside looks like. Surprisingly, the food wasn't bad. I had a large order and the entire order was correct. I wasn't missing items nor was the food cold or nasty. The fries were hot and so was the chicken. The employees also were very courteous making sure that I had everything I needed. I just hope the owner of this location fixes the exterior of the building. Looks pretty bad.
Gravy was like brown water. Original chicken very salty and had an odd after taste. Biscuits a little doughy in the center. Green beans pretty good.

I'm hooked..great fast casual healthy option. I love the sofritas bowl. Great app for ordering no wait take out. Happy employees who seem like they don't hate their jobs..A good change from other fast casual restaurants.

I tried White Castle years ago and didn't like it. I discovered today that I still don't really like it. The fries and mozzarella sticks were good. I ordered a BBQ slider expecting it to be, well BBQ. But it was just a regular slider with BBQ sauce. Which I guess was my fault because I should've read the description better. Anyway it was nothing special and I hope I never have to go to a white castle again.

My name is Anika reese Your workers are rude didn't make my order right I ordered 3 wing dinners and a number 6 my side orders was not in the box I spoke to the manager  and he was rude as well I will never come back to your restaurant I asked for fresh chicken and the young lady said I'm not making no fresh chicken she was dark skinned  heavy set with glasses and I came through the drive through around 5:30 1/24/17 I'll just take my business some where else.  tweettie76@gmail.com

This location is exactly what a three-star review on Yelp entails: A-OK.

It's Chipotle. You know what you've signed up for when you walk in the door. High sodium Mexican food that tastes great for the price and is filling, but leaves you wondering if you've just contracted the latest virus. Chipotle seems to always be in the news for the wrong reasons. I've never had an experience like those though.

Can't complain with this location as the staff was super friendly and the food was fresh. Line moved quick and there's ample seating, inside and out.

Comparing to other locations I've been to:
-PRO: Portions were fair. Not the biggest bowl I've gotten, but by no means the smallest. I've had worse.
-PRO: Fajitas were fresh as can be. This is always the tell-tale of the quality of the Chipotle location you're in. Bright and crunchy, you're in the clear. Droopy and soggy? Not here, but watch out when you see that. Thankfully, this location does it right.
-CON: "The Guac is extra." Yes, I know!
-PRO: They didn't skimp when scooping the Guac on.
-CON: Steak seemed fattier than normal. If this location has a true negative, this is it.
-PRO: Outdoor seating. Get out and enjoy that SB weather.

got the daily special (original),  the chicken I have never seen so greesy!!  and for getting this in the brunch time and nobody in the lobby area,  all the tables were dirty!! and the prep area behind the counter seemed so cluttered.

I know it's a little silly to review a fast food restaurant but this one needed a bashing!  
Have ya ever had a craving for something and when ya finally got it you were totally disappointed?  Well that's what happened to me this evening.
First of all, the darned speaker thing at the drive through was all jacked up so ordering was a pain in the patoot!  So we drive up to the window to find a very animated and friendly fellow.  He may have had our order all wrong and forgot our drinks but at least he was pleasant. He may have been a tad high but really what can you expect.  I certainly don't hold it against a restaurant worker who's been smoking. It goes with the territory.
So we drove home to find nearly in eatable chicken in our bucket!  I use the term Nearly cuz we were starving and ate the entire box, but it was pretty horrid.  The chicken was almost damp.  Instead of crispy, golden brown the chicken was slimy and over cooked.  Kind of black.  It wouldn't have bothered me so much but I had a long day and didn't have the strength to drive back to have the order fixed or to go somewhere else.  I've eaten at this location before and it was ok.  This may have been the last time we hit KFC. 
Lesson learned.  I should've taken a little extra time and searched Yelp to find what nearby place offered the best fried chicken and just gone and gotten myself some wonderful food!  Then I'd be writing a four or five star review of a great new place right now instead of ranting about how bad a chain is!

Was a fav of the family for a long time...... But the last 2 months something has went sideways with their steak and chips.  Every 3rd piece of steak is pure grissle, and the chips are over salted and stale.  No issues before but like I said....the last couple months has not been very good.   Don't order the steak!

Went through the KFC drive-through last night to get dinner. We ordered a 4-pc basket of the new specialty chicken, and two chicken littles.
The girl who took my order acted like she would rather lay in the middle of a busy street than take my order. I had to repeat the order three times, and she was really rude.
Waited in the parking lot for about five minutes for the order to be brought out us, which was fine up until we got halfway home and discovered they forgot the Chicken Littles. 
We went back and the manager was great,  it was just annoying and kind of a bummer how little that girl cared about her job. 
We got home and discovered that the meal was also wrong.
I'm sure we will return there, but next time I will check the order before I leave the parking lot!

I'm not a huge "Yelper" but this place deserves it.  I eat at chipotle everyday. No joke.  I'm on the road driving and it's my healthiest (affordable) option. 
This chipotle is on it! The team is on fire! Every team member down the line and the guy on cash is awesome.  
The food was great. I'm a huge veggie person and my veggies were cooked just right.  Both my meats were perfect as well (chicken and steak). Shoutout to the manager on duty doing table touches and clearing trash. I've never seen a chipotle run this smooth and have such good quality in food.  

This is for sure the A team! 
( I would know, I eat and chipotle all across the US ) 

Thanks again!

I genuinely love Dunkin' Donuts, but this location needs some help. The drive-through employees argue with each other in front of customers, and I don't think I've ever seen anyone in the entire establishment smile. I don't think I would mind this service if my orders were correct, but they have not been on multiple occasions. I ordered a breakfast sandwich through the drive-through and ended up turning around and coming into the building after I realized there was a mixup. I told one employee that I ordered a bagel with cream cheese not a sausage egg and cheese on an everything bagel. Two of the employees just looked at each other and laughed. No apology and no attempt to correct the mistake. They told me another customer took my order, which is strange because I came through the drive through...  they finally corrected the mistake after showing my receipt for proof. I had already handed them the incorrect order. I try to avoid this location, and go out of my way for another Dunkin. But it's starting to get convenient, and I'd rather spend the extra dollar on Starbucks across the street.

Please please please do not eat at the Willow Grove KFC!!!!!! the customer service is unprofessional and rude I went through the drive through and ordered a 15 piece bucket of original chicken the five pieces of chicken on top were cooked the remaining 10 pieces at the bottom of the bucket were burnt and it was old chicken it was absolutely disgusting I will never eat at this location again this is my third time at this location and all my visits have been horrible and disgusting!!!!!!!

Meh. It's a Chipotle. I'd been wanting to try the vegan sofritas. Burritos are my favorite. I'm just not that enthusiastic about chain restaurants. But it was fine. I might go back to a Chipotle again in a yearish if I'm in a hurry for fast food or something.

Oh, & it was crowded so Ed C & I had to sit with strangers. Weird.

Apologies for my lack of enthusiasm. I suppose I might have been better off with no review at all. Whatever. It's a Chipotle. At least it's vegan friendly.

I can't see this place to hang in business much longer. The left door into the business does not work, hasn't for months but of course putting a sign saying don't use this door is too much trouble. Typically you have to stand around and try and flag somebody down to take your order. Then wait quite awhile because they are typically out of chicken. Today go to use the bathroom and the door is already part way open because it's broken and won't shut all the way. The fluorescent light is just dimly flickering. There was so but there is no way to dry your hands because there's no air dryer or paper towels in the dispenser.

I bought an impossible burger for the first time today. It will be the last time.
It had an odd flavor. I have had impossible burgers at four different restaurants. They were all delicious.
This burger was not.
AND... an hour after eating it I got the runs and my system has been upset the remainder of the afternoon.
NEVER EVER AGAIN!

In a recent Popeyes review I said I would prefer KFC over them. When I wrote that I had actually not had KFC for about 3 years or so. I would like to formally retract that statement and apologize to Popeye's after eating here. Popeye's, your chicken is marginally better than KFC's.

I want to stress there's nothing really wrong with the location or the employees here, so don't fire anybody. It's purely the food, which I'm sure is at chain level. KFC has apparently decided to go absolute McDonalds-level bottom-barrel quality with their food and just load it with salt hoping you can't tell the difference. 

Rubbery, flavorless chicken. Instant powdered mash potatoes drowned in super-salty gravy. They're too cheap to even give you a pat of butter with your biscuit, instead you get The Colonel's Butter-esque Spread, half of which you can't even get out of the one grudging packet they give you. Same with the "honey sauce", look at the ingredients: high fructose corn syrup, corn syrup, sugar, finally actual honey at #4. 

Only possible excuse I can see is if you're at Duds N Suds doing laundry on a M-Th and you creep over for some cheap filler lunch, they have like 2.99 chicken and corn on Monday, etc. As bad as this stuff is I'm not sure it's even worth 2.99, though.

I love Chipotle. This is a review for this specific location.

I have been here six times in the past few months as I love chipotle. I've decided to find a new location for the following reasons:

-Steak is almost always dry and very tough. I'm not expecting amazing steak, just not something that feels like it's been sitting there for half a day

-service is crap. They seem to hire a lot of younger people that don't give a damn. Twice now an employee has MISSED the bowl while putting on sour cream. How does that even happen? 

-condiment area is often empty of forks or napkins

This was probably the cleanest KFC I have ever been too. We went on Wednesday July 3rd at 930 at night. Jason was the man who took our order. He really made our experience the best. He was polite and gave great suggestions. Was honest about the selection the had for that time of night and why it was lacking. (Even though we didn't need on bone chicken) we were never rushed about the lobby closing either

You know, I've got to think of opening one of these joints 'cause it's the only business I can think of where service is not required and the house is always packed.

My recent trip here was most amusing: I ordered an egg and cheese croissant and received it with sausage. When I checked my receipt I saw that I was charged for sausage. When I requested a change, the woman who rang me up seemed to no longer understand my English. I requested the help of the manager, who upon entering into dialog with my cashier, became hostile and said I should just eat the sausage sandwich. I explained that I did not eat pork [which seemed to annoy him further] and would like a new sandwich with just egg and cheese. He complied with my request but was again chagrined when I requested a refund for the overcharged price. His response was, "It's just a few pennies".  I looked at the menu on the wall and could see that it clearly was more expensive with the sausage; about a dollar more. When I laughed and said it's, "more than a few pennies", some other customers behind me laughed as well and demanded they refund my price. This only resulted in more chaos: 1 - the manager seemed to lack the requisite knowledge to perform a refund, sending him into further tailspins of rage. 2 - the woman cashier who helped me was so enthralled by this process, she was incapable of helping other customers who were getting quite annoyed by this time. I offered the manager a deal: an order of hash browns in exchange for the sausage and I'd happily go on my way. Seeing this as his only way out, he complied and I exited the store, swearing to never return.

I'll probably be back next weekend.

We went to Carl's Jr. because they started having beyond meat veggie burgers. When we got there no one was at the counter and the guy that was doing trash didn't seem to notice us. When he did come over "Jose" he didn't seem to speak much English and put things on our order we didn't ask for and over charged us. Finally he called his manager to try to figure it out. Finally had to give us cash back instead of credit. The place was dirty. Very hard to find a clean table. The Beyond burger was good but I won't go back to this location ever. If ICE goes here they will probably find several people to check in system. Don't eat here. Hope we don't get sick. Fries were cold. Meat quality in my husbands burger not good quality. Hope another location better.

I don't usually leave reviews but I feel it might save someone from making a big mistake. 

First of all, after asking twice for breakfast slider with bacon they gave me a wrong order. No surprise, after all, it is so difficult to tell apart sausage from bacon.

Secondly the hash-browns tasted like they were soaked in chemicals. Even now after couple of bites my stomach is flipping. 

Lastly, even the bread was dry like it's been sitting there with an open bag for a week. 

Do yourself a favor and avoid this establishment. 

This was my first and last time for sure!

Stopped here had a taste for chicken! Well here is the deal.. please  get out of your car don't be LAZY  even if its cold .. I decided  to go thru drive thru I WILL NOT do that again! My mental note to self ..you want good chicken go inside and order  so you can see what they are giving you. If you go thru drive thru know you are going to get old old chicken! Had enough of bad food. I 
won't be going back!

This is a Taco Bell / KFC.

To quote someone else's review, "Always dirty, always a wait."  My last time here was MY LAST TIME HERE.  We waited a good 15 minutes for our food, as we sat waiting, we began to notice how filthy the place was.  There was an old rug hanging on the wall next to the kitchen too.  What manager decided that was a good idea?  "Alright guys... the new entrance rugs are in.  Take the old ones, clean them the best you can and mount them on the wall next to the kitchen."

Asked for grilled chicken and was told, "Ummm.. like... we don't like ummm have grilled chicken I don't think."  She then went to the kitchen and returned to say that they indeed not have grilled chicken, despite the fact that I had ordered it in the past.

Got our food home, and my woman's tacos were literally (not figuratively) half filled.  I mean, it would have taken malicious EFFORT to pull off how little ingredients were in these things.

Never again.

I like stopping in to this location for lunch! The service moves fairly quickly and it is always fresh! I normally get a steak bowl, light on the rice with black beans, all the veggies, hot sauce, cheese, sour cream and guac. Perfect every time! I'll be coming back!

This place is DEFINITELY not better than Popeye's chicken! I They didn't get my husband's order right, I felt the biscuits were overpriced and the chicken little sandwich was VERY hard!! I won't be returning!

This place is awful! They made us wait in line for 10 minutes, and then came to the window and told us they were out of biscuits......  as if this wasn't bad enough, I made a comment on how sad the other drivers in line would be.... to my astonishment the guy leans out the window and says, " oh they didn't order any, just yal...."  Never again will I put my dinner on the line at this Store.... Good bye 4eva!

P.S. we ordered Grilled chicken and got Original Recipe too... terrible!

Walked in at 8:40 for dinner. The music is so loud the staff couldn't hear me ordering and loaded up my burrito even though I like them a little smaller and was trying to tell them to give me half. (I will pay full price, but I'm very pregnant so I can't eat a full bowl). Completed my order and ran to the bathroom while my husband finished ordering his. 

The bathroom is easily in the top 5 dirtiest public bathroom I've ever been in. It smelled and looked like someone peed on every surface. I just held my breath and washed my hands thoroughly. Ew. 

When my husband and I took our seats he said he struggled to get everything to our table alone but when he asked for a tray he was told they were all dirty. Two and a half strikes. I was hopeful the food might still be good even though everything else was neglected. 

I dig into my sofritas bowl and it is completely inedible. The rice is so undercooked it's crunchy in my mouth and the red onion in the pico is sliced so large it's bigger than my fingernails. Though my husband encouraged me to go back in, this combo is what I wanted-- apart from waiting for them to re-prep everything in my burrito at this hour, I am not going to have my needs met tonight. 

I will not be returning to this location. The worst part is that I have some medical testing tomorrow and need to fast after dinner. I was really hoping for something filling before I can't eat again for 15 hours.

For fast food, this place has good eats at reasonable prices with ample choices. Beer too. Several choices of protein: two kinds of beef; chicken; and carnitas or pork. Dress your tacos, Burros (too big to be called "burritos"), or bowl with a variety of cheeses, beans, salsas, veg and so on. As they say at Chipotle, "Our Menu Isn't Long, But It's Long On Choices."  WIP

Why is the line always long? Why? Please someone tell me the problem. Anyways, go here and get the classic hot wings with a medium macaroni and cheese. Then order a bowl with chicken; extra chicken, extra corn, and cheese, without gravy. Yeah boy, talking about chile. Ignore the customer service if you can, if not... don't go

Who doesn't love KFC? - Especially when you have delightful service and a clean location.  Most fast food places are what they are.  You're not going there for the culinary arts, but because a craving needs to be satisfied.  You can bet that with this location, you'll get your fried chicken, your wedges and a smile.  In the event that you have to wait, staff offered us a biscuit to tide us over until our order was ready.  I would definitely recommend this location highly.

This location is typically so-so anyway but today they scored a zero so I'll give it a net 2.

DUMB decision on not honoring Whopper coupons on the new Incredible Whopper (fake meat).  The Buy One Get One clearly states it is for a Whopper but is no more specific than that.  Fine print of course says free one must be of equal or lesser value.  THAT alone indicates that the offer is not restricted to one exact version.  The new one IS a Whopper.  So if I'm willing to pay the up charge for fake meat, why not honor the coupon.  You ARE trying to get people to try these, aren't you??

As for the up-charge, it't only $1 but somehow that seems excessive.  Surely they got very favorable pricing on the fake meat given what a coup this is for the Impossible guys.  I get that there are costs to roll it out but that's business.  If you really want people to do more than try it once don't up-charge more than either patty, or probably both together, costs you in the first place.

I was happy to see them give this a try but so far execution is a FAIL.  I left without purchasing anything.

BTW, I've had an Impossible burger elsewhere and it's perfectly edible IMO unlike many early veggie burgers, but still tastes like meat with lots of filler.  Probably not so noticeable in something like a Whopper with a thin patty and lots of other stuff, but I can't swear to that since they wouldn't honor a BOGO coupon.  :)  But it's all good.  I've been meaning to check out the latest offerings from Dublin (Ohio), so I'll see you at Wendy's.
Slow, dirty, gross. Got a steak salad here, the steak was hard as a rock and the guacamole was way brown and hard- which is an anomaly.

I remember when I had to drive all the way to NY or MD just to get a taste of the delicious Chipotle burrito.  But no more!  

There are a lot of people who'll walk in and say "Oh it's just like Qdoba."  I say, you my friend, have broken tastebuds.  Sure it looks the same, has the same format, but the meat!  The meat at Chipotle is cooked sous vide which allows for the most tender, flavorful meat.  (If you don't eat meat, then you have very little say in food quality.)

I've never had a problem, and they are more than willing to heap on as much of anything I want.  Their price is soley based on the meat you choose, and the rest is essentially free.  I like to get a Bol Barbacoa and Steak mix.  (Bols allow for more heaping.)  I get all of the toppings with all 4 salsas, extra "hot" sauce, and extra cilantro. I wish they had more bottled hot sauces to choose from, but the green tabasco mixed with smoked chipotle is really good.  Plus, my bol is so heaped with food that I can take it home for a second meal.

Great food with this chain however this one must have been told to buckle down on portions. I ordered steak tacos and one scoop of steak was spread across all three tacos. One taco literally only four cubes of steak on it.

I asked if I could have the more meat and was told I would be charged double. My tacos were able to be rolled up like little cigarettes from the insane lack of toppings provided (cheese included).

Just confirmed my loyalty to Qdoba going forward. Plus there I get free Guac.

You know, I normally have a good experience at chipotle, I love their food, but when my dad went in and ordered for the family, all the people behind the counter got PISSED at my dad for "not letting us know ahead of time" when he was only ordering 5 bowls and 2 kids meals. The staff was NOT helpful and messed up all of our orders. How do we go from a Steak bowl with white rice and cheese, to a CHICKEN BOWL WITH BROWN RICE, LETTUCE, BLACK BEANS, AND HOT SALSA?!!?! WHERE DID THE LETTUCE COME FROM?!?!

Fast and up to the normal Chipotle standards.

My husband is a huge Chipotle fan. That makes me a huge Chipotle fan, because if he is eating here that means he isn't buying Taco Bell/Wendy's/McDonalds/Burger King/Chick fil a, ok you get it. This may still be fast food, but at least it is generally healthy! 

Even though it is a small parking lot, we've never had problems finding parking. Granted, we usually go when the other shops surrounding the Chipotle are closed, so I can't speak to lunch time. It can be 9 p.m. at night and there will still be a line! Luckily, they have this down to a science so the line moves fast.

My husband always gets a double steak/chicken burrito bowl with a tortilla on the side and chips. He loves him some hot sauce, but if we're sharing he will get that on the side as well. (Most of the time he has them slather it on so he doesn't have to share with me.) 

Just from a taste standpoint, I literally would not be able to identify one Chipotle from another. Honestly, I see this as a good thing. The point of chains is that you always know what to expect.

I like the food here, I think it is a decent price, so I'd have to say I'm a fan.

Yes, agree with the other reviews, they are always out of ingredients and don't seem to care , I'm sure it's a busy location but tonight it was a mess and they were out of chips, corn, cheese, chipotle tobasco, fajita veggies and the meats were looking sad. The steak was chewy..guac was good though. Needs a manager!

I've never met a burrito bowl that I didn't like...until today. I stopped in five minutes after this location opened this morning, so imagine my surprise that my burrito bowl to-go included barely warm carnitas and barbacoa, both of which also had a dry, stringy texture.  These meats were definitely not prepared today. My guess is they sat in the fridge for awhile, and the staff had about two seconds to warm them up.  I don't mind leftovers -- in fact, repurposing food is responsible. But don't serve it to me cold when I'm expecting it to be close to piping hot. 

This is my first time visiting this location, and probably my last time, because I'm not a big fan of paying $15 for room temperature fast food. Although the burrito bowl was disappointing, the service was good. The three ladies that assisted me were all pleasant, but service alone won't convince me to spend my money there again.

I love love love me some White Castle burgers and it was a treat for me this weekend since we do not have them in Atlanta. I ordered the double cheese burger with chicken rings and fries. I also tired a chicken slider and that is why I took off a star. It was horrible! And I also did not like the fat free honey mustard they put in the bag. Other than that the burgers were still very good.
Love the new app for ordering etc, food waiting for us when I arrive. Try the new soy based protein option, Sofritos.

KFC was giving away 1 piece of their new GRILLED chicken on Monday, I didn't make it in till Tuesday and I wasn't expecting it to be all that great - it is KFC, but I was surprised! The new grilled chicken is awesome! It has a smokey backyard grilled taste! I guess KFC took my advice and did something to reinvent themselves as I suggest in my other review! 

It was $11.00 for 2 - 2 piece grilled chicken, 2 sides, and 2 biscuits! Who knew two people could eat dinner for $11 bucks!

Drive thru staff was super friendly.

Ah, this is one my favorite fast food chain out there. I have recently taken an interest in nutrition and health documentaries (Forks Over Knives, Fed Up, GMO OMG, etc.) I believe Chipotle has the least amount of processed foods compared to most fast food joints. In fact, they now advertised no GMO in their ingredients. The only thing that has a bit too many artificial ingredients is probably the tortilla skin, but they announced on their website that they are going more natural and less artificial on that soon as well. 

The line can get long during peak hours sometimes, but this can be easily avoided by ordering online or using the phone/app beforehand. 

Since this is close to my work, this is my staple and my go to if I am (or we are) not sure what to eat. I enjoy getting a burrito bowl and splitting it into two meals. Also, you can get extra sides, such as corn salsa or tortilla skin, if you ask.

Overall, definitely recommend trying Chipotle if you have not tried it before. All of their meats/sofritas are flavorful with spices.

Hi I'm Tony & I'm in the restaurant business with 4 locations. So stop by the Golf Links rd location because the wife love KFC, anyways I waited more than 30 minutes and was next in line, the staff was not trained on a register. Then more people come in and the still can't take an order. Like other reviews the same has been my expieriance, AVOID place this terrible service, dirty & yes they had no chicken on my visit prior to 8/11/19 All the tables,floors,and work stations. They were out of extra crispy. But by that time I was so blown away that I went from KENTUCKY 2 LOISIANA chicken that is. The place is a joke I'd have given less than a 1if I could but it's still the Colonel Recipe. Good luck Thanks for reading

Went there at 930 at night. But we were told we could only get 8 pieces of chicken and no tenders.  Because they didnt have enough and to late make any more. Lots of cars in the drive thru though. I guess they weren't getting anything either.

Every time I go to Chipotle.. I usually know what to expect.. decent mexican food that is also quick. I emailed some time ago about this restaurant, because they served disgustingly stale chips and I thought since my complaint before things may have improved at this chipotle location. Unfortunately, it has not gotten any better.  Today, I went to this store and I ordered a bowl. The restaurant was pretty busy, but the workers were particularly cranky. When the woman asked what I wanted on the salsa station, I kindly asked for corn salsa, sour cream, guac and lettuce. She didn't smile and just slopped on the smallest amount of guac into the bowl (picture attached below). As I was going to the register, the person working there also just took my card, said nothing and handed it back to me. No thank you or even a smile. Additionally, when I got home (which is a 5 minute walk as I live on city line ave as well), the steak and rice were cold. Overall, I was pretty disappointed in chipotle again. I wanted to give this location another try, but it seems like I am more often disappointed than not... and it feels like I wasted $13.00. It definitely is quick fix for mexican food, but with the experience with the stale chips the last time and now the poor customer service.. it makes me not want to return.

The line at 5:30pm is ridiculously long!  Probably spent 20 minutes in line to place our order.  The line never got shorter while we ate inside, so not sure how long the long line actually lasts.  This location is located adjacent to SLU dorms and apartments, so lots of college kids eating here.  The first worker we encountered was having a difficult time comprehending what we (and the people in front of us) were ordering, so I'm thinking he was the reason for the slow down.  White rice?  You mean brown rice?  You want both rices?  Maybe the first person in the assembly line needs to have the best training, and the new guy can be the one asking about cheese and sour cream.

The bright side of going during the busing dinner rush: all the food was fresh!  We had brand new steaming hot rice, perfectly cooked medium rare juicy steak, and right off the pan peppers/onions.  If you are so inclined, you can ask for cilantro to be added to your burrito/bowl (they have some chopped up in the back that they will add for free if you ask).
Don't go to this location if you actually want food in a timely fashion or you don't want to be treated badly for wanting your money back after being lied to about wait time. ALWAYS out of chicken

Had to try the new chicken donut sandwich!
Was actually very good, nice hot donuts and juicy chicken tender covered in a hot glaze!

I'm so glad this place is near where I live.  It's one of the few places where I can get a healthy breakfast.  They offer so much more than doughnuts.  If I want to be bad and have a doughnut I can but most of my visits are for the unsweet tea and lo-cal breakfast options.  Their unsweet tea is addictive.  My favorite breakfast is the Turkey sausage and egg wake up wrap at under 300 calories.  Sometimes I add on the hash browns.  They are seasoned differently than any others I've had and I love them.  

They have great lunch options also.  It may not be as low cal or healthy as my favorite breakfast but I love the chicken salad croissant.  There are many mornings that the drive-thru line is pretty long but they get everyone quickly served.  The line is never long inside if you are in more of a hurry.  Everyone is always friendly.

Well.. it is KFC.  

The chicken was hot and not greasy.  The mashed potatoes were... well potatoey?    Good cole slaw... the service was what you'd expect from a KFC.  Nice enough to get me my food quickly so I could eat and get on my way.  I love KFC fried chicken, so unless there is something out of the ordinary -- it's a winner winner chicken dinner (or lunch in this case) in my book.
I tried the impossible whopper. It was amazing. I couldn't tell the difference between the impossible  whopper and the regular one!!

Typical chipotle. Got my typical steak burrito bowl.  It didn't seem as huge as usual-- I was able to easily finish it, which is not usually the case.  There weren't line when we were here, it was fast.  It satisfied my chipotle craving, but not my favorite location.
BK is trying to improve customer service. .. I was asked my name to "better serve" me. Whopper not hot...barely warm :(

For fast food after midnight, it was excellent! All fresh and hot! The BBQ brisket slider and fried pickles! This southern girls fav fried food!

Kudos staff!!
My entire family loves Burger King and the famous whopper. It is a family place for sure
My bad, Didn't read the reviews before I paid this place is lame how can you run out of chicken When that's in the name of the business

For me, going to this KFC has almost always been a real -------- show, either with slow drive ups, their being out of something I wanted, or getting tiny pieces of chicken when I was able to place an order.

Their TV ads show their eight piece bucket brimming with chicken, but the last eight piece bucket I bought was the one pictured below, in 2015. I've since seen fit to avoid the place.

So, Wednesday night, July 24, 2019, when the wife sent me out to get one of their Taco Bell five dollar boxes, I knew I'd be in for a real "treat." 

The drive up line wasn't moving so I parked and went into the lobby. It was 8:55 p.m. (their posted closing time 9:00 p.m.)  Yet the manager(?) came from a back door to usher me out, telling me "The lobby was closed," and locked the door behind me.

I went someplace else.  NEVER again.

My son and I had lunch here this past Saturday. He had a Vegetarian Burrito and I had a Steak Burrito. We were very pleased with our choices. I was not able to eat the entire Burrito and had half the following day for lunch. The servers were friendly and efficient in preparing our food. The place was clean. We will return.

This location is just horrible.  They give you very small portions (not filling up the bowl), the food isn't fresh at all, and the employees don't want to keep the items fully stocked.  I have came here a few times thinking maybe there were just off days but they have been consistently horrible here.  Today they were out of chicken. The two servers looked around all confused. A third one rolled her eyes and then went to cut the chicken up, taking her sweet time.  You'd think the chicken was fresh and would be warm, but boy was I wrong.   They were out of almost so many things: carnitas, chicken, and brown rice. The guac did not look fresh either, even though it looked untouched. It was slightly brown so it was probably out for awhile.  I will not come back here anymore.

I frequently went to this chipotle on the weekends. It's clean, employees are always welcoming, and the line moves fast. The food is of good quality like any other chipotle location. I especially love the burrito bowls with carnitas or steak. There is plenty of parking and seating inside and outside. Good place to stop if you're running errands in the Elmwood shopping center.
I go here for lunch a lot, Get the $5 fill up, Chicken always fresh. Very popular at lunch.

I know I'm eating at Dunkin donuts so I should not expect much.  Some mornings you're in a rush and you think to yourself "I walk by that dunks every morning, maybe I'll treat myself.". About one of the least appetizing things is watching them screw up your order and then fix it by piecing another order together.  That's what happened to me.

I ordered egg-white, turkey sausage on a multigrain bagel.  I wasn't trying to be healthy, that's what they had.  I guess I can't complain too much because it is Dunkin Donuts, but try a little for pete's sake.  If you mess up the order and put my egg-white turkey sausage cheese concoction on a flatbread, just politely let me know and remake it.  No biggie.  Don't scrape it off the flatbread, put it on a bagel and then hand it to me.  If I next ask you why you could not just make me another one, don't pretend you don't understand english.

Skip this place.  Like other things in the gallery (from what I've heard), this place is trash.

Tried this place again even though I had a bad experience the last time with the dollar  tacos  pulled in the drive-through ordered four regular whoppers and four impossible whoppers and one more impossible whopper with cheese only .I'm not kidding if I had to repeat my order eight times ?? I asked the lady if she had any assistance to help her and after no response she still asked me what my order was again ??? I just can't believe that this Burger King cannot get management that will make the process fast and convenient.

Chipotle is like gourmet fast food for us college kids! Skip the greasy fries and burger, or the boring deli sub. Grab a chicken burrito bowl, add rice and black beans, slap some guac and salsa verde... my golly my parents would be thrilled to know I'm eating healthy AND organic! 

There are different varieties of meals a person can "customize" at Chipotle. They have anything for burritos, salads, tacos, burrito bowls (burrito minus the flour tortilla), and a new one to me, quesadillas. Your meat choices range from beef, chicken, barbacoa (sp. shredded beef), and carnitas (raised pork) as well as black or pinto beans. If you're a pansy, you can opt for tomato, corn, or green salsa - but if you're hardcore like me, you can get red chili salsa that would have your tummy grumbling an hour after ingestion (you didn't hear it from me, kay?). 

Don't get the misconception that they're trying to portray an authentic Mexican fast food - because it's far from it. It's the yummy modern approach to convenient food that StL needs, and I will go as far as saying it's better than their *competitor*.

I ordered a little Chicken Sandwich. It comes with mayonnaise and pickle. I Said no mayonnaise when I ordered it. About 10 seconds later- the woman comes back and says "OK little chicken sandwich with mayonnaise and pickle." And I said "NO I want no mayonnaise! I don't like mayonnaise but I love the pickle". Drive-through woman says "OK. No mayonnaise"....weelll Can I tell you how mad I was when I got home. Looking forward to my sandwich and saw that there was no pickle just mayonnaise! I was looking forward to this damn thing all day. I should've checked before I left. Minimum wage minimum work,  I guess. I guess if you order from this place you better check your food because there's a good chance it's wrong no  matter how simple
How do you mess up something I told you at least twice how I wanted it. Simple. No mayonnaise. Told them at least twice. Threw the sandwich in the trash

This is probably the most consistently bad Chipotle I've ever been to. The recipes are never followed to Chipotle standards, particularly the brown rice, sofritas and guac. The beans always taste old if you come in before noon, the cheese is in clumps, and sofritas are dry. Today, half of the guac we ordered was just the avocado peel. I only had to bites today before leaving and throwing away the rest. I love Chipotle, but this particular location needs to up their consistency.

I ordered the 12 piece meal (thighs and drumsticks only) with cole slaw, macaroni and cheese, and mashed potatoes. The meal also came with quite a few biscuits.
The fried chicken was delicious. It was tender and perfectly crispy. The sides were pretty standard and unimpressive. All the sides tasted artificial and what you would expect from a fast food place. 
The wait was horrendous. I ordered through drive through and it still took over 20 minutes to get my order. It wasn't exactly "fast food".
I would recommend ordering ahead if you plan on getting something from this location because chances are the wait will take some time even for a quick drive through.

Chipotle isn't my favorite because I've had food poisoning in the past, but I thought I would try it because I heard the salad is a decent Keto option. This was my first time at this location and it was the same as always. Food was pretty good and it was fast. I got the steak salad no beans no rice, extra cheese, some sour cream, a bit of red salsa and guacamole. Salad dressing was ok, but unnecessary because of the guacamole, sour cream and salsa. Guacamole really made this dish... I wouldn't skip it if you aren't getting rice, beans, etc. overall, I was generally pleased with it as a Keto friendly option.

I was happy with my burrito bowl here. This location didn't commit the cardinal sin of being skimpy with steak, so very thankful for that. Service was fast and the flavor was on point, too. Definitely a solid option if you're looking for a quick bite.

I was reluctant to order the chicken wings I was craving. Only because the last time I ordered a full bucket, the taste just wasn't the same. I went ahead and ordered, and they were great! no sauce like I wanted, hot, here in 35 minutes. They are pricey because they add tax, service fee, delivery fee and tip. 30$ something for 24 wings. I'll order again.

I came in to order 2 chicken quesadilla kids meals, exactly the same. The First girl didn't wait to listen to me and before I was done talking, she made the quesadilla and rolled it up in foil when she came back I let her know the other half of my sentence was requesting chicken. Once that was fixed she filled the boxes with rice and beans. The next lady looked at me, and slid my box to the cashier. Didn't ask if I wanted sour cream, salsa or anything. She then proceeded to ignore me and stare through the front window, after about 10 seconds of awkward silence I asked if I could get some salsa. She said I can only get two, and she's going to charge me extra. This was weird to me since at this point she hasn't given me any. I told her which two I would like and she sucked her teeth and rolled her eyes. Then told the cashier I had two steak meals instead of two kids meals, thankfully he only charge me what I ordered. I think she was the manager so I figured I should just tell corporate on her. When I got to the register the cashier was kind but when I asked for his co workers names he said that it's "confidential" and they don't wear name tags. I asked for his name and he said he can't disclose that either. Once I got in the car I looked at my receipt and it had his name right there! SABASTIAN! These people are liars and rude. Very disappointing experience.

I was highly disappointed that I went to the KFC and was advised that they were out of chicken and all they had was mashed potatoes and corn. How do you run out of what you sell and you are still open? Just a question!

I only came here to try an Impossible Burger which, by the way, is delicious. I haven't been to a BK in YEARS. I'm very impressed. It was clean and modern. The staff were very courteous. The food was prepared to order which took a few extra minutes but was totally worth it. BK is back in my life now.

So this is the worst thing ever.I hate this place because the biscuits were not cooked. The chicken was dry, trash all over the place and the dustbin was leaking and no one cared. Never going back here
Drive thru team member young blonde male- very unpleasant. Also they ran out of chicken...? Quoted us it would be 10 minutes...it was 30.

Doing a delivery, stood by pickup and wasn't even acknowledged for 20 minutes, so I sat down and someone who wasn't even delivering stood there and still wasn't acknowledged. Nobody in the place gas their food, looking at the reviews the quality of the food is terrible (hopefully that doesn't reflect badly on me) and I can't imagine ever having a good experience at KFC. I've never eaten there when the old wasn't like 6 weeks old. A woman's food was left at the pickup window without even calling out what it was or who it was for and it was only because she overheard the cashier's saying it was for some lady and her kid that she even got her cold food at all. Finally got acknowledged at the thirty minute point, guess it's their policy to ignore people who've already spent the money and focus on the ones who still need to sink their cash into the shit hole. God I wish they could make food well because I love chicken much more than burgers. Every KFC I've been to has bad food, this one also has long waits and poor customer service.

I dealt with Mohinder and had exceptional service on a very large order of chicken.  I needed an order for 11 buckets by 10:00 in the morning and though the restaurant wasn't open at that time she she didn't hesitate to help me out.  She went in early, prepared the chicken and packaged it well to help maintain the heat.  She then had another 11 buckets ready for me to pick up about another hour later.  The chicken was hot and enjoyed by all.  What fantastic customer service.  Thanks Mohinder you helped to make our function a great success.

The guy in front of me had a large order which gave me time to refamiliarize myself with the menu. I think it's been two years since I had Chipotle and that was in California.
