Thankfully the menu hadn't changed much and carnitas were available. I got a bowl, basic which kinda confused the employees "no sauce, no sour cream?" However, I did forget to get onions and peppers but the guacamole made up for it in flavor.
-Quick service
-courteous 
-easy to pay and leave
-store itself was in good order for the amount of people in there (trash not overflowing,no random spills on the floor)
I'll return.

Have eaten here plenty of times regularly, yesterday was our first experience getting catering from them. Will absolutely do it again! Very easy to order, food was great as always, and they really prep you for everything for your party. They supplied plenty of napkins, utensils, serving utensils, etc. We got steak and chicken and it was great, they give you plenty of all the extras (beans, veggies, cheese, and yes, plenty of guac!). We ordered for 20, had 17 guests and it was a perfect amount. Had some leftovers, which I am extra happy about! The chef there also surprised us and added some new type of spicy queso with our sides, which was awesome. Overall, great food (as always) and a great experience with the catering.

I definitely won't be ordering from this location again, and this is after giving them multiple chances. I've been shorted 3 bags of chips, my sons apple juice from his kids meal, and paid for a large guacamole and received a small. After all that, I still opted to try ordering through them once more on DoorDash. All my items were in the bag, but I was absolutely appalled to open my bowl and see RAW chicken. Nope. Never again. Not to mention the carnitas in my son's kids meal that looked like wet cat food.

I try to like Chipotle, I really do. I remember my first experience with their brand in Denver CO, I was hooked. Years of consistency and quality (anti-biotic free, fresh, local) food and generous portions kept me coming back.

Yet, Chipotle is suffering from an identity crisis, a customer service crisis and a management crisis at just about every location I've been to in recent days.

3 out of the 4 counter staff were rude and looked ticked that we even had bothered to enter the building. The ladies ordered their usual burrito bowls, me the crispy steak tacos.  My taco builder either was in need of glasses or a bit more corporate training-barely any of the steak, pico and salsa made it into the crispy shell.  Seriously, he just dumped it on top with no concern that it make it into the shell. 

At the table during my first bite I noticed that the steak was at well below standard serving temperature-even cold in some places. I certainly feared getting some food borne illness from improper storage.  Guac was $2.05 for rather smallish scoop-if you look at their spoons you'll notice they are smaller while prices are higher. (Thank you minimum wage increase-passed on to the customer)

The restaurant looked like it hadn't been cleaned since the lunch rush and the trash cans were over flowing.

Dear Chipotle, I think we are done. I've tried and honestly, you keep letting me down. You are not who you once were-far from it. It seems as you've slid into middle age you've let yourself go and we're all sad, you were so beautiful.

Drive through to get their $20 bucket special. I ordered original. Got home and all the chicken was burnt, oil tasted burnt.  Very greasy.  No quality control.  Sides were fine. It will be awhile before we go back

I've never written a Yelp review but this situation deemed worthy of one. I've been to this location twice and I've ordered the KFC bowl which is supposed to be made with popcorn chicken. Both times were a fail. The first time I went there was about 3 months ago, I ordered the KFC bowl and when I got home I realized that there was no fried chicken in the bowl let alone popcorn chicken. It was greasy shredded chicken thigh. They also had me pull into a parking space and wait 10 minutes for the "fried chicken"....when they didn't even make it with fried chicken. I was annoyed but blew it off as a fluke. Tonight I went through the drive thru and ordered a KFC bowl again. This time they STILL didnt make it with popcorn chicken but instead made it with chicken strips that were just ripped up (again having me pull into the parking lot and wait 5 or so minutes) Apparently they were out of lids for the KFC bowls and they wrapped it up in plastic wrap, making a giant mess of gravy everywhere. They forgot to give us our mac and cheese that we had ordered as well. When we called to see how we could resolve this, they didn't sound apologetic but instead sounded irritated with us. They did however agree to give us our mac and cheese if we came back. I decided to drive back and get the mac and cheese. When I got there, I spoke with who I can only imagine was the manager on duty. I explained to her that both times I've gone there, I had ordered the KFC bowl specifically for the popcorn chicken and neither time did it come with that. When I asked how we could ensure that we get the popcorn chicken with our meal next time, she firmly told me that "they only had ONE deep fryer that they were working off of and I would have had to wait 20 minutes." First off....no. I've worked in fast food before and it doesn't take 20 minutes to deep fry chicken. Secondly, there were no other customers there! So what on earth was taking so long? Third and finally, I've been a manager at many different establishments and if that was actually the case, the manager should have told us that the popcorn chicken was going to take a long time to cook and should've given us the option of waiting "20 minutes" or asked if we wanted different chicken. Instead, I got attitude from someone who didn't want to ensure her customers' satisfaction. I got no apology and no compensation. Even when I asked if I should call ahead next time if I wanted popcorn chicken, she told me that it takes too long so they just do this instead because she "didn't think I wanted to wait 20 minutes". Sooooo I guess this location just doesn't serve popcorn chicken guys. Sorry, you are shit out of luck. Because it takes TOO long. Because they are just TOO busy. Oh yeah.. did I mention that 3 hours before their closing time they had the OPEN sign shut off and all the chairs were upside-down on the tables? I'm pretty sure these KFC employees were trying their best to make the public believe that they were closed so they could get out of there and spend their hard earned minimum wage paychecks on cheap booze and hookers. Ok, that last part was mean (funny...but mean) and I'm sure they aren't spending their money on hookers. But I'm just annoyed and if this situation had been handled with any integrity at all on their part then I wouldn't be spending my night writing this review. I'm not a complainer and I'm a pretty easy going person. I don't expect orders to be perfect or even correct every time. We're all human and we all make mistakes. But laziness and sheer negligence can either make or break a business. Instead of fighting me on how long popcorn chicken takes to cook, a simple apology and maybe offering me some free dessert or something would have sufficed. I don't know these people personally and this is not a petty attack on their character as individuals. But if you want your business to thrive and your customers to come back with smiles, just be sure to give them what they order. If you can't do that, be sure to make that clear to them and offer them sincerity and some compensation. As for me, I'll not be returning to this location. I think I'm going to get into the popcorn chicken industry and make the best goddamn chicken you've ever tasted.....And I promise it won't take 20 minutes ;)
Ordered my meal with gravy, they gave me what I think is supposedly gravy, at least it looks like it, but it tasted like water with a hint of chicken flavour...tldr Flavourless watered down gravy.

I ordered the Sofritas bowl with lots of toppings (corn salsa, pico, sour cream, fajita veggies, cheese, lettuce, etc.) and it was great! I had to mix the bowl before eating because the rice was at the bottom and the veggies on top, but it was very saucy and flavorful! I love the spice and all the ingredients and definitely a large portion--I was able to eat it for dinner and lunch!

This is the worst BK I've ever been to ever. First time I went as I pulled around to the drive-thru, all the employees were out back smoking dope and scurried back inside like roaches when they saw me. I ordered a Whopper Jr. and the buns were stale, the meat was gray and cold and it only had lettuce and ketchup on it. Gross.
I decided to give them another chance a while later and ordered a Crispy Chicken sandwich. Once again, the bun was very stale and the chicken itself was so dry. It was like they threw a day old piece of chicken back into the oil. Portions of it were black. I got so sick the next day. Avoid this dump. It needs to be bulldozed.

This is the worst location I have been to; I have better service at a Kindergarten luncheon. No utensils were provided for my mashed potatoes,my chicken was original when I very clearly asked for crispy. The only thing correct was the box that it came in.

This is my neighborhood KFC and it's usually a hit or miss.     However last night was the worst.   For $42 my family got cold bucket chicken that was saturated in grease,  the macaroni and cheese was so cold it had no creaminess left, the biscuits were stale and we all were sick after the meal!  we spent the night in each of the bathrooms so thanks KFC $42 for a terrible meal and food poisoning

I know it's a chain,  but location and staff training makes a big difference.

They have plenty of staff on hand, but none seem to know when to take over with the fillings.  Twice doing the process, one person asked me what I wanted, only to hand it off to the next person in line that proceeded to ask me the same question.

They just added the carne asada option, which I chose.  Once I said it though, I was asked/told several times that its $9.  After the second time, I just said " You know it's only 50 cents more than steak?"  Every protein has a different cost; so I get it, but saying it 2x?  It's a scene straight out of Sebastian Maniscalco bit (Google him and Chipotle).

Overall it was decent and plenty of parking.  Maybe a new location? Seems like the staff needs additional training or experience behind the line.

I received an email from Chipotle the other day advertising they have a new flavor, so I actually opened it today and read it, lol. 
 
The pix of this "new limited time flavor/ingredient" that they call "Carne Asada" looked so yummy my mouth watered!!!  It said it was their tenderest steak, cut into slices and seasoned with lime wedges and cilantro (2 of my favorite ingredients in Mexican Food!). 

So, of course I showed hubby and wrote a text message for him to take with him of what I specifically wanted so he can just read it to the person at the counter for our carry out order.  My son does the same and hubby decides what he wants while in line. (Hint: if you do this list ahead of time and just read it to them it makes the whole process there so quick and easy. If you don't know your options, just go online to their site and view the menu before heading over). 

Anyway, I ordered The Bowl with a Double Portion of the Carne Asada with a small portion of the cilantro lime brown rice and Fresh Tomato Salsa on the side to add in myself.  OMG!  It was off the charts of Yumminess!!  That Steak (Carne Asada) is 5 Stars all by itself!!  You have to try it while they carry it. I wish they'd put it on their permanent menu!!  Then, I added just a couple spoons of that fresh tomato salsa and I was in heaven. 

I gave hubby, Anthony and Catarina a taste and they all loved it & wished they had ordered it too. 

Anthony ordered the Salad Bowl with Chicken, Cheese, The Tomatillo Red Chile Sauce, Sour Cream & Guacamole and he hobbled the entire thing in minutes. 

Hubby ordered his usual order:  The Steak Burrito (not the new Carne Asada I had) with Brown Rice, Black Beans, Fajita Veggies and Sour Cream. He always enjoys it. 

I'll be posting pics so you can see for yourself. 

Again, I really recommend that Carne Asada and hope Chipotle decides to offer it as a regular item, rather than limited time!!

If do really wish one could put a zero star rating. 
An ill neighbor want KFC, so he gave me his cash and I went for him. I had to travel 10 miles to the store, whereas, there were other chicken store much closer. I waited in the drive up line for almost 15 minuets, then made it to the ordering speaker, only to be told, " We are only accepting credit and debit cards..."No cash."
"Really?" I said from my car. "You mean, I waited 15 minutes in line, only to be told I can't order because I have cash."
"Yes." she said.
I had to pull out of line, and drive back 10 miles, to a close chicken place...Church's Chicken, which he was just as pleased with.
If they were in that situation, shouldn't they have posted a sign letting all drivers know they weren't accepting cash because of (their Covid procedure)?

Like I said ZERO star rating.

This place is horrible.  It's definitely the slowest fast food place in town.  I've gone there once, maybe twice successfully as a delivery driver.  Tonight marked the fourth visit in a row in which I drove over there to pick up an order and had to leave 20 minutes later with no food, abandon the order, and drive off knowing I wasted half an hour trying to pick up chicken from a chicken place that just couldn't get organized enough to actually give up some of their chicken in a sale.  The people working there tonight never once greeted me or acknowledged my presence upon entering, delivery bag in hand.  I went in, waited in the lobby for five minutes, and I was completely ignored.  I went back to the car and considered my options.  I tried calling the restaurant from the parking lot, but of course they have hidden their phone number on Google from the public like shifty degenerates trying to avoid being served a notice.  I went back in and waited another ten minutes in the lobby.  Still I was completely ignored.  And that's why you had to wait forever for your dinner;  you know who you are.

I don't remember the last time I had KFC. The husband decided he wanted chicken from this location to go with some sides  I made. Let's see what happens... 

After about a 5 minute wait in the drive thru to place my order, another 3 minute wait to pay for my order ( maybe because they had to drop more meat since I ordered all white meat)...

10 minutes later total... The bag and bucket feel steaming hot so let's hope that the food is better than the reviews.

We got burritos Saturday night, and they tasted fine. I got carnitas as my meat and it was the bottom of the container... Clearly this was not a good sign as I spent my Sunday in the bathroom with food poisoning. I'm done with Chipotle. My fiance only ate a quarter of his barbacoa burrito, but then his dad finished it. He also was in the bathroom all day. So, if you want to projectile vomit corn, this is the place to go!

I ordered a carnitas bowl to go. I live 2 blocks away from this location and as soon as I got in my apartment I started eating my meal. STONE COLD rice and meat was also not heated. After all their problems with illness from bacteria, I decided not to continue eating my take out. Very disappointed but lesson learned. Step it up Chipotle or just close up.

Seriously this place NEVER seems to have pot pies. Idk why, but when I went to order they told me they had it. Pulled up to the window to pay, "ma'am I'm sorry it'll be a 20-25 min wait" and I said okay I'll pull into a spot and wait. Worker comes out, "sorry but our manager said we don't have crust to make the pies, would you like something else? No I came for the pot pies. I feel like the pies are like pre frozen or something so I'm not sure why they wouldn't have dough for the crust. Maybe some lame excuse to not bake more. 

I also got a $5 fill up box with tenders. The lady didn't get it and asked "can you repeat that" because she said grilled or crispy chicken i said no I wanted tenders. My mom went inside to get the money back for the pies we didn't get and I got my $5 box and was on my way. Get home and it's a thigh and leg when I SPECIFICALLY asked for crispy tenders. She even verified it with me the second time around when I was asked to repeat my order. I ALSO asked for buffalo sauce for what? MY TENDERS. Who eats regular fried chicken with buffalo sauce.. shouldn't that be a sign that I ordered tenders or something? 

They were also out of ALL diet drinks on one occasion. I can't drink anything that isn't diet soda because I'm a diabetic. When the food is correct, it's always good hence the 2 stars. But not having pot pies on 3 occasions already when I've been is just annoying. Kfc isn't the closest to me either and they should've kept the one on lapalco across from Taco Bell. Maybe it's a sign to make my own chicken pot pies.

Del taco is the king of cheap Mexican.  Way way way better than Taco Bell.  Their Menu is great!  59 cent tacos?  You bet!  Want an order of fries with your burrito?  You got it!  Want your burrito combo Macho sized?  Oh yeah!  Or instead of a taco maybe a hamburger?  They have it.  Cherry Coke on the fountain?  They got that too!

The food here is quite good for fast food and very inexpensive.  Good burritos and tacos, and the fries are a great touch.   Some of my favorites include the 99 cent Macho Bean and Cheese Burrito, the classic chicken burrito, and of course the standard hard shell taco.  

The service is hit or miss.  I have had some long waits here, but the food has always been pretty consistent.  I just wish they would open more locations in Saint Louis.

I honestly wouldn't even give this place a participation trophy. We came to the drive thru and ordered a $30 family fill up. The lady on the mic sounded annoyed while taking our order. We pulled up, paid, and were told to go park in front of the restaurant. Under 2 minutes had passed when an employee came out, confirmed our order, and had us on our way with our order in hand. When we returned home, we realized the gravy from the order was missing. However, my biggest problem I had with the order was one of the eight pieces of fried "chicken" was basically a breaded and fried bone. The chicken must have been on a hunger strike before the colonel sent him to the cage free chicken farm in the sky. I crushed the piece with my fingers and came to find there wasn't even any meat on the bone. See pictures. I firmly believe this wasn't a simple lack of attention to detail, as it was pretty obvious that this piece was made with no love......just remnants of the chicken's bitter hate and anguish. I don't know if the lady taking my order wanted to make sure my visit was terrible or a server shrugged and thought I wouldn't notice. They won't notice one less customer *shrug*.

My son, Anthony, has been eating Chipotle on and off for years but lately he's been ordering from this location often so it peeked my curiosity. 

So, My hubby and I decided to try finally try it with Ant's latest order especially since the Chipotle commercials have been running slit and it looked pretty good. 

While my son is more familiar with their food and their different ingredients, he ordered a burrito filled with a combo with lots of fillings that I've never had (and some never heard of), so I went to their website to explore. 

I chose the burrito filled with steak, brown rice, extra tomato salsa and extra sour cream as well as a side of Chips and queso. Hubby trusted me and ordered the same as he went personally and ordered in the store & brought everything home. 

OMG!!!  The burrito is humongous!!!!  I cut it in half bc I knew I'd never get thru all of that. But let me tell you how I was so surprised of each delicious bite!  (Can't wait til tomorrow when I eat the other half, lol!)

The chips were so crunchy and yummy alone with that hint of lime, then to dip into that queso....mmmmmm!  (I also took a little queso & put it on my burrito...yumminess!!!

It was so good, I now downloaded their app so maybe next time I'll try the in-app ordering. 

Looking forward to venturing out on this menu & trying more combos!!

Believe it or not, I've never been to a Dunkin Donuts before. My first experience here was in a drive-thru during a quarantine... not the best first impression. 

Even if there wasn't a quarantine going on the food would be any better. Squished greasy donuts and flat flavorless savory sandwiches, why do people love this place? That's being said there were two shining lights in the donut box. The veggie breakfast sandwich with turkey sausage, spinach and egg whites. This savory item had good flavor and wasn't super heavy. The Boston cream pie donut was soft and the cream inside wasn't overly sweet. 

During the quarantine there's almost no donut places open and even before COVID the donut selections in Mt. Juliet have been slim to none. I took a chance on this chain and I don't think I'll be back. Donut Time, I'm coming for

Out of chicken at 9:30. The person taking the orders told me they were out and that it takes 25 minutes to cook chicken and....well that's when I drove away and went to Taco Bell. If you aren't going to serve your product til 10 then close early.

As a fast food alternative, Chipotle is GREAT! 
If you are looking for great food, Chipotle is just MEH.

We stopped in for some lunch while shopping out in the 'burbs recently.
There was a short line but it moved rather quickly. It is sort of like a subway sandwich counter where they make it to order right in front of you with a selection of fresh ingredients, chicken, beef, and even vegetarian options...sound promising, right?

While the overall quality was fine, the flavors were just OK. I got a trio of tacos and my partner got a carnitas burrito. Maybe it was the toppings, or the fact that they didn't heat the tortillas very well, but my tacos were down-right cold and therefore disappointing.

I had never tried them before but had always heard great things about the company and the quality of their food. Sadly, I left disappointed in the experience and not sure if I would return....we shall see.

As a side note, I found the decor to be sort of unfinished, and, the bathroom sink handle was broken so it was hard to figure out how to wash your hands before eating...meh.

This particular location has terrible customer service. There seems to always be an issue. Today for example I go in and wait for my food I ordered online. I ordered a box of popcorn chicken and they tried to give me a small. When I corrected them and showed them the order on my phone they became rude and the manager was even rude. I work law enforcement all day dealing with rude people and people yelling at me. Don't need it from KFC workers. Never coming back and hopefully others start finding this out too.

As others have noted, this location always seems to be out of something. I'm not sure about you, but I'm not in the habit of calling Chipotle in advance to make sure they have carnitas, sofritas, or guac. And when strolling up to the entrance, there seems to always be a sign that reads, "Sorry we're out of X today." 

The line is always ridiculously long, and the staff don't seem to be properly trained to handle high volume. There is also inconsistency with the amount of each ingredient that is added to your bowl based on the server. I've actually had to ask for more veggies because they only threw on a couple of peppers. Don't be stingy, or at least be consistent so I know what product to expect! 

I love that they have a decent outdoor seating area, and the parking is obviously a big bonus since it's in Elmwood. Other than that, if you're really hungry, just be prepared to wait in a long line and possibly have to (almost shamefully) ask for more of something depending on who is making your food. Awkward.
This location is usually my favorite, HOWEVER, the last time I went, the sofritas made me sick! Sad to say it will be a while before I come back!

Jan2021 update: I enjoyed yesterday's bowl with riced cauliflower with my carne asada so much that I ordered it again today with carnitas; however, I suspect that they ran out because my to-go order had a side of guacamole and no cauliflower rice. Maybe next week I'll try again. 

Although I don't like paying an extra $2, the cauliflower rice is worth it. Limited time only so treat yourself.

There are times when the system breaks down... arrived at KFC around 7:15, one party ahead of me. Unbeknownst to me someone had just phoned in an 80 piece order just before I arrived. Consequently they were unable to meet the needs of the people ahead of me because they didn't have enough chicken. Fifteen minutes later when I finally was able to order, the line was out the door and people were restless. There has to be a better way to compensate when there is a large order. Moral of the story, KFC is not fast food, the question is, is it worth the wait.

One of the nicer Chipotle's I've been to in Philadelphia. Lots of seating, very clean, and nice ambient lighting.

Went here a little after 6pm. There was no line when I got there but by the time I sat down there was a line almost to the door. Seems like one of the popular restaurants out of all the choices in this plaza.

I got a carnitas bowl. Everything was perfect except the white rice was undercooked. I like brown rice but I avoid ordering brown rice when I go out since it's almost always undercooked because of how long it takes to cook it compared to white rice. But this time even ordering white rice didn't prevent me from getting undercooked rice. :( 


Everything was 5/5 stars otherwise but undercooked rice sucks
This KFC is notorious for wrong orders, over-cooked, and dry chicken, and a long wait to get the food.

Okay so I waited in line for 30 minutes (no joke) and when I got to finally order they didn't have lettuce. She never offered another solution to the problem or apologized. Why did I wait in line for so long if they didn't have a key ingredient??? How frustrating. THEN - she got my order wrong. I ordered carnitas in one bowl and she gave me carne asada in both (which I can't eat)!! Literally such a terrible experience. 

The picture is what my bowls looked like UNTOUCHED. I hadn't eaten at all and they look pitiful. 

if you're looking for good food or good experience or the confidence that they will have what they should, look elsewhere.

I come here quite a bit and it's usually really good. This is my first experience this this particular cashier and it wasn't that great. Shorted my order, I only got 1 sausage wake up wrap and I ordered 2, when I came back in to have them fix it he said he only charged me for one and wouldn't give me the second one I ordered. It's a shame, my business spends a lot of money here.

As a hungry college kid, in my hometown I used to grab a burger after the basketball games-when out team won it was buy one get one whopper free or half price or some such thing-I went b/c it was cheap and I was hungry.

So, when this location tore down the old facility and built the new I had an itch that needed scratching. I rolled in at the lunch rush, the fixtures and finish of the place are really great-the new model and set up look really nice. 

A pretty steady crowd was inside the building and cars were lined up in the drive thru. I ordered a #1 Whopper w/cheese and fries and a drink. The counter staff person had a bit of a difficult time getting my order correct-I wanted medium fries and a drink, her co-worker helped her and she got it figured out, I thought so until I ended up with two orders of fries-I didn't make a fuss-chalked it up to new staff-gave them a break.

The drink station and ketchup and napkins are all together so it can get a bit backed up when the lunch crowd stands in line waiting for the orders. The Ketchup cups are really small-be careful or you like me will look like you cut your fingers off and are in need of medical assistance-fortunately I got it under control and hands clean, I grabbed a two top table.

You go to BK for the flamed grilled flavor, and the upon the first bite I was transported back to my college days from the taste. As I continued to consume the burger I remembered that most burger kings whoppers have a very cool taste with the mayo, lettuce and tomato, it's overwhelming cool on first bite. This makes it necessary to have a more than just warm meat patty-at least in my opinion. Well, the patty on my burger tasted like it was flame broiled but it was not at all hot and honestly barely warm.  

(Ahh yes, it's coming back to me now) This is the BK I remember-a cool sandwich w/o the warmth and heat of the beef patty. I also noted that the pickles were strong-like overpowering strong. Not bad, just strong.

The fries-super hot and tasty and VERY SALTY.  I liked them best out of the whole meal....but man, they were salty.

Itch scratched, memory jogged.  I'm good BK, you do your thing and I'll do mine.  All the best to you with your new building and location.

I have a bone to pick with you Chipotle!! I only give 2 stars because your food is delicious.

I ordered Chipotle yesterday for pickup. Me being a dummy I forgot my mask, so I called and a worker was nice enough to run my order out. I really appreciated that.

I drove 20 minutes home to eat my food and when I got home, literally NOTHING in my order was right. Should I have checked it before we left? Maybe. But this isn't Mcdonald's and Chipotle has never messed up my order.

My boyfriend got his quesadilla which you'll see below and he loved it.

AS YOU CAN SEE IN THE PICTURE, I ordered a kids meal. 2 crispy tacos with steak, veggies, and cauliflower rice. I literally received a quesadilla (I don't think thats steak? Not sure) with rice, hot sauce, and sour cream. Not a single thing I ordered was right besides my kids chips and side of guacamole.

I don't really know what happened here. Did they put my label on the wrong order? Did some poor guy trying the quesadilla for the first time get a kids meal? I want to refuse to believe that someone was that lazy or wasn't looking and actually made that when they read mine. It HAD to be a mistake.

Luckily, I ate it and enjoyed it. Quesadilla for the win!!

However, this review gets low stars because for someone who is on a diet, is a picky eater, or has allergies- this could've been a serious issue. I was so upset at first I almost drove 20 minutes back but I realized it wasn't worth it and to just enjoy what I had. I wasn't charged for a quesadilla so might as well take advantage!

Chipotle, you are delicious, but please please double check your orders!

Chipotle is one of my favorite places to get food to-go because I know it will always be fresh, delicious, fast and priced reasonably. Today I placed an order from Chipotle on US 19 in Palm Harbor through their app for pick-up. When I walked up to the store, I was surprised to see that the door closest to the parking lot was locked with a sign on it indicating customers should use the main entry doors. I am not sure why this was done - I would think it would be better to have both doors working so that one could be used for entry and the other for exit to limit the need for guests to come in close contact with each other. Having only one door available for entry and exit forces guests to use the same door and run into each other when entering and exiting. It was nice to see that they were taking social distancing seriously by having small signs standing up on various tables indicating that they were not to be used. There was also a sign on the entry door stating that guests were required to wear face masks inside the store. Three small tables with umbrellas were available for outdoor dining too. 

Pick-up was a breeze (as usual), with my order bagged, ready and waiting for me on the shelf close to the register. I was able to check for my name and get my food without having to interact directly with any staff members - helpful during this pandemic. 

Now for the important part, the food! I ordered a burrito bowl with brown rice, carnitas, black beans and fresh tomato salsa. The oblong bowl was filled with so much food, I was able to save half for lunch tomorrow. The carnitas was cooked to a tender perfection, falling apart with only a fork. I was surprised that this time the carnitas did not have much flavor (compared to previous orders) and tasted as if someone had forgotten to season the meat. Luckily, the salsa made up for it and combining everything together made for a great meal. However, if this happened again, I would probably start ordering a different protein so I can enjoy the flavor I have come to enjoy from Chipotle. 

If this is your first visit to this Chipotle location, it is in a small building with AT&T store, close to US 19 (not in the main plaza). The building is close to the Boston Market restaurant. There is a small side street next to Chipotle (Coral Landings Blvd.) that connects to West Lake Rd., if you want to avoid making a U-turn on 19.

Burger King is great because it has the impossible burger. This location is not great because it's impossible to get a burger in under 10-20 mins. Currently sitting in the drive through and it has taken on average 8 minutes per car. When I pulled up, it took 2 minutes for someone to speak over the mic and ask what I wanted. Wouldn't leave this review but it has happened so many times now. This is the closest Burger King to work and it would be faster for me to drive to another one order there and move on then it would be to wait.
Just don't go here. Period. The chicken tenders we got were hard as heck and the bone in chicken was so gross I didn't even eat it

Everytime I come to this KFC,  they're always out of original recipe chicken. I told them I'd wait for a new batch(10 minutes). When it was ready, I was told that there were no breast for my 8 piece meal....2 legs and 6 thighs. No subsitutions on sides either. The employees and manager don't know the meaning of customer service.  Hope they're proud of their one and a half star rating, but I don't think they give a crap.

This is the worst Chipotle location I have ever been too. The staff and customer service are awful. Store and counter tops are unclean. The chicken and steak I ordered were both overcooked and dry. The rest of the ingrediants lacked flavor and overall freshness. Chips were stale. Additionally this location is always running out of food. On multiple occasions I have seen them completely close the store during peak business hours due to "running out of food". Chipotles Corporate Office would have a field day with this location.

The Walmart of fast food . The only reason I have been to BK is the impossible Whopper and Apple Pay . They have a newer coke machine, I got Sprite Zero because it doesn't have any sugar in it but they didn't have any ice I had to ask for it behind the counter. Both the workers and the customers were trashy. Probably won't be back

My one cooking deficiency is that I cannot make good fried chicken. I don't know what went wrong in my genes, but I can't. Therefore, we go out for it when the craving strikes. The great KFC location around the corner from us closed quite some time ago, but the family really wanted KFC, so we gave this location a try. We ordered, through the drive thru, the basic 8 piece family meal. We were told that they were out of mashed potatoes. My husband said, "Cancel the order then, because the mashed potatoes and gravy are my kids' favorite thing here." Then, the guy said, "Well, I guess I could make more, but it will take a few minutes". First, how does KFC run out of mashed potatoes and second, since they're obviously from a box, why was it so much trouble to add hot water and mix up a batch? Later, at home, the chicken had obviously been pulled from the oil, not drained, and dumped into the bucket. It was the greasiest fried chicken we have ever experienced. The skin was so drenched in grease that it was inedible, so we ate fried chicken with forks and knives so we could pull the skin off. The biscuits were rock hard and inedible, but the potatoes and gravy were actually pretty good, which is why I'm giving 2 stars instead of 1. The coleslaw was, well, KFC coleslaw. This was definitely our last visit to this location. I guess we'll try another the next time the KFC craving strikes. I must add, I really wish that we had read the previous reviews beforehand!
Small chicken pieces  mixed in the bucket. This happens every time we buy buckets at drive thru.  Just a rip off!

My son loves KFC and so I decided to take him to grab some chicken and qnmat about 9:45pm we pull into their drive through and 2 other cars pull in behind me. I sit there saying hello and no ones answers, so I call KFC on the phone and ask if they have any chicken left. The gentleman on the phone says yes and asks if I'm the one in the drive through and I say yes and he tells me to come to the window. I pull to the window and an older woman opens the window and tells me they are closed. I tell her I just spoke with a man and he told me to pull up. She proceeds to tell me that KFC closes at 9:40 and she threw the remaining chicken away. I understand that I shower up 15 minutes before they close, but to throw away ALL the chicken 15 minutes before you close is just ridiculous!! I called to double check what time they closed before I drive all the way over there! Not to mention the woman who opened the window was extemely rude and lazy if shes throwing everything away before they actually close!

My husband and I would never normally wait in a long line (almost to the door) for food. We were famished and had limited options getting into town late on a Friday night. Chipotle seemed like a quick, easy option.   Yeah right.

Apparently this is where all the kids hang out on a Friday night ! It was packed. The line was long but I will say that the employees seemed to work quickly to move the line along. There were a few hiccups like the fact that they were out of carnitas so some customers in line had to quickly pick another protein option.  I ordered a burrito bowl with chicken to go. We had planned to dine in but most of the tables that were available were not wiped down. And it was too chilly to eat outside.

We were happy with the quality of our meals but not the long wait time and dirty tables. This location may need to look into scheduling more employees on weekends!

To be honest, we've kinda steered clear of Chipotle since the recent food safety event that affected the chain nationwide. We decided that today was the right time to give them another try. 

The food was about average, nothing special. I had a Carnitas Bowl and P ordered a Chicken Quesadilla. We've enjoyed better at Moe's Southwest Grill or even Juan's Mexican Grill. During our meal, we did notice some curious things about the menu. 

1. It's the only "Mexican" place we could name that charges for chips & salsa with a meal. $1.40 for a small bag of chips or $2.05 for a small bag of chips and salsa. 
2. While P was able to order a Chicken Quesadilla, the only Quesadilla shown is the Cheese Quesadilla on the kid's menu. Not sure if there are other options. Also, it was presented uncut, leaving P to attempt slicing it with plastic utensils. 
3. There doesn't seem any option for ordering a-la-carte from the menu. For example, if I wanted a bowl with a taco on the side, I can't just order a single taco. I have to order the taco meal. 

Like we said, nothing horrible to report, just not someplace we'll rush to visit again in the future.

Went for the impossible burger (which was amazing) and only realized I paid for a drink o never received until I left. 

Try the impossible burger!

Went through the drive thru in 6/6/2021.  My dr. Peppers was completely flat! Fries must have sat under the heat lamp for hours, old and stale. My whopper was thrown together it was lop sized in the wrapper with blobs of mayo all over the side!!! Lady working thr window didn't speak at all once at the window...no hello, no have a good day. I requested bbq dipping for my chicken nuggets, didn't not get any. By far worst experience ever, horrible food, sodas and customer service! When I drove off I said "have a good night" and she said you too and close the window. It will be a matter of time before this location gets closed down. Nothing positive to say!
Biscuits doughy and under cooked. Manager hung up on me twice. I could come get more biscuits but when i said chicken would be cold, he hung up. Sounded lime he was 13.

Waiting at the drive through window, my friends and I notice that all their reviews are about people trying this because of the Harold and Kumar movie... we were not an exception.

There was no one ahead of us and no one behind us. We ordered maybe 6 bacon cheese burgers and one impossible burger.

The bacon cheese burgers were terrible and we waited about 15 minutes in the car for it. The impossible burger was a bit better but there are so many fast food options out there this is probably more of a gimmick chain. Sorry but soggy buns and low quality ingredients does not do justice (I'd probably pick the Costco frozen burgers over these).

If you're curious, be curious to be disappointed.

I used to love this Dunkins so much. But recently they got a new manger "Ms. K" or something... 1. This is not preschool??? and 2. Every time I go in or go through the drive thru she either is yelling at an employee for doing their job, or firing someone for doing their job. A few weeks ago one of the crew members didn't want to sell the frozen turkey sausage (as they shouldn't, I've previously worked at a different Dunkins and this is against corporate policy) and she said just cook it twice, when the crew member wouldn't the manager told them to clock out and not come back. Today I went through the drive thru, but when I got to the window, there the manager was telling a different crew member that she could leave and not come back, because the crew member was saying how they needed to go faster (it was pretty slow) I don't understand why then you would send someone home if the line is slow. I find it EXTREMELY unprofessional for a manager to talk to the employees this way in general, never mind in front of customers. If I was "Ms. K's" manager I would tell her to clock out and never come back.

We decided to come and give the impossible whopper a try. So we went to the drive through and We ordered a medium meal with a large water and the drink that came with the meal was a sprite. The whopper was good but not great, it didn't have much flavor but a nice alternative to beef. The food was hot and well prepared but the sprite didn't have any syrup so it was carbonation water so we had to throw it away. But other than that the meal was fine

Huge Disappointment- take out terrible 
Ordered Vegan Bowl got home to bowl of rice and black bean fajita veggies a littler lettuce and tomato No Plant Based Protein Sofritas
Ordered guacamole and chips : chips stale soft and chewy guacamole had huge seed which filled half the bowl
To tired and hungry to go back 
Which is why I ordered take out

I travel a lot across the country and I can say that this KFC is much better than most.  Quick and easy drive through.  Order was filled in about a minute.  Chicken pieces were big and tasty with original recipe.  Side dishes were standard but packed full.  Biscuit was fresh.  Everything positive!

I was on the way to the Philly half with a group of friends and was on the look out for a Dunkin Donuts for breakfast. We were already running late but DD is a pre race ritual for me and I was so glad to have come across this one. We arrived at the drive thru and I started to order. Between going back and forth between my group asking what they wanted and ordering multiple food things (one with special food restrictions) through the speaker, I was worried they would mess up the food order, as this is not unusual for Dunkin Donuts. Many a time my food orders have gotten messed up even when I ordered one item. We got our food quickly and everything came out as requested. We happily ate our sausage, egg and cheese croissants, washing them down with hot DD coffee, as we continued to head over to Eakins Oval ready to tear this half marathon up!

Where the hells them gizzards !!!!   Don't a chicken have gizzards?  Well then why don't you cook em?  Ain't nobody wants livers every day and night!  You think gizzards is nasty!  Well they ain't!  They good. And KFC used to cook em!  But now for some reason they don't. Everyone I know eats em!  Now let's get on that and cook what your customers want!  Why the Colonel roll over in his own 11 herbs and spices if he knew that y'all don't use them gizzards!  And Popeyes! I won't eat. Nothing but breading!  WHERES the CHICKEN?

For fast food, this place really does a nice job! I'm generally not a fan of Burger King, but this one was convenient during one of the errands I was running. I went here for lunch and had the Whopper Junior meal. The staff seemed very together, very friendly and knowledgeable. The restaurant was also extremely clean! Again, for fast food, this was a very pleasant experience!

Chipotle never disappoints when it is in my mouth. I ordered a burrito with steak, white rice (just because i like it better than brown or no rice) , queso, cheese (because I'm not lactose intolerant like a coward), LIGHT mild tomato salsa, sour cream, and guacamole. It tasted delicious so good so fresh love it love you. I also ordered a side of chips and guac to eat when i am hungry either now or later. I was blessed enough to get my guac, however they forgot the chips. This is not the first time that they gave me a side of guac with no chips. It's the SECOND time. As the old adage goes, the first time is a mistake, the second time is an attack on my character. I hope next time they don't forget my gosh darn chips. Good burrito will try again. 5 stars

I wish I could give this place no stars but to write this review it requires a star. Well first off they offer me a famous bowl I ask for 3 originals and 1 spicy and they didn't have enough for my order. Second I ordered sweet tea they didn't have any so I order unsweetened tea and they didn't have any. Thirdly I ordered an individual Mac and cheese for my daughter and they repeated my order too me at the drive thru speaker and again at the window however when I get home lone and behold what I had was coleslaw. I call to complain about the coleslaw mistake and they hang up on me. Call me crazy for going back but this isn't the first time I have gotten sick bad service at this particular kfc. I guess I'll just have to switch to a different chicken place.

Taco Bell can kiss my white ass. They need these in the rest of the Midwest.  Nothing quite like waiting in a drive-thru line at 2:30 in the morning for 40 frickin minutes for a quesadilla and chili cheese fries, then heading back to the frat house.

Had to try the new impossible burger. The packaging is better than it used to be it's back to paper wrapping which is environmentally more friendly. The impossible burger itself has the consistency of beef, tastes very good, all of the lettuce and tomato and sauce on top or fresh. Pricewise I thought $13 for two impossible burgers was a little on the high side but since it's a new product that could make an impact on our world environmentally I had to try one. If the World could raise less beef and emit less greenhouse gases we may be on the way to saving the planet. On the other hand eating food made out of Pea protein I myself felt like some livestock.Friendly service brand new store on VanDyke was clean .Two drive thru lanes-pretty impressive although slow.

This Burger King was recently demolished and rebuilt. I have to say that the cleanliness and employee service is much better than before. I don't know if this was a franchise owner change or what, but it has made a world of difference.  Order accuracy, quality of the food, and overall service are very good.  I've always been a whopper fan so that's what I got when I came here.  The whopper is just as good as I've always remembered. Hopefully, the quality and service stay level, but until then I'll be back.

This Chipotle is literally so unpredictable. 

Nothing worse than having that feeling of binge hunger and arriving at this particular Chipotle and they don't have steak, cheese, pico, corn AND lettuce all at once. Every now and then, they'll run out of the occasional ingredient and it's quite annoying - but still manageable to order your favorite meal without it. But most recently this past week (way before closing) they were literally out of about 6 ingredients all at once which is pretty unacceptable if you ask me. 

Employees are obviously unhappy to be there - they move painfully slow, with zero sense of urgency and slop together the food so carelessly, it's possible to actually lose your appetite watching the way they make your  meal.

Absolutely disgraceful experience. I had placed my order at 9:00 for pickup at 9:30. Not only was it not ready, they ran out of the food it took to make what i had ordered. Had they started the order at 9, or even 9:15 like they were supposed to, they could've been able to contact me not to drive 10 minutes out of my way (mind you I'm out of my hometown at the moment, about 40 additional minutes I drove) to tell me they did not have what I had ordered. I was told as I got there, "we are out of steak." Okay, fine! No big deal it happens. I told them to refund me. Then, after they refunded me for just the burrito (I paid thru the app) they go to make the bowl (again, this should've been done at 9 or 9:15) and they said they're out of white rice and fajitas. Their excuse was "it was a busy day so we ran out." They had FIVE people behind the counter, too, three of which were doing absolutely nothing, talking to 2 customers like they were friends or something. Not one of them could've started my order sooner? They didn't even offer to give me my drinks for free for the drive. So I paid $7 for an absolute shit experience.

We went to pick up our food, as we usually do so it will still be hot and fresh. But the chicken was old, the biscuits older, and had no coleslaw. We called about six times, but no answer. Thinking about not returning to Whiehouse as a whole, seems like the same places in other locations offer the best . Spent 28.76, really on only one eatable thing, potatoes were ok. The the thigh was even dry, usually has the most juices.

So after the iconic movie Harold and Kumar. I was on a long road trip and I had seen a White Castle. So doing as the movie did I pulled into the location. It was the most wonderful atmosphere. A retro styled diner, super clean and to my surprise there was no customers. 

I went and looked at the menu they had not 1 but 2 vegan burgers. One being the impossible burger and the other was a vegan slider. The vegan sliders were only 99 cents each. Wow !! So I bought an impossible burger combo and of course 4 sliders with another fries and a drink. 

The total of my items came to $12.00 this was 5 burgers, 2 fries and 2 drinks. Which I think is pretty amazing. We waited for approximately 10 minutes. 

The impossible burger was not my favourite but it was a good choice. I found the sliders were so delicious and a cute size. 2 were perfect for me. I'd highly recommend checking them out for their vegan options. Also this location is clean, efficient and has great service too.

Terrible in almost every way! Nothing is ever ready!  How you specialize in chicken and do not have any!   Customer service is awful; you have to repeat the order multiple times to finally get it correct! Ridiculous!

Why can we not operate in the USA the the company is run Europe! Efficient and the food is still the way I remember it when we knew it as Kentucky Fried Chicken as a kid in the 80's, 90's and early 2000's!

Not very good as far as Mexican food goes, and not very good as far as Chipotle goes.

I've been here a couple times now, as it's down the street from where I work, and I've never come away terribly happy. I've always come away full of a giant burrito, so that may explain it, but in overall quality this Chipotle just doesn't stack up. I've had both the steak and chicken and, while the flavor was fine, I got really bad pieces of meat (like lots of fat and yuckiness). The service is 'meh' at best...friendly enough, but quite slow. 

I do LOVE the guacamole...I am an avocado fiend and will basically eat it in any way, shape, or form, but Chipotle's is quite good. That's about the only thing worth coming here for.

Run of the mill Chipotle. Got a steak burrito. It was a little light on meat, which is pretty standard for Chipotle. The meat didn't taste as good as usual unfortunately. That said, big props to the cashier for accepting an expired free burrito card, so I can't complain too much about the underwhelming size and taste of my burrito.

I'm surprised this place is still in business. I have visited 3 times in the drive-thru and 2 out of the 3 times they ran out of what I wanted. I might want to add that the visits were spread apart and I wasn't ordering anything unusual. I just wanted some chicken on one visit and the one other time I wanted a pot pie. What gives KFC? Obviously not this establishment...

First day landing in St. Louis and spotted a White Castle? SCORE!
I have always wanted to try this stuff after it has been glorified in the  Harold and Kumar movie.

The price: dirt cheap. Around 60 cents per slider? Count me in!
The service: it was slow. I think there was a total of 3 people to receive orders, cook the food, and help out at drive-thru.
The taste: bun was moist, meat wasn't dry, condiments in it totally hit the spot. We got the original sliders, slider with bacon, and slider with jalapeno. The bacon slider totalllllyy hit the spot because it gave a nice chewy and crunchy factor to it.

This is definitely worth a decent  3 star to me for the taste and price, definitely worth the experience of trying. Thank goodness they don't have this chained in California... otherwise people would be swarming to buy it since it's soo cheap and convenient.

My husband went to the drive thru lane this evening at 7:46 pm.  He ordered a "Family Fill Up",  which included chicken, slaw, mashed  potatoes, gravy and biscuits. When he got home,  there was no gravy.  I called  and spoke to  the manager  and his  response was that he would take my name and the next time I'm there, he will give me more geavy.  I tried to explain to him  that giving me additional gravy would not be helpful  because the meals already  come with  gravy.  He said  that  was the  only solution.  The manager was Lamont Whitley.  In addition to the  missing gravy, the wings and legs were overcooked  and dried out.  I didn't bother calling  back due to the Manger`s attitude.

Drive thru is only open. If you get bad chicken, and you're white, you get the line that its YOU'RE problem and if you demand a refund, they will inspect the chicken and only give you refund if you haven't touched it!!

I arrived at the KFC located at 4302 N Armenia Ave Tampa, FL 33607 at 10:07 PM on 4/10/21. I ordered a pick up online through the KFC app. My order was processed at 9:49 PM. When I arrived for pick up, I tried to enter the lobby, but it was locked. So I sat in a ridiculously long drive-thru line. When I get to the window, I said I was picking up an order, the lady responds with oh you're Chelsea Harris? I said yes. She told me She had to get the manager. The woman, who claims to be the manager, named Sabrina, asked me "you had the 16 piece meal right?" I said yes, then Sabrina tells me they didn't have enough chicken to fill my order, and then she tells me that the KFC portion closes at 10 P.M. anyway. I explained that I ordered BEFORE 10 pm. She told me that she got my order, but she "didn't care" because they are closed now. I told her that online it says they are open until 10:30 P.M., she said "I don't care what it says online, I'm telling you we close the KFC portion at 10." I explained to her that I ordered this same order earlier at a different KFC closer to home, and it came back as cancelled, twice. I couldn't figure out why it was being cancelled until I arrived at the location, it was because the other location was closed. So my card was not charged for those two orders. That's when I ordered through the Armenia location on the app, and it was processed, so I was charged. I asked why I was charged and it was processed if her location was indeed closed? She said she didn't care AGAIN and that online doesn't matter it's what happens in store. So of course I asked for a refund, and she tells me that she (who processed my order, but "didn't care" to fill it) could not refund me my money.....??? She told me you gotta go through Uber to get it. I said no ma'am I- and she cuts me off to say "or door dash or whatever app you used. Then I informed her that I order it through HER KFC app, and SHE approved it.... but she told me that I needed to get out of her drive-thru because -I- was holding up the drive-thru....So I asked for her name and her position, she asks why, I told her I'm reporting this incident to corporate. So she tells me she is Sabrina and she is the manager. It says on the app that this KFC is open until 10:30 PM. The app let me process the order and everything. I would like you to fix the main issue, LACK OF CUSTOMER SERVICE..... from a manager?!?! Sabrina was rude, condescending, and showed zero customer service. I hope this is not the reflection of KFC you wish customers to see. Also fix your information online, for the KFC app, to show the correct hours of this KFC. Also, you should probably make sure that customers cannot order through the app when it is close the closing time, you know, just in case a manager doesn't care to fill the order because it is too close to closing. It is a shame that a national chain restaurant would need this kind of advice in the first place.
Asked for a Chocolate Glazed Donut, receive a Chocolate Donut with a Glaze. Asked for a Double Sausage Breakfast Sandwich without the bread, they put the cheese on top against the wrapper.

Wanted a crispy spicy chicken sandwich and decided to give KFC a chance.  Ordered the Nashville Hot Crispy Colonel.  It was crispy but had no spicy flavor to it, what they did was pour this oily hot sauce over the fried chicken to make it spicy.  Crispy fried food cannot go with an oily sauce poured on top because it ends up soaking up the sauce and loses crispiness, and it also gets soaked into the bun.  Besides, the hot sauce itself did not have much flavor to it.  Portion was small for the price.  I lost confidence in KFC chicken a while back and after this time I don't think I'll ever have KFC again.

I cannot recommend this location. I have put off writing a review for a while but it is obvious they are doing nothing to improve their service. If you don't want chicken for dinner, then you are in luck, because they often run out.  What KFC runs out of chicken before 5pm?? The last time I gave them a try they had run out of chicken fingers by 4:45 pm. Next time there is a family picnic or gathering and the family wants KFC, I will go to another location.
Every time I order,they always short me on chicken.Call the corporate office what a waste of time. They care less about as much as the restaurant.

Ordered wings and the inside of the chicken at this location is nasty black and spoiled. Imagine biting into this. Every piece of regular hot wing chicken was black inside. 

Customer service number leads you to an attendant that can do nothing. The business itself won't answer the phone either. Calling corporate leads you to the same customer service rep who can't do anything.

DT was the only option available. Who ever took my order to a good 5 min to say hello. Not even a hello when they came on, just mumbled so i went on with my order. Horrible customer service. The person sounded MISERABLE. Went to the first window and it's obvious the person that took my order was him. Went to the second window and i asked for zesty sauce. ( i ordered a whopper meal) and the guys said I'm not allowed to give zesty sauce it's only for the nuggets. Now that really shocked me. What kind of business says no to a customer for SAUCE ? What the fuck. Even the fact that they limit who to give it to. Onions on the burger looks disgusting. Fries were super cold with NO salt.

I've been a loyal Chipotle fan for 15 years but my order always gets screwed up here. Tonight I got a burrito bowl (now 10.15 for steak plus 2.65 for 4 oz of guacamole...). It had the tiniest amount of meat and mushy rice, small portions of cheese and salsa, and no lettuce at all. Since it was with Uber, I obviously couldn't do anything about it. Very disappointing.
Worst service ever. Will not return. I asked for butter twice and the woman didnt put one packet in my bag. Also food was cold and the chicken was so soggy. I just ate the meat not the breading.

Its like Izzo's without as many choices. 

Best thing on the menu is guacamole and the steak is spicy and good. 

The workers had no clue about the product. For example, they didn't know what salsas were which or what kind of peppers are used in any the foods. 

Also, we paid $7.50 for my child to eat steak and lettuce

Not impressed.

pretty sad meal we had. Never had this problem before at this location.  I think its because we took it out and couldn"t complain right away. Ordered the five dollar menu. was told  couldnt get a breast with that. .Huh?? then ordered two extra cole slaws.. got one. got one bisquit for two orders.. chicken tenders were so tough even my dog would NOT eat it. sad.. my chicken breast was not cooked all the way thru.. worst meal we have ever had.  no bbq sauce.. and staff acted like we asked for a million bucks.. too bad .. probably wont eat there again. I know I would NOT take out from there.. Managers need to train their staff in courtesy.

No wonder this place has only 1 1/2 stars. My stepson went there to get a meal tonight. They waited 25 minutes to get the chicken cooked. They served people coming in after them first, Chicken fingers were over cooked and Dry. Paid over 40 bucks for the meals. The Mac & Cheese was the only thing that was good. The mashed potatoes tasted like they came out of a box.  Will never go to this KFC again. The Manager and the Franchise owner should read these reviews

As a chain restaurant if one location makes a certain item a certain way shouldn't all locations do the same? Horrible customer service was experienced today where they even declined to give me the name of the manager. Gentleman that was making the plate was so rude to the point where he started adding items that were not asked for, basically sick of his job where he didn't care and started to add whatever he saw onto my plate. I asked for a secret menu item that I have been getting for years. Quesadilla with steak in it, girl behind the stove was telling they can't do that. I explained how I have done it before many times, and all 3 employees in the front denied that chipotle makes this item. To make it even worse, they made me pull out a receipt from a visit I had at another location about 3 weeks ago that showed I ordered and they indeed make a quesadilla with steak in it. Yet again, girl behind the stove said no and to come back next week and they will be able to do it??? Does not make any sense, really frustrating, and very unprofessional how they handled the situation.

The saddest pieces of chicken ever. Very small pieces of meat compared to other places. Not worth the money. IMO kfc has gone downhill fast. Bland and flavorless which is strange when I should be tasting 11 herbs and spices. Would also be nice if the owner got smart and converted it to a KFC/ TacoBell good location for one since the only other tacobell within in a 20 minute drive is in ellerslie and absolutely nothing heading north.

I ordered two 12-piece chicken dinners. One extra crispy and one original fried chicken.  My six sides were: 2 Cole Slaw; 2 Mashed Potatoes and Gravy; 2 Macaroni and Cheese.  The two orders combined included 12 biscuits.

When I got home, there was no Cole Slaw whatsoever. The extra crispy chicken looked very bad, nothing like it should have, but medicore edible. The original fried chicken bucket was a disaster. Over cooked, actually looked like yesterdays chicken refried. The meat was very dark, dry, the meat split and gross.  The biscuits were like hockey pucks and non edible.  The Mac and Cheese looked like it had been scraped from the bottom of a bucket and was a neon orange glumpy gacky gumpus of glue paste cheese, and over bloated macaroni noodles.. unedible.

I called and talked to a counter person who answered the phone.. he actually remembered they forgot the cole slaw.  He didn't have the authority to handle the chicken problem and the Mac and Cheese and Biscuit problem.

So, Cody Berman, the "General Manager" got on the phone.. YELLED at me, was abusive to me.  Said I had to drive the 40 minutes each way and he had to inspect the chicken and other products.  When I told him I was in a wheelchair and I had goine to KFC to serve 20 children and they were due to arrive in 20 minutes.. and time and my mobility didn't allow for me to drop what I was doing and bring it back immediately.

He went insane on me, made crude remarks about my problems (handicapped and living too far away, and guests due in minutes)  weren't his problem, they were mine.. and he was a miserable monster.  

I had to yell over him.. and tell him I was going to report him to corporate offices, and I would make sure his "quality" product would hit the internet..

Disappointed. Got a carnitas bowl with chips and guacamole. The pork had globs of fat, the rice was clumpy, and there was too much vinegar in the salsa. The salsa overwhelmed the entire dish. The chips were very stale and had way, way too much salt.  I'll try a different location next time.

This is by far the worst KFC probably in America. They were extremely disorganized and I believe I saw at least 5-10 different cars leave the location due to the fact that they said they wouldn't be having any chicken for at least 45 mins. Mind you this is during the dinner rush at about 8:30 , when the restaurant closes the lobby at 9 and the drive thru at 10. One would think they would have an abundance of chicken for the 2 remaining hours they are open. I then proceeded to order a large Mac n cheese and was told they wouldn't have any for the rest of the night without an ounce of care coming from the drive thru worker. He almost seemed excited to be denying all of these people food. Good luck staying in business not selling food when your a restaurant.

Went through the drive thru and ordered a famous bowl.  The chicken was cold and cold unmelted cheese on top. Restaurant refuses to answer their phone to hear complaint.  AVOID THIS LOCATION, CUSTOMER SERVICE IS PATHETIC!

Sad to see this Chipotle operate like it does. No steak available during a Saturday lunch rush. Undercooked rice. Nasty sweet tea. I think the manager finally came out to help the poor cook who was by himself. Saw ppl walk out. Overall not good.

They need to close this place. How are you out of chicken and biscuits. This is the 2nd time we came here for din din and they where out of chicken legs, strips, biscuits and some sides. They didn't even have their new Nashville chicken. So after finding something on the menu I get home and they still didn't even give me what I ordered which is not a lot considering they were out of half of their menu. North Co has some of the poorest attitudes and work ethics in the restaurant Industry.

I must say... I got more of a seared bite on the impossible whopper than the actual whopper-no no no no no jo (I AM IN DENIAL!!!)..... no no no no no no no no.. ladies and gentlement, im not gonna trip, i wanted to come in a 100% hater... but imma just flat out say it-- your impossible whopper, BK, is BETTER than your actual whopper, BK !!!!!!!

Well, we tried this place again because we were in a rush and needed quick food. Ordered an 8-piece meal, with thighs and breast only. Guess what? We got 3 breast pieces, 2 drumsticks, 2 wings and 1 thigh. Really? How difficult is it to count to "4"? Four breasts, four thighs, no big deal, but apparently it WAS a big deal for the person filling the bucket. Got the food home and it was DRY DRY DRY. It must have been sitting under the heat lamps for hours. We had to throw out 6 pieces of chicken because it was inedible. And the wait in the drive-through was 20 minutes too long. So, we will never again get anything from this KFC, or any other KFC for that matter.

Went through the drive up at 6:30 pm last night (Saturday).  Ordered 12 pieces of original chicken.  When I got home, I noticed they had only given me 8.  When I checked the receipt, they had only charged me for 8 but did not tell me they had changed my order.  The chicken was very over done and dry. (Under heat lamp too long?) Also, received 2 wings and 3 legs out of our 8 pieces.  Popeyes next time.

Went yesterday to get dinner. Ordered the fill up $20 which was to have the chicken, 2 large mashed potatoes, large coleslaw and a large gravy with biscuits.  Arrived home and unloaded the car, finally sitting down to eat with my wife.  Open the bag and find the chicken, biscuits and one container of potatoes. Attempted to call multiple times over the next three hours with a continuing busy signal. Tried to call the national number and received no answer except "for English press one." I believe that Popeye's is now delivering.

My family stopped here and I had asked them to pick up a pot pie. The location was out of pies so they brought home a "Famous" bowl which I  have never had before.  I hope this was not an example of what they usually contain because there was nothing that could be described as popcorn chicken in this bowl. It looked like bits of the junk that ends up on the bottom of the fryer after frying the large pieces of chicken. It was awful.  The colonel would be ashamed.

Burger king is a quick place to grab food on my lunch break. i usuallygo through the drive thru and get the same guy wait on me. He is very pleasant and is very upbeat. He usually takes my order and gets everything right . Burger King usually cooks a good burger. i used to order nothing but chicken but now im ordering the Jr. Whopper its so tasty.
Sometimes i order kids meals the portion is just right for me and it always taste so good.  i will go back the service is A-okay there is 1 concern is the drive thru they need to remove the pole that is there it scares a lot of people including myself i am always worried i might dent up my car. 
Be very cautious

. went to KFC for dinner.  Ordered and before we finished filling our drinks the order was up. Pretty quick.  Went to the first table.  It was very dirty.. with dirt as well as grease. This table had NOT been washed in a very long time. Went to the second table. It too was filthy.  dirt.. grease.. I asked the staff for something to clean the table with since it was sticky and greasy. She gave me a rag to wash it and I hear her tell the rest of the staff...I washed that table.. I knew better.. so I cleaned the table, sat down to eat.  I had a five dollar mean. chicken breast.  the meat w was so tough I could not even eat it. so be it.. its too bad cause there is always a sign out help wanted. I understand why.  There was very few customers in the store. tThose tables and the floor which was also very sticky should have been spotless.  too bad good help is hard to find. If I was the manager, I would have been embarrassed to hell to have a customer ask for something to clean the table with. .They should have been spotless.. and the food was NOT fresh. that was plain to see as it was like eating shoe leather.. I dont think I will be heading in that directiion soon. too bad cause its close
Grabbed a bucket and hot wings for the Super Bowl. Chicken was not totally done and no flavor. If I'm crazy and go back it will only be for strips.

My wife and I returned from an appointment in Phoenix on Monday, 7/20/21 around 5:30 PM.  We were tired and looking for a place to pick up carryout.  We decided on KFC on our way to our home on the far eastside.  We went through the drive-thru and I ordered a 1 piece breast and wing dinner and my wife ordered a 1 piece breast dinner.  We arrived home in less than 10 minutes only to find what was the worst dinners we had ever had from a KFC.  The wing in my meal was so overcooked that I could not eat it and both of our chicken breasts were almost as bad at the wing.  I had to toss the wing out and managed to east about half of the well done breast as I was quite hungry.  My wife could only eat about 3 or 4 bites of her breast and we threw both of the remaining breasts away.  The rolls were as hard as rocks which neither one of us ate.  Seriously, I think you could have at least cracked a window if you had thrown them at one.  Our mashed potatoes were room temperature.   We have picked up dinners there before and found them to be mediocre but edible. Based on this experience, it is doubtful we will ever return.  We should have called management but we didn't. As a business owner I would want to be aware of this and speak to all of my employees to hopefully rectify the problem so future customers have a belter experience and edible dinners.

This Mexican Grill is still my all time favorite. The new location is no exception. Enjoy a Carnitas bowl with presses Apple Nantucket drink. Try any bowl and you can't go wrong. The chips and salsa are wonderful.
Tried the beyond burger and lovedddd it! The line wasn't so bad and the staff were friendly. Will be back

This is the worst KFC I've ever encountered.  They are routinely out of either chicken or biscuits.  It's a business that sells two products and is constantly out of one or the other.  It's legitimately surprising if you can actually get everything KFC is supposed to sell on a given day.  Be sure to check your order before you leave.  They're unlikely to tell you that they haven't given you your whole order and resist giving refunds when caught.  It's been like this for years.  How can they fail to find competent management and staff for so long?  How is this location not a hair salon by now?

I live in area so I have been there numerous times. Today I went there and ordered the new grilled cheese melt. It was disgusting. It was just 2 pieces of bread with cheese. It was like they threw it in microwave to somewhat melt cheese and warm bread. I will NEVER get that again. Also my son got a sausage, egg and cheese croissant. The cheese was cold like it was just thrown on as an afterthought. If I wasn't in a hurry I would have went back but I was unable to. I just hate I spent money on food like that.

Came for late night tacos but ended up with late night disappointment with disaster.
This has to be the worst taco place I've ever been to. When we first arrived the place only had a handful of people. After placing our order and paying, the restaurant filled up instantly. Had to be over max capacity. The line was all the way out the door down the sidewalk.  A fight broke out by the restrooms for non Wanted entertainment. 
I ordered an agua de horchata and it was ridiculously sweet. I wanted a quesadilla but was told they were out. So I chose 2 tacos de asada, they arrived cold, there was more fat than meat on my taco.  There was not enough staff nor security. Needless to say, don't eat here!

Gross!! Mac and cheese looks like it's a week old
Chicken was cold.. 
Will definitely not go to this location ever again!

Giving them a one is giving them one too much!  This place has been an epic fail my entire 10 yrs in Tucson.  The service sucks!  The management sucks, and they rarely have chicken available and sometimes have to wait 20 min or more.  I thought I would give them one more try. What a huge mistake on my part!!!! HUGE!!!!!!  I ordered a 4 piece extra crispy, but they didn't have a wing in EC which was ok for the wing, but not for the other pieces.  I got home to discover I only got one side.  As I ate my chicken I found a hair cooked into my chicken leg.  Nothing is more gross than that.  I immediately got into my car and returned asking for my money back. Keisha Christian who said she was the manager said NO to my money back.  She didn't care that I was going to report her to corporate either, and btw, there was no one inside, and only 2-3 cars in the drive thru and 3 workers standing around.  No reason she had to be on the window and not come and speak to me.  I created a stir, and she came over but still refused to give me my money back stating it was not something she was authorized to do. ISN'T SHE THE FN MANAGER?  They insisted the hair could NOT have come from them, but I said it wasn't mine, and even though it wasn't one of the male workers it very well could have been on their clothes or from another worker.  I left in a huge huff and was followed out by a worker.  Not only will I never return, I will continue to berate this store everywhere I can!  I see I'm not alone, however. Does KFC care about their shameful workers!!!!!!

I've been to this location several times for the buffet and I've always been satisfied with the food and service. The quality of the food here is reliably good, and KFC fans will definitely satisfy their craving for those unique KFC chicken recipes.

There is plenty of seating, and the staff has always been friendly and accommodating. There's self serve fountain drinks with a good selection, and the restaurant has always been clean when I've been there.

I personally feel that the best value for the money is the buffet, and if you leave hungry it's your own fault. Nobody is hovering over you to leave the restaurant after a couple of trips to the buffet like you see so many times at Asian buffets. 

I saw some complaints in previous reviews about empty slots on the buffet, but if anything is out on the buffet line all you need to do is ask politely and they will promptly refill the empty slot. Nobody has ever refused to refill the buffet line while I was there. I like when they refill only as needed instead of overfilling and letting the food sit for an hour or so.

This has been a good location from my personal experience.

I've had it with this store. I've done everything I can to make it a pleasant experience and they just won't let it be. This time I even downloaded the app and ordered online so I wouldn't have to wait. No dice! "Chicken will be about 10 minutes " came the response. Waiting 10 minutes didnt exempt me from leaving off my 8 tenders.  Meanwhile all my food was cold because it was bagged up and ready to go and it (like me) had to wait another 10 minutes. They can't get it right here.

I would give no stars! Got a whopper meal.... gross are you out of tomatoes? And thanks for cooking my fries because my burger was still mooing ... tried to microwave it to rid it of its blood...ya no that was so gross I feel sick..... NEVER AGAIN
