I love chicken, I love KFC chicken. I've made several visits to this new location. The restaurant is immaculate, the service is great, and the chicken is... well KFC. Which means it's great!! Kudos to the manager.

Ended up pulling a long curly brown hair out of my impossible whopper. Extremely disappointed as thats what I've been craving all day. It takes a lot for me to leave a negative review, but an employees hair in my food is simply unacceptable and disgusting.

Well not so much a review of the restaurant per say. More of an item.   For months have seen nonstop advertising for the plant based impossible whopper.    Waited for them to join the 2 for $6 menu to try.   The regular Whopper for long time my fast food guilty pleasure.   I was so hoping that this possibly healthier version would taste as good as the regular.   Going into it I assumed that the toppings would be a reason to not tell much of a difference.   Well I could very much tell a difference    It is not bad, but with all the advertising saying it was just like a regular whopper it was disappointing.   If it stays on  2 for $6 likely I may try again.   Will not pay the  full price for.

The absolute only thing I eat from here isn't even chicken, it's biscuits and gravy. Because, to me, there's no one...not even your granny's gravy that can compare to theirs. So you can imagine my disappointment when I pulled up to order and was met with egregious attitudes. The young man who works the drive through curses at you because he forgets items in your order...(how do you mess up biscuits and gravy?) Anywho, if you are starving and in the market for good food, drive a few miles to a place that will appreciate your coins because this staff does not.

I ordered a steak bowl on DoorDash. KNOWING that chipotle is very light on their portions I ordered extra steak. I open up my bowl and this is what I get.. I guess if you order extra steak that means you have to give up half your bowl too.

This KFC is worse than I thought it would get. Imagine opening your family's mash potatoes to see mounds of powder. Then the chicken was severely burned and dried out. Stay away from this place as this was my second time with terrible food. STAY AWAY!

Hey you want a quick meal to go at a reasonable price try these guys! I have had several dinners to go from this location and I have always enjoyed them. I usually get the burrito bowl with chicken, I love their chicken. They load the bowl with all the good stuff you chose and my friend that is a big meal. The other item i really enjoy is the queso and chips. I have to tell you I'm kind of a queso snob. I've been to Austin too many times and sampled the worlds best queso and trust me this is not it. Haha but its not bad either, some of the best I've had lately that's for sure! Definitely a good choice if you are a fan of the cheese. 
Go to Torchys in Austin if you really love your queso (just saying) Hey I like Chipolte I have tried most everything here and the steak is another good choice in your salad or tacos. Reasonable, fast, take it home and sit in front of the TV and eat yourself silly.

Was in town visiting family, hadn't been feeling well all day so we got something we knew I could tolerate, KFC. I ordered the Pot Pie. We used the app & where at the drive thru order box by 7:10. we wanted to place an additional order for a 3 strips meal. We were told they were out of strips for the night. Odd, but whatever, I wondered if our chicken littles would be in our order then. We get to the window, where the window taker tells us the wrong name, we correct her, and after a brief pause of confusion she realizes she's one order ahead of us. All good, we got our food and drinks. We pulled over to check the order accuracy (as they've had issues before) and after untying the tightest knot I've seen, we discover there's no pot pie, but a famous bowl is there instead. We head back to the drive thru, noticing all the lights on the place are now off at 7:40pm, and explain we didn't get our pot pie. She told us to hold on, and comes back to say they don't have any more pot pies. There's no offer to refund the money or replace it with something else. (To me this suggests they knew they weren't going to make it and instead decided to sub a similar priced item & not tell us at the window.) 

All of this aside, the customer service being not good (I get it we all have long days but there was a big sense of we were bothering them by being there at all) the bowl want very good either. It didn't have the cheese (which was good cause then I wouldn't have had anything to eat) the chicken used was not hot at all, there was hardly any gravy and the corn was very over cooked. 

This was the worst KFC I've been to in all my years.

I grew up with White Castle and they being the sole place open 24/7 in the 60s and 70s.  I loved having a place to eat hot food during a "graveyard" shift.  You don't fully appreciate something until you can't get it anymore.  I lived in New Orleans for 20 yrs and no White Castles.  Then I lived in Tucson, AZ with no White Castles west of the Mississippi River.  Finally moved back to my hometown state after leaving 42 years ago.  White Castle is one of the top five things I missed the most!

The frozen burgers sold in the grocery are just not the same.  A movie theater in Tucson offered them too and they still were frozen but prepared with a steamer. It's better than a microwave but still not the real thing.  Now that I'm back its a weekly visit for me. 

I tried the Fish Slider today and it was good.  Definitely worthy of ordering again. Now if I could just get lil miss to try their chicken I would have someone to come with me to eat here. 

The Sloppy Joe was a bust imo but hey; you keep trying new ideas. You never know what will be the next hit.  Did you know Shrimp Nibblers started today?  Man I love shrimp!  Gotta try them next!

Lady with short dark hair and an arm tattoo gave me and my girl straight trash service. Team member asked after every ingredient "anything else?" during an extremely simple order (rice, beans, cheese, and steak) like yeah I want more than just rice on my burrito?? Suuuuuper skimpy on the portions too, on my way home now and damn I'm still hungry. My girl said she didn't want any sour cream about 3 times but still got some. Although the redhead and the employee with the cute burrito earrings were super nice, sadly I will not be returning to this location.

It's hard to mess up a Dunkin.  But this is the worst one I've ever been in.  Asked for a sourdough breakfast sandwich with sausage.  Got a sourdough sandwich with bacon.  Asked for a powdered sugar donut ( pictured on the menu), told they don't have powdered sugar donuts.....actually felt scolded for asking.  My wife got a toasted bagel....charred toasted like a hockey puck.  The staff was not friendly.  This place is nasty Andrew bad!

We love kfc but tonight I'm very annoyed. We get home everything smells great.. then we realize our order is wrong, my boyfriend got Nashville tenders & I got a 3piece combo meal. We both ordered mash potatoes for our order. They gave him fries instead of mashed potatoes which is fine because I ordered 2 so I gave him one of mine. Then realized they forgot the biscuits which is my favorite part of my meal. Then icing on the already messed up cake I'm eating my chicken and pull out a long silver hair... I have very dark brown hair so I know it's not mine. We are so sad and it will be awhile before we come back.

This place needs to get it together! I understand we are all in hard times, but if you are going to be offering in app ordering you NEED to get it right. I literally don't understand how every single time I've gone  (don't ask me why I go back every week) I get a completely different order. This is a joke. I waste time going there and get home to something different and then go back to get it "fixed" and once again end up with a different bowl. The exact ingredients are on the sticker label so someone plz explain how the preparers completely switch it up. Sticking to Panchero's from now on. 

Ordered: white rice, steak/barbacoa, peppers, black beans, tomato salsa, corn, cheese, sour cream 
Received: white rice, chicken, pinto beans, and sour cream...I don't get it.

I'm not typically a fan of Dunkin', but I must review their Beyond Sausage® breakfast sandwich. It was surprisingly good for a sausage patty that is plant-based. It was meaty and filling. I actually liked the mild taste of this sausage with the egg and cheese. The egg on this one didn't have any unusual additives. I've had other breakfast sandwiches at Dunkin' that had too much pepper. That was a turn off to me. This one was actually quite good and I would get it again. The English muffin was also fresh tasting. This may be my new fast-food breakfast spot.

They also have a very good café con leche. The one I had today was excellent and made with skim milk (as ordered). I don't typically stop at this location in the morning since it's the busiest Dunkin' in Brandon. There's a line at the drive-thru at night too. Maybe because they also serve their Baskin-Robbins ice cream. This could be why it's so popular. 

The donuts are okay, but not the best. They are still hard to pass up when someone brings them into the office. My favorite ones are the strawberry and vanilla frosted donuts. 

The service is really good at this location, and the drive-thru moves quickly as others have said. They always have their donuts out on display. Not all of them do when it's early in the morning, and they should. It's always clean. This is why it's my favorite location.

This Dunkin is awful!!! Closest one to our house so stopped there.. "under new management" sign was hanging .. well idk if that's helping any. We ordered a ham egg and cheese on a croissant and turkey sausage egg and cheese on a bagel. Open the bag after we left and there is a flatbread and English muffin???!! The English muffin was cold on the inside and the cheese not even melted. The bread was stale... We were the only people in there at the time. I'm so confused. Our coffee was also super weak, watered down and no flavor. I was so annoyed I didn't even wanna go back and complain. 2 stars only because the hash browns weren't messed up........

Really. Look at this chicken wing. What a joke. They put two like this in our bucket. I tried to call they didn't answer. There is no integrity in treating your customers that way.

I am so dissapointed with this store. I traveled 65 minutes to get here just because I was craving KFC sooo bad and the store near me was out of everything.  I came through the drive through ordering a Nashville hot tenders meal, sub fries for a small Mac n cheese. 
Another Nashville hot tenders meal, sub fries for biscuits and gravy. Then I ordered a 5 piece chicken tenders meal. And finally another small side of Mac and cheese by itself. I got my food. I was told no by one of the employees who claimed to be the manager. They all, always want to claim to be the manager  I then asked who was above him. He told me Ernesto. I wouldn't even give this place a 1 star! Went drive thru

Referring to KFC Store #L747030 Highway 19 N, Tarpon Springs Florida 727.938.3927 NOT the one in Palm Harbor.  

Last Sunday night we had a sudden yearning for extra crispy Kentucky Fried Chicken... had to have it, so we visited the KFC in our neighborhood.  We normally don't indulge in such, but this was just one of those nights when we wanted something satisfying.

After we ordered, I walked outside and took another look at the store sign to reconfirm I was really in a KFC (hehehehehehe).   

DAAAAA service was incredible!  Couldn't really believe it, the young man servicing us was the kind of persona a business wants to steal way from a competitor.  It is people like this that set businesses apart.  I assumed he was a non professional, armed only with whatever KFC employee training provides. 

Being that I deal exclusively with FOH customer service/customer loyalty, Thomas pretty much blew me away with his stature and sales penache.  So what set me off, to go so far as to Yelp KFC?  Well it was the unexpected level of service, one does not expect to receive at a fast food counter.  

~ It was the soft sell and not the dull scripted cyborg sell.  
~ It was his tenure and sincerity.  
~ It was the fact that he "focused" on getting the order right the first time.
~ Thomas transmitted the importance HE held in pleasing us.
~ Thomas was neat and well groomed, and his nails were clean.
~ Thomas took time to explain the differences in the menu, pinging the 10 piece special, regardless of the fact that a small line had formed behind us.
~ It was the fact that he CARED!!!

I strenuously urge you to YELP wait staff who provide you with exceptional customer service... it deserves your time and effort.  

We thank you Thomas & KFC,
Ciao
Pen

They were pretty good but I will stick with my hash brown bites moving forward.   My favorite sandwich has egg whites with veggies, turkey sausage, yummy cheese and nutty whole grain bread.   I never go through the drive through, its always busy.   I can pull in, go get my food and get back into my car before the drive through lane has moved.   I have always been greeted with a smile and considering they are always busy ...that means a lot.

I couldn't believe how old the chicken was not to mention them forgetting the biscuit the fries were old. The fact you can't even get ahold of them by phone made it even worse Orderd two thighs an leg an got two breast an leg guess it's what they want.

I ordered and it wasn't until I got to the window that they said they didn't have any chicken. I said you are a chicken place but you don't  have any  chicken . He laughed and said no. The portions are generous but the price is a little higher than I'd like. Their tortillas aren't very good, but everything else I've tried I've enjoyed.

Why do bad experiences always happen to me!?  Today was already a crap of a day and this was just icing on the cake.  I think I'm going to stay home for the night and also never have BK (at least this location) on my list of joints I may visit should I be in the mood.  I came here 2x's today -- 1st time was to get my grandmother a whopper and a strawberry milkshake to deliver to her @ her care facility.  Despite the drive thru being extremely slow, the clerk at the window was very upbeat and made me laugh.  Returned on the way back from dropping off her food to get my hubs and myself some food given I had an ok experience the 1st visit and it turned immediately bad!!  

I ordered 2 whopper combo #1's w/no bun, I asked if they had side salads to which they said yes so I ordered 2 side salads in place of fries and 2 diet cokes.  Get up to the window at again, the clerk is so friendly.  I get my bag and head off...something told me to check.  So I pull over and reach my hand into the bag to feel fries! Umm, I'm confused since I said no fries.  So I continue to check the food --- yep, missing 1 side salad, 2 fries are in the bag despite me saying no fries (sub the salads), and then the whoppers....ummmmm, they had no condiments @ all on them.  Just a burger and some lettuce and 1 onion in a bowl!!?? No condiments in the bag either.  I'm confused.  I pull back around and park @ BK, walk in and wait patiently in line.  The clerk that took my order is @ the front now...I begin to let him know the situation and he then says "we don't sub fries for salads"....ok, well, you should have told me that in the drive thru when I was ordering....so instead of disclosing that, you charge me for the full combo mean w/fries + 2 salads....but wait, I only got 1 salad.  Then I bring up the whopper issue of not having any condiments.  He says that they aren't allowed to put condiments on them when not on a bun so he can give me condiments to take.  I decline and tell him I want a refund.  He calls the manager over who is extremely rude from the beginning...coming over saying "What do you need".  I explained the situation to her and she said that when we order no bun that means everything is a la carte so she can give me condiments to put on myself. 
I explained that I've never heard of that despite the many places, including Mc D's that put all items on the burger as is just w/o the bun. She smirks then she says "Well what do you want, another salad".  I said no, I'd like a refund.  She storms away while I'm in process of taking out my wallet to get my card for the refund.  I wait a good 2 minutes....she then comes over tosses the money at me and snatches the bags of food and walks away.  As I'm exiting, I then hear her say loudly to her staff "Guess she's going to McDonald's now!"  I cannot tell you how much this infuriated me....I had to maintain my composure and keep myself from turning around and saying something I would regret....so I took the higher road and just proceeded to leave.  First, I cannot grasp why you would ever treat a customer this way when the issues clearly resides w/your staff not being clear when the ordering is taking place and the customer was missing product from what they ordered and paid for.  I really don't appreciate being treated in this manner and especially coming from a manager of BK who is supposed to be representing the core values of any company.  I plan on escalating this situation to corporate as well as I believe that this management needs to have some additional training.  Now I probably won't hear from anyone on this issue however would greatly appreciate it if I actually did.

So i came to this stupid ass fucking location and the dum ass thats preparing the food can't fucking read...I ordered Georgia gold chicken little and got regular shit...seriously management hire people thats gonna do the job right is not rocket science.

Del Taco on Grand is now slated for the wrecking ball. Perhaps it's time has come and as it goes, so go the scallywags, leering homeless bums and other urban varmints that populated this dump late at night. 

Its round design testament to a prior life as a super-sized gas and service station in the 1970's. Hopefully they removed the hazardous materials from the ground before Del Taco moved in - ya think????? 

But Del Taco was a site of many of my late night romps through the vile, violent lands of Midtown when I was a SLU-bie and for that, DT will be a happy but foggy memory. The fare was predictably greasy but tasty and the prices helped feed a lot of hungry students and bag-ladies who caught a few quarters and dimes near the bus stop. Adios, amigo!!

MISSION IMPOSSIBLE

I haven't eaten at Burger King in over 9 years but I'm a huge fan of the plant based impossible meat. I've been following the company Impossible for over 2 years and I decided that today was going to be the day I try it. I also wanted to see how Burger King was serving it. 

I was on a road trip and had been driving for three and half hours straight, when I  pulled into the drive thru. And here is how it went..

BK Girl: Can I take your order?

Me: I'd like to order the Impossible Whopper with everything on it. I also want cheese too. 

BK Girl: Would you like cheese? 

Me: Yes please put cheese on there! 

BK Girl: ....your total is....please drive thru!

(I drive around and I pull up to the window.)

BK Girl: Did you want cheese on that?

Me: Yes I want cheese on the burger. 

BK Girl: Ok

Guess what? I bit into my Impossible burger and there was no cheese. Maybe we should call it Mission Impossible the Cheese episode because apparently I'm not getting any cheese. 

I was a little upset. I parked my vehicle and I went inside. The man behind the counter who looked like the manager asked if there was a problem. I told him that I ordered cheese and there was no cheese. He said, "Sorry! I'll make you a fresh one!" I was happy that he was making it right. 

The girl who took my order and got it wrong several times decided to tell the manager that I didn't want cheese and that it was my fault. Honestly this really truly upset me. I was standing right there and listening to a lie. I literally jumped in and set the story straight. Sorry BK Girl but you were wrong on this one and secondly don't ever talk about the customer in front of the customer. That's not good customer service. I'm not sure you can multi task in the drive thru, if you can't simply get cheese right on a burger, when the customer is asking for it. 

I want to say thanks to the manager who handled it the right way with no questions asked. Your service was 5 stars and it did not go unnoticed by me. Thank you so much. This is why you are the manager. I am sorry to give your location only one star but the way BK girl handled herself in a truly unprofessional way and that will not go unnoticed either. 

I also need to review the Impossible Burger. I love the product but they way you are using it is horrible. The patty is completely dried out like a thin hockey puck. Impossible Meat chars up really nice on a flat top and it's plant based so it doesn't need to be over cooked. Going through the flame broiler is just wrong. I'm so sad that after nine years with no BK, I returned to eat this horrible mess. I'm also sad for Impossible Burger because this is not a perfect place to showcase how amazing your plant based meat is. This is a gimmick for Burger King and it is accounting for a rise in their burger sales but it needs to be good. You must go back to the drawing board on how you are cooking these patties. Now I've only had this one Impossible Whooper and maybe this location doesn't get it. Maybe they serve them dried out here but at almost $6 I'm not going to be going out of my way to try one again anytime soon at another BK. 

Unfortunately I choose this Mission and it was a totally failure. No one is winning in this episode. I just wanted cheese and I was denied. It's not that hard. I also hate be talked about when all of the facts were wrong. Goodbye BK...see you in another nine years.

On Saturday, I was moving my son and his family to White House, TN.  At the end of the first day of the move around 7pm, I stopped at the Kentucky Friend Chicken at 803 Hwy 76.  We ordered a 16 piece meal for $42.79 for 4 adults.  I asked for plates and utensils.  Cashier stated they no plates. The Cashier and Servers were not wearing COVID mask.  When we got home (45 minutes later), there were NO biscuits and only 2 sets of utensils.   I immediately called, and reported the findings to the manager, Crystal.  I asked to be compensated for the partial dinner and that I was not going to drive 45 minutes back to their White House store.   Crystal stated she needed to discuss it with her regional manager, Keri.  She was not empowered to help.  It's sloppy customer service to provide a partial dinner.  And I had higher expectations for KFC.

On Sunday, I stopped by the White House KFC store at 3:00pm, and spoke with Keri.  She stated she was NOT THERE on Saturday, and she would only offer either 8 biscuits OR 4 pieces of chicken.  I don't know about you, but I did not want 8 biscuits at 3:00pm on a Sunday.  During our conversation, she keep breaking away.  Saying she could not discuss it.  I pulled out my camera to record this bedlam, and she went running away.  I asked for a business card - no way.  I asked her to write down her name, title and her manager - she wrote down Crystal name and her title as "General Manager".  I askeed her if the store was a franchise or Pepsi owned store.  After 2 or 3 break-away and returns, she finally relented and stated it was owned by "Alice Sdheler".

Alice is this how you train managers?  Is this good KFC service?  I need some help given the partial service, partial dinner, and partial response.  Help.  I'm rating my KFC experience as a 1.

I say no JUST CANCELLED THE WHOLE THING .. like what the heck is going on at this location? It's dirty and unorganized, and not supervised.. worst experience I've ever had at chipotle!

Poor service! The staff was extremely rude didn't give me a receipt they got my order wrong and then was rude when I had to drive all the way back and get the right order. Will not be going back. I just wanted to try the new chicken sandwich ‍

This order today doesn't even deserve a 1 star rating.  I got all the way to work and opened my Dunkin bag to find veggie egg white wraps instead of turkey sausage wraps.  The tag on the outside of the bag said turkey sausage but someone put the wrong wraps in my bag.  Plus, I had to ASK for napkins, AGAIN.  This location is the worst.  I only go once a week, but have found another location that I'll switch to.

This Dunkin Donuts has an amazing crew. The employees are dedicated to service first with quality being a priority too. I don't know his name but the shorter older guy there in the morning works so hard to make sure every customer is completely happy, served quickly and accurately.  He is so respectful calls me sir. He remembers exactly how I like my extra large coffee with extra extra cream and 4 equal. (Yes, I like it light and sweet like my men.) I don't go often maybe 12 times a year so him knowing my order is impressive. I get my crew for election days breakfast, snacks and lots of coffee and that large order is made quickly and always with a smile and I'm made know my business is appreciated. The boss a bigger guy not so friendly but I've never seen him wait on a customer and I'm glad. He's never even said hi. My only issue is the parking lot is too small unsafe and inconvenient. The township, Middletown should not allow high volume businesses to not have safe adequate access and parking and that unfortunately is not the case in a number of examples, thus Wawa on Oxford Valley Road. I don't eat doughnuts or muffins but the breakfast sandwich of sausage egg and cheese on a croissant is really good. (Better then BK that shapes a burger bun like a croissant and sell it as one to people that don't know any better and it's light, buttery and flakey. I have tried the hash browns but am not a fan a bit greasy and heavy.  I am impressed with the mobile app, I don't have it but watch customers walk in the door and out in less then a minute with their order. I asked a couple people using it and they said it's the best on phone app. 
Thanks, it's nice to be appreciated!

The worst location I ever ordered from. Had a mobile order that was suppose to be ready by 6:20pm and yet I waited an extra 40 mins for it to be done. Yeah I should have walked out of there but it was $27 worth of food, which by they way was disgusting. The burrito looked like a freaking smooshed up sandwich and the burrito bowl had a whole half of avocado and no steak to be found but about 2 cups of rice was there. There was a big line of people waiting to get their mobile orders and staff members just chit chatting with each other. I'm pretty sure I wasn't the only disappointed. Chipotle please do better!

Every time I go to the Old Hickory Blvd location in Hermitage, Tn Dunkin' there is always an issue with my order. I speak with the manager and there is an excuse as to why the order was messed up. (someone did not show up.. someone quit... the list goes on and on) Missing items.. not correct.. I ordered cold brew and got iced coffee. Totally not the same thing. 2 times that I ordered the steak and cheese wrap we only got 1 out of the 3 ordered. We were told to go back to pick up the missing items and when we arrived at the store we were told that they were frozen and they could not replace them at that time. Today I ordered 2 sourdough sandwiches with egg (Over cooked of course), sausage (got Bacon instead) and cheddar cheese (no cheese on the sandwich at all). No answer when I call the store ever. Left a message 2 hours ago and still no call back. One employee was reaching into the ice bin with her hand and putting ice in drinks.. no scoop and not changing the gloves after handling money or touching every thing in sight. Will not be back!! I will go out of my way to a different location and stay far from this one. 3 strikes you are out!!

I bought the single and double whopper meals.  Both meals had burgers that were lukewarm to warm.  Both meals had tomato and lettuce that were wilted and soft (likely from being placed on the burger while it was still very hot).  Each meal had hamburger buns that were too soggy against the hamburger meat (likely from the Mayo and pickles sitting too long from being made ahead) while the exterior portion of the bun was toughened.  The fries were old, lukewarm and tasted like they had been sitting a while.  I'm used to tasting the made to order freshness from other Burger King locations so I was deeply disappointed and ultimately angered by spending $21 for mediocre food that had sat around too long.  I DO NOT recommend this location!!!

I would give zero if I could. We Arrived at KFC and no one else was there ordering. We had to pull the car around and wait for 10 minutes to get our food. Ended up paying $20 For four chicken thighs, One wing, one mashed potatoes with gravy and a coleslaw. When we got home we saw that they had forgotten the two biscuits we ordered, the drink, And the cookie. There were five people working at this location!!! 
My wife repeated the order three times!!!
Next time just invite me in and I'll do the cooking so we can get the order right.

Went through the drive thru to get a bucket meal for Fathers Day. When I opened the bucket the chicken looked like slop. The skin was half on & half off.  It was swimming in grease!  I ordered extra crunchy & half of the chickens crust wasn't even done & was gross whiteish looking.  The macaroni was over cooked. The gravy was like water.  I had to bake the chicken to make it edible!  I called the manager John who sounded like a 13 year old boy.  He hug up on me!  Never going back!!!

Haven't had a bad experience at a Dunkin' Donuts location in a while. They were slow and very unorganized. The cashier couldn't hear anything and you practically had to yell your order multiple times. I ordered 2 items and watched several people that ordered more than me, get their items and leave. I finally got my order and it was completely wrong. I ordered a sausage, egg and cheese croissant with added bacon. They rung up 2 separate sandwiches until another employee came to correct the order because they actually heard what I order. I ended up with no egg and I was just over it at that point. I just left.

When I heard Burger King was getting the Impossible Whopper I was very excited. This Burger King is located right down the road from one of the power plants I work at so I decided to drive down to give it a try. Both times I came here there was only 1 or 2 people in front of me but it took quite long to order and to get my order. They seem like they might be severely under staffed. The fries weren't very good at all and tasted super bland. BUT the Impossible Whopper was delicious and and nice crisp lettuce on it. I wouldn't go out of my way to come here again but they got the job done.

Have it your way... this is a pretty standard location for Burger King. You know what to expect, it's fast food and gets the job done. This location is clean and their service is friendly. They just remodeled about a year ago and the changes are nice. 
      Honestly, there are times when I go by here and the smell does make me crave a whopper...and a glass of tea. Their breakfast menu has something that will keep you full for about a day- try the eggnourmous burrito...it has everything type of breakfast meat in it ... and then some. The only complaint is that they can be slow at certain time of the day and night, despite putting in a double drive through. This is not constant however, which is good to know.
At 5pm on a Tuesday night they say they have nothing besides chicken. Not one side item was available. Ridiculous. Lazy LAZY lazy employees here.

Worst experience ever, after ordering we got to the window to pay, asked to add a chicken little, which went totally downhill the manager who was preparing our order said he was done and he wasn't making anything else for the order. His exact words, ended with I'm done. I'm sick of this location, their hospitality is so poor and pathetic. When you go online to explain your experience they make you go through a kind survey so that you can forget to explain your situation. This place needs to be shut down!

I stopped by on Sunday 05/10 around 3:30 pm.  I ordered the $20 fill up, chicken little sandwich and large fries.  I picked up my bag after paying and ate in the parking lot with my SO.  I noticed the sandwich and fries were missing.  I went back to the drive thru just to let them know because they perhaps forgot due to the heavy volume of customers (it happens).

I got to the speaker to explain and this was the interaction:

Me: Hello, I just ordered a....

Employee: what's your name?! Is this Doordash?

Me: no, I just ordered from the drive-thru

Employee: What's your name?!?!

Me: My name is Andrew and I just ordered from the drive thru and am missing a sandwich and fries.

Employee: you ordered what?

Me: I ordered a $20 fill up, sandwich and large fries.

Employee: ok, just pull up.

I finally get there to get my missing food and the one girl with the headset just stares at me and the other lady just hands me the bag without saying a word.

Absolutely horrendous service!  I don't care about the missing food but don't give me an attitude when I am just trying to get what I paid for.

Stopped in yesterday to get some chicken.  Went inside (didn't know they aren't using inside). This place starts with liquid on the floor in the entrance.  No no one wiped up the ice cubes but put up one of this floor is wet signs.  Very dangerous walking in. Two employees could not help me but they sure could yell at me something about how busy they were and they didn't have time to...  Place is filthy.  I am going to try to contact who owns this particular store to find out if they have any cleaning policies in place and wonder how they could serve food out of there.

Worst food pick up experience ever.  Pick up was scheduled for 6:10, food was not ready until 6:50.  Our vegetarian salad had giant chunks of STEAK in it, making it completely inedible due to dietary restrictions.  The burrito was soooo tiny and cold.  

Zero stars for employees. They could not have cared less that we waited nearly an hour after our pick up time.  The store was full of very unhappy customers
Waiting 20 minutes in line for a impossible burger with hair in it ! Didn't realize until I got home. Never again

We used to love this place for authentic, quick, affordable tacos. When we went in this week, it had changed. They've sterilized the appearance and the lighting is completely over done (eg Superbright). 

To start, we were greeted with a scowl by the cashier/waitress, who was fancily dressed! The food was mediocre. Taco pastor was lacking in flavor but we love the fresh cilantro and onions. Steak quesadilla, gigantic but uninspired. Chicken burrito bland but improved with their hot sauce. The salad was dried out limes and tired cucumbers, where in the past it was super fresh and bright tasting. They are now serving with plastic utensils which is always a disappointment to me for in-house dining.

It was unpleasant to observe the waitress picking her teeth at the counter between customers and the cashier picking clogs out of the tips of the salsa dispensers with her fingernail, then promptly delivering them to someone's table. No hand washing was witnessed.  I am not a germ freak, but this was disturbing. Even if we would come back here to give the food another try, the lack of hygiene will dissuade us.

I know most people will disagree with me, but I prefer Chipotle over Panch! My heart has always belonged to Chipotle!!

Every Chipotle I've ever been to always has nice stuff and only a few of them have ever been "stingy" with my portions. 

My classic order (I normally play around and mix and match): bowl with white rice, black and/or pinto beans, chicken (sometimes steak), lettuce, cheese, veggies (always extra), salsa, corn, sour cream, guac, and queso. Absolutely delicious!!

I was super happy when they added queso but let's be honest, Panch's queso is better. But Chipotle's guacamole is hands down the best! Their queso is still delicious and does not turn me away from them!

Recently, they added cauliflower rice. I did a doordash order and paid extra for the cauliflower rice. It came with no cauliflower rice and we never got a call that they were out or anything. I was very upset but this was my only bad experience with Chipotle so I shrugged it off.

Anyways, I love coming here. I have eaten inside, outside, and done multiple pickups to eat at home. When I went to college and had a Chipotle on campus, I knew I was set.

Love you Chipotle!!!

Just left this location, and I will never go back.The wait is just crazy,then while checking our order three things are missing.We informed the cashier about the missing items,and we were looked at like we were trying get over on them for a couple of tacos.Eight minutes the "manager" asks us "what is missing" about five minutes after that we got our missing food, and of course the fist part of our to-go order is stone cold.While waiting the first time an elderly lady came in to collect some chicken because of an order being filled wrong the day before,then while waiting for our second order ( our missing food) another customer came in for the same reason.Another patron asked for mild sauce and was told they were out,and they very well may have because they gave us two giant handfuls and there were piles of them on the filthy tables in the dining room.The soda area was full of trash as well as the floor,including large amounts of food.Were we given the excuse that they were busy,not on the inside you were not,and the drive thru was steady but not crazy.My question which I should have asked was "if you were so busy Mr. Manager,why did you think it was a good time for a smoke break?". Only one star because it would not let me post no stars.

My 1st time at this location and it was about 830pm on a Wednesday night.  I wait patiently to make my way up to order. Mind you,  there aren't any notices of then being out of anything  or low on product. I get greeted by the employee(dirty blonde hair girl). I proceed to order a steak bowl after seeing another employee making one not less than a minute prior.  She proceeds to tell me they are out of STEAK! Isaid," no you're not, j just saw him make one"  she proceeds to tell me that it's only for ONLINE ORDERS?  I was like wtf?  There was plenty of steak in the pan and I just needed 2 scoops to get me by. Just can't believe that they would say something like that.  I left as I couldn't believe she gave me that excuse.

DO NOT EAT HERE 

The chicken was burned and when we asked for new chicken they said no. Lol 

Not much more to say...what the heck is going on with food places. I understand everyone is understaffed, but come  on. The chicken was burned and they wouldn't replace. It's not like it's cheap either. 

Will not be returning

FORGET TRYING TO WASTE HOURS TRYING TO GET A $.37 WHOPPER!!!!

I'm done with this fuc*ing POS burger queen imitation restaurant!
After being cajoled into loading app on phone, I tried for hours to get some "four digit code" that would grant me this "pleasure!"
The app stalled numerous times....in showed NONE of area BK's had online orders available!
Then it had 15 minute time limit. Then it blanked out again.
At this Van Dyke location...the doors were locked and the staff complained their credit card readers were OUT---cash only!
WHO CARRIES CASH?
I explained to "manager"...Mark?  and asked for rain check for this ONE 37 cent whopper that was only Friday and Saturday.
He said no; problems? Call Burger King. I thought he was BK? I offered to show him my cell that had app opened showing all locations NOT TAKING ORDERS....but he wasn't a bit understanding....or couldn't care less!

A "good" manager would've comped me a 37 cent whopper with SINCERE APOLOGIES! Yeah...I forgot....you can't hire any "good" staff even @ $15 hour!

This is most definitely the most disappointing Chipotle that I've been to. The order frequently gets messed up when I order through the app - I don't get double meat, or the vegetables are missing in a VEGETABLE bowl (i.e. NO MEAT/Sofritas!). The vegetables here are typically chopped into really thick strips that end up not being cooked fully. Last time I came here, I was given a meager amount of vegetables in my VEGETABLE bowl and barely half a scoop of guac in that bowl. The guac I've gotten has also been brown a few times.. I've given this location MANY chances, because it's the closest one in the area, so know that this review is a cumulative review over the two years of living near this area and ordering from this Chipotle. Truly disappointing.

On 8/15/16 at 3:10pm we experienced the following:
1-Employees were constantly going in and out, smoking or visiting with passerbys.
2-Tables were all filthy as well as the floor.  God forbid what the restrooms looked like. Slipped several time with my cane.
3-Flies were terrible landing on our food, table and bodies.
4-What we ordered was not available.
5-Got a chicken fried steak without a knife.  Asked the young man for a knife and butter for our biscuits, but never got them.  Went and stood in line and by the time I got back to the table my husband had eaten his steak with his fingers!
6-Poor service, poor food and deplorable dining area.  We won't be back and will let our friends know how your restaurant presents itself.

Shame management!

Fucking sucks. Sucking sucks. Got charged twice one and Uber eats delivery meal had so much food missing (beans, STEAK, onions) Disappointing, my first review ever and this location deserves it. Staff sucks ass they need to see who the fuck is managing this place.

Went down to get to $24 dinner well thank they took my order which was coleslaw mashed potatoes and of course chicken and gravy, got home didn't have any mashed potatoes or coleslaw I did have a gravy to two baskets of gravy can you believe it and one little box of macaroni and cheese, if I could get him a zero I would give him, If you by there go to the parking lot and check your order otherwise you're going to be screwed ,

Just picked up an eight piece meal, the green beans were disgusting, very sour, they tasted like they had too much vinegar or something, me and my mother both have just a little bit, and Are saving them for testing in case we get sick. The chicken was cooked too long, dry and tasteless, I can't believe this restaurant serves food like this, I will never be back. If this was the last restaurant on earth and my family was starving to death, we will starve to death before going back to this so Called restaurant

The Impossible Burger is another reason (first reason was the Morning Star veggie burger) was BK is ahead of the game! They were wise to create a burger that all the vegetarians out there can enjoy. I've always enjoyed the Morning Star veggie burger but had hoped they could have put it on the grill for a little crisp. But they weren't allowed to. Now with the Impossible Burger, they will throw it on the grill (unfortunately, it is shared with the real beef patties), or you can request it's put in the oven with the original veggie patties. I had mine done on the grill and it was really good! Not sure a meat eater would look at it and not question the patty like the commercials suggest. Maybe that's why they filmed  at nighttime? Hmm. Any who, the patty lacks that greasy shine but still has the charbroiled taste.
Had to $20 fill up for our big family and the chicken was very good, by the looks of it maybe we were lucky by the looks of the yelp, used I said it 81 year old chicken eater

to bad,the oil must be rancid,the  chicken was not bad but the breading was nasty,the oil isnt cleaned and changed as should so you get nasty fried chicken,dark in color,which will make you sick,its an issue for a lot of reasons,like the minimum wage,would 15 dollars an hour change the quality control in these places ?wont be back,publix isnt much better,there the chicken is foul,seems all fast food is pretty bad.

If you're looking for a family friendly and high end restaurant this is the way to go. Service was amazing and obviously hired top-notch workers! Food was too die for...If i could give them 10 stars I would. Gravy melted in my mouth and chicken was cooked medium rare and to perfection. If you're looking for high end luxury food don't bother to look anywhere else but here.

I came to this restaurant with my partner. We ordered online, because the drive thru line was ridiculous. Their lobby was closed. "No problem," we thought, "we'll call, and see if they'll bring our food out to us." 

No one answered the phone. 

So we waited for 20 minutes in the drive thru line. When we got to the speaker, they told us to wait out front. We were given an order that was missing a drink, with chicken strips half the size they usually are. My drink was just carbonated water, and they were out of iced tea. They asked what I would like instead. They did not wait for a reply from my partner, and just gave us two of what I asked for.

On top of all this, the drive thru is atrociously designed, trapping you in the line as soon as you get there.

Avoid this location.

Ordered 80 pieces of chicken for a get together. Was told when I picked them up they would adjust the price so I would get a better deal than retail. Chicken was wrapped in metal containers when it first came out of the fryer and it sweated in those containers and became soggy. Nothing like 80 pieces of soggy chicken. Called to speak to their manager twice and still have not heard from anyone. Plus I was charged complete retail. Lousy service and no communication makes for a sour customer that won't be back.

This is not the fist time I have ordered from this restaurant, and each time there was something wrong with my order. Just today I have placed a simple order of burrito bowl and nothing else through the delivery app. My estimated time was 20-30 mins max. I received my order hour and a half later. The food came cold, there was almost no rice, little amount of steak, they completely ignored that the order had guacamole. If I could give this specific restaurant 0 stars I would.

Kentucky Fried Chicken I think gets a bad rap, because they keep coming up with stupid menu items like the Double Down and the Famous Bowl a/k/a the Failure Pile; because it's employees are particularly ignorant, even by fast food standards, rivaled only by people who work at Taco Bell; and because they won't put a KFC buffet in an area with a significant black population. But never mind all that. It's the chicken that matters - and the chicken is fucking delicious. There's a reason you can hardly get out of a KFC without spending $7+. Quality costs money.

KFC gives you the choice between Original Recipe or Extra Tasty Crispy, or - god forbid - Grilled, but for my money you gotta go with that Original Recipe. Otherwise, why even bother going out of your way to go to KFC (if you notice, KFC is always out of your way), when you could go to Lee's Famous Recipe, or the grocery store - or the almighty Popeyes, for that matter. IIRC they only introduced Extra Tasty Crispy in the 1980s, to compete with the ultra-tasty Lee's Famous Recipe, which tried to undercut KFC by taking any competitor's coupon. But I could be mistaken. I was a very young man at the time. At any rate, I think it was after the Colonel died. I remember seeing on A&E Biography how the Colonel was this skeevy old man who traveled from city to city down south trying to sell his patented blend of 11 herbs and spices to restaurants. Then some corporation bought it from him for [Austin Powers voice] $1 million, and turned around and almost immediately made like a billion dollars from it, thus making him look like an idiot. But then he ended up dying a few years later, so it didn't really matter. Being alive to witness the advent of Extra Tasty Crispy would have only added insult to injury.

The meal I went with was the two piece, all white, with two sides and a biscuit, for seven dollars and some change. I'm actually partial to a chicken thigh, but I like to get a breast from KFC, because I'm always amazed at how big they are. (Schwing!) The one I had today was roughly the size of one of the smaller chickens you might get at the grocery store. KFC is obviously pumping them full of some shit the Russians invented to cheat in the Olympics, like in Rocky IV. In fact, I don't even want to know how the chickens at KFC get so big. I just wonder if there's any way the same technology could be applied to women. But I digress. Anyway, I lucked out today. KFC is notorious for trying to sell you the same chicken that's been sitting under a heat lamp since 10 AM, in hopes that some degenerate might want a two piece and a biscuit for breakfast. Especially if you go to the drive through, in which case you can't see what you're getting until it's too late. But this chicken I had today was obviously hot out of the grease. The inside was as moist and tender as... I'm not even gonna go there. I don't know if I just got there at the right time or what. Or who knows, maybe KFC is trying to step its game up. This location in particular was recently remodeled, and it looks hella classy.

I have had some bad experiences at various KFC locations in the Reno/Sparks area. And the reviews on Yelp show that I am not alone. You can't find a KFC with more than 2.5 or 3 stars. However, I am a fried chicken lover and I love KFC no matter what. I came to this location for lunch with a co worker expecting a mediocre yet "did the job" experience and it was surprisingly much more than that. I don't know if I just happened to be there on a good day or if the KFC owners or perhaps corporate representatives started noticing that diners were not happy with the restaurants in this area, but from the service to the food, everything was great and vastly improved. The staff was very friendly and attentive, and the food was fresh and delicious. They took care in the aesthetics on how my tray was presented to me. Most importantly the fried chicken was cooked perfectly. Most of the time I get old, dried out, soggy pieces with hardly any meat on the bone. I hope to see more of the locations start to take greater care and pride in their service like this one.

I don't understand how this kfc is still in business. I am not a fan of fried chicken but my family is so I usually give in when they suggest KFC. It NEVER fails though that they mess up the order. I cannot tell you how many times I have been given incorrect food, or missing items. When you bring it to the staffs attention they just shrug it off, no pride in their jobs at all.

The KFC I typically go to when wanting fried chicken is currently closed for renovations. So my brother and I took to yelp to read some reviews and find a different want to go to. After reading a ton of reviews of several KFCs near me, my brother and I decided to go to this location because it had the least amount of really bad reviews. I have to agree that the staff could care less about anything. The guy at the counter that took my order didn't say anything at all when I came up, so I had to say ready? Still not saying anything he nodded, told him our order (2 buffets) , and then he grabbed cups and plates and didn't give me a total or anything but I figured out it was time to pay from the pin pad flashing at me.  The place was packed when we got there (we ate around 6 pm). Cleanness was like a 2/5. Some tables were wiped down/cleaned but a majority weren't. The table we say that we had to clean it off of all the crumbs and such left behind.  

4 stars you may ask? The food was great! It was hot and they were constantly filling it up. In the end, I don't care if the staff didn't say anything when ordering because buffets are well, self serve. I'm fine cleaning off my own table, it was really busy so benefit of the doubt that no one had time to clean the tables off just yet. The buffet, they kept all the food coming by stocking the line several times within minutes during our visit. They had crispy and original chicken options, mashed potatoes, gravy and a lot of other fixings, they also had salad. I could see us going back to this location again because the food was hot and wonderful.
Worst KFC they need to shut this place down or get new management. Terrible service always out of chicken and they only KFC I been to that don't serve grilled chicken.

What else can I say about Chipotle? It's almost like Moes or Qdoba, staff are very pleasant and fast, food is good as always i usually order a burrito with steak and rice, it's a good enough meal for me, it fills me up and it's affordable too, it's always a good place to have dinner here but it's not my first option

Chipotle really needs to reevaluate this location. The food quality and consistency is lacking at best and down right bad at worst(I ordered a steak salad- not a bowl of cheese with no protein). Customer service couldn't care less about resolving issues from this store. Chipotle is my go to take out and experiences with this location and customer service have me staying away.

I really hate to give anyone a one star rating but I do believe in honesty and, if managers and/or owners read reviews, then businesses can be improved which in turn, benefits both the customers and the business. I was running errands when my daughter asked me to pickup some Popeye's biscuits and chicken. I wasn't anywhere near a Popeye's so thought I'd grab some KFC. I hadn't eaten there in years. I won't be going back soon if at all. I ordered an eight piece extra crispy bucket with some biscuits and wedge fries. They were out of breasts so they gave me grilled ones instead. I remember their grilled chicken being really yummy but these breasts were hard and dry. We couldn't chew them, they were literally like leather! The fried chicken wasn't much better as it had too much batter and seemed a little undercooked. The biscuits were tasteless. Wedge fries get a B and we gobbled them up. We threw away most of the chicken and all of the biscuits. It sucks to have paid $25 for something we couldn't eat. I don't know how they stay afloat serving food as crappy as that.

I love chipotle but this location has turned me off to it! 

Every single time I order, it's an unpleasant experience. When I order through the app for delivery, my order either never arrives or arrives with missing items. So I decide for next time to order for pickup, however that's always terrible as well. I end up having to wait almost a half hour past the "promised" pick up time to get my food only to find out my order is either completely wrong or missing items! You can never win at this place.

Last night I placed my order 30 minutes before for a 6:00 pm pick up. Now I know it's prime dinner time so they're going to be busy. I showed up at 6:15 expecting my order to be ready but it wasn't. I was waiting with 10 other people inside the store while only three workers work in a small section trying to fulfill all the orders in a rush. I don't understand why they can't work  in the assembly line they have in front of the store? If it's for COVID safety reasons it makes no sense because everyone is instead crammed in the store not social distancing for long periods of time while waiting for their food. Service would be faster if we were allowed to order in person and it would ensure order accuracy. Anyways, 25 minutes later I finally got my order but when I got home discovered it was completely wrong. I ordered a chicken bowl with veggies, lettuce, brown rice and black beans. What I got was a half filled bowl with sofritas, white rice, and pinto beans. Totally innacurate! I submitted a complain and have yet to hear back but I won't be coming back to this location until I can order in person, maybe.

Wort customer service I have ever received. They didn't have chicken one night and another night they only had grilled chicken. How can you be a chicken restaurant and not have chicken?? Someone explain to me!!! I would give no stars if I could.

We have purchased donuts here 3 times this summer.  We go through the drive through, not sure you can go inside.  The first time our ice tea was missing.  The second time was fine.  The last time they left out an egg and sausage breakfast sandwich. Before leaving the window my husband asked if  the breakfast sandwich was there and he said yes.  It wasn't.   I know the are busy, but this pattern is really upsetting.  We never try to go back to pick up the missed items since the line of cars is so long and we don't get a receipt.

I eat lunch here regularly and have no issues with this store.  I find it much better than the one located at Cortaro & I-10 because for one their playground here is inside and the Cortaro location the PG is outside and un-usable in the summer.

Right now, BK is promoting three new Memphis BBQ sandwiches that rival Carl's Jr.  I had the Whopper today which comes with swiss cheese and a Carolina sauce.  I left only tomato & lettuce and excluded the usual condiments along with the bacon and cole slaw.  Yes, it is a Memphis BBQ promotion which typically has cole slaw on a BBQ sandwich.  It was very good and I'll be ordering it again.

But the pulled pork sandwich I had previously tried at the Cortaro location.  Assuming they would have tasted the same regardless of location, I did not order it again. Not that the sandwich was bad, it was ok, but I was ruined by the Carl's Jr sandwich which added the pulled BBQ to a burger.  That thing was the BOMB!

They also have a new BBQ tenderloin sandwich I have yet to try.

I also like the variety of ice cream desserts here much better than other fast foods chains.  The ice cream rivals Dairy Queen and doesn't taste like that ice milk that comes free at so many buffets.

Beautiful place. Recently opened. Super friendly staff. However, with no line, I had to wait quite a while for them to make carnitas only to move on to another meat cause it was taking too long for them to cook. Probably sat for about 3-4 minutes. The line girls apologized and continued with my bowl (which was now cold) when I got the new meat. The lettuce looked like it was a day old. It was soggy. Not as fresh as usually seen at Chipotle. It didn't taste as fresh as it usually is from other Chipotles so I scooped it off. When I got home, I didn't even finish half the bowl :( 

You would have thought they would have offered a free drink, free guac, or free chips for the inconvenience of waiting, but nope. nothing. I was SO excited for this one to open, but honestly, I'll stick with my Pinellas Park one for now.

I thought it was only ok. So many people hype about this place, but it's average in my opinion. I ordered a steak burrito, and I felt it was a tad plain. Maybe it's my fault for not throwing in much veggies, but I felt the meat and rice could have used a little more flavor. Don't get me wrong, it was good, but it just wasn't great. I think they need a special sauce to top it off! 

Service was good. Fast and welcoming. Price is also pretty reasonable.

Overall: If you need something fast and want to avoid your typical fast food, this could be your spot. I prefer their competition, but to all their own.

I hate to add to the 1 star reviews. This place sucks. I feel the biggest problem is this chain is owned by a cheap SoB. Rather than throw out the old stuff it's all this place serves. I've ordered a few times here out of sheer laziness not going to the one on Ina. Rude, lazy staff, and dirty old food. I've even been shorted 2 pieces of chicken on the family 8 piece. When brought back they basically called me a liar. This place is a joke.

The 99 cent bean burritos (order them extra green) are classic.  Del Taco is everything Taco Bell should be, but isn't.  Avoid their burgers, cheese fries and don't let them squirt their "secret sauce" on anything, and certainly not their caulking gun "guacamole."  The chicken burritos are great in every incarnation, again, just make sure and have the secret sauce replaced with green sauce.  The green sauce is good, like Alburquerque good.

3-16-18 time 2:00PM
Drive thru - whopper & small fries......It was EXCELLENT and lightly salted.  This is a winner 
Junior whopper & small fries.....Excellent 
Keep up the high quality......we love it

Former Steak n Shake customer. Good not as good as BurgerKing. 
McDonald has low quality in some of their product line.  Burger King is a winner hands down

God Bless Del Taco.

Let me start this shit off by saying that I think Taco Bell sucks.  Dicks.

That being said, there are very few taquerias in St. Louis, let alone fast Mexican joints that are not Taco Bell.  Fuck El Maguey, Fuck Casa Gallardo, Del Taco is where its at, Motherfucker.

Veggie Works Burrito, Spicy Jack Quesadilla and some crinkle cuts?  Yes, please!  This place had kickass vegetarian options years before Taco Bell even had the common courtesy to come out with a fucking quesadilla.  

I don't care if this place makes you shit your ass, eat it.  Your children will thank me.

went here for Friday night dinner because no one felt like cooking. The chicken was moist and tender, the mashed potato's were quite good as was the biscuit. They even included a chocolate chip cookie , which was unexpected, this location is always busy and I have never had a bad meal here. I doubt you'll have a bad meal here either just be prepared to wait for food because quality takes time.

Tldr; Staff at this Chipotle told me that their sofritas (which are supposed to be vegan) are not vegetarian and have never been.

I usually love Chipotle. And I've had satisfactory experiences here in the past. After today's visit, I may never frequent Chipotle again.

1. Asked for a veggie bowl with soft tacos on the side. Lady started getting the large tortilla they use for burritos ready, and eh I didn't care enough to complain. But first strike.

2. She asked me which meat I wanted. Suddenly thinking of the lack of protein in my diet, I asked for sofritas. Her response: "They're not vegetarian." What?! They're widely advertised as vegan, there's a "v" next to it on the menu! So I asked again: "It's tofu, how can it not be vegetarian?" She mumbled something about how their sofritas aren't vegetarian. Whatever, I moved on.

(Heard her ask one of the cooks why they 're not vegetarian, unfortunately couldn't catch his response)

3. Got to the cashier. Asked her: "Hey, have your sofritas here always not been vegetarian?" Her response: "Yes." Me: "Then why is there a v on the menu next to it?" Her: "I don't know."

I've ordered sofritas many times there in the past, so I'm very concerned that either their sofritas here aren't vegetarian (as they're advertised to be everywhere). Alternatively, none of their staff has a clue. Not sure which is worse.

Relatively small portion (compared to the past) means that I'd give this place negative stars if I could.

I ordered 2 medium fries, cold and hard, I ordered a big fish sandwich, super small portion, cold and hard.  I ordered a dbl whopper with ketchup, mayo, onion and cheese, i got no cheese and 3 times the amount of mayo i should i have, i had to scrape it off.  I ordered another dbl whopper with ketchup mustard cheese and pickles, i got a dbl whopper with lettuce, tomato ketchup.  

awful awful awful, i will never go back.  the manager acted like he didn't care and didn't act like he knew what was going on.

Terrible staff, including the manager. 

Came in and inquired about chicken fingers... the employee taking my order was super short with me with his answers. I was able to gather that the 12 of tender meal was what I needed.... was told it came with 6 sauces as well.

Got home and they forgot the sauces. Called the restaurant and the manager "Antoine" was 0% apologetic and told me it was my responsibility to specify that I wanted sauces, even though they told me it came with sauces. No attempt to rectify the situation.

Won't be coming back. Gives KFC a bad name with the attitude-filled service

I could go here for an early lunch and be content for the rest of the day (and that's saying a lot). 

I'm a chipotle advocate in general, but when speaking specifically about true chipotle on 39th and walnut I can't say enough positive things. 

There are times when I walk in and see the line and immediately get a bit depressed, like today for instance. It was literally out the door, but I was so pleasantly surprised when I found myself ordering in under 10 mins. 

The food itself truly needs no explanation, but for all my non-believers out there let me just say you are living an unfulfilled life. They almost always serve up enjoyable portions and have options for both Jose that love em at and those that don't. 

I found today's visit to be especially enjoyable because I received a healthy help thing of both carnitas and chicken on my burrito and straight up gorged. I always love going there as a quick lunch or dinner and will always be a staunch supporter of their food. If you haven't gone yet you are truly missing out.

Chipotle is pretty consistent wherever you go.  This Chipotle is right on the Vanderbilt campus so it stays pretty busy whenever school is in session.  The food is great and I usually go with a burrito.  One negative that I have noticed about this Chipotle is sometimes the steak is undercooked and seems to be very rare.  I usually like my steak medium but this steak is pink and I've noticed it on more than a couple occasions.  This doesn't stop me from going though and I would recommend Chipotle to anyone who loves burritos and tacos.

This is the world's worst Carl's Jr. We have ever been to. The Onion rings were cold and over cooked. The Crisscut fries were cold over cooked and hard! My husband's Chicken Strips were tiny and rock hard, over cooked and like chewing leather! My mom's Grilled Chicken Club was over cooked dry and cold.  I have always loved Carl's Jr, but will never return here. I'd rather serve my family 7-11 burgers and toquitos, with a slurry chaser. 
This specific location is highly not recommended by us for anyone breathing. 
I'd gladly give this dive a half star, but the Yelp site won't let me.

went to pick up dinner but was told they did not have anything cooked.  was told it would take 15 minutes for the biscuits and 10 minutes for the chicken.  this was 6:00 pm and they have no food. really poor management. went somewhere else for dinner.

This Burger King has it together! As a child, my family would go to BK once a month as a treat and it was always good. In recent times, it seems that all BKs have gone downhill collectively. I seem to give  them a chance once or twice a year and I'm always disappointed and mad that I wasted the calories.

Today was different. My whopper was hot. The fries were crispy and streaming. I actually got the extra pickles and onions I requested. 

No, it's not gourmet and is terrible for you, but this BK got it right. 

I haven't had a decent whopper in years.

Originally I gave them a 5. Now they are a 1. 

I used to work for kfc so a part of me understands how long chicken takes. However, these are my complaints. 

1) they changed their phone number but still have not updated up Yelp or google. So I couldn't pre order my hot wings that take 9.5 minutes to cook 

2) they never chicken seriously, it's a fast food chicken restaurant. I understand that it takes time to make chicken. However, more than 4 separate occasions has he wait been 20 minutes. At this point, I have no idea why they don't understand to prepare during dinner rushes and lunch rushes. So no there is no excuse for this many times already

3) they have a sign plastered on their glass window that shoes $15 for a 10 piece meal. They said they cannot honor it anymore because the deal is over. It's still up. Seriously that is false advertising. I am calling on corporate on you and seeing if there is any legal action I can put forth. 

You guys are terrible
I am a Chipotle fanatic, however this location is the worst Chipotle I've ever been too. They are consistently out of everything (white rice, steak, sweet tea). They over season also.

If you like Chipotle food, and I do like Chipotle food, then you'll like this UC Chipotle because they make their Chipotle food just like all the other Chipotle restaurants. 

I always get the same great thing - burrito bowl, black beans, carnitas, medium hot salsa and corn. Also ice tea. 

If you go online, in terms of calories, you can see what you should and shouldn't order (jesus christ kiddo, skip the tortilla!).

What I don't like about this particular incarnation of Chipotle is the seating area. Loud and uncomfortable, so I always get my burrito bowl to go. (Seating area at the Urbana-Champaign Chipotle is way better)

Also....in my opinion, Mexican Food in Philly sucks, so in Philly this is about as good as it gets. If you want good Mexican food go to El Paso, New Mexico, or Arizona.

My daughter and I had a lunch date yesterday and decided on Chipotles.  

We have had Chipotles before but this time was quite disappointing.  I wanted to place two orders. The first, a bowl. The second, a burrito.  Both with the same ingredients.  I noticed that the initial server was in an unusual rush thus making her 1st mistake in giving me chicken when I asked for shredded beef.  When I informed her of her error, which wouldn't have occurred if she was listening, she then stated that if she added Barbacoa, it would be additional costs to me. That was her 2nd mistake.  I didn't ask for a mixture to begin with. And once she realized her error, she grabbed the bowl and threw it into the trash.  I had no idea Chipotles took part in throwing away food just for the hell of it.  

Now, onto the burrito.  Once I began nearing the end of the line to pay for the order, I realized that after they added the Barbacoa to the burrito, someone then placed cubed steak onto it. Which I never asked for.  I made it pretty clear to the servers that rushing orders causes unnecessary mistakes and voids quality customer service.  The cashier apologized for the inconvenience that the servers should have given.

Overall, although my daughter and I enjoyed the flavor of our lunch, it wasn't a pleasant experience ordering it. I wanted to get out of there in a hurry from being so aggravated.

Do better Chipotles.

Luv Hardee's! They are not the easiest to find but when I get a chance I'll go. Staff was friendly to all that came through the door, they also bring yu yur food. Got the 1/3 thick burger combo with fries, no real complaints. The fries were warm, not hot & the burger was the same, burger seems pretty dry but had lots of tomatoes,lettuce & condiments on it which made it better. I will take off one star for the burger & the working staff being a little too loud in the back, no biggy. They were having a good time. Then some guy comes & sits at another table right next to me to eat, face to face, what is this I said to myself? The place is empty, yu have to sit right next to me.....gezzzz!
received very fast service and the food was good.  The new rotisserie chicken is excellent and I will be recommending that my friends and family all try this product at this location!

Holy tergiversation batman, am I seriously giving Carl's Jr four stars?  Have I lost my mind, my palate, my conscience?  Has some ecdemic North Korean agent hacked my brain and left me an addlepated, mass-consuming, excrescence of corporate-cartel-klepto-bureaucratic America?  Well, um perhaps.  You see Carl's Jr has come out with a rather shocking, startling, odd, antithetical concept, a healthy, all natural, grass-fed, steroid-free, free-range Australian burger patty at $4.99 for a single and $6.99 for a double, thereby giving away the real price of the patty at $2.00.  So I was conflicted.  This is like the Nazi's coming out with a health camp for Communists where instead of killing them, they make them do calisthenics and read about Buddhism and meditate and debate each other freely and think critically about epistemological neologistic paradigmatic exegetical heuristics.  I don't know how far I can milk that metaphor, but you have to give me credit for trying.  You notice I don't say Jew, because quite honestly, I don't want Jews telling me it's an inappropriately insensitive metaphor.  I'd rather Communists tell me.  

Can you really applaud a chain that otherwise abuses animals and customers?  I'm making a statement here, and it may not be entirely sincere, but the point is, well, are these reviews really all about sincere criticism or a bunch of entitled conscientiously gourmandizing Millennials b tching about the most negligible trivia just to get back at their parents who substituted consumerism for affection.  Hence, consumerism is like how I show you how much I love you, but if you don't get love at a restaurant, you must one star them.  Anyhow, Carl's Jr deserves something for admitting to the world that there is actually something called an unnatural burger and that's just about every other burger on its menu and the menu of every other fast food chain in the universe.  But how did it taste?  Honestly, it could have tasted like a wet toilet paper roll and I would have been happy.  It didn't taste actually great or bad.  It was a thin, flat, irregularly shaped patty which meant that it was making at least an attempt not to look like a conformist stamped circle from a mould and perhaps suffered a hint of imperfect human handling.  Nonetheless it was flat like Wile E Coyote after the bulldozer driven by a demented bird.  Perhaps Wile E Coyote is a metaphor for humanity, but let's not go there.  I was actually more impressed by the bun, actually a tasty lightly toasted product with the slightest hint of fermenting yeast.  You may consider this a rather euphuistic aberration of a review, but if you like to insult yourself with the captious screed other reivewers pass off for information, I can understand your confusion.

On August 26, 2017 I had a baby shower at a local banquet hall two blocks from this KFC. I had ordered 100 pieces of chicken from a local supermarket but when I went to pick it up, the supermarket had not made my order and there was no one to make it. The baby shower had started and I had no entree to serve. I called this KFC and asked if they could make a large order. They asked how many pieces I needed. I tentatively said 50. The guy who answered put me on hold for a few seconds and then came back to the line and recited a price. Desperately needing an entree I accepted. He said give him 30 minutes and the chicken would be ready. True to his word he chicken was ready in thirty minutes.  The food was tasty and they saved the party.

I ordered a bucket of crispy chicken. They had to check to see if they had enough of both original and crispy. I also ordered two sides of green beans and a side of potato wedges .
By the way , waited and waited at the drive through for someone to take my order .

When the give me my order and I check it, they gave me two order of mash and gravy and cole slaw. I explained that I didn't order this and no one at home eats those items . I asked her why gave them to me and said, they were out of green beans and only had enough potatoes wedges for the car behind me (ahmmm don't get it ). I explained to her that I ordered the wedges first , I'm in front of the car behind me (lol). 
She is arguing with her manger on what to do . 
They give me two side of Mac and cheese , my potato wedges and forget the biscuits. 

I didn't mention how loooong I had to wait at the drive through window . 
Horrible service!
Every time I come to this location they are out of white meat chicken and the service is extremely slow and disorganized.

I would give this Burger King a half star if I could but I can't. I went there twice in two months and both times they got my order wrong. On my last and final visit I specifically asked for my Whopper with no sauce (dry), and cheese. I even avoided the drive thru so there would be no confusion. I went inside and at the time I was the only customer in line. Ten minutes later (seriously) my order is ready and by then there's a large line. (One customer even came inside yelling they got his drive thru order wrong). I go to my car and proceed to eat the whopper in the car. Bad idea. Ketchup and mustard are oozing out of the burger and onto my pants. The burger itself -the one that I patiently waited 10 minutes for- is cold and lifeless- not to mention the slice of cheese thrown on top of the burger has absolutely no chance of melting. They even forget the only veggie I requested. Pickles. I immediately take the meal back in and have a nice talk with the manager. The manager assures me that he is working with the recently hired cook and to give him time - he's learning. The manager then proceeds to replace my burger with a fresh burger and upsize my fries for my inconvenience. I go back out to my car. This time the burger is piping hot and sauce less. However, the pickles are missing again. On top of that, the new fries the manager gave me are stone cold. I have all these remaining Burger King coupons. I'll just have to find a different Burger King to use them at.

I came here with a coupon to get some food to go. 

I had the $3.99 coupon for a 2 piece chicken (drumstick and thigh), coleslaw, mashed potatoes, and a biscuit. I got the chicken extra crispy. The food was good but I felt like they gave me small pieces just so it would fit in the box with the sides and the biscuit. I've been to other KFC's and they would always put the chicken and biscuit in the box and then the sides in a different bag or separated from the rest of the food. The coleslaw is cold so putting the chicken next to it would kind of warm up the coleslaw. I don't think coleslaw is supposed to be warm. 

The service was ok. This was the main reason why it's only 3 stars. I ordered and the cashier had an attitude. She didn't seem like she wanted to be there and work. She didn't even ask if I wanted grilled, original or crispy. I had to tell her crispy. After telling her, then she proceeded to ask about the sides I wanted. I gave her my coupon and she gave it to her coworker to do the transaction. I gave her the money and then she just gave the receipt and forgot to give me my change back. I said "what about my change?" and then she forgot and had to get the key from a manager or something and had to open the register again to give me my change. 

The interior is alright. It's clean but it's because no one was there. The parking is fine too. Enough space for a lot of people. 

Note: This location is also a Taco Bell.

Well  I just grabbed a steak bowl to go. It is the first time I had been in  Chipotle in a while and unfortunately I probably wont go back for a while. Everyone was very friendly and rhey gave me tons of guac (score!) Which is the reason for 3 stars instead of 2. However, I was told the steak is grass-fed  but it was tough as nails and very grisly.
