And I dont mean just one or two pieces..but thw whole thing. I was deeply disappointed. The grilled veggies were good but I didnt pay.10.50 for veggies .

I know its weird for someone to ask for a burger, with no meat. But its not a hard thing to understand. Like im a vegetarian and i just wanted everything else on the whopper EXCEPT the meat. And they put everything except the bun in a salad container. And on top of everything my fries were cold as hell.

Not at all impressed. First the  bathrooms are dirty and don't smell good at all. I found the chicken quality cooking to be sub par. It was quite greasy.

My biggest beef is customer service. There was an older couple in their 70s next to my table. They had a question about a charge on their bill that they did not understand. I overheard their conversation with the staff and I think they were definitely spoken down to. Hey said they will never return and neither will I. Happy to argue with customers but too lazy to clean the bathrooms.

I have never had Chipotle before- so maybe my review of this specific store is biased.. But I don't care! This was good and a stellar option in University City!

I came in to try the new Sofritas. Being a vegan always on the lookout for new and exciting vegan goodies, I was desperate to give these a try. And I do not regret it. 
I had them in a bowl with brown rice, black beans, grilled veggies, pico de gallo, lettuce, and some spicy sauce. It was a very spicy meal overall. The sofritas themselves were the strangest thing- it tasted nothing like tofu- the pieces were so crumbled up that it was all sauce, aka DELICIOUS and a great cruelty free way to enjoy the benefits of tofu for those who don't like the taste. 

Eat all the sofritas!

Have been coming here for several years. Whopper Wednesday is where it is at. Only reason I give it 4 stars is that sometimes the Whopper comes out not so good and dry but generally you can expect the awesome Whopper most of the time.

Another thing - I've only recently become aware of the harmfulness of MSG - and KFC uses more of it than any other fast food place.  These KFC's really have nothing in common with the original Kentucky Fried Chicken.

It always smells great when I drive by here. If only the food I bought from here was as good. I don't normally eat fast food but when I do, I usually regret it. 

I didn't want to spend too much here so I just opted for a couple things off of their "value menu". The Whopper Jr for 1.99 isn't exactly a value, it's a joke. The 1.29 BK stacker? it's really sad, I think it's supposed to have bacon on it but I couldn't tell. Both were dry and really small.

I guess you get what you pay for. 

What ever happened to the "buck double" that kicked so much ass? oh yeah, they advertised the hell out of it then realized they weren't making any money on it, so it's gone.

Just like the buck double, this place used to be good. The parking lot is pretty bad too. Getting into the drive thru lane can easily create a traffic jam at one entrance if there are more than 2 cars waiting.

I usually have a good experience when I come here until last time when I got the carnitas instead of what I usually get which is chicken and steak. The carnitas were not fresh at all. I let my mom try it because she's usually I better judgement of food and she told me to throw it out.

Chipotle was the best bet for some quality protein (for me). I purchased the usual: rice, beans, veggies, and guac. Whatever is vegan. Not the sofritas though. Not a fan. Too salty. At this location, the employees were more knowledgeable compared to my last experience. So glad they knew that the guac is free with veg meals. Awesome! The service was quick! Chipotle in Brooklyn Center, MN...take note! 

Rating: 5 stars for good and quick service!
Chipotle is consistent and good. I always get the steak burrito with corn and hot salsa. The staff was friendly and efficient.

Didn't want to cook tonight so ran to Burger King on Big Bend Rd and 301 area and ordered 2 fish filets, 2 whopper jrs, fries and 2 soft drinks. NASTY is all I can say about the fish filets, NEVER again.

Since when does a fast food employee think they can call a customer a BITCH?

I went through the drive through today. I asked the girl if there was a price break for 6 or 12 biscuits. I guess that was too hard of a question for them because in less than 2 minutes I was livid. I even had to ask for how much it was going to cost for only the chicken 3 times and the last time I yelled it.

I will say that during the argument, I asked the girl..... "After all these years of going to school, are you not capable of doing simple math?" Her response was that she had not worked there for very long.

Finally, I tell her to forget the order and drive off. As I'm driving off I hear her yell out the window.... BYE BITCH!
How does a CHICKEN place run out of CHICKEN fillets?!?!?! We stopped here traveling home and try to order a chicken sandwich.....they ran out of chicken fillets.  Pathetic & frustrating!!

My drive through order was unsweetened iced tea and the turkey sausage flatbread.  The breakfast sandwich stunk so BAD that I had to throw it out immediately.  I did not pull through the drive thru again to complain or reorder.  I just will never go back.

If I could give minus stars I would.  I find it amazing that they are still in business!  They always run out of food.  8 PM Tuesday evening and they are out of corn and green beans.  They stopped selling corn on the cob at this location yet it is still on the menu.  There have been times when they run out of chicken hours before close.  The Asst Manager claims they have to follow the projections of their software to minimize waste.  Their software is utter garbage.  I think I might get what I want only 10-20% of the time.  The wait is always too long cause they are always understaffed.  The manager here needs to be fired.  Stay away!

It's chipotle. What else can I say. I know what to expect. It doesn't disappoint and always dog friendly. I always get the same thing here a burrito bowl with carnitas, fajita veggies, corn salsa.  All is good in the world of Chipotle.

Worst KFC ever!  Don't know what the problem is with this branch but we have tried them four times and every single time they have screwed up the order.  All were drive through.  First time we had no slaw.  Second time they gave us a container of gravy that was only one third full (when I called she said that was all they had.  What?!!?)  Third time we were missing biscuits - not the entire box just shorted us one (?).  And this LAST time we received grilled chicken instead of original.  Now how in the heck do you confuse grilled and original?  Never again - the one up on Missouri & Belleair usually does a much better job and is just as close.

Food was amazing; Whopper was fresh and right off the broiler.  None of that microwaving a four hour old burger crap.  But what made this visit perfect was Kenneth the cashier.  THE MOST polite, respectful and well-mannered employee (of not just Burger King or any fast food restaurant, but ANY restaurant, period) I've ever had the pleasure of dealing with.  Best BK I've ever been to and I've been to a lot!

I went to this chipotle last night around 7pm and there was a line of about 5 people ahead of me and an almost full restaurant, so you could say that they were a bit busy. Being busy is no excuse for the poor service I received. They were confused about my order and almost gave me the wrong meal, they were sloppy and threw my food together with no care. Because they were not paying attention to to what I ordered the guy asked the person behind me what to put on my tacos. They were flustered and did not handle being busy very well. To top it off I had two crunchy tacos, they did not drain the sofritas and when I asked for it to go they threw it into the foil and folded it all up. When I opened my food 5 minutes later everything was all smashed and the shells just fell apart. Terrible experience!

I've been a huge fan of Chipotle for over five years now. I was so glad when I saw Chipotle opened in Philly, and even more glad to see one in University city. 

BUT...
All i can say is that the staff at the university city location is downright sloppy, which yields a lousy burrito

The quality  of the burrito is all over the map. It's either too much beans or too much rice, too little salsa or too much steak. Scratch the too much steak part...I kinda like that. In the end, there is only so much a tortilla can handle.  The worst incident I remember is when a server mercilessly squeezed the living daylights of the burrito and tore it. Instead of rewrapping it, she just tacked on another piece of foil, and handed it to me as if saying," I wrapped it, didn't I? It's your problem now."  The servers at this particular location give this  joint a bad rap. If you head over during weekdays in the afternoon, you may get a better burrito.

I have to say that the spicy salsa (red) at this location is better than the city ave one, where it seems a bit milder.

This is the worst KFC I have ever been to. Twice now they are out of mac and cheese, biscuits and tenders. The staff is slow and always seemed to be surprised you ordered chicken. Same excuses given both times as to why they don't have anything and can't bring you food on time. Never coming back.
This is the saltiest food I've ever had a Chipotle.  I got carnitas since it was off the menu for a while and omg I can't believe the amount of salt in my food.  This is subpar for the chain.

Pathetic!
I waited OVER half an hour for my takeout. Slow-really slow service. Two of the workers there were coughing and hacking. Gross.
When I finally got it the chicken was over cooked, dry and inedible.
On top of it all when I complained to the manager-Jason-his comment was 'sorry'. There are two kinds of 'sorry'-this one was a "So what?-Do I care?"
These people are representing a great company-I suggest retrain or replace.
I will not be returning to THIS one!

Good food that allows you many options. Try the burrito with The steak filling is a favorite, grilled there and good portion. The only negative is when you come at  lunch time 12:00 to 12:30, plan on a longer lunch hour, long line.

First, let me say that this review should not be a review of ALL Chipotle restaurants as every one of them EXCEPT for this one I have been to (and I have been to Chipotles in at least 4 different states) is EXCELLENT.  This Chipotle, however, falls well below the mark. 
  First off, I used the Iphone app mobile ordering.  Often, when you get to a Chipotle, there is a huge line for burritos, so I do the mobile ordering.  That way, you get to skip the line and go straight to the checkout and just pick up your food.  The app asks you when you want your food to be ready, so I specified 5:35 pm.  I showed up at 6:05 and walked up to the cashier and asked about my food.  He pointed me to the end of the line.  Confused, I walked to the end of the line and stood in it.  Apparently, they hadn't even begun to make my food.  So much for using the app and not waiting in line and paying ahead of time.  Never have I encountered that at a Chipotle before.  Never.
  Secondly, as the woman was making my burrito, she put maybe a 1/4 cup of rice, layed three small pieces of carnitas on top of it and passed the burrito on to the next assembly line guy.  I was shocked.  The whole point of Chipotle is that they make GIANT burritos.  That is their whole deal.  That is their image.  Their drink cups extol the greatness of their huge burritos.  However, the burritos made at THIS Chipotle are nothing like that.  So, when my burrito got to the next guy, I complained about the tiny portions I had been given and told him that was not what I had come to expect from a Chipotle.  He said that that was the right amount and that the carnitas portion should fit in a tiny salsa cup.  Wow.  Usually when they finish my burrito, they can hardly wrap the tortilla around it because it is so full of their awesome ingredients.  However, at THIS Chipotle, there was so much tortilla left over that it was wrapped over on itself several times over.  I had a tiny torpedo burrito that cost $10.  Amazing.  
   Skip this Chipotle.  If this is the only one you ever go to, you'll think that they are all like this and be sorely disappointed.  Go to any other and you'll be happy.

"Have it your way." Wasn't that Burger King's slogan just a few years ago? And if so, I want my Whopper cold and my fries old. I thought I would give this BK another try because on my last visit they nailed it. (I ordered breakfast last time). This time I was in the mood for lunch and it was 10:45 am so chances are the burger and fries will be fresh. You would think. I ordered the Whopper with everything except Mayo and they got it right. Only one problem, the flame broiled hamburger patty was so cold the lone slice of American cheese placed directly on top of the burger had absolutely no chance of melting. I literally pulled the piece of cheese out of the sandwich- completely in tact. Like a magic trick. If only I could make my frigid fries disappear. I know- I should have ordered them unsalted and the staff would've dropped some new ones. My bad. But no excuses for the burger. I'm tired of taking everything back. The manager (or staff) wouldn't eat a cold burger so why should I?  By the way, what is Burger King's current slogan?

For the past 12 years all of my friends have been telling me not to eat at Del Taco.  Del Taco is gross.  Del Taco is dirty.  Well, yes it is dirty but I have never found Del Taco gross.  Ever.  Until now.  I was in St. Louis 2 weeks ago and on my way out of town I stopped at the Del Taco and was sorely disappointed.  I got a spicy-jack quesadilla and Veggie Works burrito...the same 2 items I have been getting for years.  The quesadilla was fine, the burrito tasted like nothing.  Literally, nothing.  It was like biting into a semi-warm tortilla filled with bland mashed potatoes.  Just mushy and flavorless.

Fuck.

This is the first time in my entire life I have been let down by Del Taco and it has really been bugging me.  I mean, I have sworn by this place all through my adolescent and adult life!  And now it fails me?  I don't know what to believe in anymore.

I stopped into this Burger King late morning for a REAL breakfast.   A Whopper!   This is a very nice BK.   Clean and open.  I liked sitting at the high top table in the middle of the dining room.  Service was quick and very friendly at the counter.  Whopper was great.  Overall experience was 5 star.

Originally I gave them a 5. Now they are a 1. 

I used to work for kfc so a part of me understands how long chicken takes. However, these are my complaints. 

1) they changed their phone number but still have not updated up Yelp or google. So I couldn't pre order my hot wings that take 9.5 minutes to cook 

2) they never chicken seriously, it's a fast food chicken restaurant. I understand that it takes time to make chicken. However, more than 4 separate occasions has he wait been 20 minutes. At this point, I have no idea why they don't understand to prepare during dinner rushes and lunch rushes. So no there is no excuse for this many times already

3) they have a sign plastered on their glass window that shoes $15 for a 10 piece meal. They said they cannot honor it anymore because the deal is over. It's still up. Seriously that is false advertising. I am calling on corporate on you and seeing if there is any legal action I can put forth. 

You guys are terrible

The food is great when they get the orders right.  If you order by phone or through the app, double-check your order (seriously open the salad lid and unwrap the burrito) because they frequently forget something and you will be charged full price.  It's super frustrating to get home and they even forget to add the steak on a steak salad!

Not even worth one star! This is the closest KFC to us and for the past year every time we've been there there's been once that they had grilled chicken! They never get orders right and never have enough help and on top of that they take forever! This KFC needs a new manager desperately! It's horrible!

I've always loved KFC. And this is the closest to my house. The chicken is always well cooked and delicious, and the service is usually not bad. They are usually not the friendliest, but certainly not bad. I've never had a truly bad experience here and I think you should try this location.

The star is for the friendly staff... thats it!!
We spent $32.00 (not a ton of money... but still) and couldn't even eat the food!! When we opened the bucket of chicken the breading was falling off, mushy, and dripping with grease. Not even the kids would touch it!!
It's not the first time I've had problems with this location. But it will certainly be the last!! 
I emailed the corporate office about it and they said they'd send me a meal voucher... L O L

If I could give zero I would. I don't eat this food often but just felt like a bacon cheeseburger whopper to go with a beer to end a busy day.
What a huge mistake picking this BK location. Lukewarm burger, NO BACON, 5 bits of lettuce and a slice of tomato was the end result and cold, hard fries that could've doubled as a prison shank.
Felt much better after giving the manager (a loose terminology I'm sure) some phone feedback after getting home about investing more time in training the staff to provide customers with what they order and PAY for rather than spending time pissing them off.  After he asked what he could do to fix it, I replied "you make Burgers for a living, it's not that F'in hard, just get it right!"
Do yourselves a favour and avoid this place, the service and food quality is non existent.

The food at this location is generally pretty good. The service on the other hand is by far the worst of any KFC I've visited. Rude staff, extremely long wait times, and cleanliness issues just to name a few reasons for the bad review. If you want chicken, be prepared to wait 15-20 minutes and hope that they get the order right the first time.

Pretty standard Chipotle set up/service. The line can get a little sloppy and rushed, but overall my recent visits have been pretty good.

I've been doing the Whole30 program, and Chipotle is one of the few chains where it is pretty easy to dine out and be Whole30 compliant.

My go-to Whole30 order is: lettuce bowl with Carnitas (only protein that is W30 compliant, double if need be), mild & medium salsas, and guacamole. It's actually delicious AND you don't end up feeling bloated and weighed down (like you might with a burrito).

First time I have been to a Chipotle. I am not sure what the craze is all about and why there are lines out the door of every store that I see. We must have gotten lucky today, and it was after the lunch crowd and empty.

Not much to the interior felt more like I was in a college-town upscale fast food eatery. Benches, tables, chrome in  a very open room. You have two interactions with employees, the person taking your order and the person checking you out. They were nice. 

The menu is simple: burrito, taco, bowl, salad; choose the rice (brown or white), sauce, beans, sour cream and your pick of meat.

My wife got the salad. She liked it. I have to admit for $7 she got a large serving. She also enjoyed Corona Light. I got the Burrito, with steak. It must have weighed 3lbs, 2.97lbs of filler (rice, beans etc) and about 3 oz of steak. I figured the late night crowd would love this place after being out late - then I saw they close at 10pm.

It was a large $7 burrito. Yes I did eat it. Yes I took a power nap after getting back to the house. Will I count the hours until I get a chance to get another one of their burritos. Nah.

My son ate at Firehouse subs after school today; a large combo sub with chips and a soda for $9.88. My personal choice, join him next time. My wife would probably repeat the salad.

Bottom line, I expected a great burrito from all the hype. It was just lunch.

I'm not sure if there's a chain out there that has more consistency than Chipotle. The food is always fresh and tastes great regardless of the location. 

The meat, whether you go steak, chicken, or pork, always gets the job done. They don't skimp on portions either. I prefer the burrito bowl, but you cannot go wrong with the burritos or tacos. Just don't plan on moving for 30 minutes or so after because you will be fulllllllll.

The manager of this location was very helpful answering questions while we were on the road needing a chicken fix. He made sure even close to closing that he could get what we asked for, and also made up for what they could not make. Great customer service.
No matter the location Dunkin' Donuts never fails me. I got the turkey Sausage wake up wrap and it was good.

Good food, bad service!!! I am a pretty big fan of the sofritas, so a lot of times, Chipotle is my go to for my quick meal "cheat days." I usually get the salad burrito bowl with sofritas. There is the honey vinaigrette that comes with it, which is a good balance of sweetness and tanginess. The sofritas are seasoned well and is not too overwhelming in flavor when combined with the salad. If you're looking for vegetarian/vegan and a quick meal option, Chipotle is a good go to.

However, for this restaurant specifically, I go because it is close...if I had all the time in the world, I would drive across town for good service. This restaurant in particular is not always staffed with friendly people. I've ordered online before and when I got there 10 minutes past my pick up time, my order wasn't even made. On top of that, there wasn't even a "sorry" of any kind to acknowledge that they didn't have my order ready. I've worked in the food industry and I know not all customers are easy to deal with, but at least an apology would suffice than just looking at me like I was crazy for asking about my order for pick up. Occasionally you do get the welcoming hellos when you go in, but majority of the time I've been, the staff service is below average.

Sometimes you have a long day of day drinking, and someone gets a random craving for staple fast food items.
That's how you end up at White Castle at 12 midnight Sunday morning.
I really can't say anything ultra prolific about fast food, it's enjoyable and I eat it from time to time. But I assume all have had it at one point or the other so there's nothing that I can't tell you that the reader wouldn't already know.
Hats off, this place is super clean, and they even have a nice little tribute table to service men and women.
They also have White Castle swag that I'm super interested in.
Got the classic 2 slider meal with fries and a drink. 
Sliders are greasy delicious goodness that are awesome when hot, and they heat up again very well.
Everyone needs a little chain fast food indulgence once and awhile make White Castle it!

I had a terrible recent experience here. When I arrived they said it would be 18mins before chicken would be ready. I waited over an hour inside! 

They were so unorganized. They asked what people's orders were over and over again. At one point a staff member told us they didn't have a cook so that makes me wonder if inexperienced people are preparing my meal. 

After an hour of waiting they told me they dropped my chicken on the floor and it would be another 20 minutes before they would make more of the kind I ordered, unless I took whatever else was almost ready. I took what I could get at that point. I would never had waited that long if they hadn't kept telling me my food was almost ready. 

The staff did not seem very apologetic about their behavior or the situation. However, I suspect a part of the issue was poor management because they seemed to be understaffed for their needs. Either that or their employees weren't trained well.

Wow! I love me some Mexican food!!!!Chipotle always does it with the freshest, yummiest ingredients, and as long as you're not there during the lunch time rush, it's a quick bite! I always come in looking to try something new everyone I walk in, or a different combination to my typical order. But I must admit... It's way too hard to stray  away from their seasoned steak wrapped up in a warm burrito, with alllll my favorite fixins. I'm so pleased that there is a restaurant located near me and its a much better option from their competition across the street 

One pitch I want to make, if their HQ ever reads these reviews, is DELIVERY!!!! Hello?!? They have branded themselves well, with locations popping up from coast-to-coast. Why not launch a delivery service for lunch timers who can't wait in that awful line for their addicting food?! I would be soooo happy to pay a flat rate delivery fee for the little extra convenience to order their food through an online app or website and have it hand-delivered to me, at my office.

I've noticed that people here just don't understand how absolutely amazing Dunkin's coffee is. I just moved here from a city where there is a DD on every street corner, a convenience I took advantage of 3+ times per week. St. Louisans look at me oddly when I say I'm a devoted Dunkin fan like "Hey fatty, lay off the donuts, geez." 

I don't actually get donuts- just coffee. Lots and lots of coffee. Well, that and veggie egg white flat breads. And MAYBE a bagel sometimes. 

Mixed reviews about this location though. Major points for it merely existing, but I had questionable service during my last trip. Okay, flat out BAD service. 

We ordered two egg white flat breads, a bagel with cream cheese, two donuts and two iced coffees. For two people. Whatever, I was getting my 25 minute drive's worth.

So... they messed up my order. Wrong type of bagel (plain although I said everything), cream cheese on the bagel (asked for it on the side) and my flatbread was turkey sausage instead of veggie.

But I'll forgive you, Dunkin. They were all still delicious. Maybe it was just the universe's way of saying I should stop being a DD glutton. I only ask that you get my order right and open 17 more locations. Please?

Whoppers! Whoppers! Whoppers! And no, not those nasty chocolate malt ball candies, eww... We're talking flame-broiled burger with lettuce, tomatoes, onions, cheese and mayo, even extra-mayo if you're feeling daring. And boy, am I a daring guy, haha. 

Now, eating at Burger King is something I try to keep to a minimum for the obvious health reasons. But every now and then, I go and grab a Whopper meal. It comes with the juicy, flame-broiled Whopper, fries and a drink. Brings me back to the "big" days when I used to eat five of them in one sitting. Mmm, mmm, mmm. 

The Burger King in Creve Coeur is definitely the closest to where I live, and the service is nice and friendly. If you're on the go near Creve Coeur and need a cheap meal, grab a Whopper meal!

Stopped in this afternoon. Very hungry with little time for a sit down restaurant. Haven't been here in years. Another patron told me staff turnover is high here. That explains it...  A Whopper without the tomato, isn't a Whopper. Must be a new cook.  Pleasant background music. Clean store.

UPDATE:
Nobody from the restaurant reached out to me after my review was submitted with either an apology or a promise to get it right next time. I guess quality control aren't high on their list of concerns.

I have absolutely nothing of value to say... It was late. We were hungry and unfortunately this city isn't big on cheap, late night eats. The service was awful..... Through the drive-thru. I know it's always up to the customer to check their orders but c'mon, I ordered chicken. How do I end up with that ridiculous black whopper in my bag. I should've known better. Smh

I'm a regular to several Chipotle's, and notice when they change the price. They recently increased the price of the meals I get, and when I questioned the price, the cashier got defensive and told me I was wrong. Then, after I paid and walked to a table, she continued to talk to the other employees about my question, like I was the issue. Really sloppy customer service. 

I went back and checked old receipts, and they increased the steak. No problem, just don't make me feel like I'm out of line.

I have stopped at this location a few times for Whoppers, and each time, the burger is juicy and piled high with fresh crunchy vegetables. My order has always come out just as specified. I like to hold the ketchup and mayo. The reason? Burger King has little packets of something called "zesty sauce," a mayonnaise-based topping with horseradish and cayenne. A little of that on the Whopper (or the veggie burger) adds some nice zip, and it's great for dipping French fries, too. (I can't remember how I found out about zesty sauce. No counter person ever asks "Would you like some 'zesty sauce'?" One needs to know to ask for it.)
Today, this location advertised a "Mac and Cheetos" side. Deep fried mac and cheese that looked like an oversized pool noodle-shaped Cheeto cheese curl. I often avoid fried food, but for some strange reason, I felt compelled to try this concoction. It was salty and crunchy and tasted like a Cheeto, and the middle was satisfyingly mac and cheesy. I was glad that there were only five in the container, as these were... addictive.
The chicken is good, but the service isn't. I have walked out when there was no one behind the counter to take my order.

I am a really big fan of Chipotle because every time I leave with a full and satisfied stomach! Each time I go in feels likes it's the first because it always tastes really good and the first bite reminds me why I go there so often. First couple of times when I first started going to Chipotle I stuck with the regular burrito. It was filled with brown rice, chicken/steak, red hot sauce, guacamole, cheese and sour cream. It was done in about two minutes so it was really speedy! Overall the burrito was really good and you can usually save some for seconds if you want but make sure to have a good grip on it because sometimes it tends to fall apart on you. From now on I get the bowl and it's basically like the burrito just without the tortilla and they place all the good stuff in a bowl. You can grab a spoon and fork and dig right in no more mess from explosive burritos!

I really do enjoy this place and the only reason I didn't give it a full five star rating is because the tables usually tend to be pretty messy and that's one thing that they can improve on. Overall though I recommend this place to anyone who enjoy fast Mexican food!

Ay, caramba! I guess Havertownians must be starved for Mexican food because I just stopped in here and it was mobbed!  At 1 pm on a Sunday afternoon the line was out the door.  It took me just about 15 minutes to get to the counter.  Technically this is Havertown's only Mexican restaurant.

Once I made it to the counter, everything was exactly as expected for what I think of as a well-run chain.  I built myself a steak burrito and added a small fountain soda.  The bill was just under $9.

Everything in my burrito was fresh and tasty.  The two people that built it were friendly and efficient. The portion was large and I was stuffed.   I sat inside and my table was clean.  Note that there were already folks taking advantage of the small outside seating area.

This is one of two stores now open in the new Quarry Center and they get a solid three stars from me.  I'll be back!

I never really wanted to go back ever since my last review. For some reason I thought I would give it a second chance.

I ordered a breakfast sandwich combo. After paying I realized I overpaid a $1. I mentioned it to the cashier. She said that it was because I asked for sausage instead of bacon and she forgot to give me a discount. She apologized but didn't offer to give me my money back. A pretzel sandwhich, a tiny coffee and 6 tiny hash brown bites cost me over $8

The sandwich was nasty. My first bite was gritty like the yolk in a hardboiled egg. I looked at the egg and it was egg white that I bit into. My only guess is that the eggs are frozen then heated prior to serving. I had egg bits all over my lap. The egg had no integrity.

The coffee is still good quality, but I can't get over how they do the cream and sugar for you.
Maybe this works in the big cities where everyone is busy, but I like condiments and making my coffee to taste. No soda/ice machine either. I can't ice my coffee to a descent temperature. By the time the coffee is drinkable I am either at work or can't drink it.

Due to lackluster service, quality of food and lack of condiments,  I have reduced my rating to 2 stars
Never had a problem till tonight it was like they dug the chicken and fries out of the garbage to give us.  I got what was ever left in the fry bin and my hubby got half cooked chicken.

Be careful unless you've a lot of free time. I was in last weekend and it took over 45 minutes of waiting just to provide a 10 and a 12 piece bucket of chicken.  No apologies or compensation, just wait.  I got no more or less than what I'd ordered 50 minutes before.  Emailed an inquiry to the corporate response line and heard absolutely nothing.  The chicken was good but hard to justify when there are places like Lucky Wishbone and Church's willing to bend over backwards for you.  I gave them a week to respond to their email, but nothing....  I guess they just don't care.

As with all Chipotles, the food here is quite good. Personally, I am partial to the steak burrito with double meat. However, a few things hold this location back. For one, the lines can become astoundlingly long during peak hours. Like, there are people out the door at noon waiting for their burritos. Another complaint is that several members of the staff are just over the top in their efforts to make conversation and seem cheery. I would much prefer they simply take my order and allow me to proceed to the next station rather than demand to know what I'm up to, if I'm particularly hungry today, whether I've been to the bar represented on the t-shirt I'm wearing, etc.

Again, good food, but there are better Chipotles out there.

Only go here occasionally because I like BK croissan'wiches. Breakfast is usually alright. Just went there for a whopper craving and the fries and sandwich were cold. Strange thing is that they made me wait and "pull up" for food that had obviously been sitting around for at least 30 min. It is also strange that they say 24 hours on the big sign, but were closed at 11pm on different Saturday night I tried for a whopper fix.

Awful.  The chicken is way too salty.

You can get fried chicken at the Fry's supermarket down the block that's much better, and cheaper too.

Aw, everyone's being so harsh on this Del Taco. Come on! It's Del Taco, people, not some fabulous epicurean experience. It is what it is and that's why I grade on a curve.

Between the hours of 1-3 am, on my way back from the East Side (of Skinker), there is a critical juncture where I can turn right and continue home, or go straight and into the seductive glow of Del Taco.

More often than I'd like to admit, my vodka-fueled auto pilot leads me right into the Del Taco drive thru. My order is always the same and incredibly boring. Two tacos, no lettuce.

There is an older woman who is always working these god-awful shifts. Always. She knows my car, she knows my order, and just tells me to drive to the window without even giving me a total. We both know it's $1.30.

At first I felt bad for her. Imagine your mother working the Del Taco drive-thu at 3am! Surely they could give her a nice middle of the day shift. But, then I thought, maybe she enjoys it. Maybe she gets a kick out of eliminating hangovers one drunken-carload at a time. 

She always makes sure my taco is piping hot and loaded up with cheesey goodness. I have encountered other Del Taco employees and I can tell you for a fact that this lady puts a little more tlc into her tacos. 

So, 4 stars for the older Del Taco lady. She makes the regrettable experience of 3 am Del Taco seem as innocent as a visit to the lunch lady of your grade school cafeteria.

The customer service is ridiculous. The lady who has been there both times I have been there is so completely rude. First, she tried to charge me $25 for EIGHT pieces of chicken, then when I asked her why it was so much she remembered they have a 10 for $10 .... today, as I was placing my order I asked for honey packets. She completely ignored me so when she handed me my order, i asked again amd she walked away from me! Ridiculous.

6-5-17. Still the worst place. Decided to give them another chance since I was right there. My sausage, egg, and cheese croissant was tempid at best. The sausage was barely warm in the middle. It didn't even melt the cheese. I called and complained and was told to bring the sandwich in after work and they would replace it. Really? Wish they could get there stuff together. And don't even get me started on the attitude you get when you go to this store.

Chipotle Mexican Grill earns 3 stars from me because they have a vegan option and I can customize my burrito based on how I'm feeling for the day. The service is good although they always seem like they are in such a hurry, if more than two people come in behind you they yell out for "more help on the line!" Sometimes I feel rushed when ordering my food but it hasn't stopped me from coming back. I have never been inside this location and had it be messy or found dirty tables which is a plus. They keep this place clean. 
My go to burrito is sofritas (a seasoned tofu) with a mix of both black and pinto beans, brown rice, and grilled veggies which is a mix of green bell peppers and onions. I always get the salsa on the side and add a side of guacamole. The scoops they use for the guacamole and salsa are very large and if added inside your burrito will make the whole thing cold. 
Bonus tip, if you are only ordering a bean and rice burrito the guacamole is free! 
The only downsides I have come across here are 1) grilled veggies are not always fresh, 2) guacamole can be so hard it is inedible at times 3) sometimes they do not have guacamole at all (insert sad face) 
Overall this place is quick, convenient, and cheap.

I went to drive up and the guy(long haired guy) asked what I wanted and I asked if I could have a moment and he just didn't respond with answer at all. The sign wasn't lit so I couldn't see it so went inside. The girl (shanny) was the only cashier on while the 2 other girls stood there waiting to make food with a long line. People were just standing around while this shanny girl was running around taking orders and getting food. She deserves a raise. My chicken wings were gross also.

My husband and I went for lunch. Holy cow never again. Took over 20 minutes for cold chicken, missing item and soggy tacos. When I went back to counter to get missing item I wasn't acknowledged so I said excuse me and he just looked at me and walked away. I stood there for a few minutes but I wanted to eat my cold chicken before the grease solidified. 
I kept checking the counter to catch an opportunity but realized because of their past a snail pace I wasn't getting anywhere. Will never go again. Staff is rude and gross(rang up customers with gloves on touching everything from money to customers hand then prepared food with same gloves) I gave one star because my mashed potatoes were beyond hot
Usually good but not tonite. tiny old pieces, crumbled cookie & chocolate on my chicken, balled up napkins look used. Not happy
Great chicken and I love it beat chicken ever woooooo yeah hdhahshd dhsush dhdjshsbdhje didn't dbdudhd dhdbevs I get he d it. Dues are

Chipotle was running a special tonight for bunco for all military veterans so I decided to go back after a few months of not stopping for a dinner on the way home. In the past I've been a regular at chipotle 1 to 2 times a month but since their food quality has gone down or the recipes changed such as the chips I haven't been back as much. I went with a Barbacoa bowl and a steak bowl tonight. I found the staff to be friendly and very fast which is usual for a chipotle. I appreciated the fact that they did a military special show their appreciation for the military. The reason why I've decided not to frequent chipotle as I used to as the food quality has gone down due to all the recent changes as well as their pricing has increased. This time the food was good; I found the steak to be a little dry and the rice to be a little hard as well. Part of the issue may have been going in so close to close so the food isnt as fresh which is a little disappointing since the food should be the same at closing time as it is it opening time. I really enjoy their fresh salsas hot is great as well as the fresh corn and fresh pico. I usually get the bowls with extra rice and sometimes I get the sauces on the side as well as extra cheese. You can usually get two meals out of their bowls and they make for a great leftovers. Like I said I wish their food quality had stayed the same as before but after all their issues in the past year there the food quality and taste is not the same as well as the prices have increased like I said that's why I have stayed away still if you're looking for a quick healthy Mexican food option they are definitely a good not a bad option maybe stay away from going so close to close.

Need I say more... LOL empty wrappers should tell it all. Everyone I know who has been to a White Castle raves about how great it is... Well well these guy sure set the bar high! Awesome experience! This was our first time EVER @ White Castle (from the west coast, they only believe in the frozen kind, unfortunately) superb!  The employees were super nice, and patient to us newcomers! They made everything fresh to order, went out of their way to make sure our first time was top notch! Food was pretty great, the FRIES & PICKLE CHIPS were amazing! Who knew they had a chicken slider!! They make the sliders @KFC look like knock offs lol thick n juicy mmmm && the zesty sauce they have was pretty good too. Only in town for 5 days, I will definitely return before my departure.

My friend Steve is a funny dude. I had offered to drive he and his wife to the airport from Malvern last year. A few days before he sent me a Facebook post that said,
 " I'm thinking around 9am for our Taco run"

I replied that maybe 9am was a little early for Mexican food but I'm not one to pass up a Taco run. He soon answered back with a mea culpa that autocorrect changed " Taxi" to "Taco" and we gad a good laugh about it. I mean, how often is the guy typing "taco"?!

After successfully getting him to the airport, Steve cleverly rewarded me with a gift card to Chipolte. Thoughtful and funny but I couldn't for he life of me think of one near me or my work. 

" We'll there's one right near me" offered Steve 

That's great , buddy, but Malvern is about 45 minutes away. No taco is that good!

Luckily, I discovered that this one in Havertown had just opened. Still not totally convenient but near enough. The gift card was for $30, which came out to 3 visits. 
Here is what I learned in these 3 experiences.

It's a huge parking lot, but I've yet to be able to park anywhere nearby. Pretty much all of Havertown that isn't wedged into the Manoa lot is here. I opt to park by Lowes and walk over. It's less stress.

An order of Tacos is about $7 it's pretty reasonable and you get full from them. I have to say, the steak they use is only slightly better quality than Taco Bell. Pretty much every other bite is chewy and gristly. I'd stick to the pork or chicken. 

In a few cases I would have been happier with 2 tacos. I almost suffered through a third one. I wish there were more options to get more or less than 3. 
 
I could take or leave almost everything here. It's not bad it's just average. They have tabasco at the counter but  Siracha  as an option would be a game changer. I know it's technically Asian and not Mexican but I think most fans would be willing to overlook it.

The big draw for me here is their guacamole . It's fresh and delicious. I always get the chips and guacamole along with my tacos and now that my gift card is used up, I may just return only for the C&G.

The place is relatively clean and the staff is always friendly and helpful. I consider my rating a 3.5. Whether you are on a taxi run or a Taco run, Chipolte could always be a valid option.

My husband & I both got food poisoning from this location on 12/30/2018 from food received thru the drive in window  at 1:50 pm. We ordered 2 med whopper w/cheese meals & they came out cold with chunks of something in the french fries. Sick as dogs all night & it definitely came from there because it was the only thing we ate all day. If you were there on that day & experienced the same issue, please report it to the board of health & burger king corp office.

I'm going to write this review with the condition of talking about the Burger King's limited edition of the Angry Burger. Probably one the few things this fast food chain has promoted worth eating. I mean, when I saw their previous ads about it, I thought how can this burger make a hit, but it prove me wrong today. I got to try it out and to my surprise it was good. Not much of a fast food person, only when I'm in a rush and don't have time to eat, do I come and eat here.

Burger King's Angry Whopper is a burger with bacon, jalapenos and something called Angry Onions, topped with something called Angry Sauce. Funny to me. But if you like whoppers and like some spice in your life, then this burger is it.

Jeez, I almost never write reviews on here, but this was quite the experience just to get an Impossible Burger for the first time. The first step into the building was sticky, and by that I mean like if someone took a mop, dipped it in maple syrup, and spread it in excess over the floor. The line wasn't bad, but while my family and I were standing there, there were three men with face tattoos pacing back and forth, one clearly tweaking while high on what I can only guess was meth. They were yelling at the staff, and the staff was yelling at them, in what sounded like a personal dispute with a couple of them. One of these tweaking men kept taking pictures of me while I was ordering, and when I left. Overall, this place is not just dirty, but sketchy. Would never recommend going here.

I have never eaten at DD before but I needed a quick snack and was in hurry.  The place makes sandwiches all day so I ordered the turkey sausage with egg on a bagel (instead of an egg muffin).  service was quick and courteous.  Granted everything is precooked.  The meal was fine and filled the hole in my belly.  The price was very reasonable.  The place is clean and offers charging areas for the phone as well as seats.  Close to my gate of departure.

I have only eaten at a chipotle twice. This was my second time. I came here a week ago and I noticed there weren't any lettuce being offered. I'm not sure if they were out or if they just don't serve lettuce anymore after those outbreaks. This is the first time I've eaten at a chipotle since people have gotten sick. I had had a bowel with white rice, steak, black beans, guac, sour cream and salsa. I also order the chips with cheese dip. I had about 2 of the chips. They had a lime flavor to them. I wasn't a big fan of it. My bowel was good though and it filled me up. The service was fast. Most importantly I didn't get sick.

I was at the havertown chipotle location last night and have to say I felt ripped off!! I ordered a burrito bowl half chicken half steak with only a few toppings, chips, and side of salsa. Total was over $10. The gentleman in front of me ordered a bowl as well with toppings overloaded  and got 2 sides of salsa and 2 sour cream but was only charged 8.49?? Am I missing something here because this guy got enough food in that bowl to feed a family of four and I felt as if I overplayed for scraps..This cashier was clearly stealing, so unethical and unprofessional. This location has the worst customer service I have ever come across in our area. Place deserves 0 stars, Will not be returning!!

DO NOT GO HERE! Had a experience a month ago where I ordered a hamburger for my child who can't handle milk. He received a cheeseburger, Waste of money. I emailed Burger King and the manager or owner was willing to give me a free Whopper sandwich for the mix up. It was nice, but doesn't fix the problem of dealing with a child that wants a hamburger for lunch and I can't give it to him. 

A month later, tonight on Dec. 29, 2015 I thought I'd give them 1 more change on my way home from overtime at work. 10:45pm at night. The food was fresh, BUT .... 9 minutes to get my food, watching a car in front of me pull ahead because they didn't have that car's food and when I got home I didn't have the right order ... I AM Done With THIS Burger King. I spent money on French fries that I didn't get. Thanks for ripping me off! Save your money and go across the street to Wendy's. At least when I go there I get exactly what I ordered and at a decent time table.
I had no idea what exactly I wanted just chicken and biscuits and the manager helped me order without ending up with a million sides. Great customer service!
I ordered the 1c whopper & medium fries. My Mastercard was charged and they refused to sever me!!! This Location claim they closed after the app took my money
The alleged classic for five bucks. Is noy worth the gad to go there. You would think that the old Whopper would be concered a classic. Surprise it not

I've been eating Chipotle since 2001 and I just had what might have been the worst Chipotle burrito I've ever had period at this location. The employees collectively looked miserable tonight. I considered asking for steak (I typically go half and half) but noticed they had none and only upon my asking did they bother throwing any on the grill. The place was packed so I don't know how that's even possible. You know what really ruins a burrito besides not being able to get the protein you want on it.... how about crunchy black beans. I ended up having to unravel my burrito bc the beans were inedible. Now I'm down $10 and completely unsatisfied. Please note my review of this Chipotle is not based on this instance alone but of all of my experiences collectively. Try sitting down and trying to have a nice meal outside normal lunch/dinner rush hours here during the weekday and having your ear drums shudder continuously to the sound of employees screaming and hollering across the restaurant like you're not there. This locations saving grace is it's proximity to Vandy and the lack of other "cheap" food options in the area but it is certainly one of the lower echelon  Chipotle's I've ever been to.

One star is to much, my wife stopped there just now for our dinner at drive thru, $22.44 for 2 chicken sandwiches and a single whopper and 2 orders of fries.

Fries were ice cold and so stale they could have been yesterday's fries- yuk

You guys take the award for worst nasty fries.

The chicken sandwiches which I've eaten all over the country on occasion for over 30 years were kinda hot, she had to wait on them to be cooked but man the grease just oozed out of them really bad.

She wasn't thrilled with her whopper either, would love a refund and we'll even bring the nasty fries back.

Poor dog even turned her nose up at a French fry.

Wow between you and McDonalds and viva nothing but potato burrito at this corner and across the street, it's like the triangle of yuk 

Won't be coming back, WOW

UPDATE: July 9th
Wife took back the nasty fries and asked for a full refund and was treated very nice by the manager and was given full refund.
Glad about that but they couldn't give that bad stuff away free and make the time and trouble worth it.

Burger King Corp should do spot checks on all franchises to make sure the quality is up to Corp standards at all times.

I'd call the franchise owner in Phoenix but have a feeling that would be a waste of time

This location is terrible. They seem to forever be out of some kind of chicken and never get the order correct. We usually have a bad experience but once in a while we crave the food we (are supposed to) get and break down. Today at 5:30pm they were out of white meat chicken and had no extra crispy. We got a family meal and they left out the mashed potatoes and biscuits. My bad for not checking before I left. I'll know better next time. Or I might try another location. Ugh. Don't bother.

Went the other day to order and the employees were very nasty with attitude, very disrespectful and I went to order number 1 a whopper and they told me we are out of whopper!!! Coke machine is out of coke, Sprite and Dr Pepper. Undercover boss show should visit this location. Never coming back again

This Chipotle location is the most consistent and reliable of the ones scattered around the valley. If you want a big meal at a low price, Chipotle is your go-to spot.

Food: Chipotle serves huge portions and typically doesn't hold back on the toppings. Treat them nicely and they'll definitely make you a worthy meal. I usually prefer the chicken or sofritas salads as healthier options (and skip the cheese, sour cream and guacamole--using salsa as my dressing), while my husband usually goes big with a giant steak burrito. 

Drinks: Self-serve soda and refrigerated case of other beverages.

Atmosphere: It's a little stiff and cold, but always super busy and generally clean.

Service: The staff is generally nice. 

Healthy Hack: Go basic with a sofritas or chicken salad with black beans, fajita veggies and salsa. Plenty of flavor, low on cals.

Tips: If you like steak, this is the one to go to! Eagle's Chipotle is rather inconsistent and often overcooks their meat. Ask for sour cream and salsa on the side if you don't want a drenched burrito. Try salsa for salad dressing instead of the vinaigrette.

The veggie bowl is great and they also have sofritas to add to the vegetarian options. Overall it's a typical chipotle. It gets really full around lunch and dinner, but the line moves pretty quick. 

This location runs out of things (like guacamole) sometimes, but I haven't had this problem often. However they do seem to have unexpected closings a lot more than often than any other location I have encountered. They often close here for bad weather or random unknown reasons (while the surrounding restaurants stay open).

I was burned w/bad chicken by a KFC at another location (which is now closed), so I was a little leery of trying the Springfield KFC.  However, I wanted to try the Nashville Hot chicken, so I decided to give the Springfield location a try.  I was pleasantly surprised, and I've since been back for the Taco Bell, which is in the same building as KFC.

First off, both times they got my order correct.  The food was hot and good (the Nashville chicken was very spicy - I'll go back again for that!).  The lady that works the drive-thru window (both times I was there on a Saturday afternoon) is very nice, efficient, and friendly.  

It's fast food, it is what it is, but my experience at the Springfield KFC location was very positive both times.  I'll be back for more!
I come here sometimes and don't buy anything and they don't bother me. They allow me to wait on rides and just sit and charge my phone without a problem. Also the whopper is really Amazing here!!

Although I'm a big fan of chipotle this is the worst location I've ever ate at. They were busy but they had plenty of help behind the counter and they could not keep up with the flow of people. I ordered a steak burrito only to find out that I didn't have any steak on it. The toppings were thrown all over the place and the burrito wasn't even rolled up. The final product looked like a ball of cold veggie slop wrapped in a tortilla.

I usually avoid reviewing places like Burger King, but after the "service" this week, I can't resist.  Your slogan is "Have it Your Way."  You even have fancy little wrappers that your employees can use to cross off anything that the customer asks you to leave off.  For instance, when I ordered a Whopper with no onion, your employees correctly crossed it off.  Unfortunately, whoever MADE the Whopper gave me onions, and didn't include the most important part of the fast food burger.  The reason that anyone in their right mind actually eats something almost guaranteed to induce a coronary at some point in your life.  The pickels.  What moron would get a burger without pickles!?!!???!? (My apologies to anyone who actually orders their burger this way.  No offense intended.)  

Combine this with the ridiculous line and slow service, and with the condescending attitude coming through the speakers from someone making $5.75 an hour to smell like grease 24/7, and it was an experience that practically forced me to come Yelp them for 2 stars, maximum.
Been awhile since I ate at Burger King.  Stopped yesterday and won't be trying it again.  Whopper was barely warm and fries were like cardboard.8$ wasted.

Unfortunately, this must be the worst KFC location on earth. I've been going here on and off for two years. Sometimes it's as advertised and the chicken is great. 
But way too often, it's disappointing. 
Tonight I had the worst chicken I've ever purchased here. 
I doubt I'll try again. The management just can't get it together.

I've read all these horrible reviews and just can't agree with any one of them. Setting aside we've never been in the drive thru our dining in experiences has always been good. There has never been a mistake on our order and staff have been courteous. I went in last night to get the Impossible burger to go for me & Mom with sides. The young man taking and serving my order was respectful and friendly throughout. After I said to him "keep it vegan" he clarified what items to leave off the bun. He asked if I wanted sauce with my onion rings and offered the package for me to read the ingredients. Not disappointed at all.

This is mostly about the "impossible whopper". Was really hoping this turned out to be anything like their marketing campaign. Mostly "impossible that it would get any drier." Or "impossible that it should taste any blander." Not a good option for meat eaters or vegetarian/vegans."

Chicken pot pie was not on the menu so I asked if they served it.  They said yes but did tell me how much it was and that there was a $5 box.  I order it and also a white meat big box.  They gave me the 3 piece and told me to pull up because the pot pie takes 14 minutes to heat.  I pulled up cause I didnt have a choice but meanwhile my 3 piece sat in the car getting cold.  The pie came and I went home.  My 3 piece white ended being an old breast with no meat on it, I think a wing and a very small drum stick whim sure is not white meat.  The chicken was uneditable and disgusting.  Stay away from this place.  It should close down.

Okay, so I really, really try to NOT eat fast food but when your husband is hungover and hangry, you pull in to the nearest drive through and start ordering! We went for the, "2 sandwiches-4-$6" deal. We ordered the Whopper with Cheese and the Spicy Chicken Sandwich (see pics). He said it worked, hit the spot and extremely filling. I was even impressed by the size of the Whopper! However, I have to say that it was not cheap. After ordering a soda, the total meal was over $9. I thought fast food was   supposed to be fast and cheap? The service was friendly and on point. So although I don't recommend eating fast food, if you have to, this Burger King is the one to hit up.

I rarely ever want Carl's Jr. because my burgers are always cold but I was craving it tonight and this one was actually warm. 1/3 way through my famous star, I realized that it was very raw. I'm glad that I got the raw burger and not my kids but the fact that it was raw is absolutely unacceptable so here's hoping I don't get food poisoning like others have unfortunately gotten from here. This was the final straw and we will never be back.

Every time I have been here, it's disappointing. The food is terrible.  Something was wrong with the chicken, it was overly greasy and soggy. The crust was peeling off/barely on and the chicken was a grayish color.  It's really messed up to pay 23 bucks for a dinner and then have it be inedible.  I would have gone back, but live kind of far away to make a big deal out of it.  

Just go to Frys grocery store for fried chicken and sides, it's cheaper for their dinners and the food is far superior, not too mention the employees are nicer LOL.  Plus, I don't think I've ever ran the risk of food poisoning from Frys.

My orders are simple, mainly coffee and sometimes egg and sausage croissant.  They are done correctly. I like the spaciousness and illumination of the room and the friendly workers, except for this overnight lady named Lisa who is nasty to white people and, according to her co-workers, allergic to any type of work, yet strangely insistent upon tips. I would love to sit in there and work on my laptop. I have my own wet wipes because they almost never clean the tables. But this place, as well as the Tioga street DD, is plagued by all the street people hanging out, both inside and just outside the door. There is always someone panhandling inside the restaurant, as though the reason people come there is to be benefactors of homeless drug addicts. How can I use my debit card to pay when a street guy, rocked off his ass on who-knows-what, is standing at the counter hovering over the keypad? Must I really consider purchasing the wares of thieves, the Coach sunglasses or good-as-new watches, when I just want to drink my coffee, read, and think? This place needs an atmosphere adjustment.

I was craving chicken a few nights ago. I have only been to this location two other times in my 17 years of living nearby as I prefer Publix chicken. I walked in around 7pm and there was only one other person in line ahead of me. I heard the employee tell him that they were out of what ever it was that he wanted, which verifies other reviews of constantly running out of food. There were many people working in the prep area, scrambling around like they were running a race or something. I waited about 5 minutes for the employee to come back to to the counter to take my order before I walked out. During my wait I noticed our disgustingly filthy this restaurant was. There was food and liquid all over the place behind the counter and surrounding the food prep area. I made a comment to the guy who ordered in front of me and he was also disturbed as he noticed the filth. I walked out without ordering and will never return. It's too bad they allow this type of mismanagement and obvious violations to continue.

First off, line was hella long. took us 30 mins to get through. Second, they were out of steak. Third, they were out of Coke Zero. Fourth, they were out of ice. Last and most disappointing, they locked the doors 3 hours before closing time because they were too busy and understaffed.

This Kentucky Fried CHICKEN has consistently been out of chicken every time I've come to eat. How? How in the world can a KFC consistently not have chicken? Is it seriously that difficult? If you actually want food to eat I suggest going elsewhere as you're not going to gag anything but biscuits and sides at this joke of a KFC.

You run into some fast food spot where you get great service and food....... but this isnt one. I asked for 10 hot wings and got 5. The green beans looked like they had been crushed,and the "crispy" chicken was soggy. however, the cashier was extremely pleasant. Maybe thinking to herself,'enjoy your horrible meal'

yay!  for the impossible burger!!!
BK Lounge goes Vegan Friendly!!!
thank you thank you thank you!!!!!
cows dont need to be ground up for a whopper anymore!!!!
keep up the good work!!

This is probably my favorite Chipotle location anywhere. They have decent dedicated parking, friendly staff, clean dining spaces and consistent food. 

Most Chipotle locations will have super salty veggies one day, inconsistent servings of items like salsa and sour cream the next, but none of those 'normal' issues occur here. They always provide full spoon sizes of all ingredients and are thoughtful to ask if you'd like more or less of each item. On days when I ask for extra of something, like lettuce, they're always extremely sweet about it which I appreciate. 

They never run out of key ingredients like the veggies (most places like the downtown Boise location I have to wait upwards of 10 minutes for them to make more) which is nice for when I'm in a rush. 

Tables are cleaned frequently making this one of the cleanest locations I've visited. I've never had to wipe rice off the table of previous guests or deal with food spills or excess napkins on the floor. 

I've seen staff members here go above and beyond for guests who were recently diagnosed with a gluten intolerance, telling them what they can and can't order, for guests who have never visited a Chipotle before, and ones wondering what the vegetarian items are. Staff members are always willing to provide samples and truly provide a memorable experience. 

Visiting this one particular location gives off Chick-Fil-A or In-N-Out vibes and it's always so refreshing. For most meals ranging from $6.95 for Veggie and Sofritas (tofu) up to $7.95 for steak, Chipotle provides a great, fresh meal that's hard to find elsewhere. 

If you're looking to grab a quick, filling meal, this Chipotle location is a must!

This review covers my meal and is not a review of this place as 
a whole.


I had the Nashville Hot Chicken and Waffles dinner tonight at KFC. 
I was amazed at how good it was. 

The waffle tasted, and had the texture of, a deep fried cake donut.  

The chicken seasoning was more flavorful than spicy hot.

Recommend trying.

Had an early flight and went to Dunkin' Donuts for breakfast.

Beyond sausage sandwich with the Beyond plant-based sausage patty was disappointing and bland. The egg part was pretty gross and the whole thing just didn't taste good. Needed a LOT of ketchup to make it seem edible.

But, I'm glad Dunkin' Donuts was open. I think it was the only food there that early in the morning, so I'm bumping this up to 2 stars.

Service was fine.

If you want KFC, drive to another location; it will take you less time. I'm not a KFC fan, but my fiance is so I stopped here at his request. They get two stars because the food didn't taste bad, but the service is ridiculously terrible (like "Am I on a hidden camera show, because I can't believe this is happening?" terrible). The first thing the drive-thru operator told me was they had only original recipe chicken. I asked for a 5-piece tender box and was told they only have 2 tenders and can give me a "Chicken Little meal instead." I came for tenders, so I asked "You can't drop any more in the fryer?" He asked how many, said he'd have to check with the cook, and following a long pause said they would fry some more. There was still more than an hour left before closing, so that's just laziness. No, I don't want the 2 old tenders you have sitting there. If I was a manager, I'd be pissed knowing employees are losing sales by refusing to make food.

I pulled up to the window and paid, then I was asked to park because there would be a wait for my tenders. I waited more than 20 minutes, and that's not exaggerated, for someone to bring my food out. I was tempted to just drive away, but I already paid and my fiance really wanted his tenders. A young lady took her sweet time bringing me the food and just handed it to me and said "Have a good day!" No apology for the wait. I also specifically asked for barbecue sauce and there was none, but I had zero interest in wasting any more of my time here.  I don't usually eat at KFC, but if I do, it won't be at this location again.

I write this review as a lover of this chain of restaurants, this gateway drug of mexican cuisine, this staple food of college students. I've even consumed the koolaid on the whole "naturally raised" meat, ethical sourcing and so on. I'm a believer in Chipotle. 

That being said, I have found myself consistently disappointed with the performance of this UPenn University City chipotle. 

The quality food varies considerably. Stale rice, dry chicken/steak, inconsistent preparation/proportioning of burrito ingredients are all commonplace here. Service is slow, stuttering and even rude at times. 

I understand that this is a "Fast food chain" and at ~$6-7 a burrito, what can you expect. But compare this specific branch's performance to your average NYC branch, or even the center city Philadelphia one, and you're bound to be disappointed. 

To the casual passer by, avoid this Chipotle! To the Chipotle fan looking for a fix, fall back on this only in the darkest of withdrawals - but prepare to be disappointed.
I love them the impossible Burger is great and the nuggets my son says there to die for

This Dunkin' Donuts always manages to screw my order up, then when I ask for it to be fixed I'm looked and treated like an idiot. I'm here right now and they couldn't seem to make an iced French vanilla coffee to save their lives or a sausage egg and cheese croissant. The sandwich was cold and the cheese wasn't melted at all, and the coffee must've been all cream. TWICE I had to go back and ask them to fix my sandwich and my coffee. I come to Dunkin every day and spend 13$ or so, you'd think I could get better service. And it's just this Dunkin I have issues at. Needless to say I will not be coming here anymore.

If you're a Penn student looking for a quick and inexpensive lunch or dinner, this is the place to be. Over the years I've been to Chipotle countless times, either for take-out or dine in.

However, I do feel that Chipotle's hype leads to a less than ideal experience. At peak hours, the line often stretches to the door, and this leads to inconsistency as they rush to quickly make burritos. It can also be a 15+ min wait frequently. I've been here when they are missing basic items like chicken or rice.

Qdoba is just around the corner (40th and locust) and offers similar fare with less crowds, but something does bring me back to Chipotle. Chipotle's steak and chicken are both delicious and fresh, and their burrito bowls are a (relatively) healthy and very filling dining option. 

Recently, I've been going to Chipotle a lot less, but it is still a staple in University City.

Yay! For serving the impossible whopper and giving us vegans another choice!
Impossible meat has a 90% less carbon footprint and no feces like regular ground beef has in it....
Came 5 minutes prior to closing and they were out of steak. The gentlemen behind the register offered to make fresh steak with a smile on his face. I'll never forget it!!

The chicken here is good, but there is always a shortage of it at this location. This is the 3rd time that I've been here when they are out of chicken. The speaker box was broken so I waited 10 minutes to find out at the window that they were out of both original and extra crispy and it would be a 30 minute wait for a fresh batch. What kind of KFC is out of chicken??? The cashier at the window said that they dont make any more chicken after 8pm to decrease waste, I understand that, but you are open until 10pm! That's 2 hours without making any chicken?! How does that make any sense? I even tried to call and place an order on the phone, but they were cleaning out the fryer st 8:30pm and couldnt drop any more chicken. 

This is completely unacceptable. If a business of going to be open until 10pm, they need to serve the product until they are closed. This "strategy" to decrease waste will only 
 result in decreased business.
Burnt and dry double whopper, $1.50 up charge for onion rings, they were chewy and stale. I was the only one there and now I know why. Stay away from this location.

I have lived in the area for over 20 years and we are doing low carb and after having this low carb meal at the Cape Girardeau location last week which btw was a really nice BK, I decided to try out the one in Manchester since our Ballwin/Ellisville location just closed down recently. Two of the 3 times I came in customers were standing at the counter with orders that had been messed up.  The booths have fabric backs which are absolutely filthy and most of the tables were filthy and all 3 times it was the middle of the afternoon (not a rush time). The people are friendly enough, but they told me they had wifi but the "Whopper" guess wifi doesn't work & the employees said they couldn't get on it either & they won't give out the password for the "BKoffice" wifi.  Bottom line is the place is filthy, they don't have wifi, and they seem to mess up a lot of orders.

Can't help it, sometimes you just have to have a Whopper. And at 2/$7, not a bad deal! 
I always use the drive thru and check my bag before leaving. No complaints. I don't like or buy many of their products, but they do a nice Whopper.

Why why why why why? Why do I continue to eat here?

It's a love/hate relationship.

Rationale: they've got a 24/7 drive-thru (I've never seen them close) that happens to be around the corner from my brother's abode. Convenience is the name of this late-night game... oh Del Taco, you're my faux Mexican booty call!

True story: I found my little dream home driving to Del Taco hungover one morning in a quest for a breakfast burrito. Saw an open house sign, wandered in, fell in love, called my parents and the rest is history! I lived in this cute little house for 4 years before moving, and now it's my brother's nest. Thanks Del Taco!

How on earth does a fried chicken place run out of fried chicken? Well this location did last night! Waste of time to go here. Notice that there is hardly anyone at this location ever. Check out Hip Bellevue for more similar posts about this place. They really need to close this location as the staff are made up of complete idiots. I don't understand how these are the people that want to get paid $15/hr when they can't even do one job correctly... fry some damn chicken!

This place cannot get a single simple order correct! I've been to this location 3 times and each time my order has been incorrect! This most recent time all I ordered was a medium double cheeseburger meal with the burger being plain and had to wait 15 minutes in the drive-thru for the order to be done. Meanwhile 3 other vehicles were served and departed! Not only did I receive the meal late but it was the wrong meal!!! They gave me a Whopper with everything on it and gave no apology for the amount of time it took to complete. They never gave me a receipt for purchase to ensure the accuracy of the order. I left with the wrong order with poor service because I just couldn't stand it anymore! They've taken my money and all I got was a burger I can't eat and a medium fry and drink. Think twice before going to this location.

Where to start with this place I wish there was an option to go less than one star. I  Try to never go to this location because there's always issues,  one always being they never have chicken.  This time was no different, 1st thing I'm told there is no bone in chicken no coleslaw and no biscuits. How the heck does KFC the place that only sells chicken not have chicken and this isn't the 1st time this has happened  Numerous times when stopping here.   Ordered a chicken strip  Baskets, Still appalled that a place that only sells chicken runs out a chicken Substituted my biscuit with a store bought M&M cookie That look like chips a  Chips ahoy cookie. Then to top it off when I asked for my receipt so I could call make a complaint the guy refuses to give me a receipt. I don't know how this place stays open. When I got home  And looked at the strips They were over cooked and dried out to the point they looked like a super size chicken nuggets.

There are more than one location of Dunkin in the airport. I went to the one in the main concourse between terminals D and E, across from the security checkpoint. Some of the reviews are for the one next to gate D12 "near Delta gates". I arrived just before 8am- a very busy time. Dunkin was the only business in the hallway with a line and the line moved fast! This was thanks to two very efficient cashiers stationed next to each other (seemed to help with efficiency and decreased the crowds tendency to form 2 lines) and several other people making the sandwiches. I was very pleased with the service. 

I ordered the beyond sausage sandwich. The plant based product (!) tasted like sausage- and it was thick and meaty. It was served on an English muffin (toasted but soft), with American cheese and a fried 'over well' egg. It was tasty though not really what I tend to crave for breakfast. I guess I also wonder about plant based products designed to mimic animal products AND served with animal products. If I had it again, I'd grab some hot sauce.

I love this Dunkin' so much! I come here every Tues and Thurs morning around 7:15 and order the exact same thing- large cold brew, no ice, vanilla and cinnamon shot. I also usually get the Power Breakfast Sandwich, which is turkey sausage, a veggie egg omelet, and cheese all on a multigrain sandwich bread. It's filling and tasty!

Did you know about their flavor shots? They're an essence that they put in your coffee that has zero sugar (or artificial sweetener) and tons of flavor. It's like if you were to order the hazelnut coffee at Bread Co, same concept. There are several flavors and my go-to in the winter is the vanilla and cinnamon since it reminds me of a holiday drink! Great option for people who like additional flavor but don't want the sugar rush from the syrups.

Onto the staff. I've only ever had super positive interactions here. I typically interact with ladies when ordering and men when I get my food (this seems to be typical staffing trend at quick service places), but everyone is so friendly, warm, and kind. It's my favorite way to start my day when I go into the office since I WFH other days. I proudly call Tues and Thurs my 'Dunkin Days', they always start my day off sunny!!

They were out of medium size cups. I do appreciate the paper straws to be environmentally conscious. 

The beyond sausage sandwich was delicious and I would recommend!

They no longer serve peanut donuts (helpful for those with allergies, for sure, but a bummer). 

We ordered an assortment of 50 munchkins in a bucket and NO CHOCOLATE. Out of 50 donuts, not one was chocolate? Yikes.

And the most important observation was that during a global pandemic NONE of the employees were wearing a mask correctly... In Florida.

This is by far the most worthless Kentucky Fried Chicken that I've ever been to. Correct that, the most worthless "fast food" restaurant I've been to. If I'm going to stoop to the level of eating any of this garbage, please at least make the food available that I'm going to regret eating later. 

The past 3 times I've been miserably hungover enough to think this place sounds good they haven't been able to deliver the basics: the popular famous bowl and the chicken sandwiches off the cheap menu.

Next time this place sounds worthwhile, drive past the kfc and go straight to the Wal-Mart.
