Walk to the deli area, grab some of their awful chicken that has sat under heat lamps for hours, and move on to picking the delicious sides and other niceties available to accompany your lush meal. Now go wait in the longest line of lovely Wal-Mart shoppers to complete the deal. 

Bam you have your equal kfc!

We had the worst experience with the food ever at a Burger King! We ordered cheesy tots, fries, onion rings, a whopper meal, chicken nuggets & 2 bacon cheese burgers. The food taste old, not fresh. The nuggets were soggy. They didn't have any onion ring sauce.  This is the second time in a few months the food at this Burger King has disappointed us!

My gosh people who put four stars are lying this place is the worst place ever... like close down already ....a chicken place with no chicken hahahaha...seldom get orders right...unwilling to help employees...un kept establishment...one time We waited an hour in parking lot for them to bring out the WRONG order after already waiting in drive through before that...it's been like this way before COVID so don't say it's ever been great because over the last 2.5 years I have yet to have one gone right experience....

Once again I was sorely disappointed in the food. I had chicken tenders which were just awful. It would be different if maybe it was cheaper than say other fast food restaurants, but in fact if anything it was more expensive. The french fries had maybe eight sticks in it, and although it was edible they didn't give me any catchup. I get this at one star, only out of charity

Standard fried chicken fare and everything else you've come to expect from KFC. This particular location is...something else. We have had some of the weirdest and worst customer service experiences here, but, it is one of the only quick fried chicken places near where we live. The service is usually very slow, whether you go inside or through the drive-thru. The food is generally pretty decent quality, but there is a high probability that you will end up with the wrong order, so make sure you check it before you actually leave the premises. Overall, this place isn't great and should probably only be visited in cases of extreme fried chicken necessity.

Stopped in yesterday to fill an order and they were out of crispy chicken for the day. They did have regular,  but were running low on that as well. In addition, they didnt have biscuits ready either so I had to wait 6 more minutes. They offered to give 4 cookies instead of biscuits but this wasnt my order so obviously we waited.  Spent about half an hour waiting for them to get things together. Looks like this location has a history of running out of food based on the reviews..
I love Chipotle for a big burrito fix but against California Mexican food this place would be two stars but we are in Florida.   Love the carnitas and barbacoa with the the hot sauce!  Yum!

Fast and friendly service here!  I have never seen this location anything other than clean and well stocked.  I love that they have lemon, lime, and orange slices available upon request behind the cash register here.  Vegan friendly too! Order the sofritas and you will not be disappointed! Beans, chips, guacamole and rice are vegan here too.

During warmer months, there is nice outdoor seating available adjacent, which is shared with other nearby restaurants.  

The only thing that I dislike about this location is that the parking situation could be a better.  It shares parking in a large lot with several other restaurants, and it can be a pain to find nearby parking during lunch hours here, especially during the work week. On the other hand, it's handy that it shares a parking lot with Sprouts!

Gave this one another try and they've been pretty good until today. Stale buns, wrong size whopper patti. Plus my credit card has been stolen 2xs . I can't accuse her because I'm not 100% sure but I'm suspicious of this Burger King because both times it has happened within a few days of using this Burger King, again can't prove it but it seems suspicious

Food is good but they are messy. I understand everything is by each other and they get a lot of people but this location needs to work on keeping their work area clean and not mixing everything with everything

While I was in line their was a door dash personnel getting an order and she asked what the order was and as she explained what it was, she cut her off and said "oh no. I cannot fulfill that order, we are going to have to cancel. I do not have the food, Or do it partial." All this happened at roughly 9:30pm. How is it that theirs no food at 9:30pm Even tho their was 5 people including me to order food. I WILL NEVER COME BACK! And I will make sure my Friends and Family Never Go to your location again. I am highly dissatisfied with my visit and the customer service I received.

I saw the huge posters on the windows for something called the Impossible Whopper.   Inside on the digital menu flashed the Impossible Whopper in stunning colors.   They obviously want me to try it.    I ask the cashier for this Impossible Whopper you have advertised all over your store.   "We don't have that."

The Impossible Burger is impossibly good. My lunch companion ordered a beef whopper and wished she had ordered the Impossible. This burger is awesome. How do you run out of chicken , and staff standing around joking. Sorry but another Tucson location I will not visit again.

BURGER KING, WHERE ALL DRAGON MASTERS EAT!

Burger King changed their advertising direction years ago to get away from "The King" and all the weirdness that surrounded him, and go a more traditional "we sell burgers and also smoothies" route; and for that I will never forgive them.

Of course, it is still pedantic fast food and they won't make you an Angry Whopper no matter how much you plead, so here we are at a big fat two stars.

This is my second time in two weeks for a chicken sandwich and they did not have any.  The first time I ordered chicken fingers instead for my kids.  When I asked the lady about the chicken sandwich, she said they were frozen. This time I just drove away.

I'm not one to write reviews but today I was really disappointed with my lunch experience at this location. My first stop in line I asked for a veggie bowl. Unfortunately, they were out of veggies on the line. The woman didn't really do much about it. I thought she would of gotten or asked for more veggies but she didn't and slid my bowl down to the next person. I, then, asked her if I could get more veggies and she responded but I couldn't hear her because it was slightly loud and she spoke softly. When I asked what she said, she responded louder with a rude tone "they're cutting them up". Neither of the women asked if I wanted to wait for the veggies. I ended up just getting steak because I felt if I even asked to wait, the 2nd woman would be annoyed with me. When I asked to add guacamole, there was barely enough left to even have a scoop. She didn't bother to get another batch nor did she seem to care she was out. Finally when I paid, sat down and began to eat, my bowl was just like my experience in line...disappointing. The rice was hard in some places like it hadn't been cooked all the way, the steak wasn't hot and my "guacamole" was basically a piece of a hard avocado with some guac in there. No one smiled or said sorry for being low on stuff that I wanted. I love chipotle, but today was just too disappointing not to write a review...I hope next time I come in, it will be better.

I went in to order two Whopper's for 6 $  and the staff were nice and the place was clean inside and outside of Burger king. The Men's bathroom was also clean and had hand soap and paper towels. I order chicken bowls a little too often from many locations. This location is consistently incredibly stingy on the potatoes. Not even enough to mix it. Over fryed chicken balls. Slow service. I asked twice for larger chicken bowl and still was given the small one. Worst kfc in the city by far.

Order was verified and when I got home was wrong for the 2nd time in a row.  Chicken was okay but not nearly as good as other KFC's.  One mistake is understandable, two mistakes says it careless.  Won't go again.

I give this place a 0 of five star review. I only still go here once in awhile because it is close and my son loves there food there food isn't the issue the customer service sucks every time I order something here it's wrong and I mean every time I order I find myself going back in to get the rest of my order everytime. Fast fresh fried chicken friendly service! Still the only place to get fried chicken in E'ville & Glen Carbon IL.

Those who know me know I usually am on the higher side of ratings but I just can't here. 

The drive through is almost always painfully slow. 
A few times when I got my meals home they were wrong. Ive learned to check before pulling away. 
Going inside usually doesn't make a difference. 

The inexcusable thing is running out of chicken parts at peak meal times as I have experienced once in January, once in March and 2 days ago in April. If you, like I consider dinner time being at least 5PM Until 6PM then running out of chicken parts when CHICKEN IS YOUR BUSINESS is something that will be detrimental to your business success. 
Yesterday at 5pm I was told they were out of thighs when I ordered the 2 PC Meal which consists of one thigh and one leg. I really like thighs! 
This is really sad because the food when available is good and the people who work here are nice. The location is Prime!!!
I am hoping they read some of these reviews and take action.

If I could give a negative star I would. I placed an online order for two bowls and a kids quesadilla. I got home to see 2/3 orders missing ingredients. My bowl was missing double chicken and steak literally the protein of the meal. The kids quesadilla didn't come with rice and beans just a sad piece of tortilla with barely any cheese in it. I called the store and spoke with "VIc" who addressed himself as the manager and stated he would be refunding my meals and that I would get confirmation of such. This was on Tuesday 5/25 and it's Friday, it does not take that long to process something of this nature. Aside from leaving this review I'll also be writing corporate.

This is the 2nd time I had to wait almost an hour past the pickup time on a mobile order. However, this time was laughable. My husbands steak burrito didn't have any steak. He couldn't figure out what protein it was. Chicken, maybe? Also, his fajita burrito had one pepper. Yes- one measly slice of green pepper and no onions. Wow. My daughters bowl was missing salsa and they were very stingy with the rice and protein too.  When I tried to discuss the matter on my app, the online customer service chat is a bot. At least I got some of my money back. 
I don't have very high expectations from these places, but they can and should do better. Maybe turning off mobile orders if they get too busy? That may help.

There must be idiots working here! I was asked to bring home Burger King tonight. I ordered the #1 whopper with no onion, tomatoes and lettuce large size with a coke. Then ordered a #1 double meet whopper with bacon, dressed and extra onions large size with coke. I ordered a extra medium onion rings. I was in the neighborhood and decided to stop, bad mistake. I am glad I looked in the bad of food. First missing the order of extra medium onion rings, also missing large fries. I figured the whoppers were ok, got home where I found the first whopper dressed and extra onions that was not supposed to be dressed and no onions and burger patties were burnt on both whoppers. The second double whopper fully dressed and extra onions to be a single whopper dressed no bacon, no extra onions. The order was completely wrong and the only large size was the coke that taste like water with a hint of coke. Need to just shut this place down. Looking into the drive up window the place looks nasty and the food tasted the same way. This is the second time having a bad experience and will be my last. The place does not deserve one star but only way to leave a review is to leave a undeserved star.

This was my first visit to any Carl's Jr. and I must say that I was extremely pleased. When big burger chains like Carl's Jr. decide to roll out the Beyond Burger at all of their locations, they will open themselves to an entirely new customer base. The vegan world is abuzz about the Beyond Burger at Carl's and I was eager to get out there and try one. I've had the Beyond burger at home and in a few other places, but Carl's preparation of the burger was among the best I've tried. I was served quickly, the dining room was clean, and my other meal accompaniments--french fries, and an iced tea--were perfect. The dining room on this first Monday of the new year was brimming with working men on their lunch hour, and everyone's food seemed to be served efficiently. I would definitely visit this location again.

I ordered a whopper with cheese meal and an order of small onion rings. When I parked my car to eat I noticed I was missing my fries, I got out of my car to go inside to let them know the door was locked so I knocked and one of the workers looked up and did nothing then another worker looked at me and made a motion with his hands to tell me drive thru only. I had to get back in the drive thru to get my fries.

The donuts are pretty good if fresh. The service is great here. The atmosphere is part fast-food, part coffee-shop, with some comfy chairs and short tables in one area. I had a sausage and egg bagel, and it was awful. The sausage tastes like chemicals, and the egg white was mush, like it was formed powdered egg whites. Stick to the donuts.

This place takes a million years for anything. Half the time they are out of chicken and even in the drive through they only help one person at a time. Like take their order cook their food for 20 minutes and then take the next order. It makes no sense to me. I waited 45 minutes just to order, and the only reason I continued to wait was because I was already boxed in. Redoculois.

Ordered through Uber Eats... Delivery driver was fast and communicated excellently. However, I ordered all dark meat and the driver called me and informed me that they didn't have enough white meat to fill my order... I was confused of course but said OK, all dark meat was fine...

Got my order and it was ALL white meat... wings and breasts. The chicken was hot and delicious but I wanted all dark meat. This location has gone downhill for some reason. Always some issue with something there... Sigh...

Such a simple thing to read someone's order and confirm it before you send it out. I guess that has become too difficult for these guys at this location...

today i placed an online order at this store and 1 of the bowls was wrong it had either sofritas or some salsa not exactly sure but we didn't realize this until we got all the way home in washoe pretty far away we went back and asked them to fix it and the girls who helped were completely helpful and clearly sorry but the manager a man named christian was completely rude when my boyfriend said he put sofritas on his food he snapped back saying i didn't put sofritas on your food and my boyfriend said okay so maybe salsa? and the manager rolled his eyes and said so? i previously worked at this chipotle it had this exact problem when i worked there i hated the management and was continuously embarrassed for them they're entitled and think they can be rude to a costumer who is clearly correct also making their staff uncomfortable i assume for being so rude to a customer who's clearly right we will be posting on your survey thing as well. you need to do some serious training with your managers

Believe it or not, this was ordered for a birthday. It had been years since my family had Burger King as a treat.

We downloaded the BURGER KING app. #NewCustomers 

We had it delivered (they use Door Dash).
Our family is disabled & we cannot drive.

It was processed late at night (10pm), but was made fairly quickly, despite the 45 min estimate time -- it came closer to 30 minutes. And we really appreciated that! 

Aside from the prices having increased, it can be hard to know how much to order. Attached are pics, so you you can be the judge. 

We cannot drink soda, and on the app it appears you can't swap for juice or coffee, so we didn't order "meal deals".

We got:

DOUBLE WHOPPER (with BACON) $9.84
Extra lettuce .25, Extra tomato .25
Extra onion & pickle = no charge

DOUBLE WHOPPER $8.44
Nothing extra

LARGE ONION RINGS $3.61

LARGE CLASSIC FRIES $4.39
(discount offers didn't apply to delivery)

Delivery fee $1

"Service Fee" $2.63 -- what is this?

Tip (for driver) 15% $3.94 
*note: driver stated they got lost due to DD app

Tax $1.76

TOTAL = $35.61

Food was barely warm when it arrived, driver was very polite and apologetic though. We chose to reheat, but it was still good, and had that proper "Burger King" flavor all these years later! 

The burger size was perfect, very filling.
Bacon was floppy, sort of hard to taste or detect, but I enjoyed trying it for the 1st time. Wouldn't bother again on a Whopper.

Frankly, we would have liked more fries & onion rings, but it was enough and we shared. The cost is prohibitive for "junk food", and the more you order, the more all those extra fees go up...so, this will probably remain a rarity. 

However, we preferred the flavor to McDonald's overall. And our nearby McDonald's location has been a hassle with strict & arbitrary Covid rules, so we're on shopping for alternatives like El Pollo Loco too.

Just ordered take out. Never order after 8. The staff doesn't restock and you are left with bare minimum and they will not make more because closing time is in their view

Ordered a steak bowl and had 4 pieces of steak. This is the third time and should have learned my lesson

Jeez, it just isn't that hard to keep your ingredients ready for when customers come in. This place always has a ton of people working, yet they are pretty slow. I've had good bowls there, but their customer service  is not the best. Walked in the guy in front us asked if something was going to get refilled, and I didn't hear what he asked about. Now it is my turn and I look before ordering and the carnitas, veggies, and some other stuff was completely out. I asked if the carnitas was getting refilled and she probably not...its was 5 pm on a Saturday. I could see if it was closing time. Seriously, poor planning, the counter was covered in food from other orders and pretty dirtly. Again, tons of people running around but no one  really getting a job done.

"Nasty" doesn't begin to describe this Burger King. The place is a pig sty. Let me count the ways:

Back door to dining room is locked. Was told it is prevent robberies. What?? Can you say "fire code violation " ?

Food scraps are all over the floor. Don't even get me started. 

An elderly female employee actually came to my table, said "We gave you the wrong food" and retrieved a sandwich from my tray to give to someone else. Uh. Health violation?  

Employees gather near the only door, smoking and vaping. You must walk through a wall of smoke, vapor trail to get your Whopper. 

The decor takes me back to when John Travolta was singing "Staying alive" which you will be singing after you eat here.

As a student going to school near here, I go here very frequently. Although I've seen my fair share of bad service, I've always been able to handle it because its Chipotle; you go there for cheap food, not for the manners of its employees.

This was another issue though. Today, I dropped by around 3:30pm for a lunch since I've been studying all day. I asked for a bowl with rice and black beans, which was dutifully fulfilled, but when I asked for carnitas, the worker put chicken in the bowl instead. No biggie.

I tell her I actually asked for Carnitas.
She doesn't say anything to acknowledge me, doesn't even look up at me.
I tell her again. 
She ignores me again. This time, she starts talking to her co-worker behind her about something else, while she starts working on a new bowl. At this point, she has not acknowledged that I've said anything at all. 
I tell her once again.
She looks straight back and yells straight in my face, "I KNOW!"

I've dealt with the other problems here, including messed up orders, the saltiness/blandness of the food, little pieces of plastic in my burrito, the "can't-be-fucked" attitude of the workers, but today was a first. 

 Never have I ever been yelled at in a restaurant for a mistake that the worker made. At no point did she seem the least interesting in dealing with me until she literally yelled at me, and there was no follow-up either. I don't even use Yelp, but I literally created an account because I'm just fed up with this.

My son and daughter in law ordered Chipotle for my grandson and I.  3 Steak soft shell taco with Extra Steak, Cheese, tomatoes and 3 Chicken soft shell tacos with Extra Chicken, rice, tomatoes, cheese and sour cream.  They came bunched up in 1 piece of foil for 3 chicken  & 1 piece for 3 Steak, San the Extra meat which we paid for & Squashed up & VERY SLOPPILY THROWN TOGETHER!!! I don't want to gross anyone out, but it look like leftover trash from someone's meal!  I wouldn't have fed this to my dog!!!!!!!   I called the store and talked with the new manager who was VERY APOLOGETIC & tried to tell me how to get my refund back from Doordash but I explained that my children had gotten it for us and they were not going to go through the trouble!  So my grandson and I ordered from Southside pizza and threw the trash from Chipotle away!!!!! I am SOOOOOO DISAPPOINTED IN THIS LOCATION WHICH IS NEAR ME but EVERYTIME I ORDER FROM THIS ONE LOCATION, IT ALWAYS LOOKS LIKE THEY DON'T CARE WHAT THEY SERVE YOU!!!!!!!!!!   Hopefully, the new manager will turn things around-HE sounds like he has the PASSION, THIS LOCATION WAS LACKING!!!!!!l

I enjoy Dunkin Donuts coffee and love my Keurig for morning coffee.  I have been bitching for a while now, that they did not offer their coffee in a K-Cup. Yesterday I was watching the Today show getting informed on the daily news and to my surprise Dunkin Donuts had a commercial for their K-Cups. No wonder I couldn't find them in the stores or the Keurig website, they are only exclusive at a Dunkin Donuts store.

Tuesday was my day or running errands and I planned my route to take me by a DD.
As I shopped their display of K-Cups, I realized the other customers at that time were also buying boxes of DD K-Cups. They had their regular blend in both full strength and decaf as well as a couple flavored and hot coco for sale. I bought two boxes, but hesitated in my purchase. They were expensive, the quantity vs price did not compute like the ones on the internet or at other stores. They were more then a high end grocery store. Drink your coffee DD and wake up. There is no middle man here, it's your store and your product. I am sure you can price your K-Cups at a more reasonable price. 

Since I was there, I thought why not grab some breakfast so I ordered the Sausage Pancake Bites. The banners made them look so cute and tasty. Besides, I thought how can you go wrong with a combination of pancake and sausage. You can, it tasted like a old corn dog, so I do not recommend them. 

Service was average. The girl working the check out was perky, but the girl who tossed my order on the counter said, "3 pancake bites" in a rather surly tone.

The preparation at this location is mediocre at best. They seem to always be overwhelmed with mobile pickup orders or Ubereats types of orders. 

I've ordered for pickup and went home to find my order completely wrong! I ordered chicken and they gave me steak or vice versa. 

Additionally, the staff can be so overwhelmed that they forget to pack items entirely! If I did not pick it up, I wouldn't have been able to remind the cashier for both my drink and my side! 

I walked in and saw 2 employees rapidly packing orders from a screen and they were scrambling! Meanwhile, a small crowd of waiting customers were forming. It looked disastrous 

The staff could definitely use better training or more employees to handle the volume of orders. Especially to improve their quality of service and food preparation.

This place is good but they forget stuff if you mobile order. The last time I went I got a burrito with white rice, steak, lettuce cheese, and queso. It was all really good. It's better to order in person in my opinion.

Something really good happened to me the other day, and I want you all to know about it.

I copped a 40 of Bud Light from a Mexican grocery store, which is one of the few good places to get a 40 out here in the county. I was getting ready to take it to the head, and I figured I'd also eat some potato chips, because sometimes I get hungry when I'm putting on a drunk.

I saw my little brother had some Burger King, and I asked if he had any fries left, because I heard they had new fries, and of course I was curious. I tried one, but they were too warmed over to issue a final, consequential judgment. It wouldn't have been in the best interest of citizen food journalism. Then my little brother informed me that he had another Whopper, that I could have, because they were running two for one Whoppers, so he got two, but he wasn't about to eat two, because he's not a very big guy. I'd say he's about two thirds of the average American man of 5'10. Which is odd, because I'm roughly the size of an NFL linebacker (but not quite as athletic). How that happened I'm not sure. He doesn't have the facial features of someone born with fetal alcohol syndrome, though he is kinda slow on the uptake. Hmm...

Fast forward to this afternoon I wanted to get some fried chicken for lunch, but I waited too late, and I had to bust a shift in retail, which is of course my line of work, and then I stumbled upon a particularly provocative spread in an English men's magazine (the best kind), and the next thing you know I only had about 20 minutes to get something to eat, including the time it would take to get there and back. Normally, this is the point when I would say fuck it and hit Wendy's, but Wendy's is now dead to me. Not just Dave Thomas, or Wendy, if that's an actual person, but the entire Wendy's organization as a staff, crew and a record label. West side! Plus, I had that good experience with Burger King the other day (no Boutros), and I figured I should follow it up with a full-on review.

The plan was to get the buy one get one Whoppers and (duh) actually eat both of them, plus some of their new fries. But when I hit the drive thru, I was informed that the Whoppers were no longer on BOGO. Today it's chicken sandwiches. Two full price Whoppers wouldn't necessarily be prohibitively expensive to a brother who just spent $25 for lunch at Pappy's the day before (by himself, mind you), but it's the principle! And the two chicken sandwiches were out of the question, because I seem to recall trying a chicken sandwich from Burger King once in 1993 and not really caring for it, and why would I want two of something I didn't want one of? Maybe if I could have gotten one chicken sandwich at half price and one Whopper, but you know how it is trying to negotiate with people at fast food restaurants.

The other main thing they had advertised were those BK Toppers, and that's what I ended up getting. They come in your choice of Mushroom and Swiss, Deluxe (yet another knockoff version of a Big Mac), and Western Barbecue, with barbecue sauce and onion rings, like on that Tenacious D album. The tagline in ads for these pieces of crap is "more meat, more value," or something along those lines, but that's a goddamn lie. The meat on a BK Topper is just large enough that it can't be considered a slider, according to the National Sandwich Rating Board. But it would be cool if it actually were as small as a slider, because then it would be a slider. (Think about it.) And I'm not sure if it's real meat either. It doesn't taste like the meat they use on a Whopper. It tastes like one of those Landshire hamburgers you get at a gas station. Don't ask me how I know this.

And then there was the fries. In theory, they were the best fries ever. They combine the best parts of the old fries at Wendy's and the fries at McDonald's, the best fries of all time, of ALL TIME. They might even be bigger around than the old Wendy's fries, but they've got a crunchy, salty exterior. But, alas, said crunchy, salty still ain't as good as the fries at McDonald's - and the extra potato inside is hardly any consolation. If I want more potato, I can always just super size it. Burger King just don't have the technology McDonalds uses to make it's food so delicious. Though I suspect they have been working on it. Their new fries must contain more than just potatoes and salt. I accidentally dropped one on the floor, and my brother's dog refused to eat it. She didn't seem to recognize it as food. True story.

Ok stopped here for lunch as I needed a quick lunch and chicken sounded good. Got a wing/breast. Look at the wing it is ONLY slightly larger than the spork thy gave me. Food tastes decent but seriously the wing!!!

My Wife went here to pick up Dinner for us.  She ordered  a 10 pc bucket. half original and half crispy value meal with sides and cinnamon buns.  We only got 7pcs of chicken of which 3 legs were original only. The rest was crispy.  We did not get the Cinnamon buns because she thought it was in the big bag with the fixings in it.  We will not be visiting this location ever again. We will spread the word about this location.

Ordered 3 things on the chipotle website for pickup. 1 order was correct. The kids meal was missing the chips and instead had fruit, no biggie. But my steak burrito bowl was MISSING STEAK. Pretty disappointed as on every receipt from the order it says steak. It even showed steak on the sticker that was on the cover of my bowl, I thought maybe it was buried at the bottom of the bowl but nope.

This is one of the worst KFC's. The service is absolutely horrendous. After giving me a thigh (I asked for a breast), I called the restaurant. The manager asked me to drive back there because she didn't believe it. When I got there, the manager adamantly stated that she the chicken she gave me was a breast. However, the chicken was dark meat... the manager (LaShara McWilliams) still refused. The manager would not give me the correct meal and continued to say that the thigh she gave me was a breast. The manager finally gave me my money back (and took back my meal). She never offered to provide me with a new meal. Also, they didn't give me a receipt after I paid for my meal.

Pros:
Remodeled and relatively clean. High top seating available. Multiple TVs inside. Check app for coupons/promotions. It's worth it! The Impossible Burger tastes good for the vegetarian and even the carnivore.  

Cons:
The TVs were tuned to inappropriate channels/shows. My children were eating and watching tv, only to hear cussing and watching inappropriate behavior / things you wouldn't find on regular tv. I think they were internet streaming. Definitely not a family atmosphere. Also they were out of chicken fries and honey mustard, in addition to drink flavors in the soda machine.

Every time I order for delivery from this location, it's NEVER right. They are extremely sparse with their toppings and they consistently forget to pack my sauces on the side! 

I've ordered the carne asada which is dry without the honey vinaigrette on the side. Of course, that would be the sauce they forget to pack! Additionally, when I order a "salad" bowl, the bowl would be HALF FULL and heavy on the chili corn while there's literally only a bite's worth of lettuce? 

I've specifically ordered "extra" fajita veggies and I would barely get a half serving of one!

Also, they are sloppy with their preparation. If I order steak, there will usually be bits of chicken or other meats thrown in the mix! 

It's a shame this location's preparation is so poorly executed. I love my Chipotle lunches but I don't trust getting it delivered anymore!

At a fast food restaurant all you asks for is your food to be fast. I was asked to pull to the front of bk to wait 10 min for an impossible burger and some chicken fries. Why sway why? Because they are slow on the cell phone and talking to each other that's why...

Boy oh boy, what an exciting  experience at my local Burger King today! I haven't been back to this BK on a while since my wife and I went for milkshakes and as we ordered them we could hear an employee in the background shouting "nah bruh, no milkshakes". Needless to say we took our business to sonic that evening and evenings since then for our milkshake needs. 

But today, when I pulled up around 1:35 to order a quick bite to eat I was "greeted" I guess you could call it by easily the most soft spoken person to have lived in the 21st century. I ordered my #5 just the sandwich and a large fries and when I concluded ordering she very boldly stated "that's it?". Why yes today in fact I am not going to have anything else that could possibly make your job any harder. She tells me "aight they Gona help you at the 2nd window". 

Cool beans food time right. Here is where it gets interesting. As I pull forward through the charbroiled smoke cloud of doom I'm greeted by a very nice young lady whom I give my payment to. As I look down for half a second to glance at my phone in my lap, shame on me for distracted driving right. Out of nowhere I hear "HOW YOU DOING SIR, MY NAMES CRYSTAL, IM A NEW EMPLOYEE HERE, WOULD TOU FILL THIS SURVEY OUT TO WIN A FREE SANDWICH?", shouted at me as a 4th grader would who try as they might can't remember their lines for the school play so they need to shout them as loud as possible when they enter their frontal cortex. She manages to somehow squeeze her way between the nice calm young lady taking care of my order and the window like an unwanted popcorn kernel between two poor unsuspecting teeth. "Crystal", now hands me a crumpled blank piece of receipt paper one side being the typical "free whopper, let us know what you think on on side and the other with the name "CRyStal" written in yellow highlighter In what appears to be a form of calligraphy used by the the blind and illiterate. I said " I'll give it a shot, knocking I would probably just crumple it up and throw it away." But now she repeats herself a second time much like a child who doesn't get enough attention at home and feels the need to repeat themselves to ensure they are heard, and I decided to hang on to it as I observed her wearing the only backwards hat in the restaurant. 

She must be important right. I can only make that assumption by the stand out uniform she was wearing, the 4x sized jacket and the backwards hat and the unbelievably low sagging pants. Like pants sagging so low you should just take your pants off as they aren't serving the engineered purpose as pants. Anyways she begins to turn her back to resume bothering any other poor person whom she might have to encounter during the day. I felt as though I was born again during my visit after she left my general vicinity. So almost time to get my food yay! Now she goes to bother someone else and says "I can't deal with y'all today, in what seemed to be a joking manner" and then I hear her say "WHAT IS THIS SOURDOUGH" it couldn't be, my worst fear, the distracting,loud,obnoxious, cringey, malibu's most wanted looking individual was going to handle my food. At any rate someone from around the corner says "that's his". Ah yes, the pinnacle of my experience when I get to have my food and leave. Shortly thereafter a lady in a blue polo come from around the corner with a bag and a receipt. The same receipt that CRyStal gave me eairler with the survey on the back, OH BOY! As she's handing my my brown sack of potential sadness she says "that was my fault" in a very monotone unsure voice. But alas, my dear lady in the blue polo, CRyStal appears to be at the fault of society for lacking some social etiquette and dress standards. 

My hamburger was pretty good. Burger King has the best French fries of all the he French fries to ever be fried. My today could have used a dash more salt, but still pretty good.

Service here is lacking and needs a lot of work and acccountability. If I wanted to be bothered in such a way I would choose to spend time around children. Which I don't have any of because I hate them. 

If Bummer King brings back the lucky charms milkshake and can assure we can order and receive it during hours of operation I will consider bringing my business back.

We just had the most horrible experience.  Because nobody would help us when we walked in, we ended up using the ordering kiosk.  My husband and I, with our 3 children, ordered 5 impossible burger meals (was supposed to include drink and fries).  We waited over 20 minutes, we were totally ignored.  The people working there wouldn't acknowledge us and even when they handed us our meals, they walked away when we said thank you...without giving us our drinks.  Assuming they were getting our drinks, we waited another 10 minutes and they all ignored us even though there were NO other customers there.  Then an elderly woman came in, ordered a shake and they messed THAT up and didn't apologize to her.  She left looking so sad.  Finally when my husband asked where our drinks were, the person working the counter shrugged and walked away again.  Seriously, we have never been treated this badly at ANY restaurant.  The kids had already found a place to sit and our food was cold, microwaved, not even grilled.  The french-fries were stale and cold.  There weren't any condiments on the burgers except for some floppy pieces of lettuce.  When we asked for a manager we were ignored.  Finally, as I took a bite out of my burger and had to pull a very long hair out of my mouth, I got upset and went up to demand our money back.  This time there was a woman there who claimed she was the manager.  All she did was tell me how they didn't have to make us happy, she didn't care what our food was like and oh - my favorite - that lots of people get hairs in their burgers.  We stood there stunned.  Finally my husband demanded the money back, I got the children from their half eaten meals and we all walked out.  Besides all this, the floors were sticky, the tables were dirty, there were flies on the counter and a cricket by the garbage can.  We are still shocked and troubled by the terrible treatment we received.

Went through the drive thru around 1:30pm, a good time after lunch hours. There was one car in front of me. The lady said 6-7 minutes for chicken tenders and had me park in front. I was waiting until 1:46 and finally got out of my car. That's ridiculous! No apology and no refund which I should've gotten. I was late getting back to work. Y'all suck!

I prayyyy that someone higher up sees this and can do something about this , because this chipotle is so horrible so so so horrible. DO NOT GO HERE. I ordered for it to be delivered and it was wrong- once again like the last time-  and then take it to the manager at the store with dreads and he literally makes the order over and i had asked for steak and no beans no cheese and he gives me tofu with beans and cheese and I'm allergic to cheese which i explained, and still AGAIN MESSED UP. he was acting as if he was stoned literally. I asked for my money back and it took him a long time to find out how to give me a confirmation number proving my money would go back on my card soon, i am Just disgusted at this location. I used to work for chipotle and this is NOT how this company ran where we worked. Its just sad. I wont make the mistake ever again - I'm done with chipotle thanks to this location.

I ordered chicken and biscuits from this KFC via Grubhub; 1 drum, 3 wings, 2 biscuits. So how it they did not put the other wings in the box baffles me? And the biscuits were dry and crumbly, like it was from yesterday's old batch. This order was total trash. It only takes once for me.

They don't deserve a star at all.  Went to the restroom first, what a dirty mess.  Not a plumbing problem but years of grime floor to ceiling.  Just turned around an left.  Sure wasn't going to "eat" there.  You think their food prep would be much better?  No!

Not only was the Drive thru lady whom is a manager extremely rude and still after repeating my order 4 TIMES she still got it wrong. I was the second car in line was asked to wait in parking lot finally after several minutes I tried to go in but it was locked but the man handed me my bag and  repeated my order he forgot 1 of the 2 sandwiches. No sauces in bag for onion rings or nuggets. But the sandwiches were so nasty with old lettuce and when the other car that was behind me which made him the 3rd in line was asked to wait in parking lot also but when he actually saw how disgusting my whopper was he pulled away without his order! Now that tells you how horrible this was . Burger King you were one a Proud family restaurant that used to care but now it's horrible food with horrible rude service. Never again ever and and 30 something years ago I was a Crew Chief for Burger King back when you had Pride . Now you honestly just don't care and if you don't believe me please have someone drive through there place a simple order and see what you get.

Each time I've come here (except maybe 2-3 times) our order has been wrong. Last time at the drive thru window I had ordered an iced coffee and received a hot coffee, ordered two bacon wake up wraps and received two egg and cheese bagel sandwiches. I had to ask the girl before she served my food if she got my order correct because my amount due was a lot higher than expected.. This morning, I ordered three bacon wraps and got 2 with a sausage one in there... 
Don't get me wrong... the food and coffee are great, but the orders are just almost never correct. 
Love the convenience of it being down the street, but highly disappointed in the service here.

This location have failed me twice, the first time I let it slide because maybe it's just a mistake. But the second time around, I can't let it go anymore. I'm writing this review in hopes they can clean up their act. Twice I reach out to voice my concerns and no response at all. We ordered Carnitas Bowl and Carnitas kids meal, the meat was so dry and the kids meal was missing the rice and fruit cup. Ordered it for dinner. These were mobile orders. Again twice I reached out and no response at all.

Becareful with this place.  I've been frequenting other Chipotle places almost on a weekly basis.  I stop by this one, and order my usual steak bowl.  When i first arrived, the employee just had a cold stare didn't even say hi.  There were no other customer in line and they took like 2 mins to get to me, so it's not a deal breaker. Now, I'm not expecting 4 or 5 star level service at a place like this.  You would at least expect a hi back when you the customer engaged them with a hello.  I asked for the extras i like (extra beans and sauce).  When it came time time to pay for my order, the casher rung up 12.99.  I was taken back.  Apparently the server put an extra steak tag on my order when i only said steak once.  She gave me a look as if I was lying.  I had to re-affirm twice that I only order steak once.  The cashier at least apologized and that was the reason this isn't a 1 star review.  I remember her scooping the steak only once, and now I'm back at my desk and no, it was not a double scoop.

TLDR:  be mindful of what you order.  They may be overcharging and accuse you of lying when you order.

Idk why I keep giving this place a chance 15 min for some chicken fries and impossible whopper is unacceptable. This is suppose to be a fast food restaurant. And who runs out of dr pepper I mean come on it's one of the 4 top drinks to order. Burger king should be disappointed in the customer service there staff continues to offer.

Th chipotle has gone downhill serving size is twice as little as it used to be. I pay for extra steak and get barely any . This only happens when you use the app, you will get less food. Only reason I wrote this cuz it's happened last three visits. But shame on me for not learning the second time lol.

Awesome place to stop by if your looking for sweets and a pick me up to start your day. The coffee is usually pretty fresh but I am a tea guy myself. There iced coffees and the frozen drinks are usually my go tos when I am here. I really enjoy the cinnamon buns and the chocolate donuts holes.   I am especially glad to see their vegan sausage that is now offered. Didnt get to try it yet but I do like seeing the healthy options around town.

I usually don't leave reviews, but after reading some of the past reviews for this bk I thought I would leave one as well. Just got two meals from this bk with prompt friendly service. My fries were hot and fresh, and staff was very nice. I ordered an impossible whopper and did not have to wait extra for the special patty like another review mentioned. Five stars!!

This was the second time I dined at this Chipotle. It was fine. Nothing exciting to write about. I ordered a steak quesadilla as did my husband. Chipotle is not known for their amazing quesadillas so I have to be fair when reviewing. 

After we ordered our quesadillas we sat down.  We finally received our food after what felt like forever and it was extremely greasy. To my knowledge they don't use any oil when making these. I wiped off all the grease then checked the inside only to discover all of that grease was from the cheese. They cooked it so long that all the cheese had turned to melted fat and oil. It reminded me of when they carve open the Turkey on Christmas Vacation and it's just steam and stringy meat.

I thought about asking for a new one. It's nothing but minimum wage workers...why do they care? I agonized then finally stood up and made my decision. I wasn't going to enjoy my food so I had to try. I went to the counter and asked them to remake it. Without hesitation the man hollered out for a re-fire (my words not his) on the two quesadillas. It was a nice surprise. I hate returning food and I can't handle being made to feel bad about it. He did not.

We received the two new quesadillas much quicker than the last batch. And they were made much nicer. As stated before Chipotle is not known for their stellar quesadillas so I can't complain too much. I did very much enjoy the guacamole. That was pretty great!

In Summary, my food was okay and of course I'll be back.

When I was a kid, I lived for those moments where dad would get us in the car, drive to KFC and order dinner .. and it was actually delicious. 

I have not had KFC in .. a long time so today I thought why not? After this so called meal, I should have asked Why???

OMG !! What has happened to KFC that made it get this bad??

Between you and me..  the colonel should be rolling over in his grave if he only knew what has happened to his food. 

I live a short distance away and by the time I got home there was so much grease/ oil on the bottom of the bag, it reminded me of the Exxon Spill.. gross !!! 

And if you dont want chicken, they have Taco Bell there as well.. UGH ! This is not 'Mexican" by any stretch.. the only thing Mexican is the salsa! 

I got home, opened the box.. those chickens are either anorexic or just hatched.. incredibly small pieces I have ever seen.

How can they serve this??? A handful of fries, small pieces of chicken and a small salad ( I love their macaroni salad ).. 

Want my advice?

Go to Popeye's or Mary Brown's .. KFC is not as good as it once was.. 

* I usually take pictures of food when I dine out.. this was not even worthy of a photo

Shame 0 stars not an option.  I realize it's impossible to find help right now with the Gov. paying them more to stay home but you would honestly be better off to shut the doors at this location until Covid blows over.  The Monetary hit you would take in the short term would be much less than what you are going see due to the damage to your reputation the rude a$$ employees working at this store are going to cause you in the long run.  

From first impression at the drive through "KFC, go" to being scolded for ordering a three piece big box with mashed potatoes and slaw rather than a #6 or whatever it was....  "what number you want. A 6 or 8."  "I don't  know...   I just want 3 pieces with mashed potatoes and slaw....  "what number?"  "Whats the difference?"  "One has 2 sides".  " well seeing as I want mashed potatoes and slaw that would be the one".  That  all?  "No i got 3 more". "What you want?" I want a spicy sandwich. What number?..... what drink?  Sprite. We don't have sprite....   phewwwww!  

Then i get home and find three greasy a$$ pieces of cold chicken with all the skin/fry purposely torn off of them laying in the bottom of a cardboard box without even a piece of wax paper to keep the grease from pouring into my lap when I picked the soggy box up.  
Oh, and one napkin.   

Chick running the window has no business interacting with customers.  Lock her in the back and let her mix biscuit dough or batter livers and gizzards  or something.   We'll drive to Dickson or Franklin next time. Won't be visiting your Bellview store again.

Worst KFC I've ever visited                   they charge extra for extra crispy chicken   Get your order wrong and give additude for their mistakes and still get the order wrong.   Stay away unless you go for additude and bad service.

No words can describe how nasty this location is.  Who could eat chicken laying in grease!  What happened to the world of fast food.  No pride and no management oversight.  When I grew up we had pride and cared about customer service and quality.
Smallest chicken in the world. Biscuits are bigger than the chicken. I guess the $20 bucket did not include adult chicken parts.

As always KFC has awesome gravy and great food except this time. The Chicken tasted old, and the gravy we paid extra for wasn't filled to the cutoff line. We asked for our receipt and they neglected to give to give to us. This was not the first time we had an issue but it was always corrected. We live approximately 21 mins. away. So it was/is not worth the drive back and without the receipt we could not call and address the issue.

Wish I'd read the other reviews before I stopped here. Ordered three chicken strips, mashed potatoes and gravy, coleslaw and no soda. After 20 minutes waiting, which I was told was because of my chicken, and told that when I ordered it I was told it would be a long wait--not true. What I received was three soggy chicken thighs, when I pick them up the skin just slid off. A biscuit and coleslaw. They tried to give me soda too. So the only thing they got right with the coleslaw. I threw the chicken away when I got home. Whoever owns this franchise should be paying attention to what's going on

Ordered a carnitas burrito with guacamole which was two halves of avocado that was so hard you could not eat it. I wouldn't complain if it was soft.
As I wait for my burrito to be filled, I noticed one of the prep guys chopping away at some meat, he was very good with the knife, my issue, he poured the fresh cooked meat on top of the meat that was already in the steam table pan, for who knows how many times.
You MUST rotate all the food hot or cold each time you fill the counter. Someone will get sick for sure.
I ordered two chicken strip meals and this is what they gave me, this place is trash!
Really bad taste original chicken.  It arrived on time I'll give credits for it. But the chicken was overcooked -- Even the thigh was not juicy at all.

My wife stopped in and picked up the Burger King answer to the McDonalds Big Mac.  Basically it was a double whopper.  It was terrible. Meat was overdone and dry, looked like a pound of lettuce, and watery thousand island dressing.  Waste of almost $7.00.  Not the norm for Burger King, but our most recent experience.

This is the slowest Burger King in the area.   It is obvious that the drive thru is "King" here.  It seemed to me that there was a 3 to 1 ratio of orders being delivered to the driver through for every one order handed out in the dining area.   This is not the first time I have observed this at this location.   I thought about driving 3 miles or back taking 5 miles to get to the next nearest BK.  I had 4 people in front of me...i have been here for 25 min.  And I still don't have 1 whopper sandwich
Ordered a bowl of steak with rice for takeout today and the rice was uncooked! There wasnt even that many customers in your store to rationalize the slacking off.

Ordered online a sofritas bowl and received half steak/half sofritas. I am allergic to meat, so thankfully I was able to notice the steak before eating it. I don't know if this was a case of cross contamination or what but this should not be happening. Employees need to be trained better to understand food allergies and dietary restrictions.

There should be an option for ZERO stars.
