2. Drive-Thru is closed half the time.  No reason just a board stating it's closed.
3. Almost every manager who has ever worked at this location is obnoxious and rude.  I have NEVER had a good experience at this KFC.  NEVER.  
4. The waits are unbearable.  It's FAST FOOD that takes a lifetime, every time.
 
I would like an executive from KFC to place an order at the drive-thru at this location unannounced and see the horrible, rude service.  The place always looks dirty from the window as well. 

I would like to know how  "Marco" and "Azhar" get a job as MANAGERS at KFC with the obnoxious people skills you will see on the video.   One screams at me, hangs up on me and yell at me and slam the drive-thru window in my face with one saying he's calling the police.  Don't you think the police have more urgent issues to attend to than chicken?  This manager actually dialed his cell phone in front of me, on video, stating he was calling the police.  How about SERVING GUESTS, COOKING CHICKEN, RINGING UP GUESTS, and moving the line along, instead of standing around like a useless manager calling the police.  Pretty disturbing to say the least. 

Whomever runs this location, needs to do a better job interviewing the people representing your brand.  They have failed time after time after time and the people who work at this location have always been rude. This location has had problem managers for years, as well as problems with customer service and the aforementioned issues. 

There are hundreds of local people who would like a job at KFC, who will treat your customers right, and yet Marco and Azhar are the best choices?  I think not.   Promote the helpful woman in the video who has been there a few days and terminate those who are driving your customers to the competition and maybe you'll see an increase in your store revenue and better reviews.  There are 10 fast food restaurants within a block of this restaurant, some in the same shopping plaza and across the street.  

NO ONE should have to wait 20 minutes for FAST FOOD and be verbally abused and screamed at for making an inquiry via phone while still on the drive-thru line, on why the line was moving so slowly.  Nor should anyone be treated the way I was treated in the video causing me to raise my own voice.   

I will never, ever go back to this KFC.  EVER.  KFC needs to clean house and start with some competent, friendly managers.

If I could give this KFC zero stars I 100% would their employees are rude, they are constantly out of product like CONSTANTLY half the time I go there they are out of chicken! It's literally Kentucky fried chicken like how can you be out of chicken? Also there's been several times where I have come an hour or 30 mins before closing and they just decide to close early? Get some better employees KFC it's getting worse every time I come...

Don't bother this place is the pits! Went to pick up my OL order which had been placed well in advance. When I got there I was told they only had legs & thighs. Really? It was a large order as we had a lot of people for Sunday dinner. They did not try to call me to tell me they had run out of chicken but waited until I came in to pick the order up & then acted like it wasn't their problem. Very rude. When are these places going to realize that they need to have adult managers on the weekends when they are busy?  Instead they just turn the place over to a bunch of teenagers.

TL;DR: Pink chicken, greasy wet breading that slid off, served legs, thighs, and breasts when strips were ordered, items forgotten entirely from order, excessive wait time.

Problem 1: You cook nothing but chicken. Did you magically forget that chicken should not be served F*****G PINK! Yeah yeah it was mostly there. You ever bite into a piece of chicken and the texture immediately makes your brain scream "danger Will Robinson!"? Cause that's what this under cooked mess did. I eagerly await the first symptoms of salmonella.

Problem 2: Again, you cook mother f***ing fried chicken for a living. Why on earth was the breading a slick soggy mess that sloughed off at the slightest touch like the skin of burn victim? Seriously, did you guys forget the proper cooking temperature for oil? I'm guessing this is also why the chicken was F***ING PINK! It also was liking spooning grease into my mouth. This is going in the trash where it belongs.

Problem 3: Do you know the difference between breasts, thighs, legs, or strips?  Apparently you don't because we ordered crispy fried chicken STRIPS and got pieces.  You bird brains can't tell the difference between a breast or a strip? It certainly wasn't crispy either as per problem 2.

Problem 4: This crap was ordered online. The order was printed. Not only did the strips turn into legs, thighs, and breasts you forgot the mother fucking potato wedges, mashed potatoes, and requested ranch.

Problem 5: We waited 30+ minutes for this sorry ass meal. This was after ordering online and driving there. We live 15-20 minutes away after working all day with 3 kids screaming in the car. 

Problem 6: Okay, this one is on us. This is NOT the first time we've had problems with this location struggling with the intricacies of putting the correct food into a bag. They have forgotten many an item, poorly cooked it, and taken far too long in the past. This is why we hadn't been there in close to a year. Sadly, we decided to try again. Hell, they even had a remodeling since our last visit which surely meant they had extracted their posteriors from the sunless place right? Or so we had thought. I pray we do not give them another chance again.

First and last time coming to this place  
Ordered a simple 3 item order on my app thinking it was gonna be easy to pickup and properly made 
Little did I know I show up with my order not ready at all come to find out someone messed it up which I understand people make mistakes . 
Then I wait about 20 min for a person to hand me another wrong bag , so after fixing that issue I finally was able to leave the store with my food , as  soon as I get back in my car I wanted to have a taste of my burrito and chips and queso ordered , yet again another mistake after another , they decided to only give me chips without queso lol and my burrito was cold as if it's been sitting somewhere before I even arrive I mean come on steak not being hot whatsoever even though it's literally grilled and chopped and kept under a heater . Incredible and the only thing that's at least lukewarm were the beans 
That is truly a disgrace to my Hispanic heritage and I don't advise even trying to go here when it's on a weekend , but lesson learned I'm gonna make sure to check on my local moes next time where even when busy they at least cook my food correctly especially after my order has been messed up in the first place.

Well this burger king was always reliable, always making that whopper that was a tall sandwich with all of the toppings. So I tried it.  The impossible whopper.  Its impossible alright, impossible to stay on the menu.  The meat was like fried bologna with liquid smoke added to it.  This is their premiere sandwich.  Always looking for a meatless alternative but this sandwich was meagerly build and looked as though it had a ring on the outside like it was sliced off of a tube of whatever.  Burger king, keep making the regular whopper. And when you know it gonna be busy, staff more people in your restaurant.

We had delicious burritos at this location. I had the steak burrito and my wife had the chicken burrito. Both were large and filling. We also had guacamole with chips. The chips are like crack. I keep eating them until they're gone. The staff were friendly and helpful.

Shrouded darkness. A yearning responsibility to awaken and choose: fight or flight? A regression to primal instincts and a final release to the euphoric abyss of the Chipotle steak burrito enveloping my mouth. My pupils were suddenly birds penetrating through the barricade of my eyelids. I was asleep, yet awake. Blind yet I could see all. Deaf to every noise except for the annoying screech of that song by Lizzo that was playing on the restaurant radio. Complete and utter bliss. Why would I open my eyes? Why would I continue to chew when it daringly warrants the fate of the bite departing from my taste buds? 

All these trivial thoughts died out when I was jolted out of euphoria when the restaurant manager turned up the tv volume to a blaring setting. It was so loud that it was virtually impossible to ignore it. I stared with a sort of unrecognized duty to remain attentive to the content inside the screen. The news station was playing. They discussed the 3 car pileup on the interstate and then the investigation of the murder homicide that took place two nights before. I suddenly felt as though I had seen it all before. When the newscaster switched to the weather segment, my heart began pounding faster. The weatherman said, "Today we have a cold front coming through..." Today... Today was Monday. If I'm not mistaken, yesterday had been Monday as well. Had the day before that been Monday? No this all is impossible and I don't need to worry myself... As I started to think about it some more, I muttered, "But what if..." All of a sudden every person in that Chipotle restaurant had their eyes fixated on me. I couldn't move. Their gaze paralyzed every part of my body. I couldn't close my eyes and make it all go away. I glanced at the tv which had gone quiet and soon realized why. The lady newscaster had begun staring at me as well. And as a break in the silence, she said my name. "Harrian." I tried to respond but I still could not move. Once again she said my name, "Harrian." And before I could devise up a way to respond she continued, "Honey... this is your mom and dad." My heart sank lower than the hardwood floor. I dismissed this thought with ease because my parents had died a decade ago in a car accident. "We miss you. Come home to us. I know that you're confused but we don't know exactly how this message will get through to you. We just need you to WAKE UP!" All I wanted to do was close my eyes to this dream and thus end it because I was mortified. "You've been in a very bad car accident sweetie and it put you in a coma. But now it's time for you to WAKE UP! Come back to us." At this point, tears were flowing down the face of the newscaster and she could hardly speak with a straight tone anymore. After 9-10 seconds she continued the newscast solemnly. The people in the restaurant were still staring deep into my soul. Under my breath, I repeated what the lady newscaster had told me. "Wake up..." As if on cue, every person in the restaurant, man woman and child, began to approach me with purpose. They reached their arms out to me. I still couldn't move. I couldn't prevent the onslaught of hands that was growing near. They began to surround me and place their hands on my shoulders and neck. And finally, in unison, they commanded me to, "WAKE UP!"

Blindingly bright were the surgical lights overhead. I realized quickly that my body was strapped down with two thick leather belts fastened tightly. I was now in a hospital room. I could hear my own heartbeat as though it were broadcast through the loudspeakers. I heard a woman cry, "Oh my God! Oh my God!" and I glanced to my left and saw a woman, my mother running to my side. "He's awake!" I heard the doctors rush into the room with shocked smiles and put their hands all over him, unplugging some tubes, unstrapping belts, and allowing me more room to move. As I stared into my mom's eyes I heard another woman's cry at the doorway. I glanced to my right and saw a woman carrying a baby. Tears began to flow from her eyes and she ran to the bedside next to my mother. She pressed her forehead against mine and I knew at that moment who she was. She was my wife. This was my child. She gently handed me the baby, my eyes fixated on his innocent face. Tears began to race down my cheeks as I looked into the eyes of a human who I had given life to. 

Overall, I'd give the Virtual Comatose Simulation at the hospital a 3/5. They didn't even let me finish my Chipotle steak burrito.

This Chipotle is absolutely terrible. Between the staff and preparation of food, this makes for a very bad experience.  Two visits in a row and both times were a disaster. For example: No steak or guacamole available in separate occasions. The food line was a mess and the staff were fighting amongst each other. Very sad for a successful franchise.

Man I don't understand the bad reviews for this location at all. I go to this location only and actually drove out of my way today to go here as I was craving it. This location is always clean, friendly, and very tasty. I got the famous bowl and my guest got some regular chicken. Both meals were perfect and very fresh. The other locations in town are absolutely horrible!!!

No chicken. No steak, no fajita? How are you a burrito joint with only 1 option for meat? Maybe if they combined forces with the west end location who doesn't have chips or TORTILLAS of all things, but have all the meats, maybe they would stand a chance

Yay for Carls Jr. offering the BEYOND BURGER, 
just be sure to order it without cheese and mayonnaise. 
The bun is vegan, their sauce (a ketchup and relish mix)  is vegan. And add some mustard to that! 
My advice is to skip the fries here and go get them from In and Out or Eeegees where the fryer isn't shared with animal products! 
I have made Beyond Burgers at home and it doesn't compare, the smoky, char grilled flavor that is imparted at Carl's was top notch!  
Now I'm not a fast food fanatic, so I will not be eating them all that regularly, but I'm so happy to know there is a new quick option available! 
Del Taco in California locations offer the Beyond crumbles for their customers.  I'm hoping more fast food chain establishments get on board because the future is vegan!

I swear every employee is stoned. Completely oblivious to the fact that there are 20+ people now forming in line behind me. They were out of steak, chicken and brown rice. I got there at about 5:30pm. How can they not stock up on their offerings before dinner time?? No way was I about to wait ANY longer for them to cook some more. Finally get to the register and the girl had no idea what she was doing. Had to call the manager twice. Just paying took another 15 minutes. I get that places are under-staffed right now due to Covid, but there has to be a standard, no?? We LOVE Chipotle, but this location is the WORST!
Get the Beyond Meat burger!  Finally, a delicious, juicy, cheesy, saucy burger that fills your craving for a burger with no guilt! (cruelty free)  Please keep this on the menu. I'll keep coming back!
they took 30 minutes to bring out my chicken and this isnt the first time. They have the worse service and it takes forever a drive-thru should never take this long

Got a Carnitas burrito with chips and Queso was ok. Girl that made it was courteous. Place was kind of dirty tables not clean etc. Place was empty. Went to use the restrooms both were locked. Asked girl at register if I needed a key or something. She told me they were "closed for the night" This was at 6:30 pm. I'll not be going back to this location.

Ordered extra steak in burrito bowl with just rice and cheese.  That's it, nothing else... very basic . $15 and nothing extra and shockingly empty.  If I wasn't home already and would have taken it back to get a refund.

I ordered delivery and would give the whole experience between a 2-3. I ordered steak tacos (and received chicken) and they forgot the chips with the guacamole and chips I ordered-luckily I had a bag here at home! Food was just okay. Probably would not eat here again.

This Burger King is pretty new and serves an area of town that is right beside I75. There is a truck stop next door, so I would assume that travelers and truckers are the most frequent customers of this location. I stopped by on a Thursday evening around 6:00 PM to grab myself a Whopper. When I got there, the parking lot was deserted, and the staff inside looked bored (as I would be). I ordered my food, and it was quickly prepared while I filled my "medium" drink cup. I selected a seat (which was clean) and ate my meal in peace.

That's it. That's the review. It's a Burger King.
Can you tell me why every time I come here around 4:45 use never ever ever have extra crispy chicken breast you say you just ran out well it's dinner time give me a break

I got an email from Chipotle about their Buy One, Get One Free Burritos and Burrito Bowls so I decided to use their mobile pick-up system to grab lunch.

Ordering on the app was super simple and also has rewards/offers/coupons that you can add to your orders. The estimated time to prepare my food was about 10-15 minutes. Once we arrived, we headed over to the shelf and grabbed the bag with my name on it. They also labeled the burritos so I would know which was which. 

The food was great, typical of any chipotle. I always get half-chicken, half-steak, white rice, extra corn salsa, sour cream, cheese, & lettuce.  My only complaint was that I didn't feel like I got extra corn salsa (just a bit obsessed with it). 

What I love about Chipotle is that it's a relatively healthier, cleaner fast-food option. It's customizable and they have nutrition calculators online for calories, carbs, fats, and proteins. 

I'd definitely recommend using their app, to earn rewards, skip the line, and have access to discounts and offers!

Consistently downhill at this location, unfortunately. Last straw was to go order for 7pm that wasn't ready until 7:50pm combined with over 60 people inside (no social distancing) and employee masks below their noses and our extra rice burrito with carnitas was about half the size of a typical burrito. Skip this location...flavors were good but I'm going elsewhere.
Food wasn't very good. My spicy chicken bowl was loaded with grease. The pop was flat. Husband did not get all of his order. Beware service is great but the food was nasty.

I usually get the steak, but changed it up and was pleasantly surprised that the chicken tasted really good. Good job whoever cooked the chicken. 
Everything was prepared as we ordered. The website wouldn't let us order for some reason so we had to come in for our order which I think is faster than a pick up order online anyway so it worked out.

We stopped here on our way home to OC from the Bay Area. I was hungry, but not feeling so great as my travelling companion weaved in and out down the twists and turns of the 101. We arrived and I had questions about one of the burgers. Nick was working the counter. He was incredibly helpful and unbelievably hospitable. Watching him it was apparent he was earnest in giving the best customer service to each person who walked in.

Because we were there so late we watched as Nick prepared the dinning room to get mopped. He was thorough as he put the chairs up and swept the rest of the room without making a scene, allowing us to enjoy the rest of our dining experience peacefully.

I hope the location management is aware of what a genuine Super Star Nick is for their location.

I ordered the BFC with a Beyond Meat patty and my friend had the Famous Star with cheese. We both enjoyed our meals.
This visit was awful. I ordered a double cheeseburger meal. And when I got home I was given a whopper Jr with two slices of cheese!!  Lol and that was even cold.

Parking is hit and miss! The line wasn't too bad they had a good amount of people on staff for lunch which is great! Food was hot and everything was good except for the excessive amount of fat that was on my steak I ordered The bowl with brown rice, they also Charlene extra for guacamole! The chips and QUESO was hot and fresh which I loved!  This was my first time here and the location was very clean.

The Burger King of the apocalypse. 

I don't like to go around writing negative reviews. And I love hamburgers especially Burger King Whoppers. This place is run down, and the food is not up to par. I don't even think they're chargrilling anymore - my last whopper tasted like it was microwaved. Judging by the looks of the place I bet the employees are treated like shit as well. I hope the owner of this place gets their shit together or sells it to someone who will. Then I'll happily start going again.

Haven't been here in a minute and wanted to see how their chicken fare's in comparison to Popeyes.  

Whelp folks, the verdict is in, and there a clear and distinct winner to me. 

There is no comparison:  Popeyes has clearly obliterated KFC's chicken sandwich.  In fact, KFC ought to reconsider life in the chicken business because that sandwich was a hot mess.  The chicken littles tastes better than this! 

Seems like I paid more for the bun as opposed to the chicken filet.  So, it's a thumbs down for me.  

In terms of this chicken sandwich, it's an affirmative never, ever again, NO!  The service was surprisingly great; hence the 3 stars. 

I'll stick with their pot pie which is hands down amazingly delicious, as well as their original chicken.

Ordered this online and opened it up once home. Pretty pathetic to say the least. Basically soup. No rice (asked for brown rice), 6 beans, three small pieces of sofritas and what seems to be a whole lot of water?? Wouldn't call myself picky but I won't be eating this for dinner.

I live fairly close to this place.  Years ago I went there to get some wings and I was impressed with how grimy it was inside and I remember noticing the crusty stains on the aprons and the hats of the service personnel.  It was very unappealing.  Now, after about 4 months of renovation they have reopened about a month ago so I decided to check it out.

The exterior has new façade and paint.  The interior is sparkly and new.  Clean uniforms on the personnel.  There were many counter people who seemed to be working on orders but the dining room was empty, and they all seemed confused in the execution of their tasks. I ordered the 8 piece mixed bucket, extra crispy.  That's a whole chicken, 2 of each part..... $14.+ !?..... I can get almost identical product at Publix for $8. and change except theirs comes in a box instead of a bucket.  I'm not willing to pay 6 bucks more for a cardboard bucket.

I guess it'll be another 5 years before I go back there again...if ever.

1 1/2 STARS OUT OF 5.  Colonel, get your act together !

KFC in this area is an ok place to go to. They're never going to blow you away with fantastic fried chicken. If anything, Publix Fried Chicken is the one to beat. But, if you're in a pinch and you need to get food and fill up, KFC is an acceptable choice. I decided to get the 12 piece bucket to go this time. It comes with 3 sides and 6 biscuits. Out of everything included with the meal, the biscuits stole the show. Nice and flaky and buttery on the inside, the biscuits are the perfect accompaniment for the meal. The three side items of corn, mashed potato, and green beans are about as basic as you can get with canned and rehydrated vegetables. The fried chicken itself was ok. In regards to size, KFC gives some large chicken pieces, larger than a lot of other places. But quality could be better. I found the batter soggy on a lot of the chicken and incredibly salty. They could do with lowering the sodium levels and cranking up the crunch factor on these fried chicken pieces. Again, acceptable place to go if you need something fast. But if you're looking for quality, you're better off going to Publix.
Cold hard impossible whopper. Must have been sitting for 30 minutes. Vanilla shake horrible.

On the dreary stretch of Rt. 1 (Lincoln Highway) in Fairless Hills, Pa., sits a solitary Colonel Sanders, aka, KFC. 

I have customered it a few times over the years.  Each time the parking lot was nearly empty, and the dining area bereft of fried chicken lovers.  Hitchcockian if pushed for a description, with an orange neon sign on the wall saying it was "Sunday dinner every night."  Yet, the actual meals were good, and the Honey BBQ wings way better than average due in part to their initial preparation as Buffalo Wings, then lathered with the thick sweet tang of the Honey BBQ recipe, giving the wings a hot sweet sallow flavor that can lead to compulsive eating disorders.  The Original Recipe Buckets are also quite good, especially for feeding teenage boys coming together for a party.

Thus, my reason for being there late last week, as I waited for the order to be packed and bagged.  I was surprised when the bucket of 20 pieces and box of 20 wings came in over $40., but nothing is too good for my now teenage son.  

My car smelled of greasy BBQ on the ride home, and it tortured my saliva glands so much that I nearly pulled over to put one of those rusty red chicken wings in my mouth.  I overcame that impulse and made it home in 20 minutes. As soon as I entered the house, my son, his friends, and a couple of parents became transfixed at the aroma, and silently rose to follow me into the dining room.  Shades of Night of the Living Dead. 

The entire batch was devoured in a half hour, and there were sated smiles all around. I am convinced that a chemical analysis of the chicken coating would turn up crack in the mix.  It was that good. 

Have to be careful as this stuff is quickly addictive, but so damn yummy.

HEALTH HAZARD!   I'm sorry to be 'that guy' with the all caps warning and one star but I really need to report this.  I was just at this Carl's Jr. at 10:15pm this evening, 7/16/2016.

While we were waiting for our order, the two cooks in the back were acting unprofessionally which got my attention so I started watching them.  I DIRECTLY OBSERVED the younger of the two HANDLING RAW MEAT THEN HANDLING BUNS AND COOKED MEAT.  I saw him do this a couple times WITHOUT CHANGING HIS GLOVES. He was touching all kinds of things back there. Equipment, bags, raw & cooked food. He was wearing clear plastic gloves, which he did not change after handling raw meat.  He would put the raw burgers into the oven and then walk around to the other side and immediately start building cooked burgers.  After the second time I saw him do this, I demanded a refund.  He heard me complain about him and he stared me down and said "What's your problem?"  I said, "I watched you handle raw meat and cooked food with the same gloves".  He said, "How?  Are you back here?", I replied, "I stood right there on the side and watched you do it!".   I then told him, "You have to care about that stuff!  Health and sanitation is everything in your line of work!".  He just ignored me.  I received my refund but there were no apologies.  Only the cook "huffing up" and challenging me.  I have several other complaints about this location but I have limited space to write a review.  I will say that this place is understaffed and BADLY managed!  Maybe it's better during the day?  Both bad experiences I've had there were later in the evening.  I will be reporting this to the health department.

This is the worst service I have ever had The representative that was working was speaking over the intercom and saying that she cannot do hot wings and so I ordered a number eight and I ended up with chicken tenders that was raw last time I checked you have to pass a certification to be a cook in Arizona there's a huge law as the United States has a law that Santa Mile is a real thing I don't know what kind of management they are having but if they're looking for a new general manager I am willing to go in there and work there for free to literally put everyone back in shape where they need to be respective to the customer and actually put out product that is quality.

Ordered a carnitas burrito ($7.85).  She put literally 3 small cubes of pork in the burrito.  I ask for more meat, she throws one more piece in. Added cheese sauce.  Total $13.15!  The cheese I knew was extra and was ok with it, but they charged me $3 extra for literally ONE additional bite of pork!  And the normal amount of meat is 3 pieces!?  What a joke.  Never again.

Quite possibly the worst service I have ever received at a KFC. I order a 16 piece, all dark meat, original and krispy chicken. The staff informed me there is a wait and I opted to I wait :15 minutes. When the crew member came out, I received the wrong order. I had to go inside to get the correct order. There was too much confusion, chaos, disorganization, and attitude. Not one crew member offered me a drink, a tender, or to reduce my bill because of their mix up. I am really disappointed in the service I received.

My husband loves fried chicken and I hate to cook fried chicken. I had a surprise birthday party this weekend for him on Saturday. So I went to KFC on Cortaro and picked up several buckets of the original and crispy and it was a hit with everyone. We did not eat at KFC, but the chicken was great at the house. I had no idea that it was even close to the house, until I started looking for it. If you love fried chicken this is the place to go.
Elijah at the drive thru is amazing and my impossible whopper was fresh and hot as well as my big fish and fries.

Tears began to flow from her eyes and she ran to the bedside next to my mother. She pressed her forehead against mine and I knew at that moment who she was. She was my wife. This was my child. She gently handed me the baby, my eyes fixated on his innocent face. Tears began to race down my cheeks as I looked into the eyes of a human who I had given life to.

Overall, I'd give the Virtual Comatose Simulation at the hospital a 3/5. They didn't even let me finish my Chipotle steak burrito.

This is a typical White Castle. Greasy food that tastes good. 
The staff is ALWAYS friendly at this location, I've never had a single bad experience customer service wise. Accuracy, is a different story. I always say no cheese on the chicken and waffle slider (which doesn't come with cheese but I always double verify) and it always comes with cheese anyways. That's my only complaint!

This was not only the best KFC I've ever had in the past decade, it was the best fried chicken I've had. Add to that a perfectly warm and flaky biscuit and perfectly done mashed taters and gravy and you have yourself a daggum fine meal. The people complaining are just busted hillbillies who will find a reason to complain about anything. "Nyaaa it took 4 minutes in the drive thru and the food costed its stated price. Nyaaaaaa!"

This is your standard Chipotle Mexican Grill. You know what you're going to get when you're going here. A review is likely not going to change your mind and this one is not intended to.

I will say that I went at 7pm - prime dinner hour. The line went through without an issue. That part was great. However, they were out of quite a few different key ingredients. I had to wait for steak for my friend's burrito. They were out of fajita veggies and hadn't even started cooking them. 

I tried the new vegetarian chorizo per the employee's suggestion. It was very dry. I feel like I need to give it another shot when it is fresh as it was almost inedible.

4 stars because this is likely an isolated incident. You know what to get when you're going to a Chipotle already.

Carl's Jr. is normally one of fav go to burger joints, however this location suffers from slow service as well as stale and cold burgers. The famous star was anything but a star tonight. Thank God, the fries were crispy and tasty. 
I don't recommend
 this location.
My fried chicken has no breading. Did they forget that step. Maybe distracted by the many other proteins on the menu.

My recent trip was only the second time I had ever been to Chipotle.  Before you chase me with flaming torches, it was nowhere to be found where I grew up or went to college!

I like it, the chicken and steak are delicious and of good quality.  Had a bogo card so I had one for lunch the next day.  Had the barbacoa in my first experience a couple of years ago and it was solid...but honestly, the burrito chains all kind of run together for me.  Chipotle is the all-around solid one, Qdoba has awesome queso, Moe's has the salsa bar and is cheap.  I really don't have a preferred destination.  Once again...don't hate me, Chipotle fans.

As everyone else has said, still can't get over the nearly $2 upcharge for guac.  I love guac and I love Chipotle's guac, but I'm not paying 30% of the cost of my burrito to add it.

I was less than happy with this place in the past. It was a bit shabby and slow even producing the most basic menu items. During the bulk of covid they wouldn't let people, even gloved and masked, walk into the lobby requiring you to use the drive through and the drive through was SLOW. Too damn slow to bother with. Something BK has admitted at a corporate level as an overall issue.

I was pretty much resolved 'that's it never again.' Then this weekend they announce it was the anniversary of the Whopper and they would be selling at $0.37, their original price, all weekend.

OK, I thought, the corporation knows they are losing market share and they're willing to make this really great offer.  The world is clawing its way out of the shut down, I'm willing to give them a chance. Maybe it has improved.

uh-no. in fact it's worse. Remember BK was advertising $0.37/whopper all weekend. This was just before noon on a Saturday and they were charging full price. when i asked about it the kid behind the counter mumbled something about 'it's not working' and would only repeat that.

"What" wasn't working? The cash register? nope they took the money. When i challenged him that it was supposed to run all weekend and they couldn't even make it to noon on a Saturday?  "It's not working" and not even a 'sorry.' 

So the place is still shabby, and slow and NOW they are clearly not willing to honor the deals put out by the chain and lack even the common curtesy to say 'sorry.' I tried to report the incident on Burger King's site but they were having error's with their complaints board. Guess they don't care either. 

BK as a business is losing market share badly and not only is it no longer challenging McDonald's, they've fallen behind Wendy's. There's a Wendy's right across the street where the staff is perfectly friendly. Let this BK join the rest of its chain and sink out of sight.

I pulled into KFC at 4:36 pm for dinner. No cars in the parking lot, it was closed! Sign says new temporary hours 10 - 4:30 now tell me why is KFC opening at 10 am in the first place. Chicken and eggs? Crazy. Then they close at 4:30 right before dinner. Why would they do that? I just don't get it. When you have everyone set on KFC then to come home saying they closed at 4:30. Everyone was very disappointed.

Worst Burger King ever! Went there to purchase a double cheeseburger in drive thru. Of course busy as ever, I decided to change the order and get a whopper meal instead, do you know they told me I had to go to the back of the line and place my order again. Are you kidding me. Totally lost my business. Please avoid.

I really hope someone from management sees this. I was there for the last time on October 30th. The line was 45 mins long, which tbh is typical for this chipotle. I refuse to order online again because the last time I ordered online my 3 steak tacos had been absolutely drenched in the hot sauce. Anyways, that situation doesn't have to do with what happened on the 30th. 

My main complaints are that the restaurant itself was filthy, inside and out. The bathroom had toilet paper strung across the floor. The tables were all dirty, inside and out. The floor was gross, and the drink station itself  is why I will never give my business to this chipotle, ever again. There was mold growing all along the cracks of the soda fountain. It was so disgusting. I know that these machines require a lot of maintenance, being that I have worked in the food industry the majority of my career. This was the worst I had seen, even worse than McDonald's. It was so bad I had a hard time eating the $35 worth of food I had just paid for. The whole drink station was an absolute wreck, trash everywhere, everything utensil and napkin wise was out of stock. I wish I had taken pictures but I was in a rush at the time. If you value your health, stay far away from this place.

Great late night food!
Cheap tacos, burritos, burgers, nachos.
I like the taste of Del Taco over Taco Bell any day.
Unfortunately, there are only 2 Del Taco's left in St. Louis.

Overrated Carl's Jr. I don't know why a company that owns both Carl's and Hardee's and uses the same logo are so different, and one is so bad.

there is nothing that would make you think this is a Carl's Jr. other then the star.

Food is mediocre at best, soda is always "off"
Very good Burger King. Clean restaurant with friendly employees.  Something about a Whopper....

I went inside because of the drive through line at 5:10, and left with my food at 5:40. When I got home, the chicken was coming apart and the fries were cold. The cashier did give me a free soda right after I finished ordering, but I couldn't finish my meal. While I've never gotten chicken like this from this location, the temperature of the food has been very inconsistent. I understand that this location may be understaffed, but there should be some level of quality control. This may be the last time I visit that location.

7:30pm on a Sunday night, and as soon as I walk in the guy behind the counter tells me there will be a 15-minute wait for any chicken.

No big deal, I just wanted a pot pie anyway.  He tells me they are all out of pot pies!  No apology.  No "I'm sorry."  He almost seemed happy to disappoint me.

They are open until 9:00pm.  Being short and running out of food an hour and a half before closing time is unfortunate.

It appeared there were just two male teenagers trying to run this location by themselves.  They desperately need an adult at the helm here.

So I ended up just bringing home some of that excellent Publix fried chicken, which was hot and ready to go...

I ordered double portion of chicken and sofritas. When I opened my food I thought my husband ate some of it. It looked like 1/3 of my bowl was gone. So annoying! Then I checked his bowl to see if he just got ours mixed up, he didn't. I paid extra for my portion. It seemed like they just gave me chicken and no rice. Wtf?!

Like what is this?? Did you run outta lettuce? And no cheese?!! I asked for cheese. Where the heck is rice?! I have to dig for rice too! And where is the corn?? Even a chicken will be disappointed by the amount of corn that's in here. This is not worth $10.50. I had to find some shredded cheese in my fridge to make this bowl look more alive.

This review addresses two subjects; the BK App and the new Ch'King Chicken Sandwich. While the first lacks intuitive feel and is frustratingly obtuse, the later is a damned good attempt to compete with all the other clucker on brioche buns coming to fast food franchises around the country. 

The app offers many benefits not available to walk-in/drive-in customers. There are free Whoppers with first Ch'King purchases, discounted sides and drinks as "points" are accumulated, and surprise gifts with purchase on a regular basis. Today, it was vanilla soft-serve in a cone or cup when buying something of $3 or more price. So, there are pleasant upsides if you like the BK menu and can navigate the app. 

Easier said then done.

I found that the special offers didn't always appear on the app from use to use. It wasn't obvious, therefore, which deals were present, and which expired. I found myself repeatedly returning to the opening page as I tried to work my way through the process. I ultimately got the ordering completed with what I wanted, but the time waste was a pain in the butt. If not for my curiosity and persistence, I would have deleted the app and driven to Popeye's instead. 

Yet, it turned out that the new Ch'King standard chicken sandwich was very good (I left the free Whopper for my son to devour). The breast was plump, juicy, well breaded/fried, and all quite tasty. The brioche roll was fresh and held together to the last bite. The special sauce was the flavor of mild mayo,tartar, perhaps a bit of Sriracha, and the pickle slices pleasantly sour and crisp. The Sammy was so thick that my jaws couldn't open wide enough to fit it in for my incisors to clamp down,without squeezing it together and shimmying it back and forth to slowly gain necessary placement between my dents. 

I chose onion rings over fries, and thought they were a decent alternative, although more calories. 

This Ch'king falls just shy of the Popeye's version, but ahead of Chik Fil-A, Wendy's and McDonald's iterations in terms of taste, texture, composition and fun. Too, it is competitive with the smaller chicken shops like Lovebird, at a better price. For someone who prefers chicken to burgers as a protein source, this is clearly a reasonable and satisfying way to go.

1st time ever posting......
Terrible!  just stopped by for takeout for my family.  I ordered 3 chicken pot pies..... they said all out of chicken pot pies.  Sad thing is.... I expected that they would be out because they never have them soooooo
I had a back up plan.  Before I could order chicken strips ( my back up plan) They said and we have no chicken strips.....it was 6:30pm on a Friday!  Do they just not expect people to order dinner on a Friday night???
Ok- this is KFC-  really no chicken strips????

Well, to Andy G. visiting.  Hats off to you for an honest review.  -but one thing you have to understand is that it's an aquired taste!  Everyone here has known White Castle since they were young. (if they're older)  Well granted, many people have never tried and many people don't like.  -but for some people that were dirt poor in college, or in the 60''s & needed a fill up, know ramen noodles and white castle sandwiches.  (now it's burritos as big as your head).  My brother (65yr old native Hoosier) flys in from California, and demands a stop at the white castle on the way to my home.  Then hits it a couple of times in his 2wk visit.  You have to know -- IT IS THE ORIGINAL SLIDER.
This is all in fun you know, and Hats Off to you for bringing the truth to Indiana!

I have mixed feelings about Dunkin Donuts. 

The pros:
Their coffee is good enough, their donuts are good enough, and their service is quick even when they're mobbed. It's like watching a well oiled machine in action. Fast food restaurants could learn a lot from this. 

The cons:
The breakfast sandwiches are just unforgivably awful. The bacon is sad and floppy. The sausage is tough and weird. The big n toasted sandwich is not "big" or "toasted" at all. The undersized slices of bread were soft and completely saturated with drippy grease. It was nearly inedible not only due to the revolting flavor, but also due to the difficulties of holding on to an overly lubricated sandwich that repeatedly tried to disassemble itself. It was as if the egg patty was trying to free itself from its horrid cellmates.

I really miss the Wawa down the street. Overall I'd still visit DD but only for their specialties. The food is just plain bad.

Really disappointed in the salad with steak. Every bite of steak was grisly.... On top of that the gal making my salad popped a piece of steak in her mouth coming back from the cutting station. How disgusting is that? $14 - not worth it. Won't be back

I consider myself a burrito snob but I try to avoid chains whenever possible because I like to support local businesses. My love of all things burrito helps me overlook the fact that Chipotle is a chain.

I won't attest anything here is the best in the area but there are a lot of items that are really good. For me the best thing on the menu are the pork carnitas. If you are counting calories, try a carnitas bowl with rice, black beans, 86 the cheese, and triple up on mild salsa. It is borderline too much to eat for $7 and the whole thing will clock in at under 600 calories. If you want to go the vegetarian route swap out the pork for guacamole and you net the same result.  This is my staple at least once a week for lunch.

Unless you are pulling a man vs. food you might want to skip the chips. The burritos are truly massive mission style and I don't how a single person could eat both the chips and a burrito unless it was for fame or fortune.

I don't know what happened to this place. This was the worst experience I've ever had there.  Went there Friday afternoon 2/19....I ordered the crispy chix sandwich combo, the one on he "fresh bun"...fries and drink....he ordered the Original Star with fried zucchini and drink. 

Even tho this is fast food, and was in the drive through, I never minded waiting because it was made fresh to order every other time I've ever been to this location. 

What we got today...my fried chix was cold, bun looked like it was stepped on, literally the end rind of iceberg lettuce, fries were cold and soggy as if sitting there for a long time. 

His burger....burger? Where's the beef?! He had an onion slice, one slice but it was 2" thick and he also had the lettuce rind on his too. Very sad.

The ONLY thing edible, hot and cooked right were the zucchini. It's too bad. 

I get that it's fast food....but no matter what your job is...take pride in it.

I would do negative stars if I could. My husband, myself and brother in law ate from this location last night (ordered steak bowls) and all ended up with severe, awful food poisoning. Avoid this location!!

I tried to order the chicken sandwich and asked if they could add pickle and the lady was rude and told me they could not add pickle to my sandwich but I could get the chicken little sandwiches bc it comes with pickle and mayo. I asked why can't they just add pickle to the chicken sandwich bc it's basically the same thing and she said "bc it don't come with pickle". I just laughed at the laziness and Ignorance and said ok well give me the chicken little sandwiches. Never in my life have I been told they can't add pickle to a chicken sandwich bc it doesn't come with pickle. This KFC is the worst one I've been to.
I don't have much to go off of. The service was slow and they were out of steak...so we left. Bye. Hopefully they get their act together soon and I can adjust this from "terrible" to "not terrible".

This Chipotle location has kinks to work out for sure.  I've gotten stale tortillas on more than one occasion, veggies that were cold and anemic looking, and sofritas that looked like they've been sitting in that bin since yesterday's lunch.  I've passed up on guacamole that was almost completely brown on top.  Why aren't they paying attention to how their food looks on the line and properly taking care of it?  Oh, I know, cause the employees are always screwing around.  On our last visit there, one employee kept screaming so loud that I couldn't hear the music over him and other customers were giving him dirty looks.  

But, on the plus side, they always give me a big portion of everything, and when I ask for more or less of something, they gladly oblige.  The drinking fountain and seating area are also usually clean as well.

I really hope this location gets itself together.  Usually they do when they start off rough, so I have high hopes for them still.

This is a priceless treasure in Delco, said no one ever.

Absolutely the worst run KFC that I have ever been to, and I have been to a lot of stores in this chain. I mean what kind of KFC runs out of chicken in the afternoon and stays open! Seriously! Do you know of someone who comes to KFC not for chicken! Slow service here for whatever reason, and be sure to double check your order when you finally get it, especially if you're in the drive-thru. Definitely a busy location, but I really don't know why. Maybe sometimes people will put up with a lot of nonsense for some chicken--that is, if this particular location doesn't run out.

Convenient location, generally friendly staff, lots of seating. But they frequently undercut scoop size here; more than a few of my burritos have felt underwhelming relative to price for the amount of meat included. Typically more common when ordering carnitas as they use tongs. It's also strange as guacamole is always piled on no matter what.

Just something to keep in mind.
Hands down. BEST Del Taco I've ever been to!!! EXCELLENT customer service!!! Clean place and the sauce is at the table in a neat arrangement!? IMPRESSED

I show up thirty minutes before closing; they're out of steak, barbacoa, guac, and black beans.  Not like the store is out completely; they are just halfway through closing process already and don't want to make more.

I am definately not a fast food fan. Occasionally, about twice a year, I want a whopper or find myself in a position of ending up at Burger King.
I am not a fan of there fries, they just aren't cooked properly. The original chicken sandwich, I am skeptical of as its like reformed parts of chicken & fried. The question is what parts of a chicken & is there a mystery ingredient?
Overall, I admit I am a skeptic, through & through when it comes to fast food. The thought of the actual ingredients, scare the heck out of me. Fries & ice cream I feel are the safest choices, but they just don't even taste good at this chain.
What's a girl to do? Try to stay away from most all fast food restaurants, with very few exceptions.

It's a train wreck of a meal that you just can't keep yourself from hankering from time to time. Give in, get that Triple Whopper, and remind yourself why you don't go there. 

It gets three stars because the only thing sadder than eating at Burger King is complaining about eating at Burger King.

The only redeeming thing about this place is the coffee. It's one of only a handful of restaurants near my office so I've been many times. The staff are as friendly as any other fast food place but the service moves on a geologic time scale - even if there are only one or two cars there. I have literally waited entire lunch breaks in the drive thru leaving me with five minutes to rush back to the office and eat at my desk.....only to open the bag and find the wrong order MULTIPLE times. Sausage croissants turn into bacon bagels, chicken and Tuna salad are interchangeable. They should just replace the menu with a spinning wheel. The milkshakes are all milk and no shake but they get the flavor right so I guess that's a plus. I'd love to say I'd never go back but they're in a location with few other choices.

If you eat here your too poor to eat at Taco Bell or Taco Bell is closed and your desperate. This is absolutely the worst quality and worst tasting food you can have in the fast food industry.... Even with the low prices it's still far from anything that can be considered a value matter of fact how is this dump even in business still?????

All of my late-night Del Taco excursions have been well worth the penalty of having fast food.  However this Del Taco is impressive.  They allow you to be finicky at 1 AM - I didn't want tomatoes on my 'big fat chicken taco', and not only did they remember that I didn't want tomatoes,  they labeled it appropriately, and then *told* me what the label meant.   Who does that these days?  Awesome.

I know it's new location but you'd think they would have better quality control. Been there a few times and it was fine. Customer service needs work. No one says anything to you while you stand there waiting to be served. Staff seems to ignore you, not wanting to be the one handling the customers until someone caves to serve you. Typically the food is fine until this afternoon I went in. The steak was burnt/charred & the queso was thick and clumpy, probably old. They literally put a small handful of lettuce on top of my bowl and when I asked if she didn't mind adding more, it was still another skimpy amount. For $~10 I'll go elsewhere if I can't even get some extra lettuce.

Since I have to rate this place, 1 star it is but it really doesn't deserve any.

There's nothing quite like walking into a place and having the stench of the bathroom hit you square in the face.  And not the pleasant, flowery smell that places use.  This is the 'rest stop in the middle of nowhere in the middle of summer in Arizona' kind of smell.

The staff informed me that there is no AC in the bathrooms because there are no vents.  Huh?  How does that even make sense?  As I said in my email to Carl's Jr corporate, "How is that even LEGAL?" (Let's see if they read their fan letters.)

The food was mediocre at best.  The fries tasted like they'd been re-fried to warm them up, the bun was stale and the soda station was nasty.  No re-fills for us. 

And no return visit for us either. Trust me. STAY AWAY!  I'll update my review if/when I hear back from the management team.

I remember going here when i was younger because my mom is obsessed with elvis and this burger king is a 50s style diner and hes everywhere. it seemed kinda cool back then but now not so much. plus it was kinda dirty but the food was ok cant go wrong on whopper mondays.

Chiptole is delicious, quick, and healthy.  Not a game changer by any means, but I eat here 3-4 times a week for lunch. I love the carnitas burrito bowls. Location is convenient and staff is usually friendly.

For a fast food place, this is one of my favorites. I generally visit during the lunch hours (11 am - 2 pm). No matter how busy they get the staff seems to be having a good time and are always pleasant to deal with. They seem to get along well with each other communicating the orders and on the rare occasion there is an issue with an order, they are quick to fix it and offer a sincere apology.
The drive through line can seem a bit long, but considering the amount of space (really only room for 3 cars), it keeps flowing quickly.
The food tastes fresh made, and is typical for the venue with good flavor. My go to items are the deluxe chili cheese fries and 1/2 lb bean and cheese burritos, with the occasional grilled chicken taco. I have had the deep fried cheesecake and it is pretty good, but a bit rich, so I do not order it too often.
I like that I can get a taco and fries in the same place, and like it even more that it comes with an honest smile.

oh, be sure to sign up for their emails to get the occasional free food voucher. :)

I made the very poor choice of stopping here for some Taco Bell late.  Just a pathetically run store.  

I went inside because the driveup window was quite crowded.  Figured it would be cheaper to go inside than wait in the driveup.  Wrong.  Even though there was only 1 person in front of me, it took about 10 minutes to get my food.    

Ordered T6 combo but when I got home I found instead of getting chicken I got beef & and instead of getting a soft taco I got a hard taco.  They did get the diet soda correct.  1/4.  Stellar.

The worst part though by far is the horrible food poisoning I got from the beef there last night.  Shaking, terrible stomach cramps, etc.  Just an awful experience.  

Guess I learned my lesson & that I should not eat ground beef at fast food places nor go to a Taco Bell in general.  Good choice for WCU college students who are drunk/high but a place that I will avoid like the plague.  

Thanks Taco Bell for reaffirming my decision to give up ground beef & fast food again.

I was glad to finally see a Chipotle open south of 86th/82nd and downtown.  This location is fantastic and the food is the same high quality Chipotle you get everywhere else.  The store is designed to more suit the solo lunch-time diner, not as many group tables as other locations. Bonus points to this location for 1) giving us free food during a soft opening to train its staff, a stroke of luck on our part 2) offering my first taste of Chipotle's new tofu sofritas....bummer that you now have a guacamole upcharge for your sofritas vegetarian burrito, but worth the cost. My recommendation: order the sofritas burrito bowl, stir up all the ingredients, and then use it as tasty dip for your tortilla chips.
The new boneless chicken at the Exton location was juicy and delicious. Stopped here to order and the same order was disgusting! Oh, and the customer service was horrible....even rude!! :(

I've been a fan of Chipotle for many years, but if this particular location were all one had to go on to base an opinion, I doubt they would last.

Service is slow, even by Middle Tennessee standards, which means it is PAINFULLY slow. The last time I was in there (a few months ago), the line moved so slowly that my prepared dish was actually room temperature by the time I got it rung up and to a table. They can also be inattentive. That day, I ordered what I usually order...a bowl with white rice, pinto beans, and carnitas...but then the young woman asked what kind of rice I wanted. So I answered "white rice, pinto beans, and carnitas". 
"What kind of beans would you like?" 
"Pintos, please" 
"And for the meat? 
"Carnitas" but she scooped up a ladle of something else and plopped it over the rice. 
"Umm...what is that?" I asked. 
"Sofritas. Would you like anything else on this?" 
"On that, nothing, because I ordered a bowl with white rice, pinto beans, and carnitas." 
"You didn't want sofritas?" 
"Nooooo. That's why I ordered a bowl with white rice. Pinto beans. Carnitas."  

Can anyone tell me how many times I uttered the words "White rice, pinto beans, and carnitas"? before it actually got through to her?

They were out of two salsa options, as well as the guacamole. Plus, as previously mentioned, by the time I got rung up and to a seat, the food was cold.

I complained to corporate and they sent me some vouchers for free food. I stopped in the other day to get one of my freebies. There was a smallish line, about half of which had made the turn and were waiting to be rung up. And waiting. And waiting. After a time someone called out "Gabriel! The line's waiting on you!". Eventually a young man sauntered up to the register, and began ringing up an order. By that time, I had been in line for ten minutes, the line had grown to reach the back wall, and the people who had ordered had still not received their food, so I excused myself and hit the exit.

Emailed corporate. Profuse apologies and the promise of more freebies. 

Avoid this location at all costs.

Update: Evidently, they have made some major staffing changes, possibly even managerial change..Lines can still be quite long, but it seems to move rather more quickly. Depending on who is working the line, portions can be a bit skimpy.

For the past four times my wife, daughters and I have gone to this place, the lack of attention to detail has gotten worse. We will not be returning unless the crew is replaced with people who actually give a damn. Here's a clue... Not all white people are stupid and some of us speak and understand fluent Spanish! The tortillas are always stale, fries are always brown, and even when my daughters ask for cheese quesadillas without green sauce, miraculously there's always extra green sauce. Gee. I wonder why. Order a steak and potato epic burrito without sour cream? Expect extra sour cream because apparently nobody in this establishment understands basic instructions. No surprise there. Will spread the word about this del taco and will never return to this location! I will gladly drive clear down to Damonte ranch del taco for food that was made properly.  HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE. DO NOT EAT HERE.

This chipotle was better than others I've eaten at in the area...but I ordered sofritas (tofu) on my bowl and got a piece if chicken on it! I'm a vegetarian so that's not cool. I think they need to slow down about and not slack off on standards.

I was pleasantly surprised with this White Castle. The service was very friendly and super fast. The food was typical White Castle, I did try their ghost pepper slider and it is spicy. It is not like the typical spicy fast food offerings. You have been warned.
I took my food to go; however, the dining room was clean and updated.
This man asked me if I liked his chicken. Yes I do sir. Thank you for your enthusiasm word up
There food is amazing every time I go. It's a great chain restaurant that has gluten free, vegan and vegatarian options. The sofritas is a vegan "meat" option and has amazing flavor.

This Chipotle has been our go-to place when I've been too tired or lazy to cook dinner. Love the simple menu and every time we've gone, they have been friendly and fast. We're used to California/ mission style super burrito, so we loved that we could add veggies, sour cream, guacamole, cheese, etc. to our burritos, and customize how we like it. The portion is great... I usually finish 3/4's of it, then the bf polishes off the rest.

The only thing is that they charge for chips, which I'm not accustomed to... usually where I'm from chips come with it the burrito, but it seems that that's how it is in Tucson. 

My go to burrito-  I usually get the barbacoa (shredded braised beef) or steak burrito with half the amount of rice and pinto beans, mild and corn salsa, light sour cream, cheese and lettuce. Yum!

Oh yea, one more bonus... sometimes they do a BOGO day if you "like" them on facebook.

I'm just going to start this review of by saying who doesn't love chipotle? 

I use to always go with the burritos, but I recently turned to their crunchy tacos w/ barbacoa, sour cream and their hot sauce. While my lady goes with the soft tacos and she goes between the steak and barbacoa w/ guacamole and their mild sauce and sour cream... And can't forgot about their magically delicious chips and hot salsa on the side!! 

Enjoy

Along with most of the stores in the mall it had terrible service.  Again most of the stores not all of them.  But any who I love Chipotle and could eat it everyday. Love, the chicken and steak and get it everytime in a bowl and some times a burrito. However this place is just something else.  We ordered two bowls, one chicken and one steak. The girl literally gave us MAYBBBEEE 15 pieces of small chicken and steak for the both of us. I say small because they're usually in chunks. Anywho it was that little where i could count how many pieces their were.  I know chipotle has a 4oz rule for their meat but really? There was not even close to 4oz of meat.  I also got fajitas, beans, rice, cheese, lettuce, and salsa on top and AGAIN she gave like a half scoop of everything and yes I asked if i could have more and extra this and that.  By the end of the order my bowl was less then half full and i stoped asking for more because the girl seemed to be getting annoyed.  I'm used to chipotle employees packing my bowls when i unlid them and giving good service.  But tiny portion is not the only reason why i give this spot a one star. Once i started eating my steak bowl the steak didn't even have that chipotle spice that i love.  It just tasted like beef with no seasoning.  A bit disappointed in this location along with a few other stores in the mall.

Even with a number of food allergies, i can still count on this place as my go-to spot! The carnitas is my fave and with either salad or rice bowl, it's always a filling meal. Also, the location on West End has beer for sale to enjoy with your eat-in order!

I am not a West Coastie. I should start off with this to be fair. So I have heard stories of Del Taco and how it is vastly superior to Taco Bell.

I had to find out. I was pleasantly surprised. Other than a lady who was sitting in the corner booth talking to herself (at some point... I think she took her panties off in the bathroom), the atmosphere was what you expect of a fast food joint-although the parking kind of sucks. I think this location is more of a drive through oriented restaurant.

I preferred the Del Taco meal deal-$4 for a large taco, big bean burrito, fries and a drink. Crazy cheap... The taco was stuffed with to the brim with cheese, like seriously amazing fresh ground stuff-not what I expected at all. The bean burrito had actual refried beans, with whole beans in it-and a ton of cheddar too. I tried one fry-not bad at all. I should have stuffed my burrito.

I looked at the menu and saw a "The Del Taco Turkey" and curiosity and a bottomless stomach led me to buy it too. I sauced it up (the Hot is actually spicy-unlike even the weak Diablo at Taco Bell) and took a gigantic bite to a very flavorful, also cheesy, taco. Honestly, the ground beef and ground turkey don't have a huge flavor difference but I like the texture of the turkey more.

Overall... Del Taco isn't even in the same category as Taco Bell. No competition it's waaaaaay better.

Stopped in to eat after my son's baseball game.   Not sure what happened....my son clearly ordered the kids popcorn chicken meal, with a side of gravy and potato wedges instead of mashed potatoes, soda instead of caprisun.   The order taker leaves the cash register to go and get the caprisun.   The gal specifically asked if he wanted soda or caprisun and he said soda.  She insisted he said caprisun.   I ordered a popcorn chicken meal, with potato wedges and gravy and I get mashed potatoes and an extra bowl of gravy.  I took the mashed potatoes back to the counter and advised that there was a mistake, the staff continued to be confused.  I've ordered the same thing for years at many KFC restaurants and never had this problem.  BTW....we are only family there at 8:00 at night.  As we left, another family had come in, ordered, received their food and appeared to be confused comparing their receipt to what they were served.  As we walked out the door, the father of the family was headed to the cash register with receipt in hand.   It is not rocket science...it's taking a simple order of fast food.
Little over $9 gets a huge, tasty burrito (without guacamole) and a drink.  I not a vegetarian, but I love sofritas(seasoned tofu) as my "meat".

This Chipotle opened its doors April 1, 2017! Welcome to the neighborhood! We love Chipotle and are happy there is one so close. They serve sofritos, a nicely spiced tofu. You can grab a hefty side of this for $2.15, but add mild salsa and lettuce and it jumps to $6.50. The employees of this location are fabulous and work well together. I am looking forward to eating here in the future.

One of the slower 'fast food' options on the Northside. Still, the burgers are terrific.  I introduced my granddaughter to the iconic 'slider' this week. She did ask, "are these onions? I don't like onions". I fibbed, albeit with accuracy, "They started out as onions, but have been turned into tiny flavor bits."  She ate the whole thing with gusto.  Fries are just okay.

BBQ Bacon Whopper gives me a baseline price on the Livingsocial PYT punch card special. For the sandwich only, same price before tip and no wait. Burger King wins. Now about the deferred maintenance, the place is falling apart. Think trailer trash decor.

Everything in my food was made incorrectly I ordered a large received a medium french fry both fries with no salt only one of them was supposed to be no salt my double whopper with cheese for some godly reason had the cheese in the middle and all the extra toppings I ordered were on top the whole sandwich turn into a slop fest because of the moron who could not build the sandwich correctly
Gave this a 5 star due to the 2 times we have been here the food and service was awesome. For fast food you can not beat it. Thanks staff!!

Was told guac is free if you get a veggie bowl. I said well what about the sofritas? Again, told since it is vegetarian it is included. I decided to try it. Got to checkout and they said "well she didn't know what she is talking about...you can't go by what that girl says since she doesn't work the cash register". They offered to make me a new bowl without the guac, if I didn't want to pay for it. Just really poor customer service. They weren't willing to go the extra mile. Disappointing when there are so many other choices so close. Won't be back.

I only stopped in this KFC to grab a few pieces of the yard bird to supplement some red beans & brown rice, seasoned with deer sausage waiting at home.  I used to frequent this place more often when they had a really nice and inexpensive dinner buffet.  Now, the buffet is only available as a lunch menu.  In fact, the menu seems to be in constant flux.  My favorite dessert item, the lemon parfait is no longer available.  The only dessert options were a chocolate chip cookie or devil's food cake.

I'm an absolute breast man and I've been trying to do the right thing nutritionally and passing on the battered fried chicken, and going for the grilled chicken instead.  Well, I'm not the only one at home, so I ordered an 8 piece mixed bucket, including 6 of the Colonel's original fried assorted pieces of chicken and 2 grilled chicken breasts.  I had to wait ten or twelve minutes for the grilled chicken to be prepared.

As good as the chicken was, the red beans & brown rice, seasoned with deer sausage was even better.  Consequently, Bear, my resident Rottweiler enjoyed a pet gourmet meal.  Even after a month since I visited KFC, Bear saves some of his evening meal until he's certain I don't have any leftover KFC for him.

My least favorite Chipotle location, seriously, we have given them several 2nd chances and it always sucks, and the employees act like they would rather be absolutely anywhere else....slower than drunk turtles going backwards through molasses...new to the area and haven't found "our chipotle" yet...despite giving this location many tries.


The tortillas are usually warm and moist ...sometimes, and the staff is majority of the time, grumpy grumperkins... or severely understaffed while others are in the back of the kitchen elbow-deep in raw chicken... or a girl slowly stocking the straws that are already full... please help the long line of people ordering, that poor guy doing it alone is STRUGLING!

If you're a Chipotle virgin, it's easy to catch on, and a new take on "fast food".  You approach the starting point and decide if you want your goodness wrapped up in a warm tortilla (or in some beefy cases double wrapped), or if you want to go with the burrito bowl option, or 3 tiny tacos.  I don't know much for the taco choice, I have seen others in my party make that choice, but it just seems like you get way less food, and meh, not my style.  I usually get a burrito bowl, with a to-go lid option incase I can't finish the whole thing in the restaurant...which is the majority of the time, as the portions are massive...massively delicious!  My husband always gets a double wrapped tortilla, because it is usually a huge burrito baby.  

After choosing your food holder, you get your pick of:
-Rice: white or brown ,
-Beans: Pinto or Black
-Grilled Veggie mix of onions + peppers sauteed together (can't get them separate, unless you ask for them to tediously pick out which pieces you do/don't want...but I've never seen that done... I would like to have just the onions, as my tummy is sensitive to peppers, so I wish they were separated, but they aren't so i avoid this step altogether because usually it is hella busy & basically always a steady stream of hungry hungry customers, so speed is essential as patron and burrito crafter)
-Meat: Spicy Barbacoa (steak), Medium-Spicy Chicken, and pulled apart pork (least spicy), and maybe another option, not sure, and they may or may not have/are introducing a tofu option?  But for vegetarian options, you can choose to go meatless, and get guacamole at NO EXTRA CHARGE, when if you DO get meat, adding guac is like 1.80 extra... I think... Not exactly sure, as the hubbs & I have been trying to severely minimize our animal products so we always get guac in place of a meat, and don't get charged extra for it anymore.
-Salsa choices...spicy, and medium? Not sure, I don't get the liquidy salsa, I just move on to the tomato mixture, and the corn mix...
-Sour Cream (when I used to get it, before going Vegan-ish, I would get the sour cream and it would always be... kind of rauncy, and tummy pain, it was tangy and not good, sketchy...
-Cheese: shredded
-Chopped Romaine lettuce
-and guac... FOR EXTRA! They will always ask you, "the guacamole is extra, is that ok?" **Unless you did NOT get any meat, then guac is NOT extra**

Then you're done assembling your mexican grill creation before your very eyes!  You can then choose the ever popular Chips + Guac side dish, and they have a fridge behind the cashier with a variety of juices, Horizon Milk boxes, Coronas, and possibly margaritas, but I think that's new...and the different variety of soda dispension and lemonade.

There is some seating inside, and some outside, if you can bear the weather...

I'll drive to a further Dunkin to avoid going to this location. Orders are wrong most of the time. Ordered coffee with sweet and low and was given packets to put in the coffee myself instead of them stirring it in. Once ordered a donut and an egg sandwich, was given a bag and I asked him if the donut was in with the sandwich and he said yes. Of course I find it wasn't in the bag when I got home but I was still charged for it. Once ordered a sausage egg and cheese on bagel, they completely forgot the egg. Drive to the Dunkin on Frankford and Knights, their service is excellent.

Super excited they opened a Chipotle restaurant close by. I love their chicken bowls and my husband lovers their carnitas. Chipotle is also great to order for work lunches. I love that they put your name on each dish, super easy to pass out. All their food is always fresh nothing frozen. Love it!

I came here with a coupon and ate in. 

The coupon was for 2 Whopper meals with onion rings instead of fries (including drinks) for $6.99. The Whopper is ok. It tastes the same as all the other locations. The onion rings were better than the Whopper. I think it's because it took a while for them to make the onion rings. It seems like most people order fries instead. 

The cashier was nice but my problem with her was that after dealing with money, she dealt with food without washing her hands. Money is dirty. Money is being exchanged CONSTANTLY and there's a bunch of germs on it. I saw her try to pack the onion rings into the container/cup using her hands. I've had this problem with other locations (a guy using a broom/cleaning and then touch bread sticks to see if they're done cooking). 

Interior was fine. I remember when it was just Burger King and McDonald's and no other food place. Burger King definitely updated their look at this location. 

To Burger King: teach your staff to wash their hands!

Gross! Too salty, fatty gross carnitas, dried out steak  and they are mean- wouldnt give my daughter any lettuce for her kids tacos.  I feel robbed, wish I had read other reviews. I may never go to a chipotle again this was SO BAD

You don't expect to get great service at a place that serves food for 99¢ and where the workers make only minimum wage, but the service here surprised me.  The 18-year-old cashier (I heard her mentioning her age to a drive-through customer) was the epitome of happy, friendly, and helpful.  She apologized to me for the wait while I was waiting, even though I hadn't been waiting very long.  When a customer returned to the counter with an order that was slightly wrong, she was apologetic and promised they'd make it right for him.  I kept thinking that if I had a retail business, I would try to hire her away from Del Taco.

What makes this BK so special?  It is apparently the only place open for dinner after 10pm in Boise!  During my epic cross country trip, http://KananAcrossAmerica.blogspot.com, I got to Boise a little bit late at night and I was starving.  Lucky for me, Burger King was open, and it was good, Double Whopper with Bacon, Cheese, and Jalapeños FTW!

Also, this location has a TV, which is great!

i visited this location after be warned by a coworker about the recent headlines about Chipotle.  I wish i would have listened. The food was worse than worse..i ordered half steak half chicken bowl not a lot of either in the bowl onion was black..I wasnt even able to work the remainder of my shift because i couldn't stay out of the restroom!!!! As gross as it may sound or is it's the truth.I WILL NEVER RETURN TO ANY LOCATION AGAIN. It would be great to get my money back but if all i had to spend was 10.00 to be reminded to never eat here again..Well then it was money well spent..I also am a cook in the restaurant business and could not imagine serving anything to my guest such as this..#1 rule dont serve anything you wouldn't eat yourself and i guaran-damn-tee the ones who work there dont eat off the serving line you feed the customers off of...#undercoverboss #YOURCOMPANYISINTROUBLE

Average service and poor food make this place low on my list of places to visit! If however you are low on funds their "value" menu is large and doesn't seem to go up in price! I recommend that you eat in so that you can make sure your order is right and if you like chicken I highly suggest their chicken soft tacos! Other than that I don't recommend this be a date restaurant or even for family dinner! A quick lunch should be the reason you eat here lol! But I will day that the food taste is there and their hot sauce truly is! Most places say hot sauce and im like you're joking right? Not here, I think they call it scorcho or something like that but man is it good and does have that little kick! I've never seen French fries and burgers at a taco restaurant but my fiance said it wasn't bad and I had the fries and they weren't gross!

I had an oil change at Walmart next door and was quoted an hour and a half wait. I had my two children (both 2 years old) with me. I saw Burker King's play place and thought, oh, cool I'll take them there to kill time. Yeah, well... 

We get there and there's one cashier taking orders for the guy ahead of me and what looked like 2 or 3 people standing around waiting on food. I go to order a grilled chicken sandwich and he puts it in. Some guy who looks like he's the supervisor--based on how he was dressed and his demeanor, came over to tell the cashier something. He never spoke directly to me which I found either him being 1) sexist, 2) oblivious (not possible), or 3) just rude. 

He told the cashier "no, the grill items are all frozen." The cashier, probably not thinking how I'd find his response a little odd (for the home of the "flame-grilled" Whopper) says, "Can't we just microwave it and then throw it on the grill like we always do?" Okay, eww, but the supervisor guy then tells him, "We can, but it won't be good." I scrapped my order and just got my kids a burger and small fries to split, along with a drink for myself. 

We sat down and more people poured in. I noticed the place was pretty grimy and needed to be remodeled about 20 years ago. I sat us down and noticed a sign on the play land doors. They were closed until further notice. Great. So, we waited and waited and I saw other people getting their food before us. Then, a guy who'd been waiting when I showed up noticed it too. I heard him arguing with the supervisor guy.

The customer was asking why it was taking so long as he only had a 30-minute lunch break and had been there close to (if not longer) 20-minutes already. The supervisor didn't seem bothered by this and gave the guy his money back, but I didn't hear so much as a "sorry about that." This guy was obviously having a bad day or maybe shouldn't be running things.
