The cashier was trying hard to be helpful and seemed to have been abandoned by the guy in charge. He made rounds in the dining area asking if people (us included) were waiting on an order. He went and got the burger and fries for my kid and apologized for the wait, which by now was 25-minutes. 

This place should probably be remodeled entirely or shut down. It's almost embarrassing for them to be directly across from McDonald's which I noticed from my daily drives has been remodeled. I saw one guy come in asking for breakfast and was directed to McDonald's and left. Sad.

they opened a chipotle right down the road from where i live! this is dangerous.

i love the carnitas ^_^ they are absolutely my favorite.

this location is on the small side, but it'll do for the volume they'll get. very clean location. fast and efficient workers who are friendly, too!

Stopped in and decided to eat in since I had the time. I had the whopper junior and fries and unsweet tea. The fries were the best I have ever had at any fast food place. The fries were cooked perfectly crunchy and very tasty with the perfect amount of salt! My whooper junior was cooked perfectly hot burger and cold lettuce and tomato. I had to wait a little while but worth the wait because it was cooked right. Keep up the good work gang!! You are settling in now and showing all Burger Kings how it's done.

Just as @Pyra-Danny V. said, generally I would think there is not much point in reviewing a national chain restaurant such as Dunkin' Donuts. However, today I tried one of their DDSmart menu items, so I thought someone might be interested in what their quasi-healthy menu items are like.

I got the DDSmart Egg White Turkey Sausage Flatbread Sandwich. When I first opened it I thought that they had accidentally given me the veggie flatbread sandwich, but if you look closely you will see bits of sausage in the egg white patty, which is also liberally flecked with something green...I'm thinking spinach. The sandwich also has cheddar cheese.

I actually couldn't find this item on the DD nutritional information website, but I did find nutritionals for the item here: http://www.fatsecret.com/calories-nutrition/dunkin-donuts/ddsmart-egg-white-turkey-sausage-flatbread-sandwich . At 280 calories, I'd say that this is a good-sized breakfast sandwich and is pretty tasty. The flatbread seemed soft and fresh and the cheese and egg white/sausage patty were pretty good. My husband got the same thing and in his words:  this breakfast sandwich is better than what you would get at McDonald's, but not as good as what you can get at Subway.

If I were rating DD on their coffee, however, I would give it five stars. DD is definitely my favorite coffee.
Del taco is a good value and this one doesn't skimp on the food.However these people move excruciatingly slow!!  It's like move faster!!

This is the worst Chipotle experience I've ever had. After pulling a plastic lid from the beans like a grouse magic show the lady making my burrito proceeded to make the worst proportioned burrito with a tortilla that was over warmed to the point where it broke apart on contact. But it didn't stop there, the Chipotle lady told my wife that sofritas didn't have soy. This is amazing to me that the person making our food didn't even know what one of the main proteins were. I could only imagine if someone had a soy allergy. They also forgot my wife's dressing. Shame.

I eat Chipotle all the time, however, this location is not my favorite. I am very skeptical to eat Chipotle ever again due to getting sick after eating a Bowl. It was an hour before closing and I got my usual, brown rice, black beans, chicken, guacamole, lettuce, and cheese. The meat was not fresh, it was sitting out there for a while and was very dry but the employees didn't seem to care to give my friend and I a new batch. I'm not sure if the chicken was bad or some kind of cross-contamination occurred, but I was throwing up, felt awful and had the cold sweats. I asked my friend if he was feeling alright, he was sick too after eating Chipotle with Chicken and Steak. One Star.

I ordered sofritas, they gave me chorizo by accident. I AM ALLERGIC TO MUSTARD SEED AND PORK. I always felt safe with Chipotle but now I just feel very upset and personally violated. I ordered to go, and my food was topped with guac and an obscene amount of lettuce so I wasn't able to double check before eating and trusted Chipotle to do their part. Part of me wonders if they were aware of their mix up and just tried to cover it with the lettuce. You dropped the ball Chipotle and now I'm paying for it!

I drove past 3 other taco places to get here, and thinking to myself I sure hope the food is good I'm driving past several other fast food joints that serves tacos!
Guess what? It was worth it, around 1pm on a Saturday afternoon I  ordered two regular tacos 1 Macho Taco and a Mr Pib.It was very tasty however in my opinion the macho taco is the mother-ship... the regular taco was fine, But the Macho Taco hit all the bells!!Now fast forward to 10pm at night, I'm driving back to the same place,this time 2 Macho Tacos and 1 order of crinkle cut fries fries were not right out of the fryer however they were still good. And another Mr. Pib yesss.The food is great here and the ground beef actually taste good and a lighter seasoning not overpowering like Taco Bell, and the shells light and crispy mmm good. Ice cold sour cream very fresh. Now hear me good in closing I'm at a Casino In Reno driving back to the same restaurant that I ate at earlier in the day, and it's after 10 @ nite! You decide, I've already decided for myself!

Zero stars would be my choice, this place is beyond horrific.  It took a total of 20 minutes to go through the drive thru. The car in front of me was trying to order simple chicken combos, they of course were out of most things and from what I could hear had refused to make it offering only choices of what was left.  I ordered a 6 piece chicken bite.... Should be simple. No, it took the them what felt like forever to actually get them once I got to he window. The quality was really bad, they were clearly old, as they were dried out and hard ( the dog didn't even want them).  The service was terrible too, the drive thru person had an attitude, was rude and clearly didn't care.  I emailed the corporate office to make them aware but haven't heard back, I guess they could care less about their establishments as well.

Went to the KFC four lunch. The staff was friendly and the service was fast. The chicken and sides were cooked to perfection. I almost didn't go because of the bad reviews but I am glad I did. I'll be visiting them again.

I love Chipotle but I hate this location. The portions of everything (especially the meat) are extremely skimpy. Last time I was there I asked for just a little extra steak and I got a dirty look and just three extra pieces of meat. The time before that, I asked for double steak, and I even paid extra. The portion was still incredibly small. I don't ever get smiles or eye contact here. When it's loud and I can't hear the employees the first time, they repeat themselves harshly. Everyone always seems irritated and flustered, and I will never return to this location. The people at the Ina location are so much nicer.
I remember once upon a time when the manager and the rest of the staff were so cheerful and nice to me and other customers. If they could get back to basics, maybe I might return.

C'mon, it's Chipotle! What else more is there to say?

In all seriousness, chipotle is great. The sofritas is amazing and the fast service is great. I love eating on the patio in the warm weather and watching the passers by on the Cultural Trail!

I won't lie: the only reason I set foot in Chipotle was because I received a "buy one, get one free" coupon and figured that was enough of an incentive to give the place a try. Don't get me wrong, I don't have anything against the place, but despite living within spitting distance of the place (yum?), I'd never frequented it simply because I don't really do fast food.

I guess the obvious comparison to make is between this and Qdoba (where I admit that I have eaten MANY times in the past). I think that for the money, Chipotle is the better pick simply because the ingredients seem fresher, which is probably reflected in the relatively slimmer menu at Chipotle. Rather than overwhelming you with myriad options, you pretty much decide whether you want a burrito, a burrito bowl, tacos (soft or hard), salad, or tacos. The filling choices are the same for all of them (chicken, steak, barbacoa, carnitas, or veggies) and everything costs about $6.25. It seems like Chipotle has decided to focus on providing the best product possible, so while eating here every day would probably get boring, what you do get tastes pretty good. Also, the portions are HUGE. A single burrito will probably get you through the day because the amount they manage to cram into a flour tortilla (how do they do it?!?) is enough to feed a small family... or the average American, I suppose.

The first time I came, I got the carnitas burrito, which was sufficiently good that my husband and I decided to come back. I think the barbacoa burrito is probably my favorite - the meat is juicy and nicely spiced. We both splurged and got guac on our burritos, and while $1.25 for this add in (the only one you have to pay for) may seem pricey, it was well worth it because this guacamole is muy delicioso. That said, if not for the coupon we had, two burritos and one drink would have cost us nearly $19... which seems kind of insanely expensive for fast food in my mind. Sure this is a healthier choice than a lot of other places, but for $20 there are a lot of other (non-fast food) places I'd hit up first before coming back here. It's a decent choice if you need to eat and run or really want a burrito, but in my mind, it's not a huge game changer.

I ordered there new burger and a kids cheese burger.. Everything was dry old and just disgusting. I was happy that I only paid $5.00 and tax.. It's fast food I understand, but this was so awful I gave it back to them... Said you can have your garbage back.. What happened to this place they used to have such great food? Won't be back. Negative star.
Steak tasted like it came from a fisher price kitchen play set. I've had better at McDonalds.

First time I've ever gotten a whopper with no meat In it. Everything was cold.

Not sure what the cook was doing. His job is literally to put meat on a bun.  How screwed up do you have to be to forget the key part of your job, wrap a bun in paper, and try to pass that garbage off and what... think the customer wouldn't notice? 

This kind of halfassery will destroy this country.

Order 90 aug 17 2018  at 9:40 pm in case the owner wants to fire everyone in the quality control chain. 

Unbelievable

Well, as far as a chain taco place, there is a spice and freshness that I appreciate. This one is always busy, inside and the drive through.

There is always some sort of special offer or new combo of ingredients put in a burrito, bowl or plate.

Especially love their fresh brewed smooth iced tea. Sometimes I drop in just for the tea. I spike it also with the other sodas for a hint of extra taste.

I also like the all you can enjoy packets of a variety of hot sauces on the tables.

Easy parking.

Lemme just say that when we lived in Chicago for 7 years, the one thing I missed the most from the West was DEL TACO! (so obviously I'm a fan)

My mom would literally steal extra sauce packets and send them IN THE MAIL to me so I could get my fix. When I'd visit them in LA, I'd head straight there and before I hopped back on the airplane to head back to the Midwest, I'd bring some burritos on the plane with me! I sorta, kinda love this place.

Del Scorcho and the Mild sauces are the best in flavor.

As for menu items, you can't go wrong with the basics. I'm a fan of the 1/2lb bean and cheese burrito with red sauce and the tostada. Their layer burrito is pretty good, too.

Tip: If you're local, check your Tuesday junk mailers for coupons. You can score seriously cheap food that way. And they let you stack your coupons! I often use the BOGO burrito ones and pay $1.08 for my whole lunch!!

Tip 2: Save your receipt. Take their short survey and they give a code that you can return on the receipt for $1 off a $3 or more bill for your next purchase. You really have no excuse to NOT get free food every time you visit!

I know it's a West Coast love thing, but I'm on board. My kids, who were born Midwesterners (thank goodness we got outta there!) love the "value taco" because they eat tiny amounts of food and they are like pennies! Seriously, I can feed both of my kids for less than $2 at the place.

This is a KFC/Taco Bell combo location.  I have eaten here a few times, mostly carry-out. The staff has always been polite and fast. The chicken received has always been fresh and hot. And the dining area has always been relatively clean when I have eaten there.

We've been to this chipotle a few times before and every single time we have been disappointed. We ordered it online and when we picked it up the bag was wet and had soaked through the container.
We opened our meals and the food was completely and utterly subpar obviously they do not have good quality control, they do not train their employees right and it is a shame because it could be absolutely delicious.
We also ordered a side of guacamole and chips,  The guacamole was salty and it  had huge pieces inside that hadn't been mashed properly of green and hard avocado, it also had little pieces of the pit.  Chips where extremely salty but I mean extremely, and they were stale they were not crunchy. The chicken was undercooked and rubbery, we could see a little bit of pink in it, also the steak was a bit chewy. 
Are you kidding me right now, what is this? Chipotle please get your act together we like it and we want to come back but you need to really do some quality control ASAP.

Normally when I go to a fast food place in the morning it's because I overslept and didn't have time to make myself a meal. Today I went through the Dunkin Donuts drive through cause I knew they would be quick. I was right, the meal came out quick. I ordered a Bagel with egg and turkey sausage on it - no cheese cause I'm allergic, the hash browns and a pumpkin donut. The donut was great but my sandwich came out wrong. The bagel had cheese on it and the turkey sausage was hard and not tasty at all. Very disappointed since I was looking forward to a good quick meal this morning.

really bad restaurant.  the order was incorrect-I didn't notice til I got home and was given green beans as a side dish.  The service was really poor-I had to wait a long time and the very small pieces of chicken just was not worth it--I won't be back...

I usually only get an iced latte and free donut. (If you do a survey on the receipt, you get a free donut with any coffee purchase). Today I decided to try something else, so I ordered the southwest steak burrito. In the picture it looked good and big so I was pretty stoked. However as I'm sitting at the window, I see the lady take some rinkadink flat burrito out and put it in a small slip and into my bag. I'm thinking to myself, this better be the best damn burrito cause I just spent $3.00 on it. 
So...I get to work, which is right down the street. I try the burrito, and its cold...its not steak, its sausage. And its just completely disgusting! It was literally the same size as McDonalds dollar burrito, but much much worse. I ended up just throwing it away because it was so gross. 
As I'm looking in my bag for my receipt so I can take the survey, I realize she obviously didn't give one to me. Now, one would think that if someone does a survey for a free donut, they must want their new receipt since they obviously USE it. Annoyed, I look up the number to let them know I never got a receipt and a lady answers the phone and says "hello". I'm sort of confused, thinking I dialed the wrong number because I'm pretty sure when a business picks up the phone they're supposed say something like "thank you for calling dunkin donuts, how can I help you". Or at least something a long those lines...Not hello, like I'm calling a friend. I said sorry I must have called the wrong number and she said who are you trying to get ahold of,  I said dunkin donuts, and she said yea this is dunkin donuts. I kinda laughed and told her she should probably let the caller know next time. 
Anyway, I know this location is new, so the employees aren't all the way there yet...but seriously? Get it together. And either take that burrito off the menu, or drop the price to a dollar, because looking at the picture vs the actual burrito, they're falsely advertising.
The worst kfc I have visited ever. Unfriendly staff and old chicken served here. I bought the nashville hot wings which was soggy and old.

Compelled to write bc of revolting disgusting steak that tasted like salted fatty rubber. Doubtful it was actually meat. I haven't eaten at Chipotle in a year or so now I know why. My stomach was NOT happy afterwards and the toilet called soon thereafter. Hope this nasty company folds soon. I'm going to short it's stock.

I dont have anything against this specific Chipotle location but more the quality of the food. The store was clean. My order was quick. The girls working were very sweet and polite. The food however was less then exciting. 2 tacos (carnitas) were tasteless and soggy after my 2 minute drive back to work. The chips were stale and queso was grainy. I wanted to give Chipotle a shot even after all their issues in news headlines but I won't be going back to any of them.

I ATE THE BONES!   I ATE THE BONES!!!!!

Isn't it amazing the power of marketing and stupid television commercials can get us to do things we'd normally not do.  It is rare that I eat KFC. I may enjoy it every other Blue Moon and since the 23rd was going to be a Full Moon, I thought that was close enough for me to enjoy some Original Recipe. 

I ordered the two piece Snack Box and it actually was not bad. It was boneless, skinless and had the Original Recipe just like the TV ads said. I was curious as to how the pieces were going to look and they  basically are drastically deformed Chicken Tenders.  Almost like a science project gone bad. 

As a side I choose Mash Potatoes with Gravy, you can never go wrong with smashed taters. KFC offers the big Steak Fries, which are my fry of choice, but they do not do them right. I will pass on them every time. The biscuit was Plain Jane and would embarrass any true Southern Woman. 

Staff and service here is typical fast food. Don't expect much and you'll get what you are expecting. A decent fast meal at a reasonable price.

I work over at Auffenberg Dealer group across the street and every time I go there which today will be my last, there either isn't any grilled chicken, its extremely dry, or it seems a lot of their pieces are mainly bone! If I'm going to pay almost 3 dollars for a piece, at least give me a good one!

Slow service.....always. Most of the employees look like they could care less. No extra crispy chicken at lunch time today. Really? How does that happen? The $5.00 fill up is a good deal if they have chicken prepared. The owner should take note of the reviews. The employees are no doubt costing him/her buisness.

This Burger King, with all the charm of a 50s style diner, has the best customer service of all the kingdom.  They are very polite, patient, and prompt.  On the rare occasion that they make a mistake, they are quick to resolve.  I appreciate that very much.

Also, this Burger King in particular seems cleaner than the others (admittedly I usually use the others drive thru more than the inside, but still cleaner all around).  The food comes out very fast here,  and well put together.  They take pride in trying to give you a Whopper that looks like the picture and not the kind that you might get at other locations, with the cheese half off and mayo on the top of the bun.

Conveniently located at Speedway and Pantano, this place is royally good.  Check them out if you haven't.  You'll be impressed.

Good food

We were next door doing some shopping and stopped in for dinner.  I had my Whopper with Cheese, a sandwich I view as one of the best fast food burgers around.  The sandwich did not disappoint.  Not very photogenic this time but had that good flame-broiled Whopper taste.

Grading one of these cookie cutter fast food places where the food is so uniform tends to come down to service.  That went ok.  We walked in as the place was trying to fill a large order, so we didn't initially get any attention at all until that order was mostly put together.  Our turn went ok and we had our food within a few minutes of ordering. My wife complained to me there were not many chicken fries in her order.  I did overhear they were out of ketchup, if true that's not good.

I was back to fill my Whopper craving.  Actually I was there because of a TV ad talking about a $5 Whopper meal deal.  Being outside the regular meal times, there wasn't much of a wait.  I get my order and was out the door in less than 5 minutes.  The meal was just as I expected, the Whopper was excellent, and the fries acceptable.

An interesting side light.  I ask what it would cost to upgrade the small fries to a large, and was told it would be over $3 and change more.

Chipotle is a heaven sent and I used to go at least 2-3 times a week. Now that money is tighter, its a real treat when I get to go. I've been to more locations than you can count and never had a bad experience. That being said : I wouldn't go to this one if it was the last restaurant standing in King of Prussia. 
Listen up boys and girls, cause I am about to tell you a story.

So many issues here. To begin, I never write reviews, and never ever complain about service because I'm a young 21 year old girl who normally has better things to do, and I worked in the food industry. But I should warn others of this monstrosity of a Chipotle. 

First, girl making food didn't wear a hairnet, which normally wouldn't bother me but her hair was EVERYWHERE. She moved EXTREMELY SLOW to a point where I was getting miserable; I normally don't care if you wanna move like a snail as long as I eventually get my stuff and I wasn't on a time limit, but this was ridiculous, especially as a line of five built up behind me. She paid more attention to the girl wrapping a burrito for the person in front of me, than to me....asked me what I wanted on my tacos, then cut me off to laugh and talk to the employee next to her so I couldn't even tell her what I wanted. Straight up just cut me off like she forgot I was in the middle of ordering. I didn't know when I was supposed to talk because I didn't know when I was being paid attention to and it made me so uncomfortable. She complained the whole time about how hard it was to make tacos, whatever, sorry I ordered them or whatever. Told her I wanted Sofritas, so naturally she put pork on them. I corrected her, and she used sofrita, but I noticed chunks of steak in the sofrita. Huge concern being that if I eat any meat I get very sick. I don't take lightly to meat mixing with the apparently vegetarian option and that's also (probably maybe possibly?) a health violation. I kindly told the person checking me out that its a health problem to have meat mixed with the sofrita and they should maybe start a new batch, they didn't seem to care. They were out of black beans, any kind of sauce, and sourcream so my tacos were extremely bland. She asked "Do you want lettuce?" I said yes please, so you can guess what that means; she closed it all up without giving me lettuce. So I just had this messy, tasteless, $11 down the drain bowl of Sofrita and pinto beans with taco shells somewhere in there, it was actually kinda laughable. I did laugh at it, my sad tacos.

My boyfriend was with me and has never been to a Chipotle and I was excited to treat him to my favorite restaurant after a long and terrible day he had, he was standing behind me so I could show him how to order. When all the employees left (Oh yeah, forgot to mention at one point when I was paying, every employee on the food line just went incognito to mingle in the back for a little) I told him that this was the worst service I have ever had anywhere and that he should go eat somewhere else, as I was not about to expose him to his first Chipotle experience this way. 

I was so sad and still sad over this experience. There are many Chipotles around the KOP area, I would take the extra few minutes to drive to one and avoid this food court Chipotle at all costs. Don't make the same mistakes as me. If you read this whole thing I assume Chipotle means a lot to you so put your money into one that will actually care about you like you care for it.
Just got a whopper with cheese. Got home to eat it it was cold. Really!  It seems like no one cares anymore. Won't be going there anymore

A fast food establishment that never disappoints me. I love the Double Del Burger. This time I tried the Taco Burger. Taco meat with cheese lettuce and red sauce in a bun. Not on the menu but you can ask for. Delish! I am obsessed with their fries, another delish. I've had every taco they make and for fast food it isn't bad. They do use fresh ingredients and you can taste that in the food.

We poor sods in Maine have no White Castle, no In & Out, no Sonic. I honestly don't know how we get by. Anyway, stopped here on a road trip from AZ back to ME. First time at a White Castle and I wasn't disappointed. 

$15 fed two of us with 2 sodas, 2 fries, 10 burgers and 1 order of onion chips. Definitely more food than we needed, but I mean, who knows when I'll be there again? The burgers are incredibly close to the frozen ones only greasier. Fries tasty and the onion chips were good. 

One thing I had never seen before and have questions about: red cream soda. What the heck is that?? 

So, would I eat it often? No, while delicious going down, I hated myself for doing it afterward and had to live with slider burps for the next 4 hours (docked 1 star) . Would I eat it again? Oh yeah.

This place is disgusting I will never eat here again I went to buy me a lettuce wrap famous star with cheese, something told me to look at my food but I normally do so it was no biggie. The lettuce was dirty Af. Lo and behold I noticed a bug on the lettuce smh this just goes to show you that they don't wash the lettuce. Cook your own food people never again will I eat here

I eat KFC probably once every few years...we wanted to try KFC's "hot chicken"...don't do it. It's very greasy....and fatty and wanted to barf a little inside. but they have good chicken pot pie! If I had to choose between the two would prefer Popeyes.

What was I thinking? Seriously, did I think I was going to get some good fried chicken here. The problem was I didn't pack a lunch,  I was not in a burger mood and nothing else was close to work. So it was KFC or starve to death and I am not ready to die. I have too many more Yelp reviews to write. 

My lunch wasn't that bad, but it wasn't that good either. KFC has just been going downhill over the years and there are just so many other places that do a better job. 
I had a Tenders combo, which included three strips (only available extra crispy), a side dish and beverage. My original side was their huge fries, but they were out at 12:30PM. Again I have to ask, Seriously? It is lunch time on a work day and you are already out of a popular side. They told me it would be 5 minuets, before they had fresh ones. I am not a patient man, I selected mashed potatoes so I could get out of there and back to work. 

I did notice this was a KFC that had the lunch buffet where you pay a flat fee and can eat like you are at a Hometown Buffet, but the only main entree is "chicken". While getting my diet Pepsi, it balances out the effects of eating "fried food", I noticed the sides available on the buffet.  The sides did not impress me, most looked like they were heated up straight from a can. What happened to making things from scratch?

Back at work, I ate my lunch. My tenders were a nice size and crispy, but lacked flavor. The potatoes were another story within themselves. Who in the hell puts "brown" gravy on potatoes served with fried chicken? You'd never see that on the farm. I also hope they know they are not fooling anyone with those instant potatoes. You could not get more instant then these. Once again I have to say, "no flavor". 

KFC, there are too many privately owned chicken joints that are putting out a better meal then you. What saved you from getting a lower score then 3 was; friendly staff, clean dining area and from what I could see of the kitchen it was spotless as well. 

I'd love to see you get back to the KFC I remember as a child when we'd get a bucket after church and go over the river and through the woods to visit grandma.

Thee worst KFC I have ever had the displeasure of visiting.

They simply just don't care.

I have been here on multiple occasions and have never been in line less than 15 mins. Normally, I wouldn't wait that long, fortunately for them, they continue to make money by trapping drivers in their drive-thru lane, You cant get out once your in! Even if you are the only car there, you still wait on average 15 mins, no matter what you order.

I have been there around dinner time, and was told there was no chicken. I've been there a little later in the evening and told they were no longer making chicken. Hello? You are KFC right?

They consistently botch orders, you MUST check your order before you leave.

The food just isn't as good as a most KFC's and the taco bell side is always sloppy, like someone just threw the food in a wrapper and rolled it up. It is edible though. 

You don't get napkins or sauce unless you request it at the window and often you get an attitude for asking - like they don't have time to throw those things in the bag,, come on.. 

Just terrible.

The food was good but the service was horrible I ordered the fresco burger with no mayo. That seemed to be too confusing for anyone there to understand. I had also ordered a large coke and curly fries which also got messed up. In total it took me 35 minutes to order. I would also like to point out I stared in the drive thru and had to go in since they were to incompetent to understand me through the drive through speaker. If you don't mind a long wait and incompetent workers it's worth the wait because the food is good. I'm only giving them one star because taking the order and correctly making that order is over 50% of the work.

We haven't been to a Del Taco in YEARS. Many years. But we saw coupons for buy one get one free chicken tacos and 1/2 pound bean and cheese burritos. A bargain I apparently couldn't turn down. 

First impression was 3 stars max. It annoyed me that even though I was the only customer at the counter, nobody waited on me for quite a LONG while...however, Dora, busy cooking in the back DID smile and greet me and promise that "someone" would be right with me...but "someone" was working the drive through and never materialized...so again with a lovely smile, Dora promised SHE would come as soon as she finished. 

At that point young Mr Someone sauntered over from the drive they window. They appeared understaffed. It was 3:30...not busy yet understaffed if they couldn't wait on be customer at the counter!

While I Waited...I noticed the floor as really not clean...papers needed sweeping and cleaning up on the floor and tables...I'm still hanging out at 3 stars!

We ordered, and paid our huge $2.18 for the meal with coupons...Went to the table...hmmmm...they now have all the sauce packets on EVERY table. Excellent idea!  I snapped a picture of the sauces and... and WHAT?  Sweet Treats $1??? Mini Milk Shakes...$1???  So I ordered one and asked for both chocolate AND strawberry combined...no extra charge...ok. I'm up to 4 stars. HOW GREAT IS A MINI MILK SHAKE?  Great enough to bring me back!!!  Comes in the little water cup!  A perfect sweet treat. PERFECT!

While we ate, young Mr Someone swept the floor and cleaned all the tables...that explains how we got back to 5 stars!

Seriously...I will be back for $1 
mini milk shakes!  Such a great idea!  Perfect for us seniors, anyone watching their waistline and families with kids!  It's plenty to satisfy a sweet tooth!

The chicken taco and bean burrito were both great!  I think we're BACK!

If you find this review helpful please vote!

This was my first time at a Chipotle. At first glance I thought it was pretty bare bones, but that didn't matter as the carnitas in my burrito bowl were yum. The one major thing missing however, was the jalapenos. I was like "wha!?!" That's a key ingredient. But the hot sauce was actually "hot" and had good flavor, so that kind of made up for it...almost.
chicken, steak are good. chips and guac are on point as well. sometimes i ask for double chicken and they give it to me for free haha

I went back and forth between 3 and 4 stars for this location, and really landed on 4 stars because I heard really good things about the fries, which I would have knocked a star off for.
First time ever eating at a White Castle. Service was awesome. Super friendly. Not just with us but with other customers I saw them interacting with.
The food...this is a fast food joint, so I wasn't expecting much. Two words "impossible slider" ... the impossible slider is why you should eat here! I'm impressed with White Castle and hope it stays on the menu. It was tasty. I didn't think much of the mozzarella sticks or Mac and cheese nibbles as they were pretty greasy, but hey, it's fast food. Hubs enjoyed the chicken waffle slider and the sloppy joe slider. I will be returning for the impossible slider and will give the fries a second chance.
One time they literally told me they were out of chicken and the lady was mad at me basically for asking. Never will I forget that! I too would give 0 stars or half a star if possible

I have a weakness, and it's Chipotle. Living downtown, there is only one location, and it isn't that easy to get too in the car. Luckily it's smack dab on the Cultural Trail, so when the weather is nice, I can bike there and avoid having to find parking.

The weather is not nice yet; however, and I found myself craving a chicken burrito bowl, so off I went to my nearest location for dinner. The line was much longer than I've seen for dinner. Someone from the front of line announced they everyone was waiting on chicken, and if anyone wanted something else, they could go ahead. Looking at the 5-7 people waiting in line and then hearing my stomach rumble and start saying "feed me", my mind went to steak instead of chicken, and to the front of the line I went. 

Service is consistent with all of the the other locations that I've been to before. I felt they were a little conservative with the $1.90 order of guacamole that I paid for, and I thought I ordered chips but apparently didn't because they weren't in my bag (but also not on my receipt) - I didn't notice until I got home.

I'm a fan and this place is really convenient in the summer on the trail, with very limited outdoor seating.

Theyre fast food so we cant hold them to a high standard as another type...

Yeah del taco is my guilty pleasure... shhh dont tell anyone...

I tried the fish taco #7 deal, two fish tacos fries & a drink... turns out its cheaper to buy this combo & throw away the kinda soggy fries...

They do not serve mcdonalds grade of fries nor do they have fish tacos like rubios...

Del taco has the lil green cheese n bean burritos i like n mcd & rubios cant compete

We are in town looking for place to live, so are eating out a lot. I ate at this chipotle twice while in town. The first time we walked there from the hotel, a bonus to not have to worry about parking! It's in a great spot with lots of other places to eat around it. I chose chipotle because it has ALWAYS  been good clean food that doesn't make you feel yucky after you eat it and my boyfriend has never been. The guy who checked us out was very friendly, smiled a lot and generally seemed to enjoy his job. Maybe he was manager? We both got the steak burrito bowls with black beans, med salsa, cheese, sour cream, and guacamole. The steak wasn't the same flavor I was used to and had grizzle. I picked 2 pieces of fat nasty grizzle out of my mouth, ( gross, right?!). The guacamole didn't have much flavor either, i make it at home and have had it at the other chipotle where I'm from, and this was not up to par. The 2nd visit I walked to get lunch, waited for them to open and got a steak burrito this time with the same combo as before. The staff seemed to be there just to collect a paycheck. No matter what you do, you should ALWAYS take pride in your work!  The burrito was not "wrapped/rolled" properly, which made the insides ooze out. I pulled more fat grizzle from my mouth again and swear if I go there again I will ask to speak to a manager ASAP! Eat at your own risk. I you have had excellent chipotle in the past, like me, you will understand why I would not recommend this location to anyone until they learn how to prepare the food chipotle is known for!!

This chipotle is always a disappointment.  I've visited several times in the past year and finally decided to write a review.  The portion sizes are not normal quantities, the rice doesn't have the classic cilantro taste, the chips are stale, and the meat is way too spicy (chicken and steak). I wish headquarters had better quality control over their franchises because there isn't another location close to us and we rarely buy chipotle anymore. This location needs some serious help. I give two stars because the store is always clean and the staff are friendly.

Long line, but they move out quickly. Guy wasn't 100% paying attention as we ordered, but we just said it again and he got it. Iced tea lemonades and turkey sausage egg and cheese is good. Munchkins are missing some variety (mostly just chocolate glazed and regular glazed) but still good!

This is my third time at this chipotle location in media. The first time I went they were out of rice, guacamole, lettuce and softita absolutely no idea how because it was around lunch time so I left. My section time here I got a softita bowl and they were out of lettuce again and the rice was under cooked, whatever. I revisited a third time today thinking it would be better. Worst chipotle ever. I ordered a softita bowl once again. The rice was under cooked AGAIN, there were multiple pieces of hair in my bowl and being vegan this biggest no for me is FINDING A PIECE OF CHICKEN IN MY SOFRITA BOWL (tofu) we got a kids meal for my sister and the container they gave us was dirty and the salsa was old and browning. Never EVER returning to this location. Also if you're vegan or vegetarian look in your bowl first.

Only giving this one star because I can't give it negative 10.   It's a Burger King - I had low expectations.  I ordered a whopper meal and stood at the counter for a full 6 minutes.   A woman came through the drive through and ordered a whopper without meat.  

They screwed up and gave her my whopper.   While standing and waiting at the counter, the customer walked in  and returned the whopper (mine) because it had meat.  They quickly made her a new sandwich.  

MEANWHILE... two employees opened the sandwich and peeled back the bun to check for meat, realized it was my order, and proceeded to put it on a tray and tried to serve it to me.  

I told the girl I wasn't going to eat it and she legit grabbed the tray out of my hand and threw it on the floor and yelled "this guy won't eat that one so will someone PLEASE accommodate him?" with such a snotty attitude.  

Again, low expectations cause fast food, but it was still ten bucks!!

I actually was laughing at the lady at the counter from my car in the drive through counting her chicken.....should have guessed something was up.  Literally sat at the drive through for a good 10 minutes before the guy came over to even acknowledge us.  Finally got the food, drove all the way home, turns out instead of the 8 pc chicken w/biscuits, mashed potatoes, and wedges turned into 6 pieces of chicken (2 being wings), no biscuits, and the wedges were soggy.  Got back in the car w/my dad, went back in, and asked them why stuff was missing.  One of the employees was staring at me like she wanted to jump across the counter at me!  While I was inside, my Dad actually spoke to someone outside w/said his order has been SCREWED UP 3 TIMES!!! so he goes inside now bc he knows its going to happen!  Kind of weird how I wasn't given a receipt either? Fee bad for this owner, should go in and evaluate what's going on in his store!

I give 3 stars because of the KFC crew at this location. They were very helpful with my order. They were as fast as possible, considering that they had to cook more chicken because they didn't have enough original ready for my order or the others in line with me...I understood and waited....they filled my order as soon an chicken was finished ( 20 minutes)..but when we got home and opened up the bucket of chicken we saw that the pieces of chicken we're so small....PIGEONS have bigger wings and thighs...Looked NOTHING like their ads......Geeze KFC has REALLY gone down in quality....I hadn't had KFC in a while, and really wanted some for dinner....Sorry that I went there...Never Again...I will buy my fried chicken from Publix from now on ..much better taste, and bigger, plumper pieces of chicken

Fine service and cleanliness but in KFC's efforts to keep prices down the food is not worth buying. I got the grilled chicken. Without the breading you can see the chicken most have been a week old before they butchered it. The legs are the size of wings and the wings aren't worth the effort. Recommend paying more and getting real value.

Hahahaah..the reviews at my local BK are hilarious!! I wanna add another one.Yes. Lots of teenagers running this business. Kudos to them! BK managers do not care because people will still purchase their burgers on the rush. It is convenient. I go with Whopper Jr..which shrunk. Helps with my diet...so no problem and I get my 4 piece tenders. Oh and Im terrible using the new machine for getting water. Restroom when Im using this place to u know....and then off to go vacuum the car next door at the gas station. Convenience. Yipeeeeee! No working Wifi.   ;( If they had Internet this would be my hangout because it is close to all the highways entrances. Whoosh! Take a drive.

This location would be perfect if you're hungry when shopping @ Walmart. We used the mobile ordering which was a breeze. I had a user error and ordered it from a location that didn't have a drive thru, so I had to fight thru the foot traffic inside the walmart. The food was made fast & accurate. Only negative to this review was that the order was cold and quite bland. I remember the good old days when a Whopper tasted like a Whopper! Not the same taste at all anymore. As usual, customer service these days are non existent. If you dont want to work with people,  then DONT work with people because your stank attitudes WILL always show through. If you dare to, come in and try for yourself this BK location.

Excellent location and opened until 10pm! So if you ever get the late munchies, you can stop by and grab a bowl ( I get half chicken half steak). I love their guacamole-always fresh and super yummy. The restaurant is always clean. Their employees are always pleasant and super fast!
I LOVE Chipotle's sofritas. And the staff here is so quick and nice. Parking is the only drawback about coming here, but if you don't mind walking or you're already downtown, it's fine.

It's definitely a 50-50 shot that you'll actually be able to order anything here. There's a very good chance that the card machine will be"down" even just a few minutes after opening. If you are able to pay, don't be surprised if there's a long wait for your chicken. I'm not convinced they follow the guidelines for keeping food fresh and stocked. I suspect it's more "sell it til we run out, no matter how long that takes".

Wow worse customer service I have ever seen. Screaming at a costumer is never ok. We just asked them to make sure it was an impossible whopper. They went off after that wow just wow and my food was very very cold like they warmed it up from the night before.

I'm a slightly confused at Burger Kings approach at selling The Impossible Burger as a whopper. I just ate one and I have to say it was decent.  My confusion is that they offer a plant-based burger that is cooked on the same surface as the beef burgers. I needed to know a few things before actually ordering the burger so the young lady had to get manager. I asked him what my options were as far as not cooking the burger on the same surface as the beef burgers. I don't know how others feel about a microwaved Burger but that was my first disappointment. I then asked him what else would come on it because I'm vegan. His response was..." it comes with everything that would be on a Whopper.". My reply "did you miss the part where I said I was vegan? how would I know what's on a Whopper? I don't eat Whoppers!" He apologized and said the impossible burger comes with mayo..lettuce tomato..onion..ketchup. So i ordered a microwaved..no mayo impossible whopper. I got it home and added vegannaise and more ketchup to it. With the exception of the parts that were rock hard from microwaving it..it was actually pretty good. My opinion is this is not something that they are doing for vegetarians and vegans. I personally think it's too get people to try something different and this is their approach. Well Burger King...you're on the right track but maybe rethink how to accomodate the Vegetarian and vegans a little more? If i hadn't asked about the cooking process...i would be in a world of trouble with my gut right now!!!

Where's the beef? Oh, I forgot I ordered the new Impossible Whopper. 
The Impossible Whopper is vegetarian and vegan friendly, because it is plant based and it taste just like real meat. 

I'm not vegan or vegetarian, but I've had a Impossible Burger at several restaurants in STL and have enjoyed them. When I heard BK was now offering one, I had to give it a try. BK is right at the  street entrance to my work location, so I stopped by on my way home. Is it good as a Whopper with "real" meat? No. Is it good as a Impossible Burger at a sit down restaurant? No, but it was still good and I am sure the vegans and vegetarians of the world are thrilled it is available at a fast food burger joint. 

My IB had good flavor, it had all the same toppings as an original Whopper. The only complaint I would have is that it was a tad smaller then the bun and that it was crisp around the edges a little on the dry side. The crisp edges and dryness, I'll give them a little leeway since it is new to their menu and it is only the second day they are making them. 
I'm looking forward to trying it again here and at the BK near my home. 

The staff here is not the friendliest, but they are always quick with the service. It's fast food fast, so I can tolerate the staff being aloof. 

I'll be back? - Sure. 

When: April 2, 2019
Why: Impossible Whopper released April 1, 2019

Okay was telling my sister about the quesadillas as we had Del Taco in New Mexico! We shared a cheddar and spicy jack with the yummy medium salsa and omg so delicious ! Perfectly cooked nothing that special but so hit the spot ! 

My mom had a soft taco and liked it as well. Fast service great tasting fast food come to Seattle Del Taco !
Great del taco!  Always nice and in a very convenient area outside the mall.

I was really disappointed in this location. I eat Chipotle multiple times a week, all around the country, and this was one of the worst. Almost everything was overcooked, and the service was agonizingly slow, even though I was the only customer. 

I thought maybe it was just a bad shift, so I went again two days later.

The guy who was supposed to make my burrito was doing other things for a while before finally asking me for my order, very slowly, all while the staff at the counter were talking about how much they wanted the weekend to come, while letting the slow guy do all the work.

The food itself was the worst I've had at Chipotle. The rice and chicken were rubbery and dry, and the guac was horribly old. Even the sofritas was watery, how do you mess up sofritas?

Only thing keeping them from 1 star was that the building was reasonably clean, and the food was still edible.

Maybe they were just having an off week, or maybe it's hard to find fresh food in Indianapolis, but based on the awful attitudes of the employees, I think they just suck. Probably bad management.

I won't go back.

Well, this place is okay. The interior is kind of stale, and I think this place has been robbed quite a few times (not in recent years). The location is okay. It is annoying incoming because it is located across from the wall and driving in the parking lot's a pain the butt. I love the chicken popcorn and the famous bowl though!

We come here at least once a week. The food is fresh and healthy. I get the sofritas bowl and you feel like you have eaten enough protein and fresh veges. The staff are extremely friendly. Great location in the Town of Speedway Indiana.

If you like uncooked chicken then this is your place. They are currently not serving the standard chicken like Chipotle is know for because of a apparent shortage. 
Walked in to this location last Friday early afternoon when there was no line. 
The food line was missing chicken and steak, the grill was full of both.
When the guy on the grill noticed a line begin to form he decided to speed it up and pull some chicken off the grill. He then began cutting it up while a line worker took a cup of the chicken he was cutting and placed it into my bowl.
I could tell from that point that the chicken was not thoroughly cooked.
Sat down, took one bite and spit it out immediately.

When showing the apprentice my burrito bowl with uncooked chicken she expressed that they were frustrated that they do not have the normal chicken.
Unfortunately that was a poor excuse for serving me uncooked chicken.
She gave us our money back and we went and had Moe's Southwest.

Poor management. There are two cash registers, one doesn't work and the other one only takes cash. I mean, we live in America, what's wrong with this KFC that doesn't take debit or credit cards let alone Apple Pay. This is 2018. Luckily my mom had some cash available. The service was poor too. We have to go back to the cashier to ask for straws and extra plates. Our order was to dine in and they treat us like it was a to go order. Never again I will step into this KFC. No wonder they're now the second Chicken fast food company in America falling behind Chick-Fil-A.

KFC sure is finger lickin good, never fails to deliver taste-wise! I enjoy the crunchy fried chicken and all sides KFC has to offer. The biscuits are delightful, flaky and buttery. Personally, I like to drizzle them with honey. The  prices are definitely economical, and the family meal sizes are big enough to feed a hungry army. 

I came to this carrollwood location since I was in the area to pick up some food for the family on a Sunday. I like the varieties of meals there are to choose from, but when it came to picking the sides this time around it was a bit of a challenging process. I originally wanted green beans to go with the family meal sides, but instead they offered mac n cheese because they were out of the green beans. I was able to still get mashed potatoes and gravy, and the Cole slaw.  

I know it's a Sunday around dinner time, but it didn't quite make sense to me how they would be out of the green beans already. I settled for the mac and cheese this time, hoping it'll be good. I'm thinking it's probably the more popular chosen side option, but I still like my green beans. 

The restaurant appears to have a more expanded seating area,  which is a nice upgrade for the sit in experience if you do stay and eat rather than take out. All in all, I still enjoy the yummy goodness kfc has to offer.

Never will go here again. Waited 20 minutes for 2 burgers and 2 fries and when I finally got my food it was just short of cold! I WILL NEVER GO TO THIS LOCATION AGAIN!! If I could give less than one star I would!!

Tonight I wanted to take dinner to my grands house. They wanted chicken, mashed potatoes and macaroni and cheese. I placed my order to my surprise they worked the magic behind the counter and I got an amazing deal!!!!! Loved them and the food

Better than Ok because it's my guilty pleasure.  I love their chicken, mashed potato and gravy.  This particular establishment whilst friendly and fast never feels really very clean but who am I to complain, I just want my chicken and its cheap.

This might be the worst chipotle of all time. This is unfortunately the closest location to my house so I've been probably half a dozen times and have had some type of issue EVERY SINGLE TIME!? Anything from undercooked rice, dried out steak, Stale tortillas, No tortillas, no sour cream and even no black beans. I have a cousin who works for corporate chipotle and I do love the chain itself but this store needs a major change in either managing or someone isn't doing a good job. The Last Time I ordered from here I got a bowl that had multiple pieces of foil in it and when I called I wasn't compensated at all. DO NOT GO TO THIS CHIPOTLE.

Reason to go to Burger King?  Their Whopper Jr. ,no cheese with fries.  I treat myself once or twice a year.  Its a good sandwich, the right size,  maybe 3 ounces, with that Burger King back yard charcoal taste that is damn good.

Went to this KFC to order take out. 
Ordered bucket of grilled chicken. Not enough grilled available. Had to substitute original. Thank goodness we did not eat in. Dining area was filthy. Had reservations about ordering take out. Service was pathetically slow. After all was said and done was charged 1-1/2% to use debit card. Good luck to them. KFC Campbell will not see me again.

This used to be my favorite Del Taco in town, but  they have gone downhill.  The past 5 times we have gone they have messed up our orders.  They tell us to come in and they will fix it and they still manage to mess up the order again.  The last time we went in they messed up our quesadilla order.  We always ask extra cheese no green sauce.  Went back to get our free quesadilla for them messing up our previous order and they hardly out any cheese in the quesadilla.  It was so flat that it was like eating a tortilla chip.  I also asked for a double del with no onions...and yup..it had onions.  I don't know what happened to this place because it used to be great.  All I know is I don't. want to go back.

Literally the worst thing you can put into your body. If you want to eat something that's not good for you go up the street to PDQ rather than this dump. I went there and there was so much salt on the chicken that I think my hands and body started swelling up before I even finished my meal. I ordered extra crispy and they basically didn't want to make any because they had not made any recently and they said it would take 20 minutes. I literally said that this is your job to make this and waited for my chicken. So if I had to guess they probably spit on my food. Save yourself the trouble and go to any number of other chicken places that are nearby.

There are a lot of bad reviews of this location.  Fast food managers and workers changed regularly so I can't say about the past.  My visit this time is great and actually much better than most.  It wasn't busy and manager is working hard to pack orders. The cashier took orders without any issue and also helped on soda and other small items.  My order has one minor  coupon (free impossible burger) issue, the manager stepped right in and clarified the issue.  Got my order completed within 1 minute. Hope they can keep this good standing.

POSITIVES: 
Speed of service, efficient staff, orderly and clean restaurant...plus no one appeared to be intoxicated. 

THE AWFUL: 
What they pass off as a steak burrito.  You may think a great steak night begins and ends at Golden Corral.  If so, these overcooked cubes full of sinew that end up unceremoniously spat out after some chewing may be for you.  

I went to Google to learn what cuts a customer can expect in a Chipotle steak burrito and wouldn't you know it!?  Each one was worse than the next, have no flavor when cooked well-done and would be perfect for serving to my mother-in-law. 

The official list includes:
Loin, ball tip, round, flat, top butt, clod sometimes, and knuckle.  What is this??  A game of Limbo?  

The list should probably add statements like "may contain peanuts," and "this product was produced in a facility that also has wheat" since those are also cheap ingredients and Chipotle seems to just buy from the lowest bidder.

Or mayyyyyybeee I'll just never order one again, move on and avoid this sadness.

Del Taco is where you go at 3 AM to buy food entirely funded by the change tray in your car. It's not the best Mexican food in the world, but it's an incredible value. The food is fast, satisfying, and absurdly cheap. I don't think I've been able to spend more than $6 here and finish all the food. 

I miss you, Del Taco.

I was looking forward to some kfc but no that didn't happen 
My receipt says Mary took care of me but it was a guy that did .
I ask for a a 3 piece and a extra coleslaw did I get that nope instead I got 3 piece chicken tenders 
No coleslaw no honey mustard but got charged $2.29 extra for it
So thanks a lot dude for ruining my dinner I will never come back to this kfc not even if I'm starving and your the only place open.

Simple the Best! I 

A 10/ out of 10.  
I have not stepped foot in Burger King in over 15 years since I became vegan - 
I first stop today to try out the impossible burger---  simply the best ! It exceeded my expectations I brought my elderly father ( meat eater) he couldn't tell the difference of the burger he who didn't know it was plant based.  
The service for fast food was impeccable - very kind considerate clean and above all a fresh burger. 

Shout out to Burger King staff on route 19 Port Richey you guys are Awesome!

I wanted to try the #beyondsausage but they wanted to charge me full price for just the sausage and muffin.  Not cool.  I ordered a latte and I asked for it not too foamy so she gave me a medium cup only 2/3 full.  I had questions but she was too preoccupied with the drive thru to pay attention.   I think she may have given me dairy, not almond milk like I asked.  
#badexperience

Being from the east coast, Del Taco rocks, as we don't have any back home!

No problems with service or food quality at this particular location.  The 1/2 lb. bean and cheese burritos are always a cheap, surefire bet, where you get a choice of red or green sauce.  The quesadillas are great too.  Believe it or not, I've found the burgers here to be far better than those at the mainstream fast food chains.  Check out the cinnamon churros too!

I'd be truly stoked if they ever put a location closer to downtown, but it's worth the trip.

All in all, a star up from Taco Bell (although there's nothing wrong with Taco Bell...)

This is the location you want to eat at. Clean and the team is attentive. They seem to actually enjoy their environment. Toya was serving the gentleman in front of me and I actually smiled because she was so pleasant. She made sure my ingredients were fresh even at the rather late hour I arrived. I get carnitas in my bowl, every time, but she suggested a barbaco sample and it was even better. David did a great job in the kitchen. The rice and beans were tender so far better than Avon. Faith put it all together nicely. I know all their names because I asked Sam as he checked me out. We shared a good laugh and he too was very pleasant. This team deserves recognition. They are doing customer service right!  I stopped coming to Chipotle due to poor experiences in Avon so I'm so happy that this location has placed a great team. I'll definitely be back!
My 1st (& last) experience with Chipotle. Ordered steak tacos to go. The meat was nothing but chewy, inedible gristle.  What a waste of my time & money!

Lupita.... please ensure the closing manager knows to refill the paper order roll, so when early morning orders are in, they are filled ON TIME. Thank you for offering the guacamole and chips, good customer service, (hence the three star rating). Though slightly delayed on delivery, my son enjoyed his sofritas tacos as part of his birthday celebration at school. Thank you.

First of all the girl i got in the drive thru was HORRIBLE. Rude, bad attitude and very unhelpful. Got off to get my fries because she forgot them and was told they were still making them but she never told us that was going on. She didnt even want to help me she literally ignored me someone else helped me. To finish it all off my whopper was COLD super cold like it just came out of the fridge. People like this shouldn't work in places where they have to give good customer service.

May 01,2019
My mother loves her chicken. We pull up to this KFC and she goes inside to order, I wait outside. I see a few other customers go in and order. Still watching my mother, she's looking more frustrated. Talking to another customer. I swear! 15 minutes go by and my mother is still standing there waiting on her order. I decided to go in and see what was going on. Come to find out, the younger employees (Victor,Christina&etc)were being disrespectful and laughing at the fact that she's didn't get her order. 
I don't stand for disrespect again anyone, especially against my mother. I talk to all the employees about how rude they were being and shouldn't talk to your CUSTOMERS unprofessionally. The manager (Mark) was no help.

My still Favorite Place, always like to get a Steak Burrito Bowl even if i know the whole menu, friendly personnel from the line to the register, fresh food ingredients like usual, proper portions, stake is great tender, rice fresh made, fresh salsas and chips as well, good and tasty looking food always, Good Job Chipotle's Team

Unbelievably rude service! Person taking the order was no help navigating the menu to get the pieces of chicken we wanted. After ordering and pulling around the cashier was short and had an attitude when asked to repeat the cost. When giving change to put with the 20 dollar bill the cashier said she couldn't take the 5 cents because she already put 20 dollars in the screen. It's basic math to round up to 5 bucks versus giving someone 97 cent in change. I would be more understanding if the register was closed but there was no reason for this as she's telling us she can't with it open. This was unacceptable!

TERRIBLE SERVICE. never had anything to prepare!! KFC but is out of tender, wings and basically all chicken ???? Asked for a mashed potatoe and they said they didn't have that either. have no idea how this place is still open when they get such little business as it is.

Went through drive-through to order a $1 Wednesday Whopper; husband and I were both in car. Asked for two of the Whoppers and was told we could only have one. I understand that only one $1 whopper per car is policy. Also ordered large fries and a shake. When we got home we only had one Whopper. Attendant should have exercised common sense and verified that we could have 1 sandwich for $1 and the second we be at full price, which is what I assumed would happen. Better training would have prevented this from happening. Will get burgers from a different source.

I love KFC don't get me wrong but I had to share this. They were out of extra crispy chicken which happens a lot at this location. On top of that they had no original ready. How the hell do you run a KFC and have no chicken ready? That's like going the to a pizza shop and telling me that you are all out of pizza. Ethier, the people working there are incompetent or the inventory is too low or both.

I only ordered from there once, but I'm not going back. 
All three steak tacos were bland and really cold. If you're in Warminster 
go to Rey Azteca for Mexican.

Chipotle is usually a good option for me and I've been going to their restaurants for a long time (like 10 years).  I've usually been happy with their consistency in terms of price and product, but I have to say I have been super disappointed the last couple times I went...to the point of totally avoiding now.  Not sure if it's just this location, if it has to do with the struggles that COVID has presented for businesses basically everywhere, if there's a lack of care, or maybe a different supplier...maybe all the above.  Regardless, the last two times I've gotten anything with meat, specifically the barbacoa and the steak, it has been super gristly and fatty to the point of completely putting me off and not being able to finish my meal.  Really really bummed.

I haven't gone to KFC (Kentucky Fried Chicken) in a long time and I had a taste for some chicken so I decided to go. Man, I wasn't really impressed and kinda disappointed. 

The sides I ordered looked like they were just stuffed in the boxes and were kinda cold. Also there wasn't much taste to any of them. I kept trying to mix them together to try to get them to taste good. I was really disappointed with the Mac & Cheese. 

The chicken was decent but not really that great. Again there just wasn't any flavor. I had the extra crispy chicken, and even though it was crunchy it didn't have much taste. 

If your staving then go but needless to say I'm not planning on going back anytime soon.

Super disappointed when I wanted to order and whopper with cheese, but was told the broiler was broke. I decided to go with the chicken sandwich meal. My chicken was something else... not crispy AT ALL, the breading was falling off the patty, and it was not very hot.. mind you I have a 15 min lunch brake and work right off Main Street so I had zero time to take anything back. I also decided to try the new taco(ya know see what the hype is about)... and LMAO.. what a joke. I'm not saying it's just this Burger King but to all burger kings the taco is absolutely disgusting. YOU DONT SERVE TACOS AT A BURGER PLACE!!!!! Hence the DAMN NAME! Thats why TACO BELL serves TACOS.. and Not BURGERS!

I love Chipotle.  I have supported them during their recent crisis without too much concern; these things happen.  The food I get from the grocery store is just as likely to make me ill.  Eating is dangerous business; the risk of choking, obesity, food-bourne illness, eating and driving, etc.  We all take that risk.

Anyway, this is my most-frequented Chipotle location.  I always get the same thing:  Salad with too much lettuce, no beans or rice, chicken and/or steak, guacamole, etc.  I have ordered this dozens of times.  I have received salad dressing 3 times.  

My last trip I specifically made it MY responsibility to make sure my salad dressing for my SALAD ended up at home with me.  I asked the young lady at the beginning of the line for salad dressing and she told me, "Oh, they have it at the end, too".  I stood there looking at it in front of her and couldn't, for the life of me, figure out why she can't just include it upon request. 

So, it's a hoppin' busy place and it's rushed as they push you down the line to the register.  So, making it *my* responsibility to get that coveted dressing that isn't given out upon request, I forget at the end of the line to ask.....AGAIN!  

I get home (20 minutes away) and find no dressing!  It is aggravating.  This time I called and spoke to the manager about this persistent issue.  They offer a salad.  The salad has one dressing to choose from.  The dressing is in plain view upon ordering.  It is not given upon request.  I can't figure this out.

Anyway, the manager apologized that they "forgot" to give me dressing.  Forgot?  No, they didn't give it to me even after requesting it.  Forgetting is what was done the past dozens of visits when I didn't remind them.

The manager was apologetic admitting that I wouldn't be able to fully enjoy my salad.  I'm not driving another 40-minute round-trip to get the dressing.  So, I am aggravated, again.  I eat the salad.  I always eat the salad.  I always order it again.  I suppose I have some responsibility in the matter.  I'm sure the dressing isn't too magical to figure out how to make it myself; probably a 30-second internet search would get it for me.  The question is, "Should I have to make my own salad dressing when it's plainly available at the counter where I order my salad?"

Yes, aggravating.

What can I say del tacos Del Taco. They did forget my drink and I was too busy on my phone to realize until I was already 5 miles away-and Almost Home I did call Lynn and they are willing to make it up to me which is a plus so they are OK and their food is OK

Couple of years back chipotle would be a great place to eat. This chipotle has been a disappointment for more than just once.
I highly doubt if I would go here after today. To start with - they were over with chorizo. One thing that would be good here. Ordered steak & chicken bowl. Chicken had pieces with only fat. Steak was over cooked & no flavor. No medium salsa! No trays to take for outside seating.

Ol Col. Sanders must be rolling in his grave at the atrocity his brand has become. This location is a perfect example. The kids here don't give a crap about how to make a finger lickin good chicken dinner. The drive through is in the business of paying it forward by giving you the next persons order. The interior is usually filthy with a mess of old, decaying extra dry chicken. The soda fountain is filthy. Overall just a poor location. If you want someone else's order and be shorted items in your own order when you back to make it right, this is your place. Otherwise, don't waste your time. 

PS- Don't try calling to let them know they messed up. The number doesn't work.

I been eating clean all week and had an urge for fast food. I admit it was 10:30 pm and I know it was late but really not that late. So I ordered two small fries one for myself and one for my friend and a whopper. The fries looked like they have been sitting there for days. They were cold and stale. I took a bite and it just ruined my appetite. I can see why Burger King is slowly going down hill. They won't last much longer if they don't make fresh food for customers. Take a look at this picture of the fries it's just beyond terrible. How can you mess up on fries? It's not rocket science Burger King!

I try to avoid fast food, and I rarely review fast food restaurants because most people know what to expect. However, I've been here a handful of times to try out their vegetarian food options, and I found the drive-thru service at this location to be generally fast and reasonable, and the food to be fine.

I had tried the cold brew, the Beyond sausage sandwich, and the southwest veggie sandwich at this location. The cold brew is nothing special, not discernable from regular iced coffee, but slightly more expensive. But the vegetarian breakfast sandwiches are very decent for fast food! The Beyond sausage sandwich is quite tasty, and taste-wise, tastes like a standard sausage breakfast sandwich, with some generic bread, egg, cheese, and all the standard seasonings of breakfast sausage. However, the texture of the Beyond sausage is clearly different from meat. I wouldn't recommend doing a taste comparison, because this will fail compared to pork sausage. The southwest veggie sandwich uses a veggie pattie  that has corn and black beans in it and is heavily spiced with cumin. If you eat it while the cheese is still warm and melty, it's pretty good, although again, the texture of the veggie patty is so smooth that you can definitely tell you aren't eating meat. 

If you are looking for calories to put into your body, and you are trying to reduce your meat consumption, going to Dunkin for one of their meat-less breakfast sandwiches is definitely a reasonable option, especially if you want to stay in your car.

I was hesitant about ordering take out during this quarantine but I decided I wanted some chipotle so why not. I was enjoying my sofritas bowl and was almost done with it. It tasted great but things changed after I took one of my last bites. I began chewing and noticed something wasn't right. I noticed that there was a piece of plastic wrap in my mouth. I found that absolutely disgusting. Even more disgusting given everything going on in our world. How can you be so reckless serving food and advertising free delivery to get more business and not even be cautious to what is going inside each meal.

Chipotle need I say more one of the best and freshest fast food Mexican restaurants a person can encounter, from their chicken to their steak to their shredded pork it is all delicious and fresh, with the variety of different salsas you can choose from always the best I've never had any problems with eating here, from soft tacos to hard tacos to their famous burrito bowl, and a price that's not too bad because you are guaranteed to be full is ever once you're done with your meal, I think I can visit chipotle once twice maybe even three times a week!

Have only visited this location once.  Chipotle is always one of our favorite places when in the mood. Can always count on fresh ingredients and savory flavors. I typically go for the Sofritas bowl which is basically marinated tofu crumbles in a savory sauce. Pair that with some fresh grilled veggies and mild salsa and sour cream and OMG. If you've never been to a Chipotle it's very similar to a Subway where instead of building your own sub, you build your own burrito or burrito bowl or salad or taco. They even have kids meals and even though not on the menu will do a quesadilla. Love their chips and guacamole -- best Guac in town!!

First time getting food from Chipotle and I was very disappointed. I ordered through Postmates, their delivery was fast but the quality of the food was poor. Queso was grainy and watery. The carnitas, rice and beans were dry and had no flavor. First and last time for me. Not impressed for the price.

The chick who threw a serious dollop of guac on top of my carnitas bowl is a winner in my book. Seriously, a solid 1.5 avocado dollop. We often find ourselves here after a BOGO coupon is thrown our way. Which seems to be quite often. 

This particular Chipotle is fairly new and conveniently located along the Cultural Trail in the heart of downtown (around the corner from Monument Circle). 

What to expect? Build-your-own bowls and burritos filled with white or brown rice, black beans, pinto beans, veggies, choice of protein or tofu, queso, etc. priced very reasonably at around $8 meal - often with plenty for leftovers. The staff has always been fairly friendly and you'll often find a line at this location given its hot proximity to several downtown offices. The space offers tall bars for eating, a handful of traditional tables and a few more tables out on the patio overlooking Washington Street.
I absolutely love this KFC they have delicious fried chicken and Mac and cheese. There is no better fried chicken than kfc

Minus 500 stars.  This is the worst KFC in America! After many battles with them today was the last straw .... ordered the new chicken sandwich and they gave me a sandwich with chicken tenders in it.  If that's your best to compete against Popeyes then let me tell you to get out of the chicken business.  Soggy glob of special sauce made the sandwich inedible! Tenders were dry and rubbery and it was 11:05 in the daytime.  As always the staff is sloppy and have the worst attitudes on the planet. Close shop , get out of the parish because you folks are horrible!!!! 2/11/21

Really dropping the ball lately. Use to be my main goto but lately its been bleh. 3x in a row now ive gone and either no sweet tea or it was absolutely disgusting! How can yall not keep an eye on it and refill when low? Im not waiting 15 mins when i ordered to go on the app. And dont bother asking for 2x the meat unless you ordering in store because you wont get squat if you order it as 2x on the app. Really disappointed the chorizo hasnt came back yet, the carne asade is dry, the carnitas is the best...when they put enough on the bowl. Topping are getting smaller and smaller with every visit

I hate that I have to do this, but I went and purchased dinner tonight for myself and my husband after work. I ordered a 3 piece original combo and a 10 piece hot chicken wings. I got my order and went home. KFC has been around, not this one I realize, but around since 1931 and for as long as I've been in the world which is almost 60 years and never have I had bad chicken at KFC until the past couple of months. Tonights purchase was so bad I could not eat the wings and the combo meal was just picked at due to more gristle than meat and what meat you could find was not cooked, still red and a bit bloody and the past couple of times it was the same and so we were apprehensive about going back tonight. This is the closest KFC to my location, but I won't be going back there anymore. I do not know what the deal is, but KFC please make sure that you purchase good chicken and not go for the cheaper chicken because you get what you pay for and cheaper IS NOT always better especially in food. If not for the patrons KFC would not be around. Just want you to be aware.

This is no chicken sandwich.   This is tenders on a sandwich.... My guess is they didn't want to cook the actual chicken patty, so they just threw on some tenders they had.

Still tasted pretty good though.

The chicken is the only reason why I come here, other than that the customer service is horrible.  Don't dine in or you'll regret it and never ask for a soda beverage because they are always out or it's flat. Drive through seems to be the safe choice here and carry cash just in case their credit/debit machine is out. Good luck!

Service is the worst to add on is the bad attitude of mgr.
When on Saturday at 6:38 pm and ordered whopper,3 tacos and 2 orders of mozzarella stick,everything but tacos put in bag.after 20 minutes the tacos was done.i told the mgr that I been waiting more then 20 minutes she was sorry,so was I everything now cold.they could care less about customers

I placed an internet order (so everything is literally spelled out on paper), I get home and every single bowl and burrito are WRONG. WRONG meat, wrong toppings wrong Everything!! Not to mention it was ICE cold. They didn't label a single item, so when I got home after a 5 hour flight I had to undo every singlw burrito to see the inside to know whose it was, to find out everything is wrong, then I had to heat all of it up. When I called, the manager LAUGHED on the phone, said he'd comp one bowl if I came back in and that was it, so $55 down the drain and have to cook dinner for my kids who won't eat the spicy meat that they were given instead of carnitas.
