And to top it off we had 3 bags of chips so stale they were inedible!!!! I am DISGUSTED by this location, their manager and their awful customer service.akes me say forget IT!

Not all fairy tales end happily ever after.

As a transplanted native son of the Midwest, one of the greatest shames in life was that, until today, I had never eaten at a White Castle. Back home in Texas, we do not have White Castle, except for the version found in the frozen food section at the supermarket. But those couldn't possibly be indicative of the real White Castle slider... could it?

Today, the 32 year stay in hamburger purgatory ended. While in town for a convention, my buddy and I arrived at the white faux-masonry mecca that is White Castle. We ordered the #7, which gave us 10 sliders, 2 orders of fries, and 2 sodas. As we drove back to our hotel, the anticipation built to a level I had not felt since Ralphie was about to take the first shot with his Red Ryder BB Gun in A Christmas Story. Finally, we arrived at the hotel, and eagerly dug into our long awaited fare. I took a slider out. I bit into it. I chewed. I swallowed. I died a little on the inside.

What I learned today is that the sliders from White Castle taste exactly like a reheated frozen mini cheeseburger that sat in the microwave for at least 5 minutes after it was done, and was then reheated a second time. I have no idea how they managed to achieve that consistency, because I saw them throw these things on the grill in the back. At least, I thought that's what I saw. Perhaps the entire thing is an intricate house of mirrors, and the entire production staff at White Castle is actually one guy with a microwave and an endless stack of frozen White Castle boxes. Regardless, their burgers were a lukewarm limp mess, and the aftertaste was so bad that it could not be alleviated until we busted out the fudgcicles we'd bought at the grocery store earlier.

If anything, today's White Castle experience was a wake up call after a 32 year nap. It goes to show you that just because something has a cult following, it doesn't mean it's any good. White Castle may be good after a long night of drinking, or after going 10 rounds with Puff the Magic Dragon. The reality though is that I'm not drunk, I'm not stoned, and White Castle tastes like a wet cardboard sandwich. And so, I sit here, a disillusioned soul with a bad taste in my mouth both literally and figuratively.  Perhaps one day White Castle will see the error of their ways and offer up a slider that is, in the very least edible. I hope that does happen, but unfortunately, it'll be at least another 32 years before I ever find out.

WTF. Really should be KF no C. 

Went tonight and was told "we are out of chicken. We have 1 chicken wing and nothing else now."  What kind of bizarro world bs is this?  A KFC with no chicken at 8:30 on a Friday night?  The colonel must be turning over in his grave. This franchisee is clearly a dufus for running this outfit into the ground. Corporate needs to pull this place and make it a corporate spot. Popeyes or PDQ it is. Bye colonel. I'm not here for your plastic mashed taters and rubber gravy.

I don't care about the calories, you can pull my twice-monthly whopper out of my cold, dead hands. When I partake in this delicious ritual, I always go to this location. They are quick, very polite and always get my order correct (which is complicated by a dairy allergy). Well done guys!
This location might not be in the best area but the chicken was good and not old which is a plus from KFC. They do have a parking lot but a lot of potholes so don't go flying in.

I have not been to a Burger King in years,. the unhealthy yet tempting Whopper being the main reason. Since it always TASTED good, I fell for the advertisement of the new "Impossible" Whopper. My opinion? while it tasted reasonably good, it is far from identical in satisfaction to my discerning taste buds to the real thing. It was also VERY expensive--$6! AND the clincher? It doesn't look ANYTHING like the bulging burger in the advertisements. Rather I was presented with a smallish,  unimpressive, flattened, processed wafer with some paltry tomato slices, a few onions, and shredded lettuce, by an unenthusiastic young millennial. No thanks BK! Fooled me once, but not a second time!

I don't think I've ever gone here and them get my order right. I rarely eat KFC, but when I do I'd like to at least get the items I ordered.

I went here Sunday in the mood for something homey and greasy. They had me repeat my order 4 times and then they still gave me the wrong food. How does mashed potatoes translate to potato wedges 4 times? Beats me. The chicken tenders were also so old they were as tough as beef jerky. Yuck!

Since I don't eat it often, I keep thinking, "Well, it's been awhile... maybe they've gotten better". Fat chance. The last 3 times I've been they've messed my order up in some major way. Think it's safe to say I'll mark this KFC off my splurge list... at least until they get their (you know what) together.

This past Friday night my husband was there for To Go orders. There was a wait so he was offered something to drink. We had ordered the grilled chicken and it didn't turn out right. Rather than send out pour quality we were asked to wait a little longer. For our inconvenience we were given a free cake. We had a wonderful dinner and wonderful service. That's what quality control is all about! We will definitely be back. Thank you!

If I could give this zero stars, I would.  This was absolutely the worst KFC I have ever been to - and I am grading it based on the buffet - so not sure if you order off the menu.  Went for dinner with family and we all got the buffet.  Wow - this has soured me on eating at any KFC for a long time.  Everything on the buffet was horrible.  The chicken was greasy and dry, the mashed potatoes and macaroni and cheese looked like they had been sitting out for days, there were 2 biscuits out that could have been used as weapons they were so hard, weird cabbage, weird broccoli cheese stuff a blackberry cobbler that was awful.  If this is how they are going to run a buffet - they need to stop right now!

Not generally into chain restaurants, but I did enjoy this visit to Chipotle. I ordered a burrito with pinto beans, carnitas, chicken, lettuce and brown rice. Because I cannot stand cilantro- they gave me rice without the ingredient in it.  I like that they will do that to accommodate their customers.

I ordered a Clementine Izze to drink.

The burrito was flavorful especially with their hot salsa! Delicious! 

Service was fast and courteous! Just as it should be. The ingredients are fresh as well! An enjoyable lunch.

Just a chicken tender enthusiast here. I had never had KFC's tendies before and they did not disappoint. What DID disappoint was the fact that I ordered fries and my fry supply was sparse upon opening the box. I consider myself a level headed person, but when I looked at that fry container, something terrifying emerged from deep in my soul. The urge to write a yelp review. Did I ever think I would sink so low? No. But did I ever expect to open a bag and see a half-empty fry container? CERTAINLY NOT.

Disappointing is the best description of a quick lunch here. I could see the drive through was busy, it was a football Sunday and there was a steady stream of cars. That was not an excuse for not getting dine in orders dine correctly and promptly. An order that was supposed to be a breast, a thigh and leg was a leg and thighs and the meat was unusually tough. The gravy on the mashed potatoes was put on so far in advance it formed a skin. The Hot Chicken was good but I would have preferred the Honey Hot Chicken that was ordered. I won't be returning here.

Really crappy place. Messed my order up just gave me cheese on my whopper and nothing else even though I did not order it that way I don't know if it was a girl taking my order for the people that either way my sandwich was a mess and only a cheese on it never will go here again

If I could leave a 0 star review I would. My household has had food poisoning twice from this Carl's Jr in the last few months. With another recent review echoing the same thing, it's a bit concerning.

Location isn't great, but I've never been disappointed after leaving.  The service is quick and overall pretty friendly.  I'm used to KFCs closing at 10pm, but it's nice that this one is open later.  Must share that I tried the new boneless chicken and it's absolutely amazing.  Tastes just like the fried chicken you're used to, but you're not maneuvering around bones and you don't need to use a fork or knife.  It also gives you more original recipe flavor in every bite which seems to pack more spice than I remembered.  Was super pumped when I opened the box and saw I got an extra piece.  Pieces are really big too.  Nothing like chicken tenders/chicken strips in the least.  Really recommend you try them if you're a fan of KFC.

Ok not even sure where to start on this one..

Been coming to this place for about 2 years because it's the only place with decently healthy food close to my work at the price. 

It's Chipotle but I would avoid this place at all costs...

- There is a bigger lady that seems to manage the cash register daily and she might be the manager. Anyway. I've watched her cuss at customers, talk bad to other customers about a customer as they would leave. She has not charged some customers for drinks because she was too lazy to redo the register. She will do other tasks and totally ignore you for up to 5min at the register at times because she only has 2 or 3 people in line. 

Beyond that individual who is most likely most of the issue I have also seen the following...

-This location has had a handful of employees smoking weed behind the restaurant in full uniform. 

-I watched everyone in line wait 10 min because 1 employee threw a sauce on the other as a joke. They both scrubbed down the employee laughing as we all waited. The worst part was there were 9 employees visible and no one said anything or adjusted to serve the customers. 

-Most employees wear headphones while they work here. They will mess up your order then get rude when they have to go back and remake your food because they didn't hear you. 

-Online orders are labeled "ready for pick up" but they actually hadn't even been created. I listened to an employee say "we don't have chicken right now? Ok then I guess they will just get steak" when referring to an online order. So in other words they intentionally got the order wrong and it's obviously they are intentionally telling people their order is ready even though it isn't. 

-When you ask to speak with someone who runs the place you are handed a card that directs you to a line that won't actually get you in touch with anyone. 

It is quite possibly the worst run facility. After waiting 20 min for food and watching the lady at the register yell at 2 customers today I have decided I will not be returning for good. 

If this happens to reach the owner. I would quickly make some changes before you have many more like me who will just flat refuse to come here. It's not the food. Its not the facility. It's 100% the people you have hired.

Was the only car in drive thru for lunch. Ordered whopper w/cheese, stressed no lettuce, as I'm allergic to lettuce, and 10 chicken nuggets. I work less than a mile from there, and my food was already cold, the burger was covered in lettuce and the nuggets were cold as ice. The crew working there looked dirty, clothes were ragged, no hair nets, no plastic gloves. Just dirty looking all around the parking lot. Will NEVER go there again

I don't think this is one of the "Flagship" locations for Burger King, but it's close to home and i had craving for a Whopper.  I walked in during the lunch rush and was in and out in 10 minutes.  I was surprised, but it is what it is.  The Whopper was what I expected.

I have come to this chipotle multiple times since I live right down the street. Every time they mess something up, are missing something or go SO SLOW!!! I went today and got extremely burnt chips, EXTREMELY BURNT, they are were awful. Also they gave me Barbacoa instead of carnitas and it was terrible and not what I wanted at all. PLEASE DO BETTER!!!!! I love chipotle but this place makes me not want it again.

Ordered a whopper, onion rings and small drink to go. The inattentive server asked if i said fries, i said no i wanted onion rings. I do not like their fries so i never order them. When i got home with carryout order i had fries and the receipt also said fries, which i threw in the trash. There wasn't another customer there at 6:30 pm on a Wednesday so they weren't swamped.

Just your average KFC, but the food is always amazing love everything me and my grandmother are here sometimes but the wait can be long some times, last few times my grandmother wasn't very happy since the people couldn't understand her and she always wants me to make sure they got the order right,  one time she became inpatient that I had to go get the food inside but it's okay the food is still very good as advertised the waffle and chicken was better than expected,
The whopper here is made succulent and the fries are to die for. Don't forget a delicious cup of their iced coca cola.

The reviews here are not joking. Consistently, this particular KFC is one of the absolute worst in terms of service quality. I've had parts of orders forgotten almost every time. I've also showed up on a Sunday at 6pm about a month ago to be told they were out of original recipe chicken. 

How the hell does a KFC run out of its signature item for Sunday dinner? 

But they get a lot of business, mainly because they're the closest KFC in a busy area. I just love KFC itself too much and this is the closest outlet to my house.  That is why I even bother.  I came here today, and waited 25 minutes in the drive thru on a Sunday evening.  I should have turned around and went elsewhere upon seeing the line of about 8 cars. 

That's right. It took them 25 minutes to get 8 cars through. 

It's so bad here that I'm definitely considering driving out of my way to another KFC if I want my fix.

A fast food establishment that never disappoints me. I love the Double Del Burger. This time I tried the Taco Burger. Taco meat with cheese lettuce and red sauce in a bun. Not on the menu but you can ask for. Delish! I am obsessed with their fries, another delish. I've had every taco they make and for fast food it isn't bad. They do use fresh ingredients and you can taste that in the food.

This is the worst Dunkin Donuts.  Twice now I have ordered an everything bagel w/ cream cheese and I ended up with a sausage and mushy egg sandwich.  They repeat the order back, and get the coffee order right, why do they get the food wrong?  And the first time in addition to giving me an incorrect order, they smashed 6 muffins into a box obviously meant for 4, and one female staffer had her mask down under nose, not cool.  I left a message for the mgr but nobody called back.  I let it go.  Today I was just going to eat the egg sandwich even though its not what I wanted.  I needed breakfast and didn't have time to go back.  The egg is so mushy and disgusting its inedible.  I can't believe people voluntarily order this.  I have had better food in the hospital.  Probably prison food is even better.  And the cashier had her mask under her chin while she hands me my order and coffee and thanks me.  Oh and prior to that i had to see her bend over and expose the top of her green/blue thong underwear. Thanks for the visual!
del taco has really gone down hill. charge normal prices for less and tossed together food! i won't even feed my dog this mess. what the heck?!?!?!

Good service, but limited bagel selection. Not sure why everyone rips on DD, the bagels and coffee are great. Can't say much for the donuts. And I will knock the new sausage pancake bites, not worth the money.

Oh boy. Where to start. 

This Burger King is not exactly the fastest. We started off in the drive thru but that didn't move for about 5 minutes until we decided to go inside the restaurant. Then it turned into a real nightmare (Halloween pun).

It was not busy at all.. there was only 1 other person inside. Bathrooms legit smelled like someone smoked a pack of cigarettes. Staff seemed very uninterested to be working. All the surfaces around the drink area were sticky. We still waited about 10 minutes inside for our food. They set out our fries and onion rings before they even started the burgers so as soon as we finally got those they were cold. 

I got a whopper jr and the bun and cheese were still cold. My boyfriend wanted to try the limited time only Nightmare King Burger (don't ask me why..) and I know food typically does not look like the pictures (I worked in fast food for 5 years) but this was pretty off. He said the taste was okay but overall presentation was pretty off-putting. 

This was not my first time at this location, and I was trying to find positive things to point out but so far it's been a struggle. Last several times my order was wrong, however today there was slight redemption as my order was right. I really hate to post a bad review but I'm hoping several of these issues  can be addressed in order to improve the experience for customers.
I love the impossible whopper and wish I could get it more often, but this is the nearest BK and they consistently burn it or get the order wrong. So sad.
This is a clean and well manage Burger King.  The staff is polite and efficient.  Besides the impossible burger tastes great, it was neatly wrapped.

Ordered a Sausage Egg and Cheese.  The shift manager, Barbara I believe, was friends with the people behind me.  She ended up giving my sandwich to her friend, for free, and forgetting to make mine again.  Stood there 20 minutes before anyone helped me.

One of the employees take my receipt, comes back with the food about 3 minutes later.  No apologies.  I prompted further, asking if they were okay with a customer waiting 20 minutes, he replies "Oh well, what to do you want me to do about it".

Complete garbage customer service, that starts at the top, with the manager/shift leader being more interested in giving away free food to her friends, at the time and expense of paying customers. 

Avoid at all costs.

I go here for whopper Wednesday ok for the price  the self serve soda is too high tech and had problems when I was there could not use it had to get soda from counter on the back it's ok when it works and can chose from many drinks but when it is time to reset it or add refill just too long to reboot.

This continues to be the worst kfc I have ever been to. I don't now how it is still open, wait 30 minutes for a 12 piece meal and the photo shows the actual size of a chicken leg?! Pathetic! People were coming in from drivethru complaining they have been there for twenty minutes, the line wasn't long. Owner was there and didn't care we were waiting for so long. Everything seemed very unorganized. Looked like elementary school kids were running the kitchen.

Steak burrito was cold, like they put in the fridge for 40 minutes cold! I know parts of a big ass burrito like this have a lot of cold items in them, like lettuce, salsa, guacamole and sour cream, BUT DAMN! Aldo, my sons chicken bowl rice, rice was crunchy, like dried meal worm crunchy.
Do not recommend!

Well today it should be called "Cheapotle" we have eaten here a few times but today I got the smallest $9 burrito ever! My iPhone was actually bigger than the burrito, well it's an 11 so...... seriously a few beans, steak, cheese and salsa. No bigger than your hand and $9. The others had bowls, the bowls were all a fair value for the content.  Needless to say I wouldn't pick it  for my Sunday choice and hope the rest of the family don't when it's their choice again.

Let me start by saying I am a lifer to the castle. I absolutely love White Castle food. But I dislike the service. Why is the drive thru so slow?? Most of the time the people are nice the line is just so long. But as far as reviewing the food. It's amazing. Who doesn't love a good ole slider and some cheese sticks?? Yum yum

Why do I keep coming back here? 

Well, that's easy.  If I need a KFC fix, this is the closest one to me.  So I've put up with their mistakes and horribly mismanaged store for a while.  

But last week did me in forever.  When you order a 10 piece bucket of grilled chicken, and you get home to find 8 pieces in it, that's the last straw.  

The people who work here should be embarrassed.  The people who run this location should be more embarrassed.  I've had more mistaken orders here than anywhere else.  

Don't trust this place.  They've done nothing to earn any trust.
White rice, black beans, fajita carnitas with hot salsa. #amazing

I ordered two burrito bowls on their app for delivery (so the order is in writing) and they got one of my bowls COMPLETELY wrong. Ordered half steak half chicken: bowl only had steak. Ordered half white half brown rice: only white rice. Ordered half black beans half pinto beans: only black beans. Ordered lettuce: nowhere to be found. Like CANT YOU READ?! I'm looking at the order receipt right now and it CLEARLY states what I wanted.
Then when I called about it they asked me to come to the store to get it fixed. Why would I pay the $3.99 in delivery if I'm just gonna have to go to the store anyways? Don't waste your time with this store. Go somewhere where the employees aren't illiterate.

Yet another terrible fast food joint.

When I eat unhealthy, I want it done right, tasty, fatty, sweet, savory and salty to make up for the days the food is taking off my life.

But what did I get?  Soggy chalupas with dated lettuce. Biscuits not as buttery as I am used to (but at least not stale). Fried chicken kind of average, lukewarm and not 'extra crispy' as ordered. Soda a little "off" tasting. If you come here, expect to be disappointed.

Guess I will just have to go get some of that Popeye chicken sandwich instead.

Service also slow and not entirely the friendliest. They make sure to take their good old time preparing your order.

While I often feel bias when it comes to Burger king because I am the ultimate whopper fan this location exceeds my expectations every time! I often let bad service or poor quality of food fog my love for Burger King at other locations being a huge sucker for a whopper but I am very impressed by this location. I have been here multiple times and my food is always so fresh and I always receive the best customer service. A friendly smile at the window and always a kind interaction. I love this BK.

I'm writing this a day after we ate there. Everyone in our party who ate there was sick. The chicken was bad.  After we ate a few bites we all realized it was bad. Even the breading was sour. The service was Slow. Very slow. I wouldn't say rude, but I would say " hood". You can definitely see why Canes is superior in service. 
I guess who ever owns this place just doesnt care or has given up.
I would fire everyone and hire older folks.
They are going to kill someone by serving this old bad chicken. 
Stay far away. As far as you can!
Drink is included so you are force to drink soda. If you buy 5 to go products, you will also have 5 drinks. This is ridiculous. They also took off the Nashville hot chicken

On 09/21/16: Horrible, disgusting, pathetic excuse for a food establishment!
I ordered a 16 piece chicken meal that comes with 4 large  sides & 8 biscuits.  I asked for half (8pcs) original and half (8 pcs) grilled chicken.  I asked for my 4 sides to be 2 mashed potatoes/gravy, 1 corn, 1 mac & cheese.  When I got home which is 25 minutes away from this location, I was missing a mashed potatoes, missing 8 pieces of original chicken, and missing 8 pieces of grilled chicken.  They gave me 16 pieces of extra crispy chicken that was old, stale, and hard as if it had been microwaved 3 or 4 times to reheat it.  In addition to not receiving the 16 pieces of chicken I ordered and paid $45 for, there were no breasts in the bucket!  They gave me 4 dry legs, 2 hard wings, and 10 hard & dry thighs!  The chicken was inedible as it was old, stale, dry, and hard (it went in the garbage).  I was absolutely livid! When I pay $45.00 for 16 pieces of chicken, I expect to receive the fresh, edible chicken I ordered and paid for.  I want my money back and will never visit this location again. I should have taken heed to all of the negative Yelp reviews this location has received but no, I thought I would be lucky and perhaps get a decent meal.  I couldn't have been more wrong.  This is highly unacceptable and needs to be addressed by the owner.  This location is horrendous according to other customers.  I am new to this area and this was my 1st visit and last visit to this location.  Fix your issues and provide me the customer service I deserved on this visit!

it's a white castle, you can never expect anything great but I was craving it. On my 3rd slider out of four I see a long black hair coming out of the cheese, practically melted into the burger. Needless to say I won't be coming here again.

For years, there is one restaurant that friends insist you must try. This establishment then gains popularity at a rapid rate and becomes one of the more lucrative businesses of its variety. You try to avoid it, because you get distressed at the hype it receives and generally phenomenon that is admired by the general public you find loathsome. 

Well, that was what Chipotle was for me, for a countless amount of years. EVERYONE loved it. 

"Jason, you've never had Chipotle? Are you a hellion? What kind of crackpot are you?"

Of course, that was generally greeted with the typical eye-roll followed by a comment such as,

"Why would I eat Chipotle when I dwell in Tucson, a city known for blue-chip Mexican fare?"

Stupid me. Chipotle should not ever be compared to any sort of Mexican grub. Ever. 

It's really in a class of its own. 

I usually order a steak bowl with brown rice, pinto beans, hot and mild salsa, cheese and lettuce. I top it with a healthy does of tobasco and order a bag of chips to snack on in addition to my bowl. I don't thing there is a precedent when it comes to the menu, however. They keep the menu pure, rendering the absolute most out of a select few ingredients. 

I adore the steak, but you will triumph when you order any meat at all. Chicken, barbacoa and carnitas are all choice selections that will fill the void / craving in your stomach. With my steak bowl, I like utilizing the tortilla chips to concoct a big taco salad. I have always manufactured a de facto taco salad with carnitas and barbacoa and the conclusion was equally appetizing.

This brand new branch of Chipotle is pristine, which is essentially par for the course. Chipotle debunked every stereotype I had established for chain restaurants many years ago when I finally relinquished to peer pressure and made the equitable accord to investigate their product further. As it turns out, Chipotle proved to be one of the best chain restaurants in the history of mankind. 

Oh, and another thing: If I hear you pronounce it "CHI-POLE-TEE" you are asking for a slap in the mouth.

The worst KFC location ever
Order was messed up, chicken was so dry and greasy.
Will never go back

Once again I tried to give the once great KFC a chance but of course they let us down! After the Saints game we ordered a 16 piece family meal with 4!sides and biscuits.... $44 in total.  We received our food and got home only to see that the Original Recipe chicken we ordered was not fulfilled and instead of the ORIGINAL RECIPE we were given the crunchy instead... If some reading this think that chicken is chicken sadly they are mistaken. Original by far is the essence of KFC and that's what has made them a success all over the world. The crunchy  chicken is just not the same. !! Period.  How hard was that to get wrong.   ??? Biscuits were so raw that they all stuck together in the box . Macaroni and cheese was one solid piece of overcooked pasta ... I'm an Executive Chef and would volunteer to come and help train your staff, because someone there isn't doing their job! This KFC has literally forced me ( when I get a KFC craving) to go to the north shore to buy my meal. Maybe it's just the folks up there pay attention to detail! DONT WASTE YOUR TIME AT this KFC AND ITS ABOUT TIME TO HAVE THEM PICK UP AND LEAVE A PRIME REALISTATE SPOT THAT COULD BE BETTER USED BY A BETTER PROVIDER!

This is the first time I came to this Burger King and I won't be returning. We ordered the fiery chicken fries which weren't Spicy. They were out of Dr. Pepper. I ordered a simple whopper with no tomato or lettuce, add Bacon. There's was tomato and lettuce on it, and no Bacon, which I was paid extra for. The food was cold and which we ate as soon as we got home but it was literally only a 5 minute drive, it shouldn't be cold so soon. The girl at the drive thru had a hard time comprehending our simple order which we had to repeat and clarify multiple times. I used to work in fast food and I try to be patient with fast food restaurants because I know it isn't the easiest or best place to work, but this visit was sub par and the food was not as good as it typically is. Don't get me wrong, I love burger King and will still be going back, but definitely not back to this location.

I stand firm on my other review drive-through is a joke. Only reason im here is the only drive-through you can get in without waiting 20 minutes to get quality food. That's why I got subpar food. Just saying if the impossible whopper taste like beef are you putting that on all my hamburgers.? We never know. I'll take McDonald's pink meat over this any day. And once again if you look at my other review a manager don't care because they are not there. I do leave good positive reviews but not for this one and it will be my last post about this restaurant, cause I walk across the street and go to Boston market..Actually anyway with some money can tear that shit down And build aChick-fil-A and can bankrupt all the other businesses.lol

Visiting Nashville from Chicago. Been a fan of Chipotle since it opened in Colorado. This is the worst Chipotle I've ever been. Service was awful. Messy counter and condiment area. Rude employees. The person in front of me ordered to go bowl with steak which was run out. The server scrapped the container and found 3 pieces of steak, put them over the rice in the bowl while waiting for the new load of steak. Mine was ready but it sat there next to the steak bowl and I had to wait too. I mentioned 'it's to go' twice since I didn't see the lid under the bowl. The server answered rudely: 'I know!' Guess what? The person in front of me and I had to ask for the lid again at the cashier. Back to my place, not surprisingly, the chicken was not grilled well, the sour cream was soupy, the rice was sticky! Yuck!

Ever since I got a mailbox in Reno, Del Taco been blowin it up with coupons ... well, it's convenient to my regular rounds anyway, so why not stop by and check it out.

Itemized Breakdown of "dollar menu" and frequent bo-go stuff:

Macho Taco: Like a Taco Bell crunchy but with a bigger shell, a little more beef, sour cream and fresher-looking tomatos. Kinda sloppy and pain in the butt to eat, but really not bad. With a BoGo coupon its a good deal for $1 each.

1/2 Lb. Bean and Cheese Burrito: Well ... it's as advertised. There are in fact beans and cheese in a tortilla. They put green or red sauce in, but it's barely noticable. El Pollo Loco's RBC is way better. 

Classic Grilled Chicken Burrito: Lotta sour cream if you're into that. Basic burrito with Taco Bell caliber chicken. $3 is a little much for it but 2 for $3 is cheap OK filler

Grilled Chicken Taco: Not bad for $1. Like a soft taco version of the above burrito. Decent size for the price.

Strawberry Lemonade: Surprisingly good for fast food, sweet strawberry chunks all in the bottom. The $1 is just enough to wash down a meal if you're not dying of thirst. Pleasant on a hot day. 

Iced Tea: Much bigger than the lemonade for $1, but the ice tea is kept in an unrefrigerated container, thus not exactly "iced" :( Also unsweetened

Double Del Cheeseburger: Tastes like a thinner Wendy's double without the mayo and mustard. $3 for one is a little much but 2 for $3 w/ coupon is alright for cheapo burger craving

Flatbread Chicken / Steak Tacos: Like a non-fried, less fatty Taco Bell gordita. Meat wasn't great but they're not bad, $2.59 for 2 w/ coupon plus a 75 cent taco made for a pretty filling meal for 3.50

Chipotle Beef Taco: A Taco Bell regular taco w/ Subway chipotle sauce squirted in. That's what this is. 75 cents a nice price but I'd rather just a plain taco, the cheap flavor didn't do it for me.

Macho Nachos: Ahh ... these are the nachos of my youth ... the nachos I have long sought! These are like high school cafeteria nachos, all gooped up in hot Cheez Wiz and sour cream, ones on the bottom getting all soaked and losing their structural integrity. I get a perverse craving for these Beavis and Butthead Nachos sometimes. 

Honestly the food is a lot of 3* at best, with some 2* mixed in, but what kicks it up to 4* for me is the price with all these coupons and sales always going on, combined with an amazingly clean interior and amazingly competent, fast and polite staff. I also never see the Viriginia Street Bummage in here spanging and sucking around the way they do over at El Pollo. 

Then you use coupons, and they ply you with Moar bogo coupons with the meal. Plus Tue. and Thu. after 3 pm are "taco night", just over a buck for 3 regular tacos, just over two bucks for 3 of the grilled chicken tacos. Not even sure how they are even making money at this point. Love it though.
Imagine this... you roll up to KFC and they are out of Chicken!  It's 6pm on a Tuesday and they are out of Chicken and corn too apparently. I guess the other reviews were right!  Won't be going back.

Where to begin; I've been coming here for years ordering the same exact thing every time I come. A chicken quesadilla, side of queso, 2 large bags of chips and 3 sides of steak on soft shells. It's a weird order, I know.
This visit was very different. They told me that if I get a quesadilla it has to have two sides, so I thought whatever I'll just get rice and beans if I have to, but it was the cashier who wouldn't ring it up as normal. I got charged for the sides that I didn't even ask for but was told I have to get. Then he wouldn't charge me for the 3 sides of steak, which typically comes up to around $11. Instead, he charged me for full orders of steak tacos. It jacked the prices up. I tried to explain that this isn't normal and it's literally just 3 sides of steak, I order it all the time but he said since it's on a shell and not in a bowl it's considered tacos, not sides of steak.

Just very frustrating to see my normal meal Jack up in price by $14. The chips were also surprising stale.

Food is always consistently good here unlike other Chipotles in town.  Employees are nice and get you through the lines in record time.  The place is usually clean unlike others in town again.  I can trust the steak here vs others in town.

They have changed their black bean supplier.  These black beans are now crunchy when warm and gut wrenching when eaten as leftovers.  I recommend getting the pinto beans instead.

I don't eat a ton of fast food fried chicken but sometimes it's just what you want and need. Of course every time I succumb to this desire I have such a bad experience I don't go back. For years! Then I forget and do it again. So two weeks ago I go for a $20 bucket. 4 biscuits, mashed potatoes, coleslaw, and 8 pieces of chicken. It's a good value. Except! I get home 4 legs and 4 thighs . Guess what I don't eat. I want wings or breast. I under stand it's supposed to be 2-2-2-2 . Basically you are buying a chicken.  By the way this happened exactly the same two years ago. So I write the complaint line and yesterday ( two weeks later) they send me a form letter telling how sincerely sorry they were. I'm definitely using this as a case study for my management classes. I don't believe they give a shit. Not even a tiny little one.

Horrible. I love chipotle but hate this location. I went here about a month ago and got a steak quesadilla that gave me food poisoning. I tried it again just today. Let's hope I don't get sick. On top of that, the staff is braindead and they are skimpy on the meat. I highly don't recommend.

It's Sunday noon and I want some lunch. I am coming up to a Burger King and a whopper sounds good to me. Masked up I aaproach the lobby door...a sign says..."Inside for carry out only". I have no problem with that. But...the door is locked. I finally go through the drive-thru, wasting mor time than I want to, and I get my food. I ask...why the locked door? The answer..."We are understaffed"......really!!??!!
Come on Mr Manager...why aren't you doing your job?? I won't be back until this place get straightened out....

This Del Taco is my favorite. I'm not rating it 5 stars for the food, although it is always fresh and tasty but still normal fast food. I'm rating it 5 stars because the manager and the staff of this store are the friendliest I've ever encountered. The staff at the counter are always polite and pleasant. I come here a couple times a month because it's convenient and I am always so impressed with the staff when I come. I think it comes down from management so kudos to the management!

Yesterday I went to this KFC with a coupon for 10 pieces of chicken.  I arrived at opening time--10:30 a.m.  First, let me say that the place was filthy, and I should have taken a cue from that and turned around.  But I didn't.  Upon presenting the coupon--after waiting 10 minutes with no one offering to wait on me--I was told that it would be 20 minutes before the chicken was ready.  I told them I would wait.  Twenty minutes passed, then thirty, then forty.  When I asked about why it was taking so long, I was answered rudely by a variety of the employees.  Finally, at 11:15, I got my order and went home.  When I got home, I discovered that there were only eight "pieces" in the bucket, when I had paid for 10.  I called them and reported this, and they said if I came back they would give me two pieces.  I told them there was no way I was going to do that, and the guy said, Well, have a nice day then.  I put "pieces" in quotes, because they were so small--actually parts of pieces--that they were hardly worth eating.  All in all, this was the most awful experience I've ever had at a fast-food place.  This filthy store, full of rude, surly employees, should be closed down.  I, certainly, will not be going back--not just to this particular store, but to any KFC.  They should be ashamed.

My family and I were in the process of moving. We decided to go to Burger King since we needed a quick bite to eat. As I was eating my whooper that I had cut in half, I noticed a piece of hair in the middle of my sandwich as I pulled it apart. I didn't think hair was one of the ingredients in a whopper. I am officially grossed out and probably won't be eating there again

waited in drive thru line for 10 minutes then backed out and went to mcdonalds
only reason i went here was location 
been there and they were out of chicken no reg or crispy but no chicken
another time green beans o were out k corn on the cob o were out
and the popular chicken will be up in 3 minutes they take your money and ten minutes later it'll be 3 minutes WTF
have had many bad Experiences and will not go back
staff is slow stupid an ignorant and should be euthanized 
I know this sound cruel but the world will  be a better place
GOBA mid-day strategy meeting, spicy! Brad said steak was chewy, chicken was fine. Rewards card needed but they do serve ZERO. #in

After doing some shopping with my friend we decided that grabbing something to eat would be our next best step. There were a few choices close bye, but we decided on Burger King. My friend ordered The Original Chicken Sandwich. That's her favorite and she liked hers and ate it all. I ordered the Whopper. I have always liked them because of all of the stuff they put on them and the flavor of the burger. The last one I had wasn't very good and I thought it was a fluke so I ordered one again today. The flavor was good, but the meat itself was dry. It's pressed hard and dry and cooked to death. When I forget how they have changed my Whopper, I might return for another one.

Standard fare at any Chipotle, which I love. My regular is:
Burrito bowl with tortilla in side
Mixed white and brown rice with extra brown rice instead of beans
Half chicken and half steak ( for the price of steak)
2 scoops pico
2 scoops corn
Lettuce on top
The servings were very generous, and I felt like it was my birthday (I have low expectations)

From the moment I stepped into line I was greeted with smiles and "good morning." Everyone was super nice, the cashier even offered to put lemon in my cup of water. 

Counters, cooking tops, and service areas were all clean. 

The dining area is the King of Prussia mall food court which is newly renovated and very nice and clean. There's usually some pop music playing overheard, and it can get a little loud and noisy during peak lunch hours. 

Enjoy!

JUST went through the drive thru, got a chicken breast meal with slaw, mashed potatoes & a biscuit..... get all the way home & my potatoes & biscuit are not in there, and those were the 2 things i wanted most........ i am pregnant....... NOT HAPPY!

I have been coming here for a while. The place is clean, the food is fresh and accurate. It's as good as it get for a KFC so yep 5 Stars. Although why does this place have such a low star rating? At the time of this review it's only 1 1/2 star establishment. It definitely deserves more than that. It's not as bad as some places in the city, it's not even bad at all. So why such a low score? 

This place suffers from the 1-review yelper. People in the suburbs who only review things when they are bad, it's unfortunate. 

I came here a few times over the years, truth be told because the last KFC by me (in the gallery mall) shut down years ago. I have come here when we were expecting blizzards the next day, I have come hear close to closing, and I have come here during lunch. My experiences were as follows:

It has always been clean. Sometimes the service is slow for fast food, but it's not slow in the usual sense of the word. If you come 1 hour until closing they are going to run out of certain pieces of chicken. They have always gotten my order right. The chicken is always fresh and hot. 

In short, I have always enjoyed my visits here, that's why I make the drive from Old City out to this KFC in Delaware County. That should speak volumes to the service and quality at this location.

New Product Review: "Beyond Famous Star" Meatless Hamburger.

This review's mostly about Carl's Jr.'s new meatless hamburger product.  This location was chosen out of convenience to sample the promotion. 

So, purchase a large beverage and receive a free Beyond Famous Star meatless hamburger for uttering the simple phrase "Beyond Famous Upgrade" on Aug. 15. Sounded like a pretty good deal!  And I'm a burger fanatic.

Looking at the patty, it looks just like a regular meat patty remarkably. And, on first couple bites, tastes the same. However, about half way thru the burger, that's when your senses kick in a tell you this is an "imposter" burger.

I mean, it wasn't BAD, just doesn't deliver that same satiation (satisfaction) level that a heavenly Super Star with cheese delivers. The plant base pea protein used in the burger also left me with a mildly unpleasant aftertaste and indigestion-acid reflux shortly afterwards. Typically, I don't get this with the meat version, so I found this odd.

Summary: BIG Kudos to Carl's Jr. for thinking outside the box and attempting a meatless substitution. I think the formula needs some more tinkering however.

Thr drive thru staff were fast & friendly at this location and everything else looked up to par.

I've been looking for a KFC with beyond chicken and stopped in here. Line was long but service was friendly. The chicken nuggets were warm and moist and had this amazing flavor. Tastes and looks like chicken. I am sold. Same size and regular nuggets. I took the chicken nuggets to go and didn't eat them for 20 mins and by then they were not crunchy but they were oh so good. Only issue is these are addicting.  First the first time I didn't even care about sides I just wanted more nuggets. They got this right.
Why does the whopper not automatically come with cheese? so you can charge 70 cents more?  go to hell.

I will NEVER return to this Chipotle location even though it is the closest one to my house. I will drive across town to a better one if I have to. (Actually, my extremely disappointing experience at this location, is the REASON I just started a Yelp account). The girls on the assembly line were no older than 18, and were more interested in gossiping loudly about the last customer's special order, than asking what I was ordering in the moment. This is not indicative of all young people in a work setting--it's a reflection of the management and the moral that the person in charge does (or clearly did not, in this situation) impart. 

When I asked why I was being charged extra for guacamole (and a tiny scoop at that), the cashier rolled her eyes, signed annoyingly at me, and said that I could have that OR a protein...but I didn't order a protein (i.e. meat or sofritas). She continued to argue with me, until I finally insisted that she actually look at what I had ordered. She gave no apology. 

Before I was even done paying, she berated a co-worker who was clearly older than her, for mis-ordering cokes....the week before, and spent about a minute arguing with him. He looked totally deflated. Whoever the manager is here needs to give their staff a reality check about overall kindness and hospitality to themselves and their customers. 

I also ordered chips, which were clearly leftover from lunch, and either undercooked or stale. Get with it guys!

Gave me bacon when I ordered sausage over cooked it to the point where I could not eat it for 10 minutes then when I could it was rock hard and gave me expired chocolate milk with chunks in it..... The hashbrowns were edible though

Never going here again and I just called the corporate office today to tell them I can always get a taco somewhere else. Four weeks ago I placed an order at this location and as I was answering the person questions who took my order the assistant manager shuts the drive thru window in my face. A week ago I stop in on a Friday and go inside and specifically order the fries well done it was even on the receipt. I get home the fries were not well done they were more on the cold side. The lady manager there calls my house this week to tell me she will replace my four orders of fries. I get there an hour ago and they only want to replace only two. This location also called me a liar three years ago when I tried redeeming a free shake coupon for my birthday and said I had used it already. I have been eating at Del Taco since Barstow in 1975 but no more I am done with the bad serve and messed up orders the last three years. I can always get a taco elsewhere.
The impossible burger is great yummy the staff was polite parking is good and the location is good

Almost everything is dairy free, soy free and I've never had an issue with service.

I was shocked when I saw the low ratings for this Chipotle. To me, they are almost all the same. I think where my experiences have differed from others is I usually go on a weekend when it isn't that busy. I did work near International Plaza eons ago so I remember what the lunch rush looked like. On the weekends, it is much emptier. 

Most recently I was here on a Saturday around 12:30 p.m. Seems like prime lunch time, but the food court was empty. That means there was no line for Chipotle! Fun fact about Chipotle, pretty much their entire spread is soy free/dairy free (other than the obvious ingredients like cheese, queso, and sour cream.) I'd be throwing stars at them for that fact alone. Try to be soy free/dairy free for a week and you'll see how killer it is. 

I got a burrito bowl with brown rice, half chicken and half steak (half and half is the way to go, I fully believe you get more meat than if you just choose one), corn, and mild salsa. I also got chips. To be honest, the bowl tasted strongly of lime (which I know they use as a preservative, but it was pretty heavy handed), but otherwise it was delicious. 

My husband loves Chipotle so we do grab food from here quite often when we are around International. He has never complained and he complains about everything (kidding, kind of.) 

Even though it seems others have run into issues, I haven 't, and their soy free/dairy free menu puts this squarely in the Yay I'm a Fan category!

First of all we ordered a 12 piece Bucket of crispy chicken and we got 10 pieces only and seemed to be original. They  all looked like they had been scrambled much of the skin was loose. 
The chicken was however tender. 

The cole slaw was a bowl of mostly juice not the usual crunchy kind we've become used too. should at least drain each spoonful into the container. 

The staff was friendly and service was satisfactory.

Used to love this fast food restaurant--but Over the last few years this Del Taco has really gone down--service and food quality. I went there today and received 2 tostadas that did not even resemble what I ordered. Cheap food-but poor product. After ten years of going here.....no more!

Ok. It's Burger King. You know what your getting with fast food. We all have our favorites from each establishment and what the interior may look like. 

But today I got the impossible burger. Boom. I'm hooked.  I see no difference between the beef and plant based, besides being less greasy. Taste is there. I hope they keep it on the menu cause I'll keep coming. Now if they could only figure out how to cook a good fry. 

The staff here is always moving trying to keep up during busy times. I rarely have an order wrong and today was no different.  

I usually only give a three star for fast food, but this new burger gives it the fourth.

I haven't had any problems with the employees like some others have said, but I had issues with my food.  I ordered an All-Star Meal with a Double Cheeseburger and told the man waiting on me that I only wanted meat and cheese in the burger he confirmed "the burger is plain" and I told him "correct" I got my food went to work and then when I began to eat my burger it had: ketchup, mustard, onions, and pickles.  That is the strangest PLAIN burger I have ever seen.  If I had wanted ketchup, mustard, onions, and pickles on my burger I wouldn't have ordered a plain burger.

The Good: Let me start off by saying the staff was warm and inviting. They were welcoming and ready to take our order. 

The Bad: No guacamole. Very stale chips with no salsa (we asked for some twice)

The Ugly: My burrito was a sad display of a meal. Less than 2oz of carnitas. When I asked for a bit more the gentleman he said couldn't. The rest of the toppings were so skimpy, it almost made me cry. Even with hot salsa, it was so dry and the soft flour tortilla cracked in the middle.

Maybe I just hit a bad time, but this location is lacking. I give it 2.5 stars.

I don't usually  do reviews for chain companies, but I felt this review was well deserved. It has been a very long time since I had Chipotle. I didn't have high expectations going in.  However, this Chipotle knocked my expectations out the roof. I had a sofritas burrito bowl, and everything tasted fresh. The portions were perfect, and I didn't even need their vinaigrette to top my bowl. Needless to say, it'll probably be another 2-3 years before I get Chipotle again, but I am very well impressed with my decision today!

So I was there 1/20 around 6:30 am. 
This dunkin is literally 2 minutes from my house but I drove all the way to the one on delsea because of how sh*t my experience was this morning. 

(I actually have worked at & managed several restaurants & waitresses so I NEVER COMPLAIN EVER bc I know the deal) Anyway.. with that being said. 

Whoever was taking drive thru orders sucks. 

Inconsistency & subpar service are two major no's. 

So first of all, I understand why some substitutions aren't allowed. Whatever I've had to tell customers no before nbd. BUT it was a practically even sub, I know the food cost/prep couldn't have been that different if at all. 

I asked for the power breakfast sand w no sausage bacon instead. 

The girl was like "we can't do substitutions" 

This would have slid fine if they didn't literally just make me the same fuckin sandwich less than 24 hrs ago. 

Also the one on delsea does it often for me as well without any hesitation. 

Anyway so I was polite and was like you guys actually just made this for me yesterday afternoon. 

SHE DIDNT EVEN RESPOND.  LITERAL NOT A WORD. 

so I was like "hello"

And she was like "we can't do it" 

I was like "I don't like sausage, you guys made it for me literally less than a day ago and the one on delsea does it no problem" 

So I was mid ordering it without sausage and was like you know what I'm going to the other one instead. 

She just said k. 

Like whyyyyy can't you instead of putting a sausage patty put bacon. It's the fuckin same as getting a bacon egg and cheese. 

But anyway. Whatever. I went to delsea and they gave me exactly what I wanted, politely, without hesitation.  

So thank you to that dunkin. 


As for this one, step your game up. 
I've had plenty not good experiences here that I let slide. 

And judging by the other reviews I'm not the only one.
No carnitas or pork?  What a Shame. Get a better quality burrito and price at local mom & pops. Be Kind To Pigs Week !

ABSOLUTLY HORRIBLE. I swear, everyone working there is very ignorant. I ordered a whopper jr asking for ketchup and pickles only. What do I get? Everything. I also ordered a "big king" with no onions and bacon. What do I get? Onions and no bacon. The bacon was found on the whopper jr. instead if the "big king" I wanted it on. Terrible. Should've gone to McDonalds.

I purposely walked in to the Burger King tonight and purposely had an issue with the app --- buy one get one for free whopper.  While the staff members were nice and kind, they offered me nothing.  I am very sad to say that I walked out of the restaurant with nothing -- They only offered me the corporate option.....  nothing more than I should figure it out.  While fast food is not the answer, it could be a means to fighting hunger.  Sad --- not going back and will not support.  Really...  I looked hungry yet nothing...  not even an option.

Trying to outweigh the bad reviews here...

Popped in to this BK on our moving day. The BK in Center City closed and we don't own a car so we don't get the joy of Whoppers too often. We took the UHaul on a detour and ordered a Whopper meal (with cheese) and an extra Whopper (with cheese) for me.

Currently there is indoor dining but a sign posted at the register says diners should limit their stay to 30 minutes. We decided to just take it outside (beautiful day) and eat at the van.

Whoppers were glorious and everything I remembered and hoped for. Fries were super salty. Not a fan.

We had also gotten a cherry vanilla Dr. Pepper and couldn't taste any vanilla. Went back for a refill and got cherry Dr. Pepper (no vanilla) instead and it tasted loaded with cherry flavor. I think the vanilla syrup was out maybe.

Overall, a solid experience. Got our order quick, it was what we expected (albeit salty), and our craving was satisfied.

I would honestly rather give them a -5 star review, this was the worst experience I've ever had with a del taco. I ordered from their store through Postmates. When my order arrived to my house all the food was either day old food, for example I paid extra to have avocado put on a few of our items, the avocado was brown and mushy. The fries in my daughter's kids meal were like biting into half frozen french fries, the quesadilla had dried old cheese and was hard as a rock. They didn't even give us my husband's food at all. When we came back the manager Shane tried to contest that all the food was made fresh and claiming he wouldn't argue no further yet was being very rude and unprofessionally arguing with me about how unfresh the food was. In the end he did include a few cookies for all the trouble. However the worst part of the experience was the 2 Hispanic women in the kitchen who kept glaring at my family the entire time we were waiting for our food. Sad part is there was another family in right before us with the exact same issue. So please next time you decide to dine here, please always check your food before exiting the building.
Editing to add: That same night after eating, I came home and was attached to my toilet with food poisoning, so much for good food right?

Sorriest $20.01 ever spent. No flavor asada. Flour tortilla weighs a ton. Kids menu sized shrimp tacos, definitely frozen breaded, deep fried then chopped. Absolutely nothing special about this joint. Should have went to Burger King for an Impossible Burger But noooooo, my dumb ass kept it Mexican and my own people being lazy as an ICE Officer named PONCH. I wouldn't eat here again if anybody else paid for it. I'd never brag about any of this here. Now I gotta go on a plane and take this bullshit with me. $5. says I'll probably get the shits. BK ain't the biz.
The chicken was not fried correctly and I was rushed making my order. I wouldn't recommend this location

THE BEYOND MEAT WAS TAINTED!!!!! I came in for a no meat taco, because i am a vegetarian and will never EVER eat meat. Well i ordered three of the beyond meat tacos, WHICH ARE PLANT BASED, and received fully meat tacos!!!! I CANNOT believe this place is trying to poison good hearted people with animal flesh. I AM ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTED AND WILL BE SUEING!!!!! unacceptable and who ever did this to me needs to be fired

I can't understand how the food here passes for "Mexican" food. The excessive use of a pungent oregano and parsley  rather than cumin and cilantro was appalling making the food taste like an East coast Italian's version of "Mexican" food. I ordered the steak tacos and the "steak" meat was tasteless other than loads of salt, and had a scuzzy film on the edges. I only ate half of my order but was violently sick for the rest of the night. What a poor demonstration of corporate American fast food.

This location had a lot of cars coming to it, but the line went through quickly. 

The cashier repeated my order back to me everytime I ordered an item which was good! 

The other cashier at the window was really friendly. 

However, the quality of our food was awful. My turkey sausage, egg, and cheese wrap was extremely crumbly and didn't even taste warm. My fiance's hash browns were barely warmed. My iced coffee tasted okay, but there was a redish hair on top of my lid which freaked me out.....

Very unhappy with this visit. Sometimes I don't get out of work until 1:30am or 2:00am and need somewhere to grab a quick bite of food if work was busy. Since this happens a weekend or two every month, I'm used to fast food places (not just burger king) taking a little longer during these late night visits since they don't have a full staff and aren't doing quite as much business as during the day.

On this occasion, my husband and I decided to go by Burger King since they were offering the buy a whopper and get a second for only 55 cents for Burger Kings birthday/anniversary. From what I understand, this was a three day promotion, and we went on the evening of the second day/morning of the third day around 1:45am. When we pulled up, there was one car at the window and one behind him/her and then one at the speaker to order and one waiting behind him/her. We are used to this situation for a late night stop, but did not expect what we experienced. 

We had the window down the whole time, thinking we would be ordering soon, and were disappointed to hear when we pulled up that the first car at the speaker was silent and had not yet placed an order. We waited in the drive thru line for over 15 minutes before we decided to leave when the car at the speaker still had not said a since word to the workers. I am not sure what was going on inside but we finally left after the car in front of us (who obviously had been waiting even longer than we had) put their reverse lights on, in an effort to get the message to us that they wanted to leave, and we figured it would be a good idea as well. When we drove by the side of the building to leave, the same two cars were still at the window and the first car at the speaker hadn't even placed an order. 

I have never worked in fast food, but my job sells fried food (not at the quantity of Burger King, but many orders on a busy weekend day). We don't have a single item that takes more than 4 minutes to cook, and that's ALL of our hot food items - not just those that are cooked in the fryer. I can't imagine burger king has anything that takes 15 minutes to cook. And you would think with the whopper promotion they had going on, they may have had a few extra whoppers ready to go for anyone who had the same idea we did. 

I am used to Burger Kings slow service late at night, but really wanted to go for the whopper promotion and also because they are one of very few restaurants that offers Diet Dr. Pepper (my favorite fountain soda). I was very unhappy with this experience and could not believe the situation that went on.

Honestly, there are days that chipotle slaps and other days where it's pretty sad. I've tried to give this place a few tries, but tonight will be my last attempt. Rice was barely cooked, the steak was boring but it's hard to tell by the picture but the bowl was half full. Cool I'll deal with that but the tortilla with cheese thrown on the side was pathetic... not coming back and I suggest avoiding this location.
Just terrible I ordered from door dash. It was the worst. They didn't add my dipping sauces, the chicken was super hard and over seasoned. It's was just awful

I ordered 8 piece chicken and too my surprise when I got home I found only 6 pieces. How hard is it to count to 8. This is the second time this has happened. I do not live in town so hard to go back up to and complain. First time I let it go but looking like a pattern  at this place.

Sadly This is the worst KFC IN THE COUNTRY ... lack luster staff that are always having issues that they verbalize way to loudly but the food is the main problem . The lack of attention to detail is reflected in the chicken and the sides.  What you need is for an Area General manager to come in unannounced and see what really happens in that place.

I ordered a 3-piece extra crispy meal (dark meat) that came with two sides (mashed potatoes and coleslaw)  & a medium drink.  In addition, I also ordered an extra side of mashed potatoes with gravy, and 6 chicken tenders.  When I got home and pulled out the sides, I had the 2 servings of mashed potatoes, but no coleslaw.  It's not worthmye time and gas to drive all the way back there for 1/2 cup of coleslaw.  Still, it was missing from my order.  The rest of the food was good,.

Still open but refused to cook any more chicken do to laziness... avoid at all cost. Yelp is making me write more for the review, not sure why I can't post a succinct one but I'll repeat myself.... Poor service due to employee laziness, avoid it at all cost.

Went through the drive thru today while out running errands with my mother. She got hungry and she wanted something small because she doesn't eat large portions. We ordered a single slider with cheese for her. Unfortunately, one of the cars ahead of us appeared to have a very large order as we had a long wait before getting to the window (7 minutes at least). By no means would I blame the staff, it was just a very large order (I could tell by the number of bags I saw coming out of the window). The staff was very apologetic for our wait, comped the slider, and even gave us an extra one. I just wanted to leave a review letting the staff know that they were friendly (I've never had an issue at this location before where I would think differently) and that we appreciate what they are doing day after day during these stressful times. I would have gladly paid for the slider since I know large orders are out of their control but the way they handled it was very kind and, again, thank you for the great service!

This KFC location is coupled with Taco Bell. The interior decor is new and colorful. One large round table is good for groups and has a fun chicken bucket light fixture above it. The donut and chicken sandwich is a fun take on chicken and waffles if you're a fan of savory and sweet, but be ready to wash your hands. Our order was prepared not the fastest I'd expect from a fast food place but still satisfactory. Drive thru and free parking are available.

Seriously #KFC. My order wasn't even that hard. You give me stale old chicken and a biscuit hard enough to crack a tooth. My wedges were stale and cold also like damn y'all just don't give a, single, solitary, fcuk do you? I order the chicken little combo with wedges, got it home and there were no wedges. The freshest thing I got was the cookie. I shoulda just took my ass to wawa  no amount of cosmetic upgrades can change the fact that your service is trash!!!! I really need to treat my body better and stop eating from these lousy places! #irritated

I love Chipotle, a little too much to be honest. I now live one minute from this location, and have continuously been disappointed. I know that with COVID-19 going on things are different, so I don't hold the slow pace against them, as I'm sure they're busy with online orders and are adapting. However,  I have rarely been greeted nicely there, and employees have had negative and seemingly rude attitudes. On top of that, the food has been poor. I have been told they are out of steak and that waiting for more is not an option, and the last two times I've gone there has been no cilantro in the rice. Lastly, last time I was there, there was two employees shouting at each other in front of everyone. One was by the kitchen, the other was at the grill.

Had a free whopper offer, placed a mobile order including the free whopper) and entered drive through. Employee told me they were closed (20 minutes early) and said I wouldn't be charged because they didn't accept the mobile order. I was in fact charged and had to email support and wait a week for refund. They told me they couldn't not refund my free whopper.   Lazy employees closing early and poor customer support. Deleting the app.
Service was prompt and on-time. Staff was professional and helpful. Chicken was hot, as were the sides.  I certainly will frequent this business in the future. Thank you KFC Harding Place.

Thank you to the gentleman working tonight 12/15/2021.  He was cashier and chef.  He did an excellent job my impossible burger was delicious.  I hope he is appreciated by the company!! Sorry I didn't get his name

Let me start this off by saying that I love Chipotle. I have probably eaten at Chipotle (not THIS Chipotle) more than 100 times in my life. Now to the review...

I have ordered from this Chipotle twice in the past month, both times online. The first time I ordered was about a month ago. I ordered a chicken salad, but they did not include any lettuce with the salad... excuse me, it's a salad! How do you not include lettuce with a salad? 

So I should have taken that as a sign, but alas, I decided to try my luck there again today. Welp, this time folks, it was even worse! This time, my wife ordered a carnitas bowl, and I ordered a steak bowl. Both bowls came with undercooked and cold meat; barely cooked pink pork and steak, and both meats were literally "fresh out of the fridge" cold! We were both amazed, as Chipotle keeps their meat in warmers, right? Well apparently not, as this Chipotle didn't get that memo and served us some ice cold and undercooked food. Also, besides being drier than the desert we live in, the steak tasted off and it was possibly rotten (yes, both bowls got thrown out).

I am now sitting in anticipation of the food poisoning I will likely be receiving any minute now. 

Tl;dr - Don't eat here. Pick a different Chipotle.

Fast service, even though there seemed to be a discussion about who was going to serve me. The lady acted like I was a criminal asking for a second scoop of steak (it was just rice, 6 pieces of steak, and shredded cheese) . $20 for a burrito, some chips, cheese, and watered down lemonade. That second scoop of meat must've brought the price up.

***UPDATE****8/23/17 @ 1:20 pm
We called corporate who gave us the # for Regional Mgr who took the complaint and referred us to the Manager at the local store. Brad (local mgr) called and talked to my husband. They resolved the issue over the phone and he offered us a FULL REFUND. I got in the car, drove 15 mins there (not as much traffic), went inside spoke to Brad. He was super nice, friendly and very apologetic. He even offered more Chicken sandwiches but I declined. He showed me the ones that he cooked and they looked awesome but my stomach was still upset over the last one. He gave me a full refund and even (get this) gave me 4 New York Cheesecakes for FREE!! AWESOME!!! Thank you, Brad.. I wish you luck!!
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/23/17 @ 11:20 am.. 
Well, I have to start my review as it was in the beginning. It was a horrible experience. We went to the drive thru and they got our order wrong at the window, then my husband went inside to get what they forgot (cheesecake). The girl behind the counter didn't have a name tag, was counting her drawer down, then she did a 45-second staring contest with my hubbi after he laid the receipt and bag on the counter. Finally, he said we were missing our cheesecake. She mumbled 3 words. Then someone looked at the back and then the front of receipt. He got the cheesecake, complained supposedly spoke to a lady manager nothing was really done. We drove 20 mins to get there. We drove home opened our meals (Whopper with Bacon & Cheese and 2 of the new Chicken Parm sandwiches). Well, we had Salt Lick Fries(we won't need sodium for a week or 2 now), the bacon was tough, the chicken was hard, brown and nothing like the ad). I threw out half of mine and 1/2 of the fries. I ate the Cheesecake it was the only part of the meal that was good...Horrible experience...

It's del taco!

Also, it's del taco....

This location has a bad history of messing up orders. Always be sure to re-read whatever you order at the window or make sure you check your food before you pull completely away. Right now during the pandemic the dining room is closed, so you have to get back into the drive-through line if they messed something up. Try to avoid that.

On a sidenote it is the only taco place around here offering the beyond meat options so vegetarians and vegans do you have a little more choice here.

I am big fan of del taco.  But last time I was there and one of the guys that was working there came up and ask my friend and I if his manager can have our numbers. To me that was very creepy.i will not be returning to this location. I just wanted to eat dinner and deal with this is not cool

This kfc is real good. What's to complain?! Better than Popeyes and it's good so they got lines. Their chicken sandwich is real good and they are real polite. Don't like it then go take yourself home and throw some food in ur own frying pan

My fiancé ordered a hot French vanilla latte (he said it didn't taste too sweet, but that's because he didn't ask for any sugar), and we ordered a sausage, egg, and cheese on a croissant, a bacon, egg, and cheese on a plain bagel, two hashbrowns, and a caramel iced coffee decaf with cream. The sausage egg and cheese did not taste that warm, but everything else was good.

To amuse myself during the Covid-19 pandemic, I've been comparing friend chicken sandwiches from various restaurants. I had heard from several online sources that KFC's sandwich is surprisingly good, but some of the reviews I saw for this particular KFC location - the one closest to me - made me hesitate to actually get it. I finally bit the bullet though; how bad could it really be?

Boy did I jinx myself.

The first time I tried to order a sandwich at this store, their computers were down. Okay, well, that I not their fault, right? So I waited a couple days and came back. On my second attempt to get a sandwich, they were out of sandwiches. That's... odd. I've rarely seen more than one car - if even that many - at this whenever I pass it. Meanwhile, the Popeyes on the next block usually has a line wrapped around the building. But fine, I'll try again later.

A week later, third time was the charm: finally got my Crispy Colonel Sandwich. I was so excited that I was *finally* getting a sandwich that I forgot to tell them that I wanted it Nashville Hot, and they didn't ask; shame. The ordering otherwise was simple and fast, and off I went with my sandwich, Mt Dew Sweet Lightning, and mashed potatoes.

When I first unwrapped the sandwich, the first thing I noticed was it's underwhelming size, both in height and circumference. I tried to remind myself that I had gotten used to bigger, more expensive sandwiches, and that it wasn't necessarily a bad sign. When I picked it up I noticed that the bun didn't seem soft, but I ignored it. Then I took a bite and... yeah. It was *chewy*. Chewy chicken on a stale bun.

How did this happen? Did no one handling the bun/sandwich - something they would do daily - notice that it felt off? And what happened to the chicken to make it chewy? Because that's surely not normal.

The fact that it took me three attempts to get a sandwich, and then that the quality control was poor enough to let such a bad sandwich be sold... color me highly unimpressed. I won't spend another dollar at this location.

They have the beyond chicken.. I have  been waiting patiently for this to be available somewhere near me and I found it.  We went pretty late. There was no wait at the drive thru. The staff was knowledgeable  and pleasant. Now for the beyond chicken. They were nugget  sized portions. The seasoning was great and had a great crunch. The price may be a little steep if you aren't accustomed  to paying vegan prices but I definitely  feel it was worth every penny.
the manager made me cry, just want an impossible whopper why is it so hard to find around here

I went to this location today around 5:55pm and It was my turn to order. I ordered the 3 piece meal. The guy said sorry we don't have that at the moment, then I ordered something else and then the guy said sorry we do not have any chicken at the moment and it will be a 35 minute wait. How can KFC be out of chicken?!!!! Its literally in their name!!!! Horrible customer service. I'm guessing the lazy ass workers did not want to sell any chicken today!!! Never coming to this location ever again.

Chicken was fine. Food got here on time-ish. Idk in what world when someone orders Mac and cheese it gets mistaken for cole slaw. That was annoying. Also, I paid for 2 extra biscuits & didn't get them. Got it delivered so there was no point in calling to complain as I doubt they'd offer to send out another driver for it. Whatever.

Not too bad & Quick!
I went inside, it's right on North Meridian St
I think there's a Taco Bell menu as well, I was looking for chicken, those commercials Work! 
My husband said he liked the mashed potatoes, the food was Hot and Delicious.
I wish they had Delivery in Indy like they do in a couple of other cities I've seen that.
I'd weigh 1200 lbs! Maybe it's Best they Not have Delivery!!??! 
Friendly staff

OMG!  Service horrible, food horrible.  I have stopped by this location multiple times when an older Female Manager is on duty and the service has been good, she had to redo burger once but usually good.  Then when I stop by and a Manager Eric is on duty, service is bad, takes why too long to get food and this is the second time my order (whopper and frys and Frappe) were messed up.  First no Frappe, did not have items needed to make them, I ordered no salt on fry's, got salt, and ordered whopper with no ketchup, it was loaded with ketchup, I ordered extra onions and extra pickles, did not get either and did not get tomatoes, WOW then when I called Eric the Manager on duty he said I would have to take to Manager who will be there Monday and that unless I drove all the way back up there that he could do nothing and could not even compensate me for the travel, with a voucher or anything.  Eric blamed service on new hires even though he was never seen working until I requested my money back for the frappe that they could not make. Wow, what an interesting stop for lunch. The counter worker "Christina" is clearly not very professional.  Spanish was the only thing I heard til I got to the counter then a snarky "what would you like" then some more spanish over her shoulder, a solid 1000 yard star at me while i recieved my change. Had to use the restroom only to find a homeless man panhandling......this isn't Kalifornia is it? Wont be stopping here ever again. I like Wendy's but not worth it.

I go to order and the only item they had in stock were wings...no thighs, drumsticks, or breasts, what kind of a restaurant known for its chicken is completely out of 3/4 of their menu? It's not just the chicken cuts, they didn't even have chicken pot pie. They were even out on their dry biscuits, not that I was even going to eat it anyways, it takes a gallon of water to get that down. Save your money and go somewhere else. I have a feeling this place will be closing soon. Sad because I really loved this place last summer. Too bad there aren't any other KFCs around...don't know if it's just this particular store or if all of the other KFCs are like this location.

Standard KFC but they had the new Beyond (meat) Fried Chicken, which is basically a non-chicken nugget.  They were awesome. I ordered them dry so I could taste them with all the dipping sauces.   Would have given 4 stars but they wouldnt give me any Nashville hot sauce on the side.  They only toss them in that sauce when ordered that way, so I tossed one of their stars in the trash with my empty container after I ate all those awesome nuggets.

Love the char-broiled taste of Burger King. The Whopper is my go-to over the  Quarter Pounder. 
The wait in line was not bad, but has become normal. At the speaker, we we greeted by, "Wait just a minute ". A few minutes later we gave our order to the faceless voice. He demanded the coupon number or my BK app offer, although it says to read it to the cashier. 
At the window we were greeted by a friendly masked face and asked how our night was, and that he hoped our food was good.
It was delicious, 30 minutes later, once we sat down at the dinner table. 3 Whoppers, 3 cheeseburgers, and 3 small fries for $12.99 - a great deal!
