Not knowing that we had to do quality control on our order, we made our way home only to find that we had mac and cheese and mashed potatoes with gravy.  Needless to say our little order came to $25.00 and we drove there and back and didn't even get what we paid for.  And yes, Jason was our cashier. We will not go back, is it that difficult, really?  The chicken was KFC good, but what happened to our cole slaw people?  We called and were told by Manager Rosa that we could come back and they would gladly give us our cole slaw we ordered, really?  Now that's too difficult.  Why didn't I just get what I ordered?

Wanted the Softitas Bowl. Hot in the store so took it to go. Got home and found out they gave me chicken. Not a good place for vegetarians to go. Too far to go back and even if I did, who wants to wait in that line twice. Very disappointing.
Can't say NO to great burrito food. Yum!!! Always a great choice. I have always been a huge fan of carnitas.

15 minutes to get a whopper junior. They would only let you choose from what they already had left from breakfast.  This is 10:00 am. Of course it's at the airport so it's over prices. The whopper jr looked like the size of a slider.

I have to give them a 5 star rating because they're amazing.  I usually go when my kids have helped me reach my daily limit.  They have an enclosed play area where one can run around & the older one can remain antisocially glued to their electronic device.  

I love their chicken tenders & fresh cut fries coupled with a chocolate chip cookie & a root beer plus they bring it to your table!

OLD/COLD FOOD, MISLEADING EMPLOYEES, OVERCHARGING, INEPT MANAGEMENT!
You have been warned about his location. If you decide to patronize this KFC, be ready for potentially one of the worst experiences in fast food you have ever had.
Of the last 5 visits, only one has been without issues, and that was because I purchased a drink and sides - a simple order that anyone can get right.
I have had them pack old and cold food for me near closing time, only to find out when I got home that it was inedible. (Called the next day and the manager was useless in helping with a refund, replacement, or anything else.)
Another time, i used a coupon, was told the coupon was printed wrong, and had to pay the difference. I was hungry and could care less, but came to find out that I was scammed by the counter-person.
Yet, another time, I arrived early in the evening - learned form last visit - and placed a normal order, only to have it packed, crushed really, into a much smaller container then it should have been. When I got home, I had a congealed mess of chicken flesh, soggy/cold chicken breading, and in a single, uniformly rectangular block. (Yeah, I called again, and was told that it was not their problem, and that if I unpacked it when I go tit, the food would be OK to eat!!!)
Take you money, health, and sanity in your own hands when eating/taking out from this location, the manager is very unimpressive, and doesn't seem like she would be able to manage a wet paper bag, let alone a supposed fast food restaurant.
I will not be returning to this, or any other KFC for that fact, in the near to later future. All fo this with this location has turned me off of their products and inept service.

Awful in every way! Our last time EVER GOING HERE! We've had a problem with them the last three times we've went. But this was the last straw. 
Matt goes to pick up some KFC & he orders corn on the cob they told him they haven't done corn on the cob in 16 years! Then he orders crispy chicken but they said they could only make 1/2 of the order crispy that they didn't have enough! He said he'd wait while they made more & they said that's all they were making for the night! One time they told us @6:00pm on Saturday that they were sold out of Mac n cheese. Do they always hire uneducated & rude employees for the night shifts? Worse Kfc I've ever been to. We've been to KFC in 5 states & I even worked at KFC in Oregon. This is unacceptable, period.
Brown guacamole, fat pieces instead of carnitas for which we paid for double meat because of a dingy server, stale chips, a long line and a $33 bill..stay away from this one.

This has been my Burger King since I moved into the neighborhood. Burger Kings are the same all over but the fact that this one is 24 and the McDonalds(which I thought were all 24hrs.) is not, makes BK my go to spot if don't want Wawa. 

I do have one gripe though, I was told I was going to get a WHopper Wendsday shirt and it never arrived. Shame Shame BK corporate.

I have gone to this Chipotle a few times.  There's always a line, but they get through it ok.  Other reviewers complained about bad service and their orders getting screwed up, luckily I haven't experienced that.  I did want a carnitas burrito yesterday, but there was a sign on the door that there was no carnitas.  I was disappointed, but it happens.  Overall, it's still way better than going to Taco Bell.

I would give them a 5 star for quality of food but the service can be so bad that it brings the overall score down. Each person puts such a different amount of food on the burrito or dish that the quanity it so different. Yes the lines can be long but again the food is good. The guac is awesome and the corn mix. I find that the Steak can be a bit chewy:( Go and try it!

The Colonel would NOT approve of what has happened to KFC.  i won't be back. had to wait for the chicken strips and then they were barely done.  very greasy -- not at all like the good old days when they had people that knew how to cook chicken right. i'll just keep getting the wings at Walmart - much tastier and half the price.

The KFC on Rte 38 In CH near the CH Mall is an incompetently managed dump and dive. They always run out of chicken, screw up your orders at the drive thru, charge you the wrong amount, tables and floor are always filthy and the food is barely warm to cold. The staff is incompetent. Put it plain an simple: "THIS PLACE STINKS! ". Avoid this Cherry Hill Rte 38 KFC If you want a good chicken meal, go to Chick-Fil-A down the street behind them and over to the right about 2 blocks away. If Col Sanders were alive he would fire the whole staff, shut this KFC outlet down, demolish the building and re-start from scratch, and that is EXACTLY what needs to be done to this dump of a joint.

I have been here twice.  Service is always fast and the food is tasty. I love crunchy tacos but so often the shells are stale.  They are always fresh here.  The employees are very polite and nice.  Both times I brought my baby and they always offered to carry the food to my table for me.  

They had a promotion for the braised tofu sofritas, so I tried it.  It is really good, even for a meat eater!

Ok so eight months later and I have to connect through St. Louis again.  Hungry no problem. I'll get a Whopper at Burger King. Get in line and see the sign.  Due to technical difficulties "Menu items with burgers not available."   Are you kidding me.  Last time they didn't take cash and were understaffed. This time it's no Burgers.  Someone call the "King".  It could be a coincident but come on.  Hope their lease runs out soon and we can get a respectable burger place in here.  I love Burger King but this location is the worse ever.

I've been to pretty much every Mexican fast food place in my surrounding area and I've been to about two dozen or so authentic Mexican places in Jersey, Philly, and NY. There's only two Chipotles that are remotely close to me and both are in areas that I rarely go to. So this was my first time having Chipotle. I've been hearing a few close friends talk about it and crave for it for a couple years now. So coincidentally, I started craving it too.
When I pulled up, I could clearly see that the line was almost out the door. I said to myself, f' that! And was about to leave. But then my inner fat boy told me that maybe the line's that long because the food's so good... Boy was I wrong. Smh... Part of the reason the line was long is because the layout is horrible. You place your order in the right corner of the restaurant, so naturally, the line is gonna back up in the direction of the entrance. The tables are huge and bulky, so they take up room that eliminated any chance of them putting a maze for the line. Sadly, from what I read in a lot of other reviews, this is a major problem. As for the line is always to/ out the door. Aside from that, the place felt like a military dining hall. Four really long tables with slide up chairs/ stools on both sides. They definitely could have went with smaller tables that not only would that have been more comfortable, but it probably would have made more space available as well. They did have outdoor dining, and the space seemed pretty large. But they didn't have that many tables out there.
As for the food, other than a couple of burger places that I've been to, I can't remember the last time that I went to a resturant that had such a limited menu. The thing that pissed me off most is that they don't have nachos or quesadillas. Most people say this place is hit or miss for the portions, depending on who's making your food. But luckily, my portions were pretty large... Everything was super fresh. I had 2 chicken hard tacos, 1 steak taco, and a beef burrito that had some serious weight to it. The taste was good, but it could have been a lot better if they offered more toppings. Seems like they only had half the amount of toppings that Qdoba and  Moe's have... And to add to that, they don't have any signatures sauces or dishes. Qdoba has a 4 cheese sauce and a chipotle bbq sauce that are to die for. And I believe Moe's has even more. Anyways, by the time I finished eating, the line had extended from the door to across the front of the restaurant. That kind of ruined my dinning experience because I felt like my personal space was being invaded. The line was seriously 2 feet away from me and I felt like everybody was all up in my plate. 
I'm not biased at all and I was super excited to try Chipotle. I'll give them the benifit of the doubt this this particular was just bad and I'm willing to try another location. So until that happens, I love Qdoba and when I need to switch it up, I'm perfectly happy with going to greasy as Taco Bell and getting "horse meat" Mexican food.

I don't hate on this location as much as some of the other reviewers.  Sure, compared to the In n' Out across the street, it's not much.  But sometimes In n' Out is packed full and sometimes you just really want a Whopper.  And when that happens, you go to Burger King!

I've always had good experiences at this location.  Our latest visit was just yesterday and we were so pleased with the service and food that I knew the time had come for me to finally write up a review.  

For one thing, we were asked several times if there was anything we needed by a helpful and friendly employee.  The only other fast food restaurant I've had that happen at is Popeyes.  MAYBE In n' Out but if so it's been so long ago that I can't recall. We were presented with some great money saving coupons (oh, we WILL be back!) and offered a free sample of their new onion rings.  We've been offered coupons and free samples in the past as well.  Both are very welcome!

The onion rings were fantastic, by the way.  I will have to order them on a future visit.  They are easy to bite through which is a tall order with onion rings, but also crisp and delicious.  The rest of our meal was yummy as well and the kids just love their chicken nuggets here. 

On the whole, I'd say there's nothing terrible about this location.  I only gave three stars because of consistency issues.
Ok ..kids love the chicken strips....but we do not like to go in the evening when Manager Kimberlee is working..she is rude to Customers and employees alike..so we skip dinners there

My chicken and steak tasted like it had been warmed up. Being I come right when you open you would think the food would be fresh! When I ask for a little more rice they give me attitude. And Gabe the manager needs to socialize more with his customers like the old GM.

I don't know what fresh hell this was and LAWD help me I can't tell you why I keep going back but it was night. It was dark. Company Burger was closed - I needed a Whopper and a diet coke and my neighborhood BK was sans "any dark cola." Seriously. A first-world apocalypse. 

So I looked for the next nearest BK and here we were. It was going to be a wait for the burger so I had to "go around" which meant I had to exit the drive-thru, cross into 4-lane traffic, make an immediate "U" to get back in the parking lot, brave more traffic just for a Whopper. I almost gave up. 

So if you don't mind running the gauntlet that may be required of you, you'll be fine. If you value your bumper you might reconsider?

White Castle's are good for the nostalgic value on their sliders.  I've had their BBQ pork sandwich for a $1, and now their breakfast (bacon cheese egg) sandwich for $1.62.  They were both tiny (should we ever expect any different?) and pretty poor values, thus the 2 star rating. I like having them around for the occasional slider craving, and I do hope they can all stay in business.  This one is clean, and particularly convenient though for a coffee stop when headed into the Motor Speedway from south Lynhurst Drive.

I love KFC food, especially the crispy chicken. In general, whenever I've been to a KFC, it is very common that they have no crispy chicken, especially during dinner. That is infuriating. in addition, the. restaurants appear to be dirty and the bathroom is disgusting, especially in Cherry Hill. The other thing is, on most occasions, the guest wifi never works and no one can explain that.

I wonder why I come here, although this might only be my second time.  The restaurant is messy as staff doesn't clean the tables (although customers should clean up after themselves) and the floors are dirty.   

We opted for the buffet, which does have a nice variety of food, except that there is never chicken sitting there.  You have to ask and then wait.....annoying.   Everything else on the buffet is hot and tastes just like the meals you may get instead of buffet.  

It really is the cleanliness issue that will now keep me away.  You just have to wonder how clean the kitchen is where my food was being cooked.  

Staff was friendly and waited on us quickly.

It's chipotle, expect long lines and young, employees with slightly below par customer service skills.  The food has been fine each time I've been here, but I have to agree with all the other reviews stating they are always out of something. I have waited on chicken twice and steak once...good news is at least the proteins are hot and fresh!

This location is the worst of the bay. The staff is not enthusiastic. They are always missing something, sometimes steak, sometimes lettuce, sometimes guac. They can't even do a simple request, mix the bowl. Even when I ask prior to starting to build the bowl. Back to the Howard location I go.

This is by far the worst chipotle branch I've ever been to. This was my first time at this location and will definitely be my last. There was only one other person in front of us when we went in to order yet somehow it took forever to get our food. The boy working the line was waiting til they were completely out of ingredients before he was telling the grill he needed more (steak and chicken). And he gave the smallest portions or everything that I've ever seen. Hardly any rice that he flattened into the bottom of my bowl, half a scoop of beans and shot a 1/4 scoop of chicken. Way less than any other chipotle. After we got our food and sat down I watched the same boy eating a taco right at the Tortilla warmer with his gloves still on. Another customer came in who had ordered ahead and this boy apparently hadn't even made his food yet. So the boy put his taco down and preceded to make the mans burritos wearing the same gloves he was just eating his own food wearing. Disgusting. He made 2 more people's food before he finally changed his gloves.  The one girl was cleaning while we were there so it wasn't dirty but there was about 6-7 people working and only her and the boy at the front counter seemed to be doing anything. Will definitely stick to the Exton location that I usually go to and bypass this one in the future.

I love the chipotle chain. This one is usually busy. When it happens to not be busy for whatever reason the staff starts slipping on taking orders. The simple process chipotle has in place makes it work even if hey make minor mistakes as they are quick to rectify the situation. 

There's always something that's not up to par in this location. Tonight there were no straws available and they had a corn outage. Tables aren't cleaned frequently enough. 

I tend to get my salad bowl to go...I usually eat no carbs here...well maybe I'll steal a few chips from my child's kids meal. It has been maybe over 2 years since I've had a burrito, taco or any rice here. I can't finish anything I order including my salad because the items are generous and quite filling. Do I get 2 meals for the price of 1. :)

The location and parking sucks. The front doors of this strip mall doesn't face the main street. The parking lot is narrow. You can't drive into this strip mall from any of the other strip malls next as they aren't connected. One in and one out. Every business that sets up next door never makes it. From the time I moved to Westchase there was a Pinkberry, another Yogurt shop and now a Which Which next door...good luck to Which, but bad choice on location when your sandwiches are mediocre.

A chipotle can make it anywhere due to the flavorful meats and amazingly fresh toppings. I have a hard time deciding between barbacoa, steak or chicken and I always get the Guac...no, I don't care that it's an extra charge. With the transition to non GMO this is such a great fast food choice for my fam. This one happens to be around the corner, so I'll always be back.

This is the best Chipotle I have ever been to.. The employees are friendly and nice.. They actually give you a good portion of food and the prices are cheaper than the Chipotle in Princeton..

There is always chicken and steak cooking on their grill which tells you they have fresh food that doesn't sit around all day..

Never had any issues with coming here and we eat at Chipotle about 3 times a week... We try to come to this location but sometimes can't and are forced to go to the one in Priceton..

5 stars!

Just wish the lines wouldn't be out the door every time ;)

This place can get very busy ecspecially if there are special events nearby, came here one time and the line had at least 20 people. But the upside is, it does normally go very fast. We like coming here as a fast but not fast food option. I get the steak burrito bowl(steak, rice, beans, salsa, cheese, and sour cream) and it is very good. The steak is kinda spicy but I have them add extra sour cream. I also really like that you can order online and choose a pick up time and they always have it ready for you.

Chipotle. Chipotle.  What can I say. You are just OK. =) 

Really really.  It's definitely not authentic Mexican food.  It is good.  I've had the carnitas and the steak burritos, both were dry. However, the last time I went, the barbacoa is the way to go! The marinated slow cooked beef is sooooo soft and moist! Along with the corn salsa, lime cilantro white rice, cheese, sour cream, and guacamole (extra charge), it makes a pretty good concoction. The good thing about it, the burritos are chubby.  They are short and chubby.  

Note: I haven't tried the bowl or salad, but I hear if you get them, you can get a lot more for your money!

First time here and for sure not the last. Had an excellent hard shell taco x 3 with a very good steak filler...loaded with fresh salsa, sour cream, and everything they had. Enjoyed it very much... Loved the hot sauce! This plac blows Moe's out of the hole and flattens Tiajuna to the ground!

As a first timer to Chipotle I was lost as how to order. So I let my fiancé go ahead of me. I ordered a burrito bowl with steak, white line cilantro rice, black beans, peppers mild & sour cream. I'm surprised at how good it tasted! My first thoughts were the steak would be tough and dry. So not the case! It was tender and juicy! Surprised ! Te lime cilantro rice was so amazing. The flavor came through perfectly, all in all I enjoyed my meal. I could only eat half, so I had the rest for dinner.  I would give it another go around.

Lemme tell ya what this Chipotle did to me today.  Story time!! 

This Chipotle opens up at 10:45AM and I had to meet my husband and bring him lunch by 11 at the base so at 10:22AM I placed an online order to be picked up at 10:45AM.  $31.90 got me three burritos, a kid's meal, and two soft drinks.  I arrived promptly right as they opened and headed inside. 

I check in with the cashier and they haven't even begun my order yet.  The cashier offered me "a drink while I wait" and I was like..."um yeah 2 plz"...and he gives me this look like I'm a freeloader so I added "the two drinks that I paid for....in my mobile order" and I guess he doesn't believe me because he goes over to look at my ticket and then begrudgingly hands me two cups.  

Finally at 11:01 my order was ready and it was surprisingly all there and correct.  Unfortunately there were a few quality control issues with the food itself.  The tortillas were harder than a death-row inmate, ya'll.  I had to rip off the top and bottom of my tortilla just to be able to chew that bad boy up.  

They were also the smallest burritos I've ever encountered at a Chipotle.  Usually they are about the size of a newborn baby but these were the size of frozen gas station burritos.  The chicken had to have been from yesterday's batch because it was completely dried up and crunchy.  I'm guessing the steak was from yesterday as well cuz' it was completely grey with no brown on the outside and riddled with fat and gristle.  

When people tell me they don't like Chipotle...I can only imagine that they have visited a location like this.  Chipotle can be much better than this!!!

So I ordered the 20$ fill up box with 8 pieces of any kind of chicken. The thing about this KFC is that they always run out of grilled chicken. They ran out of the breasts, so I got crispy instead.

Now the chicken itself was super dry and I saw them just pull it out the heater and plop it in a cardboard container. From the other KFCs I been to, the meat was more tender and warm. The sides were the same as every other KFC. I think it's just this location that has issues with their chicken.

I live in a part of Idaho without any Del Tacos and am stuck with Taco Bell for my Mexican food fast food. Having grown up in So Cal, where the two first started business, I always chose Del Taco.  Especially now when you go into Taco Bell and nothing you get looks like the picture on the menu. Go Del Taco and my favorite is the  2 for $5 Del Combo burrito.

Can I give them no stars?! We went in on a snowy day because we wanted some good old fried chicken and even went inside to order and the guy at the register kept talking to his coworkers and didn't GIVE US EVERYTHING WE ORDERED AND PAID FOR. I have never ever written a bad review but I paid for a side of Mac and cheese but it was nowhere to be found when I looked into my bag. I wish I could go back right now and demand a refund but the snow is way too bad to even go back. We even had to ask for our cups and he also gave us the wrong sauce. Never visiting this location again. It is seriously not that hard to get an order right dude. Get it together!

For basic quick Mexican food I enjoy Del Taco.  The menu has great variety and portions are plentiful (HELLO BEAN & CHEESE BURRITO!).  Notice i said QUICK and not fast.  Some previous reviews have whined on and on about how slow they are- seriously, if you WANT a microwave burrito go buy one and cook it yourself.  I'd rather wait a bit for something BETTER than a frozen burrito.  A big order in front of you?  Be compassionate and give the order taker a break.  First come first serve mean anything to you?   My one complaint is the placement of the building on the lot.  This lot is a mess because of the busy corner it's on and the easements (or lack there of) that don't allow access and egress that makes sense.  However the staff of Del Taco have no bearing on those things.   Del Taco is NOT Taco Bell.. the food is better and yes it takes more time- be a lemming and stick with what's faster or be a bit more unique, have a bit more patience and find the GOOD things in Del Taco.

Terrible service, terrible food. Emailed the corporate office about a bad experience, and never got a reply. Mac and cheese was gray. I ordered a 16 piece, and watched the girl, unsuccsessfully attempt to put on the lid, which obviously didnt fit. Instead of putting the chicken that didn't fit in a different containter, she used all her force and pressed the lid down, which compressed my 40 dollar order into greasy mutilated chicken. I demanded my money back and visited the maple shade storw, which is always a good experience.

I have not eaten at kfc for quite some time because the chicken was always rubbery, stringy, and had very little meat. I wad pleasantly surprised in this case, i got the 5$ filllup, all white, and everything was delicious! The chicken was meaty and juicy, rather than rubberized stringy crap, the biscuit was hot and fresh, as opposed to what i used to get, case hardened hockey pucks. The mashed potatoes were tasty, not like the plaster of paris concoction they used to serve. For $5, I got a lot of quality, tasty good. Well done!
Love their original and crispy recipe chicken, pot pie or wings.  Don't arrive before closing because they may not have what you want. Courtesy at drive through and quick service.

Don't get me wrong, we love Chipotle and go at least once a week, however this location is bad bad bad.  The wait can easily be 20-30 minutes to get food at rush time.  People leave all the time, you would think they would see sales dropping and do something. Steak is sometimes super way more spicy than any other Chipotle.  Guacamole had a blue tint one day.  Why is the guy at the cash register fetching napkins? because everyone else is so slow, forget about lemon at the drink stand.  Tables and restaurant dirty for the last year or so.  I have been there when the one and only soda machine is not working and finally today there were no chips.  Hello high margin items easy to prepare ahead of time?  One they they had a guy in a white jacket there trying to figure it out but frankly he was not better though he asked how they were doing.  That day the food quality was better but the service was terrible.  By the way is there a manager at that store? Do they work dinner crowd? Never seen one. Corporate needs to get going on this.  By the way Chipotle iphone app should have a way to send feedback while waiting on line for 30 minutes!!!!!!!!  To bad we love chipotle, just not the one closest to our house.

Food: I am from California. The food was mediocre at best. I tried my best to not let the hype overcome my judgment but even then it did not deserve to be compared to In n Out. The original slider tastes like the frozen version, microwaved. Their chicken rings were dry so beef would definitely be the best choice. Their onion chips were a nice change from onion rings; they were relatively the same just different shapes. I would not suggest getting more than 3 sliders. After 2 sliders, I began to get sick of it.

Service: The lady at the register was nice and tried to get our orders sorted out efficiently.

Setting: Looks like any other fast food restaurant.

DO NOT VISIT THIS DEL TACO IF YOU CAN HELP IT. 

I don't usually review fast food, because how much customer service can you honestly expect? But I do expect a little decency. My boyfriend and I went in stood and waited at the register with out so much as an acknowledgement. We finally ordered and I had asked for a water cup. Our cashier never gave us one. Then we asked another employee who gave a short "yeah." And then continued her conversation with a co-worker for several more minutes before handing it to us. 

When my boy friend went to the counter to pick up our food for two, the man made a not so joking comment about it being an awful lot of food for one person and that he certainly hoped he'd need a bag to take some home rather than finish it all off now. 

I just think the whole experience was offensive and lacking attentiveness from the employees. Very disappointed, even for fast food.

Two stars for the experience of buying the meal not the food itself.  The chicken was great.  The problem starts from Williams Boulevard.  Is this place open....still in business?  Hard to tell after dark because the lamps inside the restaurant must be set on a dimmer making it unusually dark for a fast food restaurant.  I've been in low rent, dive barrooms that had more light.  Another issue is the amount of employees....four.  One working the walk up counter, one working the drive thru window, one sweeping the dining room and lastly one cooking chicken.  This means the lady taking the order also has to fill the order which is extremely time consuming.  The franchisee of this place needs to visit his business like an undercover boss so he experiences the not ready for prime time service his restaurant dishes out.

It is friday nite 6 pm  no chicken ready 16 minute wait.we asked to speed it up just mix what they have left original and crispy.no discount just giggling at the drive thru asking how we are today..not a good question..wow..
Picked up a family meal.  WORST KFC we have ever had.   Burnt chicken, small pieces and not all there.   Will never go to this one again.

Waited almost  10 minutes for our food. I order a whopper with cheese  no cheese. The manager. Was there sitting on his ass couldn't brother to get up and help get the line moving never going there again
Great service! Very friendly employees and quality food as always! I haven't had Del Taco in yeeeears but honestly just as good and tastes fresh! Will be back.

This is the worst DD I've been to. Everyday I say I'm not going back, and always do... but today I hope I don't. She couldn't even open the drive thru window to say hello or tell me how much I owed! Every morning it's the same thing. I argue with them over the loud speaker "ice coffee." "hot coffee?" "no, iced coffee!" They shout at you and are just plain rude. The other day I was sitting at the drive thru for 15 minutes. The guys gets on the speaker and says "I'll be with you in 5 min." There was no one there. Once I got up to the window, he hit on me and then explained that ever since they started to close early he needs to get things done and doesn't have time for late night customers... Who keeps you employed  buddy? In addition, I never get a receipt. I never get what I order. I never get exact change back if I pay with cash. The girl has forgotten to give me back my change a ton of times. I never get what I order. It's always a surprise at work. "Will it be a ham and cheese wrap today?" is the joke of my office now. "And no! It's a sausage mcmuffiin!" or "ooooh, that'a a jelly donut!" Don't go there. Find another place. I've lived here two months and its been two months of pure rudeness and poor customer service. Not worth it.

Del Taco is always pretty good if they get your order right. The last few times we have gone here they have messed up our order. They have always givn us the right amount of items, but they have been the wrong items. I will give them another shot next time. If they can start getting the orders right, it will be a good place to go back to.

OMG! Do I love this place I usually order the same thing a steak bowl with black beans brown rice lettuce tomatoes multiple types of salsa light sour cream and jalapeños. I've never had a bad bowl they're they are always very good also they have many types of Tabasco sauce out in the dining room if you like it spicy which I do. There is plenty of parking and lots of seating so that's never an issue and the staff is always friendly and upbeat but the lines there can be very long sometimes as I have went there and got my food and left within 5 minutes and other times it's taken 15-20 mins as the lines were so long I thought they were giving away free tacos seriously I did. Also they do not have jalapeños ready to go you have to ask for them and sometimes they need to get them from the back and cut them which can add to the wait. But over all excellent Mexican food made by an American staff. Enjoy and Happy Yelping.

Granted 100% of my visits to this Burger King have been after 11pm, this is a decent location I guess.  Food definitely tastes like its been sitting for awhile, but I guess that's what you expect.  Takes a long time cuz I'm pretty sure they only have 2 staff members for late night.  Rarely get anything but the dollar menu that late cuz the other stuff is too expensive to risk them messing it up.  Need to try it in the day light cuz that Whopper with guacamole and bacon sounds amazing.  Really can't beat a 24 hr eatery tho.
Disappointed  went to chipotle severals time and all way's the same excuse  out of carnitas the suppliers.not happy  get a new supplier.

I don't know why I come to this Del Taco. The workers aren't friendly and they rarely get my order right. 

I ordered a Macho Nachos with no beans and extra cheese and a Deluxe Chili Cheese Fries. The nachos were correct, but the fries were not. Since I went through the drive-thru, I had to park and go back inside to get the order corrected. The counter attendant was nice, but obviously new. The drive-thru attendant was snippy and told me, "There aren't any beans on the fries." She rang my order correctly, but I didn't ASK for no beans on the fries. I asked for no beans on the nachos. The manager was working in the back, making the food, and she was polite and offered to make a whole new order of the fries (they were missing the tomatoes, onions and sour cream). I assured her that I didn't need a whole new order, just to add the stuff on that I had paid for. 

I'd really like to give two stars to this restaurant just for the manager's service. But the fact that the drive-thru attendant was so rude and snippy makes me unable to give an extra star. I will be visiting the location on Eagle in Meridian, even though it's farther away and more difficult to get in and out of.

Love love love the burrito bowl. I think it's great that they have brown or white rice. Black beans ( or pinto ). Their mild salsa is more of a pico de gallo.... Fresh fresh fresh. You can get as much of this, as much cheese and sour cream. The meat choices are terrific...I have had the chicken, pork and steak. We always get a bag of chips (extra) and often guacamole (also extra). Always have leftovers...yum!

I really hate myself when I eat here. I only go on Monday for the family night specials and my kids like the clown they have in there. I hate it when the face paint smears all over the seats of my car and my kids are hitting me in the back of the head with their balloon swords on the way home. 

I once saw the cashier argue with a woman who received her cheeseburger with no burger. So it was a cheese sandwich deluxe with ketchup, mustard and pickle. He read her the receipt back and showed her clearly where it said, NO MEAT, and told her "some people just get it that way...." like she was the one who made the mistake and that a lot of people have been ordering the cheese sandwich deluxe lately. This was many months ago. 

I went back about a week ago and the dining room was a little cleaner. The floor was still a little slick. My whopper was standard, the way I ordered it. The staff was non-combative and they got everything right.

Eating at this BK is like being with a girl I used to date. It's peaks and valleys. Either I'll get my food as I've ordered it and everybody will be happy or there will be a topless man with ketchup war paint all over his face and a neck tie bandana standing on the counter fending people off with a bar stool while making the crazy eyes shouting jibberish at the top of his lungs.

Very tasty! I have gone to another Chipotle and it surprisingly tasted differently. I had the steak bowl and it was great! I took my 1 yr old and 4yr old here for the first time. The chicken was tasty to me but a little spicy for them. They enjoyed the rice and chips. The kids taco meal comes with choice of a protein, two sides, tortillas, drink and chips. Quite a bit of food for my toddlers but enough to choose from and nibble on. We will come again.

ANOTHER DRAWN-OUT REVIEW FOR THE PATIENT (WITH MANY COMPARE/CONTRASTS TO TACO BELL)

Yeah, I admit it...I'm still a sucker for the sketchy soylent-style creations at Taco Bell more so than Del Taco.  Especially walking out with four big items without denting the $6 mark.  That said, I have gotten sick from Taco Bell twice.  Considering I've eaten there probably 200+ times, I guess that's not such a bad percentage...my only advice there would be to avoid the late night (i.e. same batch of refried beans from the morning shift) menu.

Sorry I'm off topic so soon, but my point is I've never gotten sick from Del Taco.  (this is after only about 10 lifetime visits, mind you).  Overall the items seem a little fresher and less sketchy than Taco Bell fare.

It's hit and miss though for sure.  Jalapeno rings (just deep fried jalapeno slices) tasted OK but were dry and looked like they had sat under the heat lamp for 4 hours.  They're edible with the "secret sauce COUGH mayo COUGH" but without anything to dip those hard lil' nuggets in, they would have been a tough play indeed.  

Things improved with the carbonado (??) taco, I got chicken on mine.  Basically it's Del Taco's take on "al carbon" grilled style meat, kind of like the tacos they have at El Pollo Loco. (I'm comparing to a gringo fast food Mex-place here, since I figure if you're serious enough to read a Yelp review about Del Taco, you might not be a stickler for real authentic Mexican food...) Tender and flavorful, but grease-soaked tacos are usually (with the exception of drunken Jimboy's) not what I'm craving most meals.  Drain the meat a little better, people, hell, put the shit on a paper towel for 10 seconds...it'll work wonders!! Not a bad item though overall.

Things peaked with the shrimp taco.  Having had a fair amount of "Baja" style tacos years ago in the Santa Barbara area, I thought DT did a pretty damn good job on theirs.  Sliced cabbage, deep fried shrimp and that thin sour cream-ish sauce (and, of course, the required dose of hot sauce) made this item the star of the show.  Slightly less breading on the shrimp would take them from "popcorn shrimp" status to near-heaven, but hey this is fast food--can't nitpick too much.  Plus at $1.89 for a pretty hefty taco--WAAAYYY better deal than Taco Bell's fail "Pacific Shrimp" taco.  That sucker set me back $2.79 or so, had nasty sauce, and fairly tasteless shrimp.  DT hands down.

Finally, the macho burrito. I got a combo, chicken and beef I believe, and the results weren't exactly jaw-dropping.  Lettuce, cheese, beans, rice, sour cream, and a fair portion of OK (somewhat bland) meat...I've had better but it was still decent.  Size-wise, they're pretty monstrous for being $3 and change.  I can see the value on this item being a good selling point.  Fortunately Del Taco supplied packets of DEL INFERNO sauce which is (esp. for fast food) a HOT M.F. SAUCE.  I'm the guy who makes his Bloody Marys with habanero, grated horseradish and white pepper, so I'm no stranger to spice.  So when I say it buuuurnns like the neighborhood party girl, mama I mean it.  So once again, just decent food is saved by the hot sauce drown.  TB's "Fire Sauce" isn't nearly as spicy, but I'll give the flava point to TB here because it's a little heavier on the jalapeno.  

Flava points kids, remember to count them when I'm rap battling your toothless, haggard mama.  : D

So Del Taco is pretty OK, nothing too spectacular in my book.  Now I have heard great things about the fries, so next time I think I'll just hit some fries and a couple o' them shrimp tacos.  Easy on the pocketbook with most items, they also seem to have those multi-taco pack family deals as well as an intriguing breakfast special with 2 breakfast burritos and a side of hash browns for $2!!  Think I'll try that one out on the next road trip...

Location is across McCarran, just north of the Hell's Inferno, I mean the Northtowne Mall or whatever that thing is that houses WinCo and WalMart.  Being up on the hill Del Taco actually offers a good view of Reno, design-wise they should have exploited that feature a little better IMO.  But again it's a decent fast food joint, nothing more, nothing less.  Lots of Sun Valley spillover means a plethora of NasCar hats and face-chewers too, so if that kind of thing offends you (it amuses me) then stay away.

One star is too much for this place.  Never have enough staff so the service is awful.  The drive thru take at least 15 min no matter what time of day you go.  They will screw up at least one thing on your order every time too.  The manager here needs to get canned .

I hadn't been to KFC for a couple of years, there aren't many of them in the Boise area anymore.  Yelp directed me to this location while I was on my way home from work.  My husband worked for KFC for a number of years (in Ontario OR) and is NOT a fan of chicken (on the bone at least), nor does he like coleslaw- but I was REALLY hankering for some KFC so I went to nab some.   This location is one of those 'hybrids' .. housing both KFC and A&W.  I used the drive thru.  The menu board is well laid out and easy to ready.  When greeted I told the server I needed a moment, and there was no pressure.   I ordered an eight piece meal that included chicken, coleslaw, potatoes/gravy and biscuits.  I went with Original recipe. 
It's no secret that KFC is NOT good for you, it's heavy with salt, carbs and fat. It's fast food and junk food and not something most people would consider a treat.  It's certainly nothing I'd eat on a regular basis.  That being said.. I have to say that our meal was all we'd hoped it would be.. hot, tasty and convenient COMFORT food.   I paid a bit extra for all legs and breasts in our chicken bucket - it's nice to be able to customize. 
Things were a little slow when I got to the pick up window, I'm not sure if they were short staffed, it didn't seem terribly busy- but you know, we expect things in a heartbeat these days and sometimes we just need to breathe and enjoy the moment.  As I sat in my car on a rainy Friday evening, just grateful to be able to pick up some dinner on the way home.   The server who helped me was kind and personable, the food was hot and properly prepared- which to me makes for a good visit to a restaurant I'd go back to again, next time the urge hits me.

My husband and I had dinner here tonight because his parents gave us a gift for his birthday. I do have to say we eat out often. We haven eaten here a few times, but not as often as something just as fast like subway.So, first I want to say this they have a secrete menu which is totally awesome.  Tonight, I ordered off the menu they meted the cheese in the back and they brought it back out and I got half steak and chicken yes you can have both and only be charged for the one. Then I added everything which you get to add extra cheese at no charge if you would like on top of what was melted earlier. Finally, I topped it off with a secrete ingredient cilantro.  They always make the burros massive they also ask if you want chips and a drink.  Well, I took the drink and notice that 98% of cup is recycled and all meat is organic. The burro was great.  the setting isn't  very comfy its all wood or metal tops. The restaurant is pretty loud.. I will without a doubt be back

They demolished their old building and built it brand new and for weeks cars were lined up in their drivethru, but not anymore. The three times I've been there since they reopened were all horrible. The chicken was terribly greasy, (to the point I just didn't want to consume it) which is unusual because the KFC in Granite City always tastes fine to me. Friends have had bad experiences as well with them getting orders wrong. I'd knock it down to poor management.

I used to eat at chipotle all the time, and I loved the food and the whole idea of fresh ingredients. However, I've been ordering online recently and they have been non stop screwing up my order. Tonight I ordered a chicken burrito bowl with white rice fajita veggies lettuce cheese and salsa and ordered a burrito for my boyfriend. Other than waiting there for my food which was done 20 minutes prior, I got home and we opened up the bag to find that my boyfriends burrito had not been wrapped correctly and now had the permanent marker from my burrito bowl on it... So he ripped that piece off, no big deal. Then I open my burrito bowl; not only was it not chicken, but steak which I don't eat but the majority of the bowl was just sour cream... Which I didn't order because I LOATHE it. And because I had waited in line so long the food was ice cold. Luckily we also ordered the chips and guacamole, which is hard to screw up. I don't know what's going on at that place, but the service and quality of the food has gone WAY down. Chipotle, I suggest you fire EVERY employee in the horizon blvd store and hire all new. I'm terribly disappointed and will not be eating there again anytime soon.

Wow, where to start.  The place is a mess. Tables not wiped, floor not swept. The service was fast enough, but there were no vegetables. I could understand if they were busy, but we were the only ones there. There were two big chunks of fat in my burrito bowl, why wouldn't they throw them away when they cut up the steak? Do they really think anyone was going to eat them??
Went outside to eat (it was a nice day). Garbage can was overflowing, plants in planter boxes were dead, every table was dirty.  And two different employees were on break, both sitting within 10 feet of the garbage can and did NOTHING ABOUT IT. after break, they went back in, and never touch the garbage or wiped down a table.
Never again, there are too many choices to come back here again.

I wanted cheap fried chicken & that's what I got. Went in two days ago. They were out of chocolate chip cookies and chicken tenders. Oh, and I guess Pepsi but I didn't figure that out until AFTER I got home with my "Pepsi" aka Dr. Pepper. Food was standard.  Service sucked.

If i could give them no stares i would been there twice ordered 20 dollar fill up 8 pieces of chicken three sides. They gave me four extra,small legs. And two exextra small thighs chicken over cooked tuff.Macaroni& cheese watered down. Never again. Do not get irritated. I threw it out.
I love del taco but closing your dining room because your short staffed isn't ok. When some orders something as simple as a bean burrito and it's not even warm, also a issue.

I work very close to here and it's my number one go to place for lunch.  It meets my main criteria: Healthy, tasty, fast and good value for the cost.  I've been here countless times and it never disappoints.  I'm a big fan of the veggie burritos: brown rice, black beans, mild, medium, cheese, lettuce and guac.  Simple but delicious.  I've had a bunch of other stuff there though too: Steak burritos, bowls, chips.  All good.  

The ingredients always seem super fresh.  The nationwide chain has of course had some food contamination issues recently, but honestly, I feel like that could've happened with any chain as the number of sources to supply them have to be so huge.

I read an earlier review that said the portion size is weak.  I disagree.  I'm a pretty big guy and Chipotle never leaves me hungry.  I've even seen people ask for more when they don't think they've gotten enough of any ingredient and they seem to always be given it without any additional charge that I've noticed.

Qdoba is similar, and it's not horrible, but it never quite satisfies me as much as Chipotle does.

When I chose two stars yelp says "Meh.  I've experienced better."  And that's exactly how I felt about it.  I had a 3 taco meal with carnitas and thought the spices in the sauce just tasted off.  The guacamole was fantastic and the chips were pretty good, too.  

What really turns me off about Chipotle was the price.  They charged my husband 1.80 for adding guacamole to his burrito!  We spent more here than we would at a sit-down place.

I will tell you honestly that I prefer Qdoba over Chipotle.

I really was excited about my new job being near this Chipotle where I can just drop in for lunch. That was short lived. I attempted to give this place 3 chances, but every time the food was inconsistent, either over seasoned or under seasoned and the portions for meat were skimpy. I also noticed that they always let the pans run low before re-stocking while holding up line for ppl waiting on meat. . My co-worker was pissed because they scraped the bottom of the barrel steak from the pan and when she complained there was not enough meat they said they'd have to charge her extra for more! Really, like there was barely any meat!! Not only that, the chicken was so salty the last time I was there that I couldn't even finish it. I brought home takeout to my sister, who eats Chipotle like it's going out of style and she immediately noticed the under seasoned food and only ate half. I also attempted an online order as well and when I brought it back to work they had only finished half the order. My tomatoes, sour cream, corn, cheese & guac were MISSING!! As if it had only made it down half way through the assembly line and then got packed up! However, what really topped the cake was receiving a letter this past weekend that my credit card was compromised at Chipotle! This is the only location I've been to in months! Citrus Park Chipotle is in serious need of NEW MANAGEMENT!!!! STAT They've got kids working there moving like turtles while there are literally huge lines and employees standing around while tables are dirty. They also need re-training on how to properly prepare food. Chipotle please get the help you need!!! I really want to re-visit when you've gotten your crap together!!

While the price continues to get climb, the size of the burritos continue to get smaller. Had the steak burrito, chips, guacamole, (barely enough guacamole for 5 chips) and a coke for right at 15.00 dollars. Too many other options for the money. That was my last meal at this establishment.

Will not ever come back here again. Horrible Customer Service. Said it would be 10 minutes On some hot wings and chicken strips. They told me to pull over to the side. Over a half hour later when I complained they asked me what I ordered? WHAT? All of a sudden you would think aliens beamed everyone out. No employee to be found. After another 10 minutes the manager finally shows up and tells me they gave away my $36 bucks of food to someone else. I parked in the spot right in the drive through windows line of sight. This is not the first time I had to wait over 20 min at this place. Never again. Avoid at all costs. The very Definition of Incompetence.

Food/Drinks- 
Their new caramel coconut iced coffee is awesome! A must try! Definently cheaper then Starbucks, I can't wait to go get the pumpkin iced and hot coffee!! The wake up wraps are great for a little snack on the go.  If you are wanting a big breakfast I would get the Turkey Sausage Sandwich (great flavor, a little spice to it too), or get the Bacon, Egg, and Cheese Bagel (juicy and fresh).  

Store itself- 
Not very fast and it takes a while to get food, if you are inside waiting.  The drive thru is better.  Inside, it can confuse you, when they are busy, sometimes they have the ice cream area open too, and all the staff behind the registers are yelling "Next person in line" which then everyone is looking around to see if they are being talked to or thinking "can i jump out of this long line to get faster service." It just doesn't seem like great crowd control.

All in all, food and coffee outweighs the craziness of the inside.

Ok, first some context: since I discovered Chipotle, I've seen it for what it is: a slightly healthier type of fast food (despite sodium content) that tastes good, is reasonably priced and provides a consistent overall positive experience...so I don't have "white table cloth" expectations here.    Now if I were to rank Chipotle (this location, at least) on food alone, I'd probably give them at least 3 stars.  But here's my rub: service is inconsistent at best and typically not good.  On my two most recent visits, the first time there was a line back to the door and the staff was working frantically to move people through the "assembly line" as quickly as possible.  I watched as they messed up the order in front of me (carnitas, not chicken), and then, in their haste, nearly drowned my burrito bowl in sour cream, despite my saying "no sour cream, please" at least twice.  Then on my next visit, there was no one there.  Yet, (even though I was literally the only person at the counter), they rushed me through the assembly line, barely listening to what I was saying and nearly messing up my order (again). And don't get me started on how sloppy "to go" tacos are wrapped in foil.  Here's the bottom line: I can overlook a lot. And I expect anomalies - good days and bad days.  But the truth is the service has gotten steadily worse at this location to the point that it is consistently bad. On my last visit, I told myself that despite it's convenience, I will no longer frequent this Chipotle.

I really enjoy the foods here. Not true Mexican foods, of course, but good. I also enjoy the burgers, and fries. Nice to be able to go to the same place for a fresh cooked burger as the place for a burrito or taco. 
The main reason I go here often is that they are close to my home. Also since I do eat here, maybe too often, many of the workers know me by name. 
Watch them and like them on face-book and they send coupons for free foods about ounce a month.

Last time I went to chipotle I had a horrible experience .. The line was very long and this location didn't not have enough employees ... No brown rice or steak for my taco bowl..  My husband got a burrito and while eating it he found a rock ... never going there again !!!

I was surprised how nice this restaurant looked when I walked in. I usually don't pay attention to that kind of thing but it looked clean, inviting and comfortable. From what little contact I had with the staff, they were also friendly and helpful.
They also offer free wifi.
The food is still KFC food. I wasn't unhappy with my chicken or mashed potatoes, but the food isn't reason enough for me to drive all the way over here.
I love fried chicken and this kfc delivers.  I come here for lunch and honestly tastes consistent and delicious every time. It's kfc guys , it's not rocket science. Grew up eating this stuff.

This place can never get an order right. 
Not to mention they seem to be hording the taco sause. Everytime I end up driving away without sause, even when I ask for it in advance. Then everytime the tacos are slightly dusted with tomato, maybe just  one or two crumbs worth per taco. Is there a tomato shortage or somthing???

At first I was going to give the place a 2 star review then when I saw that they had a stack of antiTrump propaganda rags on thier counter. After that I decided to give them a 1 star. Also netherme nor my family will never return to this location again. 

Trump IS my president and I will not patronage any establishment that shows him such unwanted disrespect.....

I love Chipotle a lot, and eat at various ones often. They must have been training people because burrito shells were splitting, out of fajita veggies, and no salt shakers to add flavor to the unusually bland steak burrito. All in all, it was ok for the amount spent. But I have certainly had better and faster service at a Chipotle.

If i could do half a star it would work.friday nite 6 pm no chickrn cooked.i would attribute it to being busy but.the chicken we got was cold..not even luke warm.somebody dropped the ball and we paid for it..the chicken was at our request what they had lleft original and crispyto fill the order of an 8 piece bucket.when we waited for the order we heard them giggling  and playing at the drivethru. Bad management? No management? Shame we wasted $14 dollars on a bad take out but hopefully after we speak to corporate tomorrow they might improve?

I've always been very fond of chipotle, when they first came into town they used to give you the biggest burritos ever they were so over stuff but it seems like as a company.  I think they might've realized by doing so they were losing money what I really like about them is that they use very fresh ingredients The vegetables the lettuce even all the sides that how they make their salsa and guacamole.  I really enjoyed the burrito bowls, Time to time I will also get a burrito, The chicken, steak are my favorite but I've also tried the pork which is also very tasty. no need to fret if you don't eat  meat they have a veggie bowl & now they are also even offering a tofu meat substitution. I was over all please I only wish that you didn't have to pay almost 2 dollars for a tiny bit of guacamole, and that it was very delicious and I'm pleased with going,  I will definitely be back soon.

As others have said, this Chipotle establishment has gone extremely down hill over time. I've been here more times to count, and the past 4-5 times have been nothing short of abysmal. 

This past time will be my last. On top of the rude and disrespectful staff, there always seems to be core burrito ingredients missing during all hours of the day. I've been there when beans, rice, steak, chicken, chips, and cheese have been out of stock. Coincidentally, every time I've been there they have never had a shortage of their 'world famous guac'. Convenient how the item they charge $1.95 for is always abundantly overstocked.

The worst part is you have no knowledge of said missing ingredients until after waiting fifteen minutes in line. I mean c'mon. CHEESE? I feel like a place that thrives on burritos should let you know they're out of cheese before you're at the end of their assembly line. 

To top this off, every burrito I've had made in these past 4-5 times has been ripped. They simply have stopped asking if you'd like a re-wrap after their failure to roll the burritos, and rather try to sneak away with a mediocre finished product. If I wanted a taco salad, I'd order a taco salad.


In closing, don't go to this Chipotle. It's a joke of a franchise, and the franchisee in charge needs to get their act together. Go to West Chester or Wayne.

July 1, 2013 Monday - lunch 12:30 PM
3 adults & kid

Atmosphere - 1 star
Food - 4 stars
Service - 5 stars

Background - Meeting up my parents and daughter for lunch.

Atmosphere - Normal I don't use the restrooms but oh my, it was disgusting!  We will we will leave it at that.

Food
  Appetizer - none ordered.

  Drinks - I skipped it as I drink a lot of water while at work.  For the record they have coke products, daughter got a red Fanta out of the soda machine.

  My Main Course - Went with a classic, the Famous Star with cheese.  It was stuffed with lettuce a large slice of tomato.  It hit the spot.  I had a small order of sweet potato fries.  I wish I had ordered a large, oh well, they were crispy and delicious.  

  Dessert - None today.

Service - What you expect from a fast food restaurant.  What I like about Carl's is they give you a number and bring it to your table instead of waiting up by the registers

Future - Maybe if I do not need to use the restroom.

Burger King is upping their game in the fast food market place. I am liking their new menu additions. My wife and I ordered the new Carolina BBQ Whopper, the new Pulled Pork sandwich, the new Sweet Potato fries and the new Bacon Sundae. All were very good. The stand out is the Sweet Potato fries, great job on those, I hope the cooking and taste standards stay high. We liked the Bacon Sundae, they loaded on the bacon and we are not complaining! :) It is rare when a sundae is bigger than advertised but this one is. We liked the flavor of the new Pulled Pork Sandwich. Nice BBQ flavor with a nice mayo/mustard type sauce and lots of onions, which I like. The Carolina BBQ Whopper is basically a Whopper with bacon and BBQ sauce, tasted good and fresh, keep it up! Lastly, my daughter ordered Chicken Strips and they were perfectly cooked and very good. Burger King is upping their game and I hope they keep up the high taste and cooking standards. I will be back to try the new Texas BBQ Whopper which has jalapenos in it. I do wish all BK locations had the California Whopper (with the avocado guacamole) but it still seems to be only at BK Burger Bar locations. Posted some photos.
This place is always great we love the buffet we were sad that they stopped serving grilled chicken but its always hot and fresh

I am a huge fan of Chipotle. Seriously, over the past eleven years (which is when I first discovered the chain) I've been to a Chipotle at least once a week. So, it truly pains me to have to give one a poor review. 

As far as taste goes, this Chipotle is actually one of the better ones I've been to. The three times I've been, the salsa was fresh, rice was fluffy, and the meat was nicely seasoned. HOWEVER, two of the three times I've been, my stomach has gotten really upset. My order at Chipotle is always the same, save alternating my meat choice--I get white rice, pinto beans, carnitas or barbacoa, all four salsas, and lettuce. My stomach has been digesting this stuff since 2003. This is the only Chipotle that has made me sick. And as I said, it happened twice. Yeah, I love Chipotle so much that I went back after it messed me up the first time. I didn't want to admit my beloved bowl did me wrong. 

On top of the stomach cramps that had me down for quite some time,  the service leaves something to be desired. I naturally say please and thank you to everyone that helps me. I've never heard anyone say it back, but that's probably because they didn't hear me--they're too busy talking to their work buddies. I don't mind people having conversations at work, but it shouldn't be so extensive that the customer feels that they're interfering if they ask for a cup of extra salsa. 

I love the Chipotle brand, and wanted to love this location, too--but it ain't happenin'.

Original still the best.  Chicken pieces have shrunk over the years but still gooooood.  The employees are friendly and helpful and put in more than enough condiments when you get order to go.  You still can't go wrong with the Colonel.
Got my food quickly and no complaints about the service. I only ordered a Whopper Jr., which cost around $2.50--not bad at all, especially by airport standards.

Always delivers fresh good fast.  The portions are large and filling.  All of the ingredients are fresh and meat without hormones.  The cilantro lime rice that comes as part of most meals is incredible.  You can choose all type if meats or none -  the carnitas is savory.  The guacamole is very good so you may want to add for $2.  

Parking is very tight at this location so going at off time or take out is best.

Stopped by on my way to get on the 275 to go to St Pete. The BK Whopper is my favorite burger - double with cheese and bacon is how I like it. I always ask them to make it like the picture on the menu board when I'm ordering - that way if it doesn't come the way I like it they knew I wanted the burger to look like the picture on the menu board. This BK was super clean with them coming around every few minutes to clean any tables that just turned over. Good location overall. I'll come back next time I'm passing by.

I can finally say I tried the Tofu (Sofritas) and it was good!

As I'm trying to eat clean these days.. I wanted to go to here and see if I could walk out with less than 1000 calories - Mission accomplished!

Got a burrito bowl with Sofrita (sans guac and sour cream) and it was really tasty. 

There was a long line when I got here but it went really fast and the staff was friendly. I'll be back.

Haven't been to Chipotle  in a while but don't remember it like this location. The food was decent. The steak was luke warm and dry. And too much cilantro. My son's meal had almost no food on it . It had 1 or 1/2 a quesadilla, a disgusting brown orange fruit, and cold rice. My food was ok just hanging all off the plate like they were in a hurry but the funny thing was I was the only person in line so maybe if they took there time instead of trying to get in on the conversation of their friends my food would have looked better.  Next when we got the the main table in the front was full of lettuce and cheese and needed to be wiped off. Next when in line we couldn't hear the woman who was speaking to us because all of her co-workers  were talking about axe murderers and killing and what they were going to do this weekend . My family and I had to wait til they were done talking to hear the one taking our order. Next I know it's not the biggest deal ever but the teas were labeled wrong . Also I know trash smells but either the trash smelled like vomit or the soda machine needed cleaned cause it smelled so bad. Next was the dinning area, and holy cow trash was littered everywhere, chips and garbage all over the floor and tables. I had to clean off my table to sit at it. During the cleaning of my table there was an employee not on her break because she had her phone plugged in and playing on it and texting.  All in all I don't think I could come back here too many negatives and it was just a disgusting restaurant and I couldn't recommend  people coming here.  I already told a few not to go there.

1) waited 50 mins in drive thru. No way to leave since there were card in front and in the back.
2) it was finally my turn to pay and i am told they ran out of chicken drumsticks so only thighs. Fine? I'm starving. I asked for ketchup, hotsauce, napkins, and plates.
3) wait another 5 mins. My stuff comes and i drive home. Opens bag... NONE of the items i asked for in the bag.
I am NEVER returning to this location. I'll drive an extra .5miles to the oracle location if it means better service.
Fast food is supposed to be fast. 
I went today at 7pm and didnt get home until 8pm
This Del Taco has gone from pretty good to bottom of the barrel they are so goddam slow it's unbelievable

Dirtiest KFC I have ever seen. Food on floor behind the register, on the prep table, floor filthy where they put the orders together. And very very slow. The girl kept disappearing on the other side of the prep table. Made me wonder what she was doing with my food. Then handed my order to me with the lids not tight and sad "here you go". No thank you or sorry about the wait. Go get chicken from Publix instead.
Great place to eat!  Fast service, fresh food and natural ingredients.  The staff is friendly and I love the carnitas.  One of the best fast casual restaurants in Tucson.

Chipotle is basically a burrito joint. As far as burrito joints are concerned, this one is above average.  I was surprised to see this particular location hadn't been added to Yelp, and since I enjoy a good burrito, I thought I'd pass this along to my fellow Westchasers.  

The place is nice and clean, like you would expect in most chain fast food restaurants.  The menu is not extensive, so if you are not in the mood for a burrito, a taco, or a bowl or salad derived from burrito ingredients, look elsewhere.  

But, if you are in the mood for a burrito, you may have come to the right place.  You can take your pork, chicken, or steak and get it in a burrito tortilla, a bowl, a taco, or a salad.  Everything I've tried was tasty.  However, if you are on Weight Watchers, I hate to disappoint you.  This is not a place for you.  Not even the salad with chicken.  By the time you take all the good stuff (beans, guacamole, sour cream, etc.) out, you might as well not go. But, if you've behaved all week, and you have a nice stock of Points Plus stashed away somewhere (or an over abundance of Activity Points), Chipotle is one of the places I'd splurge.   I'm a fan.

DEL TACO IS NOT WORTH YOUR MONEY GO SONEWHERE ELSE. 

Worst Drive Thru Service I've ever experienced in my entire life I swear these girls either don't care about there's no or it's just stupidity honestly leaning towards the second one. My food was terrible the chicken tacos were salty and by far the most skimpy portions I've seen from a del taco ever. Pretty sure my tacos sat in the bag waiting for French fries. Whoever owns this place should take the time to read these reviews this place needs to hire some people that care. 
 
I would have went back and asked for a refund if I hadn't waited in the drive thru for 20 minutes.

Typical Del Taco with burgers/tacos/fries.

Decent food, decent prices.

Normally only 3 stars for all Del Tacos in my book.

Extra star for the funny and nice lady in the drive through who made fun of the Mr when he got tongue tied ordering.

This KFC location sucks big time!  The one on Cortaro, while not great, at least has what I want to order.  Each time I go they are out of something (chicken tenders, mac n cheese, heck even the soda machines do not work).  Plus they are slow and never get my order right (forgetting my biscuit really?).

I have always been a fan of Del Taco. Growing up, my friends always wanted to visit Taco Bell, but my go to place was DT. Depending on where you go and who is working, the consistency has never been that of a place like McDonalds where you know what you're going to get. At DT the quesadilla may be overdone or underdone, the spicy chicken burrito may have a smidgen of green sauce or a glob of it all at the bottom. Thankfully, it's more hit than miss.

But now we come to this particular Del Taco. While the food is typical Del Taco quality, which I really like (most of the time) the customer service is extremely poor. In fact, when it comes to customer service, this is probably the worst DT I have ever been too. Like I said, I have been here a number of times and not once has ANY of the employees ever smiled. Of course, that means management here is poor, and if the management doesn't pay attention to the customer service portion of the company are they paying attention to the cleanliness of the kitchen and the quality of the food?

There is even an older man that works there (when I say older I mean he's not your typical teen or 20 something employee) that refers to male customers as "guy". Instead of a verbal transaction between customer and business employee being what it should be, "Hello, how can I help you today?" a conversation with this man goes like this, "Hey guy, whatta ya have?" Really? You might as well call me "Sport" or "Buddy". So unprofessional. 

Now when I have a hankerin' for the #8 combo I drive further than I want to and visit the Del Taco in Spanish Springs where the employees are usually very nice and the food is a bit more consistent than normal.

Great service , friendly staff!! I had two piece dinner combo. Fyi. They tend to run out of potato .wedges.very popular. The 5 dollar deals have so  many varieties.  My friend had the $5.00 fillup. Great deal get full meal mashed potatoes​ biscuit and crispy chicken tenders Plus unlimited refills on drinks which is pretty cool. And also a decadent chocolate chip cookie.#crazy good!!!  Haven't been here in several years has been renovation done and nice to eat in . Def  my chicken  meal was so juicy and fresh and the sides were yummy after not eating for couple nites.I was very  hungry  , and happy I checked out this location on Route 38 next to Citgo gas. And right before the Mall. I definitely, was happy with the really sweet guys taking our orders. In fact , one of the workers was taking his dinner break and to salt and pepper everything but had none. Thnx to Goomasch, he left his food and personally got me packets.of salt,& ,pepper . What great customer service.  Def high marks and def take my word for it I tell it like it is, and  excellent.food & 
Awesome  service. I am pretty surprised at the low reviews becuz I was impressed. They also renovated the store recently it gets my thumbs up, .

This location is very dirty. They always run out of chicken, which is odd because they sell chicken! Employees here are also very rude and don't have the highest level of hygiene. The best advice I can give is, do not eat here.

Generally good quality food but order accuracy could be much better...don't dare ask for any variation from the standard burger such as extra onions..it won't happen.. I ordered a Whopper JR with extra onion and I guess to be smart they only put one slice. See my picture I posted. Pretty much either fast food choice at this exit would be a bad one..McDonalds across the street is 10 times worse with more problems.

Chipotle makes it easy to eat a healthy and flavorful meal. I always get the same thing. Salad with carnitas, cheese, hot salsa, and guacamole. This is a great low carb and high protein meal. Can't beat it!

Walked inside 10 minutes ago, no customers inside at 8:01 p.m. 8 people working in the back, all talking. The lady in front of me, never acknowledged me. I stood there for four and a half minutes while two people flirted while the big fat guy sliced steak. One of the employees eating in the lobby was conversing with the girl with the nose ring.  She completely ignored me the entire time. I turned and walked out and will take my business elsewhere. No sense of urgency, no hello, no acknowledgement that THERE are customers in the store. Pathetic. I won't go back.

I'm usually a big chipotle fan but they disappointed me today.  Their service was FAR from excellent.  I was not greeted upon arrival, nor was I treated with respect.  I ordered a steak burrito and they said "its gonna be a while" and scooped me the chicken.  The attitude of this worker was disheartening as my experiences at Chipotle are usually quite positive.  As I proceeded to check out I decided to add a drink at the last minute and the cashier huffed and puffed and slammed the cup on the counter.   Although my food was amazing, my service was NOT. In the future I will be going to an alternative Chipotle....

Time for my one fast food burger of the year, yes today and this year's choice is BK whopper and fries.  $5.29 well spent.  Nice bun though I wish they could get the lettuce concept down like In N Out do.  Meat and all the trimmings perfectly balanced.
The order taker seemed to be incapable of understanding the English Language but after four attempts at word order we nailed it.  Place could have done to be a bit cleaner, gotta stay on top of those tables peeeps.

We decide to get some chicken from KFC instead of pop eyes and it was horrible experience. We had to wait 30 mins for chicken to be cooked. We only order 12 pieces and it was half crispy and original and I want some wing and person told me they don't have any wings at the store. Last time I came here about 10 years ago they didn't have any chicken. I guess I will stop coming here for good.

Very tasty! I have gone to another Chipotle and it surprisingly tasted differently. I had the steak bowl and it was great! I took my 1 yr old and 4yr old here for the first time. The chicken was tasty to me but a little spicy for them. They enjoyed the rice and chips. The kids taco meal comes a protein, two sides, tortillas, drink and chips. Quite a bit of food for my toddlers but enough to choose from and nibble on. We will come again:-)
My order was wrong, Did not receive the requested sauce for my popcorn chicken. Soda was completely ice. MEH.

Service is usually pleasant, and I haven't had a problem at this location until I had to pick up an online order. The waiting in this location sucks. They really need a designated area, but it's small so I get it, but it still isn't ideal. However, had to order online and obviously couldn't check my order til opening the burrito and bowl...and my bowl was not to order at all. Steak was so dried up it was chewy and looked like chicken and they left out 3 of my ingredients. And to top it off, I felt like crap later that night after eating it. I don't care if it was because it was cinco de mayo and busy....it sucked that day :( 
I'll never order online again - in person or bust.

Ugh I think I changed this review too soon. Now I'm switching to two stars as an average or so of the reviews. Today I go, first they don't sell some of the sandwiches that are on the board anymore wth. So I end up ordering a turkey sausage egg and cheese, hash Browns and an iced coffee. First he forgot the hash browns so I had to ask for them, nbd, then I get home and my sandwich has no sausage. Wtf I end up eating an egg and cheese sandwich. They were trending positively getting better. Hopefully this is just one dude who messed up (twice) but it's still annoying given that they were finally getting better. I gave them another chance after they were so bad and now I'm eating an egg and cheese sandwich. Ugh.

Previous three star review edit: 


This place has gotten better so I'm bumping my review from one to three stars. Seems like they blew away the whole staff and rehired and the service is much better now. Waited a while before going back but now I've had better service a few times in a row.


Original one star review:

Every review so far has been a one star and I'm here to follow suit. Horrible service, I usually go in, they have a handful of people standing around in plain sight but it takes forever for them to come over to the counter to take your order without calling for them. They are constantly out of the better donuts and bagels so I can never get what I actually want. They don't mix up the iced coffees so you always get a separated coffee that you then have to try to mix up in the car without spilling it everywhere. If you can, go somewhere else this place is pretty bad.

After catching a movie at the Citrus Park Regal Cinema, my family and I wanted a healthy meal for dinner. I was excited to see sofritas offered as a burrito bowl choice, however, the service ruined the meal for me. The servers working the line were full of piss and vinegar which is not the kind of toppings I normally order with my burrito bowl. After served attitude, I brought it to the attention of the manager who seemed less than apologetic. We are such huge fans of Chipotle's fast, fresh healthy choices, but the poor service and attitude has caused us serious indigestion. It will be long time before we return to this location.

I prefer to use the drive thru here and get orders to go, but they do offer a all you can eat buffet inside for about $8. The food we get to go has always been hot and tasted good, except for the slaw we recently got with a 20 piece original meal. The slaw was dry and not the cool creamy yummy cole slaw that we normally get from KFC. The mashed potatoes, gravy, biscuits, and chicken were all hot and tasty.
