Their chicken and meals do cost more than going to a grocery store for hot ready to eat fried chicken, but KFC is much higher quality, so you get what you pay for, usually.

Awful service. Fast food is a dubious name when there is a 15 minute wait for certain types of chicken. Server named Quashira was extremely rude after we asked to have all our food so it all would be hot. Then when asking where we should wait she replied "can't you see all that space over there?" Honestly, the worst services I have had at a fast food restaurant.

I like Chipotle in general, but this place give skimpier portions, and the food is on the cold side.  I had the carnitas, and it was tough and in huge chunks that were hard to break apart.  Usually carnitas are tender and pretty shredded, thus perfect for burrito bowls.

The service is slow and the employees obviously aren't there to be helpful.  They're out kinds of chicken and won't/can't make more. Plus they're out of all sauces ranch... but ask what sauce you'd like.  Stay away!

They have always been so convenient for me living right down the road but this last trip proved to be my last one. Saturday 9:00 am I asked for a chicken biscuit and come to find out they discontinued it. I ordered a turkey sausage on English muffin with no cheese, 3 hash browns. They read my order back saying no cheese I pulled up and got my food made it almost back home to find out the hash browns were cold and there was cheese on the Muffin. I'm lactose intolerant so I had to go back and when I told them they said they would replace it but they did with another one with cheese. 2 strikes on the cheese and cold hash browns on a Sat and you are done in my book. The drive through girl was also very rude about giving me another even though I said I'll pay for it. Eat at your own risk.

Out of Chicken. Gave very little mashed potatoes. Fried were cold and hard. Talked to manager didn't seem to care. Ontario had it going on. This one is lame. I'll wait for kfc there again. To bad as i wanted good chicken not lame tasting and cold 
Very little bacon on the burgers as well.
I eat at this chipotle once a week. It's the best Mexican food around. My only problem is every piece of steak has an indestructible piece of fat in it. Otherwise good food.
Wow, what a joke this place is. Sat in drive thru for 15 minutes. One bag of food people. One star is being generous.

Best tofu!! 
I read reviews and was nervous but skimming them I noticed a great deal caring about the employees which doesn't bother me -I care about the food lol it's fast food. I noticed the food complaints were meat related- phew -I'm a vegetarian so I have no clue what fatty meat or digestive issues or upset stomachs are - my family eats a lot of meat followed with heart disease pills.
My review : I was so pleased to see tofu on their menu and just have to say wow! This was the best tofu I've had comparing to many vegetarian restaurants and whole foods deli etc  -I mean hands down best seasoning for tofu. I made a wrap with rice black beans tofu sour cream and cheese and hot sauce- yum ! The guacamole and chips were delicious - the chips have seasoning on them and the guac is fresh. 
The long line went fast and the employees were robots which was great for me but apparently some people want conversations during a fast line-that set up is to get you in and out not chat- if you're looking to exchange names and get to know employees behind the counter go to a local deli I guess lol. I worked at a deli and had time to do that. I also worked at a popular chain restaurant lol that picture of a mess is nothing compared to what's behind the doors at restaurants. 
No carnitas a sign read at the counter due to no supply of responsibly fed pigs - I was very relieved to see they care about their supply chain and where everything comes from -very important.

This must be the worst chipoltle.   The lines are always long and the portion sizes are small.  

The steak served here is consistently full of fat. 

It's a rare occasion if i don't have to beg for additional fajitas which are usually not made. 

In short be prepared to wait for small portions.

word is Mad Mex is coming to the area.  I can't wait!

Ive been there tons of times it was great. Im not sure what happened.  Everytime my wife gets food  from here they short her on items like meat one teaspoon of meet in a taco for instance thats unacceptable.My burrito was short on steak and when I asked for sour cream they put one cup of sour cream on it. California Tortilla is a little further away I will go there from now on or untill they fix it . Oh we ordered chips and salsa with our meal the chips tasted foul and were soggy seriously you cant even get chips right.

Chipotle has the best burritos. Their menu is very limited.  Pretty much burrito, burrito with no wrap in a bowl, or tacos are your options. I think you might be able to talk them into a quesadilla. As far as sides you can get some chips with salsa or guacamole and that's it. If you get a burrito and a drink it's about $8.

I like the trendy interior. They do a good job at keeping it clean. They always seem to have some pretty good music going.

I would recommend looking at the meats before choosing one. I like the pulled beef barbacoa, but sometimes seeing it I will go with steak instead. The chicken is good too. I think the pork sucks after trying it a couple times.

I don't know what's up with all the bad reviews on here. Do they get orders wrong? Sure, they are a 24 hour fast food restaurant that gets very busy at certain times. Happened to me too. But you can't beat the prices of Del Taco. Beans are fresh, compared to competitor. $1 strawberry lemonade, very refreshing and yummy shrimp seafood tacos! I think this place is a good deal guys!  Pretty good food for what you are paying.

Chipotle locations are basically the same wherever you go. Lines move fairly quickly and service is always great. We got to this one right before the lunch rush hit, and when we left, the line was about 8 people deep.

I know they've recently increased their prices on some things, but getting double steak is an extra $3.50. Seriously?? I'm sure there is some Chipotle hack to get it cheaper, like ordering a kid's meal or just getting a burrito with steak. 

I appreciate that this location has two entrances and a little patio area to sit on. Tucson weather is gorgeous, so 9 days out of 10 you can enjoy your food out there.

We have  tried Del Taco in the past and thought that the only reason to go there was because it was nearby...  Spend the extra gas and time and go someplace else.  Somehow you forget or figure they've had to have improved soooo...    Mostly it just comes down to what they say on the menu is "seasoned beef". I think they would be better off to just shred some pictures of beef and it would taste and feel better than whatever they are passing off as "beef".  The texture makes me think it's just some kind of oat/wheat/grain, mush they add taco seasoning to.  All of my "cro-magnum man" meat senses were blaring and clanging, shouting FAKE FAKE FAKE!!  Anyhow... Bleeeeaaack!  Just one other thing... One of the adjectives they use throughout the menu just ain't right.  "WET"? Makes me think they dropped the stuff in a rain gutter and, well you get it.

Place really went down hill. Not sure if there's a new owner or managers but this used to be my favorite quick food stop. Now I'm hardly ever there because the food has lost its quality and taste. The serving sizes have shrunk exponentially. Nothing like it used to be. Ordered via online today and my double steak bowl had a total of 10 pieces of meat in it. That's pathetic compared to what they used to be. The staff is turning over so fast that they're no longer friendly faces and all seem to be very rude and rush their guests. The line also moves extremely slow now as well. Nothing like it used to be. Very disappointing from a restaurant that used to be an old favorite of mine.

We stopped in this Hardee's one night as we were in a hurry and needed a quick bite. The food is as you would expect from a Hardee's. What sets this place apart from other fast food joints?The manager, he of has to be the best I've ever seen in any restaurant. I observed several other customers nearby with incorrect or slow orders. The manager personally visited each table, apologize for the mishaps, and offered a full refund. You don't see that type of service in five star establishments. Average food but great customer service.

Drove over 20 minutes to finally try Chipotle, was pretty excited. Took a quick glance at their menu but all I wanted was nachos. Once I get there, no nachos. I'm sorry but if you call yourself a "fast food" tex-mex and you don't have nachos, not even a queso, then you fail. I decided to do the soft carnitas tacos. Guac was extra but I got it. Took the food to go and by the time I got home it was cold. Not warm or cool. Cold. It was pretty tasty but SUPER sloppy. Overall very disappointing.  Get a queso and throw it on some chips and we'll talk.

HOW ARE THEY STILL IN BUSINESS!!??

People stop coming here so a better chicken place can take its place. It's our responsibility to run these guys out of business who are bad service. 

I REPEAT DO NOT GO HERE!!!!!!!!

I walked in at almost 12:00 on the dot. Lunchtime! No one was in line.. a little odd. No cashier at register however drive thru guy greeted me said they'd be right with me. 5 mins later the woman working the cash register came up. 

First off no smile or anything when she greeted me even when I smiled to her to say hello. Secondly immediately after I said hello, all she had to say what can I get for you like I was doing her a disservice by being there. Ok? Thought customers were good not bad? But whatever. 

Then I try to get the #6 3 tender $5 meal. Which by the way they are no longer $5! They are now $5.50! Which is whatever. However I try to watch what I eat and I knew I would probably not eat the biscuit or cookie and new I would not need the drink because I drink water. 

So I asked her kindly if I could substitute ALL 3 of the items for 1 extra strip. All I got was a firm no with a straight face. This has got to be the rudest cashier ever. 

As I am talking with her trying to figure out why (because she gave NO explanation) she just gets ruder and ruder. Saying stuff like do you understand in a VERY condescending way like I'm stupid for asking. 

Any way I eventually walked away and went to eat somewhere else. No wonder not one person was in line at 12:00 lunch time. 

But if Anyone who is looking for outstanding chicken place that's my favorite. It's called Raising Canes. The sucky part is it's all the way on wilmot and broadway. But they have by far the biggest and tastiest strips I have ever found at a fast food restaurant. I usually get 4 strips, fries, coleslaw, big piece of Texas toast, a drink, and a cane sauce for $7. And they allow you to substitute any item with whatever or just minus it off and they take off the money. There amazing I just wish they would open one on the northwest side of town. 

Anyway, hope this helps someone.

I'm usually pretty easy-going when it comes to dunkin donuts ordering. When there are long lines and everyone's in a hurry, there's bound to be mistakes made. This place, however, is consistently making mistakes.

There was one other person ahead of me in-line today. Yet, it took more than five minutes to get my caramel iced coffee and turkey sausage egg and cheese on English muffin. And that was after the female employee working there gave me an English muffin with just a 1/2 slice of turkey sausage/spinach egg white on it. That was certainly not what I ordered? When I approached her she made it out to be my fault, claiming that's not what I had ordered.

Seriously? The rudeness of that employee was the straw that broke the camel's back. I had gotten numerous incorrect orders and stale coffee in the past from that place. Still, silly me kept returning because it's the only DD in Newtown. 

I've finally had enough of this place. Hopefully they can get it together!

If I could give them no stars I would, it's a shame you have to give at least one star to start a review. My family and I are vegan and it is a challenge finding vegan fast food eats around town. One of our all time favorites WAS Chipotle. The reason I say was is because yesterday after ordering our normal vegan burritos we got home, starting eating and noticed 2 big pieces of steak in one of the burritos. Now for us all being vegan it's a lifestyle, a religion we follow and it's more then just finding something in our food that shouldn't be there it was an animal that had suffered that we absolutely don't believe in eating or harming in anyway for that matter. Not only did that make my family and I instantly sick to were we couldn't eat nothing for the rest of the night but it went against our believes and truly made us and our household just feel somber. When we first started going to chipotles as vegans we did our due diligence asking about cross contamination, how the menu ideas are stored, prepared, and served, and was reassured they make it a priority not to cross contaminate, as well as it being an option on PETA website for vegans and vegetarians, well apparently that wasn't the case for my family and I last night and we are beyond deeply disappointed and feel disgusted to have eaten there so many times. I'm currently in the process of writing PETA as well to have this addressed. Now for others I can see how this may seem like not a big deal but when you have a life style that you religiously follow and are passionate about its insulting for someone else to interfere with your beliefs. Now I understand accidents happen but weather it's an accident or not someone needs to be held responsible for it. I of course immediately called the chipotle location on broadway located by park mall where we had ordered and reported what happened trying to hold my tears back and they offered to make me another burrito, uh absolutely not are you kidding me my family and I are never eating at a chipotles again. I spoke to the manager to get a refund for the food I purchased since it wasn't hardly eaten nor would it be and he said since it was place online or on the mobile app he couldn't help and I could google a number for chipotles customers service and call them directly, wow I felt like another slap in the face I said,"no you guys are the ones that did this you need to google and give me the number to call in order to have this taken care of if you don't have it", like wtf. Mind you I was mad but kept it professional the whole time I was talking to them, the manager Luis placed me on hold then came back and said he would call the main number or whatever and have someone call me back, he took my name and number but of course here we are the next day and I still haven't heard anything. I find it beyond disheartening that not only did I find meat in my burrito but the customer service and level of responsibility was so low it's extremely dissatisfying and disappointing to say the least. If this was handled properly with sympathy and empathy then I probably would not have even written this review or contact PETA. What a shame. The worst part is it was such a small order like $15 the store could have easily fixed it. For us it's definitely not about the money but the principal, if my 6 year old son gets sick because he's not use to eating animal products then that's not something that can be compensated so let's just hope none of us ate any or any of us get sick from this experience. I just can't stop shaking my head. Again what a shame. I will be calling them back today to have this addressed for the second time which let's be honest it should have been handled the first.

Update...

I just heard from Jeremy with I think the chipotle customer service and he had a lot more sympathy and is in the process of issuing a refund check to be mailed to me.

We were in the area around noon and decided to grab a quick lunch. Burned out on turkey subs on honey oat, I suggested BK, because I had just seen an ad for $1.29 Whopper Jrs. We sat in the drive-thru line for around 5 minutes & didn't move, not even an inch. I (the girl on fire - - for a flame grilled burger) convinced my friends to go inside. Maybe the drive thru person was new, it had to be better inside, right?

When we got inside, there were about 10 people in line, 2 workers were taking orders, and, yeah, the line was moving, barely. Or it was until one of the 2 register workers walked away.  That's when I noticed a bunch of customers over by the drink station. They had ordered and were patiently waiting for their "fast food". One must have dared to ask a worker how much longer because the worker said loudly and with some attitude: "Four Minutes".

Finally, I got to order my $1.29 Whopper. I also ordered a small diet coke. The cashier gseemed shocked as she loudly asked me "SMALL?" I thought maybe I'm the only one left who doesn't desire extra large everything with extra mayo like the man in front of us got. After paying and while waiting a LONG time for our order, I realized my small drink set me back $1.79 plus tax. Then I saw on the "value" part of the menu that they offer a "value" sized drink for $1.00. Not sure if it's a little smaller or bigger than "small" but no way the "small" is worth 79 cents more. Really, I hope BK enjoys the extra 79 cents they got from me as much as I now cherish being a former customer.

P.S. Since we got our "fast" food and so-overpriced drink to go, I won't comment on the condition of their nasty dining area.

The best food in a KFC ever.  Big juicy chicken and all really hot.  The sides I got were also really hot and filled to the top.  By the way they were also great.  I am pinning this KFC in the St. Louis area.

Don't pass them by and I am gonna warn you...the chicken served here is huge...and juicy.  Thanks S Grand KFC.

I've been eating chipotles more here in stl mo then when I lived back in Chicago it's weird but I actually enjoy the steak tacos and my husband always gets the burrito bowl we keep coming back once a month for our fresh Mexican treat alternative.

You guys restored my faith in KFC. 

I have been driving across town for fried chicken at a competitor because my closest KFC is so horrible (Oracle and Ft. Lowell)

Was so pleased to find pleasant staff who, I hate to have to say looked hygienic. The chicken was fresh and delicious. I couldn't have been happier. This will be my KFC from now on.

Just a fail around. Chicken was dry, small pieces, green beans were dry out, and slaw not fresh. I am a fan of KFC it just this location is a let down. I gave them a 2 stars because the service was fast at the drive thru, and the employees were pleasant. I won't be returning to this location but that doen't mean I won't be going to KFC. You gotta love the extra crispy!

My wife and i have been to this location twice, the first time the customer service wasnt great and the food wasnt hot it wasnt cold but it definitely wasnt warm, id have to say it was borderline cold. The second time which was yesterday we went through the drive through and ordered an 8 piece meal deal the lady who took the order who's name was Stephanie actually interrupted me several times while i was trying to order she was very rude and before i was done ordering she said she would have my total at the window, i actually was forced to tell her that i wasnt done ordering but she couldnt have known that because she just kept running her mouth and interrupting me, i finally got to the window the lady was still rude to me i got my food and went home. I ate the food it didnt taste too fresh more like 8hr old chicken. Anyways the next day my wife and i both got Diarrhea from the chicken, needless to say our whole experience at this location is ruined. I think its only fair for some kind of way for the owner to right this wrong, or atleast stop hiring shitty staff who dont know how to shut their mouths when customers are trying to order their food. Also maybe better inspect your food and get rid of old stuff instead of feeding it to the lublic in order to save and make a few bucks.

Authentic Mexican Food?  Why, because they say so?  Go three miles west to Trevose to Peppi's Mexican Grille and you'll find authentic Mexican food.  

My biggest problem with Chipolte is the business model.  Why does it take 15 people to make my taco?  It's the most frustrating experience to have to order just a taco shell from one person, pick meat from another, rice or beans from the next, vegetables from the next and so on.  I can't keep track of who is doing what to my order or if they are even listening to me.  Subway has been making hoagies with one person for their entire existence and has been successful, Chipolte should take a hint.  

Not to mention the quantity of the food that goes into my tacos is always inconsistent.  The ratio of meat to rice and other toppings is completely imbalanced.  I want a carnitas taco, not a sour cream and lettuce taco.  

Finally the menu selection is so limited and they really don't offer anything that really has a WOW factor to make you deal with all the chaos.  Likes others said, the lines are always long and crammed, but it's because they have too many people taking your order, miserable people at that.  Is it that hard to smile?

Love love love the burrito bowl. I think it's great that they have brown or white rice. Black beans ( or pinto ). Their mild salsa is more of a pico de gallo.... Fresh fresh fresh. You can get as much of this, as much cheese and sour cream. The meat choices are terrific...I have had the chicken, pork and steak. We always get a bag of chips (extra) and often guacamole (also extra). Always have leftovers...yum!

Since KFC is a chain restaurant, my review will be geared more toward service because you can expect the same food at most. However, I really do love their grilled and extra crispy chicken.  The chicken bites are actually pretty good too, lots of meat, and very juicy!  Their prices are fairly reasonable as well.  

First, I'd like to give the lunch crew some props cause the afternoon crew sucked! This particular KFC location is right by my work, so whenever I need a quick bite to eat or am on a time crunch, it's my go to place to pick up something quick and easy for lunch. The service at lunch is always consistently very friendly & fast at the drive thru and dining area.  

On this last visit, service was not so good.  I wanted to pick up some food for dinner on the way home.  Chicken sounded good, so off I went.  When I got there, the drive thru seemed busy, so I decided to go inside.  There was a couple of people in the dining area, and no one waiting to order.  So I thought "ok, maybe this will be quick!".  WRONG!  There were only 2 people working at that time.  One gal doing the drive-thru, and one person in the back cooking. He wasn't even helping with the orders.  When I got up to the counter to order I had to wait at least 5 minutes before another worker showed up.  And even when he did, there were orders that needed to be filled in the drive thru, and come to mention, the other people in the dining area were waiting too!  I wasn't in a hurry, so I patiently waited.  He finally took my order, and I had to wait another 10 minutes for my order, which I didn't mind at all.  What I thought was not so great, was the lady doing drive thru talking about how she wanted this other girl that just called in sick AGAIN to be fired and just talking crap about how she always call in, blah, blah, blah.  Not something I wanted to hear.  An apology for the wait because they were shorthanded would have done just fine. No need to hear about the drama behind it. Poor guys that were helping her didn't want to say anything else to fuel the fire.  Lol.  When I finally got my food though, the guy did apologize for the wait.

I can't say I won't be back, because I most likely will, just not in the late afternoons.  =)

If I could give zero stars I would. I am an avid no carb eater. I don't have a wife that cooks. I don't cook either. I'm not bashing my wife but simply put if I want food I go out to get it or I don't eat. I have to walk to this place because the car is otherwise occupied. Having to walk to get some grilled chicken to be told too bad we are out it will be 45minutes to make more. I know for a fact it takes less than 20 to make. So I just walk away and go without because I would rather stay within my dietary restrictions. Oh did I forget to mention that it was on 4pm when this happened. Sorry it inconveniences you to do your job to the point of lying to me. My mistake and I won't ever be a patron of your establishment again. I will just walk over to another place.

The colonel does it Again

The Nashville chicken special is a wild ride of flavor that you should not miss. The sauce kind of reminds me of the Chinese sauce used on general chicken but with a hint of kick to it. Baring the greasy mess that's left on your hands, you will smile at the flavor not common to fried chicken you use to eating. The pickles that accommodate are really an accent that works together with the flavor. A professional panel rated this chicken in their review of being 9.1 on a score of 10 being the best. I would say I agree with this independent review. Get plenty napkins when you eat this chicken.

This was the worst fried chicken I have ever eaten. The chicken looked like it was cooked to long some of it was hard to chew the sides were awful the mashed potatoes hade no taste if it weren't for the gravy it would have been terrible the bens hade no flavor at all. I am done with kfc for good.

Ordered chicken burritos last night for me and my husband. They were the COLDEST, worst burritos I have ever had in my life. There was something seriously off with the chicken, it just did not taste right, and I have had an upset stomach since last night. Even my husband, the most non-picky eater I have ever met, said the chicken was absolutely disgusting and didn't finish his. You guys are literally in the parking lot of my job so I won't pretend that I won't ever stop in for a sofritas burrito. Because your tofu has never literally killed me like your chicken did last night. For the most "organically" raised and grown meat out of all the Mexican chains (Moes, Qdobas), your chicken sure does taste like utter and complete crap.

* When I walked up to the counter, three smiling young ladies wearing wireless headsets were seeming waiting for someone to talk to. They did not welcome me and look me in the face, so I wasn't entirely sure if they were talking to the drive-through squawk box, or a hidden manager with a camera, or me.
* About 25 years ago I was a single guy with nobody to come home to, and cooking for one was tedious and disappointing. I often resorted to Burger King for a whopper. This was during the era of "have it your way" advertising, and flame broiled beef patties. It was the best franchise burger available, for a decent price.
* That era has passed, along with the spatula shaped door handles. I still think the whopper is pretty good, but the meat just doesn't delight me. I can't say exactly why. I usually only get it with a promo coupon, because without it, it's out of my budget. I understand beef prices are going up, but my wages are not.
* Only a few weeks ago I had a great fish sandwich at another location. Here, the fish had some tough dry bits that I couldn't chew. It might have been bits of bones. You would think that after years of offering this Big Fish, they would get the right recipe for tartar sauce.
* I will say that their chicken nuggets were about average. Not too good, not too bad. The honey mustard sauce here actually had honey in it, unlike a major competitor I recently reviewed.

Do yourself a favor and go to the Dunkin Donuts that's less than 2 miles away.  I drove it and it takes 3 minutes.

I just gave this location their last chance about 10 minutes ago.  I've had nothing but problems with my orders here since I moved to the area last summer.

The employees are aggressively disinterested from the time they take your order incorrectly until the time they hand you the wrong food and drink.

On this particular visit we went through that first usual step where they repeat your order back and your sausage has somehow become turkey sausage and the hot drink you ordered with your mouth apparently became an iced drink before it got to their ears.

After that I pulled up and waited over 5 minutes while the confused employees struggled with the car ahead of me, handing drinks back and forth more than once.

When I finally got my moment to shine at the window they were once again confused what drink I ordered.  So much so in fact that one lady eventually came to the window with a medium drink in her hand and asked me if this was my small mocha latte.  I just stared at her.  First of all, how do I know?  And secondly, I'm looking at a medium cup in your hand so.. 

I digress.

After that they had to ask me if I had paid yet or not.  I was starting to think this was a joke.  I was actually looking for the cameras.

Then a particularly dirty looking fellow strolled by the window and I wondered to myself if I should even eat this food in the unlikely event that they ever get my order right and give it to me.

But wait, there's more.  After they got my drink figured out they just sat there and stared at me and then finally asked (rudely, I might add) "Isn't that it?" and I had to tell them that I still don't have my sandwich.

I spent like 7 bucks or something but I feel like I spent a lifetime and all my patience.

Seriously, there's a Dunkin Donuts within WALKING distance and I used to laugh about that when I first moved in but now I know to thank whatever god you wanna worship that the other Dunkin Donuts is so close because this particular location is one wrecking ball away from being perfect.

Went last night for dinner...Waited in drive-thru line for 15 minutes w/ ONE car ahead of us who hadn't even placed her order yet. We thought we'd go inside instead. Went inside-should smell like fried chicken right? NOPE smelled sour and nasty! Then went to place an order 2 people ahead of me took 15 MIN just to get to the front of the line. Ordered food and waited, and waited, and waited. 5 people in front of me waiting ran out of biscuits and they didn't tell anyone waiting until they were getting their food. No proactiveness as people were ordering "hey we are out of biscuits what would you like instead?"  
There were 2 people cleaning in the back not helping with orders?? Go figure....Everyone got their order and when it comes to me guess what?!  We are out of chicken!!!! It will be another 20 minutes for another batch to be done!? Are you kidding me!? I asked for my money back and went to Fry's and paid 1/3 of the price and got MUCH better service!!

Just tried the sofritas burrito and it was totally edible. I appreciate a protein option for vegetarians, but the sofritas (I was told they have tofu) really look like a mystery concoction. Nothing extraordinary here- well, except the total. I added guacamole to the burrito and it totaled almost $10. What happened to the days of adding guac being 75 cents or $1? They ought to give you options on the portion/ pricing. I like the ethos of chipotle in general, but I dang near could have had an entree at a quality sit down restaurant. Also, they did offer to add the 'fajita' peppers/ onions to the burrito, but they looked like they'd been there all day. Or maybe longer. The whole bin of it looked grey. 

The place is clean, the staff is friendly, and I like the food better than QDoba.

If you have to go fast food once in a while, I guess KFC would be my place of choice. Fast food giant that has been around since the 50's I'd guess. Most KFC's are intermingled with Taco Bell garbage. The KFC Original Chicken recipe is still the juiciest and best tasting of the choices, if you don't mind seriously going off the diet for a day. The broiled Chicken is a little bit healthier and almost  as good as a 2nd alternative.  Tenders and bowls are all good but don't expect to live a long life if you live off of this stuff that is for sure. Guilty pleasures are their mash with gravy, mac and cheese and biscuit's. This particular KFC is average, smaller seating area,  clean for the most part, and employees are usual friendly and efficient no issues with the service. Clientele that goes in these places can be a little shaky and I'll leave it at that . Parking can get cramped when busy, but never too bad. bathrooms are clean but 1 stall. All in all, a decent fast food joint for chicken, this or Popeye's flip a coin. KFC has their loyal following including me for as long back as I can remember!

None of the employees paid attention to what I asked for so I had to repeat myself just to get my order right Even when we checked out, the cashier didn't even look sty us when handed us the card back so we didn't know if we were waiting for a receipt. Either way wet didn't get one and they just cried in their own conversations add we walked to our seat The steak was probably made incorrectly because it has absolutely zero seasoning and this is hands down THE worst Chipotle in operation I have ever been to.

Last year, after enduring over 24 hours of nearly constant white-out sandstorm conditions out on the high desert, our 1st meal back in civilation was at another Del Taco in Reno. As a sort of homage to that night, our last meal before returning to BRC was at this Del Taco.

I ordered a 3 Chicken Soft Taco special & Jalapeno Rings. The girlfriend ordered a Chicken Macho Burrito & Jalapeno Rings too. We both went Macho on our soda & got bucket-size cups with free refills. Though it wasn't busy, our food was a little slow. The cashier, who was possibly the manager, was courteous & professional in telling us the food would take just a bit longer.

Indeed, we got our food after not much longer. The dining area was clean & well lit. My 3 Chicken Soft Tacos were OK but small. All 3 together looked like less total food than the girlfriend's Chicken Macho Burrito. The Jalapeno Rings were awesome. Jalapeno Rings are my favorite fast food side order of any fast food chain. I like spicy.

Del Taco is decent fast food. The Jalapeno Rings always makes Del Taco a preferred fast food option for me.

Took 20 minutes after ordering to get my order (1 single Whopper, nothing fancy), and they over charged me for my drink even after I questioned the price. Watch out and check your receipt -- I think they're charging most drinks as "Monsters" which are the more expensive ones.

DIRTY DINING ALERT!!!!

http://www.abcactionnews.com/money/consumer/dirty-dining/dirty-dining-kentucky-fried-chicken-temporarily-closed-after-live-roaches-seen-in-the-kitchen

Dirty Dining: Kentucky Fried Chicken temporarily closed after live roaches seen in the kitchen

On-the-bone chicken pieces seasoned with Colonel Sanders' 'Original Recipe' may have had some extra ingredients put in after inspectors found live roaches crawling near their food.

Our ABC Action News I-Team uncovering that state inspectors shut down the Kentucky Fried Chicken at 3102 E. College Avenue on Friday September 23 after seeing 18 live and dead roaches near the prep table, ice machine, hot holding units, fountain machine.

They also found roach droppings on a hole in the wall behind the prep table in this Ruskin KFC location.  

In addition, other violations documented include:  Salad at 47-degrees in the reach-in cooler when it should be at 41-degrees or colder, employees not washing their hands, equipment in poor repair and floor tiles cracked, broken or in disrepair.  


KFC Statement:

Following the health inspection on Friday, the restaurant repaired structural issues, and underwent cleaning, as well as inspection and treatment from a licensed pest control provider. The restaurant was given permission from the health department to reopen on Saturday morning. All of our restaurants are required to follow strict operating standards, and we will continue to work with the franchisee who owns this location to be sure it meets those standards going forward.

Lori Eberenz

KFC Spokesperson

Never had a bad burrito bowl here and this one had steak in it. It was very good, as usual. It lasted 2 meals, which made the cost an even better deal. I read worried about coming back after the issues the company had a year or so ago but it was worth it

Been here 2 days ago. Door was already closed at 8:40 but the guy opened it for me since he saw me looking at their hours posted on the door. So i bought and got my chicken and got back to my car. I haven't had anything that day that I couldn't wait to get home to eat my food but instead i ate it inside my car. They closed at 8:55 with all lights turned off when it's posted on the door that they close at 10. Somethings going on with how this store is being managed. Owner should know about this.

Done coming to this location.  Consistently has issues with quality and inventory. 30 minutes after the store opens they have no chicken,  scarce amount of steak, not to mention everything looked dried up. So I wait 5 minutes for them to finish cooking the chicken while my food is partially made dying on the line (seriously stop making until everything is done..) get my food, drive back home so my wife and I can eat lunch super undercooked chicken. Drive back to chipotle,  inform them of what happened, no issues and they replaced my food... drive back home for round 2 of my lunch, some lovely medium rare chicken... You were just told that you gave someone undercooked chicken and don't quality check it a second time around? Very poor management.

Unfortunately you can't give negative numbers on reviews and the Burger King at Grant and Oracle deserves about a negative 5 stars they are the most unprofessional people I have ever been involved with we went there for lunch placed our orders my brother ordered the spicy BLT whopper with extra cheese and extra pickles the braindead cashier behind the counter tried telling him a chicken sandwich which is clearly not what he ordered he explained to her and pointed out to her on the sign what he wanted with extra cheese and extra pickle when our order was delivered he had neither extra cheese extra pickle he went to the counter and explain the situation and got a ration of s*** from the idiots behind the counter said he did not order them and I was standing next to him when you place the order and he got nothing but grief from the dumb stupid b****** do not go to This Burger King you will live to regret it

I used to be a Store manager 3 years with KFC(Gainesville ) area , today my daughter wanted some drumsticks ( that only part of chicken she likes) - so I went to this store ask them if they have any dark meat special , front register guy said yes we have but the drive thru lady (probably shift supervisor ) said no - then I asked them can I buy 4 pieces drumstick only ( if you need you can mix up original and crispy ) - he said ok , but when I reached home I found they gave me 2 legs and 2 thigh , I believe these people don't care about customer

We waited over 25 minutes for two whopper meals.I was in the mood for fries and they weren't even done.   This was at 6:00pm and there were only two people waiting ahead of us.Unbelievable and Never again!!

My wife and I went for dinner.  Posted on the window was a poster meals 4,5,6.  I ordered a whopper meal regular, my wife ordered the two grilled sandwiches for 6 and $1.49 nuggets, one extra drink, total of 18.05.  When I got the items they said only charged for one sandwich.so they charged annother 2,14.  So we are at 20,19 for 13.49 food ordered.  When we asked the manager. He said you have to specify that you want the special price when you order.  I will not return to this location.  I would call this a scam.  Do not go here

we don't have chipotle in hawaii. it's a sad, sad, unfortunate thing. when i'm on vacation on the mainland, i really enjoy stopping here for lunch or dinner at least once!

this particular chipotle is always clean, and their staff is always friendly. the only disappointing thing about this particular visit was that they were out of carnitas!! it's my absolute favorite and i was super bummed i wasn't able to get any. apparently, the pig farmers weren't being humane to the animals and that is why they are currently out of carnitas. poor little oinkers.

anywho, i got the chicken instead. it was yummy, but a little spicier than i'd like.

If you like KFC, than this is one of their better locations. Would you believe it's been here for more than 30 years? 

Located adjacent to my neighborhood, the quality is consistent at this location. 

Unlike some KFCs, I've only gotten fresh coleslaw and chicken here. 

I took me years to figure out why KFC's coleslaw has an addictive kick. It has a dollop of horseradish mixed into the sweet slaw sauce. Now that I know this, I can duplicate their slaw at home. 

I'm not much of a chicken fan, and fried chicken is my least favorite preparation. But I've found that if I peel off KFC's fried skin, the meat beneath is tender and moist. 

I like the new grilled chicken, and KFC's mashed potatoes are perfectly calibrated to go with its chicken. 

Cons: For some years now instead of honey to go with their biscuits, KFC gives "Honey blend." This is essentially half honey, half corn syrup. You'd think that when you pay $18 for a chicken meal, this chain would spring for little packets of real honey. 

Here's something I always do when ordering to go at KFC. Ask the order taker for some of their paper plates. KFC uses the thick, sturdy ones that I'm too cheap to buy. Tell them you've got double the people you really have eating because the food usually lasts for more than one meal. Also, ask for extra napkins because you're going to need them. KFC chicken is messy eating.

Bean and cheese burrito with red sauce, yes please for medium hot sauce! Ok, make that two! $2.12 at the window, hot diggity.

I came here out of spite (another quick Mexican food joints burritos weren't cutting it and were double the prices). Now this is a guilty pleasure. This is my go-to for lunch when I forget my soggy sandwich in the fridge. I frequent for dinner too when I am too lazy to cook.

I have never been inside so I only speak to the drive-thru but man, they are so nice, happy and well nice. Like niiiiiice!

I have had their taco Tuesday tacos and I wasn't impressed so I just stick with the dollar menu (hello strawberry lemonade) and I have never gone wrong.

Its Chipotle. What can I say? It's neither really good nor totally awful. But it's a place that I go to when I'm low on cash and don't feel like cooking because I know I can easily get more than one meal out of a burrito bowl. I started coming here about two months ago and have been here a handful of times since then. There were several things I noticed very frequently in my time, which I can now comment on because I can confirm that these are very regular occurrences.

The first of these that you'll notice is that the place is absolutely disgustingly unclean almost all of the time. The staff of 20-something-year-olds doesn't do a thing about keeping the place clean. It seems as if there is no order in the place whatsoever. Employees take forever to clean up messy food-laden tables after customers leave. Worse, the area where the employees themselves serve food tends to be gross. Today, for example, the checkout counter had sour cream smeared all over it and lettuce and cheese. When the woman put my order in the bag, she didn't even pay attention to the table and the bottom of my bag got run through old sour cream. Disgusting. I had to go wipe the bottom of the bag before leaving.

The second important thing to note is that the meat and beans are usually consistently burnt and low quality. I usually get a steak bowl, but the pieces tend to be mostly blackened, and/or taste gummy as if they're just fat pieces or other low-quality pieces. I understand that you get what you pay for but it's just gross how chewy the meat is.

Overall, it's not amazing, nor is it authentic. A solid 2/5 or less, taste wise. I'd probably recommend the sofritas if you have higher standards for your meat, since it's vegetarian and doesn't usually taste as bad as the meat does. I only come back because it's the only place I can ask for a little extra rice, beans, or other ingredients, and some tortillas on the side, without any upcharge.

The guy at Chipotle made my mom a fresh hot carnitas taco (really a small quesadilla) because she said the tacos aren't warm enough, but she's really just a super picky eater. This is a great example of excellent customer service and I'm super great full and impressed. Thank you! You rock! I'll be back.

We come here weekly with a group of folks, every Tuesday. It's a Del taco, chill environment and if you dine in they have never messed up an order. That's for years my dude.

Drive through though messes up sometimes.

The inside is always clean, stuff is always stocked, and I've never had food that didn't taste fresh.

It's fast food but it's a good place to hangout with friends. Easy parking and next to a Dutch bros and Fred Meyer (I tend to go grocery shopping after so I'm not shopping hungry)

By far the worst chipotle I have ever been to. I traveled from Chicago to Tampa for the first time to visit family and stopped in for a quick burrito with my girlfriend. She order a burrito bowl with barbocoa and I asked for steak. Her bowl was ready quickly but I was delayed for about 5 minutes my tortilla sat getting soggy with rice and beans setting in. While I waited for steak to be sliced several customers had to mosey past me to complete there orders. When the steak finally came up I received the last greasy,
cold bits of the old bin followed by about a 1/4 spoon of actually fresh meat, which was extremely rare. The manager was quick to take our payment, but not keen enough to notice the person who rolled my burrito spilled nearly half of my salsa and meat onto the counter. I don't like confrontations at restaurants because if I would've said something I would have been irate and lost my temper. If I wanted something vegetarian I could've easily purchased fresh vegetables and rice and cooked them myself. Having previously worked for this chain, not a single aspect of their food line was operating according to company standards and training.

My favorite is the $5, chicken pot pie, soda and a cookie. It's a awesome bargain for sure and the pot pie is big with a nice balance of ingredients including chicken. The soda is always perfect blend of syrup and carbonation and the cookie is a chocolate chip and very tasty. 

The staff is very nice and quick with preparing my order.

They suck

Also the works are eh some are nice but others unprofessionalism and eye rolling really over shadows the kind ones.

Pull into drive through, ordered my food. Was told there would be a 4 minute wait on the popcorn chicken. Okayyyy. 

Pull to the window, give my card for payment and I'm told it'll be a 10 minute wait for biscuits. 

This wouldn't be an issue if this happened every once in awhile but I've noticed it happens every time I come to this particular location.

I feel bad for downgrading my review, but I've been to this Burger King every time I'm at this Terminal, and the experience ranges from "okay" to "oh gawd, that was horrible."

Today's experience was pretty horrible. I just wanted my usual Whopper Jr. and onion rings, but the cashier rang it up as a Whopper Chipotle, which was about $1 more expensive than what I had ordered.

I then mentioned this to another employee, not expecting a refund or anything since it's just a dollar, but I'm concerned that the cashier was overcharging other customers who do care about the money. 

The cashier then proceeded to argue with me that she was undercharging me by only ringing up the [wrong] sandwich and not the onion rings, which apparently weren't available anyway and weren't going to be available any time soon. The $6.09 I got charged was apparently less than the $2.29 + $2.39 + tax (so around $5) that I should have been charged. Eh. Still trying to wrap my head around that one. 

After a lot of waiting and confusion, eventually a manager came out and refunded the transaction. So that was nice. But I was still super hungry and wanted something to eat. The manager let me have the Whopper Jr. that had been sitting out for a while for free and informed me that there weren't any onion rings being made. The comp'ed burger was a nice gesture that I appreciate. Having to wait around for so long for onion rings that were never coming, for which I would have overpaid if the manager hadn't shown up, was not so nice. 

I try to be understanding. The lines were long, people at airports transform into something subhuman, so I can only imagine how miserable an experience it can get working in customer service... but man, I felt like I was getting hosed today.
I love this place I don't get why people don't like it. It's the best fried chicken I've ever had. The biscuits are the best.

This KFC has a buffet, chicken is put out hot as needed. The workers are nice, they wear gloves. The dining area is clean, we went at lunch and the tables were wiped. It was very busy, this kept the chicken and the sides hot.

The buffet is $7.49, drink included. Plenty of parking. It's off exit 240, off I 75.

I received raw chicken from KFC. Did they refund my money yes. However the lack of concern from the manager was just incredible. I'm 8 months pregnant and you gave me raw chicken. I don't normally eat fast food but after this I will never eat fast food again. I am so disgusted.

Terrible! Young guy has zero concept of moving faster to get through the customer backlog and can't get some of the orders right to boot. 

Customers waiting in line for over 10-15 mins just to order.  A double whopper and chicken fries took me 20 mins to get. 

Now one of the front line folks went back to help the cooks because she's tired of waiting for food to get made lol. 

No management on-site.

I like Chipolte's and will go out of my way to give them my business rather than visit Moe's or Taco Bell, or one of the other Mexican fast-food chains. Sure, the menu options are about the same -- Burritos, Tacos, Salads, Burrito Bowls... but Chipotle takes a stand for sustainably-raised food. That means that the meat is from animals which are raised without the use of antibiotics or additional hormones. They source organic and locally-grown produce, and that they use dairy products from cows raised without the use of synthetic hormones.

Yeah, that's kind of a big deal to me. You pay a bit more for quality products, and you help support the environment. That all makes good sense, and it makes me feel better when I'm chomping away on my steak burrito bowl.

OK, on to comments about the restaurant... the decor is very modern and very sleek. Lots of stainless steel and recycled wood inside.  You enter and take your place on the buffet line and begin making your selections. First, do you want a burrito, a burrito bowl, a salad, or a taco?  Then you choose the rice - either the regular white stuff or their delicious cilantro-lime rice? Then it's the beans - pinto or vegetarian black beans? And then the meat choices - braised carnitas or barbacoa, adobo-marinated and grilled chicken or steak? And finally the toppings bar with 4 different types of salsa, homemade guacamole, sour cream, cheese, and pico de gallo.

You can add chips & salsa and a drink, and be happily on your way to a table or for carryout. The food is served on round, stainless steel pizza trays, wrapped in heavy recycled paper to cut down on dish washing, unnecessary trash, and general good business sense. They don't have one of those fancy soda machines, but you can certainly get your fill at the standard Coke fountain.

You'll find this location in a little strip mall along Gunn Highway, just as the road turns to the right. If you continue going straight you'll find yourself now on Citrus Park Drive and about a block from Citrus Park Mall.

Enjoy your burritos, and feel better about protecting the environment!

Totally give up on this del taco! My husband and I have given this one a few times to get things right cause it's the only one on the way home, well after tonight we're done! Tacos were all busted up! The chili fries had nothing on them even tho we paid extra for toppings just to make sure we get them this time and nope didn't work! The beef burrito ended up being bean and completely solid cold in the middle ended up tossing it all out! We didn't drive it back because we live out on the edge of town and it wasn't worth it and I was done at that point! Skip del at north town save a trip and do Jimboys in Lemmon valley! Because you shouldn't have to take everything out of the bag and unwrap your food to check the order before you leave the counter!

We went to Dunkin' Donuts to pick up the breakfast sandwich with the beyond sausage.  Turns our this location didn't  have them. 

We wanted to get something, so we asked about whether the donuts are vegan. The attendant chuckled and said "she wouldn't know." She directed us to the website even though we were sitting in the drive-through. 

We then asked if the nutritional information was available at the store.  She told us she couldn't help us, we needed to look on the website.

Customer service was completely lacking.

Love me some carnitas! Having a ketogenic bowl, no carbs; carnitas, guacamole, Monterey Jack cheese and pico sauce. Simple, filling high protein brain food. Kinda pricey, but well worth it for this simple but flavorful dish!

I wish this location had a drive thru, but it is fine walking in. The employees are always nice. I wished my drink had a little more syrup in my iced coffee, but it was still good. I enjoyed my hash browns and turkey sausage, egg, and cheese sandwich on an English muffin.

After exploring the canyons of The Home Depot, we walked out into the cold dark gray sky. We were a little hungry after all the hiking and saw this Burger King on the edge of this shopping zone on Bromley Road. 

The store was nearly empty @ 3:00, and there was no wait to order. Whopper meal and a double cheeseburger from the value menu. Easy. Right? Except, our order taker thought we wanted a second meal when we only wanted the sandwich along with the Whopper meal. Small miscommunication that was fixed with managerial assistance after the register froze. 

The burgers were typical of BK cuisine. Adequate fast food. The Freestyle sodie dispenser was still fun. So many choices at my fingertips. Absent were any condiments on this side of the counter. No ketchup pump. No salt or pepper. No plastic utensils. I'm sure they had their reasons. 

This experience wouldn't have been particularly notable if I hadn't been to another BK two weeks before in Pa (Oxford Valley), where we were literally treated like fast-food royalty, in a shop that even has a fireplace lounge, and a real personal touch.

Spoiled us for future BK visits.

I don't know what to say about Chipotle, because until a couple of months ago I never ate there, but now I eat there every week. I usually go to the location that I'm reviewing because I forget they built one closer to me. Maybe subconsciously I like this location better. I have never had an issue with food. I love the sofritas burrito and the queso is stellar as well. The food is always fresh and delicious. The portion sizes are gigantic as well. You definitely should eat here if you haven't. Make sure you get a side of queso!!!!

I am not going to say that I have always loved Chipotle. I used to think it was stuck up and not good.

But I finally gave it a chance and have not looked back since.

I love the rice bowl (naked burrito bowl) with Steak and pretty much everything on it. 

I love their chips which have a hint of lime and a really great tasting chunky salt. I am a big fan of using the tabasco sauce on mine and I like to change it up between the green, chipotle and regular.

Our kiddos usually get the kids cheese quesadilla kids meal and share 2 of them between all 3 of them. 

I don't think I have ever had a bad experience at this location and its really close to our regular trader joe's location so I consider it to be my favorite.

I would also be leaving out one of my favorite parts if I didn't say that they have removed all GMO's from their food. I just wish maybe they would maybe get some better soft drinks, maybe pure cane sugar.

I went here on Wednesday the 2nd of October. I was asked to pick up 2 chicken pies on the way home. I arrived at 9:40 pm, went inside, waited  for some one to come and help me. When I someone did I was told there were no pies available. I asked what else they had the employee turned around , looked at the empty hot holding bins, looked back at me and said " there is nothing you can have, we're just about to close". I reconfirmed that there was nothing I could order and left. As I got into my car the employee rushed to the front door and locked it.  This was at 9:45 PM. Their closing time is 10 PM.

We used to go to Carl's Jr. just for fries or the zucchini coins, but NOW with the Beyond Burger, my husband can eat here regularly. Every time we go to Carl's Jr. now, we both get the Beyond Burger and it is delicious every time. (The customer service has improved at this location as well).I can't tell you how much this means to us as vegetarians, you don't even understand how long it has been since I've been able to go to a fast food place and order a burger without having to say "without X, with X." I literally go up to the window and order the Beyond Burger and I'm like "That's the plant based burger right?" And they're like "yeah!" We aren't treated like we are weird for wanting it. Also it tastes fantastic! MEAT EATERS you must try this burger! I can't even tell it's not meat, I sometimes have to check just to make sure. It is fantastic. Please don't ever stop selling this.

This is the first and last time that I order from them. We got tacos. The steak was so tough that I couldn't chew it. Their chips are way too salty. Flavors, in general, were not good. We are very disappointed.

Imagine this. You got on the Hinge dating app, and you got a match. Hinge set you up with a Chipotle date with a coupon, buy one meal get one free. So your date is all into mindfulness of not spending much and figured it's a good idea to save money on a first date and do Chipotle take-out and go watch a movie. She happens to be vegetarian, so she orders a sofritas bowl. 

K, you pick up your bowls and you drive 20 minutes through this holiday traffic back home. Pop open the lid, and low and behold, it's not sofritas: it's actually carnitas. Aka pork. Your date is all... "oh... it's okay... I guess I'll just eat the chips and guac." Turns out the chips were super stale and her jaw got tired chewing it. You can guess how the rest of the night went. 

Thanks, Chipotle.
Came in to try the new beyond famous star and it did not disappoint! Crazy hot fries, fast service and one delicious burger. I will be back often as long as they have the beyond burger!

Giving this 3 stars because the food is always so fresh and amazing.
Great place to go get a veggie filled bowl of Mexican food. The sofritas are seasoned perfect!  
The unknown item that's not on the menu that I also love is the sofritas quesadilla! So cheesy and tastes great!

The only thing that this location is lacking is knowledgeable staff. The people making my food didn't know what was in their food and also didn't listen well and got confused easily. I have definitely had better experiences at other locations. 
Checking out was a hassle too bc the cashier wanted to argue about how to apply a couple, was really unhappy with the lack of customer service. 

With a little managerial pep talk this place should be in great shape like some of the other better ones. Still Great food just disappointed it's the service and knowledge here.

service was quick. i pulled in the drive thru on a rainy indiana night and only one other car was in front of me. i received all of the sauces i asked for (honey mustard as some ranch i didn't ask for lol ) everyone knows how important this is. the workers were nice and moved quickly. 

i don't know why my chicken was barely covered in hot sauce and dry though.  at first glance i thought it was okay because the container usually has some collecting but no - none.

I've seen a lot of "concern" about voting fast food chains and gas stations with 5 stars. Well, I see it differently than those people. I am not ranking the "chain", I am ranking the particular location and the people that work there. I've visited this Carl's Jr 4 times. Each time has been very quick, very very friendly (yes two very's), with quick hot food and it is for that reason that I feel that this particular Carl's Jr. and the people on the inside, get a 5 star. 

I once worked at a Carl's Jr when I was 16-- it was my first job, and it was in a mall. Hard work and customers can be so mean. Hard to keep a smile on your face. 

Carl's Jr. has a breakfast menu, which is small but good. They even have sausage/country gravy, which isn't found in many fast foods. They have a killer breakfast burrito with the gravy on the inside-- pretty good people. They have fresh biscuits, too! 

This location has a green burrito inside it as well. I am not a fan of the Green Burrito side of Carl's Jr., but that's a personal preference because I am a foodie-- nothing to do with CJ! 

I personally thank those working in the morning, for being so kind and cheerful in the morning when I just wanna go back home to bed! The store manager often works the drive-thru which really says a lot for her employees. She isn't setting in an office, but rather in the front line, doing her share. Good job, all!

Its fast food , but its better than Micky D's. They have "Whopper Wednesday" where you get a single whopper for a ridiculous amount. I rarely do up fast food spots but this in my opinion is a little better and i just wanted a whopper.
Their drive thru is a little slow , kinda defeats the purpose of "fast food" Service is slow inside aswell.
 Inside it  looks clean for a fast food joint, they also have a tv inside so you can eat and watch.

I had a Whopper w/cheese had it my way ,asked for no O's(onions) no tomatoes and no lettuce. but only pickels . They did everything right, but they forgot to add the ketchup and mayo(or whatever the combo they use). You ever eat a dry burger.. yea not the tastiest. Prob this was my last time i will hit this spot up.

more like 2.5 stars

Who would've thought they could get EVERYTHING  wrong?

I walk in to this guy who's measuring the temperature of the food, (everything was cold) and rather than greeting me, he walks off the the side behind a wall while he waits for this new hire to come take my order. I understand maybe it's not your job to take orders at the moment, but you could at least welcome me and say that someone will help me out shortly. 

The new hire who takes my order clearly isn't trained well enough. She didn't ask if I wanted any beans, she didn't know what sofritas were, waited until we were half way done to ask me if I wanted my order for-here or to-go, and gave me the smallest portion sizes I've ever received at a Chipotle. 

But it doesn't stop there. The girl at the register rings me up without asking if I wanted a drink, gives me to wrong change, and when she rang me up for the drink in a separate transaction, she gave me extra coins and the wrong change again!

Still not done. I'm eager to finally be leaving this wreck so I head out the door with wha I thought was an unsweet tea, only to realize half way down the road the they had put sweet tea in the unsweet tea dispenser. 

Whoever is in charge needs to pay a visit to see how much of a disaster the evening shifts at this location truly are. The management at this location is severely lacking.

Update: now 4 stars, this place seems to have a better and more efficient staff now, they also don't try to charge extra for sides with my quesadilla which is a plus. 

Come here for some non gmo fresh tasting Mexican food :) lots of vegetarian options!!!!!  




Giving this 3 stars because the food is always so fresh and amazing.
Great place to go get a veggie filled bowl of Mexican food. The sofritas are seasoned perfect!  
The unknown item that's not on the menu that I also love is the sofritas quesadilla! So cheesy and tastes great!

The only thing that this location is lacking is knowledgeable staff. The people making my food didn't know what was in their food and also didn't listen well and got confused easily. I have definitely had better experiences at other locations.

Second day here this week.
First time I stopped by  I waited 20 mins for steak which wasn't that big of a deal but today I come in and they say they're only accepting online orders. I asked how's that make sense because two days ago I ordered inside just fine. They claimed the cash register is down so I had to go outside and order online . The staff is polite and the quality of their ingredients is better than the locations nearby but I'd like to just come in one day and not have an issue ‍.

Racist rude cashier. I ordered a "Large #12" and she kept screaming "what?" and "Speak English" at me. Despite saying very clearly and slowly. Large. Number. Twelve.
Then she said "we don't got 12s."
If you're going to ask me to "speak English" then at least use it grammatically correct."
Manager proceeded to slam my food onto the counter when it was ready.
Oh and the #12 is a chicken sandwich. They gave me a whopper instead.
If you like being degraded and your food being smashed and not even correct in the first place, then this is the right place for you.

There are several constants when it comes to an airport Burger King:

1. Every meal will be 4 digits instead of 3. 
2. The common area for filling your soft drinks will be a landfill of straw covers, napkins, discarded cup caps, and general stickiness

And in my case, my Cheese and Bacon Whopper will come with No Bacon, and no sauces (no mayo, ketchup, or mustard), and one patty instead of two like promised. 

However, they did have a fine collection of sauces to self-serve from - bbq, sweet and sour, ranch, buffalo, ketchup. So, yay! That deserves a star. 

By the way, it wasn't busy - it was just me and one other person waiting for their food. It was definitely my order - because the named it and called my name. I think it was just a matter of:

1. It's too hard to get the order correct, or
2. They just did not give a flying f*k. (Since this was an airport terminal, that pun is free).

I came to this DD with my dad ordering a simple 2 black iced coffees and 2 breakfast biscuits. We were number 112 so we expected a small wait. We ended up talking to the customers next to us and hearing that they were #94 and had been there since 10:30. it was 11:05!!!! they had been waiting for 30 minutes for a simple sausage biscuit! Drive thru customers were being asked to pull up and park! The staff out numbered the customers, and they were yelling in the back about how they had no help and seemed to be calling out the customers in an aggressive manner! The girls next to us had also been waiting for 40 minutes and their other friends had gotten their food within 5 minutes of ordering. everyone in the staff seemed very negative and overwhelmed. There seemed to be no manager in charge and the rush was left to over 10 highschool kids to handle. We finally got our VERY SIMPLE ORDER after a man that had just walked in got his. no joke this guy walked in and ordered 5 drinks and got them within a minute. The staff was so negative and overwhelmed that the cashier had to start making drinks for customers because the rest of the staff just walked out. I was very disappointed in my visit and will not be recommending this place to anyone else. Customers even went to the parking lot and told other people not to come in because of how horrible the customer service was.

I am the mayor of this place on FourSquare for a reason! I have a specific order that any of the Chipotle crew members will know it is my order.  They know us by our first name. To say that I eat there almost every other day is still an understatement.

About the Food:

I love the food, specially the carnitas and guacamole.  I never tried the chicken or other meet so I am not sure about its taste. I can definitely tell you the the carnita is worth a try. Carnitas are the shredded roasted pork, it is always well seasoned and perfectly done. They do offer the Barbacoa, which is a spicy version of pork, but I am not a big fan of spicy food. 
The guacamole is also amazing, very simple recipe but always very fresh. 

If you don't have time to cook and need to come up with some fast and affordable dinner option, I will definitely recommend Chipotle because of how fresh and healthy it is comparing to other fast food options. 

About the Place:
I believe the parking lot is the only reason why I didn't give 5 starts.  The plaza is really small and the amount of parking spaces are very limited and shared with other businesses.  They do have the 2 first spots for online pick up only. 

If you try to get there before 12pm or after 12:30pm you should be fine. During that time, it is a lot of spinning around until finding a parking space. 

5 Stars to the Food! 3 Stars to the Parking Lot! Enjoy!

It's almost midnight on a Thursday night and here I sit sick to stomach in the bathroom! I had the 2 chicken littles and fries and my husband had the three pieces meal (thankfully he is feeling okay) for dinner! The first chicken little was good, the second one didn't taste very well! I guess I won't go to this drive thru anymore! I'm glad I have tomorrow off! Ugh!! Now my husband is very sick and had to call in to work!

Last time I was here chicken was so dry..
Today at rush hour weekday no original .
I'm not coming here no more ...

After a great night on the town me and my family stopped by to get some food. The customer service was absolutely horrible. We were greeted through the drive through speaker by "what do you want!" I asked if they had chicken nuggets and the employee said "why would you even ask that?".
I continued to order and asked for a icee and she told me that the machine was froze up. As we pulled up to the window the employee rolled her eyes at us and literally threw the bag of food in our car then slammed the window shut. As I got home and opened my whopper I found spit right in the middle of my burger. I am very upset and can not believe how horribly me and my family were treated. I tried calling over and over but got no answer. Didn't catch the employees name but she had blonde weave in her hair. I will not stand for such behavior. No customer should be treated in this manner no matter what situation. I need to be contacted immediately this is unacceptable.
Delicious delicious delicious  The chicken is hot and delicious a biscuits mass potatoes and gravy  Can I beat it for the price thank you very much

Kind of dingy inside, definitely dirty outside with trash in the parking lot. Lots of homeless roaming around too. 
The guy that helped us behind the counter was nice, but he kept disappearing. Later I saw a lady go up to the counter and wait like 3 minutes before somebody noticed her because there was nobody at the counter. As we ate I'd periodically look over to the counter and see nobody. Why do they keep disappearing from the counter?
My order came with a biscuit but they didn't include it so I had to go back up and get it. 
Honestly I feel like paying almost $10 for what I got (5 small chicken tenders, small cole slaw, small mashed potatoes, small drink) wasn't really a good deal! 
A KFC employee came in and sat for like 30 minutes at a table before management noticed her, it was kind of weird!

I jus order a whopper with cheese and those fuckin dumb asses put all this fuckin bacon on my whopper ... I didn't ask for that and since when whoopers come with a shit a shit load of got damn bacon ... a bunch off dumb asses now what if I was allergic... fuckin dick heads

1/5 stars because they closed 45 mins early. Yes shit happens to systems and whatnot or for some odd reason you run out of chicken, but at least have the decency to hang a note up on your front door/drive thru window explaining to your customers. Don't just lock the front door and automate your drive thru speaker to say, "please return during our NORMAL store hours."

Came here on a Friday night right at 11pm as all the lights were on. I didn't realize they were closed. I've never heard of a Del Taco that closes that early on a Friday! I waited at the call box for 5 minutes as there was no sign on the menu saying they were closed and the lights on made me think they were open.

I ordered family 8 piece dinner. When the chicken arrived, the chicken was so small, only half of the box was full.  Delivery was prompt, but cost an extra $8. Mash potatoes tasted like instant potatoes.

I went here today because McDonald's was out of food due to electrical outage. I ordered my food (a whopper with just cheese) and the employee walked away to make my shake without giving me my receipt. I stood there and then asked another employee for my receipt. With a ton of attitude she hands it to me. When I got my food my plain whopper with cheese it turned into a patty with burger and ketchup nothing else. As I am writing this the staff started yelling very loudly and messing around throughout the entire place. If you have the choice between McDonald's or this stable of an eating establishment, pick McDonald's. If McDonald's is closed, make a conscious effort to go somewhere else.

For fast food Mexican,  Del Taco is my go to for sure! This location is always clean and the staff is nice. I love that you can get fresh avocado added to anything which I have started to do regularly.  My favorite go to is the vegetarian 8 layer burrito. Always tasty.  If I'm not so hungry a bean taco add avocado is tasty as well.

Extremely rude service. 
I went through the drive-thru to order just a coffee with a sausage, egg and cheese on a plain bagel. 
The person taking my order was basically yelling at me. After I ordered my coffee and bagel, he didn't respond for almost a minute. He didn't tell me how much it all cost, or to pull up to the window. And then rudely yelled "Can I help you?!" And then he repeated my order without my bagel. I told him I wanted the sausage, egg and cheese AGAIN, and then he yelled at me what kind of bread and I said a plain bagel AGAIN. 
The customer service is horrible. It took more than 5 minutes just to take my 2 item order. If you want terrible customer service with bad communication, then you have found your Dunkin Donuts.
Edit: they didn't even get my order right. Got a bacon, egg and cheese instead of sausage, egg and cheese.

My first experience with this restaurant was that the Barrito was three parts Salad one part meat very little meat and my second experience  plenty of steak but was extremely tough and gristly  would not be my first pick for a Mexican restaurant.I speak of the new restaurant at Granite run 1075 Baltimore Pike.

Multiple times do they mess up the order, This time around really made me upset, I asked for 3 chicken little's with Georgia Gold Sauce, and then stated for extra Georgia Gold sauce on the sandwich. (I did this because most of the time they never put more than a dab of the sauce in the middle which gives no flavor.) Anyways after they confirm my order. I receive my bag, all of them have yet another dab of Sauce on them. And I'm quite upset. 
2 words of advice: 
1: Check the bag and open your food every time right in front of their faces.
2: Ask for sauce on the side to dip it into, manager did it for me with ease another day ago so there shouldn't be a problem in doing that.

Most all places around here Suck anyways though. Such a bad neighborhood I'm disgusted to live here.

This particular Chipotle is definitely not my favorite. Once again, I am disappointed with my order. I ordered online and when I got home the food was not even warm. Mind you, I arrived before my pickup time. Both bowls did not have sour cream and they were very small. It is almost like they used the last of certain ingredients like MEAT. I ordered half chicken and half steak which we got very little of. I will not waste my time anymore with this restaurant because all of the employees do not follow the same standard. They could learn a few things Wawa's and  Chick-fil-A where I will be taking my business.

I've been there several times and every time I go there is gets worse and worse and worse. last time I went to this Del Taco. I ordered two for $5 beef burritos, two fish tacos, and a order of chili cheese fries. fish tacos had no cabbage, the beef burrito tortillas were hard as a rock when when I got them they refused to make me a new ones. and theworkers are too busy flirting with the one young female co-worker to even come to the counter and serve the six people in line. And the female supervisor she was laughing and joking with everyone and flirting with the one young man who work there. did not care about my problems, or how many people are in line or how they weren't being served. no thought was given.
when I first went there it was great after my last visit I will never go there again and I understand Reno is a college town and there's a lot of college students trying to get a job, but they need mixed aged employees.
I just need someone to pay attention to customers their thoughts their problems. They're happy enough to take our money but they're not happy enough to help us and serve us.

This location should be closed by the Health Dept ,dark dining room employees playing on their phones.You will be served after they finish their call.The food was so old it seems like yesterday's chicken.It appears that they sell more Long John Silvers.When we arrived home we became ill.Please Don't Eat here you will regret it!!

Honestly this place is ok, and the food is usually good whenever we go. Once in a while during a rush you might get chicken that's a tad cold but usually it's hot and fresh. I like this location better than other KFCs, like the one in Riverview off of 301 tends to have really slimy old tasting chicken for some reason, but this one does much better, in my opinion. They have a great value for the food you get too, like we usually get one of the $5 boxes and it's delicious. Even comes with a cookie. :3

I would say they do ok on cleaning, like the place isn't a total mess when we go but could probably be a little better on the upkeep... 

The one thing that bothers me the most about this place is that ALL of their tables in the dining area are wobbly! Like every single one, you cannot just move to another table because it will always be the same or even worse. It's ridiculous and they need to fix this problem. Would have given 4 stars if not for the super wobbly tables. I hate it because it always makes my soda go flat! Gross.
