First of all let me start by saying who the heck doesn't like Chipotle?! Everything is made fresh and delicious daily! 

One of my absolute favorite things to get is the sofritas! You may be asking "Daniel what are sofritas?!" Well let me tell you friend it's a delicious blend of spices and tofu. Now you may be thinking. Hold the phone why would you lie to me and say that tofu is tasty?! To that I would respond with WOULD I LIE TO YOU?!?!?! It is very well seasoned and I promise you that it doesn't taste gross or like tofu! To be honest all food COULD be gross but it is all about how it is seasoned. 

I would also highly recommend getting a bowl instead of anything else as it will most likely give you the most food. Other hacks I have found to get the most food possible from one of my favorite fast food places on earth would be to get half of each meat (I suggest half chicken/half sofritas). Chipotle also has a great mobile app that you can order from and it makes it very easy to see what you can put on your bowl!

I ordered a steak bowl told the girl waiting on me I wanted mild salsa she put hot. When I corrected her she got mad and tossed the other one away. I then said I wanted sour cream and she said they ran out. Yet they had a to go station where I saw they had sour cream.

I have to say this is absolutely the worst Burger King I've ever had in my life! The staff is undertrained and rude. The food is always cold and stale. I have limited options on fast food being a pescatarian and I was so excited about the impossible Whopper but was quickly let down! My husband and children have all had the same experience with multiple things on the menu. We've tried multiple times because the impossible whopper and the fact that they are so close to home but I feel like I am better off setting my money on fire as we have never been able to make it through half a sandwich at the most. This location really needs new management and a complete overhaul, they are a detriment to the Burger King franchise especially when there is a McDonald's across the street which is absolutely incredible. Maybe these Burger King folks could learn something from their neighbors. Don't waste your time or money at this location just make the extra drive to the 54 and little road location.

Del taco slogan - fresh mexican food. A blatant lie. Open 24 hours. Try some 10 hour meat served by a fast food expert at any hour. Just plain gross. Went in to get my money back and they offer me the same order that i want my money back for. No thanks i dont want to be sick again. Eat at your own risk. 
This place proves again that in Boise there is no mexican food. Boise has zero culture and is desperate to find any.

This place was absolutely filthy and there were flies all over the food. When we walked in, a staff member helped himself to huge portions of meat, leaving no steak left for any customers and none was being made. The restaurant was out of half of sides and meat options at 7pm. No staff member wore gloves and one employee wore his pants below his underwear line. The food that was served was cold and the staff locked the doors at 8pm when their hours claim to be open until 10. I most definitely would never recommend this chipotle to anybody.

My wife and I actually wanted to have lunch at Arby's, but the Arby's was closed for renovations.  So we visited this nearby Kentucky Fried Chicken instead. 

We both had a "Drum & Thigh Combo" ($5.99 each), which includes a biscuit, chocolate chip cookie, side and soda. I had mashed potatoes with gravy as the side, and my wife had cole slaw.

The food was all fine. The chicken here really does taste finger-licking good, albeit not good for you.

The interior of this KFC was very clean and I would say this place is an above average KFC.

After missing a breakfast, and being in school from 12pm-2pm.  I worked up an appetite! I was starving, but I didn't want too much. On my way home from school, I though of having a $5 box from KFC. I pull up to the drive through, and was greeted by an automated voice. As soon as the voice stopped speaking, the actual representative got on and quickly told me to give her a moment. (I thought too myself "No problem, I need time to look") I heard something from the intercom and I thought she was talking to me, so I said excuse me? And she replied "l said one moment please!?!" ... oookaaayyyy... so she comes back like "how can I help you" I start asking questions such as, " does the $5 box come with grilled chicken"? She says yes. And I'm like "wait, like a sandwich?" And she responds as if annoyed.. " WHAT!?!" ... I didn't even want her to talk to me anymore  I pulled up to the window and the first thing out her mouth is "NEXT TIME, YOU NEED TO STAY OVER THERE"  um excuse me !?! Next time, if you don't understand me, or couldn't hear me, you say, excuse me and ask me to repeat!! She responds - I didn't say "What" ( lies). I say, can I speak to your manager, she says I am the manager!?!  wonderful. So I left without food- hungry and annoyed. Instead I went to TOKYO right down the street and at delicious sushi with wonderful customer service understanding people! It was better than eating KFC anyways!
Go see Krysten! Great customer service and food prep. Yum. Best fresh steak and guacamole compared to recent visits at other locations

This is a good Dunkin Donuts. It is not phenomenal in their choice of donuts, but they do make a good cup of coffee and amazing Americanos. The facilities are clean and it is a good place to meet for business or to catch up with friends. The service is decent, but they could simply be understaffed. Great happy hour specials and hopefully things will continue to get better. 
     Like any Dunkin Donuts, they have good donuts and coffee. I enjoy their sandwiches ( but would not dunk them), especially the sausage egg and cheese croissant sandwich. This location has a way to go on service and selection but it is a good place.

I'm extremely pissed! I've tried to give this place the benefit of the doubt!!! However I'm convinced that this place needs to be shut down! I placed a order for the impossible burger with fries plus some other stuff! I received my order however there were no fries! I had to turn all the way back around to get my fries! Mind you I'm on my break. I walk in and there's a few more people in the restaurant complaining about how there order wasn't right! I asked the young lady if the fries were hot she shook her head no! While having the fries in her hand to give me!!! Wow so she was giving me cold fries until I asked if they were hot!!!! The restaurant it's self is filthy! Cigarettes in the common areas, trash pulled up! I'm actually sorry that I walked in! Management needs to be retrained and the owner fired!

This may be a three-star Chipotle location, but compared to the Red Robins, McDonalds, and Cracker Barrel abominations in the vicinity let's bump it up a star, shall wee?

The food is consistently good for a Chipotle, and they have all the ingredients available, no need to worry about a dearth of sofritas, etc.  I have found the ingredients fresh, no wilted lettuce or brown avocado, and as usual you can see them cooking up the chicken on the grill.

The staff is friendly, especially the day manager, and there is a positive energy to the place.

A few caveats are in order, this space is loud.  Think communal dining in an upscale cafeteria in terms of decibels!  There tends to be a line around lunch, but that's a good sign, and the orders move fast.  I don't like that not only the kitchen is exposed, but rather some of the storage space is in view.  I don't care to see boxes upon boxes of cartons, cups, and other assorted detritus.

Finally, there is an outdoor seating area, but considering the view is of a parking lot and a wall blocking the adjacent McDonalds, I'd as much rather eat inside and people watch.

I don't typically rate chain establishments buuuttt I visit here more often than I'd like to admit, so I feel the need to bump up the overall rating. I don't think it's fair for anyone to give somewhere one or two stars because there's a long line. I don't think it's fair to give a "fast food" restaurant one or two stars because the staff moves your food quickly down the line. To me this just means everybody loves Chipotle and they're trying to help you along! I go during non peak hours and have never had an issue.

Everything about my burrito bowls are always fantastic. The steak is super flavorful and tender, the brown rice is cooked well, the mild salsa and lettuce are always fresh, the corn salsa is sweet, and the cheese melts in a way that you know it's hand grated. 

I'm pretty obsessed with Chipotle. That should about do it.

Waited at least 15 minutes in drive through after being told to "pull up" with no one behind me.  
The cheesy tots were ok, but not very hot. 
The taco was awful.  I liked the shell, but the meat tasted like it was pureed - just mush.  
The Whopper Jr. w/ cheese had no cheese and the bread was stale & hard, and nearly inedible.  
I asked for a receipt and was told, "It's broken".  Not a hello, sorry, or thank you.

Terrible experience. Will not return. Ever.

Really disappointing. I was a big fan on Chipotle but for the last year up to now my experiences have been poor. Today is no exception. Ridiculous to require full digital ordering due to Covid-19 and masks when the employees are lounging around l, talking with their masks pulled down. Surprisingly my order was ready on time this time but there were still problems. The large guacamole side order was already partially brown when I pulled the top off for the first time and had a sour taste. Chips were stale and the chicken in my burrito bowl was dry. The steak burritos we ordered were better but the steak was chewy but good flavor. I think we have shown that businesses can reopen normally due to the covid vaccine and still wearing a mask is fine but these employees need to get back to norm and be more visible while making the food and have to serve customers again. Please make some changes soon because El Limon is basically next door and they have consistently great food and they are the reason I don't go to Chipotle as often. Pretty soon I will forget Chipotle altogether and give our every 2 week orders to El Limon going forward.

We ordered 2 famous bowl meals, a regular chicken little and a honey mustard chicken little. 

We received 1 spicy famous bowl and 2 honey mustard chicken littles. No cookies with the meal either. Terrible terrible service.

I have never seen such incompetence. I've been to this location 4x total. They never once got my order right, so today I checked before I drove off and they got it wrong. I asked for an English muffin with an egg, no cheese. I got a sausage and egg !!!  Went back to the window and he had no idea how to refund me so he gave me a dollar from the tip jar ( that I just gave 2 min before)

It's been a long time since we have eaten at Chipotle.  The wife said she would stop on the way home and pick something up.  I asked for the Carnitas bowl.

When the wife got home, she said that the lady put very little meat in my bowl.  When I removed the cover, I noticed a couple of globs of something sitting on top of the rice.  I picked up each glob with my fork and noticed that all I got in my bowl was globs of FAT.

I hate fat, so I removed the globs and only had beens and rice to eat.  I will never order from Chipotle again.

If you want good Carnitas, go across the street to Rigoberto's and get the Carnitas Plate.  More than enough meat (not FAT) to feed the both of us.

No one seems to have experience with customer service. All employees were confused about what order goes with what. As the soda was handed to me I'd ask what kind but employee was unsure which soda was which out of 5 drinks. Asked for taco sauce enough for 5 different meals. I was handed 4 packets of sauce. What a joke! I was handed 4 bags of food for everything & they only want to give me 4 packets of hot sauce. Lack of communication due to not knowing what their job is or what's going on around them. This was super frustrating as employees could tell & started working slower just to be rude.

This Del Taco on Northtowne Lane above McCarran apparently doesn't like those of us who are not Trump cultists.  They didn't like my Biden mask, clearly hatred in their eyes.  They were rude, shorted my order, and made extremely inferior food.  When I told the Manager, she was unresponsive and not even an apology...and then when I got home, this is what the Del Tacos looked like.  Did they spit in the food?  I don't know.  I just threw it out.  I will never go back.  If they only like to treat Trump worshippers fairly, they need to put that on their signage...that only Trump Cultists are welcome there.  It was a waste of about 15 bucks for the order.

If I could give them a negative star, I would.

Horrible. The cashier was extremely rude and bothered from the beginning. I asked what kind of flavors the chicken littles come in and she said "all of them" so I asked what those flavors are and that must have been the nail in my food coffin. I received chicken littles completely DRENCHED in sauce. The outside of the foil packaging, the inside of the packaging, the outside of the bun, and the chicken were SOAKED in sauce. It got everywhere when I pulled it out of the bag. It just proves that the employees are LAZY and truly don't care. Hopefully KFC will hire people who don't mind working their job or better yet, people who actually want to be there and do their job correctly. Won't be back.

This is the worst chipotle I've ever been to it's pathetic. The line is crazy long they'll be making ur order and then they told me to hold on they had to fill an online order first even though they were literally in the middle of doing my order. They were out of half the ingredients at like 1 in the afternoon. I wanted to get steak and he said they're still waiting on more to be cooked I wanted hot sauce he said they were out of hot sauce how is that even possible at a restaurant like this... I got a drink and there was already 3 signs on the soda machine saying a certain drink is out I saw coke didn't have a sign I tried it and of course they were out of that too I tried to get another drink and they were out of that too. Idk what the staff does but they definitely don't do their jobs properly.

I had never been to a Chipotle in CA since the closest one was 15 miles away.  Since there is one in the area I moved to, I figured why not.  

The line was super long, but I got through to the front pretty quickly.  I ordered a carnitas burrito with no beans.  I felt like it was mostly rice, but what meat was in there was pretty tasty.  I also think $2.00 for guacamole is excessive, but I suppose you pay for convenience.  I don't know that I would be a regular, but once in awhile would be ok.

Fast Service but WRONG order . I asked for $20 fill up With 12 pc tenders ,coleslaw , Mac n cheese, Mash potatoes . The ticket shows mac. But I got regular chicken on the bone . all small pieces No Mac n Cheese and two Mashed potatoes. I drove 20 miles home when I found out the order was wrong. I tried to call but it just rings ... no answer. Looks to be a common issue at this location.

The staff here is beyond rude. You could truly tell how deeply they did not want to be here, and did not care. 

Orders were wrong - Pork instead of steak, no lettuce when asked. Fountain drinks were completely out of order. No changing table in the oddly huge bathroom.

My boyfriend and myself found ourselves with the strongest craving for FKC and took a twenty minute drive just for some delicious chicken. The only problem was when we are arrived we were treated horribly the server was ignorant and kept passing us to different people. Upon completing our order the woman would not give us our total and we had to wait 15 minutes for potato wedges. I love KFC, but I will not be spending money with this pitiful establishment anymore

Ordered Family Pack Whopper, Bacon cheeseburger  and buy one get one whopper. Burgers were cold and overcooked,(totally hardened), buns were stale, mayo ketchup tomato a mess sliding off burger with enough condiments for 2 or 3 burgers on one.  It tasted horrible its going to be a long time before I eat this slop again, I'm used to going to other Burger Kings where the fries are actually warm and the patties aren't hard and cold.  I feel like I just threw my money in the trash. I had to tell one of the guys in the cooking area to put gloves on, the other guy didn't have his mask on properly. Check this place out if you want to see how NOT to run a restaurant. Most of this meal went in the garbage.!!

It's an hour to closing and they dont have mac and cheese, they don't have chocolate milk, and I'm surprised they have chicken. 

These people are just trash.
This shit is truly fucked up. These bitches fucked upon my order does this shit looks like a whopper jr with cheese meal the only thing these bitches got right was the fries

Worst fast food restaurant in Delaware. They serve only one good item - the chicken pot pie. Problem is, if there is more than one person in front of you, you could wait 20 to 30 minutes to get your order. Last night I was one of four cars that pulled out of line because the wait was so long. And there was only one person at the drive thru window. Also the buckets of chicken are priced ridiculously high. You can buy buckets of fresh delicious chicken that are neither too greasy nor too dry from Acme for half the cost. Shame on KFC for keeping this establishment open. BTW - it is filthy.

Let me start off by saying I am a Dunkin Junkie.  I grew up close to a 5 Star Dunkin.  I know what good food and drink is.  Overall, this Dunkin is not a 5, but I am giving it that to balance out all the hate this location gets.

I've eaten here three times in all.  The first two times were probably about a 3/5.  This time however, it is a 5/5.

The first two times was in the morning hours.  The staff wasn't very friendly, and the coffee wasn't mixed and the bacon egg and cheese sandwich was a little under cooked.  That being said, they were quick, got my orders completely right, and the food was still pretty good, despite all that.

This last time though, wow.  They really stepped up their game.  I got a bacon egg and cheese sandwich on an onion bagel and a sausage egg and cheese on an onion bagel (I like onion bagels), and a medium sweetened ice coffee, just cream.  They got the order completely right once again.  The food was cooked perfectly and was even nice and hot when I ate it.  The coffee was also mixed perfectly.  The staff was incredibly nice too and the dude who packed my sandwiches even told me that my bacon sandwich was on top.  Wow, that's thoughtful!

So, in conclusion, no this isn't the best Dunkin in the world.  However, it is a lot better than the previous review writers are making it out to be.  I feel bad that they had poor experiences, but my experiences have been okay, and they've gotten even better the more I go!

If I could give them no stars I would absolutely horrible they gave us the wrong food twice then argued with us like kindergartners then gave us one of the right items and it had a hair in it ans wouldn't give us the other item because she argued with us and said we never order fries we order nachos after she had asked us twice if it was fries and made us seem like we were trying to lie to her but we just wanted the food we payed for don't come to this deal taco it's an absolute joke the employees are so so rude and don't take care of there customers at all I'm blown away

This location reminds me why I haven't eaten KFC in years. When my I walked in I was in immediate disgust. The dining area was filthy and there wasn't a single clean table. The trash cans were over-flowing and there was trash on the ground. The buffet had crusted food that had clearly been there for hours upon hours probably sitting at unsafe temperatures. I ordered the chicken tender combo with Mac and cheese and mashed potatoes. The food was cold other than the tenders. The only reason I agreed to stay is because my grandfather had his heart set on the chicken. The drink station was a mess and the tray on the machine was full and close to flooding. The place also smelled like dirty wet rags. All in all I feel as if the problem was due to being understaffed as the girl behind the register was also responsible for cleaning the lobby. Overall I was really dissapointed and won't be back.

Oh you little grease soaked carb filled bites of heaven! I have waited years to have you again! The moment I landed in St. Louis I was on a mission to find you! If you are a White Castle fan, nothing in this review matters, you will eat here no matter what. However, if you aren't used to that "slider feeling" down your throat, you may not be a fan, but I encourage to try them or a few or a box! The establishment is white on the inside, it seemed clean and that's about it. The bathrooms were clean too, cause you need to know that!

It was a Saturday evening. The sky cleared after a heavy rain. My hunger began to rise quicker than a batch of biscuits. Natural instinct set in, and I decided to order 12 beautifully seasoned chicken tenders. Upon request, I asked for the charcuterie of outstanding dipping sauces that KFC not only promotes, but swears true for the tender to sauce perfect ratio. I held in my appetite for hours so I could appreciate this experience. After an hour of pacing and irregular outbursts, my order arrived. I savaged through the bag. Found the crispy golden treasure. But what transpired next will haunt me for eternity. The sauces that I begged for, pleaded for. Not one to be found. Not a single hickory bbq, nor a KFC sauce, nay not one measly honey mussy.  With extreme anger I bursted in tears. How could this happen to me? Everything I've ever known has become a lie. I question reality and my will to live. Tonight, I not only dine with a lack of succulent dipping sauces, the only way I can enjoy my 12 piece crispy boys is gently using my tears to mimic the possible enjoyment that I ever could have had. The mashed potatoes were cooked to perfection. Thank you.

Went through drive thru super quick! HOT CRISPY YUMMMM! The fries were definitely missing some but they were super hot, fresh, and super crispy! The chicken was super hot and fresh as well! I don't ever eat biscuits from anywhere and let me tell the you biscuits ARE BOMB!!!! Super buttery!!! This location is definitely recommended the workers were super nice and quick.

Stopped by here on a Wednesday night and had a pretty decent meal considering it was fast food. Ordered a Whopper meal, chili cheese hot dog, and 10 piece nuggets. The burger was flame grilled with nice flavor and seasoned well, however the chicken nuggets were on the dry side. Another cool thing is that this location has several computers to browse the internet while waiting for your food.

I don't know if I'm a fool for wanting to try it or I have been fooled by deceptive advertising, but the new Nashville Hot Chicken and waffles sandwich is what brought me here. Once I received my food the smell of sweet waffles and spicy chicken permeated through the air, which intensified my hunger and excitement.

Upon my first glance, the disappointment started setting in, because the sandwich was made so messily and you could see the proportions were off. Two giant waffles and a small piece of chicken isn't going to be very pleasant. The taste followed the appearance as it wasn't a well proportioned sandwich. It was the worst rendition I've ever had of Chicken & waffles. Don't fall for the hype like I did. Or you will pay $8 for a combo meal full of disappointment as well.

Fine today at 1040 AM for a quick Jr. Whopper and small fry. Fries hot, burger not. Service quick and $4.50 not bad for airport food. 

3 stars is a-ok. They were A-ok.

When I heard we were going to chipotle initially I was excited. I had ordered my burrito to perfection. When I received my what was about to be "perfect" burrito... I was surprised to see that there was no queso know fajitas only white rice when I ordered both white and brown and no sour cream. Imagine a Barito that dry. White rice and steak. Not amused chipotle. Next time I'll save the headache and hit Qdoba

Left your drive thru about an hour ago and while placing my order for a whopper jr combo meal
I asked for easy mayo and heavy onion. It was read back to me incorrect so I repeated myself again. When I got to the window I asked the manager (she) to please make sure my order was
correct and she assured me it was. It was NOT! Lots od mayo and no onion. And I received no receipt. This happens too many times.

HE SHOULD BE FIRED!!! I CANNOT BELIEVE THE SERVICE I RECEIVED THERE! I stood in line for 10 minutes, and when I went to order they told me they were out of steak and it would be 10 minutes. While I am waiting, I see another person go to order a quesadilla, and was told she had to order it online, when she went to go order it online, the website said it was down. Instead of working with her, he just said "oh well" and when she asked to speak with a manager, HE WAS THE MANAGER. While waiting, I saw FOUR people go to order the steak, and after a condescending sassy response from him, THEY ALL LEFT TO GO SOMEWHERE ELSE. After OVER 10 minutes of waiting for the steak, I asked if it was ready and his response? "It will be ready when its ready, if you don't want to wait, you can go somewhere else." YES, THAT IS CORRECT! THAT WAS A MANAGERS RESPONSE TO ME!!! Another girl (who was also waiting for the steak) quickly came to my side and said we have all been waiting here over 10 minutes, which is how long you said it would take, it is lunch rush hour and this is completely ridiculous. Now, a decent manager would just say "you're right, it is taking a little longer than expected, here is some free queso while you wait". Instead, this boy goes to yell at us!!! As you can see in the attached picture where he starts raising his hands and getting loud and defensive with us! We both ended up leaving after that altercation because to be spoken so rudely to by a manager is COMPLETELY UNACCEPTABLE. I had to drive 10 minutes away to another Chipotle, wait in another 15 minute line, and when I got to the front I asked to speak with their manager on how to report him (from the other store) to headquarters. She politely explained that they are over-worked, under-staffed, (and I also know that they are under-paid). She apologized for his behavior and gave me my meals for free. She was a saving grace in what is becoming a terrible institution. 

PLEASE FIRE THIS MANAGER! In the 20 minutes I was there he lost SO MANY CUSTOMERS completely based on his rude, condescending, arrogant behavior. HE DOES NOT DESERVE TO BE A MANAGER. THERE IS NO TRAINING OR DEMOTION THAT WILL EVER SOLVE SOMEONE WITH HIS TYPE OF PERSONALITY.

I usually do not review fast food restaurants unless they were exceptional. We have so many stories about fast fun restaurants. Their either closed due to staffing issues, closing early, out of chicken or sides, wait 40 minutes for our order, rude employees or orders incorrect to name just a few. I know giving a 4 star to KFC seems silly but they deserved it on this trip.

This experience went so smoothly we were shocked. When we placed our order there was no commentary from the pleasant employee. Just proceed to the window please.

The employee was friendly when we paid. Our order arrived quickly and still no mention of being out of anything. When we arrived home the order was hot, correct and condiments were in the bag. Everything was as it should be. 

Pleasant employees, chicken prepared when we arrived and all sides available. All of the food was hot, fresh and the coleslaw was cold. We were excited and made for a pleasant dinner. Let's hope they continue with these practices. Thank you for a pleasant drive through experience. It almost restores my faith in fast food restaurants.

This is by far the worst location ever. They have NEVER gotten a single order right and I don't know why I keep giving them chances. You don't get the right drink or sides, they never have extra crispy chicken or they give you regular and pretend it's extra crispy. We get no honey or butter even though we mention it multiple times. They also have the rudest employees who lack basic common sense. It is not that hard of a job people, stop being so horrible at it!!

This is the worst Dunkin Donuts location and I'm not sure how it remains in business. This is a picture of the sausage, egg and cheese on a croissant sandwich. This croissant is either burnt or a week old. This is disgusting. After getting home, I drove back for a refund because I was not eating this. Does anyone take any pride in their job anymore? This location should just be closed.

Im not much of a fast food fan.  However, sometimes i do have cravings.  I went to Arnold today to a clock shop.  The owner had stepped away by the time i arrived and would be gone an hour. I was hungry,  so i walked next door to kfc. For $5 i got a bowl,  full of mashed potatoes,  corn,  chicken,  and gravy. Say what you will,  but kfc does have good gravy lol. The meal also came with a cookie and a large drink. The place was very clean.  The staff,  older ladies,  were extremely polite.

Ordered food through DoorDash was told the lady was rude and rushed. Missing half the order a drink and large fries. Wrong food, ordered a triple whopper and a regular chicken sandwich received a bacon one patty burger and a spicy chicken sandwich. Food was cold! Just threw it away and got a refund. Looks as if due to them closing shorty the worker just threw what was left in the bag to further their "clean up" process to leave. Obviously no integrity at this location and poor work ethic. Order somewhere else. Let this location die and become something else like the IN n Out across the street

Over the last three weeks I have gone to the Chipotle on Broadway at the Park Mall three times. I was hoping I would see improvement, but it seems like it has gotten even worse.  Each time the place is a mess, where they leave empty food containers that have hardened over for the public to see. 
 They are missing a couple of food items, today it was steak and fajitas, and they are so stingy in their portions. As there was no steak I had to get chicken as that was the only meat that looked half way edible.  The woman puts the scoop in to get the meat then starts hitting the sides of pan and decreases the scoop by half. I had to pay to get another scoop of meat just to equal what I would normally get for one scoop. She did the same with the beans, the corn, and the cheese sauce (oh, which you have to pay extra for that). She went to do the same with the guacamole and then stopped when she looked up and saw the expression on my face. The entire meal for one bowl came and a drink came to over $17 for a protein I had to take due to lack of other options and portions that are far below what they normally are when being served.  If this was the only time this happened I would not complain, but is now the 3rd time it has happened.  

I did ask why there was limited options for protein and other items as it was right before dinner started, but the woman said oh, well, we were busy at lunch.  

My recommendation... never go to the Chipotle at Park Mall!!!!
You guys are selling OLD food ! My biscuit I bought like 5 min ago is hard as a rock, & chicken looks disgusting.

Chicken was delish, however they ran out of biscuits in the drive thru during lunch rush. They offered an extra side to make up for it. All food was hot and fresh when I got home. Very yummy. Need to try their buffet soon. Close and convenient to our rv park :)

The most terrible experience I've had in a restaurant in a while. I placed my order in the app and went to the pick-up shelf when I arrived. My order wasn't there, so I waited. And waited. Other customers kept arriving, until there were half a dozen of us waiting around the pick-up shelf. After 15 minutes, I noticed there were orders behind the counter. I stepped up and asked if pick-up orders were being put on the shelf, and the employee told me I had to give her my name! Three employees in this store, watching half a dozen customers standing there waiting by the pick-up shelves and it didn't occur to any of them to let us know we had to ask for our orders at the counter or maybe call out a name as they packed up an order?! What did they think we were standing there for?! After I finally was handed my order, the other customers immediately stepped forward to get theirs. We were all waiting for no reason and just being ignored by all the employees! And as she handed me my order, the employee didn't say a thing. No apology, no show of concern, nothing. 

And to make things worse, when I got home I discovered that not only was my food cold because it had sat needlessly behind the counter for 15 minutes, but my order was incorrect. The sour cream was left off my bowl. And the food I did get was so poorly prepared I ended up throwing most of it away. There were big soggy clumps of cilantro in the rice, and some of the steak pieces were so red that they looked almost raw and unsafe to eat. My chips, which were a laughably small portion of maybe 10 chips total, had no salt. Next time you get a craving for a burrito bowl, drive past this Chipotle and go somewhere else.

I don't have the highest expectations when going to fast food. But I was super starving and it was late and I just wanted some onion rings and a whopper jr. I was looking forward to it because I rarely eat fast food so it was considered a treat. I pull up. Wait in line for 15 minutes. Then I finally get to the speaker to order and no one said anything. I said hello like 3 times. So at that point it'd been 20 minutes with like 6 cars ahead and behind me I had lost interest. No one ever came on to take my order. I was trapped between cars so I couldn't move. So i just waited in the line and planned to just drive through and get out of there. Turns out, 3 of the 6 cars did that as well. I drove by the window and saw a group of girls all talking and laughing not even paying attention. I was actually pretty shocked. Did they not realize they had a huge line of people trying to order food? Or just not care? The car behind me also drove through. And this was at 10, and they don't close till midnight. But oh well. Maybe it was a sign we shouldn't be eating that crap food anyway. If I do decide to eat crappy yet delicious fast food- I would never go back here. Management needs to get their staff under control. So unprofessional.

Ordered whopper jr no onion. Got whopper jr with cheese and onion. Gave it back. Got another no onion but still cheese. She said there's no cheese. Yes there is. Don't want cheese and don't want attitude. Worst BK in town

Oh and the French fries were raw

I was very excited for the Dunkin Donuts to open so close to my house.  Went here for the first time today with my elderly father.  Ordered two turkey sausage egg and cheese sandwiches, some donuts to go, a coffee for my father and a glass of water.  The coffee was handed to me first, and I took it and it was hot so I walked over to a table to hand it to my father and the man at the counter called out very loudly...ma'am...were you going to pay for that...as if I was going to go running out of the store without paying...for a coffee...even though I also had a credit card in my hand when the coffee was handed to me.  I said of course, paid, and then walked over to get my food at the pickup area.  Sat down to eat and realized I did not have the glass of water I ordered and went up to get it and the man at the counter thought about it and said...umm...that will be about $2 for a glass of water.  I said no thank you and we ate and left.  Sandwiches were very good and the donuts, which I ate later that day were fantastic but the manners of the employees and service were seriously lacking and need improvement.  I will give them another try over the next few months as they have not been open very long and I understand that it takes a bit of time for a new restaurant to run like a well oiled machine. I sincerely hope their service improves between now and then.

Was waiting in line and I saw that they gave the wrong cup size to the person in front of me. When she handed the drink back to the cashier she was holding it with her fingers on the inside of the cup. 
The cashier put it back on top of the stack to be given out even though it was now contaminated. When it was my turn I asked the cashier to throw that cup away because it was unsanitary and told her what I saw and she rolled her eyes and scoffed at me before pulling it out and setting it on the counter. She took my boyfriend's order and still hadn't thrown it away by the time it was my turn to order I just pointed at the cup and she finally sighed and slammed it into the trashcan. But with that kind of attitude and disregard for sanitation in a restaurant I don't trust them to not get me sick. Didn't order anything and I don't think I'll be going to this del taco again. A restaurant that ignores food safety rules doesn't deserve to sell food.

I was very pleasantly surprised with my experience at this KFC tonight.  I had read some prior negative reviews mentioning poor service, but I had great service tonight.  No wait in the drive-through, very polite staff - even offered a free drink while we waited a couple of minutes for our fries.  Everything in our order was perfect (even all the sauces I asked for) and was confirmed before I left.
The chicken was juicy and flavorful - just what I was craving today.  The family all loved the mashed potatoes and biscuits.  A new item we liked was the fries seasoned with the fried chicken spices!
Overall a great experience getting our fried chicken craving satisfied today!
Normally I love kfc but just wow who dosent put gravy in the bowls knowing it's the best part then only 2 sauces for the dry ass chicken smh will not be going back

I don't normally have problems with the Cinnaminson location. My husband likes the Impossible Whopper and this is the most comvenient BK. 

Tonight, I got home to find part of my order was incorrect...after waiting for 10 minutes for it to be completed. I returned to this store to get a replacement sandwich and a refund. I was given a refund but told I would not be given a replacement sandwich. All they would do is replicate my ENTIRE order...no refund. I didn't need an extra sandwich and onion rings. If I had wanted that much food, I would have ordered it. And this stuff isn't stuff that keeps and reheats well.

I wasted my time and my money having to return and get a replacement. They weren't going to offer any meaningful compensation. The person in charge finally gave me what I was looking for...but it took a lot of arguing. Not how I planned to spend my evening. 

We won't be returning to this location.

Waited in the drive thru for 20 minutes so assumed food would be hot. There was two cars in front of us and they have them wait until there order us complete. Was I wrong by the time we got home everything was cold. Not much fun heating up a whopper in a microwave with tomatoes. I ate one of the onion rings on the way home and tasted like shoe leather. With the age of here is your food take it and eat even if it not right I did not think I could have the worst experience with fast food but you changed that. Everything was cold and tasteless and won't be back to this location ever again. The need to learn how to use the waiting for food parking spots while other sit and wait for their food.

Typical Burger King experience. No hello, goodbye, or thank you. I was just handed a bag at the drive thru and she continued on to the next person. No ketchup, salt or pepper in the bag, and most frustrating whopper jr with no cheese. The whopper part is my fault.. I forgot you have to specify cheese at BK.

Went here this evening and witnessed the cashier and the food prep person engage in a loud foulmouthed argument over 2 biscuits.  While waiting on my order (14 minutes for 2 KFC bowls) I watched 2 drive thru customers come back inside because their orders were wrong.  Employees here are unprofessional and an embarrassment to watch, orders come out slow, and orders stand a 50/50 chance of being right.  Go to Po Folks, Publix or even Church's for your chicken.  This place is an atrocity.

Perhaps I'm not going to the right places, but generally speaking, the chain restaurants around Temple University suck. The one exception is Chipolte. 
Chipolte is a fresh addition to Montgomery Ave. and a good choice for vegetarians and carnivores. Customers have the option of choosing Burritos, Burrito Bowls, Tacos or salads,and meat selections include chicken, pork, beef and tofu.  
I get the vegetarian sofritas (like a spicy tofu version of ground beef) in a salad or soft shell tortillas every time I go.  The restaurant is clean and the service is quick, so the next time you happen to be in the area, give this place a try.

I love this place! Of course, it doesn't hurt that tacos are my favorite food. And yeah, I know, you're a suburban white chick that lives in green hills. But quit being such a cracker and venture out some. Get the carnitas. On anything. You'll thank me. And as much as I love hot sauce, theirs isn't great. The medium has much better flavor.

This location has a taco bell in here and we can get what ever cravings you may have at one shot.. Taco, fried chicken and those little fired doughnut cheeseballs. The service isn't anything to rave about but they are open late and have what you need when craving some fast food

I just can't. I know popcorn chicken doesn't have much meat, as it is; but dang....... All I got was a whole box of skin with crust. It was awful. I don't imagine any other day will be different from today. Lol

Worse KFC on the planet!  How this manages to stay open amazes me.  It is not necessarily the food.  It standard. What you expect KFC.  But the management, service, staffing is pitiful.  80% of the time the drive through will 1 get your order wrong 2 not understand your order or 3 leave out part of your order.  Before you leave check your order throughly!  The turn over of staff must be constant.  I never see the same person twice.  My wife craves KFC and this one is the nearest so I go here.  But I am slowly convincing her to go to Lucky Wishbone - bigger & better Chicken and superb service.

This establishment deserves a 3 star rating.  Only because, based on my observations, there seems to be a shortage in workers.  This is about the 3rd time that I have seen this problem.  Several times I was told there was a shortage of employees due to late callouts. Customers do not care why, they want their meals.  During my recent visit we ordered a #1.  No problem but they asked which #1!  That's not confusing.  The time from order till receiving our meal was 18 minutes.  Two other families were waiting as well.  My wife and I love the food.  A great burger when your tired of the competition. The restrooms were acceptable as well as the front end of the restaurant. The person bringing our order was polite and apologized for taking so long.  I suggest the situation with employees be addressed.  This place could easily be a true 4 star restaurant.

My wife and I were in the area and I was craving some fried chicken so we decided to eat here. I felt like the buffet was a little pricey but food was great like all other KFC's. I was really happy to see that they had chicken livers and gizzards on their buffet. 
I give this location 4 stars because it could've used a little cleaning, especially the restrooms. Besides that, the whole staff were friendly.

Oh Chipotle. How you have completely taken Moe's out of my life. I used to be an avid Moe's eater,  but then I find you right down the street from my University and the rest was history. 

Chipotle is an amazing restaurant, and this particular location is no exception. The first thing the visitor notices is the "feel" of the place. The eater walks in and sees the sleek, stainless steel look and is completely enveloped by the smooth techno music. From there you get in line, with your mouth watering from the smell of the cooking beef, chicken, pork, or steak. You will probably have to wait in a short line, but this is just because it is so good that everyone wants it. When you finally get to order the people will pile on the toppings ranging from a variety of salsa, veggies, and sauces. Once you sit down it is time to chow down. You open up you item of choice and bite and it is like a bit of heaven. You keep going until you're full, which is usually about half way. Then the eater sits back, sighs, and cannot imagine eating anything else for at least 24 hours. 

 Mission accomplished.
I always come here. Chicken spots are always slow. . Not sure why, but the food was warm and clean. Yummy.

I feel like KFC has been getting worse and worse. The food is very soggy and not crispy. After that day my favorite place for fried chicken has to be Popeyes! I used to love KFC don't know what happened the place was empty and dusty. I wonder why the KFC is a house i wonder if we go up to the third floor?

Trips to BK usually happen when I utilize coupons that come to my house (Seen the price of a whopper lately???) Got my oldest son a chicken sandwhich combo and my youngest son can either be good to eat nuggets and fries, fries only, or nuggets only. Coupons helped tremendously and Im all about keeping cost down however that is done.

Went through the drive-thru and they charged $16 (didnt put receipt in bag) for $5 fill up chicken breast, $1.69 chicken little and a famous bowl. Didnt realize this til after we left. He ripped us off.

As I make my way through each of the food vendors at the Nashville Airport, it became time to give the King of Burgers a try. Well, it's consistent with the retail locations you've seen elsewhere. The full spectrum of Whopper sandwiches, from the regular Whopper to a "mozzarella bacon cheeseburger" and even the "Rodeo Burger."

Fries and onion rings, too. Drinks are Coke products.

Eat here in the food court or take it with you on the plane! Grab some extra napkins.

Yay! A new, closer Chipotle!

This location is really nicely decorated and very clean inside, probably because they just opened. The ceilings are super high making the place look bigger than it actually is. The seating area is a little on the smaller side, but I've never had trouble finding a table.

Quality is on par with all the other Chipotles I have visited. I would say what sets them apart is their customer service. Their employees are really nice, and this location has no problems giving you something free on the side if at any point they have inconvenienced you. For example, the first time my wife and I met, we were waiting on the sofritas and they were out. So they offered to buy us free chips and guac or a drink.

I highly recommend this location. I'm not sure if it's because they aren't on google maps yet, but every time I have gone there hasn't been a line (around 6pm).

Asked for our burgers to be hot w/ cheese melted.  First of all I do not know why you have to ask for your burgers to be hot in the first place, but if you do not they are cold.  When we received our burgers, they were cold.  There is nothing that is more gross than a whopper that is COLD.  It also makes me sick.  So I went back to the counter and asked them to heat it up.  The MANAGER in the back stuck it in the microwave and heated it up so hot that no one could handle it without burning their hands.  It was dripping in grease and the bun was like rubber and the vegetables were just disgusting.  Called this am to get the number for the district manager, which i was offered my money back and replace my food...Ummm no thanks at this point.  She asked me to call back when the manager was because she wouldnt give me the district manager's phone number.  I called back, she said please hold he is outside....On hold for 10 minutes, hung up and called back.  Then was told that he wasn't there.  I'm pretty sure he was there and just didnt want to answer the phone.  I have the district manager's phone number and have made a call.  I don't expect much out of that conversation but at least they will know the situation.

Where do I even start with this hell hole?

Typically I leave reviews days later so no particular people get in trouble, because Lord knows, it would be my review that got someone fired and ultimately led to their demise - but tonight is not such a night.

There was one person in line when we got there. The cashiers name was Katie (Brownie I think he last name was) and she was a sweetheart... that's where the kind words end tonight from me. After taking the persons order in front of us, she asked a much taller, older woman to 'watch the front for her'. I didn't get her name, but she was probably 5'9, 5'10. This 'woman' took one look at us, rolled her eyes and proceeded to talk at 104 decibels to someone else behind the cook line about something I couldn't quite make out. I watched as four of the workers stood behind the counter working with as much efficiency as my grandmother breathing in salt water - which suffice it to say, is 'with none'. 

So fast forward five minutes... my wife and I are still standing there, still waiting for someone to even acknowledge us (ten minutes total at this point), when another woman, much shorter than a normal person, but not quite legally handicapped due to her 'shortness' came over and yelled out at the top of her lungs, "I got four tacos, a taco supreme, blah blah blah blah blah". The guy comes up to get the food for his family only for her to tell him that, 'yeah, we's out of chicken nuggets, chu' want sumtin else'. When he asked her 'what can he choose from'... you would have thought he said something un-nice about her four inch fingernails and how brightly they were painted. His daughter, a cute little thing, said, 'daddy, but I really wanted chicken nuggets', he told her he was sorry and just asked for strips. I looked at one of Willy Wonka's escapees with disdain and wondered, 'why would you wait until the last second, as you are handing someone their food, fifteen minutes after ordering and then tell him 'you were out' of something'. I just didn't get it. 

So anyways. Katie (the nice cashier) comes back and apologizes for no one helping us... my wife ordered, I ordered and then my wife asked for some cinnamon twists. Katie informed her that 'the cooks had not made any in a few hours and that she kept telling them customers were asking for them'. We asked 'what did they say', she said, 'they didn't care'.

Now mind you. I am not sure how one could surmise that the obviously overworked and underpaid (that's sarcasm) individuals in back wouldn't care about making such a staple menu item. But I can assure you, from listening to their dribble, cursing and going on and on about their lives and what they were going to do later - they did not. 

'Now Clider;, I can here you saying... why would you give a place with expected terrible service such a bad review. My answer to that is, 'I am a glutton for punishment'. We had not been here since the remodel so I figured, 'maybe they took out the riff raff and hired a bunch of people that actually gave a shit about their jobs and would make quality food'. This was not the case in this instance. I'll never go back to that hell hole. The food we got was luke warm. The chicken (boneless original recipe) was riddled with fat and my wifes Doritos Locos taco shell was rubbery as hell. But whatever. It's Taco Bell/KFC, guess I should have expected that.

Here is where my visit gets odd. Something strange happened while I was there, it only happened one other time when I got into a car accident, hit my head and saw my great-gam gam telling me things would be okay. The Colonel was there with me tonight, trying to place my mind at ease. As I felt the anger build up inside me about the travesty that was my visit, I felt a warm hand on my shoulder and a quiet whisper in my ear... and I knew it was Colonel Sanders. Everything kind of went black and there was a soft skinned, white bearded fella looking down on me and I just knew...

...he said, "Clider, if I could come back from the dead and burn this restaurant down, I would - and I am really sorry". At that point, I knew that it was a lost cause... I refilled my awesome Mountain Dew Baja Blast and left. 

Note to the managers and franchisees of this establishment.

All levity aside. Your restaurant is terrible. To say most of the staff was discourteous at best would be a compliment. I realize they deal with a lot of rude people, but not all of us are jerks (we are actually very nice and understanding). And not that a few bucks missing a month from us will kill your bottom line, but it will start to add up... or at least, I'd like to think so. Try replacing some of the managers with people that actually know how to manage. Try hiring some more well rounded people and pay them an extra two bucks an hour and FIRE the rest. I think you'd be surprised how many people would come back knowing they wouldn't have to wait, get shitty food and have the employees look down on you with general disdai

Definitely the most disgusting CHIPOTLE I've ever been to. Do not eat here, here are the facts. They don't care about public health and have no concern for foodborne illness. 

So I walked in and noticed it wasn't super busy. I ordered three flour tacos with Carnitas. The Carnitas were swimming in a pool of juice that it probably been sitting there for well over three hours. Not to mention all of the meat that was crusted in between the pans, not cool. Moving towards the end of the line there was sour cream smeared underneath every pan contaminating the saucers, corn and cheese you name it. Let us included. The trash cans are overflowing, nobody cares to restock utensils or napkins, there are dime size pieces of salt on the chips, completely unedible. The best part was not being treated with respect... there was no eye contact, the people on the line mumbled and didn't care about your guest experience. It seemed as they're concerns are just collecting a check.

Absolutely the WORST customer experience I've had at a food place anywhere. I waited in the drive thru 20 minutes for 2 burgers and two drinks. The employee that took my order did not know the difference between a double cheeseburger and a double Whopper. It took her 3 times to get my order right, after I made her repeat it to make sure. I was not given the correct price for the 1/2 off milkshake and $1 large sweet tea, so I asked her to fix it at the window. She said she didn't know how to fix it and wanted to just take my money for full price. I said I wasn't paying till I had the correct price and she asked "Do you want me to ask someone?" I said I wanted the correct price. She had someone get the card from the manager, not the actual manager to change it. She only fixed the shake price, not the sweet tea price. Another employee came to the window and I told her, and she refunded the difference. We finally got our burgers and we're waiting at the window for drinks, and had to ask for the sweet tea and shake. I got the tea and tasted it and it was unsweet, so I waited again for her to come back, and she said "Oh, we don't have sugar for sweet tea, we only have unsweet." At least 4 employees talked to me about my sweet tea order and not one could say they don't have sweet tea?! They had problems with everyone's order from the looks of it and how long it took the drive thru line to move. This is a disgrace of a place. I stopped her for a quick bite to eat, and I could have sat at Sonny's and had a full service meal in that amount of time with none of the hassles...
I am not happy with customer service I ordered a famous star burger with another burger. They forgot to put it in the bag and I have to turned around  after I have gotten home. Not happy right.

Chipotles is the best in southwestern mexian grills period! The food is excellent, and incredibly fresh. I am addicted to their steak bowls. I just wish they had one closer to my area so I don't have to drive as far.

Yes, I realize reviewing most any fast food joint is mostly silly because we all often have lowered expectations in advance.

I previously reviewed this location just as a warning to others, because it was so abysmally bad.

But I also feel it is important to give credit when it is due, thus this update...

Ok, so it's 6 months later and Mom just wants a Whopper and fries for a quick dinner.

This place has totally changed.  I got our food, hot, fresh, and fast in just about 3 minutes, and without some big crappy headache and hassle.  Wow...

This BK location FINALLY seems to have some form of adult at the helm!  Bravo BK!

There may just be some hope yet for this long-suffering location!

Why did I come here??
The drive thru is the worst. 
My running boards get scraped up so badly because it's soooo tight.

Be prepared to scream your order into the speaker, and repeat it over and over.

I'm not giving them 1 star, because my food was good, my Dr Pepper tasted great.
I did order extra cheese on my whopper - didn't happen. 

I was craving a burger, I got my running boards on my car scraped and destroyed. 

I'm annoyed with pretty much about this whole experience. 
That's what I get for eating Burger King

Mmm... Chipotle. My boyfriend got me hooked on the chain. I don't really like their rice (well, the cilantro really), so I normally get steak or veggie soft tacos with black beans, lettuce, and cheese. I like that I can order just two of them, since I used to always take the third in the normal order back home.

I love their diverse drink options. I usually get either the organic chocolate milk or the all natural Izze (which I've convinced my BF that this is better than soda, so point for Chipotle).

The outdoor seating area here isn't as nice as most Chipotle's, though that's mainly do to location. It's nice and shaded in the late afternoon, but faces a parking lot. I never really run into a line here, so I get my food uber fast.

I have tried to deal with the inconsistency of this location but after today, I will not ever eat here again. Every time I order food here they almost always give me the wrong thing. Today I asked for a sausage and cheese wake up wrap. That's all! I'm allergic to eggs so I never ask for eggs. Well they put egg on it anyway and not noticing the wrap had an egg on it.. (eating while I drive to work) took a bite and tasted the egg. I got sick needles to say. Then I called to report the issue, to be told the manager wasn't there and I could call back.. I insisted to leave a name and number but I will never go back to this location. Honestly, I am over Dunkin Donuts itself completely. 

Post incident: 
The manager did return my call (sweet guy!) offered to rectify the situation by replacing my meal with a free meal and was very upset that this happened. I appreciated his concern and diligence. Although I feel better now and with the manager returning my call setting me at ease, I am still not comfortable. Food allergies are serious.

I gave an update to stars based on the customer service!

They forgot my cheese bit they weren't especially rude or polite- very average. Don't expect anything especially incredible. But it's not bad. We were on the way and didn't want to wait am hour for dinner.
The burgers and fries were good. My boyfriend got a four cheese whopper which was actually pretty good, and he got an Oreo shake I tasted. It was ok I thought. He really liked it though.

I have to say this was by far my worse experience in a KFC ever. Though their drink choices have improved, and the employees were nice enough, the buffet was past disappointing. The hot food (what there was) was not hot, most were crusty on top, there were 2 half pans that were just empty and never got filled while I was there. Their cold side included turning lettuce, and celery with brown ends. They had a whole 2 choices for dressing. They no longer carry gizzards at this location witch was always the best thing on their buffet. Had to ask for chicken because they did not keep it filled and there was only 4 if us eating the buffet. They had 2 biscuits out that were hard as a rock and when I asked if I could get a fresh biscuit I was told it would be 11 minutes before they would be ready. Thus giving me time to write this review.

Okay so this is the Hilton of KFCs. Set in a beautiful, restored, Philadelphia mansion, this is the KFC of chicken dreams. The staff is super friendly. There is actual art and candelabras and a blocked off spiral staircase. It's cheaper and nicer than any other chain. Food is fresh and fried. I highly recommend this KFC for your Colonel Sanders needs! Go get the chicken.

stopped here and got a Star Combo to go, there is one thing that erks me and that is when they are so lazy that they have to microwave the lettuce along with the patty.. one of many things you have to list to get the order right next time.
Haven't been to Kfc in yrs. Now I know why. All we got was an 8 piece of chicken.  It cost $15. You can get the same thing at publix for half the price.

This is the rudest location of any fast food place I've been to. They're always out of things like chicken (?) and then give you attitude when you ask how long it will take to make more. Have had the same issues on the Taco Bell side as well. Would avoid if you can.

Trying to place a large order for Christmas eve and the phone online and in the phone book is not a working number. I call customer service number not once but twice and they give me non working numbers. Apparently calling the stores isn't a option. So I'm taking my business to Church's chicken

Polite via intercom but not FAST food. Didn't see enough staff. 

Perhaps I was just impatient for a very rare treat(bucket of chicken).

This store gets more stars because of the friendly lady at the drive through window. She is always very friendly and has a nice smile. In the past I have had no major problems with this location, however today was different. I ordered a whopper with no tomatoes and no onions. It tasted like it contained onions, and upon inspection it was clear that tomato seeds were in the sandwich. The staff had just taken the tomatoes and onions off of a pre-made sandwich. On the positive side, my sandwich was ready in record time! Although my order was technically correct, this is no way to run a business. Now we know why it's always so easy to find a parking spot here.

I have tried to deal with the inconsistency of this location but after today, I will not ever eat here at this location. Every time I order food here they always give me the wrong thing. Today I asked for a sausage and cheese wake up wrap. That's all! I'm allergic to eggs so I never ask for eggs. Well they put egg on it anyway and not noticing the wrap had an egg on it.. (eating while I drive to work) took a bite and tasted the egg. I got sick needles to say. Then I called to report the issue, to be told the manager wasn't there and I could call back.. I insisted to leave a name and number but I will never go back to this location. Honestly, I am over Dunkin Donuts completely. The only reason why I gave this place two stars is because the staff can be very friendly, but not at the expense of my health. They should ask questions if they are not sure!

Chicken is always great. But the place is not clean, orders slow and wrong. Staff simply does not give a damn.  Love KFC chicken, but rarely come here due to the bad overall experience. If I were the owner, I would be appalled.

Terrible! The mac n cheese was old and brown and nasty. The biscuits were old. They didnt ask what kind of dipping sauce i wanted. But that doesnt matter because the chicken tenders were old and hard. I had one good potatoe wedge. The rest of the meal i threw away! A waste of my money. Next time i will go to the checkers next door to this location. At least their food is always good and the service is way better!

Not the friendliest staff, no smiles, no good morning or hello, just "what can I get you". I have noticed how McDonald's has 20 employees in the morning while BK only has 4. I used to be a big BK fan but after watching them steal every concept that McDonald's ever came out with & Wendy's has even passed them in fast food rankings,They stopped flame broiling their burgers, I have learned it is over for them & not sure if they will ever bounce back. Hell, they even stole the concept from the 2 flaming guys in the Sonic commercials. Rant over. I'll give it 3 stars due to they have good sausage biscuit & very milky smooth caramel Frappes, plus I do still prefer the Whopper over the Big Mac but even that has gone down hill over the years.

OK, I love Chipotle but this location's service was very sloppy.

Yes, the rice tasted good and I'm glad they have the brown rice options now.  My chicken was tasty and tender (thank goodness for dark meat!).  But when building my husband's burrito, the beans went flying and so did pieces of steak.  I had to repeat my order several times (and I already have a clear loud voice) to make sure they gave me the right ingredients for both the burrito bowl and burrito.  *sigh*

I understand the busyness of the meal time and their rush to get through the line but I firmly believe that speed does not mean compromising good service.  I wouldn't mind waiting a few extra minutes! :P

In the end, food all goes down the same, chewed and mashed up.  But I prefer to leave that to my mouth and stomach.  I don't expect perfection, but a little quality control goes a long way! ;)

When I crave Chipotle at work, this location is closer to me. It's sort of hidden behind a building. It provides a filling, healthier-than-fast-food meal for cheap. And you customize you meal, which gives extra points in my book. I always get the "bowl" option - the burritos are too big and hard to eat for me, and the fillings are never equally distributed. The bowl option lets me go without a tortilla and lets me mix everything up. I also think you get more food that way. My preferred bowl is sofritas (tofu), black and pinto beans, brown rice, cheese, lettuce and sour cream. Very flavorful and delicious!

This location is preferable to Kirkwood - less busy and faster staff! Now if the people who come in and order SEVEN MEALS could just be BEHIND me next time... it always happens to me, seriously.

Everytime they are out of something and behind cooking it.... Every time I tip I can't even get a thank you.... This time I gave the tip to the homeless lady on the way in.... At least she said thank you.... Of course I did not eat here ended up walking out.... They were out of onions & peppers again.... And out of steak and just started cooking it.... Where's management? You know when you're running low to start prepping/cooking BEFORE you run out of anything.... Will I come back here.... Probably not...

Has KFC changed since I last had it? The quality of the chicken was really mediocre, or maybe I have an overly nostalgic view of their food. The chicken was swimming in grease (much much more than it should have been) and the Nashville hot chicken was was the most boring, bland, un-hot chicken I've ever tried. 

In addition, the restaurant was incredibly dirty (spilled soda on the floor, sticky floors all around, terrible bathroom, dirty windows). I should have known better, but given that I was starving, just off the plane, and desperate...well, there ya go. Never again.

My chipotle experience when I was much younger was scarring (had severe food poisoning that lasted two days from undercooked steak), so I was extremely hesitant to try them again. However, I'm glad I decided to face my fears... Sans beef. 

There is no exaggeration when people talk about the line here... To the door at 3 pm on a weekday? Cool, not like I had things to do (luckily I just got off work, not going in, otherwise I'd be screwed). It does also seem to be running rather slow, but there is also a sign on the front door saying they're hiring... Nothing like a literal warning sign to let you know things will be slow. When we got to the front of the line, there's a small sign that says due to circumstances that aren't their fault, they are out of certain ingredients. Turns out, that ingredient is black beans. And my my, people were upset bc we just waited in this ridiculous line to find out at the front that you have no black beans. Cool. Luckily, I don't like black beans, so I'm not phased. 

I got the chicken bowl with no beans, fajita veggies, cheese, salsa, corn pico, and lettuce. The boy got the same with sour cream. We got chips and guac (pretty sure you get more guac if you buy it that way) and a small coke. $18 and change. Ok, I can swallow it, but it's more expensive than my tex-mex restaurant of choice. 

After fighting a little bit trying to keep all the food in the bowl trying to mix it in, I was satisfied with the taste and the amount of food I got. If I want tex-mex but don't want to drive to my favorite place, I could handle eating here. But probably just the chicken bowl. Nothing else on their menu appealed to me.

Had a long day at work so decided to pick up something quick and easy for dinner tonight. Unfortunately when my husband and I got home we didn't get our full order, a piece of chicken and a side mash potatoes were not in our box. Very disappointed and way to tired to drive back just to pick up something that should of been done right. I guess we'll just have to go to Church's next time. PLEASE check your order before u leave for those who decide to go here.

As far as drive thru goes, the line always seems to move quickly. Food typically is done quickly and is hot. It's an awkward location that isn't always the easiest to get back out of, which in annoying. 

The Go Cups are our favorite, but steer away from the "bites." There's next to no chicken in the tiny balls of breading.  Also, don't forget to ask for butter for your bisquits. They don't just include it, like other locations. 

And this Strawberry Lemonade connoisseur highly disapproves of their processed, horribly sour mix. No beuno.

Portion sizes are very inconsistent. Yesterday the bowl was 50% full. They used 1/2 of spoonful for each of the toppings including rice, steak, and guac. They charge $2.00 for guac. I at least want a full portion.

Was here for early dinner yesterday. Nobody at the counter when I walk in, one guy working the drive-through doesn't know I'm there but someone finally comes forward. It's the evening rush hour, I get that. 

Friendly guy takes my order, a breast with mashed potato and biscuit, original. Right behind him is a female saying sorry for the wait. Another kinda-surly guy brings my order, OK, could be better could be worse.

Big delicious chicken breast, done just right, and tasty mashed potato plus biscuit. Only one or two others in spotless dining room done in wood and burgundy. Portraits of Colonel Sanders adorn the walls. They are still the chicken kings after all these years.

This is a combo KFC/Taco Bell.  As far as KFCs and Taco Bells go, this one is good. It's clean, service was prompt, and the food consistent.  I've developed quite a taste for the Taco Bell Fresco Menu.  For those of you on Weight Watchers, the fresco tacos, hard or soft, chicken or steak, are 4 points each.  A fresco burrito has 8 points.  So if your trying to give yourself a little taste of something naughty but stay within your points budget, this is great. 

This particular KFC has grilled chicken. The other combo Taco Bell I go to has a Long John Silver's, but because it's a combo they don't have grilled fish.  So if you're in the mood for healthier chicken choices, this place works.  Just stay away from the cole slaw, the mac and cheese, and the grilled stuffed burrito XXL, and you'll be fine.  I'm a fan!

Whopper Wednesdays! Awesome prices and not having to cook makes my family a happy family.  Whopper Wednesdays is a promotion that they're having at this (not sure about other) Burger King

Whopper Jr.: $1.29
Whopper: $1.89
Whopper Meal: $3.89

Not a part of WW, but 50cent ice cream cones!  Some barely give you anything and make you wonder why in the word you came here.  But ,there is this one employee, however, who is SUCH a SWEETHEART (infectious bright personality) and gives you way more than you expected. Plus, she makes them look beautiful.  From now on, if it's not her, I'm not buying ice cream and I'm just going to leave.

They also have the updated touchscreen soda machine with so many different drinks  and flavors to choose from.

I just went here because I had coupons, and it was convenient on my route.  Well, and I admit I'm a Yelp addict, so part of the reason was to Yelp about it, and get another cool-point for being the first to review this place.

Cleanliness is decent, cleaner than average.   But I did go right before the lunch rush.

My Whoppers (2-fer!) were pretty good for a Whopper.  The burgers are pre-cooked, but the sandwich is assembled to order, so all the ingredients are fresh, not wilted and squished (like McDonald's often is, if you get a standard pre-made/pre-assembled sandwich that sits around for up to 15 or 30 minutes.)

The men's room was decent.  I've seen better, but it meets my expectations for a Burger King franchise.

They have free Wi-fi.  You don't have to give them your email address, but you do have to click through an "I ACCEPT" (terms of use) screen.   Unfortunately, the Wi-fi Internet connection was so slow as to be useless.  It was like a dial-up in terms of speed.

We have not had BK in a long time. Got 2 Whoppers with fries and a drink.The whoppers came with no cheese! They were ordered as cheeseburgers. The burgers were dry and somewhat tasteless-but, we could taste the char flavor a little bit. Had to be nuked and reheated. Fries were also tasteless! We put our own cheese on the burgers when we got them home.
BK was a total waste of money. Very disappointed! 
What a sorry excuse for a Whopper!!! Now I know why we haven't bothered with BK for a long time.
Will not spend our money on lousy food again.Your competitor across is much better! Just thought you might like to know.

Rude workers. They constantly are out of food, this place shouldn't even be open if they never even have food.  The coworkers got mad that customers were coming in and yelling at each other to stop helping people because they want to go home, an hour and half before closing time. If you want chicken DO NOT GO HERE!! If I could give 0 stars I would.

My name is Collin, and I am addicted to Chipolte. The steak burrito is my biggest life-altering fast food experience that's not an In-N-Out double double. 

The steak is spicy and delicious, and the rice is flavored with the perfect amount of cilantro and lime. I also love the corn salsa, it is amazing. They also serve Coke Zero at this location, which is awesome for those of us that want to justify our stuffing hundreds of delicious extra calories in our pieholes by drinking the better tasting diet soda.

The line at this place, particularly around lunch time during the school year, can be out the door. Their fax ahead service can be a life saver, and I've heard of rumors of people now ordering over their smart phones with an app. I haven't tried that yet, but if you're in a hurry to get back to the office it might be the way to go. 

If they had queso and chips to go with the rest of this awesome Mexican food, I'd give them five stars in a second. I asked a manager once, and they said they can't find a way to make it fresh enough, so they simply don't carry it. I respect their commitment to having only the best and freshest ingredients, but it does mean I'm missing just a little something. Find a way, Chipolte!

This particular Burger King is another fast food place that is open late (I think until 3am). So it makes for a decent late night spot.

Burger King isn't the greatest but it can get the job done sometimes. It isn't as popular as McDonald's and that's probably because the food isn't as good. It does have some classic burgers though. The whopper and the whopper Jr. with mayo are burgers that I will never turn on. They have always satisfied me. I am not a big fan of the fries here, they are too fat and not crispy enough. Kind of dull to be honest. They do offer mozzarella sticks now and those are really great. 

It doesn't offer the best food but it does have some winners.

Literally my favorite place in the world. I would have a baby Chipotle burrito as my last meal on this earth. I prefer the steak, but the other choice are also spectacularly tasty. My favorite items are the sour cream and cheese, you can taste the freshness!

What ever happened to my favorite Burger King at Veterans and Transcontinental?  It dirty as heck - floors, condiment station, tables, parking lot and even the trash cans.  I actually glad they made my order to go because I doubt  those are clean when all else is not.  There not even music in dining area.  Thats not lazy, thats no clue..  They used to have an American flag out front but its just an empty pole now.  They place could care less, ss long as they get paid.  And a small dized Whopper Combo is $7.50?

Terrible! I am not a huge fan of kfc and I never eat at taco Bell. But one day I was really in the mood for their popcorn chicken. Big mistake! It was over cooked and almost broke a tooth. The cashier was extremely rude and the area where you would grab your drink was disgusting! 

I have never been in a fast food restaurant that was kept in such a manner. Please if you are going to eat here be mindful of how fresh the food is before leaving the restaurant. Gross!

i wish i could give this place a NEGATIVE 10 stars that's how awful my service was. its almost like more then half the employees are ignorant, ratchet kids, that are only working for money and not to better the company. "no fucks given" at this place! i went through the drive thru and asked how many chicken pieces are in a certain bucket and was told "its all written on the menu in front of you.. look" i wanted to leave right then but there were cars behind me.. so i ordered a soda and a random chicken sandwich.
