When i get to the window i was handed my drink and the top was not on all the way, and it spilled a very little on my hand while it was outside the window. i asked for napkin and the girl at the window rolled her eyes and said "its in the bag have a good day" and handed me the bag, while the spilled drink was still dripping from my hand. i grabbed my stuff and immediately pulled over, and called the store to speak to a manager. woman answered the phone and when i asked to speak to a manager, she put the phone down on what must of been  counter and i heard at least 2 or 3 employees talking among them selves and mimicked me "wahh i want to speak to a manager meeeeeh meh". i then heard the manager say "ok well i dont have time for this tell her im busy" i just hung up before she could pick the phone back up. i did write a letter to corporate and store manager but of course was never called back, or anything. i will be going to popeyes and taco bell for now on.

Of the three times my husband and I have tried to eat here, this was the first we were actually able to. Both of the other times, they were closed during the day for whatever reason. Anyways, we got in line behind about 40 people, but stayed because we were really craving their food. After waiting about 20 minutes, we were finally able to order. Our order wasn't very complicated, a carnitas quesadilla with a side of sour cream, steak burrito and chips and guacamole to go. Now I understand they were busy, it was a Saturday at 2:30 and I get the lunch rush thing, but when we got home, some food was missing (the sides of guacamole and sour cream) and my quesadilla had been mixed up with a previous customer. We were both very disappointed with the service we had received and will not be returning to this location.

There is a notice posted at the door of this Burger King saying that photography is not allowed. I wonder why they would even need to have such a notice but they have never asked me to stop photographing the food. Anyway, that's just the strangest thing about this place. Service here is the pits at times. For some reason, I had enormous difficulty getting the cashier to input the correct item into the cash register when I tried getting something beyond the usual regular Whopper or chicken sandwich. It was like trying to get through the fog of her mind. Also, they tend not to prepare the food with care. More often than not, the buns come out all squished. Their main advantage though is the location is conveniently near Prices Corner.

I find the service here to be lack luster and non professional.  My order is more often than not incorrect when I get home. 

The Managers do try their best to remedy the problem after the fact.

I find they are often out of chicken in large quantities and I have to
wait or go somewhere else for my meal.

My 1st time at this location. The food was consistent with what I have had at other locations, it was good, hot, fresh, and everybody who handled any food wore new, clean gloves. The beef was a bit on the salty side, but perfect for me because, I often add salt to my food. I had the barbacoa tacos, on corn tortillas, with pinto beans, Pico de gallo, hot salsa and cheese. The kitchen appeared quite clean, the front steam table was spotless, and well maintained, All of the staff looked well groomed as well, they should.
        I read a bunch of bad reviews before I decided to give this location a try(as long as I won't get sick I like to form my own opinions). I walked in not expecting much, and I was in somewhat of a bad mood from a rough day at work. I looked at the menu board, decided what I want, then, as I'm about to basically demand exactly what I want, and how I want it, (Marissa)the girl taking my order and starting my tacos has an infectious smile, and a genuinely happy demeanor. She explains all about the proteins for the tacos to choose from(there are a few pork, chicken, steak, and pulled beef), gives me a small taste to be sure I like what I'm choosing(I wasn't sure at that point, due to selection), after she added the first couple of ingredients to start off with, she handed my tacos and I down to the next contagiously happy and smiling pro taco girl(it was kind of busy, and they handled it very well). I didn't catch her name, but she was just as friendly, well spoken, and like the others I interacted with, seemed genuinely happy to be working there, and was great at hospitality/customer service, as well as preparing our food. She put the final ingredients on my food, and it was clear that she wanted to make the tacos absolutely perfect for us by watching the way she put them together, you could tell she cares, they were good. The girl at the register also was happy and smiling, she was more than happy to be there it seemed, she went out of her way to get me the salsa I wanted for my chips, I asked her to mix them all together. All 3 of the girls serving my wife and I are without a doubt, an asset to that restaurant, and parent company. Going in there for dinner tonight definitely lightened mood. it's good when the people serving you, and handling your food are happy. I'll will be back often, I hope those 3 girls are working the next time. Leave them tips, they deserve it! I give the 5 stars for the service,  and 3.5 for food. It is without a doubt eating there, my tablet was $17.07 after tax, we ordered; chicken tacos, barbacoa tacos, and chips and salsa. I left $4.93 I tips, they deserve it.

I am really conflicted about how to rate.  The staff was pleasant enough, but not very knowledgable.  They did handle a boorish customer with grace.  I felt bad for them, as this boor was berating them for something they have no control over.  Another plus is that they have chicken livers on the buffet.  However, this restaurant could be MUCH cleaner than it is.  Floor is filthy, and trash cans are overflowing, and it was well after lunch rush.
Will come again! The biscuit was fire ' and they give good portions of chicken and Mac n cheese !

The computers were down, so I had to go inside to order.  Once inside, the order taker seemed confused about how to take an order without the computer.  I ordered the 8 piece meal, with cole slaw and macaroni and cheese.  Instead of mac and cheese I got green beans.  Silly me, I did not check the order before leaving and driving home, so I am stuck with the canned green beans.

When it came time to pay, they could not take my credit card because of the computer problem.  I usually do not carry much cash, but I did have just barely enough to pay for my order.   Now I wish I had just walked out.

The place is filthy, ugly, and the service terrible.  The chicken was OK, just like KFC always is, and the cole slaw is really quite good.  But the only reason I stopped here was that I thought to myself, "I have not gone to a KFC in 20 years, so why not try it?"  WRONG MOVE.  This restaurant needs to just go away.  I will never go to KFC again in my life.  

Why the corporation allows this franchise to exist is beyond me.

Since it's the closest one to me I come often. However, I do mobile orders all the time and the majority of the time the orders are not given to me correct. I ordered two Texas toast sandwiches with sausage instead of bacon. Well you gave me bacon on both. Then I wanted a XL dark roast and you gave me original blend. Figure it out.

The first meal of the year?... White Castle at about 3 in the morning after a long night of work and then NYE celebrations.  Go ahead and judge me... but I love whities.  Not everything is good here, but the Bacon Egg and Cheese sandwhiches are the bomb... and I wanted to try the new veggie slider.  IMO, it was better than the regular greasy-meat slider.  

And the window attendants, are actually in a good mood... I'm not sure how... dealing with drunk after drunk.

I think I am losing faith in Chipotle....When I heard they were opening in D-town I was ecstatic, but after 6 trips in the last 4 months, I have come to the conclusion that the simplistic menu is actually bumming me out. How many times can I come up with a new combination of meat, veg and cheese that is somewhat exciting?
Plus, when are they going to get carnitas back in stock? Come on already.
Nice staff at this location, saving this rating from only 2 stars.,

If my choice was here or Taco Smell, I'd choose here. Otherwise, my first isit was nothing amazing. 

Very typical American "Mexican". I got a bowl. Carnitas were ok. My son's steak was better. Beer wa good though!

Went in this morning and ordered a bacon egg and cheese on an everything bagel, and a turkey sausage flatbread sandwich. Only got one order of hash browns, and noticed that the turkey sausage flatbread was actually a sausage, egg, and cheese on an English muffin. Walked back inside,  and told the woman and the front counter that the order was incorrect,  and she looked up the prices then said "okay they're the same price so I don't have to change you again" which confused me since I didn't get what I paid for and was bringing it back so clearly I didn't eat it. She then walked to the man making the food and let him know and he looked at me and his response was "bread is bread,  what does it matter?" then proceeded to start making more sandwiches before turning to me and stating very rudely "well I have other orders so you'll just have to wait" 
very unimpressed with their service.

I wanted to treat myself to lunch today and stopped by KFC.  It's a dining experience you'll want to remember.  

This was the first time I dined in and ordered a one piece breast meal.  I sat near the window and enjoyed the tv, which was airing golfing.  David, GM circled the dining room and greeted guests.  You're treated as a GUEST and not a CUSTOMER.   I had a wonderful experience.  The convo started from a question from David to the history on the building and the previous owner... to the present KFC, this KFC has been in business for over 2 years.  

The decor inside is mainly white with two separate dining rooms.  You'll have plenty of window seating to choose from.  I definitely felt like I was in a regular restaurant instead of a take out. 

All in all...I scored a one piece boneless chicken.  Very crunchy cause it's double battered and juicy.  Thank you David!  I will visit again.

This location makes me angrier than the Angry Whopper.  Why?  Not because of the employees, they're friendly.  No, it angers me because of their prices.  Now, you might be wondering what I mean.  Well let me explain.  Their prices are horrendously expensive when compared to other Burger King locations within a 10 mile radius of this location.  Let me go into further detail so that you can understand why I say this.  Tonight, prior to writing this review, I decided to do a little research to see if this is still the case (I hadn't been visiting this location in a long time due to the huge price differences).  The results of my research were as follows:

Menu item:  Angry Whopper (SANDWICH ONLY)

Location: Blackwood Clementon Road; Price: $5.19
Location: 594 Cross Keys Road; Price: $7.19 

Are you *angry words* serious!?  A $2.00 difference?  That's absolutely ludicrous.  And it's not unique to this sandwich either.  ALL their items share a similar pricing issue.  Why is this locations so outrageously expensive compared to all other Burger King locations within a 10 mile radius?  

The -only- items that share similar pricing are the universally set promo items that are highly advertised at a set price on TV commercials (for example, the 2 for $5 deal).  The rest of the items are priced insanely higher than all other BK locations, for absolutely no reason.  Whoever owns this location really needs to stop gouging prices and price to stay competitive with other Burger King locations in the area.

This location is right down the street from my house, like 1 minute away, and I drive like 8 minutes to the Blackwood Clementon Road location just because the pricing is MUCH more realistic and fair.  If you want my business, then lower your prices.  You LOSE business when you are SIGNIFICANTLY (as my above example illustrated) more expensive than other BK locations people can choose to go to by only driving a little further.

Their staff is VERY friendly though.  So that's a plus, but not enough for an extra star.  Also their food is typically decent for fast food, so there's that, but it's not any better or worse than other BK locations.  It's typical of all BK food.

Please don't give this location business if you value paying a fair price for your food.  There is no reason that this location should be so much more expensive than other BK locations in the area.  Go to one of the other nearby locations, and save a decent chunk of your hard earned money.
Just went to drive thru and was told it would be 30 min. before chicken would be done. Naturally I left. I thought KFC was a fast food place.

This is a rather unusual KFC because the restaurant is a converted two-storey house. It still looks like one of the original buildings in the Main Line area. The dining area inside uses two ground-level rooms in the house. The order area is fairly standard and the food is standard KFC. On this visit, I tried KFC's latest food experiment, the Double Down. I can't say I haven't been warned. The two original recipe chicken fillets were very greasy and made a bit of a mess without a bun to hold it all in. However, it tasted exactly the way I imagined it would taste.

I rarely give 1 or 2 star reviews on any site. I'd rather not write the review at all, but I felt it needed to be said. I recently ordered their country steak biscuit via their drive-thru service. This biscuit is one of my guilty pleasure favorites. I've had it at many locations in the past (including this one) and loved it. Unfortunately, I didn't get the opportunity to eat it. They gave me some other kind of breakfast sandwich. I didn't realize it until I drove all the way home. I was very disappointed. I was the only car at the drive-thru, and they couldn't get the order right. The only part of the order that was correct was the Blueberry Biscuit. It was an impulsive order that I made after seeing the pretty picture of it on the drive-thru menu. It was a microwaved mess when I got it home. The blueberry filling and the glaze were all shifted off the biscuit to one corner of the box. They must have nuked it too long. It looked nothing like the picture and didn't even taste that good. I don't think I'll be back to this location.

I really like Chipotle and their competitor Qdoba. It's a great fast place to get healthy (if you want) food that is really tasty. 

My one complaint about Chipotle is that because their policy is to make their steak medium rare to medium (because it sits and continues to cook while waiting to be served), I have to have them throw my portion back on the grill to cook it more. This shouldn't be a big problem, but you'd think I was asking the world of them to have the employees do this for me. I just started ordering chicken because honestly, it wasn't worth arguing with the people behind the counter over the fact that lots of red in my steak is gross to me. 

Typically they're friendly. Their location is good and parking is pretty easy to find most of the time.

I actually really like Chipotle when I'm trying to watch what I'm eating. It's easy to get a low carb, high protein, super flavorful meal here. I usually get salad with carnitas, black beans, cheese, spicy salsa, and guacamole. It's a low carb dieters dream meal!

Horrible Service.  I normally place a $500.00 order for my company to feed 70 people on a weekly basis.  The management has changed several times in the past year.  Since then, I can not order anymore food for my staff.  Here is why.

On Tuesday, September 1st, I called Shannon, the manager, asked him for 130 pieces of chicken with some sides.  He stated to me that he would be able to accommodate me for Saturday, September 5th, 2015.  He asked that I call him in the morning since I wanted to send someone to pick up the food at 11:30 am.  When I did at 10;30 am this morning, he had not even started the process as of yet since, so he states, "a lot of things on his mind."  I am not sure why the GM of a store would not be ready 1 hour prior to my pick up, but, at that point, I was not going to give more of an opportunity to let me down. I immediately hung up the phone and called Ardmore Pizza.  Those guys are great and I will never shop at this KFC again.

If I can give a 0 Star I fucking would!! I got my food through drive thru and when I got home to eat the chicken in my spicy chicken sandwich was BURNED and it looked disgusting so I threw it in the garbage. I will never eat from here again!!
Nasty buffet.  Kept having to ask for more chicken.  Area not clean at all.  The water was a combination of pink lemonade.  Will not be going back

Worst Dunkin experience I have ever had. I ordered a ham wake up wrap and they had no wraps so I got it on an English muffin. 10 minutes later they come over to tell me they are out of English muffins so I had to get it on a bagel. My boyfriend ordered an angus steak on a bagel and then we found out they were out of steak so he had to substitute sausage. We ended up waiting in the Dunkin for over 20 minutes to get 3 sandwiches. It was absolutely ridiculous and unacceptable. Do not go to this Dunkin.

It's usually a hit or miss. It depends on who is working on the amount of rice you get in your burrito bowl. But, I do have to say I do love the food when I get it. Steak or veggie burrito bowl, white lime rice and the sweet corn salsa. The chips are addicting because of the hint on lime seasoning. It's a nice mix of salt and lime.

Stopped in here Saturday morning 10/18/2014 first time at this location. The wife and I ordered spicy sausage breakfast sandwich meals and to say we were highly disappointed the hash browns were horrible the package had five of the tiny circular type.I had gotten a coke out of the cabinet which they all were hot so I asked for a cup of ice.We were going to eat inside but all of the tables were filthy.I will stick with donuts and a different location.What a waste of money.

We've eaten here many times but tonight we were very disappointed in the cleanliness. When we first went in, we immediately noticed how dirty the floor was. Then looked for a clean table but none had been cleaned. We ended up sitting at one of the hi-top counter and stool tables, which, with two small kids was not very good. The entire time we were eating no one ever came to clean the other tables. Then when we got our food, we had to wait another 5 minutes or so because there were no forks.

And I also thought the steak on my salad was different. Not seasoned well. 

Maybe we'll try a different location.

Ok guys so I'm a chipotle finantic!! The food was amazing and fresh!!! I had the opportunity to have a nice young lady fix my bowl and she was very nice and interactive her name was Alexis I believe. She even suggested I try sofritas and it was AMAZING. Thanks to her!!! Unfortunately, my experience was a little compromised when I got to the cashier section. The cashier was very nonchalant, rude, and was overall not attentive at all. She didn't ask cash or card, chips, juice, etc. Clearly she did not want to be there so that was very disheartening. I asked an employee for her name they said it was Danielle! I hope the manager takes care of this small issue. But overall great food, clean restaurant, and Alexis was very sweet!

Portion sizes much smaller than other Chipotle locations. I asked for a half steak/half barbacoa and the woman serving me made several attempts at picking the smallest possible amount of meat. She literally put the meat back because she thought it was too much for one order. Very odd experience. Likely won't be coming back here again.

The worst ever!  

Ordered a whooper junior.  Came to $2.13.  I gave the guy 3.00.  Then I said I had change so give me the extra dollar back.  I gave him 2.20.  He gives me a dollar back.  I said you owe me seven cents.  He says real sarcastic that he didn't know I wanted it.  Really?  If it is a penny owed to me I want it.  Then I can decide if I want to leave it or not.  I was amazed.  He was the manager too!  Stopped about 7:00 on June 11.

I love those little whopper jr s.  It was so cold I had to double check to see if it was cooked.  It was and it was like they took it out of the freezer.

Chipotle is awesome most of the time. Don't go right before they close. Definitely my favorite burrito chain. 

Make sure to put your phone away when you're ordering, they don't like that too much.

Yum yum yum. Carnitas or chicken, veges, pico, medium and corn salsa, just a dash of sour cream with lots of cheese and lettuce. Don't forget the smoked tabasco.

Ive given this location several opportunities, but the staff seem not to know what they are doing, no matter how many times I order the same thing, they find a way to get it wrong. 
I asked for a Big n' Toasted, asked if I could get extra bacon, the cashier told me of the up charge on which I replied no problem. 
I drove off and when I opened my sandwich, it had a piece of sausage.
The next time I went through the drive thru, I had to ask the young lady at the window if I could get my straws and some napkins to wipe off the cups that had stuff spilled on it, she gave me a single napkin and a single straw though there were three drinks ordered. 
Though this store is one of the newer ones in the area, I wont be going back, will go to the one on 16055 State Road 52
Land O Lakes, FL 34638.
They are much quicker even when they have a  line and get their stuff done properly. THe employees overall are nicer and smile at the customer, which this one doesnt and they are incredibly slow even when there isnt a line, that is mainly directed to the night crew.

The only time I've eaten KFC is when my parents bought big buckets of fried chicken.  This time, however, I had a coupon for their chicken bites, so I decided to stop by for lunch and give them a try.

The servers were nice, but they didn't have napkins out in the condiment/drink area.  I had to request extra napkins, which I thought was weird.  It's a basic fast food place, so I won't get into details about the environment (although, this place was relatively clean).

The food, was surprisingly good.  I've always been a fan of popcorn chicken/chicken nuggets/etc. and these were really pretty good.  Even though they were probably frozen at one point, the chicken was juicy and the breading had a nice flavor.  I also liked their potato wedges, which were their version of french fries.  They had a relatively large selection of sauces, and I chose the creamy buffalo and the bacon ranch.  Both sauces were very good.  While fast food isn't always my first choice, this place was actually pretty good.
Disgusting. Haven't had KFC IN YEARS.  Not even close to what I remember. Wrong color, greasy as hell, inedible. If I was in the mood to drive back I'd throw that nasty ass chicken in their face.

Wanted a quick lunch before getting a massage. I went thru the drive thru. The person taking my order was very pleasant and informative. I ordered a Jr whopper meal. I requested cheese on my burger and no salt on my fries and soda. Service was quick but the window person was not smiling. 
My meal was made as I ordered and tasted good. 
The parking lot was clean. The parking lot also had a trash can in the parking lot. I thought that was a great idea for people who eat in their cars. I will most likely return to this Burger King. The location is prime especially that it is near the Regal Warrington.

We stopped into this Dunkin on a Sunday morning and the line was crazy long. The way the store is set up there really isn't room for more than 4-5 people to stand in line so we were wrapped around like a cinnamon roll blocking the door.

When we finally got up to order the gentleman that helped us was nice and seemed attentive. Got us our donuts, and gave us a number for the rest of our things. After waiting about 10 min and still not getting two of our things, we went up to the counter only to realize that he never rang up two of our sausage biscuits. By this point, we didn't want to stand in line again and wait, so we left.

This Burger King sits in the neighborhood of Sesame Place and Oxford Valley Mall.  As such, it is in a premier location to attract families and shoppers for a fast bite.  Anticipating this, BKHQ designed this shop with extra space, large kiddie room, and a multitude of dining areas with different looks and feels. They even put in a fireplace lounge for those in a romantic mood. It may be the nicest Burger King I have ever seen. Almost date-worthy.

My Whopper was as good as a Whopper can be, and still fell short of much of the competition. It was hot, but the large slices of onion were not. Mayo and lettuce caused slippage, and my fingers bore witness to the consequence. Fries were decent and there were plenty of carbonated beverages, while not the excessive choice of the new fangled sodie machines. 

Service was amazingly efficient for a fast food operation, with servers walking my tray over to me beyond the counter. The space was very clean. 

If BK figured out how to make a first rate burger, a joint like this would be a revelation.

How poorly can a place be managed. Always out of things. Somedays chicken today coleslaw. Place is tired and ran down. There is no pride at this KFC by ownership or management. Employees are usually nice enough but come on man get your ordering down. How can you always be out of something?

Wow what a complete disaster.  Inaudible ordering at the drive through.  Person taking order could not explain why menu offerings did not agree with the prices or choices offered by the order taker "Ronesha."  See receipt.  I would fire her immediately.  No "please," no "thank you," ignored my requests for what kind of chicken I wanted.  In my opinion she deliberately ignored me.  Rude.

I was *very* careful to keep the bag upright.  All the gravy spilled in the bottom of the bag.  I'll never know for sure but it's my belief this was *deliberately* done by your staff.

Chicken lovers I saw 2-3 other places within a few blocks.  Go there.  Do not go here.  KFC Headquarters:  consider pulling the license for this franchisee they are destroying your brand.  Mr.  Manager:  I deserve to be compensated for the entire disastrous meal.  Congratulations  :  worst food experience I've had in recent memory.

It has been 30 years of enjoying Kentucky Fried Chicken that has compelled me to write this review about the Oracle location in Tucson.
I am not naive regarding the challenges of running restaurants, I have owned three. Good News.. Food is fine at this location.
Bad News.. the staff is consistently horrible. Seriously ... They are destroying the Brand at this location. 
From reading other reviews people are avoiding this location. 
The  store is filthy every time we go. 
The staff has gotten so bad that last night my husband stood completely ignored at the counter for 8 minutes while they were yelling and horsing around. He was the only person waiting , they never acknowledged him waiting , he walked out and they didn't even notice.

I thought this might be a bad night until i started looking at the reviews on Yelp and they did the same thing to someone at 4:30 that decided to take the time to write something.
THEY HAVE A BIG problem at this location! FIRE THE MANAGER, TRAIN GM AND FRANCHISEE AND CLEAN HOUSE.

They have signs up that they are short staffed and all of the doors are locked. They only allow drive thru. I ordered 10 chicken littles and a zinger sandwich. I returned home to find not even half of a tender on half of the sandwiches. I tried to call and they wouldn't answer the phone so I went back only to find the store had no customers just employees. Needless to say after being told that the tenders come in different sizes, I finally was compensated for a slighted order. But the manager did make it right after debating the cost of the sandwich and telling me the process in which the tenders get broke up.

This Burger King is located in the Acme shopping center in Medford on Rt 70. Where the dmv is. 
This has got to be the worst Burger King. The drive thru wait is ridiculous. I actually writing this while im waiting in the drive thru. Just order a whopper meal. No biggie. It took just under 10 min to get my food. Only 1 car was in front of me. Crazy
First time going there today complete order was wrong chicken was soggy I ordered a three-piece white and got a two piece dark tried to call the store but nobody answers just awful

Where's the beef, more like where are the breast pieces. If I pay almost $40 for a family meal and wait over half an hour for the damn order to be filled, I expect to get some breast pieces, makes sense, right. Well common sense is definitely lacking at this god forsaken franchise. 

I pull up and order, was told they only have original and extra crispy mixed available now, no worries, I like both versions. And I'm well aware that they cook their chicken in batches for maximum freshness. But when I get my order home, my chicken pieces are shriveled and dry. And, no breast pieces are to be found. Again, if I'm going to pay full price for chicken, I expect to have all pieces included. 

The verdict, what's the point of voicing my disappointment to the store manager, it seems that person doesn't give a damn about customer service, judging by my fellow Yelper's feedback. I would advise steering clear from this dismal franchise. I only wish they would do the community a huge favor and close down permanently.
Just spent 20 minutes in the drive thru behind only one vehicle.  No apology just "$7.84" for the chicken strips that I'm sure we're cold.  No thanks.  You guys eat em.

This is by far the worst KFC I have been too!! I ordered 16$ worth food and then was asked to pull to the front to wait on fresh chicken strips. After 12 min of waiting I go inside to be told that I never ordered anything but a chicken little.... even if I did order one thing...why would it take that long to make it.... then attempted to tell me they would start my order over....  the restaurant was dirty...the food on the bar looked old and food everywhere!! A lady who claimed to be manager said she was off work as she went back and forth between the bathroom and walking around and sitting in office.. totally inappropriate and uncomfortable for someone to deal with !!  I won't ever come to this location again!!  The place wasn't even busy!!!! Come on now. And if you are going to step outside to smoke..at least wash your hands before returning to fix someone's meal!!  If I didn't order anything then why was I given a refund?!  Crazy place

I can't even begin to describe the horror of this restaurant. How KFC allows it to stay open is perplexing. 

This is our closest KFC restaurant. I keep hoping it will improve but it never does. First, it's filthy. The drive thru is always surrounded by flies and looking inside the floors look nasty. 

Second, the food is horrible. No matter whether you order original or crispy chicken you're going to get something with minimal breading (if any). More than once the side dishes have been spilled out in the bag or box. 

Finally, the staff seems to care nothing about doing their jobs. They take forever to take orders and if you get tired of waiting and drive up to the window they claim the speaker is broken despite no sign. I loathe to say this but every time I've been here the staff also look unkempt and unhygienic. Their uniforms are dirty, Ill fitting and often with holes. Obviously, the manager and franchise owner have no pride and this filters down. 

I didn't realize how bad they were till I stopped at the KFC on Ina and found a friendly and attentive staff and chicken that was fresh and delicious. 

Just give this place a pass. The best they can do is take away the franchise, close it, remodel and hire a new staff from bottom to top.

I have been to this location a few times. They are not friendly. Twice they have shorten the pieces of chicken in m bucket. I do not recommend this location. They need to be trained on how to treat there customer.

My past few experiences haven't been to great, but tonight my mind was changed! I believe it was Rocky, Camille, and Summer behind the line. They were really friendly and I didn't feel rushed like usual.  I got to talk to them while waiting for steak, which they made fresh because I've had recent bad experiences. Best bowl I've had in weeks :) thanks

HORRIBLE! This location deserves 0 stars. The restaurant wasn't crowded, so I'm not sure why the young girl behind the counter was moving like it was rush hour....I watched her slap rice and beans like she was training for the Chipotle Olympics (let me know if there is such a thing!)

Back to the Burrito Nazi: she stares at you, asks you if you want steak, rice...then proceeds to slap it on your burrito sloppily. The kicker: all the mess from the rice, beans, lettuce, etc ends up on the dirty counter and she wipes it with her hands to the trash and proceeds to use those same dirty hands to roll numerous burritos! 

Maybe she was pissed off because 4 of her co-workers were chillin in the restaurant eating burritos and tacos...I dunno, but damn....
Will NEVER come back again....I'll take the drive to University City and grab Qdoba instead.
Avoid at all costs. Ordered a veggie burger and got a bacon cheeseburger. Requested a whopper with no cheese and tomato and it had no mayo and cheese and tomato. Missing kids chicken nuggets

I ordered a steak bowl online for pickup to eat on my lunch break. Went to pick it up and went back to work, all fine. Until I begin to open my bowl and realize they forgot the steak in my steak bowl... Had to drive back because I wasn't about to eat just rice, cheese, and salsa. Then when I arrived at Chipotle again they told me "Ok, here you go." One little cup of steak pictured... One of the worst food experiences of my life, never coming back to this Chipotle.

this kfc NEVER has food past 9.....i just left there 5 min ago(they close at 10:30pm its 9:30pm) and they have no chicken....LOL really? no chicken? not the first time its happened im sure not the last
I so far have been pleased at this location. Staff was quick and food was good. I like to get a 50 centcone then use the review on the receipt to get the whopper and a fry and drink.

Since it opening. I have been going to this location. I never have any problem until just last week. I order half chicken and half steak bowl with guacamole. Came home and eat my bowl, as I use my fork to grab some guacamole, I find a big shell of avocado. I was not happy.

This location is terrible. Every single time I go there they mess up my order. I have never in my life gotten my exact order. They always forget my sauce, my cookies, some of my chicken, sides that I order... And I get charged for it all. Half the time they overcharge me because they don't understand that I am not adding a side, I'm just changing the one that comes with the meal EVEN after I clarify with them what I want. I order the same thing every time and I've been charged $17+ or $10.70 so it makes no sense. Do not recommend at all.

I went to this KFC earlier this evening and wasted $25 on the 8 piece meal. Ended up spitting out my food and throwing all of it away, it was that bad. My roommate agreed. I ordered extra crispy chicken but only received 3 extra crispy pieces, the rest were regular and undercooked. The chicken was also super greasy and had a bad oily flavor. Coleslaw was old and slightly brown. Biscuits were burnt. Never coming back to this KFC. Where are the managers at this place???

So, my experience with the crew was I heard a employee using foul language as he explained a story about an incident that happen. Then the 2 customers ahead of me was told they were out of potpies...wow! They left. I was next I ordered a #6 only to find out they were out of coleslaw (REALLY? I asked) SMH and asked for the potato logs and said it was for here. I received my order in a bag...COLD chicken and the potato logs were not only cold but old like they were made hours ago...NOT FRESH at all. So basically if I were you I'd would avoid this place completely. They need new management and a crew should be spoken to on the language, and behavior in a place of business.

TLDR: if you're reading this, just go to a different Chipotle nearby and save yourself a shitty meal.

As a very big of Chipotle in general and someone who is typically quite forgiving of bad experiences as restaurants I really don't like writing negative reviews for things, but this is seriously the only Chipotle I've gone to where I leave unhappy every time I visit.  These guys consistently short you on food, at least if you're going in for lunch.  You shouldn't have to ask for double or even TRIPLE servings in order to get the same standard sized portions you get at every other one.  Normal Chipotle: one large to heaping scoop of steak, sometimes even another partial scoop if the person doesn't feel it was adequate.  This Chipotle: half scoop at absolute best.  Yes they are stingy on other ingredients, but the protein is what I care about most.  Not only that, but do you even season your meat here??  At nearly $9 a bowl this is pretty ridiculous, and this restaurant is an outlier to the overall very consistent Chipotle standard.  At this point, I would literally rather drive 20 minutes from Green Hills to either Thompson Lane or up to West End and fight that horrible traffic than go to this particular restaurant again.  Every time I do it feels like a waste of money to someone that doesn't eat out often.

Only positives are that the restaurant is clean and new, but that's all it has going for it.

I would give this specific location a 0 star rating if I could, every time I go there they get something wrong, I've had hair in my food found a roach on the floor and the staff is so incredibly rude, specifically the girls. I pulled up to the menu and she said "yes" I ordered what I wanted and she said "ok $--.- at the window." And I pulled up and she gave me dirty look. That's not the way staff should be run there. The boss really needs to fire everyone in there and rehire a complete new staff, and actually know  what their doing. They sat there at the window before she even took my card and were talking for a good 3 minutes before even opening the window. People like that don't take their job seriously.
The impossible burger was great, best idea and marketing Burger King could have ever made. Much healthier and something on the menu for the people.

Vegetarians Unite!  Burger King is now offering the Impossible whopper at all its locations!  For about $5 you can have a large filling vegetarian burger with whopper sauce. Since I haven't eaten red meat in over 10 years I do still think it it taste very very similar to a regular whopper. My only concern would be how would I know if they actually use the impossible burger or not a regular burger. I can definitely say this is better than the veggie burger they've had in the past

Came after a day at Busch Gardens with friends and was highly disappointed with the food quality, customer service and restaurant cleanliness. One of our group members ordered double steak, but really received triple rice and half a serving of steak. Several guests in the group asked for guac on top of their bowls and received actual pieces of avocado slices and and avocado peels. Doesn't sound like a big deal, except there were no plastic knives to cut up the slices. When sitting down, EVERY SINGLE TABLE was dirty. Only after cleaning the tables ourselves with the napkins that were within the original paper packaging, did the employee come out to clean the remaining dirty tables. Overall, food quality was poor in addition to the previously stated inconveniences. My friends and I are regular chipotle customers in our hometown, so we were happy to see one nearby. However, for the first time, we were gravely disappointed by this particular chipotle location.

So I hate doing this but this is the 2nd time this has happened to us. We've tried to order the beyond meat burger and they have been out. What I think is happening is that they have them frozen and aren't willing to thaw them. I get it that it's an extra step and probably an adjustment to have to cook vegetarian friendly. But it's really frustrating as a vegetarian that wants to pay the extra money to have a burger. I know this is in the beginning stages and it will take time for both buzz to die down and the franchise to figure out the best way to sell this. But it shouldn't have happened twice.

This was our first time here. Had been wanting to try for a while.  What a disappointment. Not only is it beyond outrageous in price for what you get but also the food was not really that good. Even my husband who is pretty forgiving disliked it.  Grilled chicken had a burnt taste and the steak was all tough fat and chewy. One and done. For a good meal and a whole lot cheaper try Moe's. Won't be back. Also everything you want in your burrito bowl is extra. Queso and guacamole over $2 each and chips and quest over 3 bucks. At Moe's it come with your meal add queso for nominal amount.

The place is filthy. Not a clean table anywhere. The drink dispenser has spills, lids and trash everywhere. Napkins are all over the floor. No one behind the counter smiles. It's obvious this place is in need of new management. 
Got the bucket of chicken home and it was burnt. Check the picture. How does that happen when all the ovens are automated?!

I know, I know, I'm reviewing a Burger King. My expectations should be low, and not require a review. But, I had to share. I ran through BK last night to get an impossible whopper meal. The ordering process was smooth, and I got my food. When I pulled away (Drive-Thru with kiddos is the only way to survive) and grabbed a fry, I was stunned. They smelled strange, and were inedible. I think that their oil may have been rancid, because it was unlike any fries I've ever had. I love fries (who doesnt?), but these were not edible. The burger was good, I asked for no pickles but they were still on the burger, but that's ok. I'd rather eat pickles than taste those strange fries again. Be warned!

So my husband and I had a long day and we saw KFC and decided to stop. We pulled into the drive through and got an extremely over done welcome. We decided on an 8pc chicken meal, extra crispy, with coleslaw and potato wedges. Simple enough??? Hardly!!! We got to the window and the cashier took our payment. He disappears for a more that reasonable amount of time. He comes back hands us a bag with a bucket, a box of wedges, and a small bag which contained coleslaw. I should have checked the order before I pulled away. Never. Never. Never!!! Always check the order. The extra crispy chicken and wedges were not fresh at all. The biscuits were missing completely and the coleslaw was almost 25% liquid. My husband loves slaw and was very upset at what he had in front of him. You are supposed to have 2 breasts, 2 wings, 2 thighs, and 2 legs with an 8 PC meal. We had in our bucket, 2 breasts, 2 thighs, 3 legs, and 1 wing. I called the store when we discovered that we had been mistreated and the manager on shift acted like we were trying to get a free meal. We just wanted fresh food. For $22, you want something decent at least. The embarrassing part of it all is that I worked for JRN associates and actually was part of the crew that opened this store back in 2000. Management and crew alike do not seem to care what you think and I definitely recommend that if you are going to dine here definitely dine in, check your order, and if it is not fresh (which it probably will not be), demand better. Sorry KFC, this store has become an epic fail!!!

We drove through to get the new Impossible Burger, luckily, there was no wait and our food came out the way it should. The burger itself claims to taste like meat but it's a bit dry. After looking up the nutritional facts, its only 30 calories less than a regular Whopper with sodium levels that are sky high (probably to season the patty to taste like meat). So I definitely think you should try it once if you're curious, but you're not missing anything and if you eat meat, I highly recommend a regular Whopper over the Impossible one. Also, the burger is ginormous, I could eat half of it and be full. Service through the drive thru was decent and since there was no wait, I have no complaints. The only thing about this location is that it can get pretty congested and depending on traffic, if you're on the opposite side of the road, it can be hard to turn into BK.

First time I have ever had White Castle. It's decent, I had some fries that were not as great as some other places. The sliders were solid, I had the standard slider with Cheese. I will note that in comparison to Krystal, these are a bit less greasy I guess? The most impacting thing was the heavy cheesy taste, which I liked!

The King of Burgers.  I meet up with a friend and mentor once a month at Burger King.  It is a nice relaxed atmosphere with a full selection of burgers including the Jr., Whopper, and Double Whopper.  The burger is served on a sesame roll with pickles, romaine, catchup, and mayo.  Sides include salads, fries, and chicken fingers.  I personally got a Whopper Jr. and a small coffee.  The coffee is quite good.

The regulars tend to be older men and a few teenagers.  It is just a nice quiet place to sit, chat, and grab a quick bite.

They are supposed to have WIFI, but every time I ask they tell me go to another location that actually has it.  It would be a nice addition to be able catch up on emails and write yelp reviews while eating a burger.

Seriously the worst. After trying to cash a free Whopper coupon in the drive thru, I was told you need to log it in on the app. Fine. I pull around and have to apparently download an update on the app (which there's always a problem with). 

The entire process took about an hour from the time I pulled in the drive thru until I sat in my truck eating.

You won't get fresh meat here because there was talk of cooking too much meat so they shut the grill off for the night around 9-9:30. So until midnight everyone else gets old meat.

The car in the drive thru was stuck there forever too waiting and so much food in the overhead warmer, but nothing that they needed. They were joking about someone back there smoking a doob. Well, "have it your way" has a whole new meaning.

I've had such bad service at this location before, but I gave it a try again today. I was once again very dissatisfied. One of the burritos that I ordered was with chicken and steak and the young Indian woman that was scooping it scooped four small pieces of steak and half of a scoop of chicken. She considered that a double chicken and double steak burrito, charging me for two scoops of chicken and two scoops of steak. She was so rude and had so much attitude towards me. 

The restaurant was so dirty and there was food all over the counters where they were preparing customer's food. The drink and condiment area was so messy and gross too. All of the tables had food on them so I had to sit outside. 

They had so many workers behind the counter doing nothing when they could have been cooking and preparing more food to go out to the line so that the girl didn't have to be scooping such small portions. They also should have had someone cleaning the lobby area.

The Chipotle workers at this location are sloppy, the place is disgusting, and the customers are left unhappy. I am reconsidering going to Chipotle again after my last visit.
I'm not from town, but this chipotle was great! Love the sofritas and the spicy salsa. Great service and a clean, nice area to eat. Keep up the good work!

This has got to be the most dysfunctional BK around.  I ordered two whoopers and asked if they can make them off the broiler, she said if I had time to wait I said that was fine.  I watched the food prep guy make the two whoppers not from the broiler the manger noticed I was watching and told him it was supposed to be off the broiler so he put one of the two on the side and put a beef patty on the broiler. I said to myself that is ok no need to waste but that's when the unthinkable happened the guy making the second whopper off the  broiler wiped his nose he was sniffling with his gloved hand and continued to prep the food. that was the last draw I called the lady at the register waited first for the register area to have no one there and asked for a refund. She said why so I told her first the one whopper was not off the broiler and more importantly about the prep guy she said ok and called manger the manger then rolled her eyes at me with discontent so I called to her and told her she need not roll her eyes and talk to staff about hygiene and she walk away as I was talking. If I can give them zero stars I would.  It's a shame because they recently rebuilt the Resturant and is pretty nice. Thank u

Horribly managed.  I was killing time so observed operations for 830 - 9:30 am rush.  Confusion behind counter with very poorly-followed roles (by well-meaning eager staff including one who chats up every customer - oblivious to the impatient line) resulting in long wait to place order.  I order Turkey Sausage Flatbread w/o Hash Browns and black coffee.  I wait ... no order.  Eventually someone notices and begins to make my sandwich.  It is ultimately inedible.  Over-cooked to the point of soggy yet dry. A culinary miracle.  With Hash Browns!  Oh, lest I forget the green - brown 'cheese'.  And the coffee was old.  Worst ever Dunkins.

I watch many people come and go on their morning commute (only place I saw for miles).  Others quickly bail out seeing 20 ahead of them.

For the 4th time I ordered a Whopper with cheese! And no cheese? Even my jr burger with cheese had no cheese? And I paid for cheese? Avoid this store at all costs! I'm tired of getting F$$ked! If you have no cheese! Put up a sign! Publix is only 300 feet away, go buy some

Worst service ever!!!! I walked in--- watched three employees sitting and walking around in the lobby, one who literally bumped into me walking around as I stood at the counter to order. I witnessed numerous employees behind the counter talking, one on a cell phone and two others joking and laughing and one eating chicken right off the food warmer. A man finally greeted me stating he'd be right with me. 

He removed a glove, took my order then proceeded to get my food without a glove and cut open cuticles. I was highly disgusted and left promptly. 

Don't eat at this establishment! Had it not been for a pregnancy craving, I'd never have even stepped foot inside!

Went here and was told it was a 30 min wait for bone in chicken an hour and a half before close. Place was empty too. sorry but the chicken comes frozen and it sure as hell doesn't take 30 mins to fry. Maybe the employees should stop doing drugs in the back and actually do their job.

Food is decent enough, but my God this is a horrible location, in terms of people that go here.  The place is also run down.  Everything is pretty much filthy here, so if you must go to the drive-thru.  Also the prices are not that cheap, so if you need some good chicken check out Church's Chicken.

this place is ghetto unfabulous but when driving from north philly and you want chicken at 12 am, don't make the same mistake i did. the drive thru line takes forever speaking to its location only. i placed my chicken order and got taco bell in a kfc bag. probably someone in front of me got fed up with the wait and bolted throwing the line off, otherwise there are some lazy *** people working hear who cant follow a simple list system. I HUNGRY!!! awful tacos, if the person whose order i got at least ordered what i like from taco bell i would nt be so hungr but i wasted 15 minutes and 10$ here, these mashup KFC/TACOBELL/BASKIN ROBBINS...or whatver places at least need to stick to the proper wrappers...i wouldve known off the bat something was wring if they gave me a taco bell bag when i ordered KFC. The mad max, bullet resistant glass box makes it tough to communicate with the person who gave you food, especially if they are the one taking orders at the speaker station too.

please shut this place down and install a proper chicken joint who is staffed by people who are not smoking hat everyone else is on a friday night, at least until they get off work.  I think theres a checkers nearby, please (owners of checkers) stay open later than these people so you can provide proper service to the night owls in the mood for late night eats.

any other time of day and I would throw my money at popeyes, a half assed popeyes meal is worth more than the whole kfc ass. hell, ill even take churchs.  so disapointing to be dring home for 45 minutes and open the bag to find soft tacos and bean burrittos, i might as well have shoved them up my butt then pushed them out again so i could just cut out the middle man.

I haven't been to a Chipotle since this location originally opened about 7-8 years ago, and I had only been once or twice way back then. I received a coupon in the mail recently for a free item, so I decided to give them another try. 

Maybe I didn't ask for the right menu item. I asked for the burrito bowl with no beans or rice...just carnitas, fajita veggies, lettuce, sour cream and cheese. The three people who behind the counter who prepared my entree barely glanced up, and they rushed as quickly as they possibly could to get me through the line (it really wasn't that busy at all, I am not sure what the huge rush was about).

I am SOOO glad I didn't pay for that meal. It tasted great, but it was so small it was gone in fewer than ten bites. I thought it tasted comparable (maybe a little better than - or maybe I was hungry) Qdoba, but given these options I would choose Qdoba any time over this. The service is friendly and accommodating and the portions are four times greater.

Yote today...11/08/2016!!!
Breakfast... "coupon special-newspaper"...Clean, clean, clean(counters, floor, windows)!!!
* Bacon, Bologna or Sausage...Fresh egg...Melted cheese..."Slider"... w/Hash brown "Tots"...
  Sm. Coffee...$2.99 + tax!!!
* Had not been in one of these for some 60+- years(Minneapolis, mid-late 50's)...then .05/.10!!!
 -What a "trip"...back in time!!!

I have more coupons...I will return!!!

**Update**

So, it has been 2 years since the last time I visited this location and I'm happy to report that this most recent experience was much better. I'm happy that I can finally update this review.

First of all, the place was packed at about 9:15 am on a weekday. I didn't notice that the last time I was there. I went through the drive-thru again and it was much quicker this time around. I saw that they had many new breakfast items. Well, new to me because I haven't been there for a while. I got the Pork Chop & Gravy Biscuit with an order of hash browns. I was pleasantly surprised with the amount of meat in this sandwich, and the size of the biscuit. The country gravy was very good as well. They also didn't mess up my order this time. That's a huge plus.

The only negative is the awkwardness of the drive-thru. The speaker mic should be in a place where you can view the menu and speak at the same time. I had to repeat my order twice because of that. This speaker mic was situated past the menu and you had to pull up over an uneven driveway to get to it.  The driveway needs to be repaved or something. It was quite bumpy making that turn toward the delivery window. Other than that, the service was really good this time. Something has changed. I don't what it is, but I'll keep coming back just for that breakfast sandwich.

*****
I rarely give 1 or 2 star reviews on any site. I'd rather not write the review at all, but I felt it needed to be said. I recently ordered their country steak biscuit via their drive-thru service. This biscuit is one of my guilty pleasure favorites. I've had it at many locations in the past (including this one) and loved it. Unfortunately, I didn't get the opportunity to eat it. They gave me some other kind of breakfast sandwich. I didn't realize it until I drove all the way home. I was very disappointed. I was the only car at the drive-thru, and they couldn't get the order right. The only part of the order that was correct was the Blueberry Biscuit. It was an impulsive order that I made after seeing the pretty picture of it on the drive-thru menu. It was a microwaved mess when I got it home. The blueberry filling and the glaze were all shifted off the biscuit to one corner of the box. They must have nuked it too long. It looked nothing like the picture and didn't even taste that good. I don't think I'll be back to this location.
us.

For a place that is supposed to specialize in chicken... me thinks not! They are always out of things, and what they do have looks like a piece of cooked chicken with mange. It tastes worst than it looks.

Got a steak bowl to go. It was ok. Asked for a tortilla on the side and chips and queso. Got it back home and found the tortilla all balled up. The chips were stale. The staff could care less about any of the customers. 
And for those who may think I did something to deserve that bad service, I can tell you that I was as kind as I could be and there weren't many people in line. Maybe 3. 
I doubt I will go back.

Not a bad spot to stop off for a quick lunch. I'm a fan of the Famous Bowl and of course the chicken legs. This location usually isn't that busy, so you can count on getting true fast food. I've stopped by here a few times and have no complaints.

Hmmm. I feel bad giving this place one star... but I ordered a sausage egg and cheese croissant and found a hair... I also ordered the hash browns and was not provided utensils to eat them. I only saw straws and napkins but no utensils, a fork would have been nice. There is also no place to sit and eat, you'll just have to go to seating area near your gate. The French vanilla ice coffee I got was good though.

As bad as most KFC's are, the one's in da hood actually make really tasty food.  It's these one's in the suburbs that employ half-drunk teens that are really nasty.  I've gotten cold chicken, a bucket of thighs when I ordered a whole chicken.  "Well, we gave eight pieces..."  WTF?!?! and chicken that was not cooked.  I've also gotten busicuits that Atilla the Hun once threw out cause they were too evil.  I don't even see this place when I drive past it because my mind is so psychologically damaged from their lack of wits, it's just blocked out of consideration.

Always friendly service, food is fresh.... very clean on the inside. The drive thru maybe a tad slow but that's because they're cooking your food to order so be patient. Absolutely love there all star meals what a great deal!!!
Just tried the KFC original recipe chicken bites. Very tasty and fresh. Drive thru any KFC for your own yummy bites

Went through the drive-through here on Sunday night at 10 to 10 through the drive-through window with my husband only to find out that they were out of chicken( how is that possible?) . as we were pulling out of the drive through window we looked inside and saw all of the staff messing around joking and laughing. I'm pretty sure they turned us away because they wanted to close early!  I would not recommend this KFC to anyone

The BK on Frankford n linden is hands down the best. The Impossible whopper is ALWAYS on point. The workers are always super friendly. I'm always going late at night and it's never an issue. Love the late hours they are open. I never review fast food places bc ... well it's fast food... but this one deserves some credit!

Why is it that this particular bk chooses to be below standard?  I'm not a fast food nut but love the whopper!  The parking lot is a hazard because the owner obviously refuses to pay someone to blow off the fallen leaves, the bathroom grout it disgusting and the hot water that runs is lukewarm. Come on, McDonald's is right across the street, figure it out.  The food is secondary by the time I pick it up. If someone fixed me a five star meal in a one star kitchen I would question my digestive tract in the morning.

Download the iphone app and order ahead. Then take it to go, because this location is crazy. The app says that your order will take 30 minutes, but ours have taken 15 minutes tops, and once when we were there after only 10 minutes, they gave us a free drink! 

My only complaint with this location is once they forgot to put steak into a steak burrito we ordered beforehand. Like literally no steak. Oh well.

I love Chipotle and will continue to go here. You can't go wrong with their buy one, get one free coupons every few months!

Since we could not use any coupons, I decided to try the rave new Impossible Burger. It did look and taste like beef, although after a bite of my beau's double whopper, his real beef patties tasted better. Not sure if the double patty pushed it over the edge. Overall the Impossible Burger is an impressive feat. If you are trying to minimize red meat consumption and your friends or SO wants a flame-broiled burger (which BTW BK does right), then yes, this Impossible Burger could be a good option. Taste-wise, health issues aside the BK flame-broiled beef burgers are still the best of fast food burger joints.

We decided to run through the drive thru to pick up a snack to hold us over until later. There was 1 person in line nobody on the inside. Once we finally ordered and pulled around the other person was still in line . They had the other person pull up so we could pay. After about 8 minutes the lady handed us our food and we left. The other car was still there. As we drove up 86th street we devoured the fries (that was why they were given a star) the only other thing that was order was 2 sliders. When we opened the box all you could see were brunt little burgers.  When I say the burgers were brunt!  crispy edge brunt. 

It was so disgusting. As an employee I would have been embarrassed to serve anything like that. They knew it wasn't right when they closed the box.  

I will say that I will not be back. I can say I have always enjoyed Hardee's breakfast but I will not visit this location again.
How can you be out of chicken? Never will I use any KFC!  You really need to rethink your strategy! Maybe sell taco's

I've experienced this one too many times with this particular KFC. Tonight was the final straw. I ordered a 2 piece dark meat meal with macaroni & cheese and corn on the cob. My first disappointment was that apparently this KFC does not carry corn on the cob. That was okay. The menu said that they had regular corn. My second disappointment was that apparently they were out. Still okay. I'll get potato wedges instead. I was really looking forward to the red velvet cake that commercials have been advertising and that their menu said they carried. My third disappointment was that they were out of that too. Chocolate chip cake it is. With my order placed, my fourth disappointment was that they were out of chicken! Of all the things to run out of, how does KFC run out of chicken?!?! It would take 10-12 minutes so I decided to wait. After I pulled around & paid, I was told to pull up to the front and they would bring me my order when it was ready. About five minutes later, the employee came out and said that another employee had accidentally given away my mac & cheese...MY mac & cheese! And guess what? It was the last one! At this point I stopped counting how disappointing this visit was. Seeing what a fail this situation was, the employee offered to give me double potato wedges and some free cookies to try to make up for it. When she brought me my order out, she forgot my cake so had to go back in for it. She was very apologetic; I'll give her that. But this is one KFC I will never visit again and neither should you.

We have a newbie in the neighborhood and they named it Dunkin Donuts! Located in the beautifully remodeled Trevose Shopping Center, along with a brand new Fitness Center! Eat a donut and go work out afterwards. No problem.

Would you believe that I have 73 Dunkin Donuts within 10 miles of my home? Eight of them are within 3 miles. 

It is amazing to me how this company continues to grow in this economy, while other businesses are closing down, right and left. I think that we keep it alive, as consumers, for that ultimate love of a fresh donut or is it the busy life styles and breakfast & coffee on the run. It surely is a combination of both. 

Dunkin Donuts has come a long way, in order to compete with the Star Bucks and McDonald's of our world. 

They sell an endless array of Coffees and Teas, hot or iced to go along with those donuts. They have a line of Hot Chocolates now and the White Hot Chocolate is divine. If you have not tried the Peach Iced Tea, then you are missing out. 

They serve Breakfast "Omelet" Sandwiches, with Sausage or Bacon along with a side of Hash Brown bites. Their Muffins have a touch of Crumb & Icing goodness on top! I love the new Pumpkin Muffin and the Pumpkin donut isn't too shabby either! 

For Lunch grab an individual Pizza or a Flatbread Sandwich and I'm telling you now, the Ham & Swiss Cheese Flatbread Sandwich is pretty darn good!

For today's busy lifestyles and grub on the go Dunkin Donuts full fills those early morning blues or mid afternoon hunger pains.  Wake up America, it's Dunkin Donut time ;+}

I really don't know what is going on with Chipotle. When I first started going here around when they were somewhat new, they were so good. Now, I'm just not a fan. Really the only reason that I go here is if someone that I'm with suggests it. Now, there has been some buzz about Chipotle finally having queso. To be honest, I thought this would be their saving grace. This was until I tried it. The flavor was there, but the texture kind of felt like it may have been a little undercooked or it was made out of too much powdered cheese. It was a little gritty, which I thought was odd. The nacho cheese you get at a movie theater is better. It's not just this location, it's all Chipotle locations. I'll probably end up going back eventually because I really think their carnitas are delicious, but only if someone else suggests it.

Extremely long slow line at the drive-thru. Took several minutes just to get an answer at the order area. Unfriendly and no customer service whatsoever. In addition when I asked about Whopper Wednesday I was rudely told they don't honor it at this location. Terrible place probably won't go back.

Love love love the steak quesadillas and the hot salsa.  My mouth is always on fire when I leave but the unsweet ice tea tastes good and helps put the fire out.  You always feel like you are eating very quality and flavorful food here.  The music is at a nice level, unlike other Chipotle's that sometimes play the music very loud.

Well it's hard to review a KFC. I usually like the food, but the service is usually a drawback. I walked in to order dinner as I don't like going through drive thoughts as I often get missing items and wait forever. Overall the inside was one of the nicer KFCs I have seen. Note you can't eat in so it's to go only. I walked in and waited and waited with no acknowledgement. I sat on the one chair and waited some more called my wife to say we may be getting something else for dinner as I couldn't even place an order. Finally someone came to the counter to take my order. They did happen to have a really good deal to good to pass up. It was three pieces of chicken, three sides, dessert, biscuit, and a drink for 8.99. I was going to try the new spicy chicken sandwich but changed my mind when I saw that deal. The food was ready fast so that makes up for the wait to order. The food did taste great once I got home. But the new original spice fries were a bit stale not crunchy either soggy from the steam in the container or not fried in oil hot enough. I probably would have got mashed potatoes instead but was intrigued by the concept of using the original recipe spice on fries. Technically they were too salty for my liking. Chicken was great nice and juicy with the extra crispy breading. Great biscuit too. I probably won't be back unless I get a desperate need for fried chicken though.

This store has seen a real change in management and performance. Food is as good as Carls Jr can get. Every time I've visited in the past few weeks, everything ordered was fresh, hot and correct. The female manager in this store is dedicated to bringing this store up to standards and quite frankly has done so. Many new staff, always friendly, with a can I get you anything else attitude. 
  The sore is cleaned, mopped, tables are attended to ASAP after customers leave. Bathrooms are even well taken care of,,,yay! 

Side note,,, please try the Beyond Burgers,,,they are a great alternative to the cholesterol filled meats, both chicken and beef sandwiches. Just try it and quit your whining,,,,,,,,hahahahahah

Been wanting to try the new impossible burger and I prefer flame grilled over McDonalds so today is the day!  

This BK had a refurbishment and is a lot nicer now. It was lunch so there was a bit of a line but the lady at the register was awesome!  She was friendly but still moved everyone along. Top notch!  

Now to the burger. I read online about the nutrition and it appears there is very little difference between this and the regular other than cholesterol content. If true I am not sure why I would pick this over the other if I was not a vegan or non meat eater. 

As for the flavor it was pretty good!  The texture was ok but I could still taste that it was different. Not bad at all and if the calories were better I would probably choose this over the regular 

The store itself was clean and well managed and service was great.

I've been cooking every day and haven't had KFC in years. So, we decided to pick some up as a treat.
The 12 piece bucket was Below mediocre. 
My last KFC. Too bad cause it used to be great. Now, flabby, greasy chicken that wasn't very fresh. 
I actually felt bad that a chicken had to give  it's life to be treated so badly.  It's enough to make anyone a vegetarian.

 The Cole slaw. The coke slaw was terrific.  There. I said something nice!

I ordered a whopper with cheese and bacon and was given bread instead of buns. Why? I didn't order this, I wasn't told I'd be getting bread instead of buns. Unacceptable. No wonder their reviews are so low. Lost another customer tonight...

Oh my beloved DD, why art thou service sooooo slow?  This is my favorite go to for sugar free frozen coffee because they offer several sugar free flavored shots.  

I also enjoy their turkey sausage wraps with egg and cheese which is a great lite breakfast to quell any hunger pangs.  

It's just unfortunate that there aren't many more DD's around because their products are really good.  Sadly the two on the Westbank closed (for good reason) so many of us locals who love them are left to come here.  

On a positive note, business here has picked up tremendously for them during COVID-19, especially when they have more customers than their neighbor, Starbucks LOL but the service is extremely slow.  

Now, the DD in Laplace has faster service but doesn't offer almond milk in any frozen beverage, citing a peanut allergy...chile bye. 

I grew up with DD's and I just love their new and expanded menu options and now that many have a Baskin Robbins in them, I'm here more frequent, although I'm not a huge ice cream fan.  There's a large  ice cream selection in the photos below.  Check'em out!

This is the second time my dad and I have eaten at this particular Burger King. And it's the second time that we came home sick with horrible stomach cramps and diarrhea. The first time it was just my dad. The second time it was both of us .  This time, I got the impossible burger and dad got a Whopper jr. And both of us were sick. We are still sick.  Looking over the counter into the kitchen, the floor was dirty and covered in food debris, dead french fries, packets of  ketchup , and such. Stay away from this place.
Manager left one Whopper out of our drive-up order but evidently didn't feel it was worth an apology.  How hard is it to just say, "I'm sorry."
Twice I've tried to buy the new chicken sandwich combo, and twice they are out of chicken sandwiches! If an item is going to be the new "it" item, you may want to have it available.

During the Coronavirus period, this fast food chain did take good precautions on serving the customers. They have the food on the tray and delivery to you without contact. 

I got the strawberry Fanta ice. This is my first time has it. It only a dollar for the promotion. It is fun to have. I have never have the impossible whopper. They also have in on sale two for $6. The burger is really good. I cannot believe it is no meat. Way to go. It has all the grilled flavor. The chicken fried is my favorite. I got the meal deal with French fried with coupon for $3. My entire meal just $10. What a steal. 

The drive through is quick without a hitch. I would give them an A.

This was by far the worst Burger King I've been to. I got charged 12.09 for an impossible burger meal and that was a medium. On the receipt said LG and I told the guy I ask for a medium and he said that was because of the fries and the drink. What an F---ing rip off.

I just left this KFC located at 6417 Harbinson Ave in Philadelphia Pa without placing an order because the person taking my other at the drive through who claimed to be the manager refused to sell me a "16 piece all dark" bucket of chicken. She said that she "wasn't going to mess up her chicken combination just for me!" Ain't this some "stuff"... not what I really want to say! Selling a bucket of chicken is selling a bucket of chicken regardless of the combination. 

Why should I spend $30+ for parts of the chicken that no one in my family eats, if it was free I'll take what you give me but if I'm paying for it I should get what I want. I'm originally from Philly (New Jersey now) and used to live near and visit this location all the time and never had a problem until today. 

I will make a point to try to contact the owner of this location and see if their aware of this policy of not selling the customer what they're paying for, that it was a take it or leave it manager on duty today.

Super bad service every time I come to this location... EVERYTIME!!! Another thing this place never ever have everything u want never.. How do they always run out of GRILLED chicken... How do u run out???? Foods cool I mean it's Taco Bell and KFC.. But the workers act like the customers forced then to fill out an application and start working there.....

This location is very hit or miss. I've visited several times over the past year and have received the wrong or incorrect order on more than one occasion. The 2 stars are because of the customer service. Chipotle refunded my money in this case, as they delivered a chicken quesadilla when I ordered a sofritas bowl (vegetarian).

Went to here for my first meal visiting Tampa and the service was horrible unlike the service I'm use to in NY. Came in to order a rice bowl with chicken. When we got to the chicken part, the container was pretty much gone and only small scraps of chicken left over and clearly needed to be refilled but she continued to try to give me the run out shredded chicken. I said I don't want that and let me get steak. She said that it's at 10 min wait for the steak. I was in a time crick and said what!!! This is crazy you guys are suppose to have things ready and refilled. So proceed to wait and so of the other two people. Once the steak was finally chopped she then used my waiting rice bowl and asked another person what they wanted on MY rice bowl!!! I butted in to excuse the worker of her mistake. She then had the nerve to ask me "so u don't want it" nooooo you clearly put topping on my rice bowl that I did not want. So by her mistake she had to Throw out an whole rice bowl and do another one. These two girls was such in a rush for no reason that they messed up ppl orders and throwing away food. Didn't even say sorry for their mistake and just had an attitude. I will never go back to that chipotle !!!'

This DD is located on the busy Gulf to Bay but don't let that deter you from fast friendly service and decent reasonably priced food and caffeinated beverages. This DD was clean and had ample parking. As I've said in the past, I don't normally drink coffee but I would become a regular for Dunkin Donuts iced coffee. 

My go-to drink is the French Vanilla ice coffee with cream and sugar, plus a little more sugar. Add the croissant sausage egg and cheese sandwich, a French Culler and hash browns and I've got the makings of the best food coma breakfast. Sadly an order of the hash browns only comes with roughly 8 large coin nuggets but at $1 and some change I'm always up for ordering more. 

A medium iced coffee, breakfast sandwich, a donut and two orders of the hash browns totaled at $8.41. If they had DD in MN I would be there everyday.

I'm not a big fan of Chipotle but there are not a lot of Mexican choices in Doylestown. I used to go to Moe's until they closed. The food is ok here and the burritos are huge however I get the salad or a bowl. I used to go a lot when they first opened but got food poisoning and it took a long time before I went back. I even said after I never would but I've been going back. The chicken and steak or my favorite meat choices and I also like the barbacoa. The "new" queso isn't any better than the original one the texture isn't smooth like other queso's I've had. Also the last 3 times the chips have been stale and the guacamole never looks fresh and has brown in it. I've never had a problem with service so this review is just based on food.
Taco bell wins absolutely terrible this is steak i guess they call it with lettuce tomatoes and cheese i guess they thought tomato was a bad idea today and didn't put it on

What happened? I use to visit Chipotle on the regular while attending USF for a healthier "fast food" option and loved everything they offered. Today I picked up a veggie bowl for myself and a steak burrito for my boyfriend and we both agreed the food just didn't have any flavor, definitely not like I remember it having. I ended up adding salt and pepper to my bowl which didn't really help much. 

Besides the change in flavor I still love the idea of Chipotle using real ingredients that you can pronounce as well as attempting sustainable environmental practices. The service is also always friendly and fast. Hopefully the next time I visit the taste will be back to what got me hooked in the first place.

Went to Burger King 3/10/2021 10:35pm. Ordered a #6 Whopper jr , with extra onions and heavy Mayo with onion rings and a coke. Also wanted a 2nd Whopper Jr the same way. I paid $8.46!
I received my meal and drove away back home near largo mall. Once I pulled in my driveway I noticed my Whopper jr was nothing more then a cheeseburger with Mayo only. Both sandwiches were the same.
