I knew it was a gimmick but was still willing to try it. Worst tasting Halloween whopper, it lacked the taste of meat and other normal condiments. The thing was large and flat, and had a black bun. The black bun turns your stool green, which is really disturbing! The place was also run down and out dated.

I came. I ordered. I ate. And I'm so happy!! No line. Pleasant attitude. My food was prepared EXACTLY the way I wanted(whopper meal with cheese & everything on it, extra mayo. My food was ready before we finished getting our drinks. Everyone was really pleasant & my food tasted like somebody put Love in it!!!! Yummy!!!! HIGH FIVE!!!!

Okay I decided to try this place. I must say the 1/3 cheeseburger is actually pretty dam good.I like the charbroiled taste and the meat is really thick.I was really surprised on how good it was for fast food.The french fries on the other hand need a lot of work.I left all the fries.It had a weird grease taste.But the star is the Burger.

I always get the same thing which I consider excellent. Three chicken soft tacos, medium fries and large cherry coke. I open the taco squirt in a Del Scorcho sauce add fries wrap and mow down on some nom nom's! It's not a 4 star restaurant so don't expect perfection. They do a wonderful job at what they do. If they had better service it would be nice.
I ordered two meals and an additional whopper with cheese and did not get the additional whopper. No phone number to call!

First, let me say, I'm a die-hard Qdoba fan. I had Chipotle from the Baltimore Harbor location about 2 years ago, and it was 100% forgettable. I mean, I went back to Taco Bell just to get the memory out of my head. Ever since, I have spent my days questioning the judgement and deleting the numbers of all who rave about this "Chipotle."

This week, though, I was at the Plymouth Meeting Mall and decided to give Chipotle another try.

It was delicious. Not five-star delicious, but yeah, I think I'm a fan.

The super-nice, heavily-tatted guy (who looks just like their doodled paper bags, in my opinion) made me the yummiest burrito ever. I couldn't decide between the carnitas and the barbacoa, so he gave me half and half - at separate ends so I could taste them distinctly - and I am now a carnitas fan.

I believe in second chances where food is concerned and this is a great example of why.

I did find myself at the register seeking a brownie for dessert, though.
Decided to give this place another chance, BAD IDEA !!! Taco shells were stale, food COLD.....threw it all out. Will never return.

This place went downhill because they started burning the steak, the steak used to be red and juicy now its charred and kind of bland. They also have a revolving door of employees. I see new employees every time I visit, and when I order a double steak burrito the beginnners get it ripped sometimes.

I wish corporate America would pay their employees better so they would stick around longer and know what they are doing. Also steak should never be overcooked. it should always be a little pink and not charred. 

Im still going to go to this Chipoltle but maybe I should try the new one in KOP.

Good value on the buffet and have never had a complaint about the chicken , it may be greasy to some but hey , this is fried chicken. If you want flavorless dry chicken go to McDonald's and have some McNuggets. Only issue I have had is going in after lunch rush is over , food on buffet is pretty much depleted , especially the chicken , but they will put out whatever you request. Service as it is , i.e. take order , ring out , is usually fine however yesterday they seemed a bit off , to the point of being rude , trying to get someone to take my $ so i could help myself to buffet. Was right after lunch rush and you could tell , all tables filthy , food almost gone , etc. But they were replenishing and cleaning as i sat there and ate. Btw , the interior of the restaurant is clearly designed like many are nowadays for you to want to finish your meal and leave as quickly as possible. Comfort and ambience will not be found at KFC.  As a side note I enjoy KFC chicken very much and have grown up with it like many of my generation , however if I had never tried lt before their new commercials would have me driving right by without ever stopping. Who are they trying to appeal to with that silliness?
Just my 2 cents.

Savory, delicious, great value, and fast (usually).

This Chipotle has a convenient location in Center City if you live in the area, but I imagine it's a real pain to find parking.  Delicious food, however the setup of space (can get very crowded) and noise level (usually on the louder range) make it not the ideal place to dine in.  I usually just grab and go.

Rundown:
Burrito bowl to go with tortilla on side
Mix brown and white rice
Extra rice instead of beans
1/2 steak and 1/2 chicken
2 scoops pico
2 scoops corn
Lettuce on top
Cheese in a cup

Outcome:
Slide that 1lb bad boy into a bag, grab some napkins and 2 forks, I'm usually good for two meals.

This meal was the single most item I craved when I was in Africa for 3 weeks.  Either very sad...or just very awesome food...maybe a little of both.

My first ever visit to Chipotle,  and eh not that impressed. Customer service was top notch but the food was just ok. Got a Sofritos Burrito with the fajita mix, black beans, corn, salsa,  and sour cream. Just kinda bland. I'll definitely try again, based solely on the popularity, the customer service and the cleanliness of the place.

I love Chipotle. Chipotle is my comfort food as their burritos got me through college adequately fed (and easy on the pockets). This is your typical Chipotle as far as design. Came here for dinner (8pm) on Saturday and it wasn't packed at all. Greeted immediately by the staff and they were all quite friendly. Ordered my typical carnitas and chicken burrito bowl. The server gave good portions and didn't skimp on the meat which can happen at other Chipotles. 

I wish I could give 4.5 stars. 0.5 star deduction for size of the parking lot and the ability to access the restaurant (can't make a left into the restaurant if you're on the opposite side of the road).

So far every time I've gone to this restaurant I've gotten pretty good portions and the food has been good.  I don't see a lot of turnover in the staff so I see the same faces often.  The amount of food I get every time is usually consistent.  The staff is quick except if someone comes in with a large order all the staff processes the order instead of allowing other customers to move through the line while only one or two people work on the large order.

The steak is usually very grisly so I've switched to carnitas.  Get the bowl & save yourself some calories by skipping the tortilla.
Freshly prepared my eye. This store is becoming a joke. Process are hugely out of line. Thank God for better chicken restaurants. We won't be back.

I wonder how many people in the city got a buy one get one free coupon from Chipotle like I have, the place was packed. Luckily I got a table in the former lofty Susanna Foo space and while I had to order lunch, my companion kept watch on the table. 

The space is pretty nice, clean and brand spanking new with great lighting. I've never been to a Chipotle since most of them are in the burbs and I don't drive. So I've ordered two burritos, one barbacoa and one steak. Frankly I didn't know what barbacoa was as it didn't state it on the menu, I know it means barbecue but I don't know whether it was beef or pork, turns out, it's beef. 

At the assembly bar, the server steamed the tortilla slightly and put the  sauce, meat and rice and beans on top, then it's passed on to another person to put on sour cream, cheese, salsa and lettuce. You can pay extra for some guacamole. The end product is huge. I can hardly finish one and I'm a big guy.

I have to say though that the sour cream mellow out a lot of flavor, so the various available Tobasco sauces were welcomed. I love the steak cubes even though there's not much of it, it provided a chewy bite that provides variety to the other textures. The corn salsa also added some sweetness. I would like the whole thing to be grilled to a crisp and I would also have welcomed a steak sauce but it'd be quite unauthentic. 

With a drink, it'll be about $10 a person which is reasonable. Again, the whole burrito is quite filling, so it's possible that you'd share one of this with a friend. They also have bowls, taco (x3), salad and chips and salsa available. If they could salt the meat a little bit more to add some flavor, it might be better but I surely don't mind it, especially with a free coupon. Nothing wins my heart over faster than free food. 

Is anybody else appalled that a tortilla is 200-300 calories? That's the downsize of listing the calorie values of each ingredient isn't it?

WARNING: The following review is extremely biased. Parental guidance is advised.

I'm pretty sure the name White Castle needs no introduction, so I'll get right down to it: I have never had a taste of White Castle. Not really. Yeah they do sell the same delicacy in supermarket frozen food section, but when it's made fresh right in front of you on that grill skillet it's just not the same at all. The meat still retain a lot of its juice (or grease however you want to call it.) The diced unions truly as described in the movie "little flavor crystal that just burst in your mouth." Be warned, if you are the kind that does not like your bun mushy, you might be a little disappointed, because those little slider roll/bun just soak up all the meat juice, and doubly so if you ordered the double cheeseburger combo.

So glad I walked in as I did, and not dilly dolly outside trying to take the pictures or checking in, because they locked the door behind me as soon as I walked in. Apparently dine-in stopped at 2:30 AM. There are certainly some colorful population coming through that front door in late hours of the day. Good food and great entertainment, what else can I possibly ask for?

Walking in the restaurant was clean and well tended too.  I ordered a Whopper meal and 10-piece chicken nugget.  The girl taking my order was extremely nice and courteous. In less than 5 minutes I had my order and sat down to snap a photo.  The sandwich was smashed, missing ketchup and had very little of anything on it.  I only got 9 of the 10 nuggets I ordered, I guess counting isn't a required skill here and they must have been in the warmer for awhile as they were not very warm and hard.  I do love Burger King as a fast food staple, but this location has some work to do as the food here is not anywhere near corporate standards.  Hoping the manager at this location turns it around.

As usual, enjoy chipotle as much as everyone else. Fast service. 
Once I went and the rice was really undercooked which ruined it as I always get it to go so I was at work and disappointed but that only happened once. Otherwise very happy with chipotle. I always get a sofritas bowl, yum

Chipotle is a burrito place in the Plymouth Meeting mall. It's similar to a lot of the other burrito places that have been popping up lately, but I think Chipotle was one of the first to open this kind of place, or is at least one of the most popular.

I find the burritos to be very similar to Qdoba in style, which is good. They offer the usual assortment of meats (chicken, steak, shredded pork), with one notable addition: barbacoa. It's seasoned shredded beef, and boy is it delicious. I'm a big fan of shredded beef and wish more places had it. You should definitely give it a try.

They make the burritos in the now-typical assembly line style where they ask you what sorts of things you'd like to put in it. They have four kinds of salsa (the hot salsa is very good). They don't have a whole lot on the menu, which is nice because it makes it simple to order and fast to get your food.

The place is reasonably nice inside, with a spartan stainless steel motif that I think looks pretty cool. It's always been clean when I've been in there.

My only complaint is more of a complaint about the mall itself: the parking sucks now. They used up a bunch of the parking lot space on restaurants and now it seems like I always have to park a half-mile away. It's also somewhat maze-like in layout. I realize that construction is still underway at the mall, so I'm really hoping they have a plan to address this parking problem it's only going to get worse as they open more of the new businesses.

Anyway, the food here is really good and it has quickly gained on Qdoba as my favorite burrito place in the area.

My brother swears by Chipotle's quality and flavors.  I've experienced much better.

I tried the Sofritas bowl yesterday, as I didn't see any veggies in the bin, and as I needed something with protein.  My first disappointment was with the proportions.  A decent amount of rice, about four black beans, a TON of Sofritas, a bit of pico de gallo, and a bit of cheese.  Understandably, when I started eating, I noticed how overpowering the Sofritas were in the dish.  It tasted like a bowl of salt.  I kept trying to get more and more rice on the fork to ease the salty taste.  

Perhaps the new American palate is all about salt, but as much as I love it, I think it needs to be toned down.

I had a hankering for Chipotle during field work one weekend.  Since I've only been to Chipotle (not this one) once before, I had to go.

It's tough to find this once since it's nestled in a corner next to a mall entrance and behind PF Chang's and some other restaurant.  We circled a couple times until I called the place.

This time, I ordered a fajita burrito with carnitas and the corn salsa.  As expected, it was really delicious.  Perfectly topped with chipotle Tabasco (I love that chipotle flavor, I'm hooked).  Definitely brightened my day after a long morning/afternoon of work.

Chipotle is officially my go to Mexican "fast food" chain.  I never got into Baja Fresh like some people I know did.  My experience at Qdoba wasn't bad too, but Chipotle, you're #1 in my book.

It's been a few years since I've been to KFC. I live next to a KFC back home and always pass it on the way to and from work. I happened to be visiting my parents in Boise and felt like finally going to KFC, so I came to this branch.

I was surprised by how nice the inside was. It was extremely spacious and clean. I ended up buying an eight piece original bucket. They mentioned it would be a few minutes, I didn't mind. The staff was very polite and apologized for the wait. 

When I got back home I was delighted with how fresh and delicious the chicken was. I can't believe I don't go to KFC more often. I'm definitely coming back here when I'm visiting Boise and when I get back home I plan to visit KFC more often.

So, I hadn't had kfc in years and was really looking forward to having it for dinner. We went through the drive thru and placed a order for two Double Down sandwiches and was told that it would take 10 minutes to make- in which we told the girl that was no problem. She then proceeded to sigh/grunt as if we were asking her to breed, kill, and pluck the chicken before cooking it. We pulled up to pay and she was even more disrespectful.  Snatching money and having a shitty tone. Seriously?  I work in customer service and I would truly hope that the management here  would hold their employees to higher standards than this because this experience was definitely disappointing.

I am only giving this location ONE star because I cannot give it ZERO STARS! I really wanted some chicken. I went to this location on a slow, rainy Sunday afternoon. There were maybe 3-5 people total in store. There were people in drive thru. Not very busy at all. Floor was slick from rain and slippery. Gentleman behind counter may have been new. He radioed over headset to ask if he could take my $20 bill. I asked the difference between the #2 (breast 2pc combo), & the #10 (all dark meat combo). Neither of which on "$5 Fill Up" menu. I ordered the #2 sub mash for fries add sm. mac/cheese. I noticed on receipt as I sat waiting for my order that I was rang up wrong with a $5 menu add Mac. I went to talk about it and he "fixed" my problem by just giving me the chicken I ordered no change on receipt. The chicken skin was visibly old and see through from grease like cellophane. The box had mashed and gravy. So, I said I wanted a refund. That made twice the order was wrong and the food looked disgusting. I didn't want to eat there. He tried to offer fresh but the wait was too long. Manager that did refund was running drive thru. Acted as if did not want to refund. Gave me incorrect change. Brought it to her attention. She got rude and refused to look at the receipt or hear my complaint. I laid all change on counter and explained I walked in with a twenty, told her the problems, COUNTED MY CHANGE, and told her she still owed me money. I told her I walked in with a $20 and I was not walking out with $18.94. She asked what she still owed me. I said what is 20 minus 18.94?!? Look at the receipt like I asked you to and you would KNOW! She offered to call police. Mind you this location has recently been in news for someone shooting someone else in self defense... meanwhile, the gentleman in line behind me had come in and is hearing this. His complaint; he went through drive thru and didn't get change. I told her she makes extra money skimming people. Someone should shut this place down or seriously look into it. I WILL NEVER GO TO KFC AGAIN!!! I went the extra distance to go to Popeyes up the street. Had a better meal for about same price, a tv, fresh food as I was ordering, friendly staff, and even pies were fresh with about 4min wait. I know where my money will go from now on.

I always order chicken soft tacos at Del Taco, I love the perfectly seasoned chicken and the sauce.  As most of us know as with any chain it can very from place to place.  This Del taco is the most consistent. Here is how  ordered they jet your order right, management is great, staff is happy and friendly and on most days they relay are fast.  This in my opinion is the best Del Taco in town.

I used to really like this place for takeout but at some point they must have changed management or something .
I usually get the 4 piece and fries .
The last 2 times the chicken was very greasy and tasted a little odd. Maybe the oil was at fault.
Service in the takeout window is incredibly slow and there is no way to get out of line once you're in so i almost missed work .
The people at the window couldn't understand english much as well i think and i had to repeat a few times to get my drink right. 
KFC as an organization has really gone downhill .I went to another outlet a few years ago and the chicken pieces were very small and bland.
I'm an old timer and when i first used to  buy kfc, maybe 40 yrs ago, when you bought a 4 piece meal you always got big pieces of chicken,fries,a buttered roll,gravy and a salad.
They even gave you little tub things of honey for the chicken.
How the mighty have fallen.
The whopper has become nothing more than a salad between two buns with a thin wafer of meat. This the LAST place you should go to for a burger.

Things seem to have improved a bit since my former two star review of this location. I stopped in on a Friday evening to get dinner for my husband and myself and used the drive thru. I noticed that the flowers planted near the speaker I had mentioned before, (which attracted yellow jackets and bees and wasps), appear to have been replaced by a different type of groundcover. The plants were not in bloom however, so I'm not certain if the issue of attracting the flying stinging things has been fixed. 
My order was taken by a young woman with a very clear voice versus the male with a thick accent I had previously encountered. I made my way to the window, and found a gentleman there who may have been the manager, he did have a bit of an accent but it was not thick, and obviously he was not the one taking orders from the speaker. 
You always have to wait a little bit for your order at Carl's Jr. it's because they make the food to order. The gentleman and myself engage in some very pleasant banter and I truly felt that he was taking an interest in me as a customer. Another issue that I had previously addressed appears to have been resolved- when our order was handed out it was not crammed into a bag that was not big enough, this was a nice sized heavy duty paper bag, that certainly left enough room for me to fold down the top to keep things hot after I checked the order. In previous visits it had been impossible to keep even a modicum of heat in the french fries during the trip home. Granted that you expect things to cool off a bit when you get food to go, but before I could never expect for there to be any warms left in the fries by the time I got home.
I am not certain that my previous review is what led to some of these changes, though I like to think that someone is keeping an eye on yelp for that business. I do have to say that this was a very satisfactory visit, and well I'm not about to jump in and give them four stars, I will add a star and say that this place is A-OK. In terms of chain restaurants, my husband and I really do enjoy Carl's Jr., they're a bit more pricey than some of the others and you do have to wait for your food, but when it's done correctly it is worth every minute and every penny spent

This is a good place to eat. I have almost gotten a great burrito. The last time the steak was tough, but that is not usual.

Good flavor and good portion size.

Chipotle is a staple in my diet.  I hit the Cherry Hill location literally 4 times a week.

I'm just trying to spread the Chipotle stoke.  I'm re-copying some of my Chipotle "lessons learned" from my Cherry Hill review here.  

1.  Don't fear the salad
--It's essentially a bowl with some lettuce on the bottom.  I throw a little white rice on.

2.  Skip the dressing
--It's unnecessary (and it's made from crappy oils) -- just get all three salsas and you are golden.

3.  Barbacoa is really dependable
--It's also the only meat on the menu that is not cooked with soybean oil (soybean oil = not desirable).  I get the steak when it looks fresh off the grill.

4.  Guac rules

5.  Get shredded lettuce on top of the guac -- keeps it from sticking to the lid.

6.  Skip the soda, pimp the lemons
--I ask for a cup for water -- squeeze three lemons in and fill with water.  Lemon aid, for free.

Honestly, the Cherry Hill location gives this one a run for its money.  This location doesn't quite hold up in terms of service and quality.  But, by all means, it's still an extremely solid Paleo / gluten free option for under 10 bucks.  Rip it.

This is a BK with no drive thru, but when I went there they other day they had renovation plans on the wall showing they will be installing a drive thru. This is great because this BK really needed a facelift. The service here is good, and the only bad experience I witnessed was when my friend got a veggie whopper with no patty. I will not deduct a star for that because it was funny to see him eat what was left.

This restaurant always provides excellent service.  There's never mistakes on orders and the employees will do their best to make sure the customers are happy.  And the new boneless chicken is addictive.

Godawful. 
Ordered the $20 fill up with crispy tenders, and received an uninspired bucket of original recipes chicken breasts. No drumsticks. Just a fuckton of bones. Chicken was comically dry. Didn't taste a single signature herb or spice. Employees also neglected to throw in any sauce whatsoever. 

I can see why KFC franchises have been disappearing in the value. Absolutely abysmal.

I ordered a sausage, egg and cheese bagel....I got a bagel smothered in butter.  When I looked in the bag it didn't look like what I ordered so I asked if it was a sausage egg and cheese bagel.  I was told yes.  After walking to work I sit down and find this disgusting bagel.   It wasn't even that busy.
One of the best KFC's around. The chicken was hot fresh and perfectly cooked. Just in case you were wondering.

Not very impressive with the beef patty here. The beef inside the Whopper tasted like plastic. I have not been to Burger King for a long time  and just passed this place as there was not much choice around here. The taste seemed to become much worse than what's in my memory. I guess the cost cutting reduced the quality of the meat. The beef patty looks cheap and smell bad also. 

The service was friendly though. Just the food is not up to par.

The reason I come here is due to the fact the food is worth the wait comparing them to any fast food is just stupid.the places where you get a meal in less than two minutes have poor quality and make many more mistakes than you guys I've never gotten a drink where the lid wasn't on all the way thanks but please bring back blue cheese crumbles and change my review to a five star rating

Love this place! I've had the steak and the chicken burrito so far and both were excellent. Extremely filling and well worth the price in my opinion. I've yet to go there when the line wasn't out the door but that's because it so freakin good!

All the bad reviews are absolutely true.  Go elsewhere.  I've been craving KFC for weeks now and finally decided to stop by, sorry I did.  Wanted an original recipe breast meal, they didn't have any original chicken so I had to wait forever and then my order was still wrong (and who the hell gets grilled chicken at KFC?  Seriously, stop that).  This location doesn't have potato wedges, they have fries.  I ordered some for my son but what I got instead was fried okra.  Kidding of course, they don't have okra.  But why were these fries slimy in the inside?

Just passed this one by I got a five dollar fill up  crispy chicken sandwich. The fries or potato wedges look like in tasted like they had been bathed in grease twice. No cookie with my order. Stars were for friendly service only

I love burgers, and will always stop to try a new one. Carl's Jr. has a solid burger. I walked up and asked them "What's your biggest burger?" And it's the Angus thick burger combo, with fries and a drink it was roughly $12. 

One interesting they asked me when I wanted the combo if I wanted large or medium. Duhr? I guess that's their way of asking if you want to supersize it.

After ordering you can head to your table with a number , dairy queen style and they'll bring you your order.

I got my burger combo under 5 minutes and it was a tasty burger, and the toppings were all really fresh tasting. Fries nice and hot, and the mammoth drink could give diabetes to a trunk full of circus clowns. Seriously share the drink if you're getting a large.

The burger itself wasn't the biggest burger/fries I've ever had 5 Guys is bigger.

What was the chain like overall?
If Wendy's and Dairy Queen had a lesbian relationship and raised a child it would be Carl's Jr. 

Worth eating here, but not worth driving out of your to come here. 

A solid 3 star : A-ok
I have been to this one numerous times. It's safe to say that 85 percent of the time, they are going to mess up your order. I last ordered chicken nuggets and got a whopper with a kids toy.

Ran out of chicken and steak at 6:15 pm. Come on. Then I waited 20 minutes for chicken and it was never flipped or moved on the grill and I still got a few suspect pink pieces.  The lettuce was funky too. Where's the feedback survey on the receipt?   Very dissatisfied.  Bummer because I've never had issues at other locations. 
Guacamole was good. Not sure what's going on here.
The wind is blowing like 60 so it's a good day to stop by Del taco and use my coupon that came in the newspaper.

Don't ask for steak in this location, I am eating a steak burrito but I haven't had a single piece of real steak, it all has been fat, soft and hard pieces of fat together. This is the worst quality of steak I've ever had and I eat chipotle very often!

Absolutely love their iced coffee and cold Brews!! Much tastier than Starbucks and much more affordable!! This location, like others is friendly, fast, and accurate. There sausage egg and cheese sandwich on a plain bagel is awesome! And their pumpkin spice is to die for! Also, if you need a sugar fix, their Reese's peanut butter square is really good too!

The service was great, very fast and friendly too. All of their steak looked red to me so I passed on what I normally get to have a chicken burrito instead. This was the first chicken burrito I've ever had from chipotle that tasted bland. I think they forgot to season it or something like that. I would give them a second chance because of the service and the location was very clean. I think I just got unlucky this one time.

Update: I have come back many times and the food always has tasted great since. The staff for the most part are really nice too. Online orders are inconsistent sometimes though, I order online here a lot and about every third visit they will forget one of my toppings. The online service is new so they are probably working out the kinks right now.

I love Chipotle!!! Only one star because the food is not fresh at the one on stroke rd. Last month, I went there at night and grabbed steak bowl with everything topping except guacamole. I got bad stomach after finishing the meal. I couldn't stop shitting!!!!! Maybe the sour cream or steak went bad. This was an awful experience.

Was not as good as I remembered as a kid.  KFC was always my favorite growing up.  I always remembered how excited I was when our mom said "I'm going to KFC to bring home chicken".  My kids took me here for fathers day and food took forever to come, chicken was pretty dry and service was meh.  Had to wipe our own table so we had a place to eat.  I was kind of sad they took me there.  I'm pretty sure I will never return to this one.  KFC I love you but you have 1 more shot

This chipotle is conveniently located in front of Westshore mall. I Stopped by with my fiancé for a sofritas salad bowl and a chicken salad bowl. Wowza... thats the biggest serving i have ever received... i had to take the rest to-go. In terms of cleanliness, serving size and service speed i give it a 4 out of 5. Although it was prime time, it was pretty speedy service. I was dreading making the line but they actually served me faster than i expected. Kuddos! I read in an older review that sometimes if you order sofritas you might have cross contamination with other meats which worried me, but i didn't have to deal with that....so it's all good. I'm definetly returning here!!

Update:

They've fixed this issue and moved sofritas away from the other meats, I've experienced less contamination issues and I applaud  them for their efforts.

This Chipotle is arguably the busiest in the area, by far, expect to get in-line at this location. I don't know if they have a separate online order register, but this location is always busy no matter what.

The parking lot is small and a lot of people tend to park on the street or next door near the gas station or across the street to come here. This location is the closest to South Tampa and between two malls, so there's a ton of traffic that comes by this location and I feel like that the restaurant itself wasn't properly planned to handle this amount of traffic.

That being said, I love chipotle and I upgraded my review to 4 stars.

Walked in Sunday evening, used restroom (super clean).  We were greater by Ely, who promptly took our order. Chris was cooking and I formed us it would be a minute. Great experience.  Very friendly staff. Can't wait to eat our chicken!!

Walked in and practically right back out. Stinks like rotting meat filthy front door and floors and disorganized. Really gross. In California this chain is high end immaculate and friendly as all get out. WTH??

BTW the sofritas are excellent (in California). Just couldn't stand this location.
My friend got two steak burritos. The service was good and quick. The venue is really nice inside, and pretty impressive compared to its competitor Qdoba.

Ok I decided to give them another chance because they are the closest chipotle, dispite the fact that in other ocasions they were out of certain items (today they were out of carnitas and fajita veggies). I agree with Sammy from a past review, this is the worst chipotle I have been to in tampa bay. This place was practically empty and the survice was sub par. After I ordered my sofritas salad, i was left to wait 4 min before someone would come to charge us for it. Other employees in the back were talking on the phone. Only the girl who first attended me kept working and restocking the food line. The girl kept calling out for help (she was serving but needed someone else to charge us). After charging us, the guy poceeded to push the bag towards us. My fiancé jokes that this was the employees way of telling us "you can leave now". He is however not joking when he says he does not want to return here. I really had high hopes they'd improve... come on Chipotle Countryside....step it up...you are the closest to where I live !!

This is absolutely our go to spot for cheap eats. If you can't decide on which meat you want, they let you do half/half. I usually do half chicken and steak and they're both awesome. Just simple and good fast food.
Yummy steak burrito but too big to finish! Very nice service. They tore the tortilla when wrapping the burrito and gave us free chips and salsa to make up for it.

The best two things about Chipotle (these apply to every location):

1) picky eaters paradise! Don't like guac, etc? Don't get it. Much easier than ordering a burrito without guac, etc
2) the commitment to semi sustainable food (organic, locally sources when possible, etc)
3) jalapeno corn salsa

That's three, but you get the idea. Authentic Mexican this is not, but tasty food it is. I always get the barbacoa because I don't like the carnitas here, the other meats always seem dry, and I've had more than my fair share of dark meat in my chicken burrito, no bueno. Cheaper and closer to home than Freebirds, if Freebirds had this corn salsa I'd probably skip Chipotle. Can't get enough corn salsa!

Ok so the wife and I go here almost every Friday night but it's been really bad last few times so it's been a month or so. Did our weekly shopping and just felt like giving this del taco another try. It was about 10:10pm they are open til 11pm for sit down eating but the doors were locked. Called them to let them know and of course no answer. So they saw us but ignored us so I went to the drive in window. There were about 6-7 cars in line and they opened the window as I told the tall skinny guy who was in charge the doors were locked he told me ya I locked them. He said he couldn't keep up with the drive through and inside at the same time. He was rude and had 6 people inside working that I saw which 3 were just standing around this whole time. I told him I would call his boss and he said go for it his name is mike. So do yourself a favor and stay far away from this one. It was good a year ago but all the new people have no clue what they are doing. Food is made bad and wrong 8 out of 10 times and the wife and I are done with this place. Love Del Taco but this one is so far gone I don't think they will ever be better. Just on a downward slide..... So sad!!

I am truly not a fan of KFC anymore. I used to love their chicken when I was younger and now it seems a bit soggy.

I do like the $5 fill up box. I usually get the chicken strips, 3, with wedges, a biscuit, a cookie, and a raspberry tea.  It truly is a full meal in the box. 

I know I'll be back.

Burger King has recently really upped their game . The food and service are very good here now . The people on the drive-thru window are very nice . My Whopper was the best I have had in years . Thanks !

One of my favorite chipotle places! The area is clean and employees are helpful. The chicken and steak are always cooked perfectly here. The only downside is that it can get busy at times and they will run out of some items for a short period 

Pro tip: ask for 3 free small tortillas with your bowl and you can make your own tacos

I don't get anything, but a #9 pot pie. For $5 with a cookie and drink. It is one of the best pot pies, it's smaller than Boston Market, but cheaper and you get a drink.  
I ask for super golden crust and it is perfectly toasty. 
The last time we got chicken it was not very seasoned.

I love chipotle, and I've gone to this location probably dozens of times. When I went today, it was genuinely awful. The customer service was terrible. My friend and i stood there for over a minute until someone put gloves on to get us started.  Every single employee mumbled and had a bad attitude. My friend had pointed out the large catering order they had and that is no excuse. I work in a fast food place very similar to chipotle and it is not hard to speak up and put a smile on your face.  That aside i had ordered a bowl with sofritas and veggies. , and two bites in a pulled out a chunk of steak. Luckily i am not actually vegan so I wasn't super mad about it. But that is still a serious problem. Totally unhappy and extremely unimpressed.

Sadly, the negative reviews written this year don't surprise me. This location is the most convenient for us (close to our hotel & the airport) but it has gone down-hill. We last visited in 2014 and it was much better. This time the patties were kind of mealy & gray & the buns, over-steamed. The sandwich collapsed quickly. The promotional Apocalyptic Waffle Slider was just OK. The staff has ZERO personality & are border-line unfriendly. We come out from CA where I dream about going to a real White Castle. After this experience the boxed version in the freezer section of the grocery store is a better fix. I'd also be sure to smile when I hand it to myself.

This past weekend I was in the area and saw the sign, buy a whopper get one free! I am not cheap however I do like to eat and feel guilty when I buy something for myself and not my husband so this was the perfect deal for me! It was only on Friday but then I think saturday they had free fries or something- always doing something it seems. And the Whoppers fully loaded for 3.49 were a steal! Highly recommend this location even though it can be a pain to get into the lot!

I attended this KFC at 8:18 PM with my good friend. You guessed it, this is one of those weird KFC/A&W joints. I ordered a 3 tender fill up box. I don't know what he ordered but it was those chicken little sandos. He recommended them, but I went my own way. 

We cruised the drive thru, ordered from there. The order was taken well, considering we requested 2 different tickets. After she put us on hold she was on point. We received our receipts quick and then was told to wait out front for our food. Apparently several reviewers had to wait out front for their orders. We did not wait too long, but enough time to chat with my good friend. 

My fill up box included 3 tenders, mashed potatoes, biscuit, cookie, and drink (A&W Root beer).  The tenders were oddly shaped and probably unreal in terms of quality. The biscuit was ok, but dry. The mash and gravy was funny, playful. My good friend who I went with, mentioned the cookie "almost tasted homemade." The BBQ sauce kinda sucked, but we didn't agree on that either. 

Minus 1 star for waiting. Minus 1 star for food. But what can you expect from a KFC/A&W? My total was $5.30. 

It was cool hanging with my good friend.

March 17, 2012 Saturday - lunch 12:15 PM
1 adult & a kid (not a goat but a human child)

Atmosphere - 5 stars
Food - 5 stars
Service - 5 stars

Background - Had to take my little girl to have some free French fries and green ketchup.

Atmosphere - Before you walk in you can not help but notice the free Wi-Fi sticker on the window, gotta love that.  The place has probably been open for less than three months.  One corner had a little casual spot with some nice big comfy looking chairs, get them hooked in with that free Wi-Fi and coffee.  Menu board is a series of televisions, got to love the modern feel of the place.  The thing that really got me was their lamps hanging from the ceiling.  The glass had the look of hot flames in it, very catchy.  Something I would expect at a more hip restaurant, not fast food.  I hope you like the pictures I uploaded of them.

Food
  Appetizer - none today.

  Main Course - I went with the Whopper Jr. with cheese.  Very fresh, not the typical fast food sitting in the serving tray for an hour.  The veggies were all fresh and the right color.  Excellent job guys, I mean ladies, did not see a single guy working there.  The fries where free for St. Patrick's Day weekend, it also came with free St. Paddy's Sauce, it was Heinz red ketchup died green.  It tasted the same, but left your tongue green when you were done.  My daughter loved that, I had to take a picture for her; I will not share that one, lol.

  Dessert - One 96 cent soft serve cone, they only have vanilla but it had a great taste.  The cone was plenty big for my daughter.

Service - Fast and friendly.  Before we had topped of our soft drinks our food was ready.  They ask for your name when the order is taken, none of that number being assigned to you, and then they call you by name, next maybe I will be Frankenstein instead of Paul.  Love the little personal touch.

Future - We will probably go there since it close to my house.

Swinging through the drive through with a friend last night, I decided to try their new Fajita Burrito - on the giant over sized ad photo hanging in the window, this burrito looked like something God himself had made, it was BEAUTIFUL and even though I was not actually hungry there was NO WAY I was leaving without one! Seriously it looked just AMAZING...... and for only $3.69 

So we get to my house, and I carefully open my burrito and yum it looks amazing with strips of the grilled chicken poking out the skinny end, and pieces of the red, and green peppers sticking out, and the smell of grilled onion..... I thought COOL I've found my new favorite burrito..... WRONG 

When you order you get your choice of two condiments that you want put in your burrito. You can choose from shredded cheese, salsa, guacamole, sour cream....... and one other (I forget) So I picked sour cream and guacamole because well those go great with fajitas! 

As I was on my 2ed or 3rd bite I noticed a constant drip - drip - drip of brownish color coming from the fat end of my burrito - I wasnt sure if this was grease or what but I knew I didnt want to eat it...... so I PULLED THE PLUG (tore a small piece of the flour tortilla off) to "drain" the grease.... and all of a sudden a FLOOD of runny, watery, greasy crap came FLOODING out of my burrito!!!!!! I'm not talking a little I'm talking like at least 1/2 cup or more!!!!! 
WHAT THE HELL IS THIS!!!!!!!! My burritos was totally taking a pee and all I could do was hold it up in the air and watch this happen.... it was funny AND gross! 

I'm SOOOOOO glad I didnt try to eat this in the car because if it would have DUMPED all that all over me I'd have been SUPER pissed off! 

I dont hate Del Taco, though they are much more expensive then Taco Bell but some of their food is good, and their fries are still AWESOME..... but stay away from the pissing burrito!

This is the third time in a roll that they have messed the order up, I ordered a "double whopper" with extra onion and no lettuce and tomato, and to go and when I got home and opened the bag it was a "single whopper" with no onion, lettuce, tomato, the person doing the cooking was more worried about listening to his ear buds and what music was playing thru his phone and the girl working the front had to cover both the front counter and drive thru and I didn't see a Manager anywhere around to make sure the orders are getting fixed right and I tried to call there phone number and all i got was a "fax machine sound" And the only reason they get a star is because the onion rings that I got with the burger were cooked fresh but this was my last time going there,  I'll go next door to Taco Bell and spend my money and there is no way that I would support fast food workers getting a $15.00 raise if they can't get a simple order right.

This kfc is disgusting. It's dirty, employees don't move faster than a snail. Also how can a place on a busy highway run out of white meat? One employee tells you one thing and the next one something different. We ate what chicken we could get and it was very greasy. I've had better from Giant.

I remember when the sandwiches were almost $.48 per slider with cheese on it.
On my lunch break I would ,without thinking automatically buy five of them with cheese. I would probably eat three of them, while they were hot, and the other two, I probably wasted and eat them cold and hard but they were cheap and delicious .if there were more than one person in the car I would pay for 10 or more. Unfortunately they have price themselves right out of my market. $2.10 for  three sandwiches with no cheese.
But I can't even blame them ,this is St. Louis, nowadays you can't even get a "ham-Saint Paul" for two dollars , so what is one to do ?   go on YouTube and learn how to cook it yourself .
If you raise your prices too high...We will have to find some other place to spend our money.

I eat gluten free as much as possible and really like Chipotle for value and good food. My favorite is a burrito bowl with chicken, and I love the lime flavored rice. They add a little cheese, some fresh mild salsa, little sour cream, and lettuce. It a whole lot of food for the money. My son's favorite is the steak burrito, and he gets double meat and can never finish it. He loves the chips and salsa too.

I cant speak for all burger kings but this location is awful wait time for food is long i waited an hour for a DAMN whopper an when they gave me my order it was wrong. I stopped going here after i seen the cook drop a handful of patties an proceed to cook them!!

Upon moving to Reno from Texas - one of the first TV commercials I seen was for this strange place Del Taco..... immediantly I was turned off and vowed to NEVER to go a taco joint that served french  fries with tacos!!!!!! What were they thinking!!!!!! So I boycotted this place for about 4 years till one day I decided I HAD to go here if for no other reason then to try a fish taco I'd heard people talking about......  my friend got an order of the french fries which came in a really large cup - like a soda cup! Of course I had to try - JUST ONE...... now I understand why they can serve fries at a taco joint and get away with it!!!!! The fries were AWESOME..... the fish taco was so-so. Funny how that happened! 
The food is cheap, the drive through was quick.... what more could you want from a taco joint selling french fries!?!?!?!

I've only been there the one time, but would go back again! When I do I will take notes and update this review!

Everything about this particular location is inconsistent.

Meat: The doneness of the steak varies. Sometimes it's perfectly medium rare, but oftentimes it's cooked past the point of salvation. I shudder at the thought of well-done steak.

Portions: There are times when I go and they give me a fair portion of everything that I want. But most of the time, they give me half a scoop of meat. They don't give me much of anything, actually, so I have to ask for more.

Service: Sometimes the employees are friendly and happy to accommodate my requests for my order, but other times they just seem grumpy and don't bother faking it.

I went tonight and had to wait for the cashier to ring me up. The girl who put my burrito together had to scream out "CASHIER" twice before someone came over to ring me up.

That being said, I'm one of those people that loves Chipotle, and this is the closest one to me. So I come back every time.
I like Chicken! This KFC keeps it's lines down to a minimum compared to the other one close by on Kensington ave.

Worst Del taco I have ever been to. In 
 the breakfast items, they don't even scramble their eggs, which doesn't give it the right consistency. Very disappointed.

How difficult is it to follow instructions? I have a coupon for a 2-pc GRILLED chicken meal. I get a 2-pc original recipe meal. It's printed on the voucher, and I specifically asked for it. Really .... if you can't follow spoken and written instructions you need to question your career serving customers.

I have to say I really never liked chipotle until now. I always called it white peoples Mexican food and really held a grudge against the place I have to say. But now I'm looking at it in an entirely different light. The new sofritas option is so helpful being a vegetarian and getting a quick burrito bowl is super easy, affordable, and pretty nutritious. But what really got me hooked was these new Chiptopia cards. I'm pretty determined to get catering for 20 at this point

I couldn't leave Nashville without trying the infamous White Castle! I have seen the movie, heard the hype, and had to try it myself!

I personally do not like onions so I left the slider tasting to my fiance who hates onions but loved the slider here .. go figure?

I had the chicken ring with cheese slider .. oh man .. DELISH! Just plain DELISH!

The customer service was a bit rough but the bathroom was clean and the food was good .. 

I love the little boxes for packaging and the facility looked clean and well kept .. I actually was craving the damn slider the day after we came home to CA where there are no Whtie Castles except in the frozen section of the market and that's not the same! We only got one slider each since we had already eaten .. we just wanted to try it .. darn it!

Wish I could give it zero stars. Please spend your hard-earned money somewhere else. I went against my better judgement and tried this Burger King again only to be completely disappointed and annoyed. Went through the drive-thru and came home to find that my son's nuggets were missing, my husband's regular coke was a diet, and we were supposed to have two large fries but they look like smalls to me!  Furthermore, the food is cold, and we only live a few minutes from this location. I love a Whopper, but I will never return to this BK location again.

Super friendly staff and consistent quality. This is a newer location and seems to be less busy than the one on 146th street. Big fan of the barbacoa bowl and steak quesadillas. The sofritas burritos are yummy too.

If you go there for the buffet they always seem to be out of food and when you ask them to replenish the containers they act like they're doing you a big favor and they will get to them when they get a chance, if you go to the drive-thru at night twice I was told they were out of chicken I would have to wait another 12 minutes before they had any and once I was told they only had extra crispy the one thing that they always did seem to have was a bad attitude.
Stopped by to get the Nashville HOT chicken. Out of the Nashville Hot Sauce !!!  EPIC FAIL! Thanks for nothing.

Out of lettuce out of carnitas 35 minutes before closing. Simpering server rolled her eyes and asked the cook who was cleaning an obviously powered down grill how long it would take. Blatant passive aggressive for a fast food server and cook who don't know their place...ye/ I said it, we all paid our dues. I am sick of businesses promising a available time planing my day and getting unreliable service. Chipotle is no mom and pop, there is no wiggle room when they tap tap that extra bit of meet off the tongs so corporate makes their margin so uh pony up jerk make ur promised hours.  
Your cut off. No more from me and i was a fan tried to talk others into your food, now I will sing the song of bad chain with bad service.

While the chicken always tastes good, the big downer here is service.  They are very slow and it usually seems that 1 person is doing everything while 3 or 4 other employees are in the back socializing.

I was driving through Reno and stopped for some cheap tacos.  I expected what I normally get from Del Taco, horrible service and broken tacos.  I was shocked when I stepped into this place.  Clean, professional, fast, friendly, by far, the BEST Del Taco experience I have ever had.  Well done on this location.

I've always had a pleasant time at this particular Chipotle location. I always order the same thing. Salad with sofritas and a side of chips and guac. I see no point in eating Chipotle without chips and guac! The chips are super salty though, so keep your water close! The line gets pretty crazy long around lunchtime, but it tends to move quickly!
This kfc was awful, the woman behind the counter rude to my husband and the chicken was awful looked like it had been there all day will never return to bad we were to far away to take it back awful

I like this Burger King because they're fast and have a good staff. The staff gets a little annoying since they typically try sell you extra stuff like desserts.

I started coming here when I was next door at Jiffy Lube with some time to kill.  I avoided this location because it's only accessible when you're going south on 202.  Then I found out they have Whopper Wednesday's where a Whopper Value Meal is only $3.29!  So figured out an easier way of getting there and realized that I could park in a nearby shopping center then walk there.

Service here sucked! Took 18 minutes to get our order of a couple burritos and a few tacos. It was 4 in the after noon and no one in the lobby and a handful of cars in the drive-thru. Started to say something to the guy working there and he just said have a nice day...Twice. 

Dell beef was the only thing I ordered that was worth eating and I've had better Dell beefs before. 7 layer burrito was layered front to back instead of on top of itself. So instead of having 7 layers in one bite you got a layer in a bit. The rice only bite was discussing. Rice here was nasty. 

Tacos were not good either it was cheese and salad. Very little meat. The chess was three fingers deep. Just a tad much. Oh and the started wrapping the tacos before adding the pico since the pico was on top of the flap start and not on the taco. 

Do yourself a favor and skip this outfit

Was very disappointed with the whole operation. We did not come at an off time, so was not expecting old chicken. When I ordered the extra biscuits on top of what came with my meal, I also expected butter and honey that comes with them. Unfortunately did not find out about the missing condiments, or the old chicken until we got home 7 minutes later. At that point I looked for a receipt to call, and was not provided with one. I was also told to pull forward and they'd bring out the food. What are these employees being taught?  To pull the car forward, so that their time per car looks better? To not hand out receipts so they could keep their survey satisfactory scores high? To not give out condiments to maybe make up some food cost? Not very good service.

**I would put 3.5 stars if I could. This is the best of the burrito/taco establishments of the same type , not including Felipe's, which is more of a 4 star for me and really an altogether different restaurant. The menu is basic, and the place is always very clean. The employees are all held go strict food safety standards. The food is good , but I wish they had fresher tasting or possibly homemade flour and corn tortillas. The chicken and Barbacoa steak are the best meats. ( I haven't tried the softest yet. ). The guacamole is always good and fresh tasting. For the most part, the line flows pretty well. I will always make this a stop for some quick tacos or a taco bowl. It is very quick, organized, and clean ( unlike some of the similar places that seem to have a mass disorder going on.  For best results get the tacos or a burrito bowl. ( if you order a burrito, and get a rookie burrito roller, you will be sorry). 

Pros:
1. Managed well/ great customer service. 
2. Great service /quality/cleanliness 
3. Fresh tasting food 


Cons 
1. Inconstent tortillas ( corn and flour). Make fresh ones!!!!!! It isn't difficult and will make the product better. 
2. Tabasco doesn't really go with anything on the menu.( although I love Tabasco. Just not at chipotle). Get some Mexican hot sauce. 
3. The "hot" salsa is VERY mild. Put some kick in it.

At Chipotle, I had the Sofritas Burrito, which was warm, comforting, good clean food. It's a fair sized portion. 

Solid food that's reasonably priced. 

3.5 stars

I had to visit White Castle, in hopes of finding Harold and Kumar!  It was my first time visiting a White Castle or even being in the same vicinity as one, so it was only inevitable that I try this casual fast food spot for myself. 

The place is small and there was only one other customer in there.  It seemed that the drive-thru was busy with a steady stream of cars in line, and most of the staff there was preparing those orders.  We ended up getting the 10 cheeseburger slider combination, which also came with two drinks and we added two small fries.  After you order you can watch them make your food, as there is a window directly in front of the griddle that the burgers are made on. 

The cheeseburger sliders are simple - there really only is small grilled onions that is similar to McDonald's, one or two pickle coins and just a small, thin patty with melted American cheese on a fluffy bun.  You add your own condiments of mayo, ketchup or mustard which is right near where the pop and utensils are.  

Overall, it wasn't a bad slider nor would I ever pull a Harold and Kumar road trip for!  I could easily see myself going here though if there was any in my area, after a night of drinking, of course.  The sliders are really small, so when I found out we were ordering 10 for the three of us I thought it would be too much but it really wasn't.  You can easily down a slider in two bites, so order plenty if you're hungry.

Its hard to write this review. I'm a big fan and always was of KFC's delicious chicken and seasoned fries until I started coming here. After the fourth horrible experience, I gave up. The staff here is very, very slow. Every time I come there is a very long line and/or I'm first in line and get my food 20 minutes later, with the order usually being wrong.

One time, I'm being dead serious folks, the cashier asked me to stop the voices that were bothering her and proceeded to yell at the empty place next to me. I got freaked out and avoided the place for months. I made a mistake and came back to find the lobby closed at 3:30 in the afternoon. After looking for a sign or anyone to explain, a lady came out and said the lobby was closed since "we don't have that much food" and asked if we wanted to use the drive thru. What?? Please, avoid this location like the plague, it just isn't worth it.

I'm new to the KFC phenomenon. As a kid, I never really got on the biscuits and slaw bandwagon. As an adult, I just never ate a lot of fried chicken. Meh, it isn't really my thing. 

Then one day my sister whispered to me 'try the grilled, you'll love it!'. She was right. She knows me so well. The grilled chicken is highly seasoned, sort of crispy on the outside, and super juicy inside. Their green beans incite me to sing little songs and do little dances. But what I like best of all is that I'm pretty sure the young man working the drive through also inhabits the cover of MAD magazine. He always looks like he's up to some sort of mischief, and I kind of wish he was. 

So I'm on the Kentucky Grilled Chicken bandwagon. My sister was right, she knows what's up. 


(Oh, and they have pepsi max, bro.)

Went out of our way to come here because the wife loves it and there aren't a ton of these in town.  Came in and we got the bottom of both rices, lettuce, and corn salsa. The steak looked like it had been sitting so neither of us got it (our usual go to).  Our burrito maker put an insane amount of medium salsa in my wife's bowl to the point she had to scoop half of it out and then our burrito maker ripped my burrito wrapping it up and offered to rewrap it which I appreciated but I got less than half of the burrito I ordered.  The rest was thrown out.  First time I've ever left chipotle hungry and certainly going to keep going back to qdoba after this experience.

Jesus fucking Christ these people are slow. I can count seven employees doing various things behind the counter, not one of which is helping customers. There were two of us standing there looking at each other like, um, do we say something or just wait?
So finally the first lady puts my rice, beans and carnitas in the bowl. I asked for very little rice. She dumps a huge scoop of rice, and oh, who cares. Then I wait again. I look back and forth from one employee to the next and no one acknowledges me. 
After another awkwardly long wait, someone comes over to ask about my salsa and cheese. I get pico, a very little bit of corn salsa and sour cream. No, just a little bit of cor... Whatever. No! No cheese. No, I don't want cheese. No. No, thank you, no cheese.*all the eye rolls*
Then I'm slid down to the register to... Wait some more. Finally a cashier comes over. She gets my grapefruit izze, and, "wait, you didn't get cheese?" No. No cheese, thanks. "Oh, you don't like cheese?" Well, ma'am, I'm sure your store is OK with me getting less ingredients... what I would like is to order my bowl in less than 12 minutes next time, please.

I'm not going back to this location. The chickens that they serve are very dry and taste like its not fresh. The sizes are as small as a typical chicken finger. Service is really bad, they don't care about their job obviously. Feel bad for the owner!!!hope the owner will do something about this
Long ass wait for soggy bread and onion-flavored cabbage on my slider. Krystal is the better option.

I've eaten at this BK several times since I moved to Reno last September.  I've always driven through, and the food has always been impressively good.  Today, I dined in and was just as impressed with the interior.  Very clean, attractive, with  friendly employees.  And again, my Whopper combo was delicious.  Very fresh, crisp fixings, and the fries were perfect.  Other BK's should aspire to be this good!

This was one of the first places I came to when I moved to the Brandon area. I had never been to a chipotle before (sad because I lived in Denver) but I really don't eat "fast food" much. Because I lived in a hotel, this was a great healthy choice and I was hooked! I pretty much stuck with the veggie bowls and the sofritas, but everything was spiced to perfection and the ingredients were obviously high quality and fresh. 

 The reason I give three stars instead of five is because this location is crazy packed.... Like line out the door packed. The staff seemed to be properly trained for the volume which was good, especially since they are a fairly new location. Also, they didn't sell beer like they advertised upon the store opening which was a bit misleading (although that might have changed now).

 All in all, definitely a great choice for fast casual dining for everyone of all dietary needs. Will definitely be back, just maybe not Saturday again.

Did drive-thru. I'll start off with drinks. As i know some may feel that it's not a big deal, but my husband and I drink different sodas. Depending where we are I usually get Dr. Pepper the husband gets Coke. Drinks handed to us and we don't even know which is coke vs Dr. Pepper because we weren't told nor was it marked. Got home and unwrapped my chicken sandwich. All that comes on it is lettuce and mayo. My sandwich was pretty dry. Hardly any of either. My husband looked up from his Texas Whopper and said "doesn't look anything like the picture". My thoughts EXACTLY!

This particular Chipotle is a good one. Come to think of it I haven't been to one where I didn't like the food or the service. Lines move quick here to that's a plus since I'm usually really hungry when I choose to go to chipotle.

I usually get a Chicken bowl or Burrito with rice, pinto beans, lettuce, cheese, sour cream, and mild tomatoes. Although I've had steak as well, its good but a bit spicy for me. The chips are salty with a lemon taste to it make sure you get guacamole with it.

The only downer I can think of which resulted in a not perfect score is the place can be a bit tiny for its popularity resulting in a hunt for a seat and table during the busy times of the day. All and all good place to eat!

I eat here quite a lot. Like, an uncomfortable amount.  The food is your standard Chipotle fare, it's always tasty, and the price is right. I'm a big fan of their burrito bowls and I usually switch between chicken or steak. The portions are pretty large and often times I'll save the rest of my bowl for later as a mid afternoon snack. 

The staff is really friendly! Everyone who works here is really cool and always greet you with a smile when you walk in. Even when slammed with customers, they're always nice and do their best to take care of you.
These people don't even know how to do the kids the graves the chicken with out gloves and give you the smallest pieces ever
Great place, grabbing lunch before heading to the Apple Store, very delicious steak bowl

I love chipotle but this location isn't doing too hot. When we arrived, fajita vegetables looked like they had been sitting there for at least an hour. Steak and chicken were both old and dry. Pinto beans were INCREDIBLY over salted. Yikes.

The staff here is just so unprofessional. I went in one evening and no one was at the counter. I heard some adults talking from the back. I waited 5 minutes. Then walked over to the pick up counter and waited another 5 minutes. She finally came out. She was actually talking on her cellphone. I asked her for a turkey sausage, egg, and cheese on a flatbread. She asked "croissant?", I responded, "no, flatbread, please". She agreed and started putting on gloves and heating up some stuff. I heard the oven running. Another 5 minutes have passed. I was the only customer there.I waited and felt glad to head the overall. Go off because I have been there 15 min already. She called out to me and instead of saying your sandwich is ready she told me she is out of flatbread and asked me again if I wanted crossiant. I did not get a sandwich that night.
Long line but waited.  Steak very undercooked and bleeding.  Not happy with rude workers when asked a question.  Won't be back to this location.

This might be the worst Burger King I have ever been to. Each time I go, the restaurant is out of key items, such as the Whopper (today's visit) or the XL BBQ Cheeseburger and onion rings (previous visit). The customer service is awful, as you are never greeted or welcomed for coming to Burger King. On a previous visit, I even had a drive-thru attendant tell me they weren't working the drive-thru that day and I needed to come inside if I wanted my food. I could list many more complaints, but why waste the time. Avoid this restaurant. There are many better options nearby.
Very consistent quality and great service.  I get the carnitas burritos.  They are very tasty indeed.  What stands out is the quality of their ingredients.  Highly recommended.

Generally, I like del Taco. As a chain they have the world's best french fries, IMO.
I am finished with this location, though. In the drive thru at night you get either one of two things: great service or a bad experience you will remember for a life-time. Roll the dice. I just don't deserve to be treated like garbage coming home from work late at night.
Last time I went there I ordered the tacos and a quesidlla along with an order of fries. There was something chewy that may have been an edge of a hamburger in one of my tacos that I spit out and it tasted like someone put a pinch of sugar in my fries.
Was it done on purpose or is this place just a dangerous mismanaged health hazard cesspool ?
I don't know, it does not make a difference does it ?

While the food is typical KFC chicken, the service is the worst. I have never ordered and gotten my food in under 30 minutes. The employees are always confused and yelling at each other. Always check your order before you leave, it is rare they get the order correct.

I'm going to be real. This is my go-to fast food minus chick fil la- not open on Sundays so I can't be totally devoted to that. 

I come here 3-4x a week on average. I don't like cooking very much so this is the place I go to for lunch and dinner.

I get the half chicken/half steak...I think this is the best way to maximize the amount of meat you get. You get charged for the steak but I think it's worth it. 

This is first on my top ranked fast food place. Close tie to chick fil la but like I said..chick fil la ain't open Sunday so I give it to Chipotle. The line goes by pretty quick which is also why I ranked it above popeyes because who doesn't like fried chicken? #amiright

I went to the Del Taco on North Mc Carran in Reno to get the National taco day shredded beef taco, buy one get one free, and went to the drive through to order and a person named robin started yelling that it didn't start until 3 pm. No where on my e mail does it  say 3pm! said I didn't know but am a disabled senior who has a hard time getting up and down the stairs to my apartment and she said she didn't care and she wouldn't help me and to go somewhere else. I was upset because she was so callous that I asked for her manager and she yelled I am the manager. I had to sit behind a car while they got their order and went to the window but she would not come to the window so I had to park in the handicap and go inside where she started yelling at me. I just wanted her bosses number so I could file a complaint but she wouldn't give it to me. I will never go there again. Very horrible way to treat a disabled person and a 66 years old one at that!

Every time I see a White Castle (I'm from the West Coast, where we have none), my initial reaction is "Mmm, that sounds good" even though every single time I go, it ends in dissapointment.  I like the first bite of the first slider, but by the end of the second slider, the taste in my mouth is of pure mediocrity.  Sliding scale of diminishing returns, I suppose.

 
It happened again on this trip.  It was bound to happen, as one of our co-travellers had never been, and I would never tell a man who's never been to White Castle not to try it at least once. This particular White Castle is attached to a Church's, which I haven't had since I was a kid.  All in all, this location isn't bad, and the service here is quite good.  The people behind the counter are helpful and friendly.  But it's White Castle, and... yeah.

1 star for White Castle
 3 star for Church's
4 star for the people that work there.  
0 star for Neil Patrick Harris not being there to pay for my meal. :)

Well I love Chipotle it's an awesome chain of restaurants. So I hope it was just an off day. I had the steak burrito and it was very grisly and tough. I know free range beef can be tuff, but they are getting ripped off if this is what they are buying. The other thing and maybe it's just me since I started to reduce my sodium intake, but it seemed very salty . Five minutes after eating my mouth dried up. I didn't even have chips just the steak with cilantro rice and pinto beans.

Cold chicken, crappy service and NO CHIPS?? This franchise should have its license revoked for giving chipotle a bad name. It's like they didn't even care they had no chips or steak. I would've ordered differently (or not at all) if it were known before the cash register. What is going on? Seriously, even for airport food.

GROSS! I haven't had KFC in a million years and I won't be having it again ever!

I wanted a combo which had one side, 2 piece chicken and a drink, when we were ordering at the drive in the woman said that it came with two sides, I persisted that 'no, it says one on the menu' but lo and behold I got the combo with a biscuit and two sides, and paid for it too, which I didn't want. 

After that annoying experience, I attempted to eat the chicken and it tasted like pure grease. If you life extremely greasy unhealthy fried chicken, then this is your place, otherwise stay away!!!

Lastly, what happened to KFC having spicy options? I'll make sure to stick to Chick Filet from now on.

The only thing that saved this from one star was the person who waited on me was really friendly.  The food was probably some of the worst KFC I have ever experienced, the chicken, tasted old, and the wedges had seen better days as well. The ice tea was fresh, but pretty strong and the biscuit I kept in case I need to break a window out of my car.

This is the ONLY kfc I will eat at , it's clean,  the manager is so nice and so is all of the staff , I always go in after a target trip for grilled chicken and green beans (that are seriously so delicious) and I just had the pumpkin cake and oh my gosh it's soo good !!!!! Mmm!!! Thank you!

I LOVE Chipotle. It's way better than the other "fast casual" Mexican restaurants (ie Qdoba & Moe's). The ingredients are fresher and you can taste a big difference. My only complaint is that there aren't enough locations around town, and I hardly ever make it this far over on the 96th St to enjoy it. :( 

I always order a burrito with the carnitas... I haven't found any better carnitas in the city than what you'll get at Chipotle. My other favorite burrito element? The cilantro lime rice. It is truly amazing- deliciously seasoned and with the perfect texture for a burrito. Add guacamole and some fresh tomato salsa or roasted chili corn salsa, and you can't go wrong. Plus, these burritos are HUGE. You will never leave here hungry!
Can't really complain... the Sausage Egg and Cheese crossiant was good, the hashbrowns were great (for to-go hashbrowns) and the service was quick! Keep up the good work!

Today would make the second time they have messed up my order. I generally order grilled chicken two piece white, with two sides. The last time, I got extra crispy. Tonight due to grilled chicken not available only on Sunday, I ended up with original thigh and crispy wing. I asked for white meat original. 

I could go on, but its just not worth going back to this KFC.  I'll go to South County in Missouri if I crave this stuff ever again.

I really like Chipotle and haven't had it in awhile so I decided to go with the wife when I was in the Plymouth Meeting Mall. Anyway when we went in I noticed some of the workers had an attitude. 

The place was dirty and had a smell to it. The floors were grimy. They didn't have any forks and when I asked they told me to eat with a spoon because they were out of forks. I didn't want to eat my lettuce and steak with a spoon. 

The food wasn't as fresh as I remember and the steak was grisly and fatty in some spots. 

I won't be picking this place again. I had better on City Avenue.

The bowls are typically very good but recently ordered the steak bowl and was very disappointed. I ended up with a bowl of junk steak parts - every piece full of fat & gristle; was inedible. A $10 meal down the drain. Stick with chicken or pork. The steak is not worth the upcharge.

So-so speed of service today, though the first employee started off my order with a friendly smile and fast service...downhill from there.
I thought I had kept a close eye on my two bowls as they were assembled.  They were almost identical (carnitas, brown rice, black beans, mild salsa, corn salsa, sour cream, quac, cheese) except for one that had added onions/peppers.  I watched as they were passed down the line, but must have lost sight for a second as they got close to the register.  I should of had a clue when the register person tried to give me just one bowl...shame on me for not double checking.

Got home, sure enough mine with the onions/peppers was fine, the other (apparently someone elses order), missing quac, and had medium salsa.  Yikes!  My wife ready to kill me for getting hot salsa and no quac.  Could not even eat half of it because it was just too spicy for her.

Thanks a lot Chipolte!

This place completely made me hate KFC. I say that being someone who loved KFC, it was one of my favorite guilty pleasures until I came to this one. Chicken was not cooked all the way, items were missing and what was edible was completely cold. I had no idea people could mess up fast food up this bad. On top of that the staff was extremely rude to us. I'm not sure if this is a franchisee, franchisor, or employee issue but one thing is for sure this place completely turned off what was once a lifelong KFC customer. If KFC corporate had any sense they would shut this place down before it causes damage to the brand. Either that or tear it down and rebuild anew with all new employees and management. Disgusting!

I've never had any issues with this location. They were out of glazed munchkins once but it was 5 pm so that was understandable. 

This morning I went and the drive thru line was super long so I went in. No wait. I ordered an iced vanilla latte and a sausage croissant breakfast sandwich. Both came out very quickly and both tasted good. Service is not overly friendly but I'm not very talkative in the early morning hours so it never seems to bother me. 

I frequent Starbucks more but if I want a little bit of a cheaper option I'll go here.

I no longer patronize BK; haven't for about a year now. The sole complaint is the tiny size of their burgers, specifically the sadly misnamed "Whopper". I don't want to even crack a joke about it anymore - that sucker has gotten so tiny. Other than that, it was great to get a burger there on occasion. Kind of a shame, really. Oh well.

Ordered 1 extra crispy breast deal with mashed potatoes and biscuit. The chicken breast was extremely small, burned and not extra crispy. Potatoes were very watery. Biscuit was okay.  It has been about 5 years since I was there and now I know why. I won't be going back.  I threw most of it away. Waste of money. Staff was pleasant, though.

Welp- Carls Jr. thanks for reminding me why we don't spend money at fast food restaurants & especially yours.  

I enjoy knowing that Carls Jr. offers low carb/luttuce wrapped burgers & chicken sandwiches- & unfortunately they're the only fast food restaurants that do. 

So we gave it a try, for the first time in a long time, & what an epic fail- especially at this location.  

To the driver- thru employee: "do you guys still offer turkey burgers?" 
Him: "No & Thanksgiving is over". 
We thought he was being funny but he didn't laugh or act funny, he was really a jerk. 
We proceeded to ask for grilled chicken sandwich options & he totally rushed us- "oh, so BBQ grilled chicken." 
"Ummm, no...is there a diff..." 
"So santa fe grilled, ok. 
Whatever, I'll have that.  "In a meal please, large sized." 
"& water for your drink?"
Ummm...no! I'm paying for the damn drink.  

$18 later for 2 sandwiches (1 was a meal) & after waiting for 15mins (no one was there but us), we get our food with no receipt & no ranch that we requested + a small fry instead of large!! 

My sandwich was a sloppy mess BUT it's lettuce wrapped so to be expected & it tasted good (only reason this is a 2 star review I instead of 1). 
My boyfriend's fried chicken sandwich tasted nasty & sweet- almost fishy or like a fish sauce was used accidentally.
