Dragged Mr. Dave H. With me since he never had a Chipotle in his life.
I can always count on you whenever I am.

I recently went through this drive thru to order a Carl's famous star hamburger meal. Although this would see simple, it was not. The order taker could not hear my order, upon driving to the window with frustration, she refused me service. I asked for a manager, and she stated that she was the manager, and again refused service. I honked my horn in frustration, in which a man appeared in front of my truck to pressure me to move on, in a very threatening way. I grew up with Carl's jr in so cal, I had friends that went to school with Carl Karcher's kids. It saddens me to say I will never ever go to this Carl's Jr., or any other because of this ridiculous ignorance to customer service.

This store has gotten our business a few times for different reasons and the inconsistency in quality and customer service have been so far off it's not even funny. 

This morning, my wife wanted to try their new French toast sandwich for breakfast.... well... uhhh.... yeah....that!  Unless they did something wrong in preparation, NOT A FAN!  It's salty, soggy, and just OK. 

The store didn't pay attention to specific order and customization notes - basic English and attentiveness would allow for someone to easily read "NO EGG" on another persons sandwich. Ham was out ?  How is that even possible, unless the store just doesn't understand their ordering needs for the time they have between orders, which I thought was daily or weekly?  Sad...

And then... THEY FORGOT THE OJ that was part of the meals ordered.  How is that possible?  Basic product knowledge would reach the newest of employees that breakfast meals, unless changed for coffee or soft drink COMES WITH A GLORIOUS BOTTLE OF SIMPLY ORANGE OJ!  Sad....

BK, I'm sorry but you may miss my future "Whopper Wednesday" orders since this store can't seem to manage the day-to-day basics. 

What's even worse... NONE of the area BK stores have higher than a 2-star rating... SAD!!!

Two words: Burrito Bowl

You get more food in a bowl than a burrito. Also, you can ask for a tortilla on the side. 

Try the sofritas, it's vegan, has tofu, and made of a unique blend of spices.  For meat lovers, try the barbacoa, it's seasoned cuts of ground beef. 
All the veggie items comes with free guacamole! 

I have been here countless of times, probably 50 times. I know... My love for Chipotle is strong. I have never had bad service at this location. When the burrito artist gives me a baby scoop of rice, I ask for more and they always give me that extra scoop. 

I remember going there on a busy night. They were so upbeat and friendly. I knew that they were exhausted, but I could tell that they were trying to keep their customers satisfied.

What I really love about Chipotle is that they provide you with online nutrition facts. I love reading their positive affirmations and quotes they print on their bags. That extra detail shows the quality they put in.

I just walked to this KFC from my hotel, a short 10 minutes away. I walked in to order some chicken to-go and I was SHOCKED at how dirty and disgusting the restaurant was. The floor was sticky, the food-shelving in the kitchen was dirty and the floor in the kitchen area and near the frier was littered with grease, old food and was just plain disgusting.  

The service was equally as bad. When my coworker and I walked-in we were looked at like we were completely bothering the stuff because we were there. 

We walked out, disgusted and swore we'd never come back. My next call is to the local Health Department. It was THAT bad.

I write this review while laying in bed a full 36 hours after having eaten from this location and I'm grateful to finally not be throwing up, having diarrhea and the worst headache of my life. My family and I ordered burrito bowls to go for pick up on Friday 6/7. We ordered it for 7:45, got there at 7:45 and when we got home we noticed our food was pretty cold. I wondered how long it had been sitting out, but since we were already home
We decided to eat it anyway. About an hour later my stomach began cramping slightly and I was gassy. I figured that maybe I had eaten too much and was just digesting. Well, let me tell you, that was the first signs of one of the worst nights I've had in a very long time. About 12:00AM the cramps started rolling in, then the diarrhea, then about 5:00AM came the vomiting and muscle aches, 7:00AM came the headache (probably from dehydration) and the rest of my day yesterday was spent vacillating between the three. About 24 hours later I was able to keep some soup and Gatorade down and the headache finally went away. I'm still pretty tired and my stomach is definitely queasy so I'll be taking it easy for today as well. My family didn't get sick, so by process of elimination it was either the guacamole, steak or mild salsa as none of them had that on their bowls and I did. Needless to say, I won't be dining there again which sucks because I was an avid Chipotle fan until this happened.

Yum! Fast. Friendly. Delicious food! Very satisfied with chipotle, yet again! We got there upon opening and received great service from all the employees. 

Food- I wish the sofritas always looked fresh. Most of the time I go to Chipotle (in general), it looks crusty and hard. I think Chipotle could fix this issue by cooking smaller portions at a time to prevent wasting food, but also keeping their food fresh. 
They're good about picking out the wilted lettuce and making sure the food area is clean. There isn't always sliced lemon for your drinks, but I guess it depends on how busy they are when you go. 

Parking- There's a mid-sized parking lot. It's usually crazy with people coming and going, especially during lunch hours. It's not the easiest parking lot to pull out of either! 

Pricing- I'd say their prices range from $6 and change to $9 depending on your choice of protein (tofu (sofritas), chicken, pork, steak, etc.

Ok here goes my review. I had a coupon for the family meal which is two whopper meals and two cheeseburger meals for $12.99.  We were in a rush doing errands so we figured okay let's just get this quick. I asked for extra pickles and onions on one of the whoppers, well that didn't happen. It said it on my receipt but nope not on the sandwich.  Anyways I can survive I guess but my question is why are the burgers never warm. The fries were delish and very warm but the sandwiches weren't. And they weren't even really crowded so what was the problem?  I feel like this is a thing with Burger King. I honestly feel like whenever I get a sandwich from them it isn't hot.  Ugh! And I mean not just this location but others as well.  Anyway this branded them two stars. And I woulda liked my extras on my burger since that's what Burger King is known for specializing orders because it doesn't upset them lol
Worst Burger King on the planet.  Ate here a while ago aNd got a mean case of throwing up.  Forgot about it, and got a whopper tonight.  Had to puke within 10 minutes of eating.  Screw you!

This location is trash. I really should go to corporate. The laziness the listening skills
I literally just left and ordered 4 impossible sliders w/ no cheese. The place was not busy at all I had 1 car in front of me so to get it wrong was on them. Y'all hiring these young people that don't care about anything but getting a check. I'm allergic to cheese but I'll be wrong to sue. I'm vegan and don't do anything from an animal. Never again will I go here. Or to any of them. The service and food was trash and not fresh. Get some better workers or shut down!!!!

when asked what is my favorite fast food place, Del Taco is my answer.

I have not tried everything on the menu, but I love that they have such a variety and are open 24 hrs!

my go to is the grilled chicken soft tacos. they are inexpensive and quick and tasty.
for my guilty pleasure... the chili cheese fries deluxe (minus the raw onions).

Service is always fast and friendly.

Just like any other Chipotle (point of chains) so I won't go on and on.

1. They offer sofritas. For my fellow vegetarians or alternative eating friends, try them! They're very well sauteed! Apparently they have a ton of sodium (thanks Mayur C.) so if you're sensitive, check it the deets online. 

2. During lunch time, this line is basically at the door and ridic intimidating. It goes go by VERY quickly though. Pros: goes by quickly. Cons: You better know wtf you want to order cause there is no time when you are up there to ponder between black bean or pinto.

I went to this Dunkin Donuts today and ordered a bacon croissant sandwich and a sausage croissant sandwich and when I got home and opened my food I was so disappointed! It was hard, dry and burned, both sandwiches. I don't know how in good conscience you would serve this to someone!! If it wasn't a 20 minute round trip drive I would go ask for my money back!
SAD. During this quarantine, all I asked for was my chicken on the side of my famous bowl. This is the second time in a row they didn't listen. I left my house for this!!! I don't want CHICKEN!!

I hate this location. I eat enough Chipotle to know I'm getting skimped hard on the meat at this location. Whenever I have any requests, some tall blonde schmohawk has to come in and turn down my request. If it weren't that every other Chipotle provides the same exceptionally great service, setting the bar high, I would think I was being unreasonable. Instead I just cry on the inside and die a little while I bite into mostly tortilla. This place basically makes me a vegetarian but I'm probably slowly becoming anemic from iron deficiency. GIVE ME THAT MEAT BABY!!

Extra stars given because the blonde guy wasn't here today and the rest of the team (sans meat provider) were efficient and polite and wrapped one fine "steak" burrito.

Good and quick service, order was correct, food was what you expect from KFC! We tried the new Beyond chicken and it was decent! The chicken itself had no flavor but, as a vegetarian, is was super exciting to be able to try that seasoned KFC breading finally. I would get it again.
The building looked well kept but the area surrounding looks run down.

When I first came to this KFC a few years ago the experience was awesome!! The lines were swift, employees were friendly, food was fantastic and there was even a gallon of lemonade that came with our chicken and waffles! We enjoyed it so much we wanted to come back and we did, which was last December 2018. This expereince was not as great. The lines were long and slow, the food was extremely greasy and when we inquired about the lemonade we were told nina. Nasty way "they didn't do that anymore." We haven'tbeen back since, which is a shame given how great the first experience was. We might come back if its convient, but we won't be rushing back.

I haven't had a White Castle burger since I was a kid, and it was the frozen kind you get at the grocery store. My dad loved these little slider burgers. I wasn't impressed at the time. Well fast forward through time and space. I found myself in Nashville for a short time, and we saw White Castle on our way back to our Airbnb. My friends discovered that White Castle now carry Impossible Burgers.  We are a group of vegans, vegetarians and an omnivore. We all opted to try the Impossible Burger slider. If you deduct the cheese, the burger is vegan. They were in the midst of renovating, so we had to take our burgers to go. I would have given 4 stars if we could have dined in. Wish we had a White Castle in Portland, OR!

Don't know why I keep bothering to come back to this particular KFC. It's the worst staff and care not attitude toward customers that I have ever seen at a fast food. Maybe all they can hire are under performing staff? 

Last three trips . 

1- they closed at 8:50 saying we close at 9. And laughed when I confronted them. Staff didn't care at all. 

2 - cold chicken sandwich and missing items from bag and no receipt given (down maybe) 

3 - missing items again , no chicken sandwich and no biscuits as ordered, everything else was fine. 

To say I'm frustrated with the KFC is an understatement. The employees just don't care at all.

I have always been a fan of chipotle and a loyal customer but this location is awful with my share of 5 or 6 times ordering from them. My recent visit: My bowl was practically half full which it usually is not because I order the same thing every time. I pay more for extra steak and there was BARELY any. For all chipotle lovers like myself, I HIGHLY recommend that you dodge this location. They are extremely stingy with their servings. However, the chips were fine, the bowl was devastating.

We have gone through the drive through a couple times now for dinner. Both times they have gotten our order 100% correct and given sauces, which does not always happen. I would not recommend going on a Tuesday though unless you are specifically going for the taco Tuesday deals, because the line for the drive-through is much longer.

This location is no good. Either they are out of soda or the food is horrible. My experience last night again reminded me why I don't come here often. Couldn't decide on dinner so the wife said let's go to BK. I had my reservations of course due to prior experience lol. Ordered a Texas whopper, get home to a cold, flat burger. Barely wanted to eat it. Wife got the chicken sandwich on which she needed to add extra mayo. Won't be going back for a while.

If I could leave a review with 0 stars I would. This location upsets me every time. Today, no grilled chicken. At least they had original recipe, one time I came here they didn't have any chicken but tenders. Service can be hit or miss, unfortunately more on the miss.

I have had problems with orders being filled correctly/unacceptable customer service  at KFC in Royersford, PA over the years and stopped going there because of that.  We tried again last night and were again disappointed.  We ordered two three piece meals original recipe with dark meat - one with mac & cheese and coleslaw and the other with mashed potatoes/gravy and corn for take out.  When we got home, there was only 1 with a biscuit, and the sides were wrong.  There were 3 coleslaws and 1 mac&cheese.  The coleslaw was completely dry and tasted "off".  We also recieved extra crispy for all of the chicken instead of regular recipe.

I called immediately and asked for the manager and she was beyond rude.  When I explained what got, she replied with "So"  "Not my problem" 
That is a  totally unacceptable response from management. I would be terminated for that type of behavior. Additionally, I dont feel that you should have to take your order completely apart to completely examine it  before you exit the premises.

I had to give it 1 star to  post comments....scould be NO stars

3.5 really.

I do love the occasional fast food fried chicken. Sometimes I prefer Popeye's, sometimes I like KFC, for the three or so times a year that I indulge. Popeye's has crispier chicken, but I prefer the sides at KFC. Let's not get too nerdy on the specifics, though. This place is usually staffed by high schoolers who can't be too bothered to break their neck to serve you too quickly. I usually opt for a 2 piece chicken with mashed potatoes and coleslaw. Not much has changed over the years except for the size of the chickens. They seem to be growing the chicken on a farm in Hobbitland, since they have progressively shrunk in size. Recently I had a chicken wing and I started to laugh at myself because I felt like Tom Hanks in Big taking dainty little bites out of a miniature piece of corn.

Still, dang good.

We have been going to this location for 30+ years.  Many times I've come home and part of my order is missing.  I called once when it happened, was told I was a liar, and learned from that point on to check my order before leaving. That alone is sad.  I shouldn't have to do their job....but heaven forbid I get called a liar again and try to be dishonest over a small gravy.    My hubby picked up tonight on his way home.  We get gravy that is like water, French fries that are hard as a rock and popcorn chicken with no sauce.  Seriously..how hard is it to get this right?  The staff here is never happy or smiling and obviously someone is lacking in the training department.  With Popeyes coming here they better smarten up, or they will be the next company to go under. Unfortunately, we will not go back again.  We will take the extra time to drive to Popeyes from now on.

After driving around all day for Uber I found myself extremely hungry and having the shakes after having a cup of green tea. I decided to stop in to Chipotle in Clayton and it was a very good choice because the customer service was excellent and the food was equally excellent. I decided to eat three tacos and I couldn't have made a better choice. I had one with steak and chicken one with steak and then one with chicken. The server's even put my rice and beans on the side which made me feel like I had even more food to eat which was great. I would definitely recommend this place versus Qdoba. I always wonder what the difference is but today definitely made the difference.
Nothing's better than coming in at 4 pm and sitting and waiting 2 hours for chicken to be cooked and not being able to get any yourself cause of other customers... thanks KFC...

I was out of town doing work in the kohl's store. Well we did not want hotel food so we stopped by this Dunkin' Donuts. They where such a friendly staff. I got a hot chocolate and a egg n sausage pretzel sandwich. My brother and his girlfriend got meals as well. I was amazed on how fast they where and accurate. I was also happy the young lady who made my hot chocolate was awesom. Guess what it was filled to the top of the cup to and then she added whipped creAm. I guess there machine does not presume wrong lol. I loved it there. I would return anytime I am I. The area again. They restored my faith in Dunkin' Donuts

I am writing this review for this specific Chipotle location. I LOVE LOVE LOVE Chipotle. I go a few times a week. However, this location is extremely disappointing. On every occasion I have been to this one (the closest to my house in Old NE), there are  never enough people working or you see the employees standing around or on their phones. The line will be out the door and they will have one person on the line taking orders. Yesterday, I got there right before the rush. I was so excited because there weren't many people in line and they seemed to be well staffed.  All of the sudden, two of the staff members also joined the customer line and the now one person on the line (this is 6pm, mind you) started making an extremely complicated call-in order. This took well over 10 minutes. By the time she started taking my order, almost everything was out. Basically, the people made to stand in this line now got the dregs of the meat, veggies, and salsas. On top of that, there wasn't much steak left and the employee in line behind me shouted " SAVE ME THE REST OF THAT. IM ORDERING THAT". By this time the line is out the door, and there are employees in line ordering food while only 2 work the line. This is not an isolated occurrence. For once I would like to see a manager step in and take control of the restaurant.  The only reason I am giving any stars is because I love Chipotle. I will not be returning to this location until something changes.

I just got the best service of my life ! The crew was amazing & friendly, & met all of my picky needs :P By far the most delicious steak I've ever had at any Chipotle I've ever been to! The managers Chong & Kenny made sure we had the best service, that's exactly what we got :)

For a fast food place I find myself waiting for food every time we come here. They never have chicken strips or potato wedges ready. Last time I was here they told me the wait was 20 minutes. I ended up driving straight through the pick up line and skipping the KFC. I can get chicken strips in any restaurant in town faster than that. It's a constant disappointment which is to bad considering the food (especially for kids) is pretty good.

Out of corn tortillas today and very little ice. Arrived early before the rush of lunch hour. Very little parking due to crane. Last time I tried to go for lunch there was no parking due to piles of snow. Food wise- ok had steak burrito and steak was over cooked and too chewey, gristle like. The tortilla/nacho chips tastes different and then realized that it sort of taste like they were fried in bad or old grease.

This place has a serious problem with their Hot Wings. Time and time again when I order their Hot Wings, I end up​ getting what's feels and taste like over a day old wings. The chicken is super dried from outside all the way to the meat. Insanely lightweight and hard. Like it's been dried out for days. Definitely not a recent fried chicken. The Franchise definitely needs to do an investigation at this place of whether or not they cook their chicken daily. I gave them tons of​ chances. No more, ever again with this place! I am willing to drive to midtown to get KFCs.

Yes, I should not be eating Del Taco when there are so many authentic restaurants near by. Okay, now that that is out of the way, they make decent food but have trouble hearing my order correctly. It's a bit irritating, perhaps they should invest in a new speaker

We go here multiple times for the big fat chicken tacos on soft pita bread and sometimes the deluxe taco salads. The low stars is for the workers and inconsistencies. Sometimes they forget the beans on the side when asking for them to be off of the salad and sometimes they forget a fork! Their water isn't filtered and it always is in a tiny cup. The most annoying thing is having to repeat what you want to the workers in the drive thru speaker. Every time I order the big fat chicken tacos, they say, "you want soft chicken taco"? Learn better English and turn up your volume or clean out your ears! I've tried other things like breakfast sandwiches and fish tacos but they were all yuck. Paying $1.69 for a small drink is ridiculous and this lady forgot the drink while I sat at the window tonight and she took an order with someone at the counter, so I asked if she'd up it to a large and she said I ordered a small. She got soda on the top and asked for a napkin and she gave to me and closed the window. How rude. I never get a call or anything when I leave feedback. Jerks. Too bad those items taste so good and we drive across town just to get them since there are no other locations in mid town or east.

Unlike the other reviewer I found this place REALLY dirty, in severe need of paint and general maintenance. On top of that the food sucks. There are much better locations than this one to get KFC but none close. If you want fried chicken, try Mary Browns on 137 Ave and 96 St. Much better food, clean, and friendly service.

EEK is right! I haven't been here for a while but am so disappointed as many others now. I went past here and planned on getting the taco salad for it's famous fluffy, crunchy shell. NOPE, I went through drive thru and the guy there said the total was $5 and change for this and 3 flatbread chicken tacos?? He repeated this total again at the window and I questioned it and he said, I meant $15(and change). I thought the bag for the taco salad was strangely small. No shell, just a few chips beside the lettuce and blah on the salad plus 3 sliced browning avocados. Yuk. I pulled around to the parking lot and went in. I asked for 2 flatbread steak tacos instead and may not return to Del Taco again unless the salad is made the same way as before again. I explained to the guy that took my order I wasn't going to eat that crap and he said they are getting this reaction from a lot of people. He graciously gave me the steak flatbreads instead. They were only .01 difference in price too getting 2 of those at 2.79+tax.

This DD is located in a gas station and they are super nice! The are ready to take your order and I never feel rushed. I ordered a breakfast sandwich( sausage and egg on an everything bagel) and it came out warm and ready to eat. The bagels here are always so soft fresh.

I also ordered six donuts for my family. I forgot the lady's name but she was helpful and answered all my questions about the donuts. I ordered coffee as well and they always make my coffee the correct way.

I will be back to this DD because of their friendly attitude, great customer service and of course delicious bagels, donuts and coffee.

The dark hair lady who took our order should not be in customer service at all. She spoke like she hated her job and hated dealing with customers. Every time we ordered one thing she gave us a total and we weren't done ordering. How hard is it to ask a paying customer "is that all for you". We ask for the Louisiana hot meal and the lady states that will be 20 minutes. 20 minutes? Making chicken fast is the whole point of the job. Then she made is sound like a total inconvenience. We don't ever mind paying $65.00 for a meal but is it to much to ask for some excellent customer service?

My wife and I decided to go to the recently remodeled White Castle in Maryland Heights. I don't think we have ever gone together before, but we'd seen the advertisement for the crab cake slider and decided to give it a try.

The restaurant was very clean, and the manager and staff were very nice and greeted us warmly as we walked in the door. The kitchen is open and you can easily see the staff preparing your food: also very clean.

The crab cake sliders were very good: they had a hint of sweetness to them. My wife got a chicken and waffle slider, and thought it was OK, but not as good as the crab cake slider.  I paired my crab cake slider with a double cheese slider, and then went back and chased that with an original White Castle. We also shared a small clam strip snacker and they were quite good also.

A pleasant experience, and we'll go back!

I like this Del Taco, for under $5 bucks I get a bean tostada & a turkey taco. I ask for extra lettuce, it's always crisp and fresh. My favorite little cheapie tacos plus they have ground turkey, and they do not charge for extra lettuce. It's good here
Why is the chicken so small here?  It's crazy!  Customer service here sucks....they always forget something!  I always end up having to go back...

Came here around 745am and ordered:
a medium iced hazelnut coffee with cream and sugar-it was made the way I like it!

Breakfast sandwich(everything bagel with sausage and egg no cheese) yum always the best I should have asked for the bagel to be toasted but other than that it was good

Has browns- love them I just wish the package was bigger haha I love potatoes ( sorry not sorry- lol)

Total: $7.81

So I eat here about twice a week give or take, and almost every time I go through the drive thru the soda is nothing but coloured seltzer & if I order a double (or triple) whopper with cheese why is there only "ONE" piece of cheese? Does the price of extra slices come out of their personal pockets? I really wish they'd step it up a bit.

I love Del Taco as a chain, I frequently drive from mountain home to Boise in the middle of the night just because of a craving. But this store by far is the absolute worst I've ever been to both service and quality. It is so inconsistent with the food it's hard to believe I'm at the same place sometimes. I have had to wait in the drive thru up to a half hour only to have my order wrong. Missing items in my bag having to turn back a few times, The fries seem to either be not cooked enough, old but sometimes fresh just being fair, but it's a gamble every time. My final straw was my visit last night, I ordered an epic carne asada burrito and it literally was a regular plain bean burrito previously made reopened and a little carne asada added in that's it... just Terrible, cold and half the normal size, not to mention missing items once again. I immediately drove to another Del taco on Franklin because I've had enough poor service from this place and I ordered the same one with a complete night and day difference... size, quality and appearance, it was exactly like the menus description and freshly made. I wish I took a side by side picture, I will never go back to the Broadway store again, way too many chances given. I Highly recommend the store on the corner of orchard and Franklin, perfect every time even if it's more out of my way... It's worth it!

Toonie Tuesday has now become something like $3.49 Tuesday for 2 pieces of chicken and fries.  That's probably the only thing they sell on Tuesday and I recently bought it.  I'm not a big fan of greasy KFC but once in a year, I have it and hope I don't feel disgusting from the grease I ingested.  

Inside the restaurant you can find the KFC menu items and Taco Bell.  Over the years I have eaten here and I find the tables aren't cleaned promptly and just dirty.  They could use a good renovation to replace the 20-30 year old tables and chairs.

I would suggest getting take out or go through the drive thru.  Last time we asked and they ran out of ketchup.  What fast food place runs out of ketchup? 

So many other delicious foods to eat, KFC won't be my next meal anytime soon.

I love Chipotle and the ingredient quality is consistent from store to store; my vegetarian burrito bowl will always taste fantastic.  However, at this location, the store was visibly dirty, out of toilet paper in the women's restroom, and the make line was running a little slow.  Granted, this location is brand new and its employees are still learning the ropes, but the restroom and lack-of-cleanliness are not items I really can give a free pass over.  I am thinking this store will catch up to its fellow locations after a few months, or am hoping anyways...especially since it is a convenient walk up the Monon for me and my sofritas-smitten partner. Bonus= the large corner table's unique design
Every time I eat here, the chicken and steak are downright sexy. They generate a perfect harmony in my mouth region. The workers are always happy and super nice too.
The food was great but the portions were very little I asked for steak they only put like 4 steak pieces it was literally the smallest burrito I ever got in my life and I am not going there again

I've been there twice since 1/2014: I have never seen a fast-food crew so committed to doing a good job! I got there late one night and placed a large order. I had no idea the place was closing in 15 minutes. The staff made no mention of that. Instead, they started my order, all the while updating me on time, offering me a free refreshing drink while I waited, and working hard. When my order was finished, the quality and freshness of the fried chicken was DELICIOUS!!  Went back a few weeks later and the quality was the same!  Try this place...you won't regret it.
Lots of new employees in there, but they are very nice!!  Chicken was damn good too!

We'll start our review with a charming haiku

Best tacos in town
Beef, pork, chicken, goat, oh god
This place is da bomb
#yelppoem

Tacos Del Rey is much better than Bakersfield with the added bonus of a Taco Bell-like price. If you are looking for tacos that will literally keep you up at night then look no further. Menu includes authentic Mexican meats and veggies from the Mexican grocery store attached.

This place is a joke they never have enough chicken made I tried to order two of there large boxes of chicken they had a 20 min wait then the manager got such an attitude she refused to serve me I have been to this store to many times and nothing is ready doesn't matter what time it is im over it I went to Popeyes chicken they are always ready my advise to KFC fire your managers they don't have a clue

I admit it, I love Del Taco's taco salads. My rating is for the food and service we've received when they are actually open. My husband works nights and Del Taco is on his way home from work. For over 6 months they have been closed whenever he's tried to get an order there. Their signs say 24 hours, the website says 24 hours, you call and they say they never close. Not true. They are not open after around 2:00 am anymore. I emailed corporate several times and got no response. I'm not sure what is going on with this place, but they have lost consistent customers in us. I know that emails to corporate are usually forwarded to the owner or district manager. Apparently they don't care that this location has changed its hours without notifying the patrons or changing the signs or website. Too bad. Del Taco, it was good knowin' you.

The last two times at this restaurant our food has not been so good. We go there because of new GMO publicity. The steak has been full of fat and tonight service was horrible. I got a bowl which was turned upside down. This insured my 1.75 guacamole was now part of the lid. The girl who did this said, " well it is mixed up for you." This is not what the objective was. The man  starting orders was pushing people through the line so fast that he forgot to drain the beans, and the tortilla was mush. The lid on the bowl was covered in liquid and so was the bag; however, we are not sure if it was the tossed bowl or the mushy burrito. One more thing...the girl preparing the meat, well most of her long blonde hair had come out of her braid so much I expected to find it in my food. Thankfully that was not the case. Worse is they just did not care. Apparently by the reviews I am not the only one complaining.

Ok this KFC can cook some chicken which we all know is rare in some cities.  Lately I have been ordering the chicken littles.  The staff is always friendly in the drive thru ( I never go in) and they are pretty fast!  The only time I have to wait is when I order 25 chicken little's which is understandable.  I am from Wisconsin where KFC is delicious and this KFC is just as good in Reno so I guess I'll see you again tomorrow guys!!  Thanks!

Horrible service. I went in on a very slow Sunday after a twelve hour shift to order a STEAK burrito. There was no line and the girl making my burrito was distracted by her coworker,  who was talking to her while i went through what i wanted on my burrito causing me to repeat myself and also prevent her from putting items on that i didn't want. When I got to the register the rude eye rolling girl who interrupted the whole process asked "what he get?" and the girl making it said ummm, chicken. (I honestly,  and perhaps foolishly, corrected her and said steak.) The cashier just looked at me with a blank stare... no "thank you" or "you're welcome" (because i did for some reason say thank you). Straight up rude employees...bye Felicia! I'll take my money elsewhere.

I was hesitant to provide a review because I hate to sound too negative but honestly, this has to be one of the worst sites in the KFC chain. If it isn't, I'm completely dumbfounded at how this chain is even still around. As for the Tanque Verde location, it is continuously ill prepared for customers as they are constantly out of most everything. Today, the wait for chicken was twenty minutes. So while I wait, in a filthy restaurant by the way, with four, of the six employees out back smoking behind the dumpster, I ask to purchase a drink. Guess what, yep, out of 16oz cups!!! The gentlemen next to me asks for his receipt, and I swear I'm not making this up, they are out of the paper the receipt is printed on. If this were an isolated experience at this location, you wouldn't hear a word from me but that's far from the case. It's always something here. Unfortunately, my children like KFC....so I often find myself stuck here giving it another chance. I've yet to be pleasantly surprised. I would like to say that the two employees, that were actually working inside the place, were friendly, apologetic, and disappointed at their situation. Sounds like management has set this place up for failure. If I were the owner of this franchise, I'd find something else to do because representing KFC is not my forte!!!

Got $24.00 worth of chicken today. Lots of money for 8 pieces of the greasiest awful chicken I have ever had. 

Someone from corporate better get over there and find out what is going on. 

Horrible!!

Not bad for fast food. The breakfast is what I usually eat at fast food restaurants, the value at this location is great. Speed of service is not so great, they seem to cater to the drive thru and leave inside customers standing at the counter. that's the reason for my two star rating.

Husband was craving fried chicken. Too bad this KFC was out of breasts and thighs unless we wanted to wait at least 20 minutes. This is not the first time this has happened at this location. How does KFC run out of chicken so often?

This place must be going out of business and should be based on the reviews.  We just got a 10-pc. chicken bucket and asked for half crispy and half original.  Just got original but that was O.K. except it was 5 pcs. of legs and 5 pcs. of thighs - no breasts and no wings.  I called the phone number on the receipt to complain and the number is "out of service".  I looked them up on the Internet and all of the listings are the same phone number???  What a way to run a business but as I said before, they probably won't be much longer.

Having just opened this place definitely has room for improvement.   The employees need to fine tune their skills in all areas.  I got a few tacos and the guy broke them all and had to start over.  Take it easy dude!  Plus all my ingredients fell out when I took them out of their foil.  Oh and they were soggy.  In addition to their manhandling of the food there's also the register confusion.  How do I ring a gift card?  Where is the side of guac?  Why isn't this ringing up right?  Then it takes a few employees to figure it out.  The line was crazy long yesterday and I was starving...and cranky.  I'm sure they'll work out the kinks and have things running smoothly in due time.  I just hope it's soon bc I do love me some carnitas!
This is the best Chipotle in the City.  It's busy , but fast.  The crew is cool and I always get service with a smile.   The steak is the best here too, with generous servings.

I love chipotle so this review is about the service. Let's just say I have never walked into Chipotle and had the workers roll their eyes at me (cashier in particular). I get it, you're tired and you want to go home, but all I want is a steak bowl. You made me feel like an evil person for wanting one at 9:50pm lmao.

I prefer Qdoba to Chipotle - but in a pinch this newly opened location (next door to a Starbucks, and down the strip from Trader Joe's) is OK for a quick bowl of carbs & fat.

The chicken was over cooked, so maybe next time I will try the steak.

Guacamole is good.

Okay, okay, four stars is probs a little grade inflation-y, but this KFC helped me accomplish my mission of having a delicious fast food lunch. I love cole slaw, and Rei R.'s recent review reminded me that I haven't been to a KFC in at least a decade. (At the KFC/Taco Bell/Pizza Hut combo joints that are so popular in the Midwest, I always go Taco Bell.) 

I roll up through the drive thru and order myself a large slaw, large diet soda, and a Chicken Little sandwich. The young woman who took my order was super friendly. In no time at all, I received my food. And it was glorious. Best restaurant (or grocery store) slaw I have had in a long time. My time in the ATL notwithstanding, I prefer Pepsi to Coke, and my diet Pepsi was so bubbly fresh. The Chicken Little gave me equally as much pleasure as a Wendy's junior bacon cheeseburger, which is my standard for dollar-ish menu items. 

Would I want to dine in? Probably not: that's for Raising Cane's, even though their slaw is so sub-par. But next time I need some slaw and a sammie and I'm alone, the Damonte Ranch KFC will be my choice.

This is the worst Chipotle location. You can tell the employees are trained to skimp on the food I stared weighing the food I get from this and other stores. Every time I go to this store my plate is no less then 25% smaller then any other chipotle. Today was the works I paid $12 for a double steak salad and todays weight 50% less then the one I got at a store across town 2 days ago. Don't bother with this store they rip you off.
I will never go back 1. Never have chicken pot pies,i asked for lemonade and it was an .30 up charge ....2. My chicken breast was dry as hell .3 i was told correct change only wth.

Generally solid quick service Mexican. I had the barbacoa burrito while my wife and kid had steak burrito bowls. Everything was well prepared and tasty. I'm not a big fan of the pressurizing burrito line but the service was still friendly and fast. My one complaint is the about half of the time we come here, some of the chips are just too salty to be eaten.

This KFC is why they are going out of business!  The worst window staff ever, looked like they hired street people who were totally disorganized. I was in drive thru for 10 mins and got to the window. This unshaven scruffy guy said "Huh.. I forgot to tell you we don't have the original chicken, but you can get crispy... or you can wait 20 mins and we will cook some?"  I cancelled the order and went to Churches where they don't lie, are more professional and treat you with respect. Stay away from all KFC in Tucson. They are terrible!

I have a hard time understanding how a chicken place never has what is on the menu. We have been to this KFC several times thinking they may have had a rush, after today that's not likely. This store has to the worst KFC I know of. Today will be our last visit!
No original recipe for the 5th visit.

KFC (8730 156th St, Edmonton, Alberta)
We have eaten there for years! We usually buy a chicken meal, beef Burritos and Chalupas. This is what we received (for $31.98):
1. 2 Beef Burritos - NO MEAT
2. 2 Steak Chalupas - 4 shavings OF STEAK.
3. 4 piece chicken meal - 7 or 8 French Fries! (And extremely dried out chicken)
I called and demanded to speak to the supervisor. She said "You only get 1 scoop for a Dollar Burrito". I told her that this is NOT 1 scoop. It is a teaspoon! She told me I would have to come back - tonight.
The Supervisor hid in her office.
The guy at the counter said "That's what you get" when I showed him the Burrito! I said - are you kidding me! So he wrapped it and grabbed a scale. Hmmm...then he said "She's right."
I spent over 40 minutes driving there (and back) to get my $$$ back!!!!No apology from the Supervisor, no offer of a replacement meal, NOTHING. So I left STARVING and pissed off!! I hope the owner noticed a loss in profits and starts to realize that people have discovered that A & W has better chicken for a better price. Their staff also appreciates their customers!

I went here last night and I easily had thee worst service at a fast food restaurant in my life. Went to the drive thru, ordered my food. and the guy handed me my bag with nothing in it but my side. He then went back to what he was doing. Mind you. I ordered $16 worth of food. Then he came back and said, "what's wrong", I said where is the rest of my order? He said what else did you get. As I'm showing him my receipt he kept saying, "what else did you get?"..

Like clearly it should be on his monitor or computer. He ended up handing me three different bags with my order in it. I also ordered the Nashville chicken and it was sitting in a watery substance. 

Don't go here. The food sucked too btw.

They were out of the Halloween Whopper and it wasn't even Halloween yet. But you don't go to fast food for the ambiance and the company. You go because you are desperate. You have needs that must be met. A hole to be filled. And you're willing to accept just about anything. That's fast food. That's burger king. Why are you even reading this? You know what it is. It tastes good while intoxicated and will help you out in the morning. Case closed.

The good: chicken and waffle slider wasn't too bad.

Meh: Fries were almost crispy.

The bad: long wait time, chicken in the chicken bbq slider wasn't crispy. 

Overall, three stars might be a bit generous. But it's right around the corner from my house so I'm remaining wishful that I find something on the menu that is good.  Otherwise this place falls into the category of "I've been drinking and just need something fast"

A previous reviewer said they are heavy scoopers here - and they weren't wrong! I've been to many Chipotle's, but here's one where I feel like the amount of food that got in my goal was actually worth the price. I found the steak a bit spicier than other Chiptoles, so I probably wouldn't get steak there again, but hey, it also could have just been the day I went.

Eat your heart out Duncan Hines..Play your yo-yo heart out Duncan Toys..When it comes to a true donut, a solid contender has been and forever will be: Dunkin' Donuts!

In order to horribly time stamp myself, I have been enjoying the world of Dunkin Donuts periodically for about 30 years and even today it tastes just as it did when I was a wee lad (Sorry in the St. Patrick's day spirit right now). One thing that is important to me in the culinary world is consistency and that has always been a staple with them.

I'm happy to be the initial reviewer of this location because I do feel it needed to be recognized. Coming from the West Coast it's funny to see these literally on every other corner in the East Coast yet each oddly unique in its own way. The staff were very cordial and that of course lead to just a very inviting atmosphere which is always a blessing nowadays (especially in this millennial society).

Although I've never gotten too far out of the norm, I was taken back by how diverse their menu really is. There are your standard hot and cold drinks, sandwiches and wraps, of course the world famous donuts and munchkins, but also a ddsmart menu for anyone who dares count calories in a donut shop. That's like taking a bull to a China store. Ha-ha!

Drink wise on this outing I noticed there was a Valentine's special for a fudge brownie iced coffee and you can't tell me that doesn't scream must try? As shown in the picture there was a hefty layer of chocolate syrup collected on the bottom, but once mixed around it felt like a massive sea of chocolatey goodness with a hint of coffee..I must admit the sweet tooth in me loves how Dunkin tends to be more on the sweet side vs. coffee after tone like a Starbucks with their coffee (all in preference I suppose)?

Food wise, I decided to try for the first time a breakfast sandwich (sausage egg and cheese to be exact) and wow was that a slam "Dunkin" (lord I apologize for that pun). The sausage had a slight twang of spice and prepared with just enough "char" to get you wigglin' as each bite is consumed. The egg was soft and had a very rich creamy taste to it (buttery almost) and partnered with the on point texture of the yolk was a triple double indeed! Go figure the highlight for me was actually the cheese and the croissant itself. The cheese was ooey gooey and the croissant was light and flaky. All in all just a well prepared sandwich and shows how they have evolved since even I remember them.

To save the best for last and a guaranteed must with every visit is their munchkins (donut holes). I don't know what it is, but each bite always entranced me in nostalgia and I felt young again. Their donuts are far from an oily mess and just so soft even if not consumed in that very moment. I'm a sucker for the glazed and jelly filled munchkins yet all flavors are championship material. This would definitely be a last meal request for sure!

In a world that has been turned upside down daily it's always nice to be able to have that go to meal that can tune out the world. For me, Dunkin Donuts was that place and still is that place which is a beautiful thing! This location does that legacy darn proud, amen to you!

Went on a Friday at 4:30 and we received completely dried out, old chicken strips, chicken breast and potato wedges.  So disgusted. This is not the first time we've had crappy food from this location and we won't return.

I would not say this was the best or worst meal I've ever had. It was simple OK. I had a two piece original recipe chicken meal with coleslaw and a biscuit. I like that the original recipe is just crisp enough but not a ton of breading that masks the chicken. The biscuit had a nice flavor and was moist. The coleslaw was basically soup. I had one bite and thew it away. If you are craving quick fried chicken, this is a decent stop but I would skip the coleslaw!

Not have again: the bacon/egg cheese biscuit was greasy and not very tasty. Back in the 1970s, I had my first biscuit sandwich at a Hardee's in Virginia and it was delicious! This is only the second time since then that I've been at Hardee's for breakfast, and my conclusion is to go to McDonald's for biscuit sandwiches. I also tried the chicken fried steak biscuit and it had very little meat and not worth having again. I was sorry to give them one star since I wanted to have the wonderful biscuit sandwich I had 40 years ago, but no luck.

Came here with my little cousin. I got a $5 fill up with the chicken pot pie. Very good. Very filling. It comes with a cookie.
My little cousin got the one piece kids strip meal & they gave her two strips because they felt the strips were kind of small, she couldn't even finish one.
Some of the employees saw how much she had eaten (she also ate some of my pot pie) and they gave her a cookie of her own for free. Everyone is very friendly and super helpful.

Every now and then, I get in the mood for fried chicken.  This KFC will probably keep me out of that mood for a while.  

I walked in at about 8 pm, and there was one other couple and myself as customers.  But there seemed to be a real problem in the kitchen finding food for our orders.  It really did take way too much time to be served.  Then, when I got my family meal home, I discovered that it was 2/3 wings.  Seriously, what kind of bird did this come from?

I don't know that I would call them the King of Tacos, but our meal was solid.  The carne tacos had quite a bit of fat to remove, but even after taking it out, there was plenty of meat left.  Go with the "spicy carne" which is not at all spicy, but cut into strips instead of small pieces that make it easier to avoid the fatty parts. 

Chicken tacos had a nice flavor and appeared to be all white meat.

Sopas were disappointingly bland and the texture was much more like a flat tamale than a sopa.

Their pineapple aqua fresca was amazing and comes in a huge mug. 

Prices were very reasonable - I second the review that compares them to Taco Bell prices.  

Wait staff speaks only Spanish (or they were totally clowning on me), but are very sweet and polite as you stumble through mangled Spanish or give up entirely and point at the pictures.  The menu is Spanish and English.

Only three stars because:
- I found a steak knife in the bathroom that I'm pretty sure they use to unlock the toilet paper holder.  Extreme ick factor when I received cutlery that included the same style of knife.
- Not filthy, but not super clean.
- Limited menu with no appetizers (no chips and salsa, guac, etc).  
- Sopa was disappointing.

KFC is KFC. Sure, anyone above 35 will tell you how great they used to be back in the day (they were).  And anyone today will tell you how far they've fallen from the lofty ideal the colonel penned once upon a time. But occasionally you stumble on an exception. I've eaten here about a dozen times in the last couple years. Every time the food was fresh and the service was good. Sunday morning at 11? Perfect. Tuesday night after work?  No problem. Even Friday night for an 11:30 crack like chicken fix. A part of me worries that I've just been lucky and the next time around them find a way to sabotage my optimism, but alas that hasn't come to fruition yet. They do a good job here (phenomenonally excellent job by KFC standards) and people shouldn't hesitate to get their fix here if they happen to be in the neighborhood.

Very good location. Very busy store. I had a problem Witt a kinda snooty employee. I didn't get her name but she was the skinny white anorexic one. Cute but mean haha. Anyways no chicken beats KFC chicken!

This is the absolute slowest fast food restaurant I have ever been to.  I am writing this in the drive-through and my car has not moved for the last 8 1/2 minutes.    Let's face it, they sell fried chicken and tacos; not exactly gourmet. So what does a place like this have to offer? Two things only. Low-cost options and speed of getting your food and moving on.    And when you can't get one of your two primary benefits correct, you're in trouble.    But hey, the price is the price and the food is fairly consistent. So, if you've got a ton of time to waste and you are really "jonesing" for something south of the border or something finger licking good, take your chances

We've gone here quite a bit over the past few years. Tonight, as usual, great service and good food. Our burritos are big--double, sometimes triple, meat with all the other stuff--and it's hard to wrap it. The employees at Chipotle have never offered to wrap them with two tortillas like they have for us at Q'doba, but they are have been happy to do that for us on the few occasions that we wanted a real burrito instread of a sloppy mess in foil. We would always go to Q'doba (for the wheat tortilla, free quac, and delicious queso) but the steak at Chipotle just has better flavor and we live way closer to a Chipotle..

I'm not really sure what friggin moron would compare White Castle to In N' Out Burger other than an absolute invalid. The two are COMPLETELY different. One is a delicious fast food greasy west coast burger, the other is a delicious fast food greasy Midwest slider. Both are fantastic, however sticking your nose up at White Castle does not make you saint or a martyr, nor does it make you or the area you live In special in any way. It makes you an asshole. 

SIGH. Anyway, this location is slow. Like really, really slow. I sat in the drive through 20 minutes and I was the only one there. The food was still hot when I got home and our order was correct. Try the mozzarella sticks, they're great.

Let just start off I was not feeling good so I decided I was gonna get some chicken from kfc. So I get a 8 piece chicken only which I paid 14.99 didnt ask for biscuit.  I think that is too much for chicken only.  I get home to eat and the pieces are so small like I got rip off. Never got a receipt. I will never eat at kfc again the chicken tasted like shit kfc you have gone down hill. If I could I would give you no stars

My new favorite location!  Today was my first visit to this particular Chipotle and it is officially my favorite!  The staff truly hustles and my burrito bowl was excellent.  I recognized a familiar face on the staff from another area restaurant and I'm very glad she's at Chipotle if she can't be at her former employer.  I ordered the steak burrito bowl and the meat was spicier than other locations but I don't mind that (I think it's actually supposed to be spicy in the first place).  This spot is much closer to work, making my lunch break last much longer.

Definitely my to go place for dinner. Fast and affordable.
I usually get the bowl with steak.  However, it would be nice if the food was warmer. By the time I sit down and eat the food it is usually medium warm:
TIP: Always ask for extra rice because they will give it to you.

The last two times I've been to KFC I ordered the leg and thigh pieces of chicken. I open up the box at home only to find leg and breasts. I'm beginning to wonder if the employees even know the difference between the parts of the chicken. Maybe if there's a next time, I'll have to double check my order which makes it so inconvenient.

Wait wasn't terribly long which is the reason I gave another star,but this location isn't great.last night,we got all our taco bell stuff,but they forget to give us my boyfriend's KFC stuff,which luckily,was sitting right there when he went in to get it,and they skeeted him on the chicken he got(which looked like end of the batch tiny stuff).the tacos we got were just leaking grease(the paper was covered),and 1 tacos was just shell,and meat.literally.probably won't be back.

Great dunkin donuts for when you are in a rush. I came in this morning around 8:00 to get my iced coffee with caramel and cream & a sausage egg & cheese croissant sandwich. The ordering process was quick and efficient. The manager asked me if I was sure I did not want hot coffee with my sandwich and hash brown combo, which I appreciate because she was covering all of her bases and making sure I was aware of the combo details.
I'm pretty sure I didn't wait past 5 seconds before they called my # with my order, WOW, the speed of lightning!
My sandwich was perfectly prepared which was great. I don't have high expectations when it comes to chains so my standards are lowered when I come into DD, but they pulled through! Also my coffee was perfectly sweet, just the way I like my DD :)

Of course it isn't "Mexican food" in that you get REAL, AUTHENTIC fare from south of the border. This is Del Taco(DT) - total, typical Americanized fast food that is unhealthy, greasy, and OH SO GOOD!! The service at this particular DT on Broadway is fine. I've never had one issue with the peeps here. They are attentive and competent. Everytime I get what I order and the food is delish! I give DT five stars as a company because every item I have had is so far superior to Toxic Hell, it's main competitor. My only wish: PLEASE COME TO TWIN FALLS!!!

After waiting 25 minutes in the drive thru I had at least hoped they got my order correct. Somehow a chicken place "forgot" to put chicken in a Famous Bowl. It's literally mashed potatoes, corn, gravy, cheese AND chicken. Really not that hard.

This place really is pretty bad. I ordered grilled Chicken breast. Ummmmm...no grilled Chicken breast. In fact no grilled chicken at all! They were "out". OK, I'll have a side of mashed potatoes, no gravy. I got home and mashed potatoes WITH gravy. So there you go!

In my definitive ranking of fast-food chicken (and boy, is it definitive), KFC doesn't even come close to the top. But when it comes to their coleslaw, I am a certified KFC TRUTHER; easily the best in the fast-food chicken tier, bar none. 

So far, my self control and better judgement outpace my slaw love, so I really only pop in once a month to grab a family meal to-go on lazy nights when no one feels like cooking. And usually, things are a-ok. All 11 ~secret~ herbs and spices are well accounted for. Chicken: decently crispy yet glistening from grease as the good lord intended. Rarely is there ever reason to expect more out of a fried chicken franchise... except when they flub your order and leave you slaw-less. The first time, they accidentally swapped it with mac; disappointing but forgivable. My last visit, they forgot it altogether. This would be much less of an issue if slaw wasn't the CRUX of my KFC meals.

Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, I'll concede to being a doofus but DAMN, quit holding out on the slaw. Obviously, the easy answer is to do a double triple take through the bag to make sure all sides and fixings are accounted for before peacing out the door, but I'd also love to believe in good faith that my order won't be incomplete or bungled every single visit. Sure, mistakes happen, but they're not any easier to forgive when they're expected. Other issues from this location include dwarfish wings/thighs and iffy cleaning practices, but really nothing compares to standing between a man and his coleslaw. FREE THE SLAW, KFC. It's what the colonel would have wanted.
They have 35 cent tacos!  That is a very good deal, even if it is similar to Taco Bell.  My boyfriend says "It's so much better than Taco Bell!!!"
Standard Chipotle fair; however, do not go to this store after 7 PM or they will likely be out of what you want.  They are frequently out of chips or guacamole or steak, etc after 7 PM.

If you come into Del Taco expecting Mexican food, you will be sorely disappointed.  While they have some Mexican type food, Del Taco and its ilk is Americanized fast food Mexican.

Within that category, Del Taco stands hands and shoulders above the rest.  Taco Bell?  Barely edible.  Taco John's?  Disgusting.  Del Taco uses fresh, basic, ingredients to pull together tasty and semi-healthy food.  When choosing fast food options - any fast food options - Del Taco is always one of my top two choices.

As the reviews and stars indicate.....the service here is at best....very slow and unorganized.  Seems of they have more then one order at time they overwhelmed.
It seems that overall KFC is in trouble. There are very few around that don't seem to be struggling and in disrepair. 
Hopefully the owners will start to take a hard look at the miserable service they expect people to endure to get chicken that has become less of a quality to its competitors. ...both Popeyes and Churches seem to have surpassed KFC in service and quality of food.
I could easily say never again at any location....however my wife does like the occasional visit.

Visited this location for the first time yesterday around 7:30 pm. Parking lot is definitely weird & hard to maneuver.  Place was fairly busy. Line moved quickly.  Staff was super fun & helpful.  Offered me samples.  Burrito bowl with carnitas was awesome.  Chips were slightly too salty but salsa (medium) was great. I can't wait to return!

Never been inside the restaurant itself but the drive thru is efficient enough. Food is done in a good amount of time. Always hot. And this is the first BK I've tried the whopper. After trying the flame grilled burger for the first time I gave up McDonald's burgers completely, big burger and cheaper than the competition you can't beat it.

I love food with a conscience and the young people who work here have that, too. I walked in on a powwow about the carnitas shortage and how the staff was supposed to explain to customers that Chipotle is having issues sourcing unsustainably grown pork.  Instead of losing their integrity, Chipotle decides to pull back a bit until better sources have been found.  The gal preparing my salad says under her breath to no one in particular: "I want to be a Chipotle farmer.." Thank you, Chipotle for not missing the point and in effect, educating your young staff about why good, clean food matters.

I've been to different Chipotles a combined six or seven times and my most recent visit to the Sparks Chipotle was the best. I liked the tacos better than Chipotle burritos. I had a coupon. I got three steak tacos and got the steak salad for free. I added the corn salsa to the salad and that was tasty. The ingredients overall are fresh. One problem was that my steak, both in the tacos and salad, was tough to chew. And their ordinary salsa is bland.
The staff was friendly. The restaurant was clean.

This KFC needs to be close or get new management because they're giving KFC a bad name as a franchise.

 I went to shop at Franklin Mills Mall yesterday, November 1, after shopping at jcp my friend and I decided to walk outside the mall and look for a place to eat. For our surprise KFC was nearby so we said, let's sit down and eat at KFC. As soon as we enter the place it didn't look very welcome neither appealing to eat, but well I just said "What the heck, let's give them a try". WOW, what a BIG mistake we did. First, I notice the there was no one at the register as we enter the place. There was a young girl cleaning a table and she 2 minutes after we enter the place she noticed that we were waiting and she ran to the register to take our order. Once she took our order I noticed that she went in the back to prepare our food. WAIT, WAIT, you just touched money and going to prepare our food. NO WAY! However, I noticed she put gloves to prepare the food but I didn't like the simple fact that she went from cleaning the table, to manage money, to prepare food. This tell you that this place has very poor management or not management at all. Our food was NOT FRESH! In the back kitchen you could see like 3 more people and, a lady with the red shirt talking loud to the young girl in the front, while also taking orders from the Drive thru. The place looks a mess, the lack of communication was very visible and that's BAD BUSINESS. 

Now the food. 

The food was awful. The chicken was very salty, cold, greasy and old. I seems like the food was cooked hours ago. The boneless chicken I ordered was very salty, I've got 2 pieces with fries and a biscuit. The fries were also salty, greasy and old. Honestly, I didn't bother the taste the biscuit and I didn't finish the chicken because all the salt I tested every time I took a bite, plus the grease. 

As we were leaving the place one person from the kitchen came out to smoke a cigarette. And people that owns these places, PLEASE, PLEASE, try NOT to hire people that smoke cigarette and are going to work in the kitchen. That is very disgusting to me because many of them go back to the kitchen and they don't even bother to wash good their hands. This was a very bad experience and I will never go back to this KFC.      



Spanish:

Este KFC tiene que cerrar o conseguir una nueva administración porque están dando mala fama a KFC como una franquicia .

 Fui a comprar en Franklin Mills ayer, después de las compras en el JCP mi amiga y yo decidimos caminar fuera del centro comercial y buscar un lugar para comer. Para nuestra sorpresa KFC estaba cerca, así que dijimos , vamos a sentarnos y comer en KFC. Tan pronto como entramos en el lugar no parecía muy bienvenido ni muy agradable para comer, pero dije "Qué diablos , vamos a tratarlo a ver" . WOW, esto fue un gran error que hicimos. En primer lugar, me doy cuenta de que no había nadie en el caja de registro al entrar en el lugar. Había una muchacha limpiando una mesa y ella, 2 minutos después de que entramos en el lugar se dio cuenta de que nos encontrábamos esperando para que nos atendieran, y ella corrió hacia la caja para tomar nuestro pedido. Una vez que nos atendió la misma muchacha fue a la parte posterior para preparar nuestra comida. Espera, espera, ella acaba de tocar el dinero y va a preparar nuestra comida . ¡Qué RAYOS! Sin embargo , me di cuenta de que ella se puso los guantes para preparar la comida, pero no me gustó el simple hecho de que ella pasó de la limpieza de la mesa, para administrar el dinero y luego a preparar la comida. Esto deja decir que este lugar tiene muy mala administración y que no hay buena supervisión. ¡La comida NO estaba fresca! En la cocina atrás se podía ver como 3 personas más y una señora morena hablando muy alto a la joven en la parte delantera, mientras que también se encontraba cogiendo órdenes del servi-carro (drive tru). El lugar parece un desastre, la falta de comunicación y era muy visible. Eso es un mal negocio.

Ahora la comida:
La comida era horrible. El pollo estaba muy salado, frío, grasienta y viejos. Me parece que la comida estaba preparado hace horas. El pollo sin hueso que pedimos estaba muy salada , tengo 2 piezas con papas fritas y un biscuit. Las papas fritas estaban muy saladas , grasosas y viejas. Honestamente, no me molesté en probar el biscuit y, no termine de comerme el pollo por toda la sal que probaba cada vez que me comía un bocado. Además de la grasa que tenía el pollo. 

Cuando nos íbamos del lugar, una persona de la cocina salió a fumar un cigarrillo. Y la gente que posee estos lugares , por favor , por favor, trate de no contratar a personas que fuman cigarrillos y van a trabajar en la cocina . Eso es muy desagradable para mí , porque muchos de ellos van a la cocina y ni siquiera se molestan en lavarse bien las manos. Esta fue una muy mala experiencia y nunca voy a volver a este KFC, y lo más seguro no pise otro KFC.

Servers are all very nice. I haven't had too many issues previously but my most recent visit I ordered a sofrita bowl (I'm vegan) and noticed while I was eating that I bit into a piece of steak. Immediately afterwards, I saw there were also pieces of chicken in my bowl. I'm not angry, but I think as far as food safety regulations and what not this location NEEDS to be mindful of not getting their meats mixed up. Should a customer with allergies or certain dietary restrictions (e.g. vegetarians) come in, it could potentially be a big issue.

All right gonna bump this BK up a couple of notches. Since I gave it a second and 3rd try and it appears they cleaned up their act a little.
1) Floors looked a little cleaner
2) Help was preparing foods with disposable gloves 
3) Counter help was efficient and courteous no attitudes this time.
Its hard to hate BKs with all the specials and coupons they give out plus free internet and good parking. I mean where else can 2 peeps eat these days for around 10 bucks (OR LESS!)  on Whopper Wednesday's and Chicken Sandwich Monday's ?  Bathrooms were cleaner too so no complaints and one of the better fast food options in Bucks if you have to do fast foods and your diet can take it!

I ordered an chicken sandwich there about 7 years ago.I only got two chicken patties an a drink they screwed up my order I won't go there no more.I can get fried chicken at Giant food store that's cheaper or Go to Jerry's chicken at Norco

(4) ~ very good food, service & value:)

MY WISH LIST:  
1.) A connecting road between the KOP Mall Lot & the Chipotle Lot, b/c currently you have to drive around it.
2.) A Dessert option (ie Chocolate Chip Cookie;)

MY BURRITO BOWL MENU(To-Go):
** Brown Rice = (4) very good
** Green Peppers-n-Onions = (4) very good
** Black Beans = (4) very good 
** 1/2-n-1/2 (Steak-n-Barbocoa) = (4) very good
** ALL (3) Salsas = (4) very good
** Corn = (4) very good
** Guacamole = (4) very good
SIDE:
** Tobasco "Chipotle" Sauce = (4) very good & smokey

By far the best service and preparation in this area for this company. Everything was put together in a timely and professional manner. I had 6 tacos(hard) 3 chicken and 3 steak... All done perfectly. I was shocked at the difference between this location and Ardmore?? This should be the role model store for some of the others to come and get trained again. If ur in kop it's fairly new and on the main road right on the outside of the main lot.

This is a fine purveyor of authentic Mexican street tacos (after all, they are the King of Tacos).   And it's cheap!   $2 per taco.   I don't know why some people chase down food trucks and stand in line when they could come to a place like El Rey, sit down, have excellent service (brush up on your Spanish because not everyone speak English), and pay less.

There are a lot of other dishes served here, too.   Soups, enchiladas, tortas, sopes, burritos, whole fried fish, etc.

I am generally not one to complain, but every time I go to the KFC on Tanque Verde the experience is awful.  I went back in June as my kids requested chicken from KFC for lunch.  I ordered five different meals and when I arrived home 4 of the 5 were incorrect.  I had gone inside to order as there was quite a line in the drive-thru.  I waited 44 minutes for my order to be completed.  The cashier then explained that the delivery truck had not arrived yet and they had no cups for drinks.  Are you kidding me???  How does a restaurant run out of cups?  I suggested that that would have been good to know when I was ordering...not 45 minutes later.  She did send me home with a half gallon of lemonade as compensation.  She went on to share that they never have enough employees and those they do have aren't trained well.  Yikes!!

You would have thought I would known better than to go back, but my husband requested KFC again this evening.  We went through the drive-thru this time.  We had a very friendly young man assist us; however, the food was terrible.  I had requested a bowl with no cheese...guess what?  There was cheese in the bowl and I had even asked him a second time to be sure there would be no cheese.  He told me he forgot to tell the back, but would go make the bowl himself...guess that didn't happen.  We ordered wedges and tenders as well.  Both were WAY overcooked and dry...not edible!!  Lucky for us the box came with a biscuit and chocolate chip cookie!!  
Beware!  Drive the extra block to Chick-fil-A...it is definitely worth it!

I was pleasantly surprised by how awesome the food was at this local taco joint. We got a bunch of tacos and they were all delicious and as authentic as I've had other than Mexico. Affordable and delicious. We had carry out so can't tell you about the service but if it's half as good as the tacos, it's definitely worth trying it out! Oh, my favorite was the spicy carne asada tacos but all the ones we tried were delicious.

Yes, I fell into the trap of $1.49 for 10 nuggets and it is the worst spent money I have EVER wasted. They are disgusting, worse than what I remember frozen supermarket nuggets to tasting as a child. 

Also I had not had a whopper jr in like 8 - 10 years, I really did think the meat was thicker. This patty was barely there, the burger is tiny, but for $1 that I paid, it was find. The meat tasted the same and at least it brought some veggies in it. The fries were perfect, which is a plus.

This particular location has a indoor play area for kids that was very clean. Actually the whole place was spotless. They also had 4 computers set up for the employment process which was intense, I didn't realize THAT many people applied here. Nonetheless, it is a cheap lunch althernative for the broke and hungry...aka me during xmas season.

This is the worst place to visit, especially the drive through.  Always have to wait at least 15 minutes or longer for chicken and they have you pull around front.  I had to place my order 2 different times in the drive through to go inside and place it a 3rd.  Not one person was there on my last visit and I still had to wait over 15 minutes.  will never go to this one again.

When you feel like the deep fried gut bomb this location is good, if it's convenient. 

I also like the challenge presented by their pricing structure. I'm never sure if I am getting the best price for my chicken.

I seriously discovered my new taco joint!!!! Its quite not the greatest looking taco place but the tacos here are the bomb! Cheap tacos at $2.50 each and let me tell you one tacos was huge! I passed by this place all the time going on Michigan and thought lets give this place a try. Its nestle in a plaza next door to an international market. You can seat yourself and dine in, or just order at the counter. The menu over on the wall was only tacos and tortas, but beware there's a menu on the counter which offer more varieties of other mexican food. If you never had real mexican tacos you probably wont know what the heck is half the stuff on the menu, look on the menu on the counter, it will translate what each taco is in english.It was even hard to communicate to the guy cause he barely spoke english and i found myself repeating myself  We ordered 5 different tacos, along with a carne asada torta. Torta came with fries which was soggy, but it could be because we took it to go and the steam from the container made them that way. This place is hole in the wall so dont expect some fancy dining. If you are in need of authentic mexican tacos this place is it. I recommend my favorites, which is the pastor (pork), lengua (tongue), and misteada (half carne, half chorizo). And let me tell you! The torta was huge!!!! Took us 2 days to finish it! Give this place a try and keep an open mind with what they offer. Its weird of all the animals parts thats going into your taco, but we are culturally open to always trying new things.

I was worried about the poor reviews, but I had a wonderful experience here! I ordered a sausage, egg, and cheese sandwich on an onion bagel and a large iced coffee with coconut flavor, sugar and light cream. The cashier was very friendly and took my order quickly and correctly. My order came quickly too. My sandwich was hot and the bagel was toasty. My coffee was just right, exactly like I ordered it. Next time I am in Tucson, I will be back!
Don't try to order chicken thighs & legs. I have been several times and they always say, "we are out of thighs, can we substitute breasts?"

This is a very rare (ok, maybe never) 5 star fast food joints. However everytime I stop by here throughout the work week  they are quick, polite and consistent.  I wish more fast food stores operated this way.  Burger King is Burger King as far as food goes, but I will drive right past the one on 44 and 141 to go here instead.  

Give it a try, it might make you drive a little further for your whopper! haha

A Chipotle is a Chipotle -- each location is basically the same, and you pretty much get exactly what you're looking for no matter which one you go to. This location is close to my house, so I find myself here pretty often. Parking can be tough as it and the nearby businesses are often busy, but you're in a giant parking lot so you might just have to park farther away than your ideal location. Inside, things are moved along pretty quickly and the staff is friendly enough. I think management might have cracked down on the scoop sizes, though, because my double scoops of chicken and sofritas used to be much more abundant. Bummer! I'll still eat here, though.
It is Mexican food done fast food style.  The Street Taco was tasty, but small.  A bit overpriced for the amount of food you get.  Stick to the daily special menu to get your money's worth.

If you're in any kind of hurry, avoid this place. Especially the drive thru. Never fails, despite short lines they seem to take a ridiculously long time to get through drive thru orders.

Food is okay, typical fast food. Nothing noteworthy. Was what I expected.

The interior is nice, up to date and well kept.

If you need a simple fried chicken fix and aren't in a rush this place works.

I visit this Chipotle at minimum twice a month. My bank account will probably tell you that the truth is closer to once a week.

I honestly have never had a bad experience at this location. The staff is friendly, the line is never too long, and the service is quick. I can make it in and out in less time than it would take me to go through a drive thru.

Do not mistake their speed for lack of quality or service though. I always walk away feeling like I've gotten exactly what I've ordered.  I order the same thing every time I go. Steak bowl, handful of lettuce on the bottom, triple serving of white rice, no beans, mild and corn salsa, extra cheese, extra sour cream and guac on the side. By the time they're done, that bowl is full and heavy! And I don't pay any extra for it. (Aside from the side of guac) If you want more of anything, just ask! They do charge for more meat but aside from that, extra rice, beans, salsa, etc, is free.

Last weekend I came into this location at 9 45, 15 minutes before closing, and aside from the fact that there was team members deep cleaning the grills and parts of the lobby, you never would have known they were closing in 15 minutes.
