The team member that helped me with my food smiled, brushed off my apologies for having a craving so close to closing, and made my order just as perfectly as normal, if not more so. I never once felt like I was a bother or annoyance.

I have spoken to both the manager and the assistant manager at this location (the manager has even made my order on one occasion) and they really are the reason this location is top notch! I live midway between this location and the Reno store but I pick Sparks every single time. The crew is friendlier, they are always happy to accommodate my request for "one more scoop of rice please" and I never have to wait very long.  I love Chipotle and I love the Sparks Chipotle even more!

What's the saying "fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice,  shame on me." What the heck is fool me three times? That's exactly what this Sparks,  NV,  KFC, has done to me. Maybe their motto is "finger licking good, just not what you ordered good." Our first experience,  after driving to this location and ordering 2 piece white meat chicken combo, I get home to find one thigh, one side (should have been two) and no biscuit,  but lots of honey for it. Maybe they didn't realize thigh is dark meat. So we give them another chance, went through the  drive through. After 5 minutes of the person not understanding we wanted crispy white meat, I was then told they are out of crispy, come back in 30 minutes,. Did I come back, yep,  the secret recipe has a hold on me. So this week we're at it again, when mama wants her crispy chicken, she gets it. So off I go again, same routine, drive 5 miles, go through drive through, only to be told, we only have half your chicken in crispy.  Im either having bad luck or going at the same time as all other crispy chicken lovers at this location. I guess next time, I'll find another location for our fix.
Finally, a del taco in Idaho. Way better than taco bell. I love the macho nachos. Pretty good for fake Mexican food / fast food.

I don't understand the chipotle craze. There is not much flavor and menu options are limited. I normally get the hard chicken or steak tacos. None of the ingredients are remarkable at all. An overpriced Taco Bell in my opinion. The staff is always nice and friendly. The bathrooms and the dining room always clean. I eat here twice a month because I work close by.

I'm an odd vegetarian in that I don't really like sandwiches made up of just veggies, ditto for burritos (though if you're ever in Boston, The Purple Cactus does a great New England version). When Chipotle announced they were coming out with the tofu based Sofritas I was excited and a little skeptical. So often tofu based burritos contain large chunks of chewy flavorless tofu. Chipotle did a decent job. Sofritas are marinated in a spicy sauce, and instead of chinks of tofu, it's more like ground beef.  As a large corporation that brands itself on ethical practices in farming and animal care, it would be nice if they extended said practices to the humans who work there and paid them a living wage.

Very friendly staff...in addition to serving up the usual good stuff.
I kind of like the Nashville hot chicken everyone seems to be complaining about.

I really love Chipotle as the food is always fresh, consistent and tasty no matter the location you visit!  Today was no different as my steak burrito was delicious.  Their corn salsa is awesome.  Also like they have a kids taco kit for with a drink for only $4.75 which is a great deal if you have little ones.

Being my first yelp review I can't help but feel as excited as the first time my tender plump lips made contact with a freshly made corn dog from Weinershnitzel, a feeling I will not soon forget.  But, I digress, this review needs to stay focused on a little place from heaven - Del Taco - and not on the Trojan God that offers hot dogs for 25cents on certain days.  So lets get to it....

It was a warm day, the sun was shining.  I knew this because I checked the weather from my Iphone 5 while I played Grand Theft Auto in the dark from my 2 bedroom apartment, yes I said 2 bedroom - Baller!!  It was about 2pm and I felt a rumble, a rumble that for most would have made them wonder how an earthquake just hit Tucson (yes, climate change is real so it is not out of the question).  Good thing for me, I knew that the rumble was my size 44 belly demanding fuel, and certain type of fuel in particular.  I knew that it was not a taco bell type of day, nor would the salty sweet nectar of McDonalds suffice, so I had no other choice but to huff it the two blocks to Del Taco.  By huff it I mean get in my 2005 Kia and drive there.

Upon pulling into the drive through - mean seriously how can any restaurant seat be more comfortable than the soft velvet of the car - I was pleasantly surprised to find no one waiting, so I knew they were efficiently quick.  Gazing into the slightly burned out lighting of the menu board, I struggled to make a decision.  Tacos, Burritos, Salads (hahaha), so much.... aaaahhhh!!  Just when I thought the voice coming from the speaker box was going to kill me for taking so long I spotted it.... a double cheeseburger.  Do I dare?  Can I come up with enough nerve to order a double cheeseburger that resides on a menu at a place with Taco in the name?  I mean, it is not Del Burger, but I couldnt help but be so intrigued with the arrogance this place must have to put a burger on a menu that is saturated with Mexican food items.  Ok Ok, you can do this, just say it....  I will do the Double Del Cheeseburger combo please, with a Dr. Pepper.  "Will that be all," the speaker box crackingly exlaimed.  About to say yes sir, my eyes fell hard in love with what seemed to be a dessert chimmichanga of some sort.  "No, no that will not be it." I screamed.  I will take an order of your cheesecake bites!!  And there it was, my second lunch of the day was underway.  

The Burger:
It was like I was taken back to grade school.  The pickles I added seemed to have been marinating on the bun, and left the green residue on the bleach flour just like I remember from the cafeteria in 3rd grade.  The patties, smashed so thin and well done that there was a crisp, in a good way.  The processed american cheese, I mean, seriously dang delicious.  Like real cheese could taste any better, ha.  Not a chance.  The bun was dry and crumbly which I believe purposely left the meat and cheese as the standout.  The only issue I have is that they did not offer a 4 patty burger, because on this day daddy was hungry. Five stars.

The Fries:
Crinkle Cut, seriously, Crinkle Cut.  Here I though Eegees was the standard in the crinkle cut game, but all this time a crazy taco joint was throwing a perfect game.  The fries were starchy and stale to perfection.  Salted with a pinch too much, yeah right like there is a such thing as too much salt!  Each fry was long and stern, and I had no issues like you find yourself having at Burger King, when you have to bunch up a thousand mini nub like fries together to combine into one actual fry.  Is it possible to request a XXXL size?  #fatboyproblems  Five Stars.

Cheesecake Bites:
Being lactose intolerant I knew I was in for a long night, but the risk of 2 minutes of bliss is well worth 4 hours of toilette sitting... I mean, I could catch up on my need to re-read Harry Potter.  Biting into these hot little pockets of dough, cinnamon, and cream cheese infused with sugar was as delightful as finding a Cadbury Egg under my bed months after Easter had come and gone.  The cheese melted into my mouth as I inhaled one bite after another.  Before I knew it, they were gone.  Five Stars.

Before I have an accident in my pants (I just at Ben and Jerrys... I know I know, I am a gluten for punishment), I will leave my final words on this amazing resource that is yelp.  So I say this, move over In and Out, you have company.

Del taco - 5 stars.  Brava Brava!

Background: We stopped at this place around a month and a half ago, ordered the 12 piece chicken tender bucket (not cheap) also wanted 1 of each of the dipping sauces.  Got the order and took it home.  Absolutely no dipping sauces and only 11 pieces of chicken.  Really for that price?  I called corporate office and they sent us a $10.00 gift card..we waited approx. a month before we went back.  My husband ordered chicken and sides, also told them about the last order.  We should have checked!!! We got home and noticed they left the $4.00 coleslaw (that we were charged for) out!!  Of course the $10.00 gift card didn't cover the order, so we paid cash for the rest.  I called corporate again, they offered me another gift card which I turned down.  I figure they must leave a little something out of each person's order that way they can make more money.  Will never return and I would not recommend anyone eating there unless they don't care that they will be short changed.
I absolutely like del taco but this location is so slow to get food out they should be embarrassed

I have ordered from this Chipotle in town before but I went with my girlfriend to eat in yesterday and I was AMAZED by the customer service. I had eaten prior to arriving but my friend hadn't so I didn't order at this visit yesterday. They offered me a free sample of their steak and it was so yummy. What really blew my mind is that Bella, the cash rep, came to our table and offered me a buy one get on free coupon for our next visit so that I can get more of the steak that I liked so much. I didn't ask for any coupons or hint that I needed one but she went the extra mile to make sure that although I didn't order this time that I'd save a little the next time. Bella, I'm coming back because of your friendliness and attentiveness.

To be honest, I am not a KFC regular. I consider myself more of a patron of the other fried poultry store that has a name in common with a certain cartoon character who eats lots of spinach.   However, I had a few coupons and hadn't had KFC in a while, so my brother and I decided to give it a go for dinner.

We pulled up in the drive through, and ordered a 10 piece bucket of crispy chicken.  They told us that there would be a 9 minute wait.  We said that was fine.  Although secretly, I was wondering if I would regret the decision to come to KFC.  After we paid, they asked us if we wanted a free soda while we waited and they also apologized again.  We were told to park, and the food would be brought out to us.  There was a van next to us, who I assume had to wait as well, since the food was brought out to them.  Once the food was brought out, they apologized again for the wait and told us they gave us extra biscuits.

Although we had to wait, I am impressed by the customer service at this location.  I truly am not one of those jerks who complains about every little thing.  I don't expect to get free stuff for waiting or complaining, but it is nice when a business acknowledges when they are inconveniencing a customer.  They did a good job rectifying the situation, and showed us that they valued our business when we could have gone elsewhere.  

The chicken was a bit greasy, but it's fried chicken right?  It was also reeeeally hot.  Maybe we had to wait a little, but I'm glad to know that the chicken wasn't sitting under a heat lamp all day.  I will be using up all of those KFC coupons I got in the mail.

Warning: under New ownership. After spending over $50 they could not be bothered to include the honey or butter for the biscuits, napkins or plastic ware as I asked for. When I called about them not only NOT including what I made a special point of asking for but they did not even include the basics that normally come with a meal, I was told the honey/and or butter is now by request only. I was then lectured that I should have read the sign they have posted (supposedly). It was dark out and the lighting is so poor on this property even if I had seen a sign I doubt if I would have been able to read it. (They know how bad the lighting Is too as they were robbed at least once last year that I know of and I have previously mentioned how unsafe I feel in their drive thru. Dark and secluded-joy joy:(. ) 

Anyway, after listening to them lecture me, I tried to eat. That was a disaster. The chicken was really fatty ( more than usual) and the coleslaw tasted stale and really really sweet. Made me nauseated. I usually love their coleslaw too. By the morning, everyone that had eaten the coleslaw the night before had major intestinal problems the whole day. I didn't bother t call again as I didn't feel strong enough physically to listen to them somehow tell me I did something wrong not them. I will just make sure to NEVER give them my business again. 

Although I wish the health dept would check them out. I will never understand why they don't have to display their lettered card in the window here in AZ. It seems common sense to me. I like to have some idea of how clean a place is BEFORE I walk in and buy something to eat.

I've been to this Dunkin twice now, and I solemnly swear to never return.  The workflow is alarmingly frustrating.  27 minutes for a sausage biscuit and a coffee.  The staff is concentrating on excuses far before solutions and I've witnessed the most unprofessional behavior I've ever seen.  The manager has a crutch and is clearly stoned.  She keeps saying "it's raining donuts and munchkins" and she's repeated herself enough that I'm becoming concerned.

Chipotle is my favorite "fast food" place as it's actually healthy (minus the sodium) and cheap. I always get the Steak brown rice bowl. Like other reviewers have pointed out, this location does tend to give you bigger servings than average so that's a plus for value. 

This location is next to the 12th St Gym, so you can expect to see a lot of good looking fit customers grabbing a bite after their workout. Good for people-watching if you're into that ;)

I tasted almost all 11 herbs & spices on my chicken.... We ate here over the weekend and we were somewhat satisfied with the food. Some items needed to be tossed out & made fresh instead of being sold to customers. The customer service was great! Order was correct. It would've been much better if the food did not taste like it had been sitting there for forever.  hopefully next time we'll be better and fresh..... If there's a next time.

I agree with all the previous reviews.  How is it that they never have any chicken.  Everytime I go here,  there's always a wait of at least 30 minute for just my chicken.  First off,  how do you not have grilled chicken.  Second,  how do you not have 12 piece dark meat.  I'm waiting on the parking lot and have watched at least 8 cars come and go.  Am I the only one that eats dark meat? When I asked for dark meat only,  the guy was kind of rude and abrupt letting me know that I can't get dark meat only.  So when I asked again for dark meat,  he then said it'll be a 30 minute wait.  This is the last time I'm coming here.  I don't know of any fast food place that makes you wait 30 minutes for your  food.  If you guys don't want to be nice to your customer and you never have what we want to order,  why are you still in business.  Get your acts together cause this KFC is the worst.  I've been to other KFC AND my wait time for grilled or dark meat is never more then 15 minutes.  Yep,  about 5 more cars just came and left and I still haven't gotten my food.  Never again.  I would give this place negative 2 stars if I could

Not bad. I absolutely love chiptole don't get me wrong but I don't know if it because it's in center city and I normally get it in the northeast but their portions were sooooo small.  I got barely any steak in my burrito and it was so tiny compared to my regular.  It was still pretty good.  This one's very crowded but I'm probably just not use to the lunch rush in the city lol

Who runs this place? Obviously some super rich person who doesn't care. Management can't train for .... or an Owner who doesn't train the management... or Corporate never trained this franchisee. Either way it rolls down hill all the way to the bottom. Orders consistently wrong. Food consistently unavailable. Then I'm sure it's not this KFC's fault but adding fuel to the fire, these got to be the little dinky chicken pieces I ever received. A drumstick the size of wing piece. Really? If I'm paying a premium price, I expect better.

Very rarely do I have poor service AND a poor meal, but this established accomplished both. Now let me start this by saying that I have Chipotle at 2 other locations which were actually really good. This place, however, needs some serious changes. First off, the people working there have no sense of customer service. They are rude, inconsiderate, and just sit behind the counter talking to each other instead of interacting with their guests (especially the tall black guy with earrings, if I took over this place he would be the first one I'd fire). Second, the food was just plain terrible. My steak was cold and the chicken my girlfriend got was burnt but also served cold. There are plenty of other fine options in the area so avoid this place at all costs!
I wish I could give zero stars.. ate here but after about 30 mins got extremely ill!!! I'm from out of town and sucks I'm spending my time sick now!! Pretty sure the steak was bad

This place is the absolute worst!! I've visited this location several times and it has been consistently bad. Slow service, terrible customer service and the order is never correct. This was my first time visiting in months with hopes that this place has improved but it hasn't. Same slow less than friendly service and to make matters worst, I nearly chocked on a chicken bone in my pot pie. Never again.
Update on my review: Del Taco has finally posted their new hours and they are now closing at 12:00 AM. It's too bad, I enjoyed their taco salads late at night.

Speedy DD coffee and this one has a drive-thru that is easy to access and easy to exit (No left turns though. Lame!). Inside, clean and bright with the usual DD accessories available.

Turkey sausage flatbread was excellent. The coffee was also excellent, especially when I asked them to add a few ice cubes to bring it down from hot-as-the-sun to drinkable temperature.

This DD is as good as it gets.

You never know what you're going to run into when going to a Del Taco. Could make you run at the site of it or runs down the pants of it. Either way, it could get bad quick. Though we love Del Taco because 59 cent tacos is the worlds greatest thing and since Taco Bell doesn't have anything under a 1.19 (remember 79, 89, 99 menu, of course we do) Del Taco is a key factor to stay full while on the road. Anyways this was the best tasting, looking, most satisfying Del Taco I've ever been too. I ate here and it taste great. Food was fresh and made to order. Best part was, I didn't feel terrible after. I didn't feel terrible at all. I could of ate it cold and it would of been great. Try that one Taco Bell. Either way, this del taco is worth your time, especially if you are broke or drunk. Much love to toss del taco. Also if you've never had those cheesecake bites, have you ever really lived life ?

Doesn't get any better than Del Taco, better selection of items on their menu. The food is authentic and fresh, and not to mention insanely delicious.

I like Taco Bell & Taco Johns, but they really don't have nothing on Del Taco.

I also have to give Del Taco props for having in store promotions to get... wait.... wait for it... FREE FOOD! Just do a simple task and you'll get a voucher, much better than the model most places use in which you have to buy something before you get the deal.

We bought the Original Recipe Boneless Chicken 2 piece Combo earlier this week. I had a feeling the pieces of chicken would be small but they weren't. The chicken was a lot better than I expected it to be.  I would buy this again.
Horrible attitude and horrible food. Chicken was cooked in old oil and it did not taste fresh at all.
Don't go there at 3 in the afternoon if you wonder what happened to all the leftover chicken from lunch it's still sitting there it is absolute crap

This is the worst KFC I have ever been to.
The place looks like it hasn't been cleaned in 3 days.
The chicken was rubbery.
The guy that took my order was standoffish and mechanical.

So I normally never rate/review fast food places because come on its sub par usually... USUALLY until this new BK!! 
I have been pleasantly surprised and shocked! The food comes out fast and FRESH! Always warm and the workers are very polite and nice also which goes a long way! The wait was pretty quick for a BK and I was in total shock when I got my food, my fries were hot and fresh, perfectly crispy and my sisters whopper was made to perfection! The right amount of lettuce, mayo, pickles, onions etc! They don't jip you here so deff make the pit stop!!
You want good BK this is the one to stop at! I don't go often but when I do I will come here hands down! This is the best Oreo Milkshake BTW!! 

Thank you to whom ever is running this establishment you put a fast food restaurant in a great position and my friends and I will be back!

My new favorite grungy Mexican dive in Indy. Taco were good, but get the Milanesa torta- ton of food for the money, and very tasty. I got that and a taco and couldn't eat it all. (The torta comes with fries). Don't take a date though it's pretty grungy :)
I tried the new Veggie Slider last week and was craving another one.  I am a closet whitie junkie.

I eat Chipotle anywhere from 2-7 times a week. This has been my main Chipotle for about a year now and it is definitely a little less together than others, but I never had an experience that made me stop wanting to go there till today.

I ordered online and came in a few minutes early. When I returned to the register, I look in the bag to see no tortilla on the side, almost as expected (hence me checking). I ask for one. The cashier was ignored by the other crew members. The cashier also didn't do anything to expedite things. This employee who was in line had to speak up for her. Also, another Chipotle employee was being served and he was looking at me not caring one bit either. Yeah, I'm waiting on my tortilla while you get your bowl made. In fact, someone put a quesadilla on the press almost a whole minute after I asked for my tortilla. Thankfully, this one guy (wish I knew his name, he's good) removes the quesadilla, puts my tortilla in and passes in down the line. Then I stare at my tortilla that is being blocked by a bowl while no one does anything about it. I don't know how much time I exactly wasted standing there, but it was ridiculous.

On top of the terrible service, somehow my online order was messed up and had "Chicken w/ Extra Carnitas." I wanted carnitas. You'd think, considering the glitch, I'd at least get carnitas. However, I didn't get any ... but I was certainly charged for it.

While I was line, another employee who was off-shift (?) was telling the cashier they need to work for their tips. They certainly do, I gave a $5 tip a few nights ago and now I totally regret it.

Another bad experience:

One time this guy gave me the measliest scoop of meat. He had to be new. I asked for double, because I don't want to be that guy who makes sure his scoop is as big as it should, I just want more meat. Thankfully, the next person in the line corrected it and added more.

Also:

Worst parking lot ever. Almost completely full at any given time it seems. Chipotle should know that any new location will be popping.

Lastly:

Lots of cops. Can make you feel a little unsafe, but there are a lot of sketchy people roaming the parking lot, so perhaps it is warranted.

I walked in here around 5:00pm maybe. I haven't eaten at chipotle in awhile so had the urge to have a steak burrito. I've never been to this specific location but it's the closest to where I reside so it was an easy decision. I drove her and parking was easy (probably just luck or it was a Sunday). 

I walked in and their were about five others sitting around eating and conversing with one another. I walked up and the gentlemen asked what I would like. I told him and he prepared my request, the second lady on the line wasn't so nice. She seemed short with me and wasn't professional at all. I asked for more corn and she just put it on there. I felt like I was troubling her. I know it's just chipotle but she clearly has an attitude.

She should leave it at the door when she walks into work. She's getting paid to be there so theirs no excuse for it. I said thank you and I didn't get any response from her which I thought was rude. The third lady that cashed me out was nice so overall it was ok. Maybe she was having a bad day or something but she wasn't professional and just think she should have common curtesy when she's working and getting paid.

The burrito was good and can't complain about that. I usually don't go on about a specific employee at a establishment but felt it was necessary. She was rude and not professional. The place was clean but when I went into the bathroom and wanted to wash my hands their was no soap in the dispenser. 

I will go back there for sure and hope that employee is in better spirits. I gave it 3 stars this place because of the lack of customer service from her and no soap in the dispenser, the place wasn't full so don't see why one of them couldn't refill it.

Went here with family for a quick lunch, and the staff helping us out behind the counter were very friendly and generous with the ingredients! It was my in-laws' first visit to Chipotle, so it took them a little while to figure out what they wanted, but the staff were very accommodating and understanding.

I like that they're adding newer items to their menu, and I've always appreciated transparent pricing in their stores and in the other fast casual chains they've inspired. I went with carnitas and chorizo, and it was a good choice!

I feel weird writing a review for KFC, but I also feel I would be remiss if I did not write this review.

My buddy and I went into this KFC craving some super crispy, heart killing, greasy fried chicken! (Hey! We only live once!) I am not typically a KFC fan, and neither is my buddy. But you know, those cravings can get you to do stuff sometimes....

So we decide to walk in, why not. I have to tell you.....we had the NICEST YOUNG MAN EVER help us! His name is GABE and this kid is something special.

Now I know it's "fast food", but Del Taco was my first job at age 15 1/2, and so I do have somewhat of a soft spot for what those folks go through dealing with me, and the CRAZY public on a daily basis. But GOOD HELP IS HARD TO FIND in any fast food establishment today, and GABE AT KFC IS WORTH MENTIONING!

But not only is Gabe worth mentioning, so is THE FOOD! OMG, IT WAS SOOOO GOOD!!! We both had the $5 meal things they have right now, where we got 2 pcs of chicken, a side, a biscuit, a cookie, and a drink! FABULOUS!! Gabe made sure TO ASK if we needed any additional condiments (who EVER asks that anymore?!), when he delivered the food to our table. The food was soooo good and it was the best $10 we've spent on food/dining in a LONG TIME!  THANK YOU, GABE, KEEP UP THE GREAT WORK, KIDDO!! :)

PS - I went back by myself a few weeks later; Gabe was not at the register but he was there. He TOTALLY recognized me!! I was shocked at that, actually. The kid just continues to impress! GREAT CUSTOMER SERVICE, GABE!!!

KFC
5 STARS FOR GREAT SERVICE FROM GABE; HANDS DOWN THE BEST!
5 STARS FOR THE FOOD THAT DAY; COOKED TO PERFECTION & DELISH!
4 STARS FOR RESTAURANT CLEANLINESS
5 STARS FOR PRICES!! $10 FOR TWO, YOU CAN'T BEAT THAT!

Went to the drive theough because I saw an ad that looked good. They told me that sale was discontinued.  Attached to their menu board was another ad for a different product ($5 fill-up famous bowl spicy chicken).  I tried ordering that one, but was told that they no longer offer spicy chicken and that the sale was no longer offered.  I let them know that they have two incorrect signs up and was told that a GM or someone was the only one that was allowed to take them down and they havent been by yet.  I figured if it was being run that poorly, the food quality control probably wasn't any better.  So I passed on eating there.

I enjoy Mexican food but this was the saltiest dining experience ever.  I had a salad and all the elements - from the guacamole, to the black beans, and the sofritas - were over-salted to the point of being inedible.  I often add salt to dishes, so it's not that my palate is salt-sensitive.

I won't be going here again.

This isn't the first time I have had major issues with this kfc.  The drive thru is always rude. This time not letting me order but I thing! Never gave me my total and was just extremely rude.  I pull up to the window and she doesn't say anything except did I want to add something now.  I say don't worry about it. Then hands me my food all while mouthing to the other girl something then staring at me.  I ordered the 16 piece meal.  And in total should have gotten 16 pieces if chicken- 2 large green beans- 2 large mash potatoes and gravy and 8 biscuit.  
I drove through on my way home from work- don't live right by there. - I get home and I have 2 things of gravy and 1 green bean.  Nothing else (chicken of course)  
I called the store to see what they can do cause it was just a horrible experience all the way around 
I was told they tried to run after me( I sat in the parking lot trying to get out for over 2 minutes (I know cause I was on the phone) so they didn't try hard.  The manager ( a guy don't remember name) was actually nice and said he would remake if I went back there.  If I lived closer that would be ok , but I don't and this isn't the first time I have had major attitude and missing food. 
I don't believe they tried to get my food to me cause I was there long enough after I received my food trying to get out of parking lot due to mall traffic.  This was not a good 40.88 experience!

I tried the new smoked sausage egg sandwich. Tasty but too greasy for me and I don't think they would have appreciated me posting a picture of the mess they threw together.  I guess I should just stick to my usual coffee and ice coffee but I am fan of this DD....  they are pretty consistent with friendly, fast and accurate service.

If there was another KFC on the Eastside of Tucson I would never go to this location again.  My son loves their chicken but they are always out of something and they have the slowest service I have ever experienced at a drive thru!  Expect a 20 minute wait at the drive-thru even if you are only the second car in line!  They can't handle multiple orders at once.  Last night they totally screwed up my son's order.  Gave us green beans instead of mashed potatoes.  The potato wedges were inedible - hard as rocks, no cookie and the chicken was over- cooked.  I don't think the owner of this franchise gives a crap about his customers!

I wish you could put zero stars because I would!  They are so rude and unprofessional here! I know its just kfc but when I place my order over the intercom and then again at the window and it still takes for forever still! I swear,  they need to stop having conversations with other employees while im trying to place my order a second time at the window.  Its no problem waiting on fresh chicken but  if you're being rude, why would  I want to come back! I mean i live across the street from one and yet I still come down to southport rd, why? Doesn't matter because never again!!!! Worse service ever!

The Original Recipe Chicken came out hot and tasty.  It was five star worthy.  The order came out quickly.  I deducted a star for the corn.  It was a little tough and I have tasted better.

Kenneth took our order.  He was attentive and polite.  I saw him give some Senior Citizens help with their orders and made suggestions to them in a kind manner.  Good for you Kenneth, your Momma raised you right.

I am a sucker for the occasional KFC and so is the family. I ordered the 20 dollar fill up with extra crispy chicken and the Mac and cheese substituted for one of the mashed potatoes. 

The drive thru asked if I wanted another side instead of biscuits because it would take another 4 minutes. I gladly declined since I wasn't in a hurry. They offered a large drink for the inconvenience. (Nice). 

A few minutes later, I received my food and during the check, I didn't find the substituted Mac and cheese. The manager got me what was missing and I was on my way. 

The food tastes like KFC and how I remember them growing up. The extra crispy skin is yummy.  I LOVE that coleslaw with the right amount of tart. Smooth potatoes and that memorable gravy.  No surprises there.

Through direct mail, I received a coupon to try out Burger King's new menu items. I noticed a long drive through line, therefore, I went inside. Big mistake. 

I ordered a Whopper Jr. and a small fry and it took over 15 minutes. While waiting for my food,  I had the opportunity to notice a number of things - the lack of cleanliness, urgency is preparing or taking orders and organization. The ketchup dispenser had no containers and there was more kitchen on the counter than being dispensed, a new customer came in and everyone was in the kitchen and it took 5 minutes for anyone to acknowledge him. 

When my order finally came up, there was no apology or single word about the fact that I could have had ordered a meal at Five Guys and eaten during the wait. 

Awful. Never again.

I don't think I had visited a KFC in the States in...over a decade? before stopping in here earlier in the month. I had overseas, but it's not as gross in Europe typically. Alas, I was hungry, we were about to stop at a brewery, and no other obvious food choices were around. *shrug*

I ordered some sort of chicken sandwich with a root beer. The chicken was okay but it definitely screwed with my stomach, which wasn't exactly ideal. I guess my body was like, "Why are you ingesting this low-grade fast food filth?" and revolted. Only thing I can think.

I think I'll continue on with my KFC-free lifestyle moving forward. I did enjoy the root beer though.

I, like a previous reviewer, had not been to a KFC in years. I went inside to order a la carte. 12 tenders, 6 original, 6 Nashville hot, small mashed potatoes and gravy and an order of potato wedges (for tomorrow's meal) $21 and change. Had not tried he Nashville hot, so was anxious to see how it was. It was an adventure in grease and a lot of breading. I understand, something has to hold the spicy sauce/grease on the chicken and the breading does the trick. It did have some heat and wasn't bad, except that the chicken was room temp. Mashed potatoes and gravy were very good. I learned one thing at this place. Never go there in the middle of the afternoon. Go when it's busy. Yes, you'll wait longer, but you greatly increase the chance the chicken will be freshly made and hot. It took the employee about the same time to put my order in the machine as when the order was placed in front of me. I knew then it would not be fresh and hot. No telling how long it had been sitting out.

This place is terrible I waited over six minutes in the drive-through just to have someone come on the speaker and then when I got up to the window after ordering two chicken breasts I was told they only had one I would have to wait 16 minutes for another it was 430 in the afternoon and they had one chicken breast in the entire store I find that irresponsible of the manager or whoever else was in charge I left without any food and I will not be back

This place use to have to best chicken ever but over the years they fell off big time the chicken is to grease and salt but some people still like eating there my favorite chicken place now is popeyes!

This is my go-to casual food restaurant. I get the same thing every time: Steak burrito with white rice, light on the black beans, sour cream, cheese, the green salsa and guacamole. While this location will occasionally skimp on filling during rush hour, they normally hook you up. One burrito is more than enough to fill you up. I sometimes have a hard time finishing it. Warning: once you try Chipotle you will never want other fast food burrito places again (Bye Bye Moe's and Pancheros).

Wouldn't even give this place one star.. Spent $30 on a family meal... The chicken must have been hours old it was so dry they didn't have half the sides the offered on the menus and then then ones they did have were also so dried out we couldn't even enjoy it. Don't waste your money and the employees had the worst customer service skills

PURE ASS. 

Decided to hit this spot up after the gym while I used the Laundry Cafe nearby. Big mistake!
My chicken sandwich was hard and the Whopper was cold and crusty! I worked in a BK in high school and it was never like this. Prolly cause I care about food. 

This was like I was biting into Asscheeks. 
And I'm not talking like Rhianna cause I'd eat those cheeks viciously... 
I mean Hermit gamer in the basement of his moms house eating Cheetos and beating off wearing the same draws for a week type of ass. 

Don't go here.

Been here several times service and quality very good. I been up down the east coast at many chipotle restaurants  never have I had steak so perfect as today 10/19 at 1:15 pm at Saint Pete on 4th st. great job

I always get the number 1 combo without meat and it's really good. There are plenty of veggie options so I really like that. You can do the Beyond burger (which I don't really care for) or sub out a meat patty for avocado, but I just stick with the sandwich w/o meat. The restaurant is always clean and the service is fast. There always seems to be something out. The napkins or the ketchup or something...When brought to their attention it's filled up real quick so that's good. The staff does seem kind of like they'd rather be somewhere else but I'm not going to hold that against them because they are very nice.

I was asked specifically not to go to the Seminole Boulevard KFC buy my wife and to go to another one well out of the way because she has only had issues after issues after issues with this place. Well I decided to short cut it and go there anyways how bad could they be? Well I went in ordered I pull up to the window the guy said they were out of breasts and mac and cheese so I substituted the mac and cheese with something else and he said he would compensate being out of breast with more pieces of chicken. I came home brought dinner to my family and two of the sides were missing and only 11 pieces of chicken in the bucket. Boy did I get my ass chewed out my wife took the food back to shove it in their ass. Worst experience ever

I concur with the reviews saying this restaurant has improved. I used the drive-thru and I was met by courteous friendly people, and my order was ready quickly. The chicken was hot, it was a bit over cooked on the coating but the chicken was still moist, and the food I received matched my order. As a basic KFC restaurant this fits the bill.

Worst BK ever under no circumstances should this place stay open , I ordered 2 whopper meals,2 whopper Jr's and a 5 piece chicken strips the veggies where way past yellow the bread hard the parties had to been cook at least two days prior , the chicken strips were rubbery and there was no way in hell I would eat this food , went back to give them their food back and get my money they said  they would never serve that and I wouldn't not be able to get my money back cause the manager was not in ,  oh and I forgot to mention I ordered a 5 piece and they have me 3 , yeah stay away from this dump !!!

They suck. We asked for two hot chicken littles that's it, not a hard order. So I get home and it's two regular sandwiches since it's next door we went back and told them this is the wrong order they then told us "we didn't hear that you have to pay for new ones then". Pay for YOUR mistake? After arguing with the manager he tells us ok we will make you hot ones, so we get the bag AND ITS BARBECUE!!!! HOW HARD IS IT TO GET TWO MINI SANDWICHES RIGHT?? At this point I'm annoyed and not in the mood to eat so I just go home. I should have just gone to McDonald's at least they don't yell at you when they get their own damn order wrong.

On more than one occasion I've picked up food at the drive through only to get the ultimate surprise when I get home. That's when I find out that my entire order is wrong. I'm not talking just a pot pie or mashed potato missing... THE ENTIRE F-ING ORDER.. I'm the one who has to drive 3 miles back and waste more of my precious time trying to make it right. When you spend $35 on fast food you should at least get the order correct. From now on I'm going to Bojangles.. chicken is just as good.. it's cheaper and they don't screw your order up...

Just happened to drive by on my way back to Cali. I'm going back to Cali (ha!) and had to stop by. I got just the basics...slider and drink, but it was beyond fast and they seemed amused that I paid for my $2.50 meal with a twenty. Seriously, first time here....I didn't know.
brought a chicken sandwich，almost lost my teeth on my first bite ！ the patty is hard as rock，meat was dry，worst experience ever！

I really don't eat at Chipotle very often, though I love their barbacoa. Last night we were out on a rare date night and decided to stop for a quick bite. While in line I saw a new protein option, "sofritas", and it could be used for a vegan or vegetarian bowl. I had no clue what it was so I did a quick online search and found that it's tofu. When I approached the counter, I asked my server if he'd tried it and though he hadn't, the next server promptly said, "Yes, I really enjoy it but some people don't like the texture. Would you like to try a sample?" Before I knew it, I had a little cup and fork and was tasting a spicy (the good spicy, hot but not overwhelming), chewy, minced concoction that was delicious. So I forgoed my favorite and went vegetarian for the night. 

Added note - the restaurant was clean and eating on the patio as the sun set was a great way to end our evening.

Absolutely the worst fast-food restaurant I've ever been to. I've given this location 3 or 4 tries in the last two years, and every single time it's a complete disaster. I will never go back...and based on the lack of customers (zero) at 6:30 on a Friday evening, I'm guessing I'm not alone in my decision. 

The gentleman working the drive-thru has messed up my order each time I've been in. He first charged me $28 for two Fill-Ups and one popcorn chicken combo. I said there must be a mistake. He tried again and came up with $23. Eventually had someone else do it and got it right ($18.00 something). Then I figured they had messed up the food order, and I was right. Had to go into the restaurant to get the correct meals. As I entered with my bag, I got no smiles, no "how can I help you?"; instead was greeted with the "manager" saying to the drive-thru man, "your order's wrong". Wow, wow, wow. I would also think the food would have been fresh between 6:30-7:00...also wrong. Biscuits were hard as a rock and the chicken definitely had not just been fried. To top it all off, not a "thank you" or a word said when they hand you your food. 

Not sure how this location stays in business.

12/24/18 
7:25pm

Called and they said they close in 30 min. Got there in 3 minutes. Guy at the drive through said they were out of chicken. KFC...out of chicken. 
Christmas eve and want to get off early. I get it. But just tell the truth and say you started to clean up already. Ho Ho Horrible.

I got a sausage egg and cheese biscuit and when I got it the biscuit was so stale, it was not at all soft and tasted terrible. The donuts and drinks are usually good though but I don't think I'll get food from this location again.

Growing up as a kid a trip to the "Colonel's" was a treat.  Moist tender chicken that truly was finger, licking, good.  Sadly 40 years later especially at this location nothing good can be said !

Where to start.  The restaurant inside is absolutely filthy, think public restroom at stadium.  The help constantly violate health codes by touching their face, nose, hair even handling money without gloves or washing hands!  The help are also incredibly rude and have a true I hate working here attitude!

Once you get past the deportable filth, poor hygiene, and rudeness,the  service is absolutely ridiculously slow especially because some genius made this also a Taco Bell!  Several times using the drive thru have waited and waited only to get home to find it's the wrong order or cheated or shorted on items paid for but not in the bag!

The chicken often sits for hours until it's old, dried out and disgusting.  Don't even think of asking for a new fresh batch because you will told they can't do so until they use up the old!  The same goes for the sides which can be left over from god only knows when. 

I can honestly say I have taken food home only to have myself and family feel ill afterwards. Bottom line if you want chicken sadly you're better off buying it from Giant's hot foods bar along with fresh cole slaw , it will be tastier and cheaper ?

One of my go to place to eat. The burrito bowls are awesome and they use the best ingredients around. I'm always satisfied when I leave here. I recommend the burrito bowl with white rice pinto and black beans and steak top it off with green chili salsa sour cream and cheese add a bag of chips and a ice cold coke and your all set.
This is my favorite del taco. The food is always on point, and the service equally as well. Victor always gives great service whether in drive thru or eating in . I will continue to come here!

It's KFC so it isn't going to be an amazing life changing culinary experience but it feeds you. While waiting in drive through the young lady on the speaker was fairly rude rushing people and getting mad if they had more than one order, but once we got to the window she was a lot nicer. The food was typical KFC classic fried chicken with a side of slaw,mashed potatoes and gravy and biscuits for $20 before tax. I hate the classic but my mother prefers it so we went with that. The chicken in parts was chewy since they use low quality chicken that has been injected with a water solution that is terrible on the texture of the meat. The slaw is pretty basic but tastes fine. The mashed potatoes are grainy since they are instant this could be improved if they used fresh butter or cream to rehydrate them, gravy pretty basic and salty as well. The biscuits are terrible, KFC really needs to improve their biscuits, dry and tasteless. If you got to eat and want an ok slightly over priced fast food chicken then come here, otherwise go anywhere else.

This shocked me, but...I ordered a 12-piece extra tasty crispy chicken bucket. That's what I got, in two minutes flat. The young lady at the drivethrough was polite and efficient. The chicken was tender, crispy and perfectly fried. This is how all fast food experiences should be. Bravo, KFC.

I am reviewing the impossible burger I ordered here. I usually do not order fast food but had to come try it. I ordered just the burger and nothing else. It seems pricey for just a burger considering it is not meat and it is cheaper to buy over beef. That was my only complaint. The burger itself was good and I could not tell the difference. This could be dangerous if someone forgot which was which lol I did not go inside the restaurant and can only rate the drive through. It was quick and easy. There are two lanes but they merge into one. That can be an issue if there are a lot of cars in the drive through.

I'm a Chipotle fan. It's quick and easy to make a healthy choice that has lots of flavor. My standard order is Salad with black beans, carnitas, 1 scoop medium salsa, 1 scoop hot salsa, a little bit of corn, and cheese. It served as a great post workout meal/filling dinner on a Tuesday evening. Skip the rice and tortillas and it's a no brainer here!

Amazing how long it takes to go through the drive-through at this place, inside at the counter is even worse. You can take 5 minutes before they acknowledge you're at the drive-through speaker. 
It used to take this long at Jack in the box down the street until they got new management and that place runs through real fast. Obviously whoever manages KFC has not ordered food or sat in the drive-through. Come on how long does it take to put together two tacos, and the chicken is supposed to be done right, and don't ask for a pot pie if your hungry they will be out. For the last three years I only go here when I have to. 
The reviews support what I say, obviously the management is not paying attention.

Our neighborhood KFC is RIDICULOUS!
99% of the time they are short staffed, out of chicken, waiting on mashed potatoes or just plain rude.

  I won't go there for awhile and forget what it's like then I go back and it all comes back to me.   No matter what time of day it is, or day of the week it is, it sucks!!!

  So, we started going a little out of our way to the 22nd & Craycroft KFC.  Great service, always speedy.... sadly, they closed!   Whhhhhhhhhy?!?!?

  Which brings me to today!  We effin' want KFC, we love KFC, kids say it's the best Mac & Cheese, bleh!  I say their coleslaw is my favorite, ever.  Just a small taste!!!

We go to our neighborhood KFC, I know, I know.... we should know by now, after years and years and years of the saaaaaaaaame thing...... we end up walking out after 15 minutes of waiting for service.   

We decide we want KFC that BAD that we're going to drive to the Tanque Verde location.
      
As soon as we walk in, we are greeted by Reggie who was extremely polite and friendly!  In the back ground I can see a bubbly young lady, running the drive thru.

  Reggie takes our order, right away, which was kind a large, $43.00.  Repeats it back, perfect!  We go sit down and wait, an unknown but short period time, which he brings out to us. We check it, as we usually do, everything was complete.  The lobby was very clean with booths & tables to sit at.  Self serve soda machine.  

I SO RECOMMEND THIS KFC if looking for a great one.  It's located on the south side of Tanque Verde, so pay attention.   Great service, clean space, decent parking and now perfect to get to from the new Sabino Canyon bridge off of Kolb!

P.S.  I love the cookies

I love Chipotle, but the steak was cold and obviously not fresh, and the manager was having a staff meeting right in front of where you order with 6 or more staff telling them that they were doing a bad job collectively.  Young people are only going to work as hard as their example, Mr. Manager.  Stop meeting/chastising where customers can hear and look in the mirror.  Won't be back to this location.

I went in recently rather reluctantly after all the bad press but it was excellent! The boy that served me at the counter was excellent, I will be going back . It was so good I'm surprised I stopped going!! Both my daughter and I had the steak bowls with all the fixings !

Worst. Service Ever.  With 5 staff, standing around, and 2 customers in front of me, it should have been quick.  After paying, waited 9 minutes, then the cashier came back and announced that crispy chicken would take another 16 minutes.  At 7PM on a Wednesday.  What fast food restaurant doesn't have food ready at prime dinner time?  After telling me, the cashier just stared at me blankly, with an "I don't give a crap" look.  It took 9 minutes to figure all of that out??  Last KFC meal in my lifetime -- especially with El Pollo Loco right down the street.

This Burger King has been here for a long, long time... It's my husband's favorite choice when it comes to fast food.  I used to bring my girls here a lot when they were young.  Used to be a nice little playground there but they got rid of it years ago.  Stopped in for a quick bite a few days ago and it was all good... Tried the Angry Whopper (a Whopper with onion rings, jalapenos, pepper jack cheese, lettuce, tomato, onion, and some kind of BBQ type sauce)which ended up being a little too "angry" for me... Not to mention too big.  Anyway, we are loving the Satisfries.  Hard to believe they're healthier than the originals - I think they actually taste better!

Wow so where do I begin okay so I got a coupon in the mail for a 16 piece meal with 4 large sides and biscuits I thought this will be easy so I arrived showed the coupon to the cashier she rang it up the 4 sides I asked for were mashed potatoes with gravy, sweet corn, coleslaw, and potato wedges. I payed for my food got the food went home thought it was over until I saw they forgot 2 sides the coleslaw and potatoes wedges. I called the establishment and they said to come back so that I can get them I arrived to the doors locked because the lobby closed at 9 and it was 9:01. I knocked on the door because there was a huge group of people in the lobby. I finally got to the front counter where the manager (I think) rudely asked What can I help you with? I currently work in Fast food so I know my way around customer service so I was very judgmental towards his posture and tone which was very rude and inappropriate. I told him what happen and he called for an employee to finish my problem while he walked away. The worker was very kind and even threw in a few cakes for the inconvenience. The next day after I finally realized that they only gave me 10 out the 16 pieces of chicken I'm done with this kfc I'm sorry for all the nice workers that have to suffer at this place

What in the world happened to KFC? I moved away from Edmonton years ago, back in town to visit family, stopped in for lunch, and ordered a combo that came with a variety of popcorn chicken, tenders, original recipe chicken, fries and slaw. Original chicken was soggy, lukewarm, and extremely greasy and fatty. Tenders were a very strange soft texture, nothing like fresh chicken I've eating anywhere else, A major burger chains chicken nuggets are 10x better than the popcorn chicken, which is inexcusable for a restaurant with Chicken in its name. Fries were soggy and not fresh. (And yes I are in the restaurant) nothing was fresh, the coleslaw was by far the best thing about this meal. One restroom was out of order and the other had not a drop of soap left, meaning the staff isn't even washing their hands properly before working. Inexcusable even for a fast food restaurant, no wonder there were no other people here during the middle of lunch. I will not be returning to kfc, probably ever.

Chipotle in Nevada rocks, not only do you get good quick bites but you can get a beer or a margarita to go with it.  Brilliant.  

Anyone who's eaten chipotle knows can't go wrong with the burrito bowl or quesadilla.  Just good.  I prefers chicken but that's me.  

Barbacoa is flavorful, a little salty for me

Steak is ok, not a big fan

Carnitas is always my 2nd choice after chicken.  

Must add avocado to everything haha

Wow. Charged .50 extra PER each chicken tender since I ordered the honey barbecue tenders, in which I ordered 6 of. Making it 3.00 extra for the whole thing, and then THEY WERE BARELY COATED, as you can see. Ridiculously over priced for what you get and the chicken isn't fresh, it's cold. Bad service.
The line is too long. They are always out of chicken. They are not friendly. I love KFC but this place drives me crazy.
Slowest service ever. Must be only one employee here because it takes 20 mins to get 5 pieces of chicken.  WTH?

I like this Burger King. People are nice, good parking, good location, & they make good food. 
I tried the Impossible Whopper & I really like it. I think I'll have another.

Awful. Went through the drive through with my buddies. We order Three Whopper Combos and Six cheeseburgers. We substituted the fries for onion rings naturally. We got back to my buddies basement as to our dismay we only got two out of three onion rings and ONLY THREE CHEESEBURGERS!!! WE ORDERED SIX, they forgot half our burgers! Very disappointing and just goes to show these employees do not care about their jobs at all
Went to this location at 8:45. All the employees were sitting at the tables eating and they were half cleaned up, "out of" veggies and steak.  Needless to say, we left without ordering.

Just waited outside for ten minutes waiting for them to open. I'm sick and haven't been able to eat solids so I really just wanted to get some mashed potatoes. I went by the drive through at 10:00AM & Noone answered me on the intercom which is fine, I drove to the window and there was an employee eating chicken and playing on her phone at the front counter next to the register too busy to notice I was there. I waited some more and another lady come to the window and they said they haven't turned their computers on yet and it would take a few minutes. You'd think they'd have the store set up and ready to go for the time they open. So I said that's fine and waited another ten minutes for 10:10 and they still weren't up and running. I waited 20 minutes for mashed potatoes that I didn't even get. I didn't get an apology or any sense of quality customer service.

Oh man, one star is generous. I ordered a chicken sandwich last week and it was STONE COLD. I told the manager and she gave me another one.. which was also freezing in the middle. I walk back up to the counter and asked wth was going on and she kinda just shrugged her shoulders and said, that's all we have, take it or leave it. She pulled a tray of chicken out and said it's all the same. I was blown away that a managers response could be that bad. I get it, it's fast food but c'mon KFC. Who ever owns that franchise needs to hire people who at least slightly care about their job. I got a refund and said some colorful things on my way out. Needless to say.. never going
Disappointed. Got my grandma food here, because she has a hard time with fried chicken. This time it was as if it was sitting all day. Hard to chew. No biscuit, no utensils, no honey, no butter.

Tonight we got thighs and legs absolutely mind blowing I cannot believe that they would put that out.
It was Greasy it was on the burnt side that dark brown the thighs the thigh bone was an inch and a half long that was the size of anything but a  KFC chicken maybe a silkie Or a squab we all know there are different types and sizes of chicken. They are buying subpar chicken in my opinion not the size that KFC serves and I even got 2 Burnt legs that were stuck together. Obviously the management has changed or else today is his day off today is Monday. I tried to call the phone just rang and rang no one answered

I have mixed feelings.  Dining inside?  REMEMBER:  One napkin per customer, per visit.  if you can't handle that, swallow your pride and ask the management for another napkin at the counter.  Don't even think of asking for a stack.  You filthy slob.  

Driving through?  Get ready for a surprise.  For all the money this restaurant  is saving on napkins, they make up for with extra food.  We always get extra tacos, and for that I am grateful.

Want a burger?  Get a clue, this is Del Taco, not Del Burger.  At least that's the vibe you get if you try to order one 2 hours before closing.  Often times they'll say they're not making burgers anymore.  Got a problem with that?

Oh my... I used to love this place until today... Let me tell you the full experience "Today" I wanted a Steak Burrito, first they messed up my order I told them no beans.. So okay they are making it again but I had to wait because the Steak was not enough, they made the burrito with rice already and put it on the side.. So I've been waiting for 7 minutes and alas the steak is done.. They were going to use burrito and side they prepared at the side, common sense, the burrito and rice is now cold, I told them I don't want that give me a new one, the lady then prepared a new burrito but was going to scrape the rice on the other burrito, COME ON!!! The icing on the cake was the guy wiping the table... He was using 1 small towel to wipe the table where the grill was and used it on the tables where the ingredients were. And the towel touched the ingredients... SO imagine that.. Now I know why all types of complaints are being published about Chipotle

If I could give zero stars I would. The service is horrible and the restaurant is filthy. There are empty chicken buckets all over the counters; crumbs scattered everywhere, and old chicken left on the floor. I had to cancel my order because I was so disgusted. On top of that, the employees looked like drug users. My suspicions were confirmed when we drove away and saw one of the employees who was just inside the restaurant behind the dumpster lighting up what looked like a crack pipe. Horrible! I don't know how this place is still open.

May I start with - it could definitely be better! My first time at this location and I'm NOT impressed. It's was din for me with the Double Whopper and two other items TOGO. Two Stars worthy honestly for order error and lack of customer service. I've been to other Burger Kings therefore this one is definitely not cutting it. Cleanliness inside is fine. No music. I came on a Thursday lunch at 137pm to dine in so my order was taken in person. I added "bacon" to the Whopper- paid for it but obviously if you see in the picture there is no bacon at all! To my dissatisfaction I calmly and courteously ask about the mistake but was quietly disregard to correct the error.   I honestly don't expect a free meal I'm just more dissatisfied about the overall customer service experience of lack of communication. Manager was on duty too at the time - sorry not sorry - not coming back to this location.

Stopped at this Burger King on a road trip, wanted to try the Impossible burger.  These plant-based burgers are currently sold at Burger Kings in the St. Louis area.  They look & taste just like regular burgers, but have way less fat, calories, & no
cholesterol.  This Burger King was clean, with friendly workers.  The cashier at the counter gave me a senior discount, even though I didn't ask for one.   I enjoyed the Impossible burger, along with some unsweetened ice tea.  From what I've heard, the Impossible burgers have been a great success, 
and will be sold at Burger Kings nationwide, by the end of 2019.   It's nice that they offer this vegan alternative to the typical fast food burger.

I went in recently rather reluctantly after all the bad press but it was excellent! The boy that served me at the counter was excellent, I will be going back . It was so good I'm surprised I stopped going!! Both my daughter and I had the steak bowls with all the fixings.

I don't know why people feel the need to review fast food restaurants, but since everyone wants to be ridiculous, here's a real review. It's KFC people. You know what your going to get. The Nashville Hot Chicken is Off the Hizzle For Shizzle and if you don't know what that means, go get you some before it runs out. The cole slaw was nice and cold and creamy. The staff was very polite and friendly.

The Mgr gave us a free replacement bucket. He said he'll put me on the list. Everyone love KFC so why can't they put a good bucket of chicken? The mgr is pleasant but he doesn't cook the chicken. Come on KFC we want good size fresh tasting chicken with the crust on the chicken not bottom of the bucket

Can you give half a star? Poor customer service, I had a large order as I was ordering the person taking my order asked several times to repeat because she couldn't hear, when I spoke louder she said "ma'am you don't need to get loud? WTH?!?!? She kept cutting me off as I was giving me order, after every item she said ok pull ahead. When I said I wasn't done she said "Jesus." Then she said she just keeps ordering more. Do they want business or not? I was feeding 5 people! Steer clear from this place there are other Hardee's out there

Today I ordered two $5 fill-up meals at this KFC, and the three piece chicken tenders meal was the very worst I've ever eaten! When I opened the box,I could tell the so-called tenders would be anything but tender! They were over browned and obviously overcooked. After biting into one, I also noticed that there was little or no breading on the tenders. So, overall, they were overcooked, tough and hard to chew, and had no breading. There was nothing tender about them at all! I also don't think actual chicken tenders were used, but they were just strips cut off the tougher part of the chicken breast. The biscuits were cold, and I asked twice for honey packets and was given none. I won't be going back to this KFC any time soon.

It was lunch. They had no chicken. They were horribly understaffed and my heart went out to the beleaguered employees. This restaurant, with it's 1970s plastic tables is an absolute disgrace. Avoid at all costs.

I normally don't have a great experience at this KFC, but I didn't feel like cooking dinner last night so I drove thru KFC. I placed my order, pulled up to pay, and was handed my food. I asked for my receipt and the man handed it to me. Went home and my family started to eat - they all said the chicken tasted horrible. The fryer grease probably hasn't been changed in months. There were black specks all over the outside of the chicken and it tasted like burnt grease. The biscuits were well done and hard. While I was eating, I found a black hair in the coleslaw. I grabbed the receipt so I could take the survey, but the guy handed me someone else's receipt - I'm assuming the order before mine. Overall, horrible experience and absolutely the last time my family or I will be spending our money at this KFC.

I don't know why I keep trying to give this place a shot at redemption... absolutely under the standard of even a street hot dog vendor. The staff has no intention of keeping the store clean so you know your food is being prepared the same way. 

The girl who was attempting to serve me clean food ran my tortilla through her rainbow weave and threw it on the building surface. Note, she hasn't changed her gloves in hours and is rubbing them all over the place including her face. I then caught her eating in the dining room with the same damn gloves on like it was her house. I have been to this location before and brought up her behavior to the store manager but obviously has not had any discussion about sanitary practices, with any of the staff. 

Literally, she was eating a piece of steak, with her serving gloves on while she was smearing grease on the surfaces of the store. 

My food tasted like hot mush, there was no flavor, no seasoning and to be honest I've had food taste better coming from a hot dog vendor at 2 am after the club who has better sanitation values than this establishment. 

The one on Olive is a bit of a drive but I will gladly go to them and take a drive then to inconvenience my body and personal integrity with this disgusting pig stye of an embarrassment to the Chipotle food chain. 

I used to work for this company a while ago, if I was a regional manager or a secret shopper/health inspector this place would get a C-.

Gross City, stay away and go to another location.

Known for their sliders aka Crave Case ... probably always will be. You get what you pay for ... it's relatively cheap for good greasy fast food. Bacon fries were a personal fav although the sliders are simply tasty because of all the oil they pump into them sliders. There is a selection of sliders so you're not completely stuck with the standard hamburger slider. Not particularly good for your heart, but hey it's better than most fast food places.

They don't care if we right bad reviews, but for YOUR sake: KEEP DRIVING!!! Came through drive through got a #3 combo and while it was "fast" it was horrible. Cold hard chicken slapped between 2 hard pieces of bread slathered on a bed of mayo and NO pickles! I mean it was a chicken and mayo brick, nothing more. Soda was flat. Fries were okay but stilllll gave them back and asked for my money. If the sandwhich was at least warm, I wouldn't even be complaining. Again I am only here to caution you, I expect no changes to the business for the better.

Don't come here after 8pm unless you want cold food and an attitude. I order the steak tacos and the steak was so cold it felt like she just pulled it out of the fridge... if you don't like ppl coming in close to closing maybe think about working somewhere else...

This location should be closed. I had a Chicken breast there today that was literally all fat, just the fatty skin part and grey gristle under that. 1 star as the mashed potatoes and gravy were good as well as the coleslaw.  Otherwise 0 stars.

Still after all this time nothing has improved. 
We love great fried chicken. KFC is my first fast food as a child,the big treat.
Why cant this store put out a great bucket of chicken? So disappointing.
Greasey little chickens that probably never grew up?
I should have listened to the reviews but I was hungry and figured I would give it a chance. Whopper bun was burned and i tossed it in the trash after one bite. Absolute waste of money.

I have been to other KFC locations & this is the best by far, everything was fresh that we purchased, chicken, biscuits, mashed potatoes & Mac & cheese, friendly staff, cashier went out of her way to make us feel welcome, and they still serve baked chicken
This Chipotle has great customer service and great food, however I was extremely disappointed in my sofritas taco because they're all soggy and I only had these tacos for 5 min.

I'll begin with this: I am and have been a loyal patron of Chipotle for the better part of my life. With that being said, this location is great when it comes to overall vibe and friendliness. Where it lacks is it's cleanliness and quality of ingredients. I've always gotten the same thing at Chipotle. Burrito bowl with white rice, both beans, fajitas, steak, pico, red sauce, sour cream, corn, cheese,  and lettuce. The hot ingredients were cold, the cold ingredients were lukewarm and tasted old. Especially the lettuce, it was almost brown and sitting in water. Come on, Chipotle! You're better than this! I'm usually pretty lenient on these things because I understand there are bad days, but I should've known that my bowl was going to be sub-par when I was paying and a man that had just about finished his bowl and came up and said "this was nasty." Again I am and will continue to be a patron of Chipotle, but I most definitely would NOT recommend this location.

Particularly awful day at Del taco. I waited 20 minutes for a two taco and french fry meal and finally gave up. I hope the manager at this location reads this and addresses the obvious staffing and training issues that I got to observe in the kitchen from the dining room.   This will be my last visit.

This place is an absolute joke and Burger King Corporate should really step in.  The service is always very slow (20 minutes for a Whopper meal with only 3 people in line).  Workers spend more time talking to each other than they do on their jobs.  If you happen to be in line waiting to order, they don't acknowledge you (even after waiting 10+ minutes).

Best of all, if you want to complain to a manager about the long wait - the lady who is part of the "management team" will turn her back to you and ignore you!

Go elsewhere!

Well once again they got my carry out order WRONG again, both of them. What is WORSE is that this is supposed to be an Extra Crispy Thigh, well maybe two days ago!!  (See photo). The biscuits are as hard as a brick. 

I ordered 2 - 2 peice chicken dinners with 2 sides, 1 Extra Crispy, the 2nd Original Recipe. The person who took my order reconfirmed what I ordered & I asked again I picked the food up and was told that everything was correct.

I was also told that you could not order a meal without buying a drink. I'LL NEVER BUY ANYTHING FROM KFC AGAIN. It's too bad that I could give them Negative Star rating.

Worst Burger King EVER!  All I ordered was a Whopper Jr with cheese. It was so cold the cheese didn't melt and when I went to eat it anyway I discovered that the bottom of the bun was sitting in Mayonnaise!  So I tossed it to the animals.  I had to ask for my receipt when I went through the drive through or I wouldn't have had the number to call them to complain!  Not that it made any difference because the manager just made excuses and offered NOTHING to compensate me for my lost dinner. Also this is a franchise store so corporate doesn't give a crap. Just pass this place by and eat somewhere else!

Finally! They closed this Ken(ucky) Feared Chicken shop down!
Very dirty inside, always out the some sort of food.

I ordered dine-in 4 piece chicken tenders with mashed potatoes, coleslaw, biscuit, and a drink. First they forgot my mashed potatoes. Then I realized they didn't provide an eating utensil. I went up to ask for a utensil and the guy complained as he turned around to grab me one and said "normally people eat with their hands." I thought he was joking but realized he forgot I had coleslaw and mashed potatoes but it still pissed me off because he was complaining. The utensils are not where customers can get to them. I didn't need him to make a comment. The soda machine is filthy. He also forgot my mom and sisters fries and they had been sitting waiting. Extremely horrible service. The food was good though, the people suck that work there and how it's ran.

Awful. Went through the drive through with my buddies. We order Three Whopper Combos and Six cheeseburgers. We substituted the fries for onion rings naturally. We got back to my buddies basement as to our dismay we only got two out of three onion rings and ONLY THREE CHEESEBURGERS!!! WE ORDERED SIX, they forgot half our burgers! Very disappointing and just goes to show these employees do not care about their jobs at all

This is the first time I have ever visited this location and I must say it will be my last. When we arrived, it was around 3pm and we were the only ones in the drive thru. our order was simple, two 10 piece chicken bites, a large mountain dew, and a large diet Pepsi. The guy who took our order repeated it 3-4 times, and each time it was wrong. I don't understand how you can get such a simple order wrong. He then tells us that there is a 3 minute wait for the bites; to which we said that was fine. He then told us AGAIN that there was a 3 minute wait for the bites. Again, we said that was fine. When we pulled around to the window there were two employees arguing over if I wanted diet mountain dew or regular mountain dew. The window was open so I heard the entire conversation. When the girl was corrected for the 10th time, she got a little aggravated and said she was just trying to help. After spending about 1-2 minutes listening to their argument she asked for our money. When our drinks were handed to us, we were asked to pull over to the parking lot to wait for the rest of our order. What was a little confusing is 1) no one was behind us, and 2) we were told 3 minutes and it was well past the 3 minute mark at this time. So we did what we were asked to do and pulled over into the parking lot where we waited another 3-5 minutes for him to bring us our chicken bites. When he got to the car he then asked if we wanted any sauce for the bites. Luckily for us we did not, or we would have waited even longer since he would have had to go back into the restaurant, get the sauces, and bring them back to the car. Inside the bag there were no napkins and the dude never once apologized for the unusually long wait. I do like KFC and have never had an experience like this before, but I certainly will not be back to this location, unless it is the last and only restaurant remaining on the planet.

My favorite fast food seems like it isn't open! 24 hours is no more, I tried to get a breakfast burrito at 6:30am and it was closed. Tried to get a late night meal tonight and they were closed at midnight on a Friday.  I grew up on Del Taco and I wish they were open 24 hours like they used to be

Oh man, one star is generous. I ordered a chicken sandwich last week and it was STONE COLD. I told the manager and she gave me another one.. which was also freezing in the middle. I walk back up to the counter and asked wth was going on and she kinda just shrugged her shoulders and said, that's all we have, take it or leave it. She pulled a tray of chicken out and said it's all the same. I was blown away that a managers response could be that bad. I get it, it's fast food but c'mon KFC. Who ever owns that franchise needs to hire people who at least slightly care about their job. I got a refund and said some colorful things on my way out. Needless to say.. never going back.

My first visit to this restaurant. Wanted to try the new Impossible Whopper. The restaurant is clean and open. Bathroom is clean and liked the handbag hanger. The drink machine had nice choices of Beverages and ice tea. My whopper was big, almost too big. The flavor was good but a tad dry. I did ask if you could ask for a fresh grilled one and yes you can. The fries were nice, hot and crispy. My husbands fish Sandwhich was fresh, hot and the bread was soft. I will probably try the Impossible Whopper again.
Food was hot and fresh.  We ordered the 2 for $10 meal deal.  Service was also good.  However, both of our sandwiches were more bun than Whopper or Spicy Chicken.  Bummer.

Running errands in the area and needed something fast and quick on a wintry day. I asked for a whopper junior, no onions and light on the condiments. Served to me exactly how I asked. I didn't want any soda, but they gave me a cup so I can just get plain water.

Bathroom was clean.

Parking was a bit of a mess, but we had just had snow and people were parking like crazy people over snow banks.

They are very inconsistent, you pay for things you don't get. It's supposed to be refried beans but they are whole beans. I order extra cheese which is .50 cents and I get five shreds of cheese? The Del Taco Taco used to be huge now it's barely bigger than the value Taco. They need to figure out what they are doing because I'm done with this one

This kfc just can't figure it out..... I mean don't you just get the stuff delivered and then make it? ....... Almost every time j have been here my order has been incorrect . Missing things or I have even received the completely wrong meal.... My most recent visit. The food was the horrible. Stale bread on the chicken littlest ( I don't mean eh does this bread taste stale?.... I mean when the person put it together I bet the they thought to themselves....."man this bread sucks....."
Also the popcorn chicken in the fa mouse bowl was torched..... Dry burnt..... Just a horrible experience. Which is pretty much every visit to this location.

I love Del Taco, and used to go to the one on Grant a lot before it closed.  I received a free shake for my birthday, which I used to get on Grant, full sized great shake, and went to the Broadway location to get it.  What a surprise when they brought me a tiny mini cup, filled with what looked like frozen ice cream.  Looked and tasted horrible. The food was not much better.  Maybe a bad day, maybe I need to wait for them to open another location.
