So, I'm a huge Chipotle fan. Been eating at Chipotle since I was a little girl, and I've come to expect a certain type of quality from each Chipotle that I visit. They're usually pretty consistently good - I don't think I've ever had a bad experience. When I learned that a Chipotle was going to be in broad ripple, I was really excited. I go to Butler University, so having a Chipotle less than 10 minutes from my doorstep was a pretty great concept.
The first time I visited this store, I noticed that the service was extremely slow. I figured it was just because all the employees were still learning the ropes, still getting the hang of the whole "working at Chipotle" thing. 
However, it's been several months, and the service has not changed. I have waited in line for 15 minutes before at this store - an experience that I have not had at any other Chipotle. The employees move at a glacial pace, and a lot of times will roll my burrito incorrectly or won't add the right proportions of ingredients. The entire restaurant is also pretty dirty compared to other locations I've been to.
Normally, this type of thing would not push me to write a review. But, the other day, I had a really horrible experience at this Chipotle that I feel the need to share.
My roommate and I went for dinner on Friday, December 12, and I ordered a steak burrito. The steak in my burrito was absolutely RAW. Now, I know that Chipotle usually serves their steak pretty rare. The center of this steak in my burrito was cold and actually RAW, not just dark pink. Like I could not even chew it because it was uncooked. Every single piece of steak in my burrito was this way - not just one or two pieces.
So we brought my burrito up to the front, and noticed that a couple other people were up there too with their raw steak burritos. The Chipotle worker accused some of us of taking the raw steak off of the other customer's plates just to get a free burrito. Not only did they accuse us of trying to scam them, but they also refused to remake our burritos. They also told us that the steak was not raw, it was just rare, because they had "temp checked" the steak. 
After about 10 minutes of arguing, they finally remade the burrito.
I would recommend going to a different location - the staff at this one is awful!
The chicken was over cooked and dry and no flavor. Same with mashed potatoes and gravy.

This place always has something wrong with it. Today I went by and they said all they had was 4 pieces of chicken to sale. The excuse was a broken "burner" but every single time I've made an attempt to buy something here they have a problem. Whoever owns this KFC should save their money and shut it down. Chick-fil-A is just a few blocks away.

Horrible customer service in drive thru! They were out of biscuits, corn, original chicken, and didn't give me everything I ordered that they DID have. I will never go here again. Very disappointed and would never recommend.
They always make you pull up and wait, manager is rude, ive gone twice and they were out of chicken. terrible service

Guess kfc is getting better or we lucked out this day -the crispy chicken was piping hot and delicious and the sides were very fresh-better than popeyes which is extremely hit and miss as they always threw in a dried out piece of chicken.  That is why we decided to try Kfc again.   We will convert to kfc in the future as long as the quality stays top notch.
Every time I order through this drive thru they get my order wrong. Every time. I wish I could give zero stars. It's a plain whopper with cheese.... it's a small thing, but I really want the cheese.

I wish I could rate White Castle on a "slider" scale (pun definitely intended).  On one hand, when I close down the bars and crave some greasy fast food, the sound of White Castle morphs me into Harold and Kumar in search of the almighty slider.  On the other hand, when I'm sober, the thought of White Castle makes my stomach turn more times than Jimmy Johnson at Daytona.  Considering I am sober 98% of the time, my review is based on this perspective.

I love dirty little burger joints.  I don't care if the local health agency gives someplace a "C" rating as long as I don't get sick from E-Coli and the food tastes good.  I also love a business that advertises selling a "20 sack" outside the state of Colorado and Washington.  While the cleanliness of White Castle probably does pass with a "C" rating and I will never get E-Coli from there (because the thickness of the patties is no bigger than an empty envelope), the quality is below average and 20 sack means 20 sliders, not something else.  

Just for the purpose of this review I had to try out White Castle again.  On a recent trip to the Manchester location in west St. Louis, I got a couple cheese burger sliders and chicken sliders with fries.  I guess I forgot how little meat you get on a slider because it tasted like a beef flavored bun.  The chicken sliders were a little better but I tasted more breading than actual chicken.  Talk about a carbo load.  To be fair, I do usually get the double cheese burgers so you can taste the meat more.  And usually I put some hot sauce on the chicken sandwich.  I didn't this time.  The prices were a little higher than I remembered for what you get.  Again, thanks Harold and Kumar!

That being said, I think the allure of White Castle has more to due with the promotion of Hollywood and scarcity of location than it does the actual quality of the food.  My home city of Omaha still doesn't have one.  I really hope we do get a White Castle though so it can be used by state troopers as a 24-hour sobriety checkpoint more than an actual restaurant.  Will I ever go back?  Probably.  I'm sure someday one of my friends will again convince me there is no better hangover remedy than "those tender little White Castle burgers, with those little itty bitty grilled onions that just explode in your mouth like flavor crystals every time you bite into one."  I just hope that day isn't anytime soon.

Cashier getting shaken down for a short drawer. 10 min wait to give order. Ordered dbl whopper, got single. 
I give up.

First off I will never ever go back to this god forsaken hell hole of a KFC. The kernel him self would turn over in his grave by the service. They made a line of customers wait (5+) at 7:00 pm in the drive thru to proceed to tell people the only chicken that they had made at the moment was popcorn chicken. Everything else was expected to take 30 mins...even for just chicken tenders..which I know takes 5 minutes to make. I was disgusted and I refuse to go back..I wish I could give this place no stars because that is what is deserves...KFC takes an L.
Why cant we get a great bucket of chicken from this store. Either dirty grease tasting .little pieces. Batter has fallen off. Corporate should check this out. Mgr is pleasant.

No one in my party had ever eaten here before, so we wanted to give it a try, and what better than late night White Castle. I'm glad we did, because I was definitely a fan!

The drive thru was pretty full, so we went inside where it was completely empty. The employee taking our order had to be pretty new (at least I hope so) because he kept having to ask what buttons to press to take our order. We got a cheeseburger and hot chicken slider, and the fish nibblers. I went in to this expecting everything to be disappointed after the White Castle hype I always heard about, but all of them were good.

The cheeseburger was pretty generic, but it's hard to be unbiased about fast food burgers when you have Whataburger around. The hot chicken slider was quite good, and the fried pickle was a nice touch. The fish nibblers were outstanding. I grew up eating fish nuggets in my elementary school cafeteria, so this was a major nostalgia moment because the taste was just like I remember way back then.

Pretty slow service and they are perpetually out of chicken. It is pretty much impossible to get a full order most of the time. I just order original recipe combos so I am not sure why they do not have what I want. When I asked the manager she said it was not their fault. They can not control what other customers buy. I guess everybody thinks they can get chicken at KFC.

Stopped here yesterday for lunch, hungry for some good fried chicken.  My wife had one of the $5 fillups, and I had a 2-piece Nashville Spicy.  Normally if a fast food place is clean and the service good I'll give them 5 stars, but...

1. The parking here is a nightmare.  ***Do not park on the side facing Eegee's, you'll never be able to back out with their drive-through lane taking up the whole lot***  Do park across the street where you'll have easy ingress egress.

2. No condiments at the fountain area.  There was one packet of honey sauce stuck to a straw, that's it.  There was no service person in site to ask for honey and butter for my biscuit.  I finally gave up waiting and ate my biscuit dry.  Bleh.

It was reasonably clean in there for a chicken place - the tables were not greasy.  The selections of fountain drinks was OK.  The food came out hot, and the cole slaw tasted very fresh.

Not sure about the Nashville chicken -- pretty sweet and sugary, but not very spicy.  Don't expect Church's.

I came here for the first time about a year ago and will say it wasn't the beat experience. What started off as a yummy meal turned disappointing and turned my stomach. I had gotten the carnitas bowl and half way through I got a bite of pure fat. A large chunk of fat. It made finishing another bite impossible. So its been a year of not wanting to go back but I thought I should not leave one experience to jade my entire opinion of the well loved and popular spot. 

So today on my way home I decided to stop off and have them another shot for lunch. 

The manager serving me (I'm so so sorry i cant remember his name) was incredibly helpful. Allowed me to sample the carnitas before choosing my order. I was ordering the carnitas bowl again and an order of chips and guacamole. He gave me extra sides of tomatillo salsa and pico de gallo. As well as free guacamole on my bowl in addition to my chips and guacamole order.  I'll plan to go back again for the service I record from him in itself. 

The bowl was quite delicious and the chips are amazing. Fabulous amount of salt seasoning

They have definitely redeemed them self. I will continue coming back now and chalk my first experience to being a fluke.

This particular KFC has become a running joke between my girlfriend and I. The crew 'working' and I use that word in the loosest possible definition, appears to be on some kind of what I can only assume to be a work release program. Whatever the owner is receiving from the State, it's not enough. If there are two things I can count on when I go there, they are 1) they will not have what I went there to get and 2) what I get home with will not be what I ordered. Let me give you an example, I went there tonight for a breast meal with mashed potatoes and a side of coleslaw. They said it would be at least 20 minutes on the breast meat because why would a place called Kentucky Fried Chicken have chicken ready to go? (Side note, a couple of months back, they completely ran out of chicken... KFC did not have any chicken... Kentucky Fried Chicken... as in... CHICKEN) I settled on the 2 piece thigh and drum with mashed potatoes and a side of coleslaw. What I got was 2 thighs, no drumstick, mashed potatoes and no side of coleslaw. Now you may ask yourself why we would go back. Well the truth is we don't that often. It has just become such a joke to us that sometimes we can't resist the adventure. It's like the Forrest Gump's momma of fast food restaurants, you never know what you're gonnna get. Only Forrest was much smarter and far more likable than any of the rude, obnoxious employees this place hires.

Went to drive they. The woman working the speaker was speaking too quietly with the traffic from Pyramid and I couldn't hear her. I told her I couldn't hear and she screamed at me, and was very rude answering about anything else. They had no biscuits ready and were apparently out of lemonade.Got home and we had no condiments for our chicken tenders, which tasted as if they'd been sitting for hours! Will not be going here again!

They do not even deserve a star after how they messed my order up. So in reality I'm giving them a negative star today. I'm not one to complain I usually just eat it my food even if something is missing I can handle if they forget maybe one thing on my whopper you know like onion even if they forget putting pickles on it. But when they forget everything that's where I get upset yes everything I said. And it wouldn't be so bad but I just got done working a 10 hr shift no breakfast no lunch and I was looking forward to whopper Wednesday but when I got home gave my son his food gave my mom her food made sure they good then I open mine it was suppose to a simple whopper no cheese but when I opened it it was a top bun two patties and a bottom bun no lettuce no pickles no onion no tomatoes no mayo no ketchup. I was like what heck so then I called after 5 times trying to get someone to pick up the phone Trish answered I told her what happen you can tell by her tone she didn't want to bothered she asked me if I wanted to come back I said no because I live on the other side of town before she could offer me a free whopper I stopped her and said I don't want one I just want your staff to listen and get my order right the first time instead of constantly messing up orders. Well then Trish said they had a lot of new hires on tonight so hmmm I responded back to her well then if that's the case Trish then it's the managers and the seasoned staff to make sure the orders are done right.

This has to be the worst fast food place I've ever been to.  Went there on a Sunday evening to get their chicken fingers meal. I knew I was in trouble  when I pulled up to a line of cars around the building, backed up to the edge of the road. I parked and went inside and counted 9 other groups of people inside just waiting for their food. My wife wanted KFC though, so I went proceeded to make the huge mistake of ordering instead of running away. 

Let me first say their customer service is non-existent.  The girl working the register was outright vile.  We're not talking about little things like not greeting me or smiling.  At one point I told her I'd like mashed potatoes and cole slaw as my two sides and she tells me "no you only get two sides". I wasn't sure where the confusion was so I repeated it... "Yeah, mashed potatoes and cole slaw". This girl literally stomped her foot down while rolling her eyes so hard that her neck snapped back, then proceeded to curse about me under her breath while she typed in the order. I was supposed to get 6 sauces with the order.  She didn't even ask and just put down that I didn't want any, then told me the total without asking if that was it. When I told her I needed drinks too, it was more stomping around, eye rolling, and cursing.  Again, I should have left at that point, but I made the decision to stay. 

As I waited there I realized nobody was getting their food. 5 minutes passed, 10 minutes, finally a couple people were called to the counter, only to be told they didn't have what they had ordered. 15 minutes... 20 minutes, 30 minutes.  I sat there and watched person after person come to the counter to order and get the same despicable attitude I was given.  I watched the girl at the counter even argue with a customer about how the meal they wanted didn't exist, even though it was on the menu right behind her. When that customer challenged her, the cashier literally just threw up her hands and walked away. I assumed she had gone to get the manager, but nope. She just started doing other things and left the customer standing at the counter.

I had finally had enough after about 40 minutes of waiting while hearing other people talking about how they had been there over an hour. I went to the counter where I was ignored. I finally had to shout to them in the kitchen area, asking who the manager was. When she came up, I discovered where the cashier had gotten her customer service skills. I told her that I had waited long enough and couldn't wait any more. I discovered they hadn't even started my order yet and got a lecture about how it wasn't her fault that they're so far behind. I told her as kindly as I could, that it's her problem to figure that and that I just wanted my money back so I could get my family some food before my kids had to get to bed.  She proceeded to tell me off, going on about how corporate won't give her enough people and that I can take my money elsewhere. She repeatedly went on to say she doesn't care.  I think that may be a big part of the problem but I wasn't trying to confront her. I just wanted my money back so I could get some dinner for my family. She continued to spew excuses and to tell me how none of it is her fault. I finally got my money back and went to the KFC in Fenton, where I got that entire meal in under 5 minutes. 

This is the third time in the last couple years I've been to this KFC and it gets worse every time. I won't be back. I'd suggest you make better decisions than I did and avoid this place.  If you need KFC, go to the one in Fenton. Even with the drive, you'll save yourself time and aggravation.

I apologize, this chain is really screwed up, at least in my area. I know some places can have a bad day and thats why before I do a negative review I give them three or four chances. Different locations tell us if it is a management area problem.

Help doesn't show up for work, new young unskilled people on shift, long hours, boring work, it happens but when a chain in an area goes consistently bad in three locations you start looking at the management team and district supervision.

LOCATION #1 - Countryside Mall Area KFC
In Pinellas county, Florida, KFC must stand for Killed the Fried Chicken! Four different times we were disappointed. One time we ordered the little Chicken Special Sandwiches and wouldn't feed them to members of the other political party. They were that bad. dried and tasteless, I handed them back and walked out. 

The Colonel, if alive , would really take a good look at what he feeds you rather than the corporation worrying about what the Colonels picture logo and public image are about. He went from suit to apron or some ridiculous public relations act like that and they made a whole big media blitz about that. 

Fix the food stupid and people return! It's about the food, the food, rule one in corporate success. Now it's all about grease, dirty tables, help from hell, and food quality went from a ten at one time to a three perhaps worse.

LOCATION #2 - EAST BAY DRIVE near STARKEY RD (Mid Pinellas County) 
The first visit: The second location was another dingy looking joint with mediocre cooked food. You can do better. we took a bucket to go with potatoes, gravy and rolls. The chicken pieces for the money were the wings, thighs and legs, little or no white meat and not worth the price nor the quality of the package. The floors and tables had that overall greasy feel you get when you are waiting for the order and we decided take it home as it was cleaner.

LOCATION #2 REDUX - 09-21-2012 - On a whim and a real desire for KFC, I used to be a big fan of theirs, I sent my secret shopper to get a bucket, potatoes and gravy and rolls. I also said could they put a breast or two in the package. I got a tub of mashed, some gravy, rolls, five wings, one unknown part, two mediocre breasts and a package of bits.

It all looked pathetic, I removed the what appeared to be "double breading to make it look bigger'. With the skin and the rest of the garbage parts in one pile, the meat in another and realized several things. 

This was not a good ratio, not healthy and not fresh. The food had to of been stale since the insides had shrunk severely from the outside. The wings had the feather tips on, which most places dump as unfit to eat, the wings and parts were undersized, and the breast looked half the size of what I had been given in the past. 

The "new" bits got dumped in the lake behind my house as the pieces were dark, old looking, ice cold and totally uneatable. The carp and bigger coy loved them.

My cousin at one time had a middle management position working for the Colonel when he was alive. They had strict policy's of food longevity and quality. If it wasn't sold in x-amount of time it was scrapped. This food is just not acceptable. The quality of the products they are getting from their vendors has diminished as prices go up and that small bucket was 23.00 dollars US. 

So I called my cousin and asked her what gives... She said not the same people, same ideals, food costs drive the retail, management cuts, drive the quality down and when three places in a 15 mile region are all bad, you have a local management problem in addition to corporate troubles.

LOCATION #3 - The KFC BUFFET STORE AT PARK and 66th Street
It was a lunch gamble so we decided to try the buffet because I was starving and had a yen for chicken. Besides my friend who never carries a wallet (I'm on to his game) got the same thus I didn't wind up stuck with a humongous bill again. Two small serving tables and a coke dispenser. The real problems were keeping the service clean, well stirred, and adding water to keep the food moist. 

The help seemed to be on another agenda. Love or a lot of grease was in the air. Very typical usually when friends get hired and the boss who laid down a lot of money is not there. If it were my place, they would be looking for jobs later that day. The gal running the line should have been the first to get rid of. The food on the hot steam tables was skimmed over and getting stale. No one seemed to care.

Again the common denominator was poor service, poor food and that relates to bad management both in the stores and area management. If you see the ground moving, the Colonel is disappointed with his current team...

And on other blogs my sentiments and indigestion were expressed

Wanted to give it a try again- and...Awful drive thru service still! I wish they had a closer one than this one! Never fails, always something is burnt or not what we order! We try and look in our bag, but people hurry us out of there! And every time we call some rude smart*** is answering rudely! And from his smart*** apology I'm scared he might spit in our food if we go back! Usually I try to hit any other KFC before I am forced to use this one. Plus they burn the chicken n it's so dry! Learn how to cook chicken! They don't really seem to care about their customers! Try anywhere else but this store!

OK if you want KFC, keep driving!  This location just doesn't care. Their website says they are open at 10AM then the drive through says 10:30 and the window where you pay says 10AM. 
That's just the beginning.  Went there to get their national promotion, chicken littles and was told they did not have any.  OK I can wait, but, no they said they do not have any at all.  Huh?  A chicken joint with no chicken.  I Love KFC and am from KY but, keep driving, go to another location or consider Popeyes.  Never been to a chicken joint that didn't have chicken.  This location on Tanque Verde is not worth your time.  I have had problems or employees giving attitude in the last 4 visits.  I tried, buy you guys just don't get it....

I gave this KFC three stars ONLY because of the food. The customer service here is usually awful. The cashiers are usually really rude. But my goodness that original chicken is SO BOMBASTIC I can't stop going back! ‍ That's all I ever get. I don't get any of the sides so I can't comment on those.

4 stars for the coffee. (I am a DD and Wawa coffee fan...not so much Starbucks.)  2 stars for food. I don't like donuts so that's obsolete.  

Yes, you pretty much get the same experience at whatever DD you are at, so I'm picking this one because I hit up the drive through fairly often. 

Try the coconut iced coffee! It tastes like a mini tropical vaca with a straw. 

Does anyone else think that the sausage tastes like a Christmas tree smells?! Okay...maybe just me. 

Wish the breakfast sammies were better but I prefer 1) real eggs vs. mushy fake ones and 2) crunchy toasted English muffins vs. microwaved ones. 

Finally, WHY can't they slice bagels in half? Does it really take that much effort?

I tried the Beyond Taco combo last night. They put so much of the filling in that it was impossible to enjoy. I was expecting filling, lettuce, tomato and cheese. They put nearly half a cup or more of the filling, the lettuce was on the outside of the taco. No cheese. By the time I got it home the taco shells were wet and limp. I can tell if made correctly they would be really yummy. The fries were cold and stale. I asked for sliced avocado on the side but instead they were shoved in the taco wrappers with the cup of lettuce. Del Taco has always been my favorite fast food place but they really let me down yesterday. I will go in next time and request hot fresh fries and 3 tablespoons of beyond meat filling, cheese, if does not come with the tacos and avocado slices on the side. Too bad my favorite Del Taco on Grant was closed.

Maybe KFC has gotten the word.
On Tuesday they have specials a leg and a thigh with a biscuit for $2.50

And the last three times it has been very good the way it's supposed to be. 
No grease, crust still on the chicken and fresh tasting. 
They have my vote now and you'll be the first to know if they slack off.
To us KFC is a legend. All American food that many of us had as children
Just got my drive through food home and it's wrong . I ordered a crispy chicken sandwich and got a whopper with no cheese. The tea taste like its days old. So bummed and too tired to go back.

I do need to update this review:

I waited 15 months to make sure it's consistent and then I would put my recommendation out there. I think they're great. We live in the neighborhood I love Kentucky Fried Chicken and something has turned around its not hit and miss anymore.
 I have to say it's consistent and it's good it's not greasy the coleslaw is not too sweet everything is right and I'm so thankful. All the Boomers big treat was KFC. It was in my mind the first fast food. I don't know if it's new owners New Management but whoever is running the place really cares. And I ask the neighborhood to give them another try!
 You won't be sorry. I have complained about this store for so many years and now I feel they have it right. I am so glad they stepped up to the plate and are hitting home runs

So I've been going here every Tuesday for going on four years. Before you think how much money I've spent there, don't it will hurt your mind. It hurts mine a little, but that is okay you know why? ROCKING FOODS.

Now I am not a wealthy human being, but I do like to eat, and I don't particularly enjoy cooking. Del Taco and I have worked out a good situation. 

Also now think on how fat I should be after eating there so much, I'm not! Also they do not use animal products in their beans so if you want a veggy friendly option you can substitute any taco meat for beans. If you eat fish tacos or shrimp tacos each is presented with a lime and you need to use it and it is glorious. My carnivore friends are never disappointed. 

I have brought people here year after year and we have never had a bad experience and the people legitimately are there long enough to get to know most of the time! So if you become a regular or not, it is a great experience.

Inside is always very clean and newly remodeled, they bring your food out if you sit down, they are always very polite!

I just looked at the rating of KFC in Tucson. My favorite chicken in the world. Every one of the stores are 1 or 2 stars why?
This is an All-American meal everyone grew up on KFC, does corporate know that KFC is getting a bad reputation. I'm not talking about the old jokes that they used to do about KFC I'm talking about putting greasy chicken in a bucket and people settling for that.

I went there about 2 weeks wanting to try the impossible burger but I only had Apple Pay & I asked the cashier do you accept it she looked at me like I'm dumb. She didn't even ask anyone for help not a crew person let alone a manger. It was some lady couldn't been much older than 35 , short blonde. That's was my dinner that night I just walked out hungry

I had a hankering for chicken...they delivered. No problems, no complaints, nothing special and there you go. I was even able to get a Taco Bell "Griller" at the same time.

It's a chain KFC/Taco Bell. I got exactly what I expected. Thank you very much.

4071 UNION ROAD SAINT LOUIS, MO 63129 (314) 892-4336 is like a monkey screwing a football... Toni (the shift manager) comes up to me after 15 minutes waiting for my order only to tell me THEY ARE OUT OF F'N CHICKEN!!!

I absolutely love Del taco. I'm sad there are not more of the I. Tucson. This one in particular is excellent and always makes everything fresh. Their shrimp tacos are so good and my favorite item on the menu.
You are welcome Jessica v! I generally love KFC and I was not happy I didn't get my chicken. LOL.

Couple of weeks ago on M
onday it was terrible. Monday is our chicken night this Monday it was perfect it was perfectly cooked it was lite tender and still steamy.
Please I asked the manager keep a sharper eye out everybody loves Kentucky Fried Chicken as long as it's the way it's supposed to be thank you so much

If you like overcooked, dried out pieces of what appears to be Cornish game hens, this is your place.  We've tried it four times over the past six months and today was the last time for us.  We have not had a decent meal here once.  The chicken strips are more breading than chicken and are just a little more tender than chicken jerky.  Their chicken wings might have a couple of pieces of stringy dried out chicken on them, if your lucky.  So yeah, not any more tries for us.

Impossible whopper did a bamboozle on myself (and my cat who also showed great interest). Great service exceeding expectations for south city drive thru. They did me a bamboozle because I love meat burgers...and if I had not known was plant based, I'd have called impossible. The impossible burger is somehow possible. My cat also thinks meat. (I'm a cat lover and only feed kitty vet recommended nom noms, but my cat sniffed with much interest and enthusiasm) 

Outstanding customer service. Fast! I hope this location remains sustainable because there is little competition for quality, accurate customer service in area.

KFC .... this is not against KFC. KFC isn't my favorite fast food drive through chicken place (in fact, if I made a list of my top ten, it'd be 11.)  But this particular review is of this PARTICULAR KFC.

Man. This place is the WORST. 

50% of the time the person on the speaker actually yells at you, or audibly rolls their eyes, or says "No" to requests. The nice ones ALWAYS explain they are understaffed and over worked. 

If you arrive within 1 hour of closing, they are inevitably out of things they simply stopped cooking already. And, in case the name doesn't give it away, the thing they're least likely to have in stock is CHICKEN.

We once went and ordered an advertised meal for 8, that included two kinds of chicken, two sides and cookies. Every five minutes, the poor window waitress came to explain which new aspect of our order they didn't have, get a replacement order, then come back to tell us they were, sadly, also out of that. Literally, they were out of all original recipe, and baked chicken, all chicken strips, gravy -- which turned out fine because they were then out of mashed potatoes--green beans, corn, biscuits ... we ended up with a box of chicken wings (no breasts, no thighs, no legs), French fries and a dozen chocolate chip cookies -- because eventually they offered us cookies for every item they couldn't fulfill! And we still spent nearly $50.

Until they get their act together, I can't, in good conscience, recommend anyone try to eat there. It's ridiculous.

Waiting patiently for my food while I watched the disgusting cashier and his unwashed hands go back and forth taking order, cleaning, and bagging chicken with his bare hands. I don't know about you but I'd like the public health service to close this nasty place down.

Omg we loved Chipoltle! And I'm so glad they got their issues figured out. 
    Today while roaming around we ran across this store. And what a perfect day for it! Cold outside and warm inside. I love the Carnitas burrito and Bf loves the bowl. Our food was quite fabulous tonite. It been quite awhile. 
    We sat looking at the line workers and two asian guys were staring looking at the menu trying to figure it out. So I swallowed and told them how it works. They both got bowls and sat down to eat!  Everybody happy happy happy!! Sorry no pix... ate it all
2 Cold chicken sandwiches,  no napkins, and 1 sandwich was extremely spicy. Not  yet lunch, so not yet busy. Why ?

Yikes.  I placed a mobile order that was supposed to be ready by 12:00.  I arrived at noon and watched the employee make other orders for five minutes.  At 12:05, probably uncomfortable with me watching him from a few feet away (haha), the guy asked me what I needed. It was chill, though, I did not mind waiting and appreciated when he acknowledged my presence.  I had ordered a steak salad, and for context, the salad I ordered only had seven components: lettuce, corn salsa, pico de gallo, steak, veggies and dressing.  As he was tossing the various ingredients into the bowl, I did not see him add the veggies, nor did he place a dressing in the bag.  Upon receiving the order, I asked him if he could throw some veggies on the salad.  He replied they were out of veggies.  So, I asked him if I could have the dressing, to which he replied they also did not have any dressing.  Clearly, they had not prepped properly as it was only noon and the restaurant had been open a little over an hour.  First off, I was surprised he did not tell me up front that 2/7 of the salad was not available.  Further, I was fairly shocked he, or any of the multitude of workers standing around, did not offer any sort of apology.  They all just stared at me for a second, incredulous to why I had not left yet, and then continued making other orders.  Clearly my conflict resolution skills are not very refined and I definitely contributed to the awkwardness, but I would have appreciated had someone addressed the issue and attempted to remedy the problem.

First time at del taco, I ordered 3  Carne Asada soft  tacos, it's made with melted cheese between the 2 soft tacos. Little bit of carne asada, tiny bit of guacamole, lots of lettuce and tomatoes and graded cheese. Absolutely nasty. Cheese was gross. After a few bites, all the rest went in the trash. First time , only time there.

It was a Saturday night and I went to get some KFC.

As others stated, the drive up is not a good choice here so I headed inside only to be met with a handful of angry customers leaving the inside.

"No chicken!" was what they said! And as I went inside I over heard them say it would be about 20 minutes.

Well that was strike three for me for this place. Never going to see a check in from me there again.
Didn't get my 4 biscuits :( but the chicken and sides were okay. It's take out ya know?

We love chipotle but tonight was a serious let down. Hours say they are open until 9pm but when we arrived they were only taking online/app orders ok fine understandable since we know company's are having a hard time keeping employees. So we ordered online. 
When i went in to get the order, i checked it because it seemed like they really weren't paying attention to all the orders. I did notice a lot of people were making online orders. Upon checking our food, the order was completely wrong. I ordered a steak bowl with white rice, black beans, corn salsa, cheese, sour cream, and veggies. First it was not steak it was Barbacoa and there was no veggies. My partner got the chicken bowl, brown rice, no beans, fresh salsa, queso, cheese and sour cream. Again barbacoa and not chicken, corn salsa was added and no queso. The young kid who was there asked if they could replace or they could refund since they were out of chicken and steak. So i was already tired and kept mine but my partner does not eat corn and asked what protein they did have and the guy said carna asada. So they remade hers, and she said no corn please. We checked the news bowl and they added a ton of corn salsa. She then took it back to the counter and said hey guys it's wrong again. They remade it again and when she opened it everything looked fine. When we get home and she begins to eat and bam it has beans in it which she did not order. It was a let down and after driving back home it was after 9pm and they were already closed. 
Unfortunately we will not be going back to this location

They had us waiting for 40 minutes for the food and when I finally called them again to see what is going on they basically forgot about our order. Once we got the food all the side containers were only filled like not even 1/3 of the way. Chicken was so greasy. Definitely wouldn't recommend.

Ordered a $5 Famous Bowl without chicken. I got it without the chicken but waited about 10-15 minutes for it. Mind you the bowl was done and the manager says to the crew member "Your bowl was done you could've bagged it", then bagged it and gave it to me. I get home, and they forgot my cookie as well.

Well...  seems like they truly don't care... I ordered thru the drive thru and the guy taking my order sounded tired and worn out.  I was literally the only car in the drive thru (i now understand why). I ordered a double whopper with cheese and onions only and a side of o rings and that's it... after they apologized for my wait while paying at the window, I assumed the food would be made right... nope... a whopper with everything on it was what I got.  If they can't get it right when it's slow, can't imagine how bad it is when they are busy.

I have visited this KFC location many times. What can I say...I am addicted to their Famous Bowls! For those who have never had one it is like having an entire meal in a bowl...mashed potatoes, gravy, corn, and breaded chicken pieces (spicy is available too). Yummy! The meal combo also comes with a chocolate chip cookie and drink (and yes, a carb hangover...but worth it!). The combo is under $7 so a great deal for a fast food lunch. The wait time in their drive thru is also considerably less than the surrounding fast food restaurants so I have been here frequently over the past few months. The staff has always been friendly and efficient. There is one young man in particular who has been working the last few times I have visited (most recently yesterday afternoon around 3pm). I didn't get his name, but he is always super nice and it makes your day a little brighter to interact with him! He deserves some recognition! I have visited another KFC location in the past, and those experiences turned me off of KFC for awhile. The major complaint being you always had to wait for chicken (at least 10 minutes)...no matter what you ordered or what time you visited. I mean it is a fast food chicken restaurant! Anyway, the Los Altos location does a great job. I just wish KFC would bring back the grilled chicken option. It was my favorite and healthier than the fried chicken (yes, I know the fried chicken made them famous).

This KFC has the worst service. I don't usually review poorly but I've given them 4 chances.  Each time they've messed up my order one way or another.  One afternoon they told me they were out of chicken and "we're closing in an hour, did I really need chicken?"  Second & third time missing food.  Third time missing food, overcharged and had to pull around and wait for food 12 minutes.  They always seem understaffed.  We won't be back.

Worst KFC ever. Service in the drive thru is so slow. We waited 40 min for a chicken meal and they screwed up our simple meal twice. We still came home with the wrong sides. So frustrating and the store did not seem to care or be in any hurry.

This is mostly a review about their new Beyond Tacos. I was so happy to hear they've got a meat free 100% plant based option now so I had to go check them out. The taco is more expensive than the regular beef ones but I'm guessing the price might come down if/when the fine folks at Beyond Meat expand. 

That said the taco was DELICIOUS! It's got the prefect amount of sauce/spices. Apparently they're cooked separately from the meat so no worries there. If you're a meat eater I'd suggest you give it a try as I'm not sure you'd be able to tell the difference and if you're a veggie, it's always nice to have more options so give it a try!

According to their website you can sub out the Beyond Meat for any of their meat options so I might do that next time.

A bit about the location, it's clean and there's plenty of room. The employees are kind of a bit meh about being there but they're polite and quick. The new Greyhound station is right next door so you're likely to find a few random people hanging around. No biggie but just a heads up.

I admit, I am a closeted Craver. It's not that I am embarrassed that I enjoy these steamed burgers cooked on a bed of onions and served on a steamed bun as well. It's just that some non-cravers just do not understand the love for these tiny burgers. After all, they were the first fast food hamburger back in 1921. OMG! in 6 years they'll be celebrating their centennial. 

My favorite slider now is the Jalapeño. Instead of the regular American cheese, they top the beef patty with an American cheese flavored with pieces of Jalapeño. The mix of the grilled onions and the Jalapeño cheese, just makes the heartburn so much more enjoyable. The fries may be frozen, but they serve the crinkle cut, hot and crisp. 

I'm glad that WC is a Coke restaurant and that they keep the fountain soda lines clean. I can personally tell the difference between freshly cleaned lines and those that have been neglected. A clean line, always makes the soda more refreshing. 

The staff here is at times a little scattered brain, but I have never had a mistake in my order, I've just had a long wait.

As far as Burger Kings go this one isn't bad. I ordered in to try the Impossible Meatless Whopper and the restaurant itself was newly renovated, very clean, and even had free WiFi. The staff was also very friendly and accommodating. Note however that they don't honor any coupons from the BK app as they don't accept "corporate promotions."

There's a Coke freestyle machine inside and it was pretty busy both for the drive through and dine in lines during the lunch rush so you know the food you're getting is fresh.

If you know anything about me, know that I like eating tacos, I'm especially fond of eating those inauthentic tex mex versions at Taco Bell and Del Taco. 

I recently had a craving for Del Taco. It used to be my favorite taco joint, but I hadn't been there for quite a long time. It is not located near where I live, so it's a shlep, but I had a craving, so off I went. 

This visit happened to coincide with the brand new meatless tacos. I came in during a slow period, and ordered one along with a beef taco. Both were well portioned with plenty of meat, cheese, and lettuce. The combination highlighted what I really enjoyed about it. 

The only problem I had was that the place was a bit sloppy. It was difficult for me to find a clean tabletop. The salsa holder near the counter was disgusting. It looked like someone dropped salsa inside, and it was sticky and dry. The taco fillings were also haphazardly put together.

Still, it was cheap, it was filling, and it satisfied me. I'll probably be back.

Normally 1 of my favorite fast foods but the past few times the orders have been ridiculously big mess ups, the first time I went a few days ago I asked for rolled tacos playing with just cheese due to my tomato allergy and I got them plain with no cheese with pico de gallo all over the top ( Covered in tamatos). 

Then TODAY,. My wife got us a few famous star burgers and get home 1 of them WITH NO DAM HAMBURGER PATTY. HOW DO YOU FORGET THE BURGER. ON THE BURGER!!!!!

SOMEONE PLEASE REPLACE THAT COOK, ABOUT AS MUCH COMMEN SINCE AS A ROCK

This KFC restaurant is pretty much hit or miss on a given day. I myself have always had a good experience here. The employees are friendly and do there best to take care of there customers without much appreciation. When I visit I like to go right after they open at 11am. I tend to get the freshest and juiciest chicken this way. I will always go to this restaurant when I'm wanting my KFC.

Have ordered food from this location 4 times and will not come back. Have been told they were did not have enough chicken for a $20 fill up 3/4 times. When they did not have coleslaw, I was yelled at that they "do not have more in the back and cannot magically reproduce any" after politely asking to ensure they included the coleslaw. The lady in the drive-thru window stuck her tongue out at me as I was driving away. This is disgusting.

I will go anywhere else for shitty chicken.

Went to the drive through and ordered chicken sandwich combo with cheese and no lettuce. After an extended wait left without checking to see if order was correct. After arriving at my destination realized I was given a whopper, taco and fries instead. Drove back and had the right order made. While inside heard employees complaining that people kept getting the wrong order.  Reached out on Twitter and no response from Burger King. Customer service issues apparently start at the top, no reason to go back!

Recently came here to grab some food before heading back to Chicago. This place is great. It's clean inside but not so clean restrooms. Staff here is beyond friendly and their impossible whoppers were phenomenal! Definitely wouldn't mind coming back here.

How bad could a slider and fries be at 11:45 am when there is already a lunch line? I would think the food would be fresh and hot.
Should have left when we placed the order for "sliders" per the menu, and the person taking the order "corrected" us and said , do you mean our BURGERS? Ok..whatever. Got our food, pulled over to eat in the car,
(no inside dining available), and the fries were cold. Not just cool, cold, like that had been sitting on a table for 30 minutes cold. The burgers....cold as well. Did someone place this order earlier and they forgot to give it to them? Was it returned? I may have received cool fries at other locations in the past, but never a cold burger.
I will never go back to this location, and my husband said he won't even bother with any Whitecastle again. (I'll go without him anyway)

We are so angry. If I could give it a 0 I would.
Ordered ahead during this virus. Menu said chicken bucket either regular, crispy or grilled. Put grilled and paid. Put email and phone for them to call if problem.
Went to pick up and the guy said" we don't have grilled during the virus". My husband said why didn't you call. Of course he just shook his hear.
Husband asked for the refund and he shook his shoulders and said " that's up to corporate".
Absolutely unacceptable.  
I never go  there but was in the mood. Emailed customer service, no response.
Maybe if I keep posting negative reviews, they will respond, but I doubt it

Manager lied, and had a bad attitude about documenting this location. This is all within the public areas, so it's all fair game to be documented, and to be used as a review here on Yelp. This would have gotten 5 stars from the service I received from another employee, but because of the manager's lying and bad attitude, this is being dropped to its lowest of 1 star. Also, if she doesn't want the documenting of public areas being done, it shows she has something to hide, because public areas are well within legal limits, so just what is she trying to hide?? I don't recommend!

I love Chipotle but was SUPER disappointed by my visit moments ago. The place was filthy. Eight employees up front doing nothing despite food all over the counters, and out of pico, chicken, sofritas and more. 

Only the short and tall Hispanic guys had their masks over their face and nose. Everyone else had it pulled down below their nose or on their chin. Given they all had matching black masks, I'm guessing it's a poorly enforced policy. 

The place felt like a high school detention room with all the goofing around and lack of discipline. If there was a manager on duty, they should be replaced for ineffective management. 

Never been so disappointed with Chipotle.
Friendly service, really enjoyed the Beyond Tacos. Was surprised by how good the guacamole is, in particular - good texture and good flavor.

Absolutely terrible. If the health department inspected them today, they'd be shut down. Nearly every table was dirty. My chicken came with a big hair in the box. I wanted to try the new peach soda, but the fountain soda nozzle was spraying wildly. I asked at the counter about it, and they told me it was just some excessive air in the line. She called me sweetie and filled it from the soda fountain behind the counter, popped on the lid and tried to send me on my way. I opened the cup to see, and it was filled with black particles floating all throughout the soda, like coarse black pepper. I showed her and she didn't even care. I wasn't even offered a new cup! She said she tried to rinse it out but it still had all this black crap stuck to the sides. I wanted to get something else, but there were fruit flies all over the raspberry iced tea nozzle. What a joke! Terrible in every level! Do not go here unless you want food poisoning or worse.
I would only recommend this place to someone who likes anemic, dried out, leathery burgers composed entirely of gristle. Too bad there isn't a minus five star ratings option.

What can I say about Chipotle that hasn't already been said? Maybe that it's nice to see one of the abundant brick-and-mortar bank buildings in our community converted to a nobler purpose.  Otherwise, nothing too surprising to say. Chipotle Edwardsville is new and clean. I like the app and alphabetized pickup shelf inside and left. Therese H. liked her steak salad, large chips & guac, and I liked my keto salad bowl, the tomatillo-red chili salsa tasty and not too spicy. There was no way NOT to notice that the portion sizes were significantly smaller when compared to the relatively old-to-Edwardsville competitor Qdoba.  I like the flavors, I like the app and easy pickup, but I wish I had more food for the price. If this is the way it's going to continue, and with several good local Mexican places in Edwardsville, CMG will have to rely for customers on relatively uninformed folks visiting the surrounding Metro-east shopping Mecca.

KFC would be unstoppable if they had decent sides. Let me say that again... unstoppable!! Too bad they don't. The stores are pretty much always filthy too. But damn that Chicken tastes so good!! Original recipe thighs is where it's at. Now remember that! 

Stop it with the stupid shit already. The Cheeto sandwiches and all of that other crap. And would it kill you to put some legit wings on the menue?

I'm giving this place 1 start because I actually do like the food sooo much. Chipotle is one of my favorite places to eat! However at this location, their customer service was EXTREMELY POOR. There were 3 girls at the front making everyone's food. As soon as I stepped up there to tell them what I want, it seemed as if she hated her job and hated being there and I could feel that energy radiating off of her. Then as I'm trying to tell her what I want, over half of the containers were empty of food. So, I told her I could wait until some things were done cooking. As I waited my partner had already gotten their food so I paid for my partners and I just told her what I was going to order (Steak Burrito), that way she can go ahead and ring me up. I waited and I saw her bring out the fresh food however she looked right at me then told a completely new customer to come up and start their order, even though I had been waiting at this point for almost 15 min. Then after she takes 3 more peoples order she looked at her coworker and started laughing then looked at me & was like "Did you go to the bathroom" like girl, you know I was standing right here the whole time. Right in front of you. Well after waiting 15 minutes for veggies, they end up running out of steak while I'm waiting. So at this point I'm not gonna sit in here and wait another 15 minutes for them to just ignore me again so I told her to just put Chicken on their instead. When I did that, the cash register lady started talking under her breath to her co worker about me & I heard her say, "He got a steak burrito, but he ordering chicken, like what" then she handed it to me and gave me a very passive aggressive "I hope you have a good day sir" then rolled her eyes. Don't be mad at me for getting chicken, get mad at yourself and your cook for not having the basics ready. Those girls honestly need some customer service training or they need to get fired... period. If you don't like your 12/hr job then idk what to tell you girl. I don't like working all the time either but the difference is, I don't treat people with disrespect. Learn to be respectful or find a job that doesn't deal with the public

I have left a long and lengthy review of this KFC. For anyone that's new to the area this is KFC on Oracle And Magee that has been wildly exciting chicken. It has the same owner but they have a discontinued the 1.50 special. Today it tasted like the grease needed to be changed.  The. Chicken was hot, but it had an old grease taste pardon me nothing ever stays the same . The biscuits were lukewarm and hard they went into the garbage. Be careful go in and look at your food when you order it.

I recind my opinion that it's a five-star KFC.

I have tried very hard to maintain my morale and positivity with this White Castle location and have repeatedly given them the benefit of the doubt, but I'm officially at the end of my rope with wasting my money there. As a native St. Louisan, WC fare is not at all new to me, though I moved nearby this location from another part of the city 2 years ago. Since then, I've ordered from the Maplewood location maybe a dozen times. I can't recall a single visit that hasn't resulted in one or, more often, multiple errors whether received in person, through the drive-thru, or delivered.

 The last 2 orders included entirely missed desserts (a total of 5) and traditional beef sliders substituted for Impossible Sliders. Any vegetarian will tell you that this is utterly unacceptable! Perhaps meat eaters are unable to tell, but vegetarians very often know when they're eating meat! So, when we receive an animal product that we actively attempting to avoid in the first place, not only is it disrespecting our choices (and in some case health conditions) but also dissipates our available dinner items down to nothing, which means we go hungry!  Most people order the Impossible Slider because they DON'T EAT MEAT! Additionally, I paid for a more expensive item when I instead received a cheaper one--doubly NOT ok.

In addition to all of that, the service is incredibly slow. I've never waited longer at any other "fast" food establishment. I regularly wait 10-15 minutes at this location and have waited up to 25 minutes. And quite frankly, it was never worth it.

I've also regularly been short-changed on condiments, even after inquiring after them and being told that they are in the bag. I've quite often found later the condiments were, in fact, not in the bag. 

This location needs to get their stuff together! Either way, I'm done wasting my money.

Nasty, mean, hateful, and ignorant staff and management. It's a fried chicken chain with a minimal menu. How hard can it be to fill an order correctly?!?! This location always seems to be "sold out" of whatever it was I specifically drove there to order. I have given them too many chances. I will take my business elsewhere from now on.

I move the very close to it and the food is good the shakes up and up the shakes are off the hook do you want a great milkshake go to Del taco I am not joking great tacos great talk everything is good there

Every single time i go I regret it! Either they are out of whatever the commercial is offering or out of chicken or just a 20 minute wait. So today is the final straw my opinion is the fast food restaurants in Burlington City hates the people who spend money with them. I'm done. Today was a 16min wait or take the original chicken. I didn't have time to wait (FAST FOOD SHOULD BE FAST) so I got the bag no napkins but whatever get home to this (see pic) where is the rest of the damn skin???? Please I want to give a half of a star but it won't let me.

I normally love eating at Del Taco and the food is good. This changed for me tonight the manager was rude and argumentative and didn't cooperate with me. The taco shells had a bad smell to them and the color was off and he told me that I wouldn't get my money back and he would re make my food. I will never eat Del Taco at night ever again.

I would give no stars if possible. 
We used the ap to order waited 15 min and went through the drive through. When we got to the window they gave us our drinks and had us pull around to the front door. There was no one in front of us in line and no one behind us. 
While we waited three other cars were pulled around. 
30 minutes later still no food. I was about to call them and make sure they were okay when someone came out and gave a car that parked after us their food. Then they came to ours and said I think this is your order. 
Luckily it was. 
My husband and I were in the car and had an order for our daughter which included a sprite. We didn't check the sprite while we were waiting 30 min for them and I wish we had because it was all carbonation and no syrup. We didn't find out until we got home. 
Our fries and onion rings were cold when she gave us the bag but by this time we judged wanted to go home. My jr whopper was a mess even though I asked for light Mayo it was oozing Mayo out the side of the bun. I asked for extra pickle and the 3 pickles were stacked in the middle of the burger. 
We get one cheat night a month and a whopper just sounded good. Well, never again!
Thanks for ruining our night. :(

Save your money for another place. These messages biscuits were raw in the middle. Chicken overcooked and dry as a bone. The only thing that was decent was the Costa and the mashed potatoes and gravy. If you want good chicken this isn't it.

Really annoyed.  We literally drive across town to eat at this Del Taco since it's the only one.  Today they wouldn't accept the coupon we had from the Save Around book that we buy to support our local schools.  The reason....it didn't have a barcode.  I can understand why these stores aren't surviving in Tucson because the employees do not know what they are doing.  First we can straight up show them the previous coupons we have used that do not have barcodes and were honored with no issue.  We can pull up the coupons on the app for the Save Around but they can't take those without a barcode.  Why wasn't it an issue the last 3 times?  Needless to say, we will not be back.  It's to far to drive for rude team members who do not know what they are doing.  Needless to say, we ate somewhere else.

I tried the King XL, it was good but too big for me. The burgers, 2 of them, I believe are larger than the whopper burgers but I could be wrong lol. Also enjoyed their sweet tea which I never cared for before. Good experience all around, never had an issue with this location.

Worst Dunkin I've ever been to. The only reason I kept going back was because it's within walking distance. I also kept thinking that, "There's no way it will be as bad this time as it was the last." I was right. It's always worse.

The people who work here are incompetent. Plain and simple. I mean, that's the conclusion you have to draw when the last 3 visits resulted in head-scratching problems. 

First, how do you mess up coffee? I don't know, but if you hate somebody and want to make them a terrible cup of coffee, stop in at this location and ask them how it's done. 

Second, I'm pretty sure the bagels here are from the stone age. Or maybe they're just made of stone? I just got a breakfast sandwich on a bagel from here this morning, and even my dog won't eat it. She gave it a sniff and a lick then promptly went and got into the cat's litterbox and ate poop.

No joke, my dog would rather eat cat poop than the food they serve a this Dunkin.

I'm also amazed at this location's ability to mess up an order. They get it wrong more than they get it right. Perhaps I should stop ordering complicated things like "bacon, egg, and cheese on a bagel". God forbid you ask for it to be put on an everything bagel rather than the normal kind...you'll end up with a sausage and egg-white on a strawberry sprinkle donut instead.

I can't say it enough...don't ever go to this location. There's another Dunkin a mile down the road that is infinitely better. 

And if anybody from Dunkin actually reads this, look for the incoming complaint on your corporate site about this location. This location is an absolute embarrassment to the Dunkin franchise.

I didn't like it the one time I had it before. The chicken & biscuts tasted like it was sitting under the heat lamp too too long. I don't trust the people who are serving it at all. I think  I saw two of them on "America's Most Wanted" before. I don't know what their doing to the food back behind the counter. My "gut" feeling tells me to stay far away.

OK, the Impossible Whopper is amazing! I've always loved that BK had a veggie burger (Morning Star Farms I think) and it was not terrible but didn't have much flavor. The Impossible Whooper is truly delicious and pretty under cover as plant based. 
The other and very noteworthy reason for this review is the customer service that is absolutely top notch. As a fellow review already noted there is plenty of fast food that comes with a side of attitude or just plain hostility in the area, so the A+ customer service was a wonderful surprise.  I mean, I got a genuine have a nice day on my way through the drive though!
What a disappointing lunch.  Everything was cold and the steak was hard.  Very little portion for the costs.
The last 3 times I've been here they didn't have the chicken I requested. 3 for 3!! Has McDonalds ever told you they are out of hamburgers???? Terrible management.

Chipotle in Sparks has always been one of my favorites...until today.   Such rude young people working in there.   I can deal with the skimpy portion of steak that you put in my burrito but I can't deal with the eye rolling,  head shaking and refusal to finish wrapping my order when questioned about the half serving of steak and the cashier telling me that I'm being rude.  The only redeeming thing for this location is the floor manager who apologized for the rudeness of the employees. I would not recommend this location due to rude employees.

OPEN YOUR LOBBY! The pandemic is over it's time to open up your indoor dining so patrons can utilize seating area. Also it is a disservice to those who are disabled and need to utilize your restrooms! We dined there on Saturday afternoon and still had to eat the food including a messy whopper in the car. I ended up getting a stain on a brand new shirt I should come in there and make the General manager pay for it.

I was happy to see maybe a vegan taco. "Beyond Avocado taco."


Beyond avocado taco??? Yuck. No avocado and the texture like mush. And honestly it's beans and nothing else. And they charged me for the Beyond Avocado Tacos. Nasty. Awful. Just like it used to be before I was vegan. I never eat fast food. This is why. This should be reimbursed.

Yea, I'll take a steak burrito bowl, hold the attitude, please!  Okay, I get it, my name isn't the easiest to pronounce (it's Leigh like Lee).  However, when you yell out the wrong pronunciation (forgivable, no big deal) and your coworker corrects you with the correct pronunciation and you respond with an audible "so???" so the customer can hear...not okay.  Cherry on the top?  When my husband's burrito bowl came up, Susie Sunshine yelled, "DAVE?  Or is it Da-Veh" while looking at me and snickering.  Ummm okay, thanks sweetheart.  

So poor manners of one employee aside, they messed up the online order for the second time.  After the first time, I chalked it up to a simple mistake and maybe that's what it was this time, too.  Either way, I'll go to the Park Blvd location from now on.  The food tasted fine and the girl working the register was nice, however, Susie Sunshine needs some remedial life training.
I've been here multiple time they ALWAYS give me a wrong order. Today I order just mashed potatoes and they gave me chicken and a Biscuit and no mashed potatoes the only thing I actually asked for.
The workers don't care , no ice , and they rushed the cooking bc they didn't prepare steak  overall not a good experience

Normally I don't review fast food spots, it's fast and cheap and is usually pretty ok for what it is. I gotta give a shout-out to this KFC though. Over the last few months I've stopped in a few times for a family meal and a couple sandwiches. Today especially though blew my mind how good and fresh everything came out. All the sides and chicken just hit the spot. I know how rough working with all the regulations plus customers that have high expectations for their dollar right now, but this location is really doing a good job so I felt like they should get some recognition.

If I could give this KFC zero stars I 100% would their employees are rude, they are constantly out of product like CONSTANTLY half the time I go there they are out of chicken! It's literally Kentucky fried chicken like how can you be out of chicken? At that point close the store?

Worst KFC experience ever. Saw a sign that said livers are back. Pulled up to order and was greeted with ,"What do you want," in a very rude tone. I asked about livers, she said, " We're out, again in a rude tone. So I ordered the $20 fill up and asked about the sides and was rudely told , "There are no substitutions." I wasn't even asking for a substitute but whatever. Got to the window and wasn't greeted, she took debit card and gave me my food with no words spoken. Terrible customer service but whatever, maybe she had a bad day and as long as the food is good then I'm good. That wasn't the case though. 8 piece is supposed to be 2 breasts, 2 thighs, 2 legs and 2 wings. I received 4 thighs, 2 wings, 1 leg and 1 breast. All of which were greasy and had clearly been sitting for a while. I worked at this location 20 years ago and no way in hell would chicken like that be sent out. This is why I go to Popeyes almost exclusively now. Popeyes customer service is usually pretty bad but the food is always delicious and on point. My last three visits to this kfc have been pretty poor and probably won't be back anytime time soon. Save yourself the headache and disappointment and go to Popeyes or Bojangles.

Great prices on tacos, cheaper than Taco Bell for the same type of items. Plus they have burgers, fries, and shakes, as well as excellent "Cheesecake Bites". UNFORTUNATELY the staff frequently shuts down the shake machine early, and even closes the restaurant early. Seriously, who takes down the shake machine during the dinner rush "for cleaning" because they want to go home early? Bad employees, that's who. I've worked fast food, it doesn't take that long. Leave the stupid shake machine ON. Also don't serve customers cheesecake bites that were deep fried hours ago and are now semi-fossilized. Y'all know better. There's a reason there's only one Del Taco left in Tucson, certain bad employees.

Passedby BK asked the Window clerk for a hot fresh hamburger. No cheese bread  toasted hard.    My way ... Then I told the window clerk at 9:43 pm , didn't his name.  He said we have lots of hamburgers which one,  I said a double whopper and thank you since he was being ass hole.  He said he wasn't going to serve,   I was so happy he said that.., So I went to Micky  D's and they served me a GREAT burger.  I won't ever go to BK again.  Especially since you have ass holes working the window.  .  
THANKS AGAIN for directing me to the best burger !   

And hot hot fries ....

Went in at 330pm and all the food looked like it had been sitting there since the lunch rush.. dried up and crusty. No grilled veggies, sofritas looked  inedible, no black beans. I mean come on geez.. The food service is the Reno/Sparks area is lacking so bad with standards it's sad. Guess that's my clue to eat at home instead of patronizing these business that constantly underwhelm you but charge a premium price. Needless to say I left without purchasing their old left out food. Lesson learned ‍

Do not go to this Del Taco. I ordered a # 2 Combo Burrito Meal Told them to make it a large. The cost $10.00 Total. Pulled out of the drive thru turned right on Broadway reached into bag for a French Fry & they were cold. I did a u turn and went back for a refund & was told the heat Lamp is 110 Degrees & they should not be cold. The Burrito was as small as a Bean Burrito & the large Coke was Packed full of Ice. Over the last 2 Years the guy who owns this place has really Messed it up. Just another Greedy Fast food chain Owner who is only into making Money & not Keeping customers happy. Remember Taco Bell Is right Down The Street. Go there instead.

I've been to this KFC before and told certain specials were "sold out." But, went here today and they redeemed themselves! Yes, it was past 2 p.m., but I often eat lunch late. So just a note for those of you looking for the $5 fill up (or whatever) a later lunch sometimes pays off. I got the original in the drive thru and, after another stop on the way home, the chicken still tasted fresh (not as though it had been under a heat lamp for a long time) and the potatoes and everything else was good. Soda slightly watery, but who cares? (It's only $5!) The service was friendly and accurate. That's a thumbs up from me.

This is the worst chipotle I have ever been to. If I could rate them a 0 stars I would, first and foremost there was no steak or chicken in the entire store apparently so I did not eat here, while waiting for them to even begin making my burrito I took a look around the restaurant and I have never seen such a disgusting restaurant in my entire existence. There were pizza boxes with flies around them in the main seating area that didn't appear to belong to anyone. There was trash all over the floors and tables in the restaurant. The serving station was covered in queso beans paper towels and just looked like a disgusting mess. The tortillas looked to be rock solid as the person couldn't even unfold them to place them into the tortilla heater.  The list goes on. Some staff were wearing masks and gloves and others weren't. One staff member was talking at the top of their lungs on the line on their personal phone not paying any attention to customers. I will never come here again personally or order because it is clear they don't take good care of the food here snd it is of grave concern to me.

Hell no. I am completely done with this location. Why? Read on.

This KFC never has anything in stock. Today was no exception. I ordered a #15 (I think) combo, and they couldn't fulfill my request due to a supply constraint with some sauce that goes on it. Okay, whatever. But this next item was what broke the camel's back; I ordered a chicken pot pie. They had to go look to see if they had any pot pies; of course, they didn't have that either. What?! Pot pies?! One of the biggest things they're known for besides their chicken, which they also probably don't have? And they're out? I would have accepted it if this were the first occurrence, or even the second occurance. But it's at LEAST every other time I come here.

I'm not sure if it's only this location, or it's all of them, but many aspects of this location leave much to be desired; from the pungent diarrhea odor on the exterior, to the rude and sometimes clueless attitudes of the staff, to the spotty food quality, I can't say I'd ever come back here again. Sorry, not sorry.

Came in here for lunch last week and again today, both times I found myself standing at the counter for 15+ minutes just waiting to place my order when there was nobody else in line. I have never seen employees work so slowly in my life. I literally watched a guy take close to a minute just to wrap up a taco. Another guy comes up from the back of the kitchen with a tray of biscuits and spends about 3 minutes "rotating" the biscuits that were already in the warmer. It was painful to watch these employees moving so slow. As of right now I've been sitting in the dining room for 20 minutes waiting on my order to come up which is literally one chicken breast, one biscuit and a small mashed potato and gravy. And now they are telling me that they don't have any breasts in original so I have to settle for crispy.....

Horrible customer service. Employees would rather chat with each other and joke around than attend to customers. 15 min wait on Whopper Jr with cheese, no pickle meal. Meanwhile 8 cars go through drive thru. Fountain drinks are out most of the time and they won't change them for you upon request. Poor management truly shows through.

Shinier newer less-rectangular than my normal Chipotles. And, at 8 at night, service was friendly and quick. 

I got my first Chipotle salad here and the steak was fresh and I like the dressing. I do miss the tortilla, but it's probably for the better tonight. 

Rounding up to 5 stars with the hope that the earlier low ratings might be time specific and others will give this location a try at night or service will even out at other times.

My friend got the nachos and I tried the beyond avocado taco and a tamale.
They weren't bad but we wouldn't order them again.
They were really busy for a Sunday night and the manager apparently only scheduled 2 people.
Things were slow but they just don't have enough people scheduled. Seems like there's a steady stream through the drive through so if more than one person comes in the lobby at a time, like we did, it really slows things down.

This place will get your order wrong over 50% of the time.  Last week I ordered a whopper with cheese and ketchup only.  What I got was no cheese and mayo.  I was charged for the extra cheese and didn't get it.  How do you get mayo from ketchup. Yesterday I ordered three different sandwich and got only two.  I also wanted BBQ source for my chicken nuggets but got Buffalo source.  They need to hire people that can read so customer will get what they ordered as it is printed on the receipt.
The bacon toast slider is absolutely delightful! I typically order mine with a double portion of egg and bacon. The wheat bread, toast sandwich is mouthwatering!

This place is just getting worst by the week. I ordered extra crispy chicken but got what seemed liked old and left over chicken pieces. They are selling old and stale food and getting away with it because customers are picking it up and have no way of checking food quality until they get home, then it is too late to go back and complete.
Boycott this place.
Burgers were kinda dry and they only put one slice of cheese on my double Whopper w/ cheese. Fries were good, though.

It's a testament to the brand that I keep trying this place... the worst KFC in the entire United States. Picked up my order at 509PM.... I received 12 inedible chicken tenders, dried and hard and most certainly leftover from lunch time if not yesterday. Not a single dipping sauce was included in the order. Not one. This place screws up every single order. The 8pc bucket was good and reasonably fresh... they still stole my money with the inedible tenders and lack of any dipping sauces.

Really Burger King?? Really? You can't hire smart enough people to not mess up?? I go today and order 3 meals for 3 people. I pull up and a manager sees the person who's about to charge my card and says, "Why aren't you checking before you charge him?" The reason was the manager saw that MY order had been given to the car in front of me.......... So then I pull forward and wait 10 minutes. She kept saying "Whopper, Whopper" when I said that it was the Bacon King, the number 3. But no, not only did I have to wait, but I was also given the wrong meals! I got 3 Whopper meals with bacon and paid the price of the Bacon King meals. 

They thought they could make it more efficient by putting in two lanes, but it just made ALL of the employees confused and highly inefficient. I am NEVER going to order drive-thru from this Burger King EVER again! They will just mess up the order!!! I would rather go inside and order and take it. However, I'm not going to go here for a while, not just to stay away from junk food for a bit, but also because I'm incredibly annoyed by the organization here.

Totally disappointing experience at KFC. don't get me wrong, fast food is in its own realm of quality and we all understand that. But my food was so terrible from this kfc!

To start off, I ordered the 3 piece tender meal with the Nashville hot sauce or whatever it's called. I got two tenders and a nugget!!! Someone put that box together and put that tiny piece of chicken in and was like "yup". 

The fries were extremely stale, cold, and sooo salty. Totally inedible. 

The Mac and cheese was overcooked and tasted mushy. I could get more satisfaction out of a Kraft box 

I think it might have ruined kfc for me forever sadness
4:30 pm.. No extra crispy chicken available... 20+ minutes to wait.. Why as a restaurant are you not ready for an evening rush

To be fair, I do not know WHICH Dunkin' did this to me, because, I ordered delivery. But, the food was so bad I didn't even think to take pictures. We just put it out of the house, into the compost pile and out to the alley cats, who I hope will forgive us one day! 

The last time I went - years ago- I got choked by a doughnut. I didn't want to go back, ever. My sister kept telling me, "But, their breakfast sandwiches are great!" 

Today, I challenged that lie and came out victorious. My taste buds died an agonizing death in the process, but, that's a small price to pay to prove my sister wrong. 

We ordered one set of hashbrowns, figuring they would come in a bag of moderately generous proportions, like most everyone else might send; and, there was only two of us. The proportions were not generous and I cannot call what arrived brown, though hashed it certainly was. 

I am not sure what they sent, but, I can tell you that it was 6 dinky, oil soaked pieces of something that I am fairly certain was dug out of the trash. I mean, why would hashbrowns be covered in bits of green and smell like they had been burnt on a grill? 

The drinks were... creamer. We ordered two Dunkincinos, or however they spell them. That they were half chocolate I can believe. That any cofee was used in their making, I cannot!
