I am now brewing a pot of coffee for the express purpose of using the creamer that was sent in the cups to add to it and make one real cup of coffee with cream and a little hot chocolate flavor. 

One of the lids was off the drinks when it arrived, by the way. I think it was the only horrible bit that was truly an accident and not some wild attempt at murder by proxy. They sure killed my appetite with whatever that stuff was in the bags that was pretending to be food! 

I see why they are no longer mentioning donuts in their name! I ordered two things that I was told were doughnuts. The one I tasted was somehow overly wet, overly undercooked and obviously so old anyone could tell it wasn't born this morning! 

This was, without a doubt, the foulest meal that I have ever met. It was so bad, I would rather sit next to a pile of dirty diapers and eat a moderately bad home-cookeed meal that was made by a person I neither like nor trust, than ever go near Dunkin' again. 

I hope the manager reads this and considers who was around, preparing food on this day,  and does something to save what little reputation Dunkin' has left. 

Hey, Manager of Dunkin': I know there is one woman in Tucson that believes, still, she can get a good breakfast sandwich from one of the local stores. Don't lose her!  She's the only person I know - and I know many - who would go near Dunkin', or its former incarnation, since the early 1990's, except when she forced them to!

UPDATE: It's been about 5 hours, maybe more, since we put those sandwiches out. The alley cats have not eaten them. The ants are avoiding them! Not even kidding! 

I think I may have a deepening PTSD from this experience. I was never scared of croissants or sausage sandwiches, before now!

Stopped by this location after doing a little shopping at the mall! Got home and was surprised to see the smallest hardest chicken strips I've ever gotten at KFC! I was not in the mood to drive all the way back 20 mins so I threw it in the trash! I know this is fast food but usually KFC is wayyyy better than this! Totally turned me off of going there or to any KFC ever again!
Extremely disappointed with my purchase today. My husbands strips looked like chicken nuggets & my pot pie was burnt & extremely dry! Never again.. such a waste of time & money!

Worst Del Taco Ever, First time I went there employees were kind of mean, I decided to give them another chance but the manager Julia (I asked for her name) is very extremely rude. Thinking about contacting corporate about her. Don't waste your time going to this location and go somewhere else.

was craving some fried chicken so I came to this KFC. dirty place. half the restaurant was covered in puddles with water dripping down the ceiling. dangerous and not sanitary. employee was less than helpful when ordering and then blames me, the customer who's paying $10 dollars for a 3 piece meal (overpriced) that bottle water is extra with the combo and I "should have told him" because there's nothing he can do. no thanks, never again.

We have been waiting for a Chipotle to come to town as we frequent them when out of town. The staff was extremely friendly but that is where the positive review ends. The portions were tiny. We were just in Columbus Ohio last weekend and were served twice as much in a bowl as this location. It actually looked like the advertisement. Here we got barely any rice, my chicken had several grisly pieces I had to spit into a napkin and a sharp bone!  The steak was the same. Even the lettuce was sparse. Like a little sprinkle. I may give it one more shot to see if this was just a miss guided employee or if this location really does short you.

The manager here is the nicest man. BK should give this location a face lift because the outside does not accurately represent the fast, tasty, enjoyable little experience I had while driving through Northern Tucson. Thanks for selling the brand new Impossible Burger. It was ready fast and is an eco responsible alternative for us vegetarians.
Do yourself a huge favor and eat Sharks fish and chicken instead. It is located directly behind this KFC.
This place is horrible. It was my first visit at this location. The kitchen wasn't clean. Salad ingredients weren't fresh. Steak salad tasted awful. I will never visit this restaurant again.

we had a mobile order last week .
the nicest young man was at the counter .it was evening,after 7 p.m. , a thrusday ,might actually have been the 12th ...
( it was a night that ran out of whopper rolls ,and he nicely explained that to every customer ).
he was a thin kid and I wished I got his name .
hard worker ,he straightened things ,replaced cups ,and even swept the front end floor.. .
I hope burger king sees the potential in this young man !
the food ,well it's burger king so ...there's that .
those jalapeño things are pretty good though, we ordered two ,with some other things .

This place keeps disappointing. Really bad driving through service, 9 out of 10 times they mess up the order which is annoying because you pick up the order via drive through with no time to check the order. The chicken which used to be the best but now is very dry and very salty. The mashed potato is now a powdered concoction and so is the biscuit. Sad.

Colonel Sanders, where the heck are you?

We came here on a Sunday afternoon to take advantage of the one cent Whopper "Detour" deal. We put in our required mobile order when we arrived.  Since there were almost no other customers, the Whoppers and fries we ordered were ready very quickly.  The food quality met our expectations 100%.  Thanks for the deal BK!

We stopped here from a road trip and we were hungry for a burger. We ordered the whopper n fries and it didn't disappoint. We placed our order and within 3-4 mins we had are food. Everything was good n hot we were very happy with our visit to Burger King. Parking lot is nicely lit up at night with cameras on each side of the building.

KFC

I have been going to this location for 20 plus years or since their doors have been open. They use to be great! The worst chipotle I have ever eaten at. I ate in. The meat on my salad was ice cold. While the portion of meat was plentiful, they skimped on the lettuce and cheese. My daughter and I decided to get take-out. Two Fill Up Boxes and a Big Box. I asked about the two Fill Ups the gal said it's a better deal than just the pot pie by itself. Every time I've come to this location, they rarely ever have chicken.  It could be 5:30 on a Monday and they ask to wait 20 minutes.  I usually just move along.  Consistently disappointing at best

KFC use to be my favorite spot for dinner... When I was in the mood for extra crispy chicken.. But this place has went down hill. I don't know if it's management or what. This was the worst I've ever been treated at a fast food place. I ordered my food got to the window paid for it and realized they over charged me. theyre awesome!! really great customer service! i accidentally ordered the sausage egg and cheese and i realized the meat was pork. i cant eat pork so i went back and asked if there was anything we could do. they happily helped and gave me the impossible burger instead. it made my morning!

I'm so unbelievably upset with this del taco. Being that it is the only one in Tucson I sometimes get a craving for it. It's also a 30 min drive from my house to this location. Worst place to go get chicken, they are slow and rude! If you buy a meal that comes with the drink, you will be charged for it even if you don't want it, per manager orders! This place customer service and preparing orders to order is ridiculous, ordered 2 chicken sandwich combos, did t receive fries for either, sandwiches weren't even wrapped properly, and the #1 combo chicken wasn't done, and when I banged on window for fries I was given 1 fry instead of 2 as the cashier proceeded to go outside to talk with her friend

Horrible experience. Ordered10 piece original recipe on line. I had been in STL partying with friends and just needed some finger food to munch on the drive home. So as I drove pass this WC, I pulled in and ordered my favorite, which is Combo #2. I also wanted a small order of onion rings, but they were sold out, so I tried the Mozzarella Sticks instead. 

The Combo #2 is; two Double Cheese Sliders, fries and a drink. For my drink, I choose a Diet Coke. Having a diet soda balances out the calories and carbs from the combo meal. My burgers were hot and gooey, just like a slider should be. The fries (Crinkle Cut) were hot, as were the cheese sticks. 

The young lady taking the order over the intercom and my money at the window was polite and efficient. I was through the drive-thru in nothing flat and back on the road to IL, with a tummy filling snack. 

I rounded up for some charity, but I don't remember which one. I'm a sucker for round-ups. 

When: August 4, 2021

I should have had my head examined before going back to this location. I'm dumb.

I personally love KFC and always bring my service to this location. I have never had any problems...until i received a red hair threaded between my chicken. Just had the worst whopper ever.  The patty was burned to a crisp - literally! This was by far the WORST EXPERIENCE with this location in all the times we've been there! Soggy buns on a whopper is more tolerable than this! (And I hate soggy stuff."

This has got to be the worst, most unreliable, inconsistent quality and dirtiest kfc in the entire chain of kfc's. Staffed by an unconscious bunch of no pride having slobs. Been in lots of Popeye's and KFC places, since I love fried chicken, but the KFC place at Franklin Mills was the worst I have been in to date, so bad that I actually got my lazy butt to write a review. horrible...I know they are busy at 12:30 but...I got 2 whopper jr.'s.with fries..one whopper without onion or cheese...one without sauce,onion & tomato..the lettuce was missing...the burgers were overdone..as I waited for 10 min...I heard her tell him 3 different times how I wanted the burgers..[what to leave off]...3 times...I heard her repeat it 3 different times while I was waiting....really...and he still got it wrong...so ...special orders do upset them...lol .I could of done a better job & I am 68 ...won't go back there

Just got our order delivered through Door Dash and both orders were 100% wrong. This Chipotle is my favorite location in Center City! I live really close to the one on 15th and Walnut, but I always walk an extra 4 blocks to come to this one because the service and food quality is so much better at this one! 

I always get a bowl with extra white rice, half chicken/steak, fajitas, hot salsa, verde, cheese, and then a vinaigrette to mix in. In my humble opinion, this is perfection! I really like the staff here, they always seem to be in a good mood and laugh with one another. They are also generous with the portions! 

I wanted the regular size chips and guac, but they only had large bags of chips left for the day. So they gave me a large guac for the price of the regular. I was happy they made that swap for me! Love this Chipotle! Highly recommend walking a few extra blocks to go to this location!
I enjoyed the Iceberg lettuce. Got a Famous star with cheese today. Found it had Romain lettuce? It was Gross and will no longer visit Carl's Jr anymore. Great choice you lost a Great customer.

Stopped by with my family for a quick meal. The outdoor seating was nice  Good service. Food however, is over priced & under portioned.  We ordered queso & chips with our food for 4$ extra. Our bag of chips was about 1/4 of the way full & the queso was in a 4 oz container. Wish I would've known. I wouldn't have paid 4$ for that.. The queso doesn't taste like queso. It tastes like Chipotle peppers. Not the yummy queso we are used to.  Our steak bowls were delectable & full of flavor. The grilled peppers & corn were great. The bowls were worth 8$ for the steak. Our son's chicken & cheese quesadilla hardly had any cheese on it. So that was a little disappointing. He still ate it, though.  All in all, our meal was good & we will be back. Except next time, we'll hold off on the queso & chips.

Worst service I've ever experienced! So stupid that even if you order online, you have to wait in the drive thru.. if I wanted to wait in the drive thru, I wouldn't order it online! No where does it state that they are ONLY open in the drive thru! The front doors are locked and say "we're open to go orders only" okay I get that no dine in. 

After waiting 20 minutes since I placed the order and waiting in the drive thru, I get to the window for the girl to tell me, it's gonna be 10 minutes for the chicken!!! Are you kidding me?! If I didn't order online and have to cancel my order thru the online shit! I would have left! Never again! Save yourself the bullshit! Go somewhere else!

So I know other reviews are bashing this place hard, but I didn't have a single problem. I went fairly early (a little after 11am), ordered, and then pulled up to the window all with no hiccups. A very nice young man greeted me and gave me my food and even made sure I got extra sauce (it's the little things you know). I don't remember his name but it was the freshest popcorn chicken I'd ever eaten from a KFC and the sides were perfect too. I was hesitant to pull up because of the reviews but man what a difference 1 great employee can make. I will happily go back.

I used to like to come here because I love kfc and all the sides but today we came in and went through the drive through to order a bucket of chicken meal. When we arrived at the window, my son wanted to add on a sprite and some wedges. The man working the drive through said "we can't add to an order". ???Uh, what?? Isn't it your job to sell food here? Anyhow, we said ok. We will just pay for this order and start another one. He proceeds to tell us we need to pull back around. Is this a joke? There was nobody behind us. We just said to forget the second order but we will no longer be back. Very poor customer service. Popeyes, here we come!

My husband and I have given this location a chance several times. They regularly forget items in our orders and mess up dishes. This time, we ordered identical meals, but as you can see they were completely different - one bowl looks like it never made it past the first station (no salsa, cheese, lettuce, sour cream, etc). Cherry on top was the chunk of steak we found - we are both vegetarians, so it was gross to find red meat in the bowl and made us nervous to eat our meals. They also forgot our order of chips and guac. Thankfully used the app to receive a refund for the missing chips and a free meal for the botched order, but this will be the last time we order from this store.

Not very pleased with my 10 piece meal.I received 5 extra crispy  drumsticks.Did not ask for extra crispy.Sat and waited for half hour  for old chicken just thrown into a bucket not even hot.We did not ask for Fries extra Crispy.Coleslaw tasted funky.I dont know whats happening at this location,but its not like it used to be. Unhappy too late to call as closed @9 pm.

First, the order taker was distracted. I gave him half my order, then he had to ask me to start over. As I result, I ended up forgetting to place part of my order (so that cost BK a sale). The order-taker sounded like he was trying hard to impress someone by using a goofy voice and slang. Then when he handed me a single bag at the window, I asked if that was everything and he said he thought so (like it was a joke). Since my order was screwed up the last two times I got food at this BK, I pulled to the parking lot and checked the bag. Sure enough, I got shorted a Whopper (which I had paid for). So, I park, I go inside, I explain the situation to the "manager" with the blonde, pink and electric blue hair. She has the crew make the Whopper while the rest of my food gets cold. I point out to her that it's the third time in a row this BK location has messed up an order by me and responds with something about how it's her first day back after being out for awhile. This BK is close and I like the menu. Each time my order gets screwed up I vow not to come back, but then after a couple months I give it another chance. Hopefully a couple months from now something will finally get fixed.

Unlike Boobie, my experiences have been terrific here. When I lived in Willingboro a decadeish ago, this is the DD we supported. We have never experienced any problems then, and never have since that time period. We go every time we visit Jersey, and we visit a lot within the weekish that we visit my mom. My mom loves this location, and I can see why. Prices are just fine. They are patient with questions, and with decisions. We ordered a Coolatta, a regular coffee, a tropical-mango smoothie, a turkey sausage-egg-cheese-muffin sandwich, a croissant with tuna salad, and hash browns. Everything was made to increase your pleasure in life. And, oh, how it did. 

This review is dedicated to the gentleman who wished me "Happy Mother's Day" at this location back in 2004.

I went to Del Taco after being at the bars and my friends and I were very hungry. I was very sober and chose to drive my closest friends there because we were all hungry and Taco Bell changed their hours. After being in the line for 30 minutes my friends stepped out of the car to pee at our friends house that is right next to Del Taco. We had already ordered a $50 meal and I made sure they respectfully made sure they peed the right way. The manager made sure to come out and lie and say that other customers saw people come out of my car and made sure to kick us out of the drive thru line. When we arrived at the drive through line they made us leave and told us to never come back. This organization is awful and has no understand to the community around it.
wow. 28$ for a bucket of dried out old chicken and 2 sides of slop... beagle approved.

It's a fuckin KFC. Idk why it has 1 star. I mean it's a $4 chicken sandwich. What do you people expect. You go there and you get a deep friend piece of some good ass chicken, eat it, feel like shit, and do it again the next day. 

Anyways. I like the spicey chicken sandwich. And I'm only writing this because my fiancé is taking forever with her friends at a bar that doesn't serve food and all I can think about is A god damn chicken sandwich.
Went in at 9:30 and they told me that they didn't have any chicken left. I find that hard to believe

Go to buy lunch on Thursday, November 3rd...around 11:30 or so. Pull into the drive thru and order, only to be told that the registers weren't working and there wasn't a manager there to fix them, sorry...are you serious???
Ok, so I try again on Friday, the 4th. It's 11:10...I need to order for myself and a co-worker. 
Again, pull up to the Drive thru--one pot pie please and a 2 piece extra crispy meal. 
Sorry, there's a 20 minute wait for Pot Pies and we only have original chicken...wait!!!! You only sell chicken and you have NOTHING prepared??? How are you still in business???? On my way to Popeyes, you guys are ridiculous!!!!

The drive through was backed up in hermitage, they were out of chicken pieces (had strips), and slow getting our food as several employees stood around. We were short some strips so went inside and had to wait again and they just handed me box - here's your strips. Service was bad, wait time was bad, food was cold by the time we got home. Do yourself a favor and go to another restaurant!

KFC isn't my first choice for chicken but that's what my family and I chose today for lunch. This KFC isn't the best probably not the worst, but like all KFC's they're all overpriced and not the cleanest. The food never seems to be fresh. One good thing about this one is that the service is good and they're nice.

I just went and grabbed some regular beef tacos add  beans.... the lady even repeated it correctly. Well somehow the only thing in the taco shell 
was beans... idk maybe the speaker box was broken because she did ask me several times to speak louder, I was yellowing out my car window haha 
Not cool man. However it was bomb, just not what we ordered. Also, this is maybe the 3rd tome we've gotten the wrong order.

The last two times we've been there they literally didn't have fried chicken.   You just can't make that up.   

Before 2018 they were fine.   

Starting in 2018 the fried chicken was hit or miss on quality.   In one bucket there were no breasts, in another it was undercooked.  

The reason for taking the time to write this is that they've simply stopped trying to even make fried chicken.   

Fire the district and store managers, just for a chance to fix the location.  

We will never know if that happened because we are completely finished with this location forever.   

FYI Chik Fil A is a mile away.  They nail orders fast every time.   Five stars.

Let me first say that I've been a Chipotle fan for years. Even when there were the store closings & contaminations. I waited an ample amount of time for that to clear up & went back..a faithful customer. Whereas, anyone else in my family swore off the place for good. I went to the Chipotle in Pinellas Park, FL today. The service was good, I got my usual - double carnitas & fixin's on the side - no problem. Got home to eat my wonderful lunch (starving ), took one bite & could barely swallow it. OMG! The carnitas is SOOO SALTY!! I cannot & could not eat it. I am on a low sodium diet &  it used to be that the Chipotle carnitas was a luxury to me as the carnitas was appropriately seasoned to fit my diet. Not only that, but I wasted quite a bit of money & I was still starving! It's not the first time this has happened. I tried a few months ago, at a different location, & had the same result. At the time,  I chalked it up to a one time thing. Obviously, I was wrong. And the nutrition calculator on the website is a joke. It does not specifically list the sodium content (for the carnitas, anyway). So now Chipotle is also on my list of "NO NOs".

In today for a quick breakfast. Employees very friendly. Coffee made exactly as ordered and my croissant with sausage egg and cheese was perfect.
There were a couple dirty tables in the dining room but other then that the store and restroom was clean and well stocked.

The service and quality is absolutely terrible. This location currently only accepts mobile ordering and when you walk in, there are 3 people hustling at the pick up order station while everyone else is lounging in the back! No one is friendly at this location and most of them don't seem to have a clue what they're even doing. Not only did they get my order partially wrong and late -- the steak was absolutely horrendous!!!! I had to spit it out it was so tough or full of fat!!!

This was a disappointment. I was just going to snack. 

I went to the doors. Both locked. 

No Signage. 

I knocked, they YELLED AT ME to do the drive thru. Both of the men. 
Like I was supposed to know.  No signs for Drive thru only. 
This was silly. And , no I did not obey. The chicken isn't that special. I went somewhere else.

Okay, I'll have to preface this by saying that everyone will probably will think I'm weird for reviewing a Burger King, but this is not just any Burger King. This is a really good Burger King, much better than other Burger Kings in the Philadelphia area. The food is prepared well, the ingredients are fresh, the workers are all working as a cohesive unit, and they took the extra step to answer a question I didn't even ask yet. My oldest son and I ordered a Whopper and I asked for a plastic knife. The young man behind the counter, seeing that I had asked for a knife, said, "The next time you order, you can ask us to cut the sandwich in half." Our Whopper was great. Our fries were crispy and delicious. Both of my sons love the indoor playground. There's even an Internet kiosk area for web surfing. Check this Burger King out. It's awesome!

I can see why KFC is only big on the west coast. How can you have this many employees standing around with 4 of us waiting good , now 15 min for our orders. They're not even high school kids. potato wedges were a 15min wait, chicken was a 10min wait. this is fast foot. are they high?

I absolutely love Del Taco back in California. I moved here to Tucson and this Del Taco sucks so bad. I'm sad because it's my favorite fast food chain. Against my better judgement I went here for lunch. I got home and noticed I only had half of my order. Last time I ordered food there and the cashier had a bad attitude, and said there was no such thing as a Del beef burrito. Anyone reading this should steering clear of this Del Taco. Go to the one on Grant Rd. A little further, but better service and you'll get all of your food.

I would have given 5 stars because the mash potatoes were awful but the fried chicken was fabulous. I just moved here from L.A,CA. KFC's there are awful. My first order here thru Grub hub was great! Really fresh chicken! Not greasy. good Cole slaw. Will be a regular..
Conveniently located in broadway in Boise. We used the drive through at this Del taco. Service was fast and friendly and our order was correct. Also gave plenty of sauce packets.

I get the Whopper with no meat (since they no longer carry the veggie burger) and it's actually really good and the staff doesn't seem confused when you ask for no meat. So for that I'm happy. The parking lot is clean and the drive thru is pretty efficient.

I came to fulfill my White Castle dream. I thought 4 sliders was all I needed, because I am a White Caste virgin. Fortunately my friend ordered the crazy pack with more sliders than I can carry with my hands. Yeah, 4 was not enough. I don't think anybody can ever get enough of these bad boys. It's like eating popcorn, you just eat and eat and eat because it's like eating nothing. 3 bites and that slider is gone.

3/5 is because as much fun as it was to eat here, the burgers wasn't really all that great. I enjoyed the activity more than the eating. If you're a foodie, you already know you have better food to eat. If you're coming here because you like the White Castle name, then you're going to eat here regardless....and I won't judge.

The food here is the same as all other Chipotle's but the big drawback is long lines and hastily made burritos. It feels like they just slop everything together and half the time they end up breaking the burrito. I hate when they have to rewrap them. The long line combined with the hastily made burritos is enough for me to drive 2 miles out of the way to the next closest Chipotle. I love the chips & guacamole (I know its extra) with a steak burrito, spicy salsa, and a Pibb Extra to wash it down.

This KFC has the worst quality chicken tenders we have ever had. They are dried out and inedible. We actually threw them in the garbage, they were so dry, hard, chewy, and overdone. They must have been sitting out for a long time. They were not fresh. Also, they forgot our biscuits. We won't go to this location ever again.
Guess I'm pretty easy to please. Whopper Wednesday $3.69 for a whole meal, it was awesome. Thanks BK!
Heard a lot about Chipotle, decided to try out their steak tacos and wasn't impressed. The meat was tough, fatty, and too spicy. Taco Bell is a lot better than this place.

I heard something calling my name, and then I realized it was Kentucky Fried Chicken.  I have always loved KFC and this place is spot on.  

My choice was the two piece meal with 2 small sides. I chose green beans and mashed potatoes and gravy.   The meal also comes with a biscuit. It did not disappoint.  The chicken was moist and tender and the sides were delicious also.  

My only problem was with the drive thru.  After waiting twenty minutes in line, (people were starting to honk) I finally pulled up to the window.  The cashier asked me what I had and I told her.  She said, "that was $5.75."  I said "I thought you told me it was $7.16"  After a little confusion on the inside I was given my meal.  They apologized for the wait.

Norm McDonald, aka Colonel Sanders, is right.  It is "finger lickin' good."
We wanted a quick Whole30 compliant meal and found it here!! We had a carnitas salad with salsa and guacamole. Delicious! Service was friendly and the restaurant is well maintained. I will be back.

If I could give NO star I would! This used to be THE DD location to get coffee. The last several visits have been mediocre at best. Today, however will be the last time I come here. I ordered an egg and cheese wake up wrap and a medium iced coffee with "mocha swirl, cream and two Splenda." I order this coffee ALL the time. I drove up to the window and the coffee handed to me was black. I said that's not mine and the lady at the window handed it to the lady working the drive-through. She gave me the most dirty look like I did something wrong and walked over to the side to put a shot of cream in it. I don't know what they did but this didn't even come close to tasting like the coffee I order between 4-5 Times a week. Then, I bit into my "egg and cheese" wrap to discover it's sausage and cheese only. Are you kidding me ?! I even put a dollar in the tip jar, before all of this that  I wish I could take back now ! When I put the tip in the jar the lady working the window didn't even say thank you. Poor service, wrong order and people work here who treat you like you did something wrong when it was them who messed up. I understand people aren't perfect but to give me the look that lady did was ridiculous and rude ! Spend your money elsewhere!!!

I love chipotle! I've been meaning to review this spot for awhile now. The food is delicious get the steak burrito the way you like I promise you won't be disappointed. The price is perfect for what you get I'm for sure stuffed when I leave there. Chipotle definitely kills the competitors. Now I will agree with most they are kinda slow management needs to step up there. but I mean hey if they have long lines food must be good right? Oh and I love the fact they have one of my favorite hot sauces Tabasco jalapeño delicious! If you've never tried chipotle do yourself a favor and try it.

Fast food is hard to get a 4 star from me. Clean bathrooms, polite staff, clean lobby, clean drink area. Food was made almost to order. Onion rings were hot and fresh. Meat on the whopper was good. Far ages were empty and clean.

My husband picked up a bucket of chicken and some sides. He requested no biscuits because we don't like them. When he got it all home, there it was- a box of biscuits. Had to throw them away. I hate to waste food! Then we started to eat the chicken. So over cooked and dry there was hardly any flavor. So disappointing! It will be a while before we get food there again.

Good location with a parking lot right next to it. This place used to be one of the best, where friendly staff gave you generous portions and fresh ingredient. Now the food lapsed into mediocrity and service significantly deteriorated since earlier this year. Perhaps budget cut or personnel changes?

My friend and I had a steak bowl and a chicken bowl. The people had problems slowing down to hear what you want. And PLOP, she just dropped something that I didn't want into my bowl... As mentioned in other reviews, they have been skimping on ingredients. I asked for some more lettuce and she got agitated and took some lettuce off my bowl...
The steak was overcooked and smelled like burned charcoal, my friend could not even chew it. The chicken bowl was alright but I started feeling hungry with a growling stomach in an hour.

Went here today to try it out for the first time looking for something healthy to eat. I was angry standing in line and the girl skipped over me and went to someone else for their order. I moved up in line and was told i need to order first before going to the register i said i know she skipped over us and didnt take our order. 
So i waited a minute then a nice gentleman that works there apologized and took my order that made me a little happier. I ordered a bowl with steak lettuce pinto beans and brown rice food was edible but not worth the price sad to say every bite of the steak was chewy and fatty. Very disappointed and will not return to this place in the future. Not worth it.

The Chipotle menu really makes it simple:

What kind of meat do you want?
What kind of beans?
Cheese?
Sour Cream/Guacamole?
Anything else?

The menus are streamlined for easy reading. No staring at the menu trying to figure out what you want, Chipotle has cut that down for you. When I'm really hungry I'm kind of glad they make my life that much simpler. 

The food is delicious. The burritos are wrapped tightly and packed with flavor. I usually get the steak, which is always perfectly cooked. I'm actually craving a burrito right now...

Well, after the last two failed attempts at a good experience at this place, I decided to review.

My first experience, several months ago, it seemed all of the employees were very disinterested in serving customers.  I ordered shrimp, and of course, they had to cook them fresh - no surprise, as all LJS's do this.  The worst part was when the employee took the frier basket out of the frier, and hand picked, yes, HAND picked the shrimp out of the basket with bare hands onto my plate.  I phoned the comment line on the receipt, and never received a call back.

Last week, I was craving some of the new KFC boneless chicken, so I loaded the car, and four of us went to the restaurant.  As we walked in, the manager came from the dining room and yelled at the kitchen, "could we stop the employee meeting and help some customers?", and left.  Someone appearing to be a new hire, stood at the register to take our order.  She was very unprofessional, which don't get me wrong, is nothing new for a fast food joint, but she subsequently told us we couldn't order certain things, here and there, when another employee came over, and started pushing buttons and basically talking over her.  Strange enough...

I ordered some LJS food, a friend the same, and we asked to try the boneless KFC original - they advertised to try it for $1.00, so we ordered two pieces (the employee didn't know what we were talking about, so the hero from behind the counter came to our rescue again).  My kids ordered chicken bites, and paid.

About 15 minutes later, the LJS plates were out, minus the KFC bites dinners for the kids.  I stood at the counter, and they asked "What do you want?"  I said we're still waiting on those bites meals and the boneless chicken.  The manager comes out from the office, and said "where is the boneless?"

She proceeded to tell us they just dropped them, and they would bring it to our table.  About 10 minutes later, the new hire hollered out to the dining room - "Sir!" and pointed to a tray at the counter.  I could only assume that was the remainder of our food.  Meanwhile, other customers were standing around as well, and seemed very irritated.

We asked about the boneless, and again, were told it would be out. shortly.

After another 10 minutes, we went to the counter to ask again, only to find out they never started cooking them in the first place.  The manager decided to give us a coupon for a buy one get one free boneless chicken, and marked out the buy one part, and signed it.  Really?

Look, I love LJS and I love KFC for a treat once in a while, but wow, this is a disgrace.

This has to be the worst Kentucky fried chicken on the planet. We've had trouble in the past, and just thought it was a fluke. We went the other day and they had no white meat chicken at all, and their fryers didn't have oil in them. It seems odd for a fried chicken place. The worst part of it was, the employee wasn't sorry at all. It's not that she had a bad attitude, she just had zero empathy. I will never go here again.

This place sucks the management is not doing no management and someone needs to step up and run this place right took us 45 min to get a bucket of chicken witch they had said it would only take five minutes stay way from this place

Wow was today's breakfast trip a whopper. It was Murphy's Law today at this Burger King. The cashier charged me extra for my OJ without telling me before and then I waited for more than 15 minutes to get my food - while listening to a guy named David who I suppose was the manager demean his employees. Then I got my food and the croissanwich was burnt. And the two guys ahead of me waited longer than I did only be told after waiting for a long time that they were out of their French toast sticks. The line was so out of order that three people tried to order breakfast right at 10:30 but were told they had to switch to lunch.
Friendliest Chipotle in town! They all go above and beyond, had to wait on steak and not only did they gave me my meal free but I got a coupon for buy one get one free!

I'm not sure what else there is to say about KFC any more. We started off as strangers and grew to be friends, bickering and squabbling along the way. Then our  relationship got old and stale, and weird new things like the Double Down fail to interest me to any great extent.

Anyway, this KFC is just off Kirkwood Highway westbound. It has a parking area by itself. I went in to check it out and the restroom and dining area seemed rather clean. Ordered a chicken filet sandwich combo. The order was handled without fuss, except that they ran out of potato wedges and asked if I wanted a substitution. Among fast foods, I tend to think KFC is not exceptional and not even the chain offering the best values. But sometimes, you just want the chicken.

Burgers tasted freezer burnt. Yuck. Dining area was dirty. Floor was so greasy my daughter was sliding on it. 2 stars only because the lady at the counter was courteous. Would never have come here except had an out of town guest that had not had a slider in probably 10 years and was feeling reminiscent.

The food is very good and superb service.  Ordered Western BBQ Chicken slider with cheese.  Received them without cheese, returned and they replaced the whole thing without question.  Very friendly!  Very friendly especially with deaf customers like me and my wife.  Definitely will come back for more!

I HATE HATE HATE this KFC.  But it's the only one in town, that I'm aware of.   Maybe they're doing me a favor by making me not wanting to go there.  But as a member of the Overweight Jerks club, i need a little KFC thrown in me once a while or otherwise, they might revoke my membership.

Where to begin...The quality's not there.  They've screwed me on sides. They've charged me for gravy that's supposed to come with the meal.  The service..eh, kinda blegh.  The  weirdest thing that happened was one lady worker was telling another one in spanish that there's an 'emergency in the back and that she needed to go back there immediately."  I wonder what happened back there.  I'll never find out.  

It's always packed in there, despite it being kinda shitty.  I have to wait in lines to get screwed over by these jerks one way or another.  I gotta find another fried chicken source...
Oh, who am i kidding, i'll be there next week...
Take me now lord...lord sanders,,,

I have bought the California Whopper and JR. 
California Whopper several times at different 
Burger Kings ... They don't put much Guacamole 
On the burger! Ever
Go to another place this is not good!!!
Super slow...super super slow....like 30 minutes to an hour slow for popcorn chicken slow

This is THE WORST fast food restaurant I have ever visited. My last visit was my last ever. They are a bunch of dropout kids who never worked before running the place. The food is always terrible. Below KFC standards I'm sure...whatever they are...because they are not monitored here. My order is ALWAYS wrong. ALWAYS!!!!!!! I finally give up...totally not worth it. I'll go out of my way to go to a different location or just go to Popeyes if I'm that desperate for chicken strips.

I dunno I think everyone on this is trippin...
Fried chicken can only be good or great... 
It was good. And FYI it's KFC which is fast food. If you expect anything spectacular then go somewhere and spend more than 10$. I got what I expected.

You can never go wrong with Chipotle [: I came to this location last night with my housemates and some friends and it was packed! This is definitely the place to hang out at, university ave. not chipotle, on the weekends if you're a college student. Parking is free in the structure on weekends so that was a plus because street parking is hard to find on these busy nights. I ordered the burrito bowl, my friends got the carnitas, chicken, and steak burritos, and chicken tacos as well. Everyone was satisfied with their order and the only complaint is how expensive that are.. They nickel and dime you for everything! If you want guacamole you have to pay an additional $1.80 for a lousy scoop of green mush.. I am not able to have mexican food without guac so I just gave in and paid the fee.. I will of course return to chipotle but probably not this location because it's in such a busy area.

I don't know what they did tonight to the chicken but for the first time since my childhood the chicken was great. The skin was crispy, not greasy and it had seasoning you could taste. It seems like they finally cook the chicken enough and it just tasted great. Typically we just go there for the kid but my wife and I enjoyed it tonight

Not a chipotle regular but the staff and food were good. 
We went on a weeknight because we have two coupons for a free burrito. My husband got the barbacos (BBQ beef) which he said was really good. I got the steak burrito bowl with fajita veggies - also very good. The kids got a cheese quesadilla kids meal which is on the printed menu not the menu board. The kids meal came with chips but no salsa. I asked for salsa to go with the chips which the staff gave to me. It seemed clean but I will say the staff moved a little slow - but were happy all the while. This didn't convert us to regulars but I wouldn't say no if someone else suggested.

I went to KFC tonight with my husband and one of are young people wanted to fight with us why I don't know but all KFC did was push us out with old chicken that cost us $25 do that make is that's right when the other prone started it that's why we can't by where we live because things like this why the company isn't checking on theam

After numerous trips, we have experienced a pattern of the chicken restaurant not having chicken to sell. Often have been told to come back in 15 minutes. The staff gives off I don't like my job and customers are just an annoyance.  We are  now driving further to try another KFC.

I made the mistake of coming here without reading Yelp reviews since this was on route to where I was going. The double whopper I ordered was completely misaligned and was falling apart before i started eating. My burger was missing pickles which is a key ingredient. I was so disappointed. The fries were undercooked. Worst of all, my sister and I both got sick shortly after.

I enjoy the occasional Burger King meal, but this is not the place to get your whopper.

I'm currently in line, been in line since 530. It's currently 555. There were only 15 people in front of me. This is unacceptable for the amount of people that work here currently.

Update: So I got in line, and waited an extra 10 minutes for steak...the steak wasnt even properly cooked...This place needs a complete overhaul. Also, close to 50 minutes of waiting when 12 people (by my count) were working. Forget this place.

The new breakfast sausage sandwich is quite mundane. The hot chocolate is extremely watery (no milk). I think you're better off taking some extra time to go to Starbucks for a good drink. The only saving grace for this place is the quick drive-thru, but perhaps a couple of extra minutes added to your morning elsewhere will result in tastier food.
Good prices and great food. Always enjoy eating a whopper. Staff was really great and helpfull.

Absolutely worst service of all time,

They were rude when saying we needed to wait in drive thru, I heard the lady with pink hair mocking my question about swapping our extra crispy chicken they didn't have for 2 legs and then tried to belittle my question and went to other people and made fun of me and my fiancé for asking and didn't think we could hear her. Then in a condescending way took her head set off, and pointed her ear out the window and set I was going to have speak up.

 This is the worst fast food drive thru I have ever been to and if you are at all lucky enough to see this review before going PLEASE READ THIS, GO SOMEWHERE ELSE. DO NOT GO

Because to top it all off there was a hair in my fiancé's piece of chicken. So if you see the lady in the drive thru with white, green, purple hair just no your in for a rude, terrible, disgusting experience that ends with hair in your food

The sad part is I never have placed a bad review on someone but this was unreal and I don't want anyone to deal with what we did. Service like that the doors should be closed. I should've known when I seen the 1 star. There is a Popeyes up the road less than a half mile go there 

Terrible service, and terrible cleanliness

The Manager is really a prick and the lady at the front had no clue what she was doing I completed a survey to get a free whopper but there was no clue of asking to buy a small fry and drink. The dumb manager who is (AA) gave me an attitude saying I am only 14 and asking a simple question. He stated (get out of my line if you are not purchasing anything). Then I asked then that's when he attitude came in dumb people and horrible service and messy counters that were not cleaned. If I could I would give one star

So. I gave it another try. Why not? I wanted a burrito and went for it. This time MY BURRITO WAS WARM!! As it should be. I got barbacoa instead of carnitas. Never tried the red salsa sauce until now. It is very tasty! Workers were friendly and knew how to roll a burrito w/o tearing it. Lol. 

I enjoyed my nice and warm burrito this time around.

Having Burger King  was nostalgic for me. We used to go there once a week as kids- our small town Burger King gave a backstage "tour" to our elementary school and we had a loyalty to them. The tomato was not good and probably brought the whole whopper flavor down with it but for $2.99 it wasn't bad

This place is horrible.  I went there today and was in line at the drive thru for about 10 minutes before deciding to go inside to order.  The kid behind the counter looked like he was upset and indefferent.  They got my order wrong and then got annoyed (including the manager) when i pointed it out.  To make things worst one of the workers dropped the tongs on the floor being used for the chicken, picked it up and put back on the counter without washing it.  Needeless to say I will not be back and I did not eat the chicken I purchased.  Disgusting!

Probably the worst service ever in the world. Fries tasted like they were microwaved after sitting on the counter overnight. When we asked for new fries, we never got them. They instead ignored us and we waited for over 15 minutes and had to leave without them. The chicken tenders were overcooked and lack luster. Don't waste your time or money on this place because I won't anymore.

I think its interesting the rise and fall of a chain like Burger King mirrors that of middle class America. From humble postwar origins to its zenith in the 90s with the politically correct BK Kids Club and dollar tacos, and finally a precipitous decline, no other fast food establishment embodies America the way Burger King does. I recently went and visited the chain's Delmar and Kingshighway location for the first time in over a year because McDonalds is far away and I didn't feel like Five Guys. 

The first thing you'll notice about Burger King is how much business Burger King doesn't get. It's been said that the restaurant trade is about as close as it gets to free market competition due to the embarrassment of choices one has when they are hungry. Given that it was empty and I was running errands, I opted for the drive-thru, which brings up another problem with Burger King: it's fucking slow.

McDonalds has industrial food production down to a T. Orders of common things like the #1 (Big Mac, drink, and fries) can be prepared and ready for deployment to your vehicle before you even pull up to the first window to pay. After ordering a whopper meal here they literally ask you to pull over and put your car in park and someone will bring it out to you. That's unforgivable for a fast food institution, and not the first time a Burger King in the St. Louis area has done me like that. At least the fries seem to be an upgrade from what they used to be.

I went thru the drive thru today. i ordered the tendercrisp sandwich. After leaving I opened my sandwich and took a bite. It was soggy bun with a dark meat soggy chicken on the roll with some kind of sauce on it. I rarely eat at KFC because every time I do I am not satisfied. I think I will just stop going

Tired of burgers for lunch, how about a couple of piece of chicken?  Santa Barbara only has one outlet and it is tiny, dirty and dingy. In addition to that, the quality of the food is suspect. 
I have had KFC in many cities and countries and this is the first time I have developed a stomach issues hours later.
It is located in an area frequented by high school kids and is a few doors away from some excellent Mexican taco and food places. I should have opted for some fast Mexican food which would have run circles around the chicken I had.
They need to tear the joint down and build a bigger unit which would have a more efficient kitchen and allow for better cleanliness. The space inside only holds about 10 people so when if you are there for lunch you are on top of everyone.
It was not my best dining experience I have had. Too bad there isn't a Popeyes in the hood.

This has to be the scariest Burger King in Tucson.  Loads of crack heads and homeless folks milling around.  There is a bus stop near by and they seam to wonder in.  I ordered the Whopper meal, fries were good, my burger was just warm, cheese didn't even melt.  If you are looking for entertainment go here, but only before dark.  One the sun goes down the crack heads come out in force.

I'm not a Burger King fan in general (I don't like their fries) but my parents like Whopper Jrs. and I have to say, it was a-ok.  We used the drive thru, so I can't comment on the inside, but the drive-thru was all that I expect from a drive-thru... easy to read menu sign, easy to understand voice-box, correct change given, prompt delivery of food, etc.
It is the worst chipotle I had ever been to. I order a bowl with half chicken and half steak. Then I got 4 pieces of chicken. That is too mean.

I normally only go to Burger King on Wednesday's as that is when their  "signature" burger the Whopper is on sale for $2.50 each or a meal is $4.99. I have never had an issue with my burgers and I normally ask for extra toppings and they have been accommodating. When they had a deal of 10 nuggets for $1.79 I had to give them a try. They were okay but I would never pay full price. I really enjoyed the buffalo dipping sauce just the nice little kick.
It's a white castle/church's chicken/gas/convenience store. So you can order a slider, grab a six pack and be on your way.

As you may have noticed, I love Chipotle. This is probably the one restaurant I visit regularly. This location, along with the Kirkwood location, see my face at least twice a week. 

Also, you may have noticed, I always say I don't care for franchised places, however this is one exception I clearly love to make. I was first introduced to Chipotle back in 1998 when I was visiting, and eventually moved to, Denver. This is a Denver-based company and I frequented the original Chipotle so much I thought I would have to move in next door. Well luckily, I now have one less than 1/2 mile from my house. 

My love for Chiptole extends past the delicious, fresh food. This is a company with great values and excellent employee relations. I am familiar with the corporate structure and know several people who have worked for Chipotle and they are top-notch.

This location, besides being extremely close to my house, always offers a smile and a friendly hello upon entering. The line moves quickly even with texting, phone using, Wash U students jamming up the place. 

My favorite:  Steak fajita bowl, brown rice, extra veggies, corn salsa, cheese and a bag of chips and quac. This is hard to beat at $8.50. It is so filling, delicious and really not too bad for me. I love to throw in an Izze grapefruit or raspberry drink on occasion.

Another great feature of all Chipotles is their online ordering service. I use it quite frequently when ordering for a few people or a group of employees. Just place the order, they name and label everything, pay online and walk to the front of the line, pick it up and walk out. It couldn't be easier.

Parking is a little difficult. They have street parking or a private pay lot and a pay garage across the street.  I walk or bike up, so it doesn't bother me. If they added delivery I don't think I would eat anything else. Of course, the exercise is probably needed.

Fresh, organic ingredients makes this place worth the trip. I love the Carnitas in a bowl with black beans!  

On the downside, the guacamole is hit or miss and the service can be a little slow.  But overall, the food wins me over!

This is usually a good restaurant equipped with some pretty awesome food items. I would NOT recommend coming here within an hour of closing time. At about 9:30, they did not have chicken, guacamole, or sour cream. I settled for a steak burrito, which contained crunchy, undercooked rice. Like I said, it's a producer of food, just not near closing hours.

KFC...where do I start? This is probably one of the best places to get good quality, organic food. I ordered the vege mashed potato bowl. The cheese was so fresh and I loved the sweetness the corn added. My fiance wanted mashed potatoes as his side but I wanted coleslaw so they gave us FREE mashed potatoes. 
We overheard another customer who complained about how he got chicken last week and it was dry so they gave him a free meal on the spot.

This is probably one of my favorite places to eat in Delaware, the decor is trendy, the cashiers are great. The only thing thats a bit misleading is the advertising. On the box it said grilled chicken but that was NOT grilled. It was definitely deep fried. But I'm okay with that, I love anything deep fried. 

The other thing that made me upset was the lack of lids for the drink cups. 

Would I come here again? HELL YES
Would I give them a tip? HELL NO

Purchased 8 piece family meal...caught them screwing up before i even left. I had to ask for my biscuits...as I was leaving....had to ask for my cup, as I was leaving...got home home to find they didn't give me one of the sides. Person behind the register was an idiot. She had no idea what came with what, nor how to ring it up. The Manager was assisting her with refunding money to a previous customer, because girl working register was, as i stated, an idiot. The manager then told the customer, "I would check my bank account when I get home, if I were you." CRAZY!! she's basically saying, I think my employee is so dumb, I think she just f----ed this up sooo much, I can't fix it with a VOID!!!!!  WTH! There was another lady that walked in irate after having just waited 15 minutes for her food in the drive through, her order was wrong. If you're just passing through Delaware on I-95 and pull off to get some KFC....KEEP DRIVING! This is not the Kentucky Fried Chicken we all remember. They sell both Taco Bell & KFC menu and do both poorly.

We eat chipotle at least once a week and normally get carryout. Decided to come here to dine in and was very disappointed. When we got the counter they said they where out of steak and chicken, We agreed to wait until more was prepared. As we waited more customers came in and they where informed of the same situation; however, they where given free drinks while we still stood there. Stood there over 15 minutes, no one spoke to us or offered to comp our drinks. We promptly left after waiting around a while. We will never be back to this location.

So, I love love love love love chipotle. I was driving north for Easter, and decided I absolutely had to have some. So I pulled over, looked up the nearest chipotle, and off I went. This chipotle is located in a mall - but not in the food court. It's on the opposite side of the mall from the food court. A bit difficult to locate. I waited in line for a bit, and then they were out of steak. So I waited a bit more. Then I enquired as to how long it would be, because if it would be a while, I had no problem with chicken. They said no, it was almost done. So I waited a bit more. Meanwhile the line of people behind me had learned what not to order, and had all checked out, so the cashier took a break.

Long story short - My burrito was delicious, the service was slow, but I got my meal for free so I'd give them a second shot. They knew they had messed up and made up for it.

I wish I would have checked the Yelp reviews before I went here.  It took forever to place my order and get my food, even though there were only two cars in front of me, one of which was placing an order when I pulled up.  The woman who ran the window had a name tag that said shift manager but had horrible customer service and was obviously in a foul mood.  She wasn't friendly in the slightest bit and was on the verge of rude and very bothered by the fact she actually had to work.  She didn't even give me a receipt so instead of telling corporate about my poor experience, I'm warning all of you.  

I ordered the 2 for $10 whopper meal deal and asked for ketchup and salt for the fries.  She didn't give enough ketchup for one of the medium fries, much less both.  That worked out fine as the fries were disgusting and inedible.  They were cold, not cooked properly and had a funky texture.  They were so gross they ended up in the garbage.  The whoppers weren't much better.  They were cold and dry and didn't have many toppings.  They were a severe disappointment compared to other locations I have been to.  The only thing that tasted as expected were the drinks.

Save your money and time and go somewhere, anywhere, else.

Dinner at Chipotle by the UofA. The staff was rushing us through the line. I had the steak bowl and the steak had no seasoning. The tables and chairs were dirty and the Sprite was out of syrup. The staff was not very attentive, one of the customers took a bottle of hot sauce. Not my favorite location.
This KFC is very convenient.  The food is fine and I will continue to frequent here because I love the grilled chicken (though to get fresh chicken I usually call ahead)...

Every time I come here they are short on chicken and I have to wait for them to make it. Usually they are nice and offer a free drink. The food is ok, not great compared to other KFC locations. The interior has been remodeled, it was crummy before.

It's only a couple of blocks from where I work, but I have not eaten that this location before. The place was completely renovated about a year or two ago. 

Today I was in a bit of a time crunch. I didn't have time for the typical sit down lunch but more time than a drive through. As I was driving down Manchester, I looked over at the KFC and thought why not.

It was extremely clean and everyone I came into contact with was very polite and actually had a smile on their face. KFC is pretty much KFC. The chicken (original recipe of coarse) was great along with the mashed potato's and cole slaw. Nice quick fast and tasty.

This store started out great but has been going down hill over the past year. No carnitas for quite some time now, orders not ready at scheduled pickup times and tonight, placed an online order at 6:45 which I can't pick up until 8pm. Cancelled order going to the Greek place. Too bad, we really loved the food.

I have been eating here for years, until now.  The last 2 months it seems like,It takes forever to get food at drive through.I have waited in line over 15 minutes, Many times, With only 3 cars ahead of me. Now they are reheating old sliders in MICROWAVE, I burned my thumb on the soggy lava hot slider, trying to get it out of the box, it was jammed into. The burger was so hot ,I had to set it on my leg, on my pants, after a nano second, it was burning my leg, I had to pick it up and drop it into the box.  All 6 sliders were gooey , jammed into box, and hot as lava. I returned everything, got a refund, I wont be back.

It is truly a sad state of affairs when a chicken restaurant has the tiniest, tasteless chicken tenders of all fast food places. I got the 5 dollar fill up with three tenders. My grand daughter couldn't fill up on the poor excuses for chicken tenders. I literally would rather get the 10 nuggets for 1.49 at Burger King. At least they are more filling and taste just as good. For what you pay at KFC you would hope for more. I won't be back.
I ate at this restaurant today.  The chicken pot pie was delicious.  The lady in the drive threw was very kind and friendly.

So, usually I like to eat at locally owned and operated restaurants, especially when it involves Mexican food (this is, after all, Tucson and you can get some seriously authentic burritos in town). But, I gotta say, Chipotle makes a pretty good burrito and because of this, when I'm on campus and needing a meal, I will often wander that way. I like the steak option, with pinto beans, brown rice, cheese, and extra pico de gallo. Just know before you go -- there is almost always a line so expect a short wait. 

In addition to the food, I like that Chipotle is built on a business model that values sustainability (Chipotle has trademarked the phrase "food with integrity") - this basically means that they source local products as much as they can. I totally dig that commitment!

Um. Exactly what did the CEO of Taco Bell do other than get a promotion? 

Came in on 05/23/2018. Have no problem with service although some kid, who probably was new, folded the guy's in front of me burrito too tight and broke it. He double wrapped his burrito! I'm glad I wasn't that guy unless he really liked the tortillas. Lol. 

Anyway, got a carnitas burrito. I'm pretty sure that at least the meat is supposed to be warm? Not one ingredient in my burrito was warm. The carnitas was very cold and pretty nasty. My entire burrito felt like it had been in the refrigerator for 10 minutes before giving it to me. 

Chipotle used to be so good but the last couple of times it has been a miss....Meh. I'm over it.

Angriest Whopper not To Be Missed 

Leave it to the King to come up with the wildest ideas for burgers and other fast food items to knock out the competition. This is a novelty burger not to be missed because of its sheer weird looking red bun and spicy flavor combination. I think it was better than I figured it would be.

This place is awful. The store is always under staffed and the employees do not care enough to do their job correctly. The managers almost consistently hire incompetent teenagers who don't care about their job, and on top of that, they don't train them well. The entire store is just a recipe for disaster.

Last time, I went with my girlfriend through the drive thru and we both just got a the #1 (Whopper meal). When we pulled up to the window, the manager handed us our drinks and she rushed us to pull up to wait for our food. When the food came out, the manager was in a hurry, barely saying a word to us, and rushed back inside. I took a look inside and it was a nightmare. Everyone is rushing around, and the whole place looks like they're in the middle of Armageddon or something. Keep in mind, it's like 3:00, so lunch rush is over, dinner rush hasn't even started, and we were one of three cars in the drive thru. There wasn't many people inside either.

Taking a look at our food didn't make anything better. The cheese on the sandwich wasn't melted, the bread, lettuce, tomato and patty tasted and looked old, and it seems like this is always the case. It's gross. The Coke was good, but it's not hard to mess that up. The fries are always old, even if they are fresh out of the fryer. The reason this is the way it is, is because they don't clean the fryers often enough and they are so gross that cleaning them before the day starts doesn't magically make the fries better. They need brand new fryers and brand new employees with a brand new management for those fryers to ever come back from the nasty and disgusting place that they're at now.

I go to school at Indiana State University and we have a Burger King on campus. That is honestly the best Burger King that I've ever had and when I come home to Plainfield, Burger King isn't even an option when thinking about food. There are too many things wrong with this restaurant. The only solution is shutting it down, replacing all the ovens/fryers/drink machines/sinks/and all things kitchenware, and having a grand re-opening with ALL new employees and managers hand picked for maximum quality and efficiency. This is completely unrealistic, but it's seriously that bad. I do not recommend going here, and if you do, expect crappy service with crappy food. Any expectations higher than that will fall short. This store literally and figuratively leaves a bad taste in your mouth.
Horrible service. Very slow. 20 minutes to get a whopper Jr. sandwich. Not friendly either.

I am a Burger King Fan, just not this one.  I moved into the area in October, 2007.  Since then I have given this BK about 10 tries.

I will not complain about the food.  They still provide the standard quality of fast food.  It's the service.

The majority of time I use the "Drive-Thru".  Every time I have gone the time it would take to actually order, pay and receive my food has been longer then any other drive through I have used.  They are consistently slow.  I would like to add that typically there are no more than 2 cars in the drive through so I do not believe its related to being "busy".

I will only detail my LAST visit to give you an idea of the service.  It was about 2 weeks ago (around 01/04/09).  I ordered 1 Whopper meal and two Kid's Cheeseburger Kid's meals.  There was no one in front of me so I pulled up to the window to pay.

The girl at register had her back turned to the window and was doing what appeared to be "texting" on her cell phone.  It took a little over a minute for her to acknowledge me.  She took my money, gave me my change, closed the window and resumed staring at her cell phone with her back to me.

I don't know the entire time I waiting since I was not paying attention to the clock yet.  I would estimate about 3 minutes went by.  During this time I witnessed 8 employees.  2 of the employees responsible for tending the inside registers were hunched over, leaning on the counter with their elbows.

By now another dirver pulled in behind me.  He was impatient.  After about 3 more minutes he started screaming "C'MON!".  That is when I started to look at the clock.  It had taken another 4 minutes for my food to come out.

I don't know if I am "nitpicking" but I estimate the total wait time to be around 12 minutes.  I would normally not complain about that but its fast food.  

In closing... the whole time I waiting the girl at the drive-thru register was texting on her cell phone with her back turned to me.

I will never go to this BK again.

Made the mistake of coming here before reading the reviews.   My significant other  had a yen for a whopper.  We had some coupons,  so we we ventured over.  What a mistake.   We ordered two whopper meals and the popcorn chicken.   The chicken was a mistake considering what is available at the Chik-fil-a down the road.   The whoppers were cold and the drinks were terrible - the diet Coke tasted like they ran out of syrup and the root beer was just bad.  The whopper was probably cold because we went through the drive through and waited at least 10 minutes to get through.  Find another place....

This place never lets me down. Usually pretty busy but the line moves quickly.

Steak bowl is delicious with some mild salsa, corn, cheese, black beans, and white rice.

You can also order online and cut the line when ya go to pickup.

I've never received unfriendly service here, not once. The crew has always been pleasant to me every time I've come here. They greet me in the mornings with smiles, ask me how I'm doing, etc. Super friendly. 

My biggest complaint is they never get my food orders right. Ever. Every now and again, they'll mess up my coffee order too. 

This place is typically always packed in the mornings, so I'll order on-the-go through the app prior to. I'd think that would make the order easier because the whole order is sent digitally, in print, to their system. Nope. Still not correct. I usually order a turkey sausage flatbread with veggie egg white. They always give me a fried egg instead. I went just this weekend but this time instead of the turkey sausage, they put two eggs--1 fried and 1 veggie egg white. 

It just seems really crazy that they can't read the screen correctly every single time. Or maybe they're just so busy they're rushing and overlooking it? I'm honestly not sure.

Simply put WORST SERVICE EVER!!! If I could I would of rated this a 0/5.

Went with 2 friends to try the new double chicken sandwich crap. We first drive to the KFC closer to the UCSB campus and they don't even have it; so they tell us (not in a kind way) to try the one here. We end up driving here and doing in asking for the sandwich.

Two ladies (who seem to be the managers) are just standing around; one leaning on the wall (looking very bored). We ask politely if they have the sandwich and we get a very disinterested reply of "we have to check if we got the chicken." (whatever that means). Almost 20 minutes pass (3-4 customers order) and we have to ask a 2nd time. Mind you, this place is small and the lady we asked was leaning on the wall the whole time with us clearly in view. Now we come up and ask the 2nd time and she replies "yah we have them", still not doing anything we ask if we can order them and she finally says "ok". My friends end up ordering it (me excluded at this point) and overall it tasted mediocre at best.

For a chain this has got to be my WORST experience from a company. I am going so far as writing a complaint letter/calling the corporate offices to complain about this lady and the poor customer service.

Great staff! Service was great, food came Out fast. Terrible whopper. I tried to put it down. No way. Cheese didn't even melt. Sandwich was warm. Meat didn't look good. Hoping I make it home without getting sick

Well it's KFC so you know what to expect. OK if you like fried chicken.

However, I've tried their drive thru 3 times and they have yet to get an order right. They need to try to upgrade the caliber of employee they hire, the ones I've encountered seem to be clueless oafs.
My chicken was swimming in oil. The workers didn't even bother to drain it after frying. They just dumped it into a box. I uploaded a photo of it to the gallery.

We had a craving for Chipotle last night and this was the closest one we could find.  We drove around for a while trying to find a spot to park, but that is probably more of a location issue rather than a Chipotle issue.  As far as Chipotle's go, this is pretty standard.  The one small annoying gripe is that you have to ask the cashier for a key to the bathroom.  That made me feel a bit like I was at a questionable gas station instead of Chipotle.  

I ordered a steak burrito and was happy knowing I would be enjoying that giant burrito soon.  The man that was finishing up the burritos must have been the manager or something like that and not used to doing it as he busted two burritos and had to ask for another tortilla to redo it.  Luckily for me, by the time my burrito was ready to roll, a more experienced person put the finishing touches on.    

We were in and out in about 15 minutes, which was what we were looking for.  Not a bad overall experience, but not the best Chipotle experience I've had.

The steak was literally red. The food was cold. No fried vegetables and my beans didn't taste done at all. They were hard and white in the inside. I did not enjoy my food and regret buying It. Now my stomach hurts! The customer service was that great either!

my first time at this location. staff is friendly but the portion was considerably less than I'm accustomed to, the flavors weren't as bold as I like and they were out carnitas. however the decor is simple, beautiful, trendy and relaxed. I still love Chipotle but I'll stick to my usual location

This location is the worst Burger King I have ever been to in my life. Starting with the first time I entered the location a few months back: I had to wait about ten minutes to use the restroom, and when the employee who was inside left the room and I entered, it was obvious they had been smoking in the bathroom. There was a strong smell and ashes all over the floor and toilet seat. Every time I have visited this location there has been something equally as terrible, like burned chicken fries or stale French fries. Every single time I have asked for no onions in my burger there have been onions on it. Tonight, literally everything I ordered was terrible. First, I was told that they were "out of tea" for the night (this was TWO HOURS before closing), then the food I got was cold, the lettuce on my whopper was brown, and the chicken fries were cold and stale. They seemed to be very old. Every single person at this location deserves to be fired.

I need to stop coming here. It's a major guilty pleasure of mine. The issue is the coma and near heart attack is not very pleasurable afterward. They always have great deals that trick me into ordering a ton. The value menu, Whopper Wednesday, 2 chicken sandwiches for $6, 10 nuggets for $1.49, or the loads of coupons I have. It's all dangerous. It doesn't help that I drive past twice a day either. And the new farmhouse burger is ridiculously good as well. I grew up a Burger King kid and I think it's in my nature to get pulled there like a moth to light when I'm feeling the slightest bit hungry. 

Staff is usually pretty good. Drive through is quick. Good central location. It's a Burger King. It is what it is.

All I can say is what happened to Burger King? Came into this location tonight and should have left. 

Events:

1. Lady from drive-thru comes back in missing food. 
2. The manager has his back turned like the restaurant is swamped, and does not acknowledge me at the register for almost five minutes. No hello, I'll be right with you etc. 
3. Guy orders after me, identical order 2 for 10 whopper meals, only I had an additional order of chicken fries. Yes. Even I remembered his order. The manager bags his food first and tries to give it to me. No, that's not my order. You took my order. Really? 
4. Guy asks for ketchup. They don't have any. Cashier offers ketchup in a sundae cup. Man says I'm never coming back to this stupid restaurant. 
5. I get my food and a worker laughs and says that man said he's never coming back. "Whatchu guys do to that man" more laughs. 
6. Get home and sandwiches are smothered in mayo. Inedible. 

The joke is the people hired including the manager. Does Burger King have standards anymore? Do they care about anything?

It's a Chipotle.  You know the drill. Its your standard burrito place.  I usually order a Steak or Chicken Burrito Bowl with Guac on the side.  I can't complain, the food has been pretty on point every time I've gone.  I appreciate the consistency.  

Positives - Again, the consistency.  The line moves pretty quickly and the food has always been tasty and good.  I'm also a big fan of their Guac.

Negatives - It is a little pricey in my opinion.  2 bucks for a tiny container of guac is pushing it.

I think KFC is the greasiest of all fast food joints but it is so good that I can't resist. I am not a fan of their chicken. Other than the amazing taste and crispiness of the skin the actual chicken itself tastes dry. 

That being said they have some great chicken burgers. The crispy crunch and the classic sandwich with spicy mayo are perfect. The fries are thin and crispy and the gravy is the best. The poutine is amazing too. 

So even though the chicken doesn't please me the burgers make me like KFC.
I highly recommend thre chicken pot pie. They're super tasty and a great alternative if you're tired of fried chicken.

This place is great! I got the Georgia Gold chicken the basket with the three pieces, and it was so good. I was able to get an extra piece for free. It was crispy. It was a little salty tho. For $5 and some change, I was Able to get that the four pieces of chicken and a small bowl of sweet corn. I'll be back

This location has the veggie sliders!  I know that not all locations are offering them so I want to be sure to point out any locations that do.  Keep in mind that the veggie slider patties are vegan but the buns are not. (Buns are vegetarian.)  White Castle corporate office says that they are working on a vegan bun. I assume that if the sliders do well, they may become a permanent staple.

As for this location specifically, this is my go to location whenever I go back home, and per my DD's request, it is always the 1st stop. So when I was home last month, I got to try the veggie sliders.  They were very good! Try them plain, with ketchup, or with the Thai sauce, which is vegan. Yum!

I know from past experience that this location commonly messes up orders, so just be sure to double-check your order before pulling away from the window, or off of the lot. 
Customer service is hit or miss.  Sometimes you'll get attitude, sometimes you won't.  But it's White Castle...home of the gas burgers; people come here for punishment.

Tried the new chicken sandwich at KFC.
Chick-fil-A has absolutely nothing to worry about.

New to St. Petersburg and was craving a flame broiled burger.  Ordered a double whopper with easy mayo and wanted the hamburger well done.  Well done turned out to be extra special time in the microwave.  Almost nothing as bad as microwaved meat.  Tomatos were meely.  Absolutely the worst hamburger I've had in a long time.  Soda machine was not working.  Crew members were sweeping and mopping the floor where we were eating.  Don't like being called "honey," "dear," or "sweetie." Will not return.

This KFC location seems to always be out of something at any given time.  If you go expecting to take home a particular meal with your sides of choice, be prepared to wait for them to make more.  I have yet to go in and get out without having to wait for something, whether it is pieces of chicken, biscuits or sides.  I have been offered a free beverage for my wait, so there's that.

I have been here plenty of times. I came here before to get a muffin and when I got home there was a hair on it! The same thing happened a few days when I went to go get breakfast. I got a sausage, egg, and cheese on a croissant and I went home and as I was taking a bite of my sandwich, a piece of hair came along with it.
