I can deal with almost anything but not hair in my food!! This is two times it has happened, and it is not okay!

Whopper Wednesdays, I love Burger King. The place is clean and the service is pretty quick but it tends to be cold in there. Not good. I do love their soda machine, I feel like a kid in the candy story every-time I'm in front of it.

It is neat that Carls Jr. finally got a chance to open a store here, in the town where they have one of their corporate offices.  Their sandwiches are in the cheaper - medium expensive column, but quality is top-notch.  This location also includes the 'Green Burrito,' which is their mexican food spinoff.  For burgers, my parents favor the Santa Fe Chicken (with grilled chicken &  a roasted anaheim pepper), the famous star (ever reliable), the spicy chicken (for the all the cheapwads out there, spicy fried chix sandwich for a buck), and the western bacon cheeseburger (it has onion rings & bbq sauce on it).  The Green Burrito is pretty good usually (I almost always have the bean & cheese burrito, it has this tasty red sauce but is mild in flavor), I've had the enchilada plate before it's alright, and the taco salad is also okay (served it its own fried bowl, wouldn't cha know).  However, as you are in Carpinteria, there is a darn good chance you'll find some way more authentic mexican/latin food at Delgados, Mi Fiesta Market, Taco Grande, Senor Frogs, Ruby's or is it Rudy's, or Taqueria Guadalajara; that is my not-so-humble opinion on that.  Otherwise, Carls is pretty budget friendly, quick & tasty.  Note: this location does not have a drive through, the lot is too small.  The 101 South on-ramp is directly off of the exit, which is simple in theory but more of a pain in practice.  The end.

****  Sending just a small amount of anger rays for their new owner's plan to move their corporate hq's to TX! I know it's cheaper tax-wise than CA, but seriously the loss of that many jobs is gonna hurt my town, you ain't gettin' any respect for that move because it is not deserved.  Over and out.

This is one of the worst KFC I have ever been to, unless the nice old lady is there everyone is lazy and rude. They will not care if they give you old dry chicken. Never go during a busy time or they make it you problem that it takes a long time and they give you sub par food and service

4 pieces of cold steak on each taco with a pinch of rice and a whole lot of corn. Chips are stale EVERY TIME and really chewy. More tortilla than anything inside the taco. Kinda disappointing because this chipotle is so close to me. And I'm not one to complain about portions but this is kinda ridiculous the girl had no clue and was acting like she was paying for it out of her own pocket. Definitely NOT worth $10. 
The Cinnaminson location is better everyone there's super friendly and they actually give you some food with your tinfoil. 

Sorry :|

Love Chipotle, and the whole family does too. This location is new and could use to get some kinks out - like not having to wait a long time for a pre-placed web order. However my instance was partly the fault of Chipotle's Web (and app) ordering system which does not allow for ordering either Sofritas, nor kid's meals (heads up Chipotle corporate!). That said, the staff were extremely friendly and courteous, helped to get the order right given the web/app missing items, and kept me informed as to the status of my order - and the meal was excellent. I'm delighted to have a Chipotle in such a convenient location!

Went here recently, the place was packed. Had to wait a few minutes for the chicken to finish cooking. The chicken, mashed potatoes with gravy, and biscuit were all hot and moist. The peolpe were friendly and place clean. A little pricey, unfortunately didn't see the $5 fillup.

There is one other Chipotle near Citrus Park mall, I prefer this one due to its fresher taste and better service. I visit here multiple times a month and am always satisfied. Carnitas burrito with every topping available and its good for 2 meals.

I've never been one to rave about Chipotle. I know the restaurant has some die hard fans, but I don't get it. It's not that the food is bad, it's just that it's fairly unremarkable to me. 

I gave them another try recently after getting a free burrito coupon while they were shut down for a couple hours to do a staff meeting on food safety. The restaurant looked tidy and bright which was appreciated. 

The burrito itself, carnitas, which I usually love at Mexican joints, was fairly bland. The flavored just don't pop for me. I get the hottest hot sauce and it's just hot, missing any nuance of flavor. While I enjoy their guacamole, I have to wipe each tortilla chip off with a napkin. They are unforgivingly salty. 

All in all, not my fav. But speedy service and clean facilities are nice.

Wow,went to BK last night,and actually went inside.This place is rockin good.The place was busy,but very clean,and the staff was wonderful.

It was Whopper Wednesday,so I bought four of those,gave two to the homeless,and of course left my usual tip of $20.00 with the cashier.

Wonderful,wonderful,wonderful!

I love this location - the employees are so friendly! The food has always been fresh, hot, and well-cooked. At other stores, you'll occasionally get cold food or under-cooked rice, but not at this one. I've now received two burrito bowls free from this location (and one from the University Ave. Chipotle) simply by making small talk and smiling - this is thanks to the customer giveaways that Chipotle corporate encourages as part of their customer loyalty campaign, so remember to be nice to the staff! Plus, Chipotle has recently started a rewards program (buy 4 entrees, get your 5th free).

Guacamole is free if you just get grilled veggies (no meat or sofritas), which is great encouragement to eat vegetarian. I also recommend bulking up your burrito bowl by asking for both types of beans or half and half meat (they generally end up giving you about 30% more meat total if you do it this way).

This place is the most inconsistent chipotle I've ever been to, and I've been to many. Some days it's great, other days they don't know their elbow from their ass. Today my husband asked for a piece of lime...you know...the same fresh squeezed lime they use in their 'cilantro-LIME rice'...they gave him a lemon and argued with him that it was a lime and that's the color they've been for a few weeks... so guess they've been making 'cilantro-LEMON rice' along with whatever they use lime for in this store...not to mention wanting to charge us about 30$ for one double meat burrito and two single steak serving tacos.... the incompetency is..indescribable..I literally cant pick a word. Funny..sad...pathetic..ignorant..are just the few I've felt writing this post

My family wanted something simple and easy for dinner so we decided on Chipotle. Any place that has online ordering is always a plus and their site is very user friendly. When my husband got back with the order nothing was right. The steak salad was supposed to have the salsas on the side and they were dumped on the salad. There was also no dressing at all, either on the salad or side. My carnitas tacos had the rice & beans on them instead of on the side. One of my daughter's meals was left out completely and the other again had rice & beans in it instead of on the side. Chipotle was heading for a 1 or 2 star review until my husband spoke to the store. Rico and Josh handled the problem flawlessly. My husband was apologized to more than once,  our next 2 meals were comped and our current order remade. That is some of the most fantastic customer service we have ever seen. They have definitely kept us as Chipotle customers. BTW our order, when remade, was delicious!

Service is terrible at this location.  Servers are more concerned with rushing you down the line than listening to what you what.  I had to repeat everything twice, or point to it. I ordered a carnitas burrito bowl...barely any meat was put in my bowl, but I did have a lovely bay leaf that I had to ask to have removed 3 times.  I asked for just a little of the green salsa bit before I could finish speaking I have a full over flowing scoop in my bowl. Same thing with the sour cream. There's enough for another bowl in here. The girl preparing it had her head tuened looking down the line instead of at what was in front of her.  It's a Sunday too, so it's not like they had the usual influx of students in line.  I would've said something there but honestly they were in such a hurrry to get me out and not listening as is I didn't really have a chance.  So I guess I'll just drink my $9 spicy sour cream bowl....

One of My favorite childhood memories are of White Castle. So, to take my kids here is a way to pass on my love of the Slider. Sadly, my wife and youngest don't like the taste of what I believe was manna. why else did the people of Israel rebel?

Tonight we tried the Ultimate Jalapeno Pepper Cheeseburger, Imagine this burger, topped with jalapeno cheese, slices of jalapenos then a spicy mayo, Add fresh fries and a cold beverage, and you have a perfect meal,

The only way to top it is to have a few plain sliders.

I've always been a fan of BK, mainly because the Whopper has everything I like and they don't put mustard on it. This location has "Whopper Wednesdays" which is basically discounted Whopper meals that day. I also like their onion rings and dipping sauce. They don't keep the dining area as clean as they should. That's a shame because they got 3-4 internet surfing stations and some big-screen TVs in there. So they should do a better job of keeping it clean. It could otherwise be a very nice hangout place for the young folks.

After a 14 hour day, I was starving! Swung by right quick to get my whopper on. Johnny was friendly and let me know that it might take a minute because they were going to throw out an old sandwich and make a fresh one. With it being late in the night and slow, they could have just as easily given me the old sandwich and I probably wouldn't have even cared. But in a lot less time then i thought it would take, another friendly face brought out some hot, delicious sandwiches with a thank you for waiting! These guys were great, and the sandwiches were put together quite nicely!

Good chicken but limited options in the sides. My family was hungry and wanted a variety of sides but no apparently you could only get a specific sides you could get with each meal... my mom could only get 2 mash potatoes and gravy and 2 cole slaws but the sign had said 4 side options. I don't recommend going here unless you have to!

I don't really like to write reviews on chain, fast food places but I'm trying to remember what I've eaten while in Santa Barbara. KFC is ...whatever. Its not Popeyes. My friend tried to convince me that they have great chicken strips. Blah. Just regular, kind of stale tasting actually. The potato wedges were good, the biscuit was ok. I'd drive 20 mins to Popeyes before I ate this.The homeless scary people in the parking lot weren't helping either.
Very disappointed yesterday. I order a 12 piece, get to the window and the gal says we won't have any chicken for 25 minutes...at 6pm??? Wow, talking about not ready for your busy time...

There is nothing wrong with this fast food restaurant, it's just that I'm not a fan of KFC. The food is hypnotically delicious, especially with all the high-sodium artery clogging greasy good, but that's just it. The food may taste great, it's just not great for you, and after you seem them drench the chicken in the grease, it makes you loose your appetite.

If you do want to indulge, go for their Toonie Tuesday deal where $2.79 gets you 2 pieces of chicken- a drumstick and thigh, with some fries. There is nothing wrong with this location. The facility is clean and well kept, and the staff are really friendly. The smell from the parking lot outside is also very luring. I would recommend going once in a while, but go more at your the risk of your own health.
Waited 20 minutes in the drive thru and my whopper was a charred POS. Don't go here. How hard is it to cook a burger properly? Too hard for this Burger King.

I'm not sure if they make your order so fresh that they begin to kill the chicken after you order or what... but I got caught in the drive thru - literally caught since i couldn't get out - and sat there for 35 minutes... yes, 35 minutes waiting on my order, took me 15 just to be able to order, the remaining 20 was spent sitting and waiting. 

I'll never go here again, that's for sure!

The Double Down is delicious -- two chicken filets (I get grilled) sandwiching two pieces of bacon, Monterey Jack cheese and pepperjack cheese. I skip the Colonel's sauce, 'cause I don't know what's in it.

3 carbs!

2 words: FOOD POISONING. Got popcorn chicken and a sandwich for dinner on Sunday, was awake until 3 AM throwing up among other things. Couldn't even hold down water for a day. After taking 2 days off of work i am back in the office but still mildly nauseous and zero appetite. Not only will I never go back to this location I will never eat at another KFC again.

I used to be addicted to chipotles' spiciest sauce, but I have a hard time giving this chain high marks as I've never had a terrific experience, only mediocre and poor. About once a year I get food poisoning from them. They are always crowded and overpopulated and amount of food for your buck is actually not that great. It is rare that the line doesn't start at, or out of the door. Online ordering just means that no matter what time you put in to pick up, they make it asap and then it just sits under the heat lamp until then, and I feel like I am bothering them and breaking their order by coming to pick up my food. Guacamole is expensive. Aside from that, all of the ingredients are fresh and excellent, chicken and steak with a nice char, burrito perfectly packed, chips fresh.

I really, really used to live for Chipotle, but I've just grown too tired of middle-tier prices for a whole lot of mediocre. I started making bean and rice skillets at home and I'm happier with that.

Another KFC that pleased me with their great customer service. I got a meal-deal and they let me replace the cole-slaw with a different side, which was nice. Also, when told there was a wait for the extra-crispy chicken breasts in my order I was offered a free soda to make-up for the wait, a nice gesture that reminded me of the great service at anther KFC I have reviewed in the past when traveling. I'm glad this one near me is great too!

The seating area was cleaned well with nary a dirty table so I felt comfortable while I waited, as opposed to some fast-food locations that seem to have tables forever coated in grime.

I am very happy with this KFC and shall return in the future when I get another craving for fried chicken.

The worst service ever in the history of KFC. The most deplorable chicken Ive ever seen. The chicken was dry, no skin, cold as if it just came out the refrigerator. The servers were rude and manager was just as rude. This place needs to have corporate come in and start over. Im really disappointed on all levels.

Chipotle is indeed one of my favorite places for fast food. Though every time I've come here for takeout they've gotten my order wrong.

After a long morning at work, there's nothing better than a steak bowl from Chipotle to improve my mood. I gleefully open my steak bowl to find chicken, I am utterly disappointed and left heart broken. It's already too late to return my order since I've already left the premises. I eat my food in despair, but with hope for this place. A few days later, I decided to give this place another shot and get a steak bowl for take out. And it happened again! My order was wrong, I did indeed get the steak I wanted however my toppings were all wrong. I had no tomatoes, missing my green lettuce and beans! As you can see my experience here has been terrible. I dont know if I can handle a 3rd time here

This Chipotle Mexican Grill location is a nice spot with good food and a clean atmosphere. The take out menu is nice as well. As far as Tex Mex goes, this is fairly decent. It is good food, but it does not full fledged beautiful ( some what unhealthy Tex Mex). This is fresh and healthy southwest style food. 
       I would recommend their barbacoa which is beef. The chicken can be gristly at times and the steak is decent. The pork dishes are good though. Burrito or burrito bowl, both are good. The ingredients are usually fresh, which means that many times there are some missing from the menu. 
       The one major complaint that I had with this location is the fact that the people taking orders move very quickly and do not put enough food on your plate or tortilla. If you want a similar restaurant with more of a true Tex Mex vibe, try Moe's Southwest Grill.

Yelp needs to revise their ratings to include a "0". This is possibly the worst Burger King in the country. It was, certainly, the worst fast food visit in my 50 year experience. I arrived on Saturday evening a little before 6. Despite there being only one additional patron at the counter, it tool more than 5 minutes for the staff to be bothered to take an order.  I thought it was simple enough, a BK Stacker, a Whopper, Onion Rings and the Sweet Potato Fries

Then the wait began.  I stood off to the side, and additional customers came in and placed orders. When I had waited about 8 minutes for the food, I asked if I had been forgotten. I was told the fries had just been put down. 

Others started to ask about their order and the staff started to get belligerent. One woman was shouted at. When she called for the manager he offered nothing, not even speaking to the employees, who were in front of him the whole time. 

Finally after 28 minutes, I managed to get my food...and get out quick. The manager smirked a 'Thanks, come again' as I was leaving. 

At least the order was correct. 

I will not return to that BK. I went to their website and put in a complaint and received a request to call the district manager, which I will. I am going to suggest the entire staff be retrained, not that it'll help any. 

I wholly recommend that everyone stay away from this location!

My husband and I ordered our free T-Mobile Tuesday deal Impossible Whoppers yesterday. Our ordering process took awhile because of the deals we were using. The cashier required the manager to swipe several times in order to put in our order so we continually waited. I was patient since the burgers were free after all. The cleanliness of this BK was pretty bad, especially in the bathroom. As for the food, the 0% beef burgers were juicy and were some of the best ones I've tasted. We ordered fries as well which were a little cold. I felt that the service could have been better but this trip was out of convenience as we were hungry. I've been to better and cleaner locations, so in the future I would prefer to go to a different location unless it's out of convenience.

Great service this evening.  I needed something somewhat healthy.  Got a bowl with chicken and steak.  Service was fast and friendly.  Brown rice was a bit al dente which is better than the mushy mess that can sometimes be at these types of places.  Oh and Coke Zero in the machine.

Where do I start with this place, the customer service was terrible. I've went there multiple times to try and order a chicken pot pie which they didnt have unless I wanted to wait 30 plus minutes for it to cook. They never had the cheetos chicken sandwich. I had to go to beech grove for the sandwich. I asked the other day if they had it and they told me it was being restocked on Tuesday the 30th which was the day it conveniently ended. I've ordered a 8 piece bucket of traditional fried chicken and every piece was over cooked and very dry. Ordered famous bowls that had hardly any chicken gravy or cheese but mostly mashed potatoes that tasted watered down. If you can, avoid this location at all costs and save yourself the grief.
This is burger king wide, not just this location. The impossible burgers are NOT vegan. They are vegetarian. They contain egg.

I went in and they were out of SEVERAL main items on the menu (e.g. steak, carne, guac, a veggies i believe) but i still got food. But then she put in very little chicken (maybe they were going to run out of that too?!?) and was not listening and rude honestly. Thought how bad an experience it was, wish i had just said forget it when she listed everything they were out of.. too bad I will be going to the other locations from now on..

So I have been ordered to eat whatever I want and right now, that amounts to burgers and more burgers.  I can honestly say that I love the triple whopper but it certainly would have been better if I had gotten it within a decent time period.   Nevertheless, my sandwich made me quite happy despite the slow service.  Free computer access and a very clean and spacious dining room with a large playground for kids.

This is definitely the dirtiest restaurant I've been to in ages, I'm almost certain that it's been at least 3 days since anything was cleaned (I mean everything table floor even the windows are really dirty) and the bathroom (men's atleast) has no tp or paper towels. this place doesn't have real ljs food, no fries and the fish and chicken don't match it at all. On top of all of this they were out of BBQ and tartar sauce. Lady up front was bitching about being here at 800 after leaving @ 2:00 last night. I can't see any reason to be here that early because literally nothing is cleaned. My mom got a Dr pepper and said it didn't taste right so she asked the lady behind the counter and she said that we ran out last night. Does this place not use out of order signs? Atleast a heads up would have been nice. Worker finally leaves to do something *refills straws* and goes back behind the counter and talks to some guy. Wow.

Went for lunch with coworkers.  They were busy and the line was about 25 deep but moved quickly (less than 10 minute wait).  Had the carnitas bowl.  Since they make it right in front of you it was hot and flavorful.  The staff was very cheerful.  Would recommend going.  Meal and chips was aboutt $12.

The reviews on here are giving pretty sub-par ratings, but I guess I'm about to flip the script. This is my go-to Burger King and honestly go-to fast food location in general because it's so close to where I live and... I just love Burger King. I've only ever done drive-thru, but I've never had a bad customer service experience and the wait times have always been virtually non-existent no matter what I order. 

Even when the Impossible Whopper had just come out and they were getting a ton of press, the drive-thru was super quick and friendly. And the burger was as impossible as could be. 

As much as I've tamed my fast food craving over the years, I'm not ashamed to say that I'll be back.

Terrible.  Been a consistent customer for years.  Been ordering 3 piece chicken and all of a sudden I notice price jumped from $5.49 to $5.99 to $6.99.  All within a span of a couple of months.  Just plain greed.  You just lost my business.

We are traveling from Utah to Oregon and stopped for some lunch. The store was a little dirtier then most Chipotle's I have been to buttery were pretty busy. It was good, it always is but we had to wait 10 minutes because they were out of salad dressing and my friend asked for chicken and got steak. It wasn't so bad that j would not return but I think just a couple little changes could make this a five star location!

Ridiculous. Ordered a sausage egg and cheese croissant, no croissant. Ordered blueberry donut, no blueberry donut. Ordered sour cream donut, no sour cream. This is a Dunkin Donuts right? Get it together please. Will never return to this location.

Best damn whopper n onion rings I have had at a burger King in forever. Plenty of flavor but barely needed a napkin... Surprisingly good and around almost midnight when ordered. Best bk around, hands down, and food was ready quick, like it should be aye!?!

Aweful.  Simply the absolute worst service of any fast food restaurant ever.  They ran out of extra crispy chicken?  The bathroom toilet was overflowing and numerous employees walked in and right back out without addressing the issue.  The restaurant is dirty, the employees are rude and the food was cold when we received it.  The manager and ownership group should be embarrassed.  Trust me on this.  It's bad.

This was a gross experience the store itself feels ghetto the cashier was friendly but all employees looked frumpy not professional... Bathroom was dirty... Chicken was sold out... They pulled my food out of some silver freezer box an nuked it. I'm actually kind of afraid to eat the food I just paid for.. Too bad I only eat KFC like once a year as a sort of past time food. This was a gross experience ... In Santa Barbara of all places.

My children wanted Dunkin Donuts. 1 order a Long John, 1 ordered the new egg & ham wrap, I got a sausage egg cheese English muffin sandwich. The egg on both the wrap & sandwich reeked and tasted like chlorine! I can not imagine why, or how this could have been allowed. We had a flight to catch, or I would have gone back to speak with someone. We had to throw both away it was so strong.

Wow. This place is nothing but consistent.  As in, consistently trash.  Average IQ of their workers is around 60.  Slow. Literally and figuratively.  I'm here,  currently,  waiting 31 minutes on a 4 piece chicken combo...and was just told, do you want your 7 biscuits,  or another side? Your biscuits will be 7 more minutes. 

So, 38 minutes.  For a 4 piece and 7 biscuits.

And one person at front counter.   Drive thru is 20 deep...and no sense of urgency.  Like, none.

I was here last week, ordered a 4 piece...got home...had 2 pieces in my box. 

Consistently terrible.

Love kfc , so in passing went to the delmar location, the wait was terrible but what's worse was the guy did  have gloves on but grabbing chickens out the warmers with his hands , like it was Halloween but he fill up buckets with a fistful of chicken. I'm watching him touch every piece of equipment, clothing, rags . The other girl hand no gloves but she was using thongs but the guy following behind her touching her gloveless items, she touched the debit cards , register , money but use thongs the guy , no thongs touching everything she touched  so it's kinda like we were licking debit cards and money . Plus the chicken was under cooked " churches chicken style " had to put it in the oven, was very disappointed and grossed out . Didn't think about it till after the fact , Makes me appreciate my local kfc

I've been to a few Chipotle locations and have never been impressed or understood what all the hype has been about; I guess I've been ordering the wrong thing.  Ordered a steak burrito this time and it was delicious and not dry like the other burritos I've ordered from here.  Don't think I'm quite on the bandwagon yet, but I don't think I'll roll my eyes next time someone wants to go to Chipotle

Definitely not the best, fastest or cleanest BK I've ever hit up, but the staff works hard and is extremely friendly.

I stopped in here last Friday just after a severe thunderstorm and the place was packed.  Both the drive-thru and lobby were crowded with hungry people looking for a quick fix.  Additionally, the storm disrupted their POS terminal and it was taking roughly 5 minutes to process each credit/debit card, it was crazy.  Normally, in these situations, folks get hangry.  They begin huffin' and puffin', and in some cases just end up leaving.  Sherlyne wasn't having it though.  She kept the line moving as fast as possible, and was very personable with everyone who was waiting for their orders.  She kept a smile on her face the entire time despite the pressure, and she stayed engaged with everyone in the lobby and preparing food.  Seeing her in action was like watching an instructional video on customer service 101.  

I waited nearly 10 minutes for a Whopper meals and some Mac 'n Cheetos, but I didn't mind at all.  My food was piping hot, fresh and tasted great.  Well, except for those Mac 'n Cheetos.  One of those things that looks good on the drawing board, I guess.

Anywho, I hope this old BK get razed for one of the new designs and I hope Sherlyne gets promoted.  But because the place is so run down though, I have to give a 3-star.

This kfc always has good chicken never oily. Even went there late at night on another day and still got good chicken. They're always nice there. We have had kfc at other ones and always horrible and oily. Stay away from these kfc Avon horrible,Plainfield Horrible, Franklin horrible. Been there several times and always good never oily. Thank you to the workers we appreciate that you do your job correctly and cook chicken and drain it correctly.your awesome

I've always liked Chipotle, even before the whole getting sick scare.  The same holds true today. I enjoy getting a steak bowl with salsa, corn, cheese, brown rice and black beans.  Warms my heart and fills my tummy.  This location has always been good to me. Parking is easy and they take orders and make your food pretty quick.  Only times I don't like it is when the line is to the door...lol. Everyone has been nice and they address any issues with the food quickly and amicably.  Tastes is similar to other TexMex restaurants, but I like this place better.  If you ever need a Chipotle buddy, call on me anytime and I'll go with you!

#242

Ok ok I know, so unhealthy but....once every 4 or 5 years one must make sure the Colonel is still finger licking good and guess what? It was. Extra crispy was just that, crunchy and yummy. The chicken was hot and juicy. I ordered a 3 piece white and got 2 very large breasts and a wing. Mashed potatoes could have used a little more gravy and remember the stick in the corn cobette? The stick is gone but the corn is still the same, soggy and yet so delicious, Soggalicious! The biscuit has shrunk some and we didn't get the butter in the tube. Who knows if they still have that but when you only go every 4 years it's to expected (I'm trying to make myself feel better about the whole thing).
This location was packed when we went, the line moved slowly but the order was right and the chicken still hot when we got it home. 
I'm good for another 4.

Had a horrible experience with the KFC on Harding Pl where they completely messed up my 16pc family meal order by not giving me the chicken I ordered,  leaving out sides, & giving me 16pcs of old hard dry stale chicken.  I spoke with the manager Mike the same day and he offered to replace the entire meal upon my return (paid $45).  I ended up throwing away all 16 pcs of the hard inedible chicken I had received.  I live 30 minutes from this location & didn't want to drive all the way back so I told him I'd come back another day. 

Well, 10 days later my family wanted KFC since we hadn't had any in quite a while.  So, I called & asked to speak with Mike in order to get my meal replaced. I was told there was no manager there by the name of Mike so I was upset thinking the person I spoke with had lied about being a manager. The manager said his name was David but he would give me a replacement order & told me to come on by.  I GPS'd the store & drove out. 

When I got to this location, I realized it was not the same store I originally went to which had messed up my order & I immediately called David & informed him I was in the parking lot but his location was not the one that messed up my order. I apologized for the mistake & told him not to bother fixing my replacement as I would call the Harding location.  

To my surprise, David said not to worry about it and said he had the order ready & for me to come get it.  This is the way you provide customer service, this is what is missing from many establishments today, & this is how you redeem your brand & let your customers know you hear them & care for their business. David did not have to honor this replacement that was in no way tied to his location but he did. I truly value his professionalism, friendliness when I got there, & customer service. I have to give this location a 5 star purely based on the level of customer service I received today. 

I was all but giving up on KFC but I believe this Hickory Hollow Ln location will be my go to KFC location from now on. Thank you David!

Normally KFC has pretty good chicken.  I tried the chicken sandwich and was terribly disappointed.  It was so bad I tossed more than half of it!  It was dry and some of it looked odd like maybe it had been sitting around for a while.  The bun was crumbly and seemed old.  As always the potato wedges are awesome!

Dunkin Donuts is the fast food chain king of donuts. Krispy Kreme has a better donut, but you will pay a little more, they are harder to come by, and there is not much beyond donuts and drinks at a Krispy Kreme.

This is not to say the donuts are not good at Dunkin. They are pretty tasty. You can find specialty places that are by far better. But, like with Krispy Kreme, you are likely to pay the price and they are hard to find.

For the right price, Dunkin's donuts hit the spot. I like their Coffee Drinks just as much as anything at the more gourmet chain, Starbucks.

I also like the average at best breakfast sandwiches. They have this new spicy sausage one that I got and really liked.

As long as you know what you are getting, you know you are getting your money's worth.

If you are more picky, this is not the place for you.

I decided not to go to chipotle anymore. Every time I'd eat a steak bowl or burrito from Chipotle, my stomach doesn't settle right. That's a sign of poor meat. This doesn't happen when I go to a similar restaurant such as Q-Doba. I wouldn't be surprised if the FDA doesn't inspect their Cow Farms. Eat at your own risk

I bought the 20 fill up deal, up charged to a Mac and Cheese.

My chicken breading was grey, I have cooked in the industry for years and have never seen grey breading. 

I ended up throwing away everything. 

It was disgusting, the Mac and cheese tasted like I was gargling with vegetable oil. This is not the first time that this has happened, it happened at a different KFC location. 

Will not return any time soon.

Bad leadership is the road for failure... Service was slow before and slower now with new building. But most Burger King are very slow that is why everyone go to the others because they are faster specially in the driver thru. But as I start all starts with good leadership. Is not employee fault is the leadership learn to lead properly. 

The impossible Whopper was very bad. Not what I expected. It doesn't taste like meat it's have a weir taste. 

What do u think.???

Leave me a comment...

I went here tonight and I ordered 2 pieces of chicken, white meat, a wing and a breast with a side of macaroni and cheese, a side of mashed potatoes and gravy and a sprite. I got the Mac and cheese and sprite. the 2 pieces of chicken I ordered came as 2 thighs and a drumstick, all dark meat. and I didn't receive my mashed potatoes.

long line but it moved pretty quick. That being said, the sausage egg & cheese english muffin is about the worst breakfast sandwich ever. the bread is mealy & contents have zero flavor outside of an odd chemical flavor i cant place - or maybe a rancid flavor? I don't know but it is NOT good.

Sucks so bad. I only come here cuz it's close to my house and convenient but every time I come they prove me right and it's always worse and worse every time. I got a bowl, asked for white rice and she barely put any it and kinda shook it and it still didn't even fill the bottom. Okay. Then I waited 10 minutes for steak and while I was waiting and she was taking other customers she let the rice just sit there in the bowl getting cold. Got 5 pieces of steak and I hate standing up for myself but if I'm paying for steak, I want a reasonable amount so I asked for a little more. Then corn and cheese and she poured two huge scoops of corn and a whole bunch of rice. They're clueless and need more training for the love of god. 
EDIT: I had to put it in the microwave for 30 seconds to make it a little warm it was just all cold. 

Picture was from my first review where I got tacos, similar situation, which I gave two stars and now I'm changing it to one star. 
Hate going out of my way to the mount Laurel one but maybe I'll just try pancheros for the future.

My chicken sandwich was hard as a rock.  They replaced it and the second one was a little better.  The potato wedges were okay but I wish they were hot.  The gentleman working the front line was very friendly but they needed help desperately.  Why go here when there's Chick Fila?

This Chipotle location has come a long way since they first opened. It sucks that every time I go, they're always out of something. Today and everyday I go, they're always out of steak. 

Thank you to the employee who apologized and asked if I had time to wait for the steak to be ready. Unfortunately, I was in a hurry and just asked for chicken instead. They were not skimpy on the ingredients so thank you for that too!

The location is perfect if you live on the south side of Tucson. Let's just hope you're not wanting steak on your bowl and you'll be golden.

Lady who took my order at the drive through window was patient and kind.  The guy at the window was friendly and kind.  I wonder if his quietness is misunderstood.  

They got our order right and gave us plenty of napkins.  I ordered the beyond sausage with only the English muffin and they gave me an extra patty since I didn't get egg or cheese.

I bought chicken from here, to my regret. Antoch is a virus "hot spot". 
The young woman at the window had on a mask and gloves, however two young men putting orders together could be seen with no protective gear and one of them looked sick, pale, red rings around eyes and unwashed hair. I told the woman at the window I really didn't appreciate their lack of concern, she told them what I said and they looked annoyed and said "have a nice day".
Restaurant management is going to have to get deadly serious about seeing that their employees clearly demonstrate respect for the seriousness of coronavirus if they want to stay in business. Employees who wish to have a job better start caring to be ultra careful in order to keep their place of employment in business.

Last night I drove to the drive through at 11:40PM. I was the fifth car in line. The worker said through the speaker, "Sorry, we're closed." Shocked, I asked "You're closed 20 minutes before your listed closing time?" He responded, "Yes." As I pulled out of the parking lot, I saw another car pull into the drive through line. He must have been able to place his order because he staid in line. I can't make any sense of that. 

I've worked in the food industry and I know how much it sucks when a customer shows up close to closing time. However, fast food is a pretty common place to eat late at night on the weekends. If you don't like it, don't work the late shift!

This was the first time I returned to this location after I found a breaded & fried TWIST TAB in my popcorn chicken. I wasn't expecting much of an improvement last night, but I was at least expecting to be able to place my order.

My wife and I went for lunch for first time today to try an Incredible Whopper.  It was just past 1:40 pm and the inside had only a couple customers waiting for food.  We stood at the counter for quite a while watching workers walk by and avoid making eye contact, or perhaps acknowledging us and saying we'll be with you shortly. No, not a word from anyone. Finally, after saying excuse me, we got someone to take our order.  A Whopper, Crispy chicken, one med fries and two small soft drinks.  The sandwiches where 2 for $6 and the bill was $12.62.  After sitting down and looking at the big signs on their window, we realized we could have gotten the same thing with 2 orders of fries and two medium drinks for $10.  A 2 for $10 special.  They neglected to tell us about that deal.  Anyway, our ticket was time stamped 13:45.  The workers appeared to be totally disorganized and we sat and watched and waited and waited and waited. After a full 20 minutes (no exaggeration here because we had the time stamp), I got up to see what was going on.  Since arriving, only one other couple came in.  They had somehow totally lost our order, but couldn't be bothered to apologize. After over 30 minutes, our food arrived and still no apology.

Stopped by with my newborn for a quick bite. It was my first outing with my baby. I ordered my usual- a steak salad with guacamole on the side. One of the gentlemen serving was super nice and asked if he could help us to our seat. When I sat down he offered to grab me any extras I needed so I wouldn't have to get off my seat. Such a simple gesture goes a long way! This was very much appreciated because any new mom knows how hectic it gets traveling or going anywhere with a newborn. Awesome customer service! Thanks guy!!

Extremely dissatisfaction with service.
I like kfc popcorn nuggets. I always buy from kfc.
But when i ask for sauce they gave me only 1 piece.
Next time I order 3 different items still they gave me only 1 piece. I like sweet n tangy sauce so i ask for more, they said we can't give more.

I'm writing this review while waiting for my wife to pay. Coke machine is broken, my wife had to ask someone after 5 minutes to help us to order food. Not what an establishment should do when someone is spending $35 on food. Employees arguing with each other. Watched two guys wait for food for 10 minutes. Manager is cooking food, this place has really tanked over years. I love me a whopper from time to time but not when it takes 20-30 minutes to get order. I believe this is my last time at this establishment.

" Sir. Sorry for inconvenience from KFC Milpas, you can return and get free Monday Special any time any day Sorry for Inconvenience. Thanks "
- KFC Manager


So, you're wondering why the above apology is a 2 starred rating. 
Monday 3,2020 at approx.7 PM. I'm currently a long-term customer at this KFC location. To be brief, I'll summarize the following unusual and explicit service experience:
. ordering 2pc Meal ave.$5.00 value
. Mgr Mr.Syed says NO chicken(thighs)available, fryer duration 13 more mins., demands sit-down via wait for chicken order and unreasonably denies sale of other assorted chicken parts chicken breast, drum stick, etc. currently available in warmer oven, Mngr reject reasonable alternative or negotiable substitution sale order of (ave.$5.00 value) chicken sandwich or 2pc. breast meal.
. Mgr persists offering 4 whole wings (approx.$2.00 value) meal, Mgr. behaving unprofessional via argumentative, issuing an ultimatum to ultimately revoke THE opportunity to use and maximize ALL available business practice Customer Service requirements by inserting an unwarranted applicable service discretionary standard...

I'm sincerely appreciative of the store manager Mr. Ansar Syed's effort to resolving thus unpleasant customer service experience and Free Meal invitation.

Mr.R.White
KFC Corp.ref #YG989
1.800.225.5532

I went there with my grandma & I asked the cashier for salt & pepper. She said  "you don't need it it's already seasoned " we both got a chicken breast with mashed potatoes & gravy we wanted the pepper for the mashed potatoes. She needs to learn how to treat customers. You never tell a customer they don't need it cause you don't know what they like. The food was ok. If I was there anyone else besides family; I would've started something because she shouldn't treat a customer like that.

I do not go here a lot. But tonight, the cluck-bug bit me, and it was kinda late. I didn't even know if they'd be open.

Hit the drive-through. Some female SFI with Connecticut plates was ahead of me. Acting like she was ordering a gourmet meal.

A guy behind me got out of his car, and started yelling about movin' the line. Holy mackerel. I just told him to get back in his car . . . there's an SFI in front of us.

She kept putting the vehicle in reverse, and I thought she was going to smack into me.

The drive-through closes at 9:30 p.m. I got to the squawk-box at 9:30 p.m. Placed my order. They didn't have much . . . nothing wrong with that. I get it.

I just said, "Give me what ya got." I got a lot for $9.31. At the last minute, I threw in a large Diet Pepsi, and I think she threw that in for me.

Because she had to deal with the SFI in front of me.

I got 10 pieces of chicken. They weren't big, and most of them were wings. But I got a good thigh (like I need more of that) and good customer service (for which I am always grateful). And the chicken tasted great, probably because I hadn't had either lunch or dinner. But it hit the spot.

And we had a good laugh about the SFI in front of me.

SFI -- I just made it up. It's an acronym for stupid f*cking idiot.

This location is one of the closest for the east side and it is the WORST
I am currently obsessed with the impossible burger & I go here to get my fix. I deal with the long wait (even when there is NO other cars in the drive thru). The workers are rude. You can easily spend 15-20 mins here.

Whew. Impossible Whopper? They should call it impossible to make it home. I ate it there......... glad we live in The Landings.  I gave it right back. Maybe they should market it as a laxative. I feel better for having ate it. I cra**ed like a poisoned goose.   But all of y'all might not be in for it. Be forewarned

I went to KFC two weeks got some chicken and biscuits and mac and cheese ,i was dispointed biscuits were dry they gave us no jelly for them,the chicken was crispy, i wish it was spicy,and mac was some what ok.

I stopped by to grab a quick lunch to take back to the office after running a few errands on my lunch and decided to get the new 2 piece Boneless Combo, which is served with 2 pieced of Boneless Chicken, a side, 1 biscuit and a medium drink for $4.99, not a bad deal right? Wrong!

The chicken was awful, it was chicken shaped together to "look" like the matching piece of chicken on a bone. I had a "wing" and a "breast" both of which seemed to be leftover pieces of chicken that wasn't served just shaped together. This is a pure marketing . My suggestion to KFC is stick with what you know, regular fried chicken and even sandwiches. The would had been better off giving me two pieced of chicken from one of their sandwiches instead of the disaster that was served.

My family and I was craving for BK @ 9:00pm tonight..while we was headed that way it seems like it was closed looks like the lights was turned off but decided to take a chance and turned into the drive through surprisingly they were still open! The cashier guy sounded like he didn't want to be working tonight. As we wait for our food for 5 minutes he hands over our bag. We ordered 4 chocolate chip cookies, 2  Jr. whopper cheeseburger meal and 2  extra jr. whopper sandwiches on the side. All 4 burgers was COLD!!!!! Our chocolate chip cookies was not fresh or warmed. It was hard like a rock! Never coming back here again! We are so disappointed!

Okay, I don't get why this place gets so much hate. Service can be inconsistent. But, the chicken here is pretty fresh for KFC. Definitely, better than most KFC I've attended. If you go to KFCs in different locations, the food and care they put in their food has definitely gone down over the years. This location is usually super-busy, so, the turn-over on chicken is much faster making it more fresh. If this place is so bad, why is this place so busy? (People can say location, but there's a subway next door and it's rarely busy.)

Nothing spectacular or mediocre about this location.
Seems the sofrito is rather salty, will not have it again.

No problem with service, and the place is clean.
Would return.

We got very polite to go service, I believe the manager may have been taking the orders at around 10:30, though it was slow she apologized for having to tend to other things. Wasn't overly busy but they did have a line, my first comment was that it was, while slow, the most polite exchange I've had with someone at a drive through. 

After waiting several minutes we arrived at the window and paid, received or drinks and waited a few minutes more to receive the best French Fries I've had at a Burger King in YEARS! Typically I get the fries and have one or two and then choose not to finish, I was in their clutches and they were going to be finished, whether I had to peel them from my girlfriends fingers or keep taking hers...

Both our burgers were fantastic, while I didn't receive the Texas Double Whopper (which I'm 99% positive I ordered) I didn't notice until 3/4 through the burger. Normally we wouldn't rave about a burger, other than a Whataburger (something NOLA desperately needs), but these burgers were fresh, perfectly dressed (minus the no jalapeños and no bacon) and TASTY. 

Thank you to the lovely lady that took the time to make those fries perfect and assure those burgers coming out were tasty as possible.

Wow, I really don't know what to say. First off I ordered door dash and I didn't get my drinks but that is not completely kfc fault...What I will say is that the food was gross. The chicken was thin and the sandwich actually looked gross. The chicken bowl was ok but not yummy it didn't have any taste and the mashed pities looked like something from a nursing home or school. I was so shocked by food as I haven't eaten at kfc for along while. I won't be eating here again cheap food is ok but it should at least look edible and taste decent instead of like forgotten leftovers from a week ago..

I ordered the steak bowl. I loved the flavor on the steak and I'm pretty picky about steak. It had the charred crust on the outside yet not tough. I like the fact that you can choose exactly what you want. You go thru the line and select what you would like. The brown rice is really good, toppings are fresh and the service is great. The place was clean. Will be coming back to this location

Extremely inefficient service, I was there with only one car in front of me, asked fir a whopper and they made me wait 20 minutes till I just gave up and drove off with a paid bill and no food.
So much for fast food
Sad service

KFC used to be the go to fried chicken gettin ' place.  

It seems it just isn't anymore; at least not this location.  
I concur with the very first reviewer about this restaurant.  The service is low quality and barely lukewarm, and the food is exactly the same.  
The food tasted less than fresh, and the macaroni and cheese was absolutely hideous.  I didn't think mac n cheese could ever be that disgusting, but they managed to master it.   
Everything else is either from a can or highly processed.  

It's a good thing I know how to cook.  
Staying at home to make dinner myself is helluva lot cheaper, the quality of the food is loads better, and I might just live a few years longer as a result of it all.
Plus,  I am not a huge fan of stomach pumping.   ;o/

Easy in and out, Covid19 style, I ordered one impossible burger and one Whopper..... I was so hungry, and both were so good, I could not they'll the difference at the beginning. Very satisfying feeling.
Ladies were polite and effective. Food was very warm and well wrapped. Nice job Burger King!

Just had two of their fill-up meals for my wife and I.  This had to be the worst meal we have every had at a KFC.  There was absolutely no spices on the chicken.  I mean - NONE AT ALL, just the coating with no spices.  There used to be some spice in the gravy - NONE THERE EITHER.  The Dr. Pepper was a very weak Coke, at best.  The biscuits have been reduced about 50% in size.

I was at the drive-up window and you can see the chaos, dirt, and poor management of the back room.  It was a TOTAL MESS.  All of the masks were pulled down off of the faces of those working there.

KFC used to be a place where you could go to get a nice meal.  Not this particular franchise (if it is a franchise and not corporate owned).  If this is a franchise, KFC should seriously consider pulling its authorization to carry the KFC name.

The cookies that came with the meal MUST have been at least 2-3 months old.  Hard as a rock and completely dried out.  Awful.

This was an absolute horrible experience.  Not as bad as we had last spring, but it clearly has deteriorated even further.  We will not be going there again until there is a "Under New Management" sign in the window.

Don't really understand the low ratings. The people working there were really polite and food was as advertised. Saw the commercials for chicken and waffles and wanted to try it out. Was really surprised how good the chicken and waffles were. Food was served quickly and I took it to go. There were a few other people eating while I was waiting. Fast food chicken at cheap price. Can't really complain or expect more than that.

Was ordering food at the drive through at around 5:20pm on the 2nd of January. The girl was terrible, she didn't want to take our order for some odd reason. Like didn't answer us besides "hi"...a guy came on and took our order. He was nice. Then when we pulled up they gave us less chicken than ordered and the wrong sides. But the same gangsta girl handed us...I mean...threw the order at us and shut the window. What great customer service. They do chicken wrong!

I got takeout from KFC since I was passing by on Naamans Road. This KFC is a pad site in a strip mall on the westbound side of Naamans Road, just past the Naamans/I-95/I-495 intersection, so the location is rather strategic. Anyway, the fried chicken is about what one can expect from this fast food chain. The meal overall was good enough, although the biscuit seemed a bit small. What's not so good was the slow service that caused the ordering line to be backed up all the way to the door. They probably need more staff or faster staff to handle the lunchtime traffic.
Got bad food at b.k  with this whopper you did not see the tomatoes  was bad  so this is just not right how could you just put this on a burger  and not see its bad

I don't come often as this location is out of my way from work & home, this was perhaps my 2nd visit. I love the potato bowls and have not had one in over a year, and then I had this craving! I made the trip and I was NOT disappointed.  Look at all that chicken!!! I sent the photo to my mom in TX and a friend in Boise and it got their mouths watering.  They both took off shortly after to get their own and sent me photos of theirs too, and we compared notes. Hint: mine was the best! 
Okay I am craving it again. I'll leave my review at that, and pick up again should I get anything different!

If I could give 0 starts I would. My family was shopping at Walmart and ran into Burger King to grab a quick snack for the kids. After 15 minutes our food still wasn't ready so my husband went to the counter, the very rude woman shouted, they're working on it! Another 15 minutes went by and still no food. We went to the counter and demanded our money back as we've been waiting for 30 minutes for some darn chicken fries and a whopper. She rudely said "it has not been 30 minutes!" My husband looks at the time stamp on the receipt and says "oh you're right, it's been 34 minutes!" When she looked at it she realized he was right and gave us our drink for free since we had already drank them then she kept our receipt so we couldn't call corporate on them. I will never go back there again! Such a disgraceful way to conduct business! Shame on them!

I ordered a simple steak bowl with black beans, steaks
mild pico de gallo, mild corn salsa, and lettuce. I got a steak bowl with brown rice loads of sour cream and loads of cheese. I ordered this online and it was completely wrong. Super disappointed and upset!!!

This review is specifically for the experience I had on 5/16/20 which was a Saturday around 6pm. When I walked into the restaurant, I was glad to see they are implementing social distancing with the stickers on the floor every 6 feet. It took about 15 minutes to reach the food area where you place your order. I let the employee know immediately that I have 5 orders. She took 1 order (chicken burrito) and the 2nd order (chicken tacos) then slid the 2 orders down the line so at this point a 2nd employee is asking what toppings/add-ons I wanted. Basically, 2 employees were asking me "what else" for 5 different orders. I asked if we can address one order at a time and they didn't say anything. From here, my experience just kept getting worse as they were blatantly ignoring me. I was literally just standing there staring at the employees because for some reason they may have felt if they ignored me, I might leave and they won't have to assemble 5 orders. 

Mind you, the 5 orders were very simple: 1) Chicken burrito (plain), 2) chicken tacos with lettuce and avocado, 3) steak tacos (plain), 4) steak burrito (plain), 5) chicken bowl with pinto beans and pico sauce.

Before all 5 orders were complete, one employee placed 2 of my orders (taco and burrito) into someone else's bag. Facepalm. I brought this to their attention. When all 5 orders were finally complete and wrapped in foil, they were placed in a brown chipotle paper bag. They then placed that brown bag behind the cashier as if I wasn't about to pay for it and leave. At this point, I decided to wait and see if they would acknowledge me. Nope. 2 customers behind me paid for their food before I had enough and brought to their attention that the bag behind them is mine and I haven't walked away. I've been standing there the entire time waiting for this food. 

IN ADDITION, THEY DID NOT INCLUDE ANY NAPKINS NOR DID THEY INCLUDE UTENSILS FOR THE BOWL.

My best friend works at a different chipotle location so I have an idea of how overwhelmed they can get during rush hour but to simply ignore a customer is egregious! The food was for friends/family so that's why I endured this horrible customer service. If this was for me, I would have simply walked out without any food.

Feedback from fam/friends: the ones who ordered steak enjoyed their dinner. The ones that ordered chicken thought it had no flavor. 

P.S. the cost of adding avocado is insanely expensive. I don't think it's worth the actual cost of 2 hass avocados.

The Colonel would have been disappointed, too. I got a four-piece meal, thinking I'll save two of the pieces for lunch tomorrow.

The sides I ordered were green beans, and macaroni and cheese. The sides I got were green beans and those absolutely awful fake mashed potatoes with gelatinous brown stuff they call gravy.

I always get the grilled chicken. I don't know if it's healthier, but it does have fewer carbs.

The pieces: leg, wing, thigh, breast -- were the smallest pieces of chicken I've seen in forever. They must import their chickens from Lilliput. And the thigh was waaaaay overcooked.

The breast was the size of a thigh. The wing had one edible section.

In no time flat, I had eaten the thigh, wing section and leg. Green beans are always great, with plenty of pepper and onion. Ditched the potatoes and the horribly dry and mealy biscuits they serve.

So, instead of having nice leftovers for lunch tomorrow, I've got a paltry breast that may be all dried out by the time I try to eat it for lunch.

Sometimes ya just want KFC fried chicken, and tonight that's what we wanted. 

First impression was not so good.  Small, crowded and not very clean (walls in entry very dirty. Wipe them down for goodness sake).  Definitely knew I did not want to eat in.  

Placed our order and proceeded to wait...and wait...and wait!! Geez. Took forever.  It was a bit busy and others were complaining about the wait as well.  Have to say once we got our food I was not feeling too confidant.

However, once we got home and settled down to eat, I noticed our food was very hot (yeah!!)  I don't have any complaints about the meal. When you go to KFC you know what you're getting, and this was very good for what we got.  2 piece breadt & wing dinner with mashed potatoes and gravy, green beans and biscuit. It was great !! 

Points off for the long wait (come on, this stuff is pre cooked) dirty walls and not so friendly staff.

This is honestly the worst KFC I have ever had the displeasure of living near. Every time I go there, there is some sort of problem. Half the time you go to order and they say "we have no chicken, it will be 30 minutes". Or you order curbside, and they won't bring it out, answer the phone, so you have to wait in a long line to get your food. What's the point of ordering ahead then? Today I went to grab some food early before a flight, and online the hours are listed as open at 10 am, on the sign at the store it says 10:30. I went at 10:45 and could not order. This location needs to be shut down permanently.

This place is an absolute catastrophe! Definitely will drive the few extra miles to Aston if I ever want BK again. The staff is rude, never get the orders correct, and told me it was my fault when I ordered delivery through their APP never got my food and refused to refund me. I'll let the fraud department at the bank handle that. 
So disappointing that they refuse to own their own mistakes and blame a paying customer when they mess up. Sorry BK, I've given you multiple chances and I don't even like fast food, but figured I'd give the Impossible Whopper a chance. You have failed miserably and stolen my hard earned money. 
One star bc I can't give negative stars.

White Castle is an American institution. If you're looking for greasy, delicious food, then you are in the right place. The classic White Castle slider is one of the most delicious things out there. And White Castle will surprise you with other items as well. Their chicken sandwiches are terrific. And I was shocked at how much I've enjoyed their crab cake sliders. I can't endorse eating here often. It's a lot for the body to endure. But every once in a while, it's a true delight.

The WORST Dunkin Donuts I have ever experienced.  I came here to get a black coffee, and my sons wanted bagels.  The order was 2 plain bagels with cream cheese and a black coffee.  The wait was over FIFTEEN MINUTES.  There were 5 people behind the counter and 4-7 people in the store at any given time (including myself and my 2 sons). Zero excuse for such incompetence.

After 15 minutes we were given a bag of sausage and cheese bagels.  Ok, take 2.  This is not my order. "It's not? Are you sure?"  Five minutes later, "well what did you order?" I gave them my receipt.  "Oh we're out of plain bagels."  The plain bagels were in our sight when we ordered.  Ten more minutes goes by and we got our order, still screwed up - they toasted everything after we asked them not to.  Thirty minutes for black coffee and 2 bagels with cream cheese.  No apology offered.

Dunkin Donuts corporate, please take note and do something about how poorly this place is run.
It's was good and hot just not as much flavor in the crispy chicken skin. I also got a bowl which was delicious. They were faster then the place I usually go just a bit less flavor.

Youngest child wanted chicken nuggets for his lunch out today and I've been wanting to try The Impossible Whopper so BK was a no-brainer. 

We didn't experience the bad service other reviewers mentioned and the wait was maybe three minutes max. It feels weird giving Burger King five stars but kudos to them for the good service and for a chain restaurant actually finally offering a plant-based burger that is not a black bean burger AND also tastes great. I got the Impossible Whopper without cheese and although I'm not vegetarian, I would go back for this over and over again. It tasted great and I wasn't left feeling tired and nap-ish afterwards like I often do after eating a fast food burger.

I would give this place NO stars. Is that an option? So very disappointed. The only good thing was the customer service! I ordered the 3 piece big box with mashed potatoes, gravy, cole slaw and DARK meat. Home to find two breast pieces, drumstick, and mac & cheese. The chicken was dry ( why I order dark meat) tough, and cold ( I live 5 minutes away). Horrible experience. Thinking maybe instead of commercials talking about how great the food is, maybe spend time teaching, training what parts are dark or white meat AND actually providing what is ordered.

Piss poor ass service! And the food I just ordered was no better! I specifically said NO CHEESE on my whopper!!!! Get home and have to scrape the fucking cheese off! Had 2 free kids meals with the order guess what fucking chicken nuggets too hard like old ass nuggets first thing when you start serving lunch?!?! And the damn whopper wasn't even fresh! Although I smelled smoke it was not freshly cooked!!! No one should have to open their bags and check to make sure the order is correct before pulling off! Then you call back and guess what they won't even answer the damn phone!!! Not even busy there were only 2 cars myself and another. And not even 12 lunch rush hour so no excuse shit should be fresh and order should be right!!!  Why would I purchase a drink if I won't be returning to order the same stale, cold whopper and fries??? The nuggets for my kids we're overcooked! I specifically said NO CHEESE on my whopper and they can't even get that right. Then I call to let them know and h Th year won't answer they phone. I wasn't even crowded no line I was the first car and a second behind me. I could smell the grill and figured I'd have a fresh meal today. NOPE. It was all horrible like left over food from a day or two before! The meat didn't taste fresh. Nor did they bread. My kids nuggets were way overcooked and dry as the hottest desert you could imagine. The only decent/good thing about the order was the drinks. I could even eat my burger nor my kids enjoy their meal it was just that horrible. I've been to other Burger Kings and can't recall ever received food of such poor quality and taste. There is no excuse for the food we received today. Everything should have been correct and freshly made. Instead we got what taste like days old food sat underneath the warmer. An absolute disgrace and shame. When people are going through especially during this covid-19 no has money to just throw away. I'm not rich. And they won't even answer the phone so that I can tell them how terrible this food is. Guess they already know. I am never returning to this location. I'd rather drive to Seffner to the location just off of I-4 for a meal cooked right and freshly made to order. That's how pissed and disgusted I am with the lack of service and don't care attitude about the way food is served from this location. There should be a complete overhaul. Or maybe even tear the place down. Nothing but stale/overcooked food coming out of here. Now I even had a former employee who worked at this location and was at Bloomingdale location told me how terrible it was there and about all the negative reviews for this location. I tried and tried but no more never again! Not this location. EVER!

It was slow day in the mall
And i was looking for a burger 

Manager toni was on the store
She offered me to make my whopper fresh of the broiler 
And my fries was fresh off the fryer 

Great day at the mall for me

I mean... it's Chipotle. Over-stuffed burritos, Salty limey chips, new chalky cheese, and smokey sofritas. It's everything you'd expect from a Chipotle, or, Chipolte, if you're an Oro Valley native (i.e. Snowbirds from Indiana.) I just like that it's close to my work, and that I went there at noon-thirty today and there was no line. Give them a try if you're in the area. You won't regret it.... unless of course you catch salmonella or something..

When I ordered the impossible burger with no Mayo or cheese I expected it to be hot not lukewarm. Then I complained to them and they told me once they put the lettuce and tomatoes and other vegetables the patty got cold that way which shouldn't be the case. I saw them get it out of the food warmer then one worker proceeded to lie about it wasn't her that cooked it when she just told me when she put the lettuce and tomatoes it got cold that way. Worst service ever

Was really disappointed today. Both my friend and I got bowls with tofu (sofritas) and both were contaminated with meat. My bowl has steak pieces, while we found chicken in hers. As a vegetarian, this was really scary and I thought I was going to get sick when I looked down at the bite I was about to eat. They replaced it, and I wouldn't have left a bad review. But then my friend finding a piece of meat in hers as well made it obvious that the sofritas were not being kept separate as well as they should have. Chipotle advertises themselves as a vegan and vegetarian friendly restaurant, and this turned me away from it.

Service was good. But size of the chickens are another story. I posted a picture to show you. The 20 piece chicken wings were about the same size of my 8 piece chicken wing!  Some of the other pieces were small as well.  But will say, at least the chicken was nice and crispy and the coleslaw was good.

I have engaged in a battle with my roommate and many others concerning the better breakfast sandwich: The WaWa Sizzlie or the Dunkin Donut bacon/sausage, egg and cheese.

To me its no contest, DD's sandwich wins with a first round KO.  I'm not a big coffee drinker so I DO come here for the food and I have only been disappointed one time...I'll leave out the details.

The staff at most of their locations are usually pretty friendly as well.  Once, at approximately 2 am, I went into a DD (somewhere around 12th and Market) with a Donut craving and a broken cell phone.  By the time I left both were taken care of.  Not only was the guy working a Dunkin Donuts counter-guy, but also was able to fix my phone.  Amazing.

Fast Food? Definitely not. Not sure what the issue is with this place but they are definitely not fast. At first I thought they were so slow because people love this location so much that it is just packed all the time. I couldn't have been more wrong. I decided to pass on the drive thru because it was just too busy and I was hoping the inside would be faster. 
I walk in and see a bunch of people sitting down...I guess when I ordered I didn't realize these people weren't actually eating but were actually waiting for their food still. I am talking there was like 7 people waiting for food. I even heard one guy say he was waiting for 20 minutes already. I think I timed it to about 25 minutes myself from the time on the receipt to the time I walked out. Pretty ridiculous actually, but it is what it is. 

Other than that, I didn't have any issues with the food. The chicken tender meal I got was still really good and the flavor is what you would expect from the KFC brand. The service is what is driving this place down!

Stopped by for a burger yesterday around 8:00 pm.  I know that it is Christmas Eve, but the staff had the door locked.   They slowly walked to let customers in.   

They had pizza everywhere.

Definitely will not be going back to this location for a Whopper.

Seriously the staff here is dangerously stupid.. I'm actually worried for them... I've been here a few times and Everytime they surprise me more. They told me they would bring my food out to my car the last time so I pulled around. One if the employees locked the doors so 30 minutes passes I'm still waiting on chicken tenders and they're closed... I was dumbfounded. They always forget something and they've doubled charged my card before

My boyfriend was just their re about 30 minutes ago and  got food for my daughter and I before he headed to work. He didn't receive half the order-- of which was the food intended our daughter. It was missinng  popcorn chicken, potato wedges and the coleslaw. We tried calling but the line just rings and rings! I wish this was the first tjme this has happened-- but unfortunately it's not.
The place is so dirty , they are rude to u .. We really need a Popeyes near here .. Next time I will go to the acme for chicken

I went to the Dunkin donuts in 30th street station to get a quick bite to eat before I went to catch my bus. It was definitely not quick by any means. The employees were professional but the service was very slow and for no apparent reason. All of the ones I go to in NY have quick service. There may be some disconnect given at this one you can't actually see them prepare the food (maybe they move slower because of it or maybe it feels longer because you can't watch as they do it). Nevertheless they were in no rush to get people out of there. You would think they would be more efficient given people are usually on some type of timeline. Anyway, I got the new chicken apple sausage sandwich. I switched the American cheese for cheddar and it was actually pretty tasty! I also ordered an orange juice -it was really overpriced but still good. There are plenty of other places in 30th to get food (like Cosi next door) but that line is pretty much always long. But given how long it took to get our food I think I'll just give Cosi a try next time!

I don't do fast food often, but of course, the one day I decided to go to BK there's an eternal wait. I was in the drive thru line for over 15 minutes- long enough for multiple levels of regret to seep in and wonder what I was doing with my life.

I finally got my whopper and fries and it was all okay. I hate to blame the staff, but they just didn't seem motivated to even be working.

Tried to order a whopper at 11:42... The restaurant it's supposed to close at midnight. No one ever came to the drive-through and I saw the employees sitting in the boots and the restaurant. If you're open till midnight... Why would night be able to place an order at 11:42 PM?

I had been hankering for fried chicken for some time, but just never was near KFC or Popeyes, but finally was close to the KFC in Meridian. Completely disappointed. The line was ridiculously long in the drive thru, and the dining room is currently closed due to COVID, so that was understandable. When it was my turn to order, I asked for extra crispy, but was encouraged to get original because the extra crispy would be at least a 20 minute wait. I'd already waited about 20 minutes to place my order, and was a little annoyed that they hadn't thought with a line around the building they should be dropping both original and extra crispy to serve. Anyway, I agreed to the original. It was cold, and soggy, and slid off the chicken. Ugh. Everything I ordered was cold, and old. Won't be going back. I wouldn't have been so annoyed about the wait if it had at least been fresh.

I do not think that the fryer and grease has ever been cleaned at this location in Ballwin, MO--the chicken is consistently horrible. I have complained and called the manager and they are very nonchalant about it. I will never eat there again.

Honestly don't come here. The food is trash. Always cold. The best part of the food was the soda and even that was watered down. Mac and cheese was inedible, my dogs wouldn't even eat it. The gravy tasted burned. The chicken strips were so so so bad. Wasted money on this place to go get the family another meal after.

The sign said they had Nashville hot chicken, so I was sold.  

But it's not on white bread with a slice of american cheese, so while the chicken was pretty good, it wasn't what I was hoping for.  

I guess I was expecting too much from KFC, considering that any cuisine involving white bread and american cheese should be pretty easy to nail down.

The food quality is on par comparing to other burger king.  So I will not comment about the food.  I got a phone coupon for a free Whopper, place my order via app, then at the drive-thru window, told the machine is broken, and rather than refund, they asked if a chicken sandwich is ok.  The customer service is good.  As for now, only drive-thru and workers do wear their mask.

This place isn't even worth a one star.  How do we give negative stars???   SERIOUSLY!!  WTF?!?    I am sitting in this line for 20 minutes now and the chick just said to wait.   I can even back up because of the way this drive thru is.   There is one (1) - freaking ONE car in front of me.   WTF. They are even out of chicken filets.     This place just plain sucks.

I took one star off. Been going here for a long time, pretty much always use a coupon because without the coupon the prices are to high for fast food chicken. Went in there tonight with a coupon and was quickly told "we don't take these", i said "i used one of these the other day" ,"ok let me check in the computer , no sorry". So i left and went elsewhere. And the $5 fillup, is now the fillup @ $5.99. I will probably go back at some point, because i do like their chicken and mashed potatoes with gravy...but definitely not very often...maybe every six monthes or so. The coupons come in the local mail and paper, so obviously they're for this area...they wouldn't spend money to print and deliver if they were for another area.

I guess I'm just a slow learner, Once again they can't get a simple order right. Ordered one whopper, one whopper junior and four orders of nuggets. Get home to find two juniors and no receipt so I don't if they cheated me or if they're too dumb to get a simple order correct.  Sad to say it's not the first time something like this has happened. Hope the new Burger King being built on 135 will be owned by some one else who will take an interest in the business.
.

Can I rate it a ZERO? Thought I'd grab a snack while getting a fill-up. Never again.

- Freezing cold in here!! Maybe 55 degrees?

- Took 10 minutes + to get a burrito & chicken box

- Chicken box was delivered with fries instead of the mashed potatoes on the menu.

- Register person was nice, but the place seemed out of control.

The most unsatisfactory delivery of food I have ever received at a Burger King.  I ordered a Whopper with Cheese and received the carnival burger in the photo - meat, bread, onions, and ketchup.  I don't think I need to say much more.
Burger King now offers cinnabons for dessert, because that's exactly what people need after eating a Whopper.

I love KFC!! But this KFC disappoints me every time I decide to give it one more shot! I've lived in this area for 10 years. The last time I got chicken from this KFC I got a bucket of roasted chicken and believe it or not all the chicken was skinless!! I called and complained and was told" yeah the skin comes off when we are in a hurry." This time I got the original chicken for dinner. I swear this must have been cooked at noon and sat there for five hours!! How can this place stay open? The Colonel must be rolling in his grave. Thank God we have a Chick- fil-a now two blocks away from this KFC!! My family will NEVER go to this KFC again!!!

I consider myself an authority on chipotle matters of food quality, food quantity, speed of service, and customer service.

I've been eating chipotle at least once a week for the past four years, in numerous parts of the country and I can assure you, this chipotle location, at Fletcher and Dale Mabry in Tampa Florida, is the best in the nation.
