This location consistently has fresh veggies and lettuce, fresh -not over cooked- steak, satisfying portions, and fast lines during rushes. Customer service is top. Most of my orders are online and there has only been one instance in which I received the wrong bowl. They quickly made the correct one and sent me away with both bowls.

The times I do order in person I'm greeted with quick, efficient service and a positive attitude.

If you live in the Tampa area and are looking for a restaurant to regular, the Chipotle at Fletcher and Dale Mabry is calling.

Sadly the last time I had KFC I got food poisoning and never ate it again until today. KFC is just not my favorite, nor will be. They hold the lowest position as far a Chain-Fried Chicken, in my opinion. 

On a stroll through the neighborhood I was starving. The only reason I stopped was because I saw a Taco Bell sign. I decided to get both! Ha. As far as the wait in Drive-Thru....long. The chicken....not that great. I wont be going out of my way to come here; at the time it was nothing more than convenience.

I used to love KFC but their food has gone way down. I stopped eating their original recipe chicken a long time ago because it's way to greasy and was often undercooked. Today I tried the Kentucky grilled chicken. It had a good taste but the breast was the size of a small thigh and the leg looked like it came from a Cornish hen. Both pieces were either over cooked from the start or left under the warmer far too long because they were extremely dry. I also decided to try their spicy chicken. It was cooked well but the pieces were extra tiny. I had corn for one of my sides and it was over cooked to the point it looked shriveled and was scorched. My daughter's food wasn't any better. Colonel Sanders would be so disappointed to see the poor quality and lack of care put into the food offered by the once popular food chain. I'm sad to say I will no longer be able to satisfy my taste for good food from KFC.
Ordered family meal 8 piece. The chicken was so old II about broke a tooth on it.  Also, paid for extra biscuits but didn't get them. Doubt I'll be going back to this KFC.

I could sit here and act like I'm too cool for Chipotle, because it's a chain restaurant, it's owned by McDonalds, it's not "authentic" Mexican food, yadda yadda yadda.  Guess what? I'm not too cool for Chipotle...this place may as well be my second kitchen. 

In fact, if I were a millionaire, I would buy a Chipotle to connect to my house, so I could do the boxer commute straight to the front of the line. 

If you haven't tried Chipotle, shame on you. There is literally no excuse if you live within 100 miles of one of these lunch oases. "But, I don't have a car" you say, in your whiny, unimportant, generation douchebag voice. Ride your bike, walk, hitchhike, commandeer a large fleet of helium balloons, or jump on a train car in Boxcar Children fashion with one of those stick-bag things to carry your doo-dads. Just get to Chipotle. 
Everything on the menu is good. I don't know what the hell a GMO is, but I am proud to say that I don't eat them at least three out of five work lunches a week. I don't know what gluten is either...Chipotle is probably gluten free too...sure...yeah, it's definitely gluten free.  So if you are a bubble boy with an over-protective Mother, you can eat there guilt-free. 
There are times where I would prefer a steak burrito bowl to an over-the-pants hand job at a major sporting event. Most times, actually. I guess it would really depend on the section you are sitting in and what game it was. If I'm watching the New Orleans Pelicans play the Washington Wizards in a battle of the most irrelevant team of my lifetime, then yes...definitely the burrito bowl. 

The ambient house-reggae fusion is just as soothing as wiping your dirty mug with a recycled napkin. 

Eat at Chipotle, idiot.

Whaddiya want for fast food? I placed order and paid ahead in their app. Someone answers the speaker in a barely audible and rather indifferent tone. "Your order will be ready when U get to da window." Click. No thanks, no real greeting. When I get to the window, the fellow attached to speaker voice greeted me by name. Okay: there we go; that's  better!

Took the two cars in front of me awhile to get their order straight, but that's par for making a decent sized order on the fly rather than planning. Not the restaurant's fault; just let me enjoy the fresh air in like a little longer.

So I wound up getting quite a deal: BOGO sandwich and two big orders of chicken nuggets for my colleagues and me. Also an order of onion rings tossed in there...$6.41 total. Their app gives fantastic deals if you sign up and troll around. 

Plenty of sauces for the nuggets. Ch'King and whopper were both delightful...colleagues seemed okay with the nuggets. I myself don't care for them as a meal. Eh...I dunno how they can make money that way. But not my chicken, not my monkey, not my circus. My thanks to His Highness...

No star for this place as I was waiting for my food, I noticed that one of the cooks was not wearing a glove.   I asked that he put on gloves, should seen the dirty looks I got.  When I got my food, I  had them to made it again.  I  watched the whole time to make sure there was no funny business.  Bad service...I worked in a kitchen that serve food to public and know proper procedures.  Only reason for the star this system would not let me post without one.
Stopped by to grab a whopper at the end of the day, there was no long wait as I had expected. Service was prompt, food was hot  and James was so friendly!! Will be back to this location!

Nasty! They were selling fries and had not ketchup. There big big hunks of lettuce and even harder tomatoes on their Whopper. I asked if they had ketchup, salt and napkins and was told they did not have ketchup. So when I looked in the bag there was one napkin and no salt. I asked for salt and was told they didn't have any. I said, "well, I was just told you did." Then I got salt. I was there because I was starving. I would rather have starved.

Meh
Prepare to stand in line waiting for a cougar from SoCal making small talk with the help, pushy people behind you trying to push the line ahead while Ms Divorcee from the OC can't take her eyes off the guy at the register, or take a clue she's holding things up (WTF is it with those people)?

Ordered a keto bowl, appeared to be the least thing that could get screwed up.
$20 for two of them?
I was wrong
Tasteless carnitas, watery salsa, bland guacamole and I had to tell the guy twice it was to go - I guess there is no Ebonics translation for "to go"

First and last time

Horrible experience.    Man shooting up heroin in bathroom.   Staff arguing in front of customers.  Good quality not good.  Mozzarellas sticks burnt. Taco cheese fell out in bag.  Whopper was ok.   Very disappointed in staff.  If you don't take pride in your appearance or work, don't work with the public.
4 stars for offering the impossible burger...... Now just offer vegan mayo naise and ketchup that does not have high fructose corn syrup in it and you will get to 5 stars

Seems like every time I go to chipotle (any chipotle) they manage to mess something up. I've worked in food, it can't be that hard to scoop an ingredient in a bowl. Chipotle as a whole need to teach their Neanderthals better on quality control and details. My last three orders. 1. Sofritas Burrito, bit into it at work and realized it was steak (I'm vegan... which is why chipotle is my go to despite their incompetency). 2. Sofritas bowl, double protein. I got pretty much half a bowl of food. 3. Sofritas bowl w/ chips and guac. They neglect to put my 2 dollar 1oz side of guac. I've had to stop ordering burritos because the sofritas are so juicy that they can't manage to not make my burrito and giant sloppy mess. No matter what you do, you can't win with these idiots. Someone please open up some quick vegan options around Boise so I can stop dealing with chipotle.

Stopped in at the Burger King at the Delaware House Travel Plaza after leaving PHL - I realized I had not eaten since noon and it was now close to 10:00 pm.

I ordered the new "Angriest Whopper" as a Double Whopper with Cheese - no bun, and a medium order of onion rings, plus a bottle of water.

It took 10 minutes to receive my order. The time stamp on the receipt - until the time I received my food - was 10 minutes. Tons of other customers who were in line after me received their orders before I received mine.

To top it all off, when I went to sit and eat it - the burger was cold. Only the onion rings were hot. I wolfed it down anyway as I was hungry - and did not want to wait another 10 minutes for another cold burger.

Hopefully this is not how they normally serve customers...

First off, let me just say I already had something light elsewhere and was just here for a taco and some Guacamole,the people making the food have no sense of food presentation , asked for a carnitas Taco, they put it together like shit, you can tell they're just there for the paycheck and don't really like cooking or making food.

I love kfc so I hate this, the worst meal I ever had, chicken tough and greasy,  I asked the girl at drive-thru for butter salt & pepper since the dining room WAS NOT OPEN, I dont want eant to eat chicken in my car! Ugh

It was pouring rain on a Sunday night, and I wasn't in the mood to go anywhere fancy for dinner. Chipotle is right up the road from me, and the idea of some quick Mexican food sounded like a plan.

Unfortunately the word "quick" does not seem to be in the vocabulary of the staff as they were busy conversing about their weekend conquests and could have cared less about the line of people standing on the other side of the counter. This was not shaping up to be a positive dining experience.

I ordered a steak burrito bowl, brown rice, pinto beans, heavy on the veggies, medium hot salsa, and no sour cream. I didn't think that was a particularly difficult order, but it took two guys about ten minutes to figure out how to assemble the bowl, and then another 15 minutes for the lady behind the register to get me checked out. By the time they were done clowning around the food was cold and unappetizing.

Most of the Chipotle Mexican Grills are generally pretty good for Mexican fast food, but these clowns were pathetically awful. Next time I'll pick a different place for a quick dinner.

I ordered 4 milkshakes and got ice with a brown sludge at the bottom. Double whopper with 1/2 inch wilted lettuce and a 1 inch tomato slice with no onion or pickle. Why hate on me to support you. A simple call saying you were out of milkshakes would have sufficed or just cancel my uber order.
The chicken i got tasted great and the man at the window, Jay was the nicest man I had ever met, he was so kind and asked if I was from out of town and I felt welcome, he was pretty cute too

I've gone there a few times, whenever I'm in the area.. and the chicken pieces I got were large and juicy. This last time (Absolutely my LAST time) I ordered two legs. The owner (I believe) took my order and while ringing me up, he turned his back on me and instructed his (I believe) wife "... Two SMALL legs".  She silently filled my order and kept her face neutral as she handed me the bag. I couldn't believe I'd heard what I heard until I got to my car and on opening the box, indeed found two burnt, tiny & almost inedible legs. Obviously he thought me a good opportunity to get rid of unsalable food at full price. I've never been treated so dismissively in my life. CanNOT recommend this business.

Horrific food poisoning from steak burrito.  Owners response:  "Too bad"  

Spent an entire day and night in the bathroom from eating at this place.  

This happened around the time of the nationwide Chipotle E. Coli outbreak.  

Wasn't offered anything from Chipotle but a rude attitude from their customer service department.

I love Mexican food and many other ethnic goods. I am amazed that it took me so long to get around to trying this location. Color me disappointed. This location is fairly busy with people in and out. 
The parking lot is a nightmare and an accident waiting to happen.  The ingredients were fresh, except tonight the meat did not taste very fresh.
I am amazed there is only 2 varieties of white rice and no brown rice option. I am also amazed their are only corn and rice taco shells, it's almost 2015. There were 5 kids behind the counter playing at work. 
I got 2 chicken & 1 steak taco and by appearances they could have come from anywhere. The steak was flavorful and had a slight smoky quality. The chicken was dry and tastelss, absolutely nothing remarkable
What blew my mind was the price of over $8.00 for 3 tacos! Definitely not wot that price tag.
Many tables were dirty and during my 30 minute visit, they remained dirty, while too many staff played behind the counter. I had a hard time finding a clean place to sit.
Would not recommend or return based on price, cleanliness, lack of staff professionalism and an average product, nothing memorable, except the price.
What's a girl to do? I went, I saw, I tried and I will NOT repeat that cycle.

Live two blocks away from this location 1000 w. Esplanade in Kenner Louisiana.  Have not received a receipt when I make a purchase at the drive up in at least 5 months if not longer. It is the same for a cash or credit card transaction . If you ask for a receipt they will tell you they can write it down on a piece of paper. NO JOKE! She said it was $5.01 and I can write it down.Today I ordered a Whopper fresh off the broiler at the drive up speaker. I was willing to wait for a FRESH burger. I pulled up to the window and the lady was already putting my hamburger in a bag then handed it to me. They merely put a dressed and wrapped burger in the microwave and reheated it.  The lettuce was hot and soggy the tomato was hot and soggy as well as the onions and pickles and the bun was hot. Because they overheated the lettuce, tomato, onion and pickle there's was hot liquid juice in the paper wrapper which dropped on my clothes. Most times I have a Burger King meal I drive to a different Burger King location on veterans boulevard, but today I was in a hurry for lunch and went to  this 1000 West Esplanade location in Kenner. I guess the long lines in the new Arby's right next door is self explanatory. Avoid this location if you can! Try Arby's like I will do next time.

Absolutely disgusted by the lack of care about their food quality.

Nobody in the drive thru to order or pick up and only one customer inside.

 Ordered a number 1 whopper meal large and they gave me what I ordered, but the fries were nasty like been sitting since last night and the whopper was legitimately cold. 

Bun was cold, cheese not melted and even burger was cold too.

I understand covid and all the troubles that come with it, but I try to help at least one business when I can due to my business being in the toilet, exactly where this sandwich should have went immediately.

$10.02 and the only thing I felt was safe to ingest was the coke.

There are a lot of fast food and restaurants that need our money and deliver quality food that is prepared appropriately, but no at this BK in Greenwood next to the mall.

Hate to see businesses go out of business during these times, but I would rather see this places doors shut and give our money to another business that cares. 

I don't mind waiting for well prepared food to the quality expected.

Avoid this location, if your heart is set on BK, then drive a few extra miles and try one of the other hundreds around.... just don't waste your money here, or worse take a chance on getting sick from ill prepared food.

Sorry for the negative review, but I don't return food, this is what I do and wish more people would respond as such.

Didn't know they had such a low rating, but now I know for a fact I will be doing my due diligence in the future and only visiting higher yelp reviews.

Female employee at the window was kind, but unfortunately that isn't the reason we eat out at places.

K, it's Burger King, it's Always going to be Burger King. Until it's not. Are they paying a living wage? I don't know. I do know it's a high volume establishment and a member of management was actively separating themselves from employees by being degrading to those in their charge but hey, y'all still got that .99 whopper sometimes or nAh?

I love chipotle, but this location had terrible service. First, I ordered delivery. I ordered a bowl with double meat and guacamole. After waiting an hour for my bowl, the steak and guac were totally missing

My husband got us a $20 fill-up of crispy fried chicken for supper tonight.  Kentucky Fried Chicken used to be good but we had the worst chicken.....the chicken was so over cooked that the skin was hard and the chicken was so dry we could hardly eat it.  We wanted coleslaw but they were out so we substituted for mac and cheese.  I realize the $20 fill-up was inexpensive for 8 pieces of chicken, two helpings of mashed potatoes, 4 biscuits and an additional side but I would have thought the chicken would have been better.  It's obvious the chicken wasn't cooked fresh and tasted like it had been sitting under the heat lamp for quite a while.  Very disappointed and won't be going back.  I didn't want to select a rating but was required to so I gave it one star.

Excuse me for cursing, but one of the best goddamn chicken sandwiches ive ever eaten is one that I purchased here. The coating may not have been as crunchy as other KFC's but the chicken was still super tender and big!

I'd like to think I'm not a food snob - after all, I like Burger King! Once in a blue moon I have the urge for a Whopper Jr. and onion rings. I've eaten here several times, and taken out from there once. This location is easy to get to, has decent parking, is clean, has carpeting, a large kid play area, and, most interestingly, has kiosks to access the Internet - for free!

Last I checked, MySpace was blocked by their gateway software, in case you're curious...

I went in to this Burger King one time expecting an exquisite dining experience. This one was an hour away from my home, but I've been told fabulous things about this particular Burger King location. My prior visits to other Burger King locations have been nothing short of top-tier. However, the service was quite disappointing, and the food was lackluster.

To preclude, I am accustomed to the finest in dining cuisine, as I live with a chef. I was actually going to bring him food on this occasion as he was sick in bed, and I made sure to alert the cashier that this order was to be consumed by someone with tastes of high caliber.

My order consisted of a Whopper, fresh off the broiler, add cheese, no mayo, no ketchup, no onions, add mustard, put in the microwave, cut in half, have the halves individually wrapped and then wrap the two halves in one wrapper. Simple, or so I thought. The cashier was a freshly-shaven white boy with a welcoming smile, but clearly no experience working at a restaurant during Friday lunch rush.

It took the cashier forever to ring up the order (I can't imagine why -- it was only one item) and he even had to go into the back to clarify with the cook. It's a Whopper. What's not to get?

When I received my sandwich, (my name was spelled wrong on the ticket, by the way) I left, and checked the burger while sitting in the car. The cashier forgot to include mustard on the ticket, so it was not added to the sandwich. I took the food back into the restaurant and was fortunate enough to receive prompt service from a manager who properly replaced the sandwich. My husband, however, was quite dissatisfied with the Whopper, as it did not taste very fresh to him. Burger King is probably acceptable to most folks, but when you serve a chef, you'd think they would want to go the extra mile. I know I did. I went 50, in fact.

Always one of our favorite places when in the mood for Mexican. Can always count on fresh ingredients and savory flavors. I typically go for the Sofritas bowl which is basically marinated tofu crumbles in a savory sauce. Pair that with some brown rice and grilled veggies. If you've never been to a Chipotle it's very similar to a Subway where instead of building your own sub, you build your own burrito or burrito bowl or salad or taco. They even have kids meals and even though not on the menu will do a quesadilla. They have queso now, which I'm really not a fan of .....it tastes like they put too much flour in it. Love their chips and guacamole though.

I convinced my family to give this KFC a try since I spotted it in a strip mall we pass by at least 4 times a week. It is very hidden amongst the other shops. I do so that this is the best looking KFC (from the outside) that I have ever seen so it made me have high hopes that the food would match. My order consisted of Large Side of MacNCheese, Individual Side of Coleslaw, Individual Side of Mashed Potatoes, Fill Up: Famous Bowl (Bowl, Cookie, Drink), 2 Honey BBQ Chicken Littles, 1 Buffalo Chicken Little, and 4 Biscuits. Upon delivering the food they forgot the Cookie, Drink, and Buffalo  Chicken Little. They took forever to bag the food even though the had virtually no line and were not serving food inside. All my Chicken Little's looking like they were squeezed inside of their fist. They forgot my drink, cookie, and 1 chicken little. I will not return to this location.

I think the franchisee and the corporation dont care. 
If you visit this location they will sell you old food that did not sell. This happened 2 me twice and now I'm the fool for giving them another chance. 

I ordered a whopper with nothing on it but ketchup and when i got home i got a burger with everything on it and it was cold,that tells me this this was not made when I was there since i live less than a minute away.

Now lets go back to my first experience i ordered a crispy chicken and a cheeseburger for my kid and when i got home both were frozen bricks and old.
Fries were cold. Given the time I'm now convinced they sell old food. 

Burger-king corporation should be ashamed of themselves.

Just your typical Burger King  place. We had the impossible burger and it was so good and FILLING! We went through the drive through and it was busy, but we also went during lunch time. The cashier was very nice as well.

I love whopper Wednesday's. You get exactly what you pay for: a nice bun and patty with vegetables and the iconic sauce. I like that their onion rings are bite size, evenly fried, and overall decent. 

Compared to what this place was before, it has upgraded greatly with a modern look and cleaner atmosphere. Also, they have a play area to cater to families.

BEST biscuits and cole slaw ever of any fast food franchise i can make a meal just with large Cole slaw and 4 bsicuits 

it's an old KFC been there forever but if your smart get there 10 mins before closing and you get ooddells of chicken for $10 because they have to throw away whats left over by law.

Seriously, y'all. This is ridiculous. Girl gives me an all busted open Nashville hot tenders and thankfully replaces it. I get home to a smash biscuit and a busted open mashed potatoes. Nashville hot chicken is supposed to come with pickles. Guess what? No pickles. Raise your standards. I am willing to wait 10 extra seconds to get food that isn't messed up in the dumbest ways. Smh.

Steer clear of this KFC: very poorly managed, and definitely not very clean.
On my recent visit, they were out of ready chicken - at 6:30 in the evening, prime dinner time. Waited about 20" for replenishment. One of the employees was very nice and tried to make it up to me, while the manager was useless.

This KFC must have a new cook, because last time I was there is was good. This time the chicken was so dry and almost burnt. Like crunching on potatoe chips. And the biscuits are not the same, taste like flour, and NO butter on the top of them. God
Ordered the impossible burger, with cheese they wanted like 9 bucks.. at the highway rest area. Highway robbery is more like it.

My kfc.com order process was clean, straightforward, and error-free. Two thumbs up! The good experience ended there. I'm also an essential worker providing nearby retail services, so...yes...I walk those shoes. I get it. And I'm not in competition with this business. Still, your KFC mileage is going to vary considerably.

This Franklin store has always struggled due to its limited access location. Even pre-COVID, its lot was usually empty while competitor lots in the same block were busy. Yet, at 4:20 PM on a covidian  Sunday, the line was long with roughly a dozen cars as I waited. I had placed an online order scheduled for pickup at a certain time and I arrived on time.

Curbside pickup spaces are clearly marked, but no one was there to answer the phone or deliver. I called the number posted on the sign..."Please leave a message." Hmm. No, I needed to actually talk to someone. Redialing received the unwelcome squeeee, buh-lee, buh-squeedelee of a fax machine modem. It's a swing and a miss for strike one.

I pulled back into the drive-through line behind other cars that arrived as I wasted time calling for pickup. Sitting in the drive-through line with everyone else just now placing new orders is the only way to retrieve your scheduled pick-up. When I arrived at the microphone I was instructed: "Please pull through." "O.K., uh, do I pull up to a specific place for online orders?" "No, just pull up to the window." Strike two. Five minutes later, I have my warm, yet, stale order with warm, iceless tea and cold, stale, oil-drowned fries. Strike three. The chicken and its batter are over an' outta here.

O.K., buckaroos. You're going to have to decide whether this is really worth your support. Yum! Brands was clearly struggling across all product lines before the challenges of the pandemic. Pizza Hut's recent death rattle is a sad thing, but was well earned by Yum! execs. Kudos for trying with KFC, but it seems too little, too late. COVID struggles or no, if you're going to give customers options, you have to deliver. And the product you deliver still has to be on the mark. My hope is that Harlan Sanders' dream doesn't finally die, but he succeeded, is now long gone, and is no longer worried about it.

I finally tried the "impossible burger." My first bite, I didn't like it. The second bite on though, it was actually quite tasty. In some ways it was actually better than a real hamburger; didn't leave me with that heavy feeling you get after eating meat. A bit pricey for fast food.

We live in the neighborhood and occasionally patronize this restaurant. Their main problem is they never have register receipt so always say the printer is broken but when you complain to corporate, they miraculously have some then next day. Today, we utilized a promotion by Door Dash that had a $1.00 delivery if you ordered $10.00 worth of food. My daughter ordered three Whopper Juniors and a large order of onion rings. 
We watched the delivery guy on our phone app make his way to this restaurant (he was 20 minutes late) so we know this is the specific BK he picked up from. The food was cold, the Onion rings were only half full. We got in the car and drove up to BK and the cashier was rude. We showed her our electronic receipt and she scolded me that they don't do door-dash, walked away and screamed this guy is being rude to me and she wasn't dealing with me. I asked to speak with the manager and informed her that her employee was the rude one and explained our problem. She said that the delivery guy must have eaten our onion rings and she promptly replaced our order. The manager did the right thing but there is no excuse for the way the cashier behaved. Won't be using door dash again and it will be a long time before I go to this BK.

I've gone to this White Castle location several times and had no problems however.... On 6 September 2021 I was told they were not offering sliders. I understand the need to clean. However, this location is 24 hours. I've come at the same time many times before. And yet... I
 Kever order slider which have never been a problem. I'd rather you just say "no... I don't want to work" then I would understand. So instead of sliders from White Castle I'm getting burgers from McDonald's. My money might not be a lot to share holders but it's all the world to me. I would tell the employees "do better people, do better".

UPDATE 8/2020 
As you can see from my previous review I only buy pot pies here. Well...last night my husband found a large piece of plastic INSIDE of his pie. It was disgusting. I don't know if I can ever eat a surprise pot pie again. I can't imagine if a child had consumed the plastic. It was large enough for an adult to choke on. See pics 





I come to KFC for one thing and one thing  only....POT PIES!!!! I avoid fast food as much as I can but the pot pies here are the best think since sliced bread! 

My favorite part is the thick, flaky crust on top. It's so buttery, it's like eating a warmed croissant. The filling can't be overlooked since it's creamy, with the perfect amount of veggies and chicken. The seasoning is perfect. Every time I have a pot pie I feel like there's someone's granny in an apron making these from scratch! 

I can't leave out the staff who has always been pleasant in the drive through. If you're looking for a quick meal, that tastes homemethen you have to try the pot pies. You won't be disappointed.

I've been a Burger King fan since I was a kid. The food at this particular location is pretty standard but the customer service can rival the worst of them. I've been twice and both times I've had issues. This particular time they handed me an iced coffee with the lid partially off and it spilled all over my arm. I actually had to ask for napkins because the person at the window ignored the spill even though he definitely saw it happen. I did get an apology at the end but it was less apologetic and more of an excuse that the morning had been crazy.

I totally get it. I'm not upset, nor does this cause me any hindrance of going back. I'm just setting my expectations low any time I want to enjoy a Whopper!

Stop to get chicken tonight and they messed up my order. I got two separate things they forgot to put one of them in the bag. I should've looked but I didn't because the bag to handed me was so big I figured it was all in there. I got home realized I didn't put it in and when I returned explain to them what happened nobody said I'm sorry nobody said oh will take care of that nothing. So when I got to the window I said hey how much I'll throw in some extra chicken and they got an attitude with me. Worst service no accountability no apology customer service horrible.

Always one of our favorite places when in the mood for Mexican. Can always count on fresh ingredients and savory flavors. I typically go for the Sofritas bowl which is basically marinated tofu crumbles in a savory sauce. Pair that with some brown rice and grilled veggies. If you've never been to a Chipotle it's very similar to a Subway where instead of building your own sub, you build your own burrito or burrito bowl or salad or taco. They even have kids meals and even though not on the menu will do a quesadilla. They have queso now, which I'm really not a fan of .....it tastes like they put too much flour in it. Love their chips and guacamole!

Lousy service. When we were in the drive thru at 9:30 they told us they didn't have any fried chicken made, only grilled and popcorn chicken, which led me to believe that nothing they had was even fresh. Their reason was that they close at 10:00.

Well. I went to order chicken for 24 people the manager rolled her eyes and said it would take a while. I said that was fine. I ordered the food and ordered 2 16 pice meals and asked especially for a rotisserie chicken. She told me it would take an hour but it was  cool as it did not take nearly that long.  When I got ready to leave the manager told me  that by the way they were out of rotisserie chicken. I asked her for refund. Again with the Eye roll. Long story short, manager said to me, you're not my mother. I'm done with KFC. Love the chicken the service sucks.

Ordered mashed potatoes and gravy, coleslaw, 2 biscuits, and a bucket of dark meat chicken.  Received fries, 9 biscuits,  4 lbs total of mac and cheese, bucket of chicken, mostly white meat.  Not what I ordered so I am stuck with a $50 bill, most of which I cannot eat!  Ordered via door dash.  Total fail!

Avoid this location like the plague. I am forced to go here because it's the only one close to my house. They have yet again messed up my order. $30 fill up. Told the girl all original recipe chicken and sub out the cole slaw with Mac and cheese. Get home and it's half chicken, half chicken strips, no Mac and cheese and sure enough......cole slaw. 

Dear owner, your employees do not care and that's probably why you have a 2 star rating.

Horrible KFC... I love KFC/Popeyes but goodness it's as if they gave me chicken that they dropped on the floor it was disgusting !! And they drenched it in salt or something it didn't sit right in my palate. Idk what's wrong with these employees, they must hate their life because they project their sadness in life into the food

Tried this place for the first time today. I hoped it would be better than their atrocious Taco Bell food but nope! Their KFC is just as bad. The chicken was super dry and was fried in what looks like OLD oil. Biscuits were chewy like the dough was still raw. KFC/Taco Bell please get better :(

We missed you! It had been a long time since we got Chipotle due to the COVID-19 pandemic. I was craving a steak burrito so we decided it was time to take the risk. We ordered online and picked up our order. My husband said the process was pretty easy. He was in and out of the restaurant pretty fast because we paid online and our order was ready when he arrived. We ordered a steak burrito (pictured) and steak bowl (not pictured). We also ordered a chicken quesadilla for our 4 year old (half portion pictured). The food was how we remembered it. It was a little cold when we got home but nothing a few seconds in the microwave couldn't fix. My standards have been significantly lowered with the COVID-19 pandemic. I'm happy to just get everything that I ordered! Our only issue was that the chips included with the kid's meal weren't very fresh but it wasn't a big deal for our family.

Probably worst Chipotle i've been to. They're currently only doing online orders. I ordered the new chicken quesadilla and one if my sides were missing. I also ordered a steak bowl which came with 80% rice and probably half a portion of steak. I gave this location so many chances but the service is always lacking and the prepared food isn't up to par. Guac is always half mashed too.

UPDATE: just found a hair in the steak bowl

When I went to pick up my food I told them that I had ordered on the Dunkin Donuts app. The workers didn't check/scan my order on the app when I showed them it. They then said "We'll start making your order now." I was so confused cause the app had said that my order was ready. I then looked on the counter and saw that there was food on it with my name on it, it could have been my fault that I didn't check, BUT I would think that the workers would see the name and order number that was on the app when I showed them the phone and then tell me that my order is on the counter‍. After I saw that it was on the counter, I told the worker "Nvm, my names on the bag over here." The worker then gave me a mean attitude and said "If it says your name, TAKE IT." 
I ordered 2 bagels with sausage, bacon, and cheese, 1 thing of hash browns, a sprite, orange juice, and an ice coffee. When I looked at the hash browns I noticed that my hash browns were burnt and they only gave me two of them (they were tiny). I then tried one of the bagels and they didn't put any bacon on it (that I got charged for) and the sausage tasted like straight up chemicals! 
All in all the workers were rude and the food was terrible!!! 
Gave two stars because at least the drinks were good‍

Had a mobile order for pickup. I was the third car in the drive thru. I got to the speaker, said which order I was here to pickup, and was told to pull forward. First window; repeated I was here for my order and told to pull forward. Second window; told to move forward to the third (vacant) window. Watched the next five cars behind me order, pay, and receive their food. I tried to ask for help but the worker in the window blatantly ignored me every time. Eventually parked and went inside. Employees looked at me like I was stupid and said they're starting on it now. I asked how it was possible when I had stated I was here for the order 20 min ago. The worker told me that I never announced that I was there, which is a blatantly lie as I had already said I was there three times. Canceled my order and left. All I ordered was a fish slider meal. I've had pretty bad service throughout the years, but this may have genuinely been the worst. Never returning.

We heard about the Impossible Whopper so we had to go to Burger King to try it. Our food was great but the service left a little lacking. 

We ordered a medium drink, onion rings and two impossible whoppers, both with cheese and one without tomato. When we got home, both of our burgers had no cheese and they both had tomato. They did charge us $.79 each for cheese. The total price of our lunch was just over $18.00. Outrageous for fast food and not getting the order correct. 

Would I come back here?  I guess if I save up some money I will. I'll also go inside to order to make sure I get it my way.

Wings are fucking disgusting!  Hate to say it but I wouldn't mind seeing this place fold.  Employees told me that these are party wings and that I'm not going to die eating them.  KFC has gone downhill in the past 10 years.  I'm in shock that this is the quality of chicken they are serving.  It's honestly my fault for ordering wings here instead of the mechanically separated chicken.

Employees are fucking stoned at all times just eating in the back. Consistently out of queso, chips, steak and many others due to the lack of prep work before opening. This is the worst chipotle location.

This is a university location so it's really hard to find parking! Also the scoopers here are so stingy but the ingredients were on point. The steak was flavorful juicy and just the right amount of fatty! Good job grill person!

I love Burger King. My dad worked at this one as a teenager, he did not enjoy it though. I will say this: Burger King is waaaaaaaaay better than McDonald's by far; if you had the choice between the a McDonald's Big Mac and a Burger King Whopper, choose the Whopper. They offer one with bacon, it is FANTASTIC.

The 2 for $5 whopper special is awesome but when the lady asks you if you want cheese they charge you extra, an extra dollar. That's not cool the whopper already comes with cheese. She asked if you want cheese not extra cheese. Rude

Been to this Burger King about a hundred times since it opened but I see no need to ever go back. It was wednesday 10/5/2016. About 1:50 pm. Not too busy. Ordered a double whopper with cheese to go and she rang it up. I gave her a 5. She gave me change. 3 minutes later a guy pulls a tray and puts a whopper with cheese on it. He asks if it's mine.  I say nope, not mine. She says well that's what you ordered. I say no, I ordered a double whopper with cheese to go. She says well you only gave me enough for a single. I said no, I gave you a 5. She says no, you didn't. I said are we gonna fight ?  She said no. So then the guy says so you want this to eat here ? Ok   Enough.   I said forget it .   So I left.... I guess the customer is never right. And she's giving me attitude on top of it. Forget it.  I'm done. Didn't even ask for my money back. Lemme outta here.  Redic with a capital R.

How hard is it to get a Drive-In order correct?  Does the customer need to check their order BEFORE they drive away? I ordered a $5 meal and a chicken breast & wing meal.  Get all the way home and half the order was wrong.  I wound up with a thigh and leg instead of the breast and wing.  I checked my receipt.  It correctly shows what I ordered but it was NOT what I got. 

NEVER trust the drive-thru to get the order correct.

I wouldn't even give this place 1 star but I had no choice. Where do I begin. The lady taking my order was rude and didn't know anything about the menu. Then when I get my food they give me 3 sauces for a 5 piece tender meal and a full box of "popcorn chicken" when I asked for more he denied me. The place is garbage. Spent $30 on a box of crumbs instead of popcorn chicken. The food was over cooked and taste like dog turds.

Have not been to this location in years, clearly I can see why. How many employees does this place need, I counted at least 7 walking around aimlessly,  they all seem to high tail it to the dumpster for a smoke it seems like. Whatever, I had the whopper bacon burger. It was good, fries were terrible, dry & over cooked. Yu can tell place is run by young people, staff seems nice. May be I'll come back in a couple of years to see how this place unfolds.

It is time for me to come out of the closet and admit, I am a "Craver". There, I've said it and I feel so much better admitting that I like White Castle. I not only like their "belly bombers", I enjoy the Breakfast Sliders and their new Grilled Chicken. I even like the crinkle cut french fries. 

I am now excited that they are offering, My Size Meals for little cravers. If I can get away with ordering one with no child in sight, it will be the perfect size meal. I'll get to pick one from four entrees; an original slider, slider with cheese, savory chicken and 3 piece chicken rings. Then I can choose between two sides; Mott's apple sauce or kid size fries. As a beverage, I get to pick between Mott's 100% Apple Juice, Milk or a kid size soda. This will be perfect portion control for someone who is dieting. 

I have always favored this location due to its closeness to my work. It makes it so easy to grab a quick breakfast slider and coffee in the morning. At lunch time, it is just a couple blocks away, making it so easy to grab the new Savory Chicken sandwich with those crinkle cut fries. A friendly staff helps the belly bombers go down. 

Now that I've opened up and came out, I will be able to go to a White Castle near my home and hold my head up in pride.

I have eaten here a couple times now and both times I was disappointed.  I went here because they went organic so I was super excited.  The first time they just put too much in our burritos and they broke open.  You had to eat the burrito with a fork.   This happened again the second time I ate there.  I watched the server make a burrito and ask the guy if its okay that it broke open.  The customer said remake it.  I got my drink cup filled it with tea and there was a body hair (not mine)on the cup.  Then when I was eating my chicken burrito, there was a piece of steak and a piece of pork in it.  I was totally grossed out and couldn't eat any more.  There food bar has been questionable each time I have been in here.  They need to work on the cleanliness on their store as well as grooming habits of employees.

I have eaten at the  Milwaukee location and they made the perfect burritio!!! and their food bar was super clean.  Totally different atmospheres.

Chicken is almost always great. We have gotten it very dry once, but that was a one time occasion. Staff does seem almost confused by the menu when you order though and it usually takes a good 10-15 minutes even in the drive-thru. Food is good, so I keep going back.

Terrible customer service.  1) this is one of the few restaurants that still only serving drive thru and the inability to have at least a couple of tables open for dining/pick up inside is deserving of zero stars.  2) because they are only drive thru some people might have trouble reading the menu outside and apparently staff also have trouble listening to customers because I ordered fried chicken strips but they gave me 5 full pieces of chicken.  There is no excuse for this mistake or it is possible that they are just dishonest and wanted to charge more for the order.

Stopped to try one of their sloppy joe slider.  Worst sandwich ever.  Hard, stale bun.  Cold, dry sloppy joe meat and only about a tablespoon on the bun.  French fries cold and limp.  Only live around corner from this location so if actually hot when received would have been at least warm when o got home.
if you hate yourself, just go here. Everyone that work her capping bruh your better off eating a chicken nugget off the ground on the side of the road.
Great fried chicken needs and biscuits. Needs work on the potatoes. Didn't try the slaw because I hate slaw.

I always stop at this location between 6:30 - 7:00 am Mondays through Fridays. As a habitual guest, I'm well aware of the slow service offered when in the drive-thru (slow being ~10 minutes from start to finish). However, as of late, their service has been extremely slow. Sometimes, it can take an employee up to 6 minutes to acknowledge a car that is waiting to order. Afterwards, the line seems to move at a snail's pace. From the time I arrive to the time I receive my order, it's been taking upwards of 25 minutes. Today, for example, I ordered a large hot coffee with cream and sugar as well as a turkey sausage, egg, and cheese sandwich. After paying, I was directed to drive up to the 2nd window. I waited at the 2nd window for 20 minutes just to have an employee come out and ask me if I needed something...yes, yes I do. When I proceeded to ask for my refund, the sandwich magically appeared (cold). I'm not sure what has changed over the past few weeks but it's apparent that this location is lacking in efficiency, communication and detail-oriented related skills.

I walked in for a take-out because I prefer to take my time looking over the options on the bigger board.  As I was making up my mind the young man behind the counter was helping a couple with a child. She and the child were one order but the cashier had to ask for change to break a $20. He got it from a young woman. That took extra time. Then the man with them used coupon and the cashier had to ask for smaller bills again. The young woman came back with  the bills, taking even more time. Then it was my turn, I ordered and found a $10 for my $8.40 order. I figured he'd gotten change twice already but I wasn't going to risk it, so that's why I didn't give him a twenty. Well guess what? He didn't have any $1!!! So he had to ask the young woman for change a third time in four minutes. Really? Total incompetence and total lack of communication.  Horrible customer service because of all this. I will say they did get extra pickles on my whopper and my fries are fine but here's the cherry on the top of the review: 
When I went to fill my drink cup, of the 25 choices on their fancy new machine, 17 were dark, not dispensing,  out of beverages. 
Unacceptable.
I filled my cup with something wet and as I walked by I said;  "17 or 25 of you beverages are out." He didn't know and offered to fill my drink from the back.
I'd already wasted enough time and just walked out.

I just waited 30 minutes for chicken strips when I was told 6 and they would bring them to me..I had to go back to the drive thru and they didn't even get my order right...


30 minutes....for chicken strips... let that sink in..

This poor KFC needs a Lot of help!  They are slow! They are horrendous at handing out the correct order! The outside of the store looks old and dirty.   

The hubby really wanted the new chicken sandwich. I went thru the drive through and didn't check the order. Bad move. It was definitely not my order so I had to return and it as told it would be a half an hour at least... 

It was a long wait for a sandwich. 
The hubby did say it was ok - the chicken was moist - but the bread wasn't great. He said Popeyes still reigns.

I worked at KFC while in high school as well as a few other chicken joints, so I know fried chicken. 

Disgusting!!! GROSS!! Describes the inedible meal we had the misfortune to purchase from this KFC location. 

The cooking oil was rancid from being overused and the chicken tasted horrible. At the bottom of the bucket was about a third of a cup of blackened cooking oil. The flavor KFC is famous for was nowhere to be found. The coating itself had not properly adhered to the chicken and was loaded with the rancid oil. This is what happens when cooking oil is overused in fast food restaurants trying to extend the useful life of the oil. 

The KFC gravy is made from a powder added to water. Obviously, in an attempt to cut costs extra water had been added and the gravy had no flavor.  

Coleslaw was OK. Mashed potatoes, made from flakes, were OK as well, but absent a flavorful gravy, were not to be enjoyed.

The restaurant itself was nasty. Watching workers constantly touch the mouth area of their Covid-19 mask then touch food was also both alarming and disgusting. 

As a former KFC cook I can tell you that even on a good day, what is served now is not the same chicken being made when I was an employee. Numerous cost cutting efforts to increase profitability have destroyed the once great fried chicken experience. Same goes for Popeyes. Once the original creators of the brand were out of the picture the quality disappeared. 

Being from New Orleans I knew Al Copeland the founder of Popeyes. He's passed now, and so is the quality of the recipes that were so incredibly delicious that a little bit of New Orleans ended up in nearly every neighborhood in America. The same now goes for KFC. Like those KFC imposters of the Colonel on TV, Kentucky Fried Chicken is an imposter of the original as well. 

Unless you wish to be abused and you enjoy throwing away thirty bucks for eight pieces of rancid chicken, pass this KFC by, and find a Raisin' Cain's.

Terrible attitudes and service was received today through the drive up window. Young lady and young man were very rude and we waited a long time for our food. Buns were stale, chicken was dry and chewy. There was no "sorry about your wait," or "thank you," just "here you go!" Don't waste your time at this KFC.

I stand by my first review - this is a chain store with many weak links....and an update:
Flunkin' the Dunkin' (Donuts Test)

1. Convenience - got it, especially within walking distance 

2. Polite, congenial staff - got it 

3. Efficiency - oops, let's move onto another category; see below 

4. Quality - oops, moving right past this one too; details below  

5. Value - fair, but only in the broadest sense of the word

I used to live near a fantastic, family-owned, high quality donut shop in the San Fernando Valley. So, moving here, I still entertain visions of that now ... far-away ... sorely missed ... Shangri La.

In a nutshell: walked in this morning with grandson to order several menu items to go for our family. Only one person ahead of us gave us the mistaken idea this would be a quick visit. Wrong. To wit: the cashier listened patiently and politely while my grandson placed our order; then gave credit to my account; then proceeded to assist the next customer in line; then moved to the prep area to help out with the backed-up drive through attendant who was working intently on filling a host of orders. After watching these goings-on for a few minutes, my grandson whispered...."there's our order".... finally being prepared. Okay, I thought, at least it will be fresh and hot. (And, it was...) A lightly toasted bagel with egg and bacon, for my sidekick, Dunkin's relatively new savory "Almost Sausage Sandwich" consisting of egg and cheese on an English muffin with a big sage-oregano-reminiscent fake meat patty that hit my taste buds just right. Yet as enjoyable as it was, I likely will stick with real meat in the future.

The big disappointment was in the donuts. Nothing has changed except the coloring of the glazes and a hint of flavoring as the seasons change. Soft and chewy blobs of dough with a drizzle of colored icing and a pathetic fake-fruit filled spritz of disappointment inside. ... and that is done with specious skill.

But the really disappointment of our visit was the Apple Fritter -- which is now a complete sham. Formally dotted inside with bits and pieces and chunks of real apple, it now is a large mound of fried dough that has a squirt of (apparently) of apple-pie filling. #falseAdvertising #misleading #disappointing lead the hashtags of this mis-named item. And the rest of the donuts fell in line with this let-down.

So, while we may return here for a breakfast sandwich on the run - those were fine...we'll be going elsewhere for real donuts and made-as-they-should-be fruit-filled fritters. 

Sorry Dunkin' you are now flunkin' the quality test on many levels with us.
Ordered 4 stripes of chicken tenders combo and only got 3. So mad... Do not deserve a one star!!

I love it ! Even though some of the workers here look mean when you walk in they still welcome you in and treat you right. Specially the lady, she seems serious but she's really sweet. She knows how to treat a customer right. Always offering things for your chicken. 
HIGHLY RECOMMEND

Judge me as you will, but sometimes, this fat kid needs fast food comfort food which is code for "ONION RINGS FEED ME ONION RINGS ZOMG!" 

Burger King satisfies me on several levels. 
First, deliciously creepy mascot. 
Second, onion rings. 
Third, long-time support of veg*ns eating crappy veg*n food. 

When I stopped by for onion rings and a Veggie Whopper today, the restaurant wasn't particularly busy so I can't blame the shortcomings on being rushed or crowds. Nope, just sheer incompetence. Short and sweet, here were the problems...

One: I ordered onion rings and was given fries. Oops.
Two: I ordered "eat in" and was given "to go." Oops. 
Three: I ordered a Veggie Whopper three times before the manager finally understood that I wanted a Veggie Whopper. Oops. 

My food was actually made fresh, just for me, which is a perk. But I don't really want to do the picky picky dance every time I want some onion rings in under 10 minutes. 

Accessibility notes: Doors open out. Plenty of space for accessibility devices. No braille menu or signage. Can get loud at peak hours for hard of hearing customers.

I rarely go to the Burger King in Golden Valley as every time I have ordered the Whopper Jr it is cold....and that's the sandwich I like....so usually I go just for the ice cream cone....a couple of days ago I ordered the cone for the low price of  $.92 the girl drops a nickel of my change and laughs....and then says I'm sorry....I replied with there is a nickel out here if you'd like to come get it....she stops laughing and looks at me like I said something horrible. I don't understand customer service anymore.

Now I have LOVED KFC original recipe my whole life & for past 5 months we have been without a kitchen due to a disaster... that being said we have eaten at KFC multiple times but tonight it was SO SO BAD ,, the chicken looked old , overcooked, tasted awful and I dont think 1/2 of it was even original recipe but you couldn't tell bc it tasted so OLD & bad ... like days old... AWFUL!
Bathroom was out of butt gaskets & hand soap. Desperate need of cleaning was required. Ordered double Texas whopper was just ok, prob won't be getting it again.

Wasted $6 on impossible whoppers cuz they ran out of bbq sauce who the fuck runs out of barbecue sauce and she claims they didn't have it in the bottles either she's lying I bet because her attitude was so fucking foul how could I believe her ?
Order was ready when I arrived and was correct. Lady at drive through was very friendly and food was very good and hot when I got home! Chicken was great!

Chicken was dry, Mac and cheese seemed microwaved because it was like lava in the center and cold around the edges. Employees were talking about how interview questions were illegal and what questions they were, and all the while I kept thinking how inappropriate the conversation they were having was lol. Well not be returning
In my opinion I thought the chicken was ratherSmall pieces and to greasy for my taste. May give it one more try in the future.

I moved to this side of the river over a year ago. I felt guilty that I hadn't been to this WC, since my move. I blame that on the pandemic. However, I have corrected that and I am pleased to say, that they are still one of the better White Castles in the metro area. 

The night of my visit, I choose to use the drive-thru. The line wasn't, too long and it was late. So I thought it was my best choice. To my surprise, the line went very quick and smooth. I might have to consider using it again in the future. 

Lucky for me, they were still offering the, 1921 Slider in celebration of their 100 Years. I just gone one, so I could say I had it. It was just a typical cheese slider with tomato and lettuce. The tomato and lettuce did give it an extra boost of flavor. Glad I had one, I am loyal to my usual order. 

Did you ask what my usual order is? Well, it is Combo #2 which is two DoubleCheese Sliders and a side of fries. I don't always add one, but tonight a side of crispy onion rings, hit the spot. 

For a fast food staff, the staff here has always been consistently friendly and professional. A few of them are still here from when I started stopping by when I commuted to work from STL. 

If you're a Slider fan, I recommend you check this one out. 

For those who are curious. Yes, I did have a buzz on when I stopped to get my slider fix.

On the run. Hungry. Ordered a take-out burrito from Chipotle Mexican Grill. Picked it up. It was huuuuge!!! Possibly because I kind of packed it on...steak, brown rice, tomato salsa and black beans. Might be missing one ingredient. Can't recall. It was still plenty hot when I got it and the flavors were great together. Did I mention how big it was? It was. BIG. Tasty. And very filling. Yes, I liked it.

We never eat from Burger King Normally, but Everything was closed and I was starving!
I ordered a few single whoppers and specifically said no onions on the one whopper, I'm allergic
We open it up and surprise onions on both whoppers!
I call and speak to a Manager, Yvette and she asks can we come back? Drive back after we've just gotten home? Nope so she just gave a credit for a future date
I ate the fries that night
Last night, I said let's cash in the free whopper before they forget and throw our info away.
Again a whopper with no onions! This time I did it through the BK app.
Behold, I open the bag and guess what? A whopper with onions again. I call again and contact the app and what do they do? They refunded me $1.07 for the mistake
Avoid this place at all costs!! Slow service, drive thru always backed up even though the line at Starbucks next door is so much quicker, and they can't get a simple order completed correctly on a repeated basis

I took my mom here. We both got Whopper JRs one with extra onion, pickles & mayo. It only had lite mayo. The other had extra mayo & pickles; again only lite mayo. The lobby was clean but the tables were dirty. The service was good. The food wasn't the best or the worst.

About a year or 2 ago they were pretty good but just yesterday I ordered through the phone application a steak bowl for 13$ nothing too crazy but they gave me my order with no meat I'm not much of a complainer so I ate it, it didn't taste bad but if I paid for something I would like to get it & on top of that there were small pieces of plastic in my bowl, I'm tired of this business letting me down last time before that I went it wasn't a great experience either my food was cold still tasted good but a long time ago it use to be really good I miss the old chipotle .

When this place opened they had good service and food. Now this place is one of the worst fast food restaurants. The service is HORRIBLE I felt like I was bothering them. The sprite was seltzer water yuck, tea had no ice. Can't even get the drinks right. They have no chicken or big whopper meat smh. When we got our food it was cold.  Where is the management? Will NOT be back!

Terrible experience - would never go back.  The staff are totally overwhelmed in there and make so many mistakes.  I just waited for about 45 minutes for an eight piece bucket - I had to repeat the type of chicken pieces I wanted to the cashier no less than 5 times for her to get it right and then I had to repeat it to the lady filling the bucket because she was putting in the wrong pieces.  She was called out for not changing her gloves which she used to handle money and then handled food with.  The Taco Bell worker was smoking or vaping in the back while filling orders.  A customer on line called her out on that and demanded a full refund before getting her food.  While I sat and waited for my order, there were no less than 4 customers returning orders that were wrong.  
The place is a disaster and really should be fully revamped or shutdown.  As well, the Board of Health should check it out because for customers to be able to pick out violations so easily, God only knows what was happening that we couldn't see.

Oh Colonel Sanders, what happened to you. You were always my go to date whenever my husband is out of town. I fear however, that last night was our final rendezvous. You really let my taste buds down. For those of you going through the drive thru be prepared to wait a minimum of fifteen minutes after placing your order. 

I got the two piece meal with breast and wing and cole slaw. When I got to the window, I asked if the cole slaw was in there and was told it only comes with mashed potatoes. I thought sure that sign said one side. Oh well, I'll take the mashed potatoes. 

When I got home, I saw that I was given a breast and leg. I hate legs. What I was really disappointed in was that the chicken did not taste like Kentucky Fried. It tasted like regular fried chicken that you would get anywhere like Schnuck's. I was so disappointed. One thing I did like was instead of a soda, I was given the option of a bottled water. 

Would I come back here?  Sadly, the Colonel and I will have to break up and I will never see him again. Goodbye Colonel. I had a great infatuation with you.

I've come back to this KFC location a few times hoping for a better experience, but end up disappointed every time. They recently painted the building, which looks nice, but the outside still has a strong spoiled food smell noticeable from the drive thru line, the menu board is dirty and frail looking, and a glance inside the takeout window shows a grimy looking kitchen space that ultimately makes me question the cleanliness. 

Outside of that, I hope for some decent food. I got the 3pc chicken strip box and was excited tor the fries. They were incredibly dried out and tasted like they were made multiple hours prior and stuck under a lamp for way too long. The chicken has been way over cooked each time and dry. And despite a lengthy back and forth about how they are out of butter and grape jelly, the employee forgot to include my biscuit in the meal. I want to be understanding that fast food in particular is short staffed during this time, but I'm not sure full staffing would change much. The place needs an overhaul from head to toe.

This location is the worst Burger King I've ever been to. I've been here several times with a mobile order and it always takes 20+ minutes for my food, a single whopper! What's the point of mobile ordering if I can't pick it up and go? I ordered at 11:50 AM and got my food at 12:15 PM.

I mention that I'm here for a mobile order, give the cashier my order number, 24, and she says she doesn't have it. Hello? I see my whopper sitting right there in the window. I ask if she can just check the whopper real quick and she tells me I have to wait. She slowly walks back and forth checking the register and the mounted order screen multiple times, trying to look busy.

The line staff member comes by to check. I mention my order number again, she says I have to reorder it and that it didn't come through. I have the order number on my phone, in my hand! The cashier already called out order 23 and 25. Where's 24? She taps my phone with her mayo covered gloves and says she'll remake it.

The cashier finally checks the whopper sitting in the window, and guess what? 24 IS WRITTEN ON THE WRAPPER. They still didn't give it to me! I see the person on the line push out a new whopper to the window and I wait some more for the remade whopper to be bagged.

The cashier continued to ignore me. After a while, the line member herself, came over and bagged the original first whopper that was sitting there for 20 minutes, not the remade one, and handed it to me. Seriously? Why couldn't they just hand it to me in the first place, earlier?

Like I said, this was not a one time experience. This place really needs better staff. The cashier is always rude and has an indifferent attitude. And, it takes forever to get your food! You're better off going to the location on East Patriot Blvd, instead of coming here.

I've always gone to this location for two reasons. They don't water down their gravy and it's close to my place. Well today was the last straw. Not only do they always say they can't give me all dark meat they NEVER have chicken cooked. My average wait time for food here at this location is 40 min. But today not only did they not have chicken. The gravy was watery and had clumps in it. I thought this location might have potential to be a good location but I see that it has fallen to the typical KFC let down. Oh yea...when I get a bucket I'm usually short a piece or two.

My husband and I enjoyed a multi-course dinner at the Château Blanc for Valentine's day. It was nothing less than exquisite. 

We arrived ravished for our 6:30 reservation. The host was welcoming and allowed us to choose the best table in the restaurant, a secluded corner booth. The table was candlelit, and tasteful decor throughout the restaurant gave off a whimsical yet regal aesthetic. The restaurant was quiet, with just the faint sounds from the kitchen in the distant background.

The waitress arrived at our table promptly to take our beverage orders, and delivered them within 60 seconds. She gave us a moment to enjoy our drinks before coming back to allow us to settle in. My diet coke was flawless. My husband said that his ice water had just the perfect amount of ice in it. 

For our appetizer course, we ordered the Mac & Cheese Nibblers and the Chicken Rings. They arrived quickly, and fresh out of the kitchen. The Mac & Cheese Nibblers were savory and the perfect appetizer to begin with. The Chicken Rings were delightful and were the ideal palate cleanser for our main course. 

The waitress paced our meal perfectly, and our main entrees arrived shortly after we finished our appetizers. An amazing and indescribable aroma swept our table as soon as our food was set down. It is apparent that the chef of the Château respects the craft and pours his soul into his dishes. The Original Slider was mouth-watering and tender, the fries were lively and flavorsome, and the Impossible Slider was impossibly divine. 

Our wonderful waitress, who was present yet not overbearing, was unable to tempt us with dessert as we wanted to keep the taste of perfection in our mouths as long as possible. 

I recommend this restaurant to anyone that strives to experience culinary greatness. Thank you, White Castle.

Horrible location. Get people bothering you soliciting inside store so you can't even eat in peace. Also wasn't impressed to order chicken strips to get what looked to me like they were run over before placing them in my box. I'm wondering where the rest of my meat went and can I get it back.

This store did me dirty. I ordered double carnitas, double cheese on a mobile order. I picked it up and took it back to my apartment to the disappointment of a bowl with only rice and beans and a literally 2 bites of cheese. I generally love chipotle. Disappointing to say the least.

This particular location is so painfully slow.....always out of chicken...seriously???? Not only you had to put up with the slow drive thru service they always give you a piece or two short of chicken...since we usually buy the buckets we won't know if we're getting the right amount of chicken as per our order and what we paid for until we get home...this happened twice already..even though this location is conveniently close to our house we would rather go to Callingwood location with no Drive Thru or the one in 178th street...

Ive ordered here 3X in the past 3 months. Twice from door dash and once for pickup myself. The last 2 times have been below average experiences with food. I order a double-steak salad and there's barely any lettuce. It just surprises me that will all the toppings, the bowl is maybe half filled even though it's a salad. It's almost as if they're so rushed they don't put any patience or care into creating the entrees. I will be back but not for awhile.
Consistently nice staff and food.  Great for big groups.  Never messes up orders even with a large order.  Food is basic KFC so if you like fast food KFC chicken, this is a better franchise

I have to give these creeps one star? I guess one star means they exist. I went and ordered my #1 with Coke at the drive thru. Well it must be prankster day because they gave me a Dr. Pepper and there was a lot of pepper on my Whopper. Not cool. This is my lunch splurge and I just spent eight bucks on a prank? Obviously there are nothing but children working here. You have lost my business.
I love getting the Beyond Sausage Breakfast sandwich here, all day, any day! I like that this place actually offered dairy free option for my iced coffee.

This particular Chipotle is the worst in Philadelphia,I've been here multiple times already the tables are filthy always and at 630pm when closing at 10 pm don't have rice and steak !Always something with this location it's def my last time coming here !Management def has to step and do something about this business,obviously I'm not the only one with the same complaints! It's not worth the one star

My steak burrito bowl was nothing by but grizzle! So discussing to bite into a hard rubbery piece of grizzle. On top of this my other half ordered the same thing not only was his grizzle free his was a full bowl mine was half the normal size with most of the steak being grizzle. Totally disgusted with the quality of this location.  I would love to post a picture but it's too gross to post chewed grizzle but maybe I'll send it to the head office and let them get grossed out.

I rarely review airport restaurants but had a super experience and wanted to highlight. 

I started off in the Starbucks line as usual at any airport in the morning. After 10 minutes in a long line that was not moving I jumped out into a nearby Dunkin. 

Approximately 15 was in line and it was moving quickly! When it was my turn I encountered a friendly cashier that was extremely fast at moving everyone along. Ordered two sausage, egg and cheese on croissants. My drink a vanilla ice coffee with cream and sugar. Total time in line 5-7 minutes. They only accept cash tips. 

My coffee was up first in minutes, then sandwiches a few minutes later. It was amazing to witness the efficiency of their operation. The sandwiches were hot and tasty. My coffee was good, but had too much sugar as it was not mixed. After a bit of twirling much better. 

Based on this single experience I will work Dunkin in more often when traveling.
Service was okay...not the best. I asked for the steak but was given chicken. Chips were stale and over all wasn't impressed or going to be back. Other than that it was good

So went to this location just a bit ago literally took 30 minutes in the drive thru with maybe 7 cars ahead of us.  We got our over priced over cooked food with no napkins or silver Ware and no sorry for the wait.  They were out of any diet soda so getting a meal kinda sucks but I got the chicken sandwich box with fries and macaroni and added a biscuit.  The sandwich was like shoe leather the bun was stale and hard the Mac and cheese had pasta like mush and biscuit with out gravy on it taste like it's brushed with some cleaning solution.  This will be my last visit to any kfc the food is trash and so is their service.

Don't bother. This KFC in particular sucks. Every time I've gone, the chicken is so over cooked that it tastes like rubber and cardboard. They drench the spicy chicken sandwich in sauce so that's all you can taste. I'd rather drive down to Oxnard to go to Popeyes.

always forgets something how you going to give me drinks, fries, napkins and zesty sauce... where is my whopper??

Just make sure you look in the bag before you go home, I ended up eating a ham sandwich with zesty sauce, fries and a diet soda. 


wow at least they remembered the diet...

We were returning home from a Covid "essential service" and were hungry for dinner, but all that appeared open was this KFC. Looks like takeaway food for us.

There wasn't any Extra Crispy to be had, so we settled.on my less than favorite Original Recipe.Of what was available,we ordered a bucket meal. My pieces of chicken were more tepid than piping hot, but  they weren't hard at all. My drumsticks were especially on the tender side. The mashed [potatoes and gravy my not have been the greatest, but I gobbled them down. My wife ate all their slaw and loved it.

Now to rate this KFC. First, there were laboring under a Covid disability. So, instead of Extra Crispy chicken, they only had Original Recipe for sale. The outside our the chicken lacked any semblance of fry. On the other hand, both buckets were filled as advertised.This KFC chicken filled up two hungry folks at dinner time. Three stars for them

I tend to visit a Dunkin' Donuts in the Atlanta area occasionally so I am familiar with them.  I have to say the staff working at this one are extremely friendly and helpful compared to my experiences elsewhere.  It was a difficult time for them in that they are working with different rules and menu due to restrictions with the coranvirus situation.  They were so helpful and apologetic with any changes that had to be made.

First we found out prior to entering through the doors that there would be no seating inside due to new restrictions for the restaurant.  No problem.  We went inside and each ordered a breakfast sandwich and a coffee.  We all ordered different things and our coffees were all different (and I had something omitted on my breakfast sandwich).  The entire order was read back to us and then they went to work.  

They executed the order perfectly and we all took a table outside to enjoy our breakfast (we had traveled down to FL for a funeral and were getting breakfast before traveling back home).  Everything was done just as we had ordered and we had full bellies at the end.  My veggie sandwich on a type of grain flatbread was very good (I omitted the sausage patty).  My husband had the same but with the sausage.  My son and daughter both had a chicken biscuit sandwich that they both enjoyed.  I have never ordered breakfast sandwiches at Dunkin' Donuts; only coffee.  I was glad they had a mostly veggie option.  I went back in to use the restrooms only to find out they were closed.  Not their fault.  We had to get gas anyways so knew we could find some elsewhere (which turned out to be across the street).

Overall, I recommend this particular Dunkin' Donuts.  We would definitely return.  The staff was great as was the food and coffee.

It's our neighborhood KFC. The drive-thru service at this location is actually quite good. I've never had to wait too long, and they usually get my order right. 

Today, I decided to try their Crispy Colonel Sandwich. I asked for no mayo, because the one thing that grosses me out the most at fast-food restaurants is when they put that big old glob of mayo right in the middle of the sandwich. I didn't want that to happen to me today. Thank goodness it didn't. The sandwich wasn't as big or as juicy as the Popeye's chicken sandwich. But, I do have to say that I actually liked it. The best thing I can say is that the chicken didn't taste like processed chicken. The extra-crispy coating is just as I remember it. It had pickles, and the bun held it all together. Now as you know, I didn't get the mayo so that may cause the sogginess that I've read about in other reviews. I didn't get any sauces either, and I'm kind of glad I didn't. 

I used to love KFC back in the day. This is one of the many restaurant chains that took a big hit when they were forced to remove trans fat from their original recipes. They recovered, and somehow survived after all these years. I just wish they would make better side items. I can't think of one side item that I like from KFC.

My husband and I went through the drive thru. We ordered a whopper, four cheese burgers, and four orders of nuggets. We got to the window to pay. A black woman, named Tina, who claims she is the manager, is worthless. She doesn't know how to smile for one, As we drove away we double checked our order and saw we were missing three cheese burgers. So we pulled around, in front of the establishment to go inside to have them fix our order, but the door was locked. And no one was paying any attention. So we proceeded to go through the drive thru again. After waiting in the drive thru line (the second time)  for 35 minutes, with our now cold food, we got to the window again, and explained to Tina we were missing three cheeseburgers and that NOW the rest of our food is cold. We wanted fresh, hot food, and the complete rest of our order. Tina replied with its our problem, not hers. And said it wasn't "their" fault we didn't try to come inside to get it fixed. I told her that we tried to open the front door but the door was locked. (Mind you, 7 pm this incident occurred) why was the front door locked to begin with? Tina said "if I would have heard you at the front, we would have opened the door for you".. well she continued on with her rude behavior. She then responds with "well we can either give you your money back or you can wait and we will make your three cheeseburgers." My husband says "we either want you to make our entire order over, the correct way, with hot food, or we will take our money. She proceeded to slam the window shut on us and get another manager named Charles to help her get the money for us. We left with no dinner and really disappointed. This woman is NOT management material and needs to be fired. She has zero concept of what customer service is all about. Tina, if you hate your job THAT MUCH, maybe you shouldn't work at Burger King. Or maybe, instead of dealing with the customers (because you obviously don't know how) you should make the orders and stay hidden away from the public. You're a rude, inconsiderate, pathetic waste of a "so called manager" I hope you get what you deserve.

I'm not the biggest fan of KFC because I've had so many rotten experiences. But fried...chicken.
