I tried to get a double down here awhile back. I failed. Well, lesson learned don't make the mistake I made. This location does not participate in national promotions. So no double down...you have to drive to Ventura for that.

The rest is ok. The people who work here are very nice, even apologetic about the lack of epicness of the franchise. Decent KFC, but that doesn't say much.

Ordered for the first time ever on Grubhub.. They didn't have sweet tea so I had to get something else..
Once I got my food my Mac & cheese was not there...
After taking a bite of the mashed potatoes that was the worst bite I've ever tasted... I'm not sure if it was rotten or old but it taste terrible..
I'm so sad it's my only day off I just wanted to eat my favorite food and relax but all I got to eat was the chicken and that wasn't even that hot.. I'm soo disappointed I will not be eating here again maybe Chick-fil-A or Wendy's next time.

If you want total chaos with your chicken along with a 95% chance of your order being incorrect, then this is your place. Workers yelling at each other. Not the first time I've been here and almost every time there has ben something wrong with the order. I have previously sent complaints to the email listed on listed on the receipt and still waiting for a response. This time I ordered a side of potatoes and gravy only there was no potatoes, only gravy. Cost more to return to the restaurant. Save yourself a trip.

It's the simple things...

...that this Burger King can't even do correctly such as:

1.  Providing an appropriate ambient temperature:  Immediately after walking into the restaurant, we were blasted in the face with 90+ degree air.  One of the customers said to an employee, "While y'all are cooking back there, we're cooking out here."  The employee barked back that they have no control over the temperature.  (However, I did noticed an thermostat with controls on the wall next to our seat.)

2.  Taking an order:  In the middle of placing my order, the same employee ignored what I was saying to dutifully check the caller ID on her cell phone that was ringing (not silent), thereby making me repeat myself.  After placing my order, I noticed that she took the time to answer a second call on her cell phone while still standing at the register.

3.  Making a Whopper:  As I unwrapped my whopper , I noticed it was a little lopsided.  I removed the top bun to discover that the burger pattie was FOLDED IN HALF with a slice of cheese resting on top of it.  WHAT!?!??!  How many of these burgers do they make a day that they still can't even put a pattie on straight?  Whoever made my burger had to have seen the folded pattie because he/she put the all the other ingredients on top of it.

Wow!  Just wow!

This place has gone to hell. When the employees can play video games in the lobby . While I had to wait to on my food for 38 minutes I had to finally go in and ask where my food was. So I had plenty of time to watch .They actually brought in their own tv and was Playing games. So after waiting I got oily old chicken which was supposed to be hot chicken cuz they were out they claimed. Mac n cheese was old. Which she said it would be 20 minutes wait on chicken. And I have waited 20 minutes. Before and got freash chicken. But let see today it was 38 minutes cuz video games are more important than their jobs and feeding little kids waiting in the car for dinner on a cold snowy day. Really . That whole crew needs to be fired. 
Really.

Irritation at its best when my food arrived.  I ordered a carnitas bowl with cauliflower rice, fajita veggies, sour cream, mild tomato and medium green salSa along with queso blanco and shredded cheese. Ordered for pickup.  Let me remind you that cauliflower rice is an additional $2 and queso is an additional $1.30!!  When I arrived back at the office and opened my meal I had mixed emotions--irritated, grossed out and disappointed. I had very little time to grab lunch and get back to scarf it down since I was running to meetings so to find this mess was more disappointing than anything. 

Look at this bowl!? Is this really representative of an almost $12 meal!? Wow. It was more like a soup -- where is the fajita veggies and the rice. I don't think I really tasted the queso either.  I swear this locations standards have lowered.  Not sure I'll be ordering for a while. Yuck!
No impossible burgers here for Vegans.  I read they were supposed to be nation wide,  guess this area doesn't have it yet

What kind of KFC routinely runs out of chicken? This one does.

Actually the worst ran KFC/taco bell I've ever witnessed. The last two times I've been here the KFC had no chicken. Seriously. 

Once you learn that the KFC has no chicken, it's impossible to leave the drive through because you're blocked in. You have to wait for them to serve everyone in front of you, or order something you didnt want, from Taco Bell.

I waited 20 minutes for 3 people to be served, just to be able to leave the drive-through prison.

Do yourself a favor and stay far away from this place.

1 star is way more than I would give them. Ordered from DoorDash tonight, a 60 dollar order for my family, we did not receive ONE thing that we ordered. They gave us a bunch of old chicken strips, popcorn nuggets & chicken littles. Probably not even half the worth of what we ordered. Everything was cold & gross. I don't understand how you can possibly get every single thing wrong. I work in food service, that's almost HARD to do.

First off the menu board at the drive up is way too far back from the speaker.  Why they changed that is what everyone wants to know,  It's beyond stupid and totally inconvenient.  The food has really gone downhill.  The fries were so overcooked they were hard as a rock and the insides were barely there.  The cheese whopper was a joke, the cheese was the size of a dime, and the whole thing was so bad my husband threw half of it away.  Needless to say the fries went into the garbage as well, the dog wouldn't even eat them or the 'meat' and I use the term loosely!  If we ever go back there, which I doubt, we will check the food before we drive off.  The boy working the drive up didn't have a clue!

Wanted Hardee's for breakfast and drove to the Wesley Chapel location. It is now closed. We decided to take a trip up to Zephyrhills to get our breakfast and we were not disappointed! To be honest, you don't usually expect much from a fast food breakfast but this Hardee's knocked it out of the park! We got a chicken and waffle sandwich and that was the star of the show. Everything was fresh and tasty. 

We will definitely put this in our rotation for fast food breakfast. Great location!

If you go through the drive thru, they will tell you to pull up past the window to wait. That is IF they will even take your order at all.. Many times they will ignore you all together at the drive thru until you give up and just leave. The staff always seems to be in a bad mood. If you are lucky enough to get what you actually ordered, the food isn't worth the money. The whopper has way too much mayo and the fries have no salt, no taste. I've given this place chances to change but it has not for many years. I actually downloaded Yelp just to warn people about this Burger King location. Stay away! This may be the worst fast food restaurant I have ever been to, and I am done giving it chances to change. Please do not give this location any more money.

Always have issues here. Never have products ready. Went at 4 pm and said had no chicken sandwiches (spicy or reg)  or wings thawed out and not ready for 30 mins! Really? At dinner time how do u not keep products ready for customers? Still waited over 20 mins just to get tenders and chicken minis. Only thing they had available. Not good.

Waited in drive-thru about 45 minutes (maybe 6 cars in front of me, not sure what took so long), then my turn to order.  The menu showed a $20 fill up (8 pieces of chicken, 2 sides, 4 biscuits.  I said "I would like the $20 fill up"; I ordered nothing else.  Was told at the window it would $26.49.  I explained that the menu said $20.  The lady said "I don't know what's on that menu, but it rang up $26.49." I argued and asked for a manager.  She said she was the manager.  She refused to fix the price.  I asked for a phone number to a district mgr or corporate office.  She just closed her window.  No customer service skills.  And no accepting of any responsibility to correct a blatant problem.  If they don't give any regard to how they treat their customers, think of what they might be doing to the food!

I come to KFC for one thing and one thing only....POT PIES!!!! I avoid fast food as much as I can but the pot pies here are the best think since sliced bread! 

My favorite part is the thick, flaky crust on top. It's so buttery, it's like eating a warmed croissant. The filling can't be overlooked since it's creamy, with the perfect amount of veggies and chicken. The seasoning is perfect. Every time I have a pot pie I feel like there's someone's granny in an apron making these from scratch! 

I can't leave out the staff who has always been pleasant in the drive through. If you're looking for a quick meal, that tastes homemade, then you have to try the pot pies. You won't be disappointed.

Dirty and unkempt are the two words that resonate in my mind when I think of this KFC. It's a shame. 

When I eat fast food, I typically lower my expectations as I know I'm not in for a first-rate experience. However, even this location leaves much to be desired. It's consistently dirty in the dining room, not always well-lit, and I've seen staff who don't wear gloves when handing money and then food, and look as though they could benefit from a shower. Not what I want to see when I'm ordering food, regardless of the price. 

The fare is decent at best. The last (and likely final) time I dined here, I was served undercooked chicken and mashed potatoes that tasted days old. I didn't get very sick, but had to discard half my meal which I normally never would do. It was just that atrocious. 

There is another KFC location in Goleta which is quite a bit better than this spot. I'd suggest you make the 10 mile trek and dine there the next time you're in the mood for fast food fried chicken.

The owner is the biggest a-hole ever! He gave me so much attitude because my internet for Apple Pay was being slow and the employee had to tell him to chill and he intentionally left my fries out and the employee had to apologize and give me a box of fries after I spent $60 on my family. DON'T EVER COME HERE. The owner is cheap and doesn't care about his customers. Mary browns is a street away, get your chicken there. He doesn't know how to handle a business.

Came in tonight to pick up food for my family. Tried ordering via phone but kept getting "this number is no longer in service ". Ordered my husbands steak bowl online but couldn't order my daughter and my kids quesadilla meals be because online doesn't allow to make additional choices or add notes to order which would make it more convenient. The order pass was filthy so one of the staff members was wiping it down. The restaurant was actually empty so I thought I'd be out quick but had to wait at the register for the cashier to return. When getting drinks there was a sign that they were out of Coke and Diet Coke so that was just icing on the cake. Not to mention that my bag of chips hardly had any in there. Yet again disappointed with service. Will continue to eat Chipotle because I love it although I get frustrated quite a bit.

Decided to come to KFC for the first time in like 10 years after seeing a commercial for their new chicken and donuts. The store was closed early (by at least 45 minutes) -- locked with the employees inside closing I guess. Several others have come (and now gone) while I type this as well. I got here at 9:18pm. The website says they're open til 10pm. The door says 9:30pm. Either way, they're not open when they're supposed to be. Definitely not wasting my time coming back.

Edit: Went to Chik-Fil-A instead. Wayyy better! And they're actually open right now, 10 minutes before closing. And there's at least 10-15 cars in line plus several people in the store. Seems like people will actually do you your business if you are actually open when you publicize you are. Maybe KFC should try it.

The KFCs in Boise are terrible.  They get the order wrong more than half the time.  They almost never have enough extra crispy chicken made to fill up a 12 piece bucket.  Often I have to wait 20 minutes or more to get some.  Tonight it was over 1 1/2 hours till closing and the manager told me they weren't planning to make any more chicken tonight, so I couldn't even wait for extra crispy.  She said that she could give me half extra and half the other, when I got home 4 out of 12 pieces were extra crispy, so her definition of half is suspect.  I'd already given up on the other locations in the area and now it's clear that this is a management problem with the company that owns all the KFCs in the area.  Never again.

I don't expect much from Burger King, but this one has some issues.

Will start with the good.  You get what you expect at BK on the food side.  I love me a good Whopper and rings.  Their chicken sandwich is really good too.  This location is recently built so the decor is newer.  Parking and entrance/exit are all good.  Not too far from I-75 and next to gas stations if needed.

The bad: the employees at this location don't care.  Walked in and stood at the register for too long.  No one said anything.  Started wondering if the inside was open when the acting manager finally came over to take the order.  Barely said anything.  My food sat in the outbin for a long time with the manager being the only one putting food in the bags.  He finally finished taking an order and brought me my burger.  I said very nicely "thank you very much".  He says NOTHING.  And if the manager doesn't care to be there or be the least bit cordial, I wouldn't want to think how the employees he manages are.  And why couldn't one of the several employees put the awaiting food in bags and on trays so people could have warm food and be on their merry way.

Just not a good experience...

It must be hard and complicated to order a ch'king meal (with fries, sandwich and drink), whopper, a bacon cheese burger, and a few fried things.
 When I arrived at the drive thru, I informed the one behind the speaker that it was a mobile order. They said okay, flashed the screen with my total and said to pull around.
 Sounds normal, right? It went totally downhill from there.
Pulled up to the pick up window, the person there asked if I was [a totally different order.] Said no, and repeated what we told the speaker person.  Perhaps it was not relayed originally, that happens. Person at the window closed it, and she and two others wandered around, looking for something and otherwise having confused looks on their faces. After looking for a piece of paper to write down the order, as the other in the car pulled out a game as we waited, I started to write down the order as I did not want to hand my phone to the people in the window. We sat in the car for about ten minutes before the person in the black shirt said "just waiting for the fries. " Okay, some hope there for the food to appear. I stopped writing. 
That hope was dashed before another 10-15 minutes go by and the window opens with a different employee (not the black shirt person) who hands us a bag with fries (there were three in there and we only ordered two, bag did not have a receipt on it either, which is usually the norm) and they close the window.
The other in the car and I look at each other and wonder what is going on, and where the rest of the food was. I started to write the order again as it looked like there was more confusion inside the building and more time before we get the rest of our food.
We checked the food (it was fine) and then had to ask for our drink.
Us: "Could we please have our sprite?"
Person in the black shirt: "ginger Ale!"
Us: "we ordered a sprite"
Person in the black shirt: "we don't have that."
(note: we were not informed of this via the app nor from the drive thru person)
Us: "can we get a coke?" as we paid for the drink and they seemed to be hoping that we would forget that. The other person with me was starting to get annoyed.
Person in the black shirt: "we don't have that either! "
Me: "a bottle of water then"
The window closes and a bottle of water soon appears and was given to us by one of the other employees. 
We thank that employee and leave only to discover that the sauce we had ordered was not in the bag.
It really should not have taken a half hour to get our food.

This location is one of the most dirty fast food restaurants that I have been into. Both toilets in the women's restroom were stained and uncleaned for a significant amount of time. The sink was similarly stained. The floors in the women's bathroom were so sticky that after I walked out my feet continue to stick to the floor in the Burger King. After I ordered I went to get a drink and found that the drink station area was covered with ants and the very few lids that were still remaining that were covered with dirt and crumbs. I waited 12 minutes and 46 seconds for my impossible whopper. While I stood there I watched three out of four employees put food together without any gloves. The one employee was wearing gloves kept putting her hands in and out of her pockets and rearranging her clothing, never changing the gloves that she was using. All of the food preparation areas were covered with food debris and the employees just kept putting new wrappers on top of the trash. There was a man with a beard who did not have a beard net. 

When I got to my car I found that they did not put the cheese on my whopper that I had paid for and the fries were cold. The restaurant had close the dining room area at that point, so I drove around to the drive-through. I was told I had to wait in line, but after a five minute wait without the line moving at all I just left.

Food has went downhill and so has the service. The chicken was extra greasy and we were shorted a piece last time we went here. I'll likely give it another try as hopefully it was just new staff, but it wasn't busy when we were there.
Decent food, sometimes the food is cold but that is rare, not sure if this goes for all kfc restaurants but I think they put too much breading on the chicken sometimes, but other then that it's okay.

It's been sometime since I felt an establishment deserved only 1 star.  I have no qualms about giving this KFC one.
 
Situated within and near the exit of a large strip mall, access to the drive through is not obvious.  I had recently seen commercials for their Nashville Hot Sandwich and their extra crispy tenders and the commercial convinced me to try them.
 
I order 10 of the extra crispy tenders and one of the Nashville Hot Sandwich.  I have fond memories of KFC from years ago when the crispy and uniquely seasoned chicken was some of the best around.  I tried one of the tenders, and was not impressed.  Not as crispy as it should have been and it seems like they changed the seasoning as well.  The other 9 tenders went to my grandson who loves chicken tenders of almost any type.
 
I had high hopes for the Nashville Hot Sandwich.  I will post a picture of what it looks like in the advertisement and another of what I got in reality.  From a soggy bun to a sad looking sandwich the pictures best describe the situation.  I took one bite and threw the rest in the trash.  Maybe some of the other KFCs in town are better, but I also felt the items were overpriced.  
 
Never again for me.

Ask for Breasts and I'm always told there are none. This is a chicken, they have to have Chicken breasts. I don't mind waiting nor do I mind paying more but to tell me no that really ticks me off. Same owner all the time. I won't be back

I didn't want to give no star... This location has the worst service ever.. I waited in the drive thru line for 20 minutes and when I arrived at the speaker I wasn't greeted or anything after sitting for another 10 minutes I said Hello  someone came on the speaker and said one minute. No apology was given and then once she was ready to take my order they only had certain chicken. No tenders, no wings.. I wasted all that time in line to get NOTHING!!! Ok

My family and I go to this KFC maybe twice a year.  We don't use the drive through in fear of errors, but even ordering at the counter has done little to save us.  The food is good, don't get us wrong.  We just wish someone would get our order right.  
We order sixteen pieces of chicken - half crispy, half regular.  It seems like we just get what is left.  Tonight we got our usual order and where disappointed to find that we had less chicken than we ordered, and less quality than anticipated.  But, imagine our shock when we opened the biscuit box to find more chicken.  But no biscuits.  What happened to our biscuits?
The chicken we ordered was cold wasn't even a full chicken on a 10 piece meal the chicken was so dry and the service was terrible avoid this place at all costs KFC has really lost it

What can I say?  I am a sucker for White Castle!  Unfortunately, well, fortunately, so is my coworker... so when we figured out that this White Castle was on the way to where we were headed... we had to stop and have a few!  We hit them at a good time and beat a rush... Also, everyone working there was in a great mood - which always scores extra brownie points.  For the first time, I had a BBQ pork slider... the sauce was too sweet for my taste, so I will stick with my regular cheeseburgers.

Horrible service. I know it's a Saturday night night and you only have drive thru open but good customer service is still expected. They had two cars. My mom went for me. She ordered 2 piece leg with Cole slaw and drink. They didn't have two legs so she supposed to be given a leg and thigh. No problem. The other one was 4 piece strips with cole slaw and drink. Asked for bbq sauce was told only have honey mustard. She said that was fine. 

She came home with the correct chicken but no sauce and mashed potatoes. And no receipt to prove what I paid. We went back to get it fixed. I asked if she could redo it all since everything was cold. Now the strips and sides were correct but they had no dark meat. It's a Saturday night between 7:30 and 8:00 pm. She got wings. Now we got all kinds of dipping sauce (was out of them a few mins ago). When I asked him f she could redo the order so everything was hot, she got an attitude, rolled her eyes and threw the bag on the counter. You might be wearing a mask but we can still hear the grunt and see the eyes roll. I still did not get a receipt; said they were out of paper. Some people like to have proof of payment. 

Last few times we've had either bad service or/and food. But when we do get the order right, it's good.

Close it down! As I was waiting (for awhile) on my order, a roach was crawling all over the counter near the restates, near the kitchen! It's unfortunate, I crave a whopper every so often and every location in St. Pete is dirty and the service is always so slow. There's staff there but they seem to be on vacation. I don't recommend!

In these times of crisis, there's nothing better than quick service to get your fried chicken fix. There was approximately 4 vehicles in front of us at the drive through and we were only in line for a matter of minutes. Food was extra crispy and perfect for getting a fried food craving. Fast service, good food and friendly workers.

Never seen so many 1 star reviews in my life, but they're all justified. This place just sucks. I have no idea how it can go on like this unchanged for so long. Employees are rude can't be bothered types. Place is filthy because the rude can't be bothered types can't be bothered to do anything but have an attitude and mess up your order. The drive thru is a joke. And when you get stuck waiting in it forever you can't leave cuz there's no way to go around, so you're trapped. They've never once gotten my order right. Never. I'll  ask for BBQ sauce and they'll hand me the bag literally two seconds later without it. And then have an attitude when you ask for it again. Last time, I opened my bag while still sitting there because I was positive it wouldn't be right. I was literally missing 4 things. And I had to ask four separate times for the stuff because I'd tell her about 1 thing missing and she'd slam the door shut go get it and come back so I'd have to keep saying what's missing one by one. Just so needlessly rude. And I have terrible anxiety so I HATE having to ask for the missing things and all that. But what can you do when they don't give you half your stuff! It's honestly the most poorly run fast food establishment I've ever been to in my life. And it's such a shame, because the chicken sandwich is pretty good. The last 2 times I tried to go- I didn't even get served. One time they said they were closed when it was more then an hour before closing time, and the second time they literally just came over the intercom and said they were "out of product." It's a Taco Bell/KFC combo, so you're telling me you were out of absolutely everything from both places?! Yeah, sure. The cars in front of us were served, so I'm assuming they just decided they were done early. They seem to do that often. Oh and like I said before, you're trapped in the drive thru, so I had to sit there and wait for the people in front of us who actually did get served to get thru. So I sat in a drive thru for 15 minutes to not get any food. That was the final straw. I'll never go back there. Really wish they would overhaul that place cuz like I said, I really like the food. Would be nice if it weren't such a miserable place to go.
